description: The PROBLEM: So much entertainment today pushes an unholy agenda. The stories we consume while our guard is down can desensitize us to sin, convince us to adopt untrue ideas and distract us from what actually matters. The SOLUTION: A Torah-Observant streaming platform! The Life Network (TLN) is a streaming service full of shows and films that inspire and help you to live out the truth. When you support this campaign, you get a subscription to the platform, which allows you to stream: The Way documentary The Christmas Question documentary The Life Podcast (ad-free!!) Groundwork: The Homestead Tour Show Two by Two: A Matchmaking Reality Show The Few : A Ladies Talk Show plus future projects, such as: documentaries, movies and series still in the minds of other creatives in our community and waiting to be produced! All subscribers to TLN will vote to choose which future projects we produce. Premium subscribers also get a LIFE Network mug and access to monthly, live Q+A hangouts with Luke, Kayte and other special guests . The minimum budget required to get TLN off the ground (with the current stable of shows discussed here) is $48,000. Anything raised above that number will fund the production of more great shows. GROUNDWORK: Homestead Tours with Experts ... in Their Fields A homestead tour show that connects you with seasoned homesteaders who share practical wisdom and their experience applying the Torah’s instructions to their homestead. The mission is to encourage and educate you to produce at least one of the " life support systems " you currently buy (such as water, food, power or sewage). STATUS: Three episodes filmed! Casting is open - apply here ! TWO BY TWO: When Being Set Apart Brings You Together is a matchmaking reality show for Torah-Observant Christians, guided by prayer and expert counsel. Often isolated in their convictions, singles are hand-selected and brought together for memorable dates and meaningful conversations—a first step towards real connection and lasting love. STATUS: Not yet filming. Casting is open ( apply HERE! ). THE FEW: A Torah-Observant Ladies Talk Show Grab a cup of coffee and sit down with Kayte and a rotating cast of ladies to chat about all things “whole-Bible-life” … from making feasts meaningful to women’s only health insights, to Shabbat-ing as a mom—we’re talking through those life and faith peculiarities to which we can all relate. Status: Three episodes filmed. THE LIFE PODCAST (ad free!): Weekly conversations that inspire you to thrive as peculiar people in a modern world. The LIFE Podcast will continue to air every Thursday on YouTube, Spotify, Apple and Rumble. For you who're members of The Life Network, you can stream the podcast ad free and in cases where it applies, uncensored. These are show concepts we might pitch to you, the TLN subscribers, if we surpass the $48,000 goal with enough margin to produce more shows. EXODUS: Family Bugout (Reality Show) Each episode follows a family who takes their bugout bags into the wilderness to see if they can survive for 72 hours with only the items they've packed. Throughout the three days, they'll complete missions, face surprise challenges and tasks meant to mimic unforeseen real-world survival scenarios. An (off-site) prepping expert will weigh in on the family's choices and challenges. Epic Bible Adventures (Kids' Stop-Motion Animation) Epic Bible Adventures is an animated, or stop-motion brick-world kids show that encourages kids to build strong faith as they dive into stories from the most exciting book of all time. From Daniel's courageous faithfulness to God in Babylon, to Abigail's quick-thinking, food-filled plan to save her family, to boy-king Josiah's big discovery, Epic Bible Adventures gives kids heroes worth looking up to and inspires epic faith in their Creator. Shepherds & Serpents (Documentary): How did Progressive Christianity "replace" God’s Sovereign truth with "your truth"? Has there always been a remnant of believers who've upheld a Whole-Bible faith? From ancient councils to modern pulpits, this film traces the destruction and the re-discovery of Torah-Observant doctrine through time and it pins the addition of church traditions and their removal to the responsible parties. Hillbilly Genius (Fictional Series): It's Sherlock Holmes in West Virginia. Mysteries keep falling into the lap of a reckless farmer with an IQ of 180, who solves them between tractor maintenance. He's helped by an ex-theology-professor at the local community college, who becomes his partner and chronicler. Red Pill Roulette (Docu-show) red pill : verb past tense: red-pilled metaphorically: to learn an unsettling or life changing truth. Every episode, a team of journalists and researchers puts a different "conspiracy" theory to the test to discover if it's conspiracy fact or fiction. Think: A MythBusters Torah Crew vetting wild ideas! A SOVEREIGN PLATFORM: Together we own the platform, the pipelines and the message. No censorship or subtle silencing. TRUTH IN ACTION: Everything we produce is designed to move you. Whether it’s forgiving a brother, starting a homestead, or pursuing a godly marriage—our stories are meant to inspire your next step. CREATE WITHOUT COMPROMISE: There are talented, creative believers in our community who’ve stepped away from their calling because of the compromise required in mainstream entertainment. At the same time, viewers with Biblical values are tempted to compromise them in exchange for solid stories and entertainment. Our vision is to make space for Torah-observant artists and storytellers to use their gifts to create high-quality media, that puts story and truth first, and aligns with the values of its makers and viewers. Just imagine ... heroes and hosts with tzitzits! We're Luke and Kayte Abaffy, the husband and wife team behind The Way documentary , The Christmas Question ( along with 119 Ministries ), the apologetics book The Truth: Reformation 2.0 and the weekly lifestyle podcast: The LIFE .  With your support, for nearly a decade we've been producing media for our community and now TLN is our vision for something bigger. Luke is an award-winning journalist, writer and director and Kayte is a homeschooling mama and creative director. We're everyday believers whose lives have been transformed by the simple idea that all of our Creator's instructions for us are good. If you haven't used Kickstarter, it’s a platform that allows creators to crowdfund their projects. The campaign lasts 30-days, and when over, we will go into full production mode. We will send out surveys, which will allow you to check over your pledge and make any last-minute modifications, and provide us with your shipping address (if required). If you’d like to know more about how to make a pledge on Kickstarter, here's a short overview.

Millions of American college students have studied abroad. Hundreds of thousands consider study abroad programs each year. No wonder! Study abroad programs combine education, travel, friendship, adventure, career training, wonderful experiences, and lots of fun. BUT ... are they safe? Study abroad programs are well promoted through glossy brochures which always display smiling students enjoying uniquely fun opportunities in desirable and/or exotic locations. But they're also ... big business. Students are recruited. Parents are solicited. And those glossy brochures say little to nothing about risks, precautions, or transparency. While most programs are safe, some families learn too late that study abroad programs are largely unregulated. Lack of shared information can increase risk of illness, injury, or worse. And tragedies can get "buried." Two loving moms - Elizabeth Brenner and Ros Thackurdeen - discovered this and joined forces to do something about it. L to R: Elizabeth Brenner and her son Thomas; Ros Thackurdeen and her son Ravi. STUDY ABROAD: Trip of a Lifetime is a documentary that follows the story of Ros and Elizabeth's sons' deaths, what these two moms learned in the aftermath, and their steadfast efforts to make student travel programs safer for others. Multiple Emmy-winning filmmaker Robyn Symon (director of "Do No Harm" ) tells this important story through the experiences of, and in conjunction with Elizabeth, Ros, and other families, with commentary by industry experts, and video diaries of students traveling abroad. Welcome to our Kickstarter campaign to help raise funds for STUDY ABROAD: Trip of a Lifetime. Please check out our Rewards. Thank you for visiting, and thanks for your support. === BY VIRTUE OF A FISCAL SPONSORSHIP through Protect Students Abroad Inc., pledges made to this campaign are tax-deductible , to the amount and extent allowable by law. See section on "Fiscal Sponsorship" - very bottom of page. === ABOUT THE FILM STUDY ABROAD: Trip of a Lifetime is about the multibillion-dollar study abroad industry. It recruits many students through regaled tales of friendship, education, adventure, and life experience. It's also an industry sorely lacking in oversight and transparency. Two moms discovered this truth through deep tragedy. They were doubly distressed when basic information as to how and why their sons died was neither complete nor fully forthcoming. Risks were poorly disclosed. Disclaimers were buried in proverbial fine print.  After the moms met and shared experiences, they joined forces to make student travel programs safer. Together, they formed PSA (Protect Students Abroad). L to R: Ros and Ravi Thackurdeen; Elizabeth Brenner and Thomas Plotkin; Ros and Elizabeth Filmmaker Robyn Symon will weave together personal stories of families touched by tragedy, augmented by industry experts offering context to both the history and the legal issues surrounding study abroad programs. The film will speak to the players involved, the free flow of money, and the less-than-free flow of information. It will feature video diaries of students traveling abroad around the world. Filmmaker Robyn Symon on location in Mali, Africa. Her documentary films have traversed the globe, making her uniquely familiar with locations commonly chosen by students studying abroad. The heart of the story follows Ros Thackerdeen and Elizabeth Brenner in their unrelenting fight for industry transparency and accountability - a journey that brings them face to face with industry leaders, all the way to the halls of Congress. In 2012, Ros' youngest son Ravi and his course mates, while on a study abroad program in Costa Rica, were treated by organizers to an end-of-semester trip to a beach called Playa Tortuga. When Ravi stepped into the bay, he was quickly pulled away from shore by a powerful riptide.  Organizers had no telephones and no rope. Ravi's body was found days later by local fishermen.  Ros was shocked to learn that Costa Ricans knew not to swim at this dangerous beach precisely because of riptides. Ravi in Costa Rica in 2012. He was his program's lone male participant. In 2011, Elizabeth Brenner's 20-year-old son Thomas, while on a study abroad program in India, went on a month-long programmed hike with his course mates and faculty. The hike included narrow trails perched 300 feet above a river. It was raining. It was slippery. Instructors lagged behind hikers by one half-hour, rendering the hike as largely unsupervised. Thomas fell, landed in the river, and was never found. Three days after the horrific tragedy, Elizabeth was shocked to learn that Thomas was his program’s 12th death. Thomas in India in 2011. He kept a journal of his experiences. Each mom believed their sons would be well looked after while studying abroad, especially since the schools connected to Thomas and Ravi were the University of Iowa, University of Utah, Swarthmore College and Duke. But these vaunted institutions became guarded with information once things went awry. Walls went up. Lawyers entered the discourse. As the moms learned more about the industry, they discovered there wasn't even a central database. They learned that many study abroad programs used subcontractors and not all were reputable. The industry was big money, and many exotic locales were impoverished environs, which sometimes led to a fateful cutting of corners. STUDY ABROAD: Trip of a Lifetime will impart useful and important lessons and tips based on these revelations: As their research progressed, it didn't take long for Ros and Elizabeth to learn that there were many tragic incidents, most of which had gone unreported. Top Row, L to R:  Beau Solomon, Jasmine Jahanshahi, Joshua Shane; Middle Row, L to R:  Andrew Mogni, Briana McHam, John Durkin, Zoe Damon; Bottom Row, L to R:  Morgan Boisson, Daniel Hollnsteiner, Allison Willen, Kassie Braun ------------- ABOVE AND BELOW: GONE TOO SOON, NEVER FORGOTTEN ------------- Top Row, L to R: Nohemi Gonzalez, Jenna Druck, Brett Crawford, Sujal Parikh; Middle Row, L to R: Aron Sobel, Nicolas Leslie, Cherese Mari Laulhere; Bottom Row, L to R: TeNiya Jones, Virginia Amato, Ashley Walls, Damion Wilkins, Olivia Chan Shipp, Elmer G. Arrieta Fueled by love for their sons and a desire to protect future students, they formed Protect Students Abroad (PSA). For nearly a decade, PSA has provided an evidence-based resource to help track student safety incidents, to promote risk awareness among families, to offer guidance and tips to parents and students, and to persuade lawmakers to enact legislation protecting students abroad. Congressman Sean Patrick Maloney introduced transparency legislation, saying: "Right now, it's buyer beware. We need accountability so parents and students can make informed decisions and keep our kids safe." In fact, the proposed legislation is called the Ravi Act, after Ros' son. Sadly, Congressman Maloney lost his reelection bid this November, so it is hopeful that the publicity from this film will also help the fight to keep this bill alive. PERILS, PRECAUTIONS, AND PREPARATION The perils that are present in study abroad programs are intrinsic to the fact that there is a complex maze of interests involved, including colleges and universities, independent study abroad programs, schools of record, accrediting agencies, professional membership organizations, federal and state governments, insurance companies, and American business. It's important to know who is running the program you are considering - as in who is really running it, and what's their safety record. Given the fiscal vagaries of an emerging global world, these varied entities have distinct reasons for encouraging the growth of study abroad, though none of them has advocated for industry wide comprehensive and transparent student safety data . Some study abroad programs involve hikes or field trips in treacherous terrain, deep brush, white water, with dangerous animal and plant life, and sometimes, human hostility and criminality. Our film advocates the mitigation of these dangers through awareness - not just precautions and preparation, like packing the right equipment - but an understanding of the truthful history of a program, and who is running it. EDUCATION, TRAVEL, FRIENDSHIP, AND FUN As part of STUDY ABROAD, Trip of a Lifetime's goal to better inform and potentially save lives, the film will shine a light on tragedies, industry-wide secrecy, and the resulting injustice experienced by students and their families.  All involved in this film believe strongly that the dead shouldn't have to pay twice, once with their life, and then again, uncounted, untold, and eventually, disappeared. All involved with the film recognize and realize the value of global education through study abroad programs, and that it can and should be a magnificent life-changing experience full of opportunity. TRAVEL, SEE THE WORLD, AND LEARN ABOUT OTHER CULTURES FRIENDSHIP, TOGETHERNESS, AND SHARED EXPERIENCES AND DREAMS PEACE, INTROSPECTION, EDUCATION, AND HELPING TO MAKE A BETTER WORLD PSA (Protect Students Abroad) is an evidence-based resource that helps track student safety incidents, to promote risk awareness among families, to offer guidance and tips to parents and students, and to persuade lawmakers to enact legislation protecting students abroad. In fact, the proposed legislation is called the Ravi Act, after Ros' son. ABOUT THE REWARDS ALL REWARDS "CHOICES" (like t-shirt size) are made AFTER the campaign; delivery dates are approximate; if you'd like a combination or quantity not offered on the page, click the round logo of PSA (top R), send a message and we'll work it out. MOST REWARDS also receive some lower Rewards - check the "bullets" at the bottom of the Rewards boxes. Here's a little extra detail on some of the Rewards (from lo to high), with a few photos: THE ONE-TIME STREAM OF THE FILM allows you to see it before its general release. THE DOWNLOAD OF THE FILM is for you to own, and it will include extra's; please note it won't be available until the film completes its primary run; all download rewards also include a ONE-TIME STREAM OF THE FILM. L: The Travel Case; R: The T-Shirt EVERY REWARD AT $95 OR MORE also includes YOUR NAME in the film's closing credits, as part of a scroll, under: " The Producers Wish to Thank ..." THE ZOOM PACKAGE ($200) is a wonderful "insider" Reward that gives you the opportunity to have several Zoom calls with the team. Each Zoom call ends with a Q and A session. LIMITED EDITION COLLECTIBLE ART ($600) is a beautiful museum-quality fine art print, signed and numbered by the artist: Reza Djahanshahi. Reza is a very talented painter and sculptor (see samples of his gorgeous work below). He is creating this unique fine art Reward for this campaign to help the film, and in tribute to his daughter Jasmine, his "dream girl," who was lost in a Paris apartment fire. The art will be shipped flat, accompanied by a personal message from the artist. IN MEMORIAM ($1400) is a Reward that pays tribute to a person of your choice who was taken too soon. When the film ends and the credits begin to roll, we soon see "In Memoriam" on the screen and we honor a select few people who are departed, by name, with brief information if you choose to share it. The names scroll slowly. This reward is limited in quantity. STRATEGIC ALLIANCE ($10,000) is our highest-level Reward. Please read the information in the rewards box, and if you'd like to reach out to discuss, please click the small round PSA logo on the top right of the page, and then click CONTACT US. Thank you. MEET THE FILMMAKER Robyn Symon Robyn Symon is a two-time Emmy Award-winner and television producer who turned her attention to the indie film arena in 2007 with the documentary film Transformation about the controversial leader of the self-help group ‘ est ,’ picked up for international distribution by Screen Media Films and Netflix . She has produced four other feature documentary films, all winning film festival awards and picked up for international distribution including Behind the Blue Veil (Journeyman Pictures), Uncle Gloria and Do No Harm (PBS/AMAZON). FILMMAKER COMMENTARY Says Robyn: “Too many families sign up for study abroad programs without understanding the potential risks. They don't realize the extent to which the industry lacks transparency, and most could not fathom the degree to which institutions they know and trust, would behave in a manner that is resistant to making study abroad programs safer for students. This is truly a case where a little information can save lives. I am honored to be working on this important film." MEET THE TEAM Elizabeth Brenner has lived all over the US, most recently in West Palm Beach, Florida, with her husband Barry Knight. She is trained as a nurse-midwife and also has a degree in writing. Elizabeth is mom to three sons, Joseph, Daniel, and Thomas, mother-in-law to Andrea and Kayy, nana to Olivia, Jacob, and Miles, and bonus mom to Alexandra and Max. After Thomas's tragic death, Elizabeth co-founded Protect Students Abroad (PSA). Roshni ("Ros") Thackurdeen grew up in Queens and now lives in Fishkill, New York. She was trained as a school counselor and has worked for the New York City public school system. Ros is married to Raj. They have three children, Savita, Sean, and Ravi, plus daughter-in-law Julie and son-in-law Giuseppe. After Ravi's tragic death, Ros co-founded Protect Students Abroad (PSA). BUDGET, TIMELINE, AND USE OF PROCEEDS WHY KICKSTARTER We came to Kickstarter so that we could tell this story truthfully and the best way to do that, especially here, is to serve no other masters and speak independently. We also want to interact with a community, some of whom might be considering educational travel like study abroad programs, who we believe will appreciate and support it. What better place for us than Kickstarter, where independent films that speak truth to like-minded communities are forged, fostered, and supported every day. QUESTIONS? If you've got questions, we're pleased to respond. At the top of the page on the right there is a small round image where it says Protect Students Abroad Inc. - click that, then click CONTACT ME to send us a message. Thanks! === PROTECT STUDENTS ABROAD INC. ("PSA") is a 501c3 company that advocates for transparent and comprehensive safety data from the study abroad industry. PSA believes in better study abroad through the science of safety. A primary focus of PSA is fatality prevention; it believes that all fatality prevention strategies are based on the knowledge of previous incidents. PSA is proud to fiscally sponsor this important film. By virtue of its fiscal sponsorship herein, pledges made to this campaign are tax-deductible , to the amount and extent allowable by law.

title: Cartoon Network Alphabet Pins
description: Full A-Z Set I'm launching this set to show my love to the early 2000's Cartoon Network shows. I started out on a type challenge where you design a letter each day A-Z. Instead of hand lettering or creating standard letters I chose to illustrate nostalgic characters from some of my favorite shows. Each letter corresponds to a character from a different show. A is Aku from Samurai Jack, B is BMO from Adventure Time and C is Cheese from Foster's Home for Imaginary Friends and the list goes on. I chose to turn this into a pin set because A) I wanted to see this project come to life outside of my laptop and Instagram feed. As well as B) the fact that I believe Cartoon Network is a great network and like me many others will see the beauty behind this set and want to get in on the deals. Kickstarter is a Pledge Now, Pay Later system. Kickstarter will not charge you until the end of the campaign and only if we reach our initial funding goal. You can always edit or cancel your pledge before the campaign ends. The goal of $8000 will allow me to print the full 26 pin set and any additional rewards backers will gain. When you pledge, you’ll be able to choose which letter(s) you would like for the reward. The limited edition poster and pin are the same throughout so no need to select those. When the campaign is over, we will send our a form to backers allowing them to choose which pin they would like to select. Style: Soft Enamel Size: 1” Metal plating: Plating will be Black Nickel or similar to keep with the black outline synonymous with cartoons and their weighted outlines The additional pieces would include the limited edition poster not available to the public as well as a limited edition pin that won't be for sale after the campaign. The poster will be shown below but I will be keeping the limited edition a surprise to try and keep people interested. Full Pin Set Alphabet Poster (numbers not available) Campaign launches on May 21, 2022 It will run for 45 days until July 5, 2022 If the campaign is successful I will be working hard all summer to produce the pins and rewards to send them out in October of 2022, I won't keep everyone waiting. Shipping : US shipping will be standard ground in 1-5 business days. Non US based backers will be responsible for any custom duties or applicable taxes as well as shipping fees in general, I'm sorry but for my first campaign I just don't want the added stress of international shipping. Refunds & Cancellations: You can edit, add or cancel your pledge at any point before the end of the campaign. Once the campaign is completed, refunds or cancellations are not able to be accepted. Make sure your payment information is updated at the end of the campaign. If your payment isn’t received, you will fall out of the campaign and you won’t have access to campaign pre-order pricing. Once the pledge is collected, it will be assumed that the backer has read and accepted all terms, risks and the estimated timeline. Lastly I just wanted to thank you all for reading through, viewing or even considering my project in the first place.

title: The Balloon - a short film
description: On a sleepy summer afternoon, we stared into the void of the floating spectre that idled between us.  Patiently, holding its ground, it stole our focus with its own. There was a volume to the feeling of being watched by something without eyes. It’s loud,  cacophenous. A discordant pairing of the natural and uncanny. Both intentional and inert. A mirror for our haunted minds that offered a glimpse of a soul. Entranced by the intransigence of its flight, we sat there for hours, wondering what it could see in us that we couldn’t? And from this question, our story took shape, as we searched for answers in ourselves and our new floating friend. The story: The Balloon tells the story of Sasha, a young artist searching for value in a society that has taken hers, save that which is placed upon her body by the predatory men in her life. To support her craft, Sasha teaches art to the young sons of businessman and leading Senate candidate Ric Hammond, who has used his power and charm to seduce her into an extramarital affair. However, Sasha is beginning to question Ric's promises of a life together. On a particularly confronting work day, Sasha attempts to inspire her screen-obsessed students by drawing a smiley face on a helium balloon. This inadvertent self-portrait forces her to confront her own lack of inspiration.  And after taking the balloon home, she begins to feel a connection to her new friend's hollow eyes and painted smile. The balloon reciprocates as it subtly begins to move autonomously, with its deadly intention to help Sasha by any means necessary. It beheads Marcus, a fine-art nepo baby, stringing Sasha along with promises of success while he uses her for his pleasure. When she wakes to see the balloon floating over his headless body, she begins to question her sanity. But before Sasha can figure out what happened, an enraged Ric arrives with a bag of hush money to buy her silence. Confused,  Sasha tries to reason with him, but upon seeing Marcus’ body, her erstwhile lover pulls a gun. Still, he can’t escape the slow marching death of the balloon, as he trips over its string and the gun fires in his face. Scared that she will be the balloon's next target, Sasha flees to the bathroom. But as the door slowly opens, Sasha sees herself standing in the doorway with Ric’s blood-soaked bag and gun. Faced with the truth about her hidden rage, it all becomes clear to Sasha. What she does next is hers, and hers alone, to decide. At its loftiest, the balloon is the story of how repression and control push people to revolution as the anxieties and resentment of the subjugated begin to surface from their subconscious. But despite these lofty themes, we find joy in telling a story that, at its core, is a fun monster movie.  An idea to make something weird and terrifying, as we play with the contrast between light and dark, fun and scary, and the real and surreal moments we experience in our everyday lives. Why are we making this film? Pursuing a career in the arts is hard. We hustle to break in, searching for anyone who might help us escape our litany of side gigs. We face the choice to lose parts of ourselves as we endure exploitation in the name of forward progress, or are forced to find another avenue that might never appear. Our dreams of fulfillment and financial stability are used against us as leverage for power and control. This short is our cathartic expression of frustration. It is fun and weird and far healthier than having our subconscious turn into murderous party decorations. It is a silly, scary salve for our souls. Our Team Kristen Waters, writer/producer. Kristen is a writer and indoor plant gardener based in Los Angeles. She studied theatre in Melbourne, Australia, got very good grades in high school English, and then studied theatre again at the Stella Adler Academy of Acting and Theatre Los Angeles. She is thrilled to debut this film with her best friend and writing partner, Jordan Mueck. Claire Chronis, producer. Claire Chronis is a Los Angeles-based producer who has worked on short films, music videos, and social media campaigns like Birds Aren’t Real. She pivoted from a biology degree to a career in production and viral marketing after graduating in 2020 and hasn’t looked back since . Cassie Howell, director. Cassie is a Los Angeles-based film director and artist originally from Denver, Colorado. She strives to tell stories that build empathy and change perspectives, prioritizing working with women and non-binary filmmakers to create empowering and aesthetic-motivated media. She is honored and excited to work with writers who trust her to bring their vision to life. Jordan Mueck, writer/producer. Jordan is a screenwriter and labor organizer whose first feature film, “Love’s Fast Lane,” has been streamed and aired on television worldwide, including in his native home of Melbourne, Australia. Since moving to Los Angeles, Jordan has partnered with his best mate, Kristen, across multiple industries and is excited to bring their new project to life.

48 hours of pledge matching! Amazing news! Two very generous backers have offered to match pledges for the next 48 hours. Whatever you pledge right now will be DOUBLED! Midway check-in 💚 Just over the midway mark, at 54% funded and 12 days to go, we have some fresh content for you. "I was surprised by how entertained I was" — a cisgender ally, upon watching this video. Share, pledge — whatever moves you. Just remember… Pride starts in 2 days… The Story “Something You Should Know About Me,” is a meditation on trans representation and desire housed in the language of a raunchy teen rom-com. You know, like Judd Apatow, but make it gay. And trans. From "Superbad" The film follows Al, a twenty-something, transgender, aspiring cartoonist who can’t draw sex. When he tries to, weird things tend to happen; human genitals turn into household objects, bodies distort, and characters say strange things. Hoping to escape from the doldrums of his daily life—a humiliating assistant job at a toothbrush startup, a gaggle of straight girl roommates who don’t get him, and bad sex—Al signs up for a cartooning workshop housed on the campus of an all-girls private boarding school with his best friend Jesse. At camp, it’s only a matter of time before Al realizes that he is wildly in love with Jesse and (this being a rom-com) he must do everything in his power to convince Jesse they are meant to be together. But competition has arrived in the form of Mason, an already-published indie cartoonist who oozes charisma. Al’s attempts to ward off his rival and win over the boy of his dreams play out in a series of hijinks so goofy, obnoxious, and raunchy that they could only be attributed to someone who has just escaped the throes of puberty and another round of teen angst. Which, Al has. Director's Statement “Something You Should Know About Me” plays with the tropes of teenage sex comedies and uses them in service of the project of trans liberation. It uproots the trans experience from where it is usually hidden in this genre and re-centers it where it deserves to be at the forefront of stories of bodies becoming new again. And it does so with an unusual tool—the language of comics. Merging artistic mediums, Al’s inner life will be represented on screen by his cartoon avatar. His many unsuccessful attempts to draw sex will play out on screen in animation, as do his self-consciously cruel caricatures of the classmates he envies. This blend of live action and animation adds a further dimension to the representation of trans bodies on screen. The trans characters we see in films often pass as cisgender, and we are shown their bodies as an endpoint, the natural conclusion after a protracted internal struggle. Animation complicates this representation and demonstrates how this process can be non-linear, evolving, and sometimes deeply funny. Background Story When I first started contemplating medical transition, it was right at the beginning of the pandemic. I had just completed my first short film “Gay Teen Werewolf,” a campy story about a girl who is seemingly at peace with her complicated (gay, werewolf) identity, but still feels like something is missing. When the world locked down, filming became complicated and I needed to find another way to express myself. So I spent a lot of time drawing. At that point, I couldn’t really do it at all. I represented myself as a stick figure with wire-rimmed glasses, in a deep hole of discomfort, misery, and self-doubt. But I had the sense that if I could get myself onto the page, in some form, it would help me figure something out. First comic ever drawn, December 2019 Once I started the process in earnest, I kept drawing. And the drawings transformed. They became colorful, silly, horny, and strange. Some of them are hard to look at now—the sheer earnestness, the optimism, the heightened emotions. But in those pages, I found another way of telling my story, and discovered there were other queer and trans people out there making cool, gay, weirdo comics too. I realized that this was a world I wanted to capture on the screen. A unique, niche community of independent comic making and the queer people who comprise so much of it. But, transition and comic making had become inextricably tied together in my mind. When it came time to make a movie about both, I knew I didn’t want to write a film about transition, where trans storytellers are so often made to start and end their narratives. I wanted to look beyond the expected and explore what comes next when you’ve figured out your body and your identity because you still have a whole life to live ahead of you. Al struggles with what so many of us struggle with, cis or trans—seeing oneself as they truly are and learning to embrace that. Meet Our Team ANDY FIDOTEN is a filmmaker and ex-punk rocker based in New York City. A Manhattan native, Fidoten creates alternative worlds distinct from and deeply embedded in our own, turning contemporary anxieties around gender and sexuality into cinematic playgrounds filled with humor, fantasy, and horror. Be it through gay werewolves, gender-bending insect-borne diseases, or post-apocalyptic breakups, Fidoten's imaginative storytelling enlivens the complexities of queer identity. Some places they have shown work recently include Film at Lincoln Center, The Museum of Modern Art, the Santa Fe International Film Festival, the London Short Film Festival, and Final Girls Berlin. Andy’s greatest accomplishment so far has been composing a song that New York Times rock critic Caryn Ganz wrote sounded " Sleater-Kinney ish ". RILEY NIGHTINGALE is a Brooklyn-based Cinematographer whose films have been celebrated at South x Southwest, NewFest, and the Chelsea Film Festival, among others. An autodidact who did not go to film school, Riley has developed a unique artistic lens, one guided by a life committed to seeking out diverse experiences, and a process informed by the on-the-ground needs of film sets. Directors who work with Riley are offered a truly collaborative and supportive experience. Her artistic process is built on trust – she sees her function as that of a film doula, engaging over ten years of experience in film to execute the vision of the Director through a collaborative, holistic process. She firmly believes that who is behind the lens matters – in the stories that get told and the ways in which they are shared. As a woman of transgender experience, Riley’s work is deeply empathetic and person-focused, always attempting to build empathy between the characters on screen and those watching. Personal projects celebrate stories of queerness, gender diversity, ecoconnection, and imagining impossible futures. The centrality of identity is alive in all of Riley’s work – she hires Black, indigenous, person of color (BIPOC), queer, and gender-diverse crew whenever possible, and seeks to work on stories by diverse creators. Coming soon: Cast announcements We have some truly incredible actors attached to this project who we are looking forward to announcing... very soon! Where Your Money Will Go

title: The Woman Who Knows: A Psychological Thriller Feature Film (Canceled)
description: The crowdfunding campaign goal for The Woman Who Knows is to initially raise a portion of PRODUCTION funds for the feature film. A mystery psychological thriller that explores disability, mental health, and chronic and invisible illness (fibromyalgia). A film that entertains with a whodunit mystery and educates on the above conditions. We need to understand chronic and mental illness to be able to show compassion toward sufferers. Hopefully, those with chronic or mental illness understand that we are loved and we deserve to keep living so keep fighting. SCHEDULE: Development: March 2025- Production: Dec 2026 to Jan 2027 Post Production: Jan to April 2027 Needed funds: CAMERA CAST TRAVELING & LODGING and… PRODUCTION $30,000 POST PRODUCTION EDITORIAL SOUND MUSIC The goal is to raise initially $10,000 with a stretch goal of $60,000. Funds are to go to cast, crew and location. Funds will also go to the sound mixer and audio engineer, the film editor, the colorist, and the music composer. Funds will also go toward the film festivals, feeding cast and crew along with housing them. WRITER & EXECUTIVE PRODUCER; LEAD ACTOR Alicia McClendon is a screenwriter, filmmaker, and actor and champions female driven and diverse storytelling. She'll play the protagonist in The Woman Who Knows as she has lived the story of the protagonist in terms of her health. Alicia has written, directed, and produced many short films from Killings of the Land to the proof of concept short film, The Woman Who Knows , which premiered at the Big Apple Film Festival in Los Angeles in 2024. Lastly, she is a martial artist and studies Wing Chun, BJJ, Kali, and JKD kickboxing while also taking an on camera scene study acting class under acting coach Jim Dougherty. AWARDS and NOMINATIONS 2024 Best TV/Web Series at the Las  Vegas Black Film Festival 2022 Best Thriller at the Bare Bones International Film and Music Festival 2022 Best Actor at the Best Actor & Director Awards 2022 Honorable Mention at the Big Apple Film Festival IMDb: https://m.imdb.com/name/nm7332811/?ref_=fn_all_nme_1 DIRECTOR Geoff Ryan is filmmaker by craft, storyteller by heart, and seeker of truths through the art of fiction. As a writer/director his first feature film, 'Fray,' was called "superior" by the LA Times and won the Grand Jury Prize at Dances With Films. Ryan's latest film, 'Blood From Stone' was called a "genre-defying" film by Variety and currently has a 100% critic score on Rotten Tomatoes. Ryan has also directed & shot commercials for Macy’s, Stetson, David’s Bridal, and more. His screenplay for Fray was selected for the Academy of Motion Picture Arts & Science’s permanent library collection. PRODUCER Keli Price is CEO and President of Price Productions. He executive produced the feature film Bandit , starring Josh Duhamel and Mel Gibson, which was number 1 on Apple TV for independent film and number 5 for all films on Apple TV in 2022. Price is currently executive producing the feature film, Hellfire , starring Stephen Lang, Harvey Keitel, and Dolph Lundgren.

title: The Aughts
description: Mission Statement Return to the 2000s with The Aughts — a half-hour, anthology multi-camera sitcom AND single-camera dramedy set in the time of flip phones, frosted tips, MySpace Top 8s, and cultural chaos. Our mission is to tell a heartfelt, hilarious, and nostalgic story about growing up in a decade that still shapes us today. Let's rewind and hit play. The Story At its core, The Aughts follows a group of friends in their late teens as they stumble through two worlds: a sitcom portrayal of hanging out with their friends and the dramatic world of what's really going on. We’re blending the wit of That 70s Show , the emotional punch of Freaks and Geeks , and the filmmaking gimmick of Kevin Can F*** Himself . With your help, we can bring this nostalgic-yet-relevant series to life. The Aughts is an anthology series with each episode focusing on a different character’s life outside the basement. This proof of concept will be the first episode titled “The D aught er”. The Pilot "The D aught er” follows our character, Rowan - a Vietnamese American teenager from Northern California. Her world is populated by best friends Kai, Sienna, and Rivers, as well as new friends, Darcy and Dario. Outside of her friend group, her closest relationship is with her mother, Lillian, who is showing signs of early onset dementia. As the audience, we view Rowan balance these two parts of her life, but, as we all know about best laid plans, she can’t stop these worlds from bleeding into each other. The Team Just some of our team! The Timeline Why you "AUGHT" to support! The Rewards We have a lot of cool physical and virtual rewards for our supporters. For people interested in coming to the premiere but are not located in Northern California, we will be offering a virtual screening with cast and crew as well. We also wanted to shoutout Rookee Studios for designing our stickers! The Goal The goal of The Aughts is to eventually produce the rest of the season. But that all starts with the first episode being really good. With your help, we are going to bring this pilot to festivals, to pitches, and to networking events with the goal of finding long-term support and create the other seven episods. Where you can find us @TheAughtsTV on Instagram @TheAughtsTV on Tiktok The Aughts TV on Facebook @TheAughtsTV on X Website

title: Golden Goalie
description: Join Me Join me in a new community of Golden Goalies as we find our way through improving our game and experience on ice. Playing inline or floor hockey? You're welcome, too! The goalie experience is unique. As such, we have unique requirements from gear to preparation. And we celebrate our achievements and approach our challenges a little differently too. This project started as a blog to document my own journey through starting on the ice late in life and navigating toward my goal of being the oldest goalie in college hockey. But it has grown to be so much more. In my conversations with other goalies, in my competition with other goalies, in my training with other goalies, I have discovered a world unto its own. I'd like to share that world and invite other goalies, goalie parents, goalie fans, and the goalie-curious to participate in that world. Golden Goalie will be a community where we share our experiences through our Web site, social media, online meet-ups, and eventually in person meetups. Some things planned and in the pipeline already are the Golden Goalie Cookbook, a guide for preparing easy and tasty meals with goalie nutrition in mind; private social media groups, podcasts, and events for subscribers; public areas in social media for sharing to a wider audience; a Web site to share experiences and reviews of equipment and other kit. We plan on having guest speakers from nutritionists to trainers (both on ice and off), to coaches and scouts, to current and ex-professional goalies. Our journey is always beginning. The position has changed, and we must always look to make improvements, adjustments, and to learn techniques. I'm sure some of you grew up playing stand up. Some of you started playing making skate saves and transitioned to butterfly. Many of you have started butterfly and are learning or have mastered RVH. Whatever comes next, Golden Goalie will be here to break it down as we share our discoveries, revel in our achievements, and salve those wonky or ugly losses. So join me! You'll be glad you did.

title: Voyage the 7 Seas to Neverland: Tarot & Guidebook
description: We’re Jerry and Matt, the creative force behind Mystic Madness Tarot Co. As partners in life and art, we combine our talents to craft tarot decks that transport you into richly imagined worlds. This deck has been nearly two years in the making, shaped by patience, reflection, and the themes that guide its story: flowing with the tides of the universe, trusting the call of the sea, and daring to seek your true treasure. Just as Peter Pan and the Lost Boys longed for adventure and belonging, these cards speak to the pursuit of dreams and the courage it takes to follow them. With gratitude and excitement, we’re honored to share this deck with you. And now, with your help bringing this vision to life, we set sail and... Voyage the 7 Seas to Neverland invites you into a world of adventure and mysticism, where classic nautical tales meet the magic of the unknown. Anchored in the element of water and guided by the allure of the moon and stars, this deck embodies the breadth of human emotion—the depths and currents, light and shadow, the seen and the unseen. ✨ First Edition Upgrades — EXCLUSIVE  KICKSTARTER  PRINT! ✨ These upgrades were originally set as stretch goals — but we’re making them part of the First Edition print run from the start. That means every backer gets them automatically once we hit our goal. ✧ Serialized Number Foil on Box Each deck is marked with its own unique serial number in foil, making this run a true collector’s item — just like our previous deck. This is exclusive to our Kickstarter print run. ✧ Metal Book Corner Protectors The box is reinforced with ornate metal corners, adding both elegance and long-lasting durability. ✧ Magnetic Flip Box with Book Clasp Design A sturdy flip-top box styled like a storybook, secured with a magnetic clasp for easy opening and closing. ✧  Painted Deck Edges Smooth matte finish on all card edges, offering a premium feel in hand and extra protection against wear. ✧ Silver Foil Accents on Card Artwork Silver foil accents are carefully placed to emphasize and animate key elements within the artwork. ✧ Stories Woven Through the Deck This deck invites you to sail alongside Sinbad, Jim Hawkins, Peter Pan, and the Lost Boys on a mythic journey to the Fountain of Youth hidden within Neverland’s jungle. Along the way, you’ll encounter mermaids, pirates, sea witches, and other timeless beings. Each card is a doorway into that world, carrying both story and symbolism for your own path of self-discovery. Peter Pan & the Lost Boys — The Eternal Child “All the world is made of faith, and trust, and pixie dust.” — J.M. Barrie, Peter Pan (1911) Peter reminds us of the inner child within us all—the part that still believes in wonder, magic, and timeless play. This theme is about rediscovering that spark we thought was gone, learning from what once blocked us, and finding joy in becoming whole again. And it’s here, in the shadows of said blockages, that we meet The Devil himself: Captain Hook. The Little Mermaid — Longing and Transformation “To all the good in our power, we receive an immortal soul and take part in the happiness of mankind” — Hans Christian Andersen, The Little Mermaid (1837) The Mermaid’s tale is not only about love and loss, but about one's own becoming. Her story calls us to step into transformation, to risk, to reach—and to recognize that even the longing itself shapes us. Each choice deepens the soul and opens new paths we couldn’t see before. Jim Hawkins in Treasure Island — The Call of Adventure “Seaward ho! Hang the treasure! It’s the glory of the sea that has turned my head.” — Robert Louis Stevenson, Treasure Island (1883) Jim’s story is about answering the call to adventure. His journey reminds us that treasure isn’t always gold — it’s courage, discernment, and the willingness to follow curiosity into the unknown. Sinbad the Sailor — Fortune’s Wheel ! “When I escaped drowning and reached the island which afforded me fruit to eat and water to drink, I returned thanks to the Most High" — The Fifth Voyage of Sinbad the Sailor , The Arabian Nights (trans. Galland, 1704–1717) Sinbad teaches us that life’s wheel turns through storms and trials, but always brings us back richer in wisdom. His voyages show that fate shapes us not to break us, but to strengthen us, so we can return home changed. Aladdin — The Diamond in the Rough “He knew he was not to blame for being poor, and that fortune must one day change.” — The Arabian Nights, The Story of Aladdin (trans. Galland, 1704–1717) Aladdin’s tale is about alignment with the universe — daring to hold a vision even when circumstances say otherwise. From “street rat” to diamond, his story shows that true magic is the courage to believe and create despite the odds. ✧ Meet the Major Arcana Our journey begins in the dark streets of London as the bell strikes midnight. This is where we take aim and flight toward that particular star, making our way to Neverland by way of the sea and sky. Each card in the deck captures a moment of this grand voyage—where legendary characters and mystical forces converge on the high seas. ✧ Minor Arcana Wands Cups Swords Pentacle Disclaimer: Our artwork is highly iterative and continually evolving until it is sent to the printers. Our goal is to enhance the imagery with as much detail and symbolism possible before it's all printed. We thank you for your understanding and we welcome any feedback you might have! ✧ The Storybook Guide This is a narrative-driven guidebook , where every card plays a role in an unfolding tale. Together, they create a mythic world of adventure, mysticism, and shadow work — inviting you to not just read the cards, but live the story they tell. *Images pictured above are prototypes and may vary slightly from actual product. Why fund our project? We are needing to raise funds in order to manufacture and ship our products nationwide. Not only would you be receiving a beautifully designed tarot deck, but you would also help us to continue building upon this epic adventure that we started back in 2023, so the more pledges, the better! This isn’t just another tarot deck. This is a meticulously designed collector’s edition, limited in quantity, enhanced with the highest quality materials, and made to be treasured for years to come. If you're familiar with our Limited 1st Edition tarot deck "Journey to Oz & Wonderland", then you will have a good idea of the quality we plan to deliver. :) Here are the specifications for Voyage the 7 Seas to Neverland: TAROT DECK : 78 illustrated Tarot Cards following the Rider-Waite Tarot format Printed on 350 GSM Card-stock with an anti-scratch matte finish, spot uv 2.75" x 4.75" (Standard tarot card size) Flip Top Box - anti-scratch matte finish 3.5" x 5.5" Guidebook (~136pg) 78 beautifully written guidebook entries –one for each card (Specifications may be adjusted depending on stretch goals outlined below.) Some of these upgrades were originally set as stretch goals — but we’re making them part of the First Edition print run from the start. That means every backer gets the indicated upgrades, listed below, automatically once we hit our goal. UPGRADE NOW INCLUDED -- Tarot Deck Upgrades Unlock this stretch goal and every card will be enhanced with foil stamping, and metallic matte-painted edges—creating a deck that feels as magical as it looks. UPGRADE NOW INCLUDED -- Magnetic flip-top box : Unlock this stretch goal to elevate your deck with a premium magnetic flip-top box—crafted for the ultimate unboxing experience. UPGRADE NOW INCLUDED -- Limited First Edition (with serial numbering): We would love to have this exclusive version feature metallic foil detailing and serialized numbering, just like our first tarot deck, "Journey to Oz & Wonderland." Each deck is meant to be a true collector’s item, part of a strictly limited print run. Please help us spread the word and make this deck truly unforgettable! Bonus Cards: If we manage to unlock this stretch goal, we'll reveal 4 exclusive lore cards that will be included in each deck! These cards are unique to our theme and are designed to expand on the world that we've created. Unlock this stretch goal to unveil a mysterious surprise—what treasure lies ahead? Add-Ons { Pre-Order }  Journey to Oz & Wonderland To pre-order our 2nd Edition - Journey to Oz & Wonderland or the Pocket Deck, please be sure to include them via add-ons! Those of you who have backed our previous campaign for "Journey to Oz & Wonderland: 2nd Edition/ Pocket Deck" will receive pro-rated shipping on their rewards. We'll get them shipped to you at the lowest rates available! :) 2nd Edition Pocket Deck Additional deck details can be found here . { Art Prints } Our art prints are printed on premium fine-art luster paper 8x12" Art Prints of your choice 4x6" Art Prints of your choice { Divination Dice Set } These sets are designed for strategic readings—the words and symbols on each die are intentionally curated to spark intuitive insight, reveal hidden connections, and invite meaningful interpretation. Each set is crafted in-house , featuring original artwork and symbolic engravings on both the box and the accompanying 10 dice. Heads up! At the end of the campaign, you'll be able to select from several design options in your backer survey , including an exclusive themed set for the new deck! *Images pictured above are prototypes and may vary slightly from actual product. *** More Add-Ons Coming Soon! Our Process and Use of Image Generation We’d like to take this opportunity to be transparent with our community. Our process is highly iterative, which means that the artwork will evolve continually until we send it to the printer. What you see here is in the final stages of completion, and we’ll be adding additional detail as we progress through the campaign. This approach gives us the space to create the most compelling and beautiful tarot deck possible for our fans. We’ll share regular updates to highlight our progress and invite any feedback you may have regarding the artwork and the deck as a whole. While we can’t guarantee every suggestion will be implemented, we will be listening closely and genuinely value your input. :) Our artistic process for “Voyage the 7 Seas to Neverland” is rooted in storytelling, and goes far beyond the simple generation of images. Each card begins with a clear vision—grounded in rich concepts, emotions, and themes we aim to convey. We use AI as a tool to generate foundational elements that are only the starting point. From there, we employ a meticulous collaging technique, layering multiple AI-generated pieces, original art, and hand-crafted elements. This is an intensely hands-on process, with each image undergoing countless rounds of refinement, including detailed adjustments to composition, color balance, and atmospheric effects. The art evolves through each stage, allowing us to enhance the narrative, incorporate subtle connections, and add intricate details that bind the deck together. PROJECT TIMELINE: PROJECT TIMELINE: This is an estimated timeline and is subject to change dependent on manufacturing, shipping, and any unforeseen circumstances that may occur (e.g., weather, global pandemics, etc.). We are so excited to share our product and are dedicated to getting this out to you as soon as possible! Unless we receive more orders than we can handle, we intend to package and ship everything ourselves. We recognize this is a significant task, which is why we've estimated approximately 2-3 weeks to have everything packaged and shipped. If there are any delays on our end, we will keep you informed! To ensure the most accurate and affordable rates, shipping will be charged closer to the actual shipping date, later through Backerkit. The buyer is responsible for all shipping fees/VAT/Duty fees. Please note: After the campaign ends, we will send out a BackerKit survey to collect shipping information. If you do not complete this survey, we will attempt to notify you. However, rewards tied to incomplete surveys cannot be shipped, and your pledge will be considered a donation toward the project. *INTERNATIONAL SHIPPING: INTERNATIONAL SHIPPING: We want to be fully transparent with our backers and let you know that international shipping rates have increased in recent years. While these costs can be higher than we’d like, we’ve done our best to curate clear, upfront rates for each country so you can plan with confidence.
