title: The Wyrmwood Chess Board
description: The Wyrmwood Chess Board ♟️ Wyrmwood, the premier creator in tabletop gaming furniture and accessories, is here to lend our touch to perhaps the most iconic tabletop game of them all: chess . The Wyrmwood Chess Board, hand crafted with solid woods and carefully selected veneers, features tournament standard 2.25" squares for the maximum in versatility and playability. But, like a brilliantly played gambit, the beauty of the Wyrmwood Chess Board isn't apparent until you look at things from a different angle. Let's take a look at everything the Wyrmwood Chess Board has to offer. Features 🧲 Magnets embedded underneath every square work with our own Flat, wooden chess pieces, designed and crafted by Wyrmwood using classic iconography. The magnets also interact with many traditional weighted pieces, exerting enough pull to keep pieces centered and avoid accidentally knocking pieces out of position while still allowing for natural play. The Wyrmwood Chess Board features a classic French cleat on the underside, allowing it to mount to the wall for storage and vertical play, and be lifted off quickly and easily whenever desired. The frame is constructed from solid hardwood, with the board surface a carefully selected layer of shop-cut veneer. Each board flows through the Wyrmwood workshop, crafted by our dedicated team. The 2.25" squares on the board are the tournament standard, allowing maximum compatibility with a variety of pieces and giving plenty of room for play. Each Wyrmwood Chess Board in this project will be numbered, set apart as one of a kind. Flat Chess Pieces? 🤔 A set of these cork-backed magnetic pieces, featuring classic chess iconography instantly familiar to anyone that reads chess books or has played chess online, are INCLUDED with every board sold in this project. Every set of pieces includes magnetic storage boards, so that the pieces can be mounted alongside the Wyrmwood Chess Board for a true statement set. This way, you'll always be ready for a game. Since you don't have to remove the board from the wall to play, the Flat Pieces are perfect for correspondence play with a member of your household. Of course, it can be easy to lose track of whose turn it is during correspondence play, so each set of pieces includes a turn tracker. You'll know when it's your move at a glance - even if it's been days! Wood Options 🪵 These configurations are being offered without limit in a variety of domestic and exotic woods. Chess Board Details: Maple & Cherry Squares, Cherry Frame Chess Board Details: Maple & Black Walnut Squares, Wenge/Black Walnut Frame Chess Board Details: Maple & Teak Squares, Black Walnut/Maple Frame Chess Board Details: Curly Birch & Wenge Squares, Wenge/Bolivian Rosewood Frame Chess Board Details: Bubinga & Ebony Squares, Wenge/Bolivian Rosewood Frame Chess Board Details: Padauk & Wenge Squares, Wenge Frame Chess Board Details: Masur Birch & Purpleheart Squares, Wenge/Purpleheart Frame Elven & Dwarven Chess Boards 🧝🏻‍♂️ These configurations, created for our custom shop specifically for this project, feature striking exotic wood combinations and marquetry inspired by classic fantasy archetypes. Each board will be made available in batches of 10 at a time, and production is strictly limited to 50 units each . Each pledge for one of these boards includes custom, hand-inlaid script in a corresponding font. You'll work with our custom shop to choose your own sentence or phrase, which will be transliterated and inlaid into your chosen board. See the FAQ for more information. Chess Board Details: Curly Maple & Bolivian Rosewood Squares, Maple/Bolivian Rosewood Frame, Maple & Aluminum Inlay Chess Board Details: Quartersawn Oak & Wenge Squares, Wenge Frame, Oak & Brass Inlay Upgrades 🔥 Crafting 🔨 Check out the crafting process of our Elven and Dwarven Chess Boards below 👇 From idea to problems to product, this playlist captures the entire development of the Wyrmwood Chessboard 😅 Shipping 📦 Orders in this campaign are estimated to ship in August of 2026. Orders ship worldwide from our Pennsylvania warehouse. Shipping & applicable sales tax/VAT will be collected during pledge finalization. International orders will be shipped via DDP, with no additional duties or customs fees billed to backers at time of delivery.

title: Tabletop Tempest Season 1
description: Mission: "If you're sitting at my table, either we all eat, or none of us do." Tabletop Tempest is a TTRPG actual play channel founded by immigrants/children of immigrants. All three Founders are BIMPOC and represent various cultures in the United States (Brazilian, Japanese, Korean, and Mexican). We, along with cast on and off screen, also represent an array of qualities that are not represented in actual plays enough, such as voices from queer/LGBTQ+, disabled, gender-diverse, and neurodivergent communities. ​Because of this, we value diversity in every aspect of the stories we weave. Tabletop Tempest believes that all participants in an actual play (on and off screen) should be paid for their work. This Kickstarter is being made to provide payment to 20 artists/creators (19 BIMPOC!) who are participating in Season 1 shows of Tabletop Tempest, both on and off screen. Where the Funds Are Going Tabletop Tempest also believes in transparency and accountability. For this reason, we are showing upfront where your money will be going, along with where extra funds will go if the Kickstarter goal is met. If some pledges do not go through, then we will calculate the met goal via how much funding we actually receive. This is our $8,000 goal's breakdown: Game Master: $400 per show x 3 shows = $1,200 Performers: $250 per player, per show x 3 shows (13 players) = $3,250 (plus $50 for a guest player) Producers: $200 per producer x 3 shows = $600 Editor: $300 per show x 3 shows = $900 Artist: up to $300 per artist x 3 shows = up to $900 Administration work = $400 Estimated fees to Kickstarter (via Kickstarter's calculator): $700 If the Kickstarter ends with up to $1,399 in excess, it will be divided as such: The three Game Masters will split 17% of the additional funds. (max $79 each) The thirteen Performers will split 46% of the additional funds. (max $49 each) The guest Performer will receive 1% of the additional funds. (max $15) The Producers will split: 8% of the additional funds. (max $37 each show) The Editor will receive 13% of the additional funds. (max $61 each show) The remaining 15% will be used for miscellaneous items used to keep the shows running (such as software for editing, website subscription, etc) and any additional taxes. (max $216, and any unused will be saved for Season 2) Artists are currently being paid out of pocket for a set fee. Some are being paid in full, while others are sponsoring a show with a discount they determined. If the Kickstarter ends more than $1,399 beyond the starting goal, it will be divided as such: All additional funds will be put aside for a starter fund to fund participants in Season 2, possible in-between shows (such as charity productions), and additional administration work. Further transparency: At this time, we the Founders are not compensating ourselves for Season 1 additional work, including but not limited to: managing social media presence; outreach (to partners/sponsors); social media edits; overlays; extra items for players that we choose to pay for out of pocket. We believe it takes a village, and starting up a project like this is something we believe in and want to put our time into developing. Show: "Beneath the Surface" (system: Masks) Release Date: July 2024 Five cousins are called back to their shared ancestral home. Invited to a family reunion, they soon find that their family has kept more than one secret. A strange algae, gifted powers, and a mission to travel into the ocean. With so much at stake, will they be able to find their place in the family? Game Master: Kris (they/she) Kris is a Latine TTRPG Game Master, player, and producer based in Colorado. They are passionate about bringing unsung stories into the TTRPG space, and giving players a place to explore parts of themselves and their histories in a safe environment. They specialize in storylines which highlight cultural folklore and impassioned emotion. When they are not trying to write-up a new horror game concept, they can be found playing fútbol or rock climbing. Players: Clare Murphy (she/her) Clare is a Singaporean actor, content creator and storyteller based in Brooklyn, NY. After receiving her degree in Acting from the Atlantic Acting School through NYU Tisch, she cut her teeth making theatre in New York as a founding member of the Joust Theatre Company; acting, producing and crafting stories with her favorite theatre makers. She now makes content on Twitch with storytelling, roleplay and ✨ magic✨ at the core (there's a little witch in all of us after all), delving into gameplay in The Sims 4, GTA 5 Online RP and more. While D&D and TTRPGs have been a passion of hers for years, Beneath the Surface is Clare's AP debut - thank you to Tabletop Tempest for taking the chance on a newbie! It's truly an honor. You can find Clare in her witchy corner of the internet on Twitch, Instagram, TikTok and Twitter all at @clare_witchbaby Danny Quach (he/him) Danny is a marketing professional by day and the internet’s emotional support himbo by night. He’s the author of Digital Thiccness (https://digitalthiccness.substack.com/), a newsletter and blog dedicated to making information about marketing, social media trends, and building digital communities as accessible as possible, sprinkled with personal musings and projects and pop culture through a queer lens. When he’s not writing and collaborating on TTRPG projects, Danny can be found lifting heavy things, eating tasty things, watching scary movies, and listening to 2000s emo/screamo music. Follow him on Twitter, TikTok, and Instagram @Br00taldan Nala J. Wu (they/them) Nala is a proud QTPOC Art Director (All the Witches, Evil Hat Productions, Metal Weave Games) and Award-winning Illustrator (Coyote & Crow, Apocalypse Keys) who works full-time in TTRPGs. Besides art, Nala does voice acting, diversity & cultural consultations, and professional TTRPG AP stream performances —  most notable is Itaewon by Night, an award-winning Actual Play featuring the first and only all-Asian cast to play Vampire the Masquerade on stream! Valiant Dorian (he/him) Valiant is a desi, transmasc, bisexual TTRPG Performer, GM and Designer. He specializes in stories exploring the duality of love & grief, wrath & justice and divinity & monstrosity. Val is also a Twitch Streamer where he hosts Dicey Banter: the TTRPG Talk Show, reviews & showcases TTRPGs and plays video games! Follow him on Twitter @ValiantDorian and TikTok @otsospiritbear to keep up to date with all his projects! Victor Manso (he/him) Victor is a Venezuelan actor, filmmaker, writer, singer/songwriter. His first love is the stage; his passion is storytelling in as many forms as he can conjure. Victor spent over ten years building online communities for written-based collaborative storytelling and roleplay, inspired mainly by World of Darkness and Dungeons and Dragons. In 2021, Victor began creating original D&D character videos on TikTok with a positive result. Through it, he got invited to participate in his first D&D campaign with some amazing storytellers. After that, Victor has continued to play and GM D&D and other tabletop games. He is grateful for the supportive community he has found in the space and is always happy to represent diversity and inspire others who may hesitate about whether or not the TTRPG space is for them. You can follow him on TikTok @ victor_thestoryweaver and Instagram @thevictormanso Character Artist: Ally R (any pronouns) — cosmicamazon.contact@gmail.com — Twitter — Portfolio Overlay Artist: Chris Hamasu (any pronouns) — contact.hamasamakun@gmail.com — Linktree Official Dice Sponsor: Whitney, from Smoking Glue Guns — smokinggluegunsshop@gmail.com — Website — Twitter Show: "American Effigy" (system: City of Mist) Release Date: October 2024 Following the tragic death of their mother, five siblings in 1930ʻs New York have their lives uprooted after learning that their mother hid their true nature from them. Now they must discover for themselves what it means to walk the line between man and myth. Game Master: Chris Hamasu (any/all) Chris is an Asian American TTRPG content creator and streamer based out of Tampa. They've dedicated their time to the exploration of tropes and non-traditional character concepts in TTRPGs, advocating for further representation in the community that departs from the Eurocentric standard. It is their hope that through this project and many others, that people are inspired to craft stories and worlds that speak truth to their individual experiences and passions. Players: Brendon Alekseii (he/him) Brendon is a writer, director, teaching artist and nerd from Trinidad & Tobago, who has been playing TTRPGs for the last decade. When he's not writing or rolling dice, he's at home with his wife and five cats, or playing Magic The Gathering with friends. Jonathan Mendoza (any/all) Jonathan "Ryomasa" is a TTRPG designer, performer, producer and writer from Boston. When not writing for TTRPGs or performing/producing APs, he's best known as an unabashed yeehaw enthusiast and maker of fun, alternate-art Magic: the Gathering proxies. Luana (Lu/Luana/they/them) Luana is a Brazilian nerd who loves cosplays, TTRPGs, and so much more! They're also part of The Chaos Brigade, they're so thankful and very excited to be in the American Effigy! Puja (she/her) Puja is a queer, Indian-American streamer, writer, and voice actor from Texas. Growing up as a third culture kid and moving between several different countries as a child, her interests as a performer lie in showcasing her heritage, upbringing, and queerness in all its intricate glory. You can find her on twitter at @puja and at @pujabberwocky on all other platforms. Sabrina Ortiz (they/she) Sabrina is a TTRPG player, producer, and GM. Outside of the TTRPG space Sabrina is a student and streamer who is passionate about nurturing safe creative spaces. You can find Sabrina on The Stranger’s Guild wherever you pod your casts. Guest: Maisie Lynn (she/they) Maisie is a variety content creator with a passion for chaos and laughter. Whether it be through her social media, Twitch livestreams, and even her guesting, she aspires to make the world even the littlest bit funnier. However, this doesn’t mean she’s afraid to go deep, with haunting original characters that manifest the more frightening aspects of the self. Her notable works include her guest spot as Brey the Midwestern werewolf on CastParty and Midwestern Dirty Talk/Midwestern BBEG content on socials! Show: "Breaking Prometheus" (system: Candela Obscura) Release Date: January 2025 A group of paranormal investigators in New Haven uncover long-buried secrets that intertwine with their own family's forgotten past. As they delve deeper into the city's mysteries, they confront supernatural entities and unearth dark truths that force them to reconcile with their ancestral legacies. Together, they must navigate treacherous alliances and confront their own personal demons while a dark force threatens to reawaken and unleash chaos into this new world. Game Master: Jonathan Perez (he/they) Jonathan Perez was born in Ensenada, Mexico, and is currently based in Nevada. As an avid storyteller, his goal within the TTRPG community is to give a voice to those who may have felt they didn’t have one; to allow stories to be told that couldn’t be told by anyone else before due to misrepresentation or total lack thereof. Whether it’s personal identity or cultural identity, they look forward to sculpting worlds that allow people the safety and comfort to explore both, and connect with their roots and heritage. Players: Jason Patrick Galit (he/him) Known as JPG in pop culture circles, JPG is Filipino-American content creator, TTRPG performer, consultant, and "geek educator" based in Southern California. A frequent public speaker for conventions and outlets like San Diego Comic-Con, WonderCon Anaheim, and Gen Con Indianapolis, his work reflects how media literacy, brand management, and cultural experiences can help inform upon the pop culture space. JPG is also the owner of the pop culture consultancy firm InterMyth, whose aim is to help marginalized and disenfranchised creators on their journey to create in the spaces they love. His work can be found on his Linktr.ee , and you can find his social media @JPG. Josephine (none/she) Josephine is a Korean-American horror creator who produces, performs, podcasts, and more in the realm of TTRPGs. Through personal and intimate horror, she explores fear, mundanity, and relationships in every character and story she brings to the table. Notable works of hers involve storytelling two seasons of Itaewon by Night (BadHouseRPG, Rainbow Roll Network: Best GM) and storytelling GUDIYA (Nameless Domain, NJ Web Fest: Best Audio Fiction Winner). Wren (they/them) Wren is a Caribbean cosplayer, content creator, musical theater enthusiast, and storyteller currently based in the UK. Still relatively new to streaming and TTRPG actual plays, Wren is in it for the drama and hopes to tell imaginative, endearing, and angsty tales with their creations.

title: Board Game Florida: 10 Events in 1 Year
description: WHAT WE DO OUR EVENTS Why This Kickstarter Exists Each standard event costs about $750 to run. Each convention costs $1,000+ due to venue size, logistics, transport, and staffing. Add in: Email systems Printing & signage Storage & equipment Tournament materials Media production Every $100 raised turns into roughly $85 in actual event funding after platform fees and merchandise production. We run lean. But we cannot run free. GOAL EXPLAINED Board games are one of the best hobbies people can have in a modern age where community is hard to find. No screens. No algorithms. Just tables and people. If you’ve ever: Made a friend at one of our events Found your first playtest group Discovered a publisher through our demos Felt like you belonged in the room Then you already understand what this campaign protects. Just hear what people say about us — BACKING TIERS Stretch Goals $15,000 — Community Vote: BLEACH or CUT My Hair Final Call If you’ve: Played at a Gaming Caravan event Met someone at a table Found community here This is the year that determines whether it grows — or plateaus. Back Board Game Florida. Help fund a full year of tabletop community.

RECENT ANNOUNCEMENTS: * Second stretch goal reached!!  By default, all of the non-boss battles in the game will still be action based, but players will now have the option to make them turn based instead as in a traditional RPG! * First stretch goal reached!!  All those HDMI-based NES clones will have a way to play Former Dawn now! * WE ARE FULLY FUNDED!! Thank you, everyone, for your generous support!  Let's see if we can hit some stretch goals! * Introducing the Former Dawn Special Edition of the TinyNES!  This is a 100% NES-compatible console that runs Former Dawn perfectly, and is a great collector's item!  Artwork created by our lead artist.  Look for it in the add-ons section for any of the physical tiers! * New version of the playable demo is now available! Get it here: Former Dawn Playable Demo Downloads – Something Nerdy Studios Former Dawn Do you love sprawling, epic science fiction sagas?  Then you've come to the right place. After a failed terraforming mission on an exoplanet light years from Earth, humanity dies out but leaves behind a child species(called the “Formers”) that forgets its own origin. You play a common hunter who rediscovers the truth and struggles against the faceless Organization which will stop at nothing to defeat you.  Along the way, you recruit a motley crew of brave Formers who are willing to risk everything for Justice. Chronicles of Astraea: Former Dawn is, in a nutshell, a story of discovery, resistance to evil, and exploration of the human condition. The world is rich, the plot complex, and the characters dynamic and multi-dimensional. We wanted to tell this story, but tell it in a unique yet classical style.  In other words, we wanted to make it a retro game with a huge amount of rich content, yet run on authentic 1980s hardware: the Nintendo Entertainment System . This wasn't possible without inventing our own memory mapper, so that's what we did. This wasn't possible without recruiting the most skilled artists we could find from the pixel art community, so that's what we did. This wasn't possible without holding ourselves to the highest programming and design standards, so that's what we did. We believe that Former Dawn is on track to be the most advanced NES game ever created.  It's not there yet, but with your help, it can be! Inspiration It wasn't until the SNES and PlayStation console generations that JRPGs took their true form. For us, the most notable ones are Final Fantasy IV-VI, Chrono Trigger, and Breath of Fire III & IV which came out steadily all throughout the 90s. Much of what we're trying to do is show that the NES, the humble 1980s console on which the JRPG was really born, is capable of supporting JRPG-style games of the sophistication and quality level of those later titles which formed the "golden age" of the JRPG. Also, there are many excellent games from other genres such as stealth, action-adventure, and tactics that inspire us to combine these diverse elements for a unique, integrated feel.  The story is the focus and the gameplay is in service of it, not the other way around. Think of the way that Half-Life was a refreshing change from the FPSes that existed up to that point and you'll have an idea of what we're trying to achieve. Gameplay The world of RPGs is divided into 2 broad camps based on the style of combat: "action" and "turn-based". Former Dawn breaks the mold by sporting fully developed versions of both! The main game engine of Former Dawn is top-down with smooth 8-directional movement, supporting real time combat, use of tool items, engagement with the environment, and dialogue interactions with NPCs. Each of the playable characters can perform their own version of the common actions: While some characters have abilities that others do not: Each character stat (e.g. strength, sword skill, etc.) is influenced by individual actions continuously over time.  Levels are assessed from the stats instead of the other way around, and there are no arbitrary hard level caps to block progress in the game. Because of the technical limitations of the NES, bosses are typically composed of background graphics instead of sprites. Former Dawn is no exception to this.  In fact, we leaned into it by creating an entirely separate turn based combat engine! It leverages our mapper tech to conduct large scale animations, making for engaging and exciting boss battles. The turn based combat engine and the real time combat engine are 100% integrated.  Your party's stats, abilities, and items carry over from one to the other. Characters As you'd expect, the (humanoid) characters can be divided up into playable characters and non-playable characters. Jekuthiel is a Hunter from the coastal village of Gerosch on the Western side of the continent.  He toils away at the lowest ranks of The Organization, but in his spare time dares to study Science, designing and building his own gadgets to aid in his forbidden pursuits.  Over time, Jeku's informal studies have suggested to him that there is something very wrong about The Org's dogma. Ava Ava is The Org's highest ranking Persuader and lives underground beneath the Tower at Cossin.  She can control the mind of anyone who is unfortunate enough to be brought before her, but she never abuses her incredible power.  Her one dream is to know the outside world... James James is Jeku's younger brother, and is finishing his preliminary studies in the hopes that he could be lucky enough to be inducted into one of the Schools of the Three Fingers.  Skilled with a spear and gifted with wits and courage, he is a formidable youth with much promise.  He looks up to Jeku and wants to accompany him on his adventures. Kwen Hailing from the city of Sai-Wun, Kwen is a Punk who enjoys sneaking into places he shouldn't be, blowing things up, and generally living on the edge.  His skills are highly valuable, especially once he joins Jeku's resistance movement against The Org.  He brandishes his crowbar in a fight but can also use it to break into locked rooms or chests! 4 More Playable Characters ...await you among the residents of Astraea. NPCs Hasofer From either his Sky Island or the Capitol at Tovnia, Hasofer rules with a secretive iron fist.  As the Prime Organizer, his word is supreme and any who oppose him do so at their peril.  Both his origins and true motives are something of a mystery... Shifra Shifra is Jeku's mother.  She's always wanted the best for Jeku, but worries that he's going to get himself into trouble some day.  Everyone in Gerosch loves her, and she lives a quiet life far away from the machinations of The Organization. Mary Although Jeku is a little hesitant to fully accept it, Mary is Jeku's girlfriend.  Although she loves him, she is also a very jealous person and wishes he would spend more time with her instead of going on his silly adventures and sneaking away during The Lesser Light to pursue his hobbies. Mayor The Mayor of Gerosch runs a well oiled machine, insofar as that is possible for a sleepy fishing village on the Western side of the continent.  He lives next door to Jeku and can vouch that nothing out of the ordinary ever happens ... William William is the local blacksmith in Gerosch.  As a hunter, Jeku gives William quite a bit of business, and has developed a good relationship with him as well. Durple Among the creatures of Astraea are those who are neither plant nor animal, but metamorphose between them.  One such creature may find you and prove invaluable in your quest, if you can tolerate his derpiness! Tri-claw Nnrnhgn Crog Org Henchman One of the things that our MXM-1 memory mapper allows us to do is to create vivid, large-scale, animated bosses. Pushing The Envelope From the very beginning, we intended to push the envelope.  Multiple envelopes, really.  We figured that bringing to bear all the tools of modern game development should allow a far grander 8-bit game to be created than has ever existed until now. The vision for Former Dawn is big.  How big? 8 characters to play 8 directions to move in 10 minutes of FMV to watch 16 frame animations (standard) 20 hours of gameplay to enjoy 30 bosses to defeat 45 areas to explore 50 base enemy types to fight 52 character classes to unlock 200 unique items to find 1,000 screens to see 100,000 words of dialogue to read Memory Mapper Development Classical NES games were limited by the memory capacities made available by the memory management chips that were soldered onto the circuit boards inside the cartridges.  Despite modern technology providing vast improvements on what such chips can do, the modern NES homebrew scene has never seen a paradigm shift related to those improvements...until now. MXM-1 Our custom memory mapper, MXM-1 (Memory eXpansion Module) is the most significant innovation in NES cartridge hardware since Nintendo's MMC5 (Memory Management Controller) which debuted in 1989.  The most advanced NES games of the original commercial era were almost entirely MMC3 or MMC5 games, which meant that their ROM sizes were capped at 768KiB - 2,048KiB.  MXM-1 allows direct access to many times more than this, and indirect access to CD-ROM quantities of data. OST Sampler The NES is actually capable of expansion audio, much like its Japanese predecessor the Famicom.  However, it does require a simple expansion audio bridge (a glorified bodge wire) to be installed into the expansion port on the underneath side of the chassis.  We've decided to include one of these for free with every cartridge edition of Former Dawn.  The sound track and sound effects are greatly enhanced by MXM-1's audio subsystem, which is something like a mix of the SNES's D-DSP chip and the Amiga's PAULA chip.  It sports 8 channels of genuine PCM audio, an echo buffer, wavetable support, and smooth sinc function based interpolation. Here are a few examples of what it can sound like.  All of these are musical tracks already written for Former Dawn and playable on a real NES with our development cartridges.  They each combine the 4 native 2A03 synth channels with 4 channels of MXM-1 PCM, leaving 4 PCM channels for the SFX and ambiance channel. Revolutionary 8-bit Background Art Style One of the most useful features of MXM-1 is its "8x1 attributes", which means that every 8 pixel wide, 1 pixel tall region of the screen can subscribe to its own color palette from the 4 available at any given time in the NES's internal palette RAM.  This means, in one sense, 32 times the color density of the base system and 8 times the color density of MMC5 which had already improved it during the NES's original lifespan. As a consequence, skilled artists can now create artwork for the NES which is more like modern indie game pixel art than it is classical NES graphics. Sample areas from Former Dawn : The House of Truth in the Town of Mita The Snowy Swamp Southern Shore of the Great Lava Lake Durple Forest! Arcade Quality Sprite Animations on the NES One of the hallmarks of the artwork on this project has been that every playable character is illustrated using about 10 colors and smoothly animated in all 8 directions.  What "smooth" means depends on exactly what is being depicted, but it's often in the ballpark of 12-16 frames per direction. Here are some of the examples we're most proud of, that we would love to match for the rest of the spritework in the game: Yes...Full Motion Video!  Who would've thought that the NES was capable of such a thing?  After learning how to develop a memory mapper for the NES, the only real impediment to achieving this was providing the PPU with enough data.  Given the rest of what we're doing, it was a natural outcome. However, we are very cautious with this newfound power.  Primarily inspired by Éric Chahi's wonderful FMV in Another World and Paul Cuisset's related work in Flashback , we want to create tasteful enhancements to the existing game instead of it dominating the design or being a mere gimmick. Recently, we were approached by a talented animator and he has created this proof-of-concept for the project: We can and will put this on the NES.  With your help, many more clips like it will follow. PC Port The vast majority of NES games that are sold on modern digital distribution platforms like Steam simply ship an emulator and the ROM packaged together.  This has the effect of making the game feel exactly like that -- not tailored for the PC.  But modern PCs overwhelmingly have widescreen monitors, stereo sound, and controllers more advanced than the NES's.  In a sense, emulation guarantees authenticity, but it sacrifices quality-of-life.  We believe we can have both at the same time! Because of these reasons, we decided to create an actual port of Former Dawn to the PC.  It is being written in C++ using the SDL2 multimedia library.  Here's an example of the widescreen mode that we're making available in the PC version of the game: Because of the fact that the core game has been developed on the NES from the outset, we are able to keep the PC version of the game in line with it.  So it's OK to play the PC version of the game -- you will get the true Former Dawn experience, but with many of the rough edges smoothed over without sacrificing the 8-bit nature of the game.  Here are some examples of what we get right that most other pixel art games in the indie scene neglect, either intentionally or unintentionally: Our sprite movement and scrolling snaps to the native pixel grid instead of being "impossibly smooth". We provide integer scaling in widescreen mode to avoid fuzzy pixel boundaries. When we achieve a parallax scrolling effect, it is a genuine one that can actually work on NES hardware instead of being a strange mish-mash of 8-bit and 16-bit aesthetics. When enemies (especially bosses) are large, it's because it's actually possible to make them that large on the original hardware. The amount of activity on the screen never exceeds what the NES's CPU can handle; i.e. the game is never "impossibly complex". All of the meaningful graphical restrictions are kept in place: Color palette of the NES (these 54 very specific colors and some of their blends) Number of colors per sprite (3 + transparency) Number of colors per background tile (3 + backdrop) Number of palettes (4 for sprites and another 4 for background tiles) Why Kickstarter? We founded Something Nerdy Studios in early 2019 with a vision towards creating the most advanced NES game in the history of the console.  After mastering the hardware itself and figuring out how best to extend it, we embarked on creating a truly unique adventure complete with all the high quality graphics, animation, and music we could stuff into the machine.  We've managed to remain independent both from publishers and outside investors, which is very important to us. The reiterative process of building something special and inspiring has led, at last, to recruiting a true dream team that is capable of fulfilling the vision.  But self-funding is no longer viable.  We've reached the point where we need the community to get involved and push this project over the finish line. The base content is there, and we have the talent to flesh it out.  All that we need is your help to make the full game a reality! The Team Follow Us Twitter/X: https://x.com/somethinnerdy Company Website: https://somethingnerdy.com YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCGR1EHaujOtDvP_j3Pts5HQ Discord: https://discord.gg/VFNAaYEVeJ Press Kit Available on the web here . Also available as a .zip file here .

Kingdoms of Ereloth (KoE) is a Survival RPG video game, inspired by a fantastic medieval era where thanks to the multiplayer system you and your friends can immerse yourselves in a vast world full of adventures. Form strategic alliances with other factions to strengthen your kingdom and weaken the enemy, thus changing the fate of Ereloth. The fact that it's a multiplayer within the survival genre allows for endless new interactions. Group together to create guilds, forge bonds, be competitive or charismatic, but above all, this game aims for players to exchange not only resources, but also stories and intertwine their destinies. Different scenarios dominate the world of Ereloth, these are some of the ones you can find when you enter. Do you dream of becoming a pirate, a King or just looking for a quiet farm where you can settle down? KoE offers you all of this and more, with a wide range of mechanics that will fulfill your fantasies throughout the great kingdoms. There are too many castles and a lack of leaders in Ereloth. If you want to become one, start by settling in the kingdom of your choice. You can climb to the top with the help of strategic alliances, or perhaps take the throne by force... You can be the lord and master of large and fruitful territories, with a huge army at your disposal willing to defend your lands and expand them. Each kingdom will have its own political system, laws and leaders. Take advantage of the intricate political twists to gain all the power. KoE offers a large amount of expandable content that allows you to use the elements you most identify with, in order to live the best gaming experience based on your own preferences. This whole experience is complemented by an extensive dialogue system and interactions with NPCs and other players in an open world. With a dynamic and complex reputation system, as well as a socio-political structure that expands the game options available, you will be able to play unique roles that will influence the day-to-day actions of those who live in Ereloth. Are you new to KoE and don't even have a crown to your name to eat a decent meal? Don't worry! Use the resources that nature provides you to create objects, learn and increase your skills, and you'll be buying a house in no time, maybe even getting a farm or having your own business, where you can establish trade routes, export valuable merchandise or expand your business to other kingdoms. There are various ways to trade valuable or necessary items, which allow you to generate a free market dynamic, where geographic location, taxes, laws, or weather station systems will affect the performance of your sales, which translates into your products being a part of an alive economy of supply and demand. The lands of Ereloth are full of very valuable natural resources that are key to the survival of its inhabitants. Explore the less inhabited areas to discover copper, silver or other minerals. Enter the forests to get wood, fiber, fruits or medicinal herbs. Everything you need to survive in KoE, nature will provide it. As your KoE experience progresses, you will gain the ability to craft more items that will be very helpful in achieving your goals or playing whatever role you want. Become the blacksmith that will equip large armies, be the most in-demand carpenter in town, build your own farm, or trade the items you create with other players. The more craft skills you learn, the more opportunities you will have to expand your wealth. Because we are aware that not everybody likes to walk that much, not even in a video game, KoE will have a very diverse transportation system. Imagine all the valuable objects or merchandise that you can transport or distant unexplored lands that you can travel riding on wild or exotic beasts, carriages and boats. You can tame animals and turn them into mounts, build wagons to transport goods or valuable items, and access the shipyard to start building your first ship, but, with enough credits, you can always just buy it from other players or on the market. Wars are almost always a must for the conquest of territories. Having expert warriors with different fighting styles and weapons provides more chances to savour victory. Specialize in the variety of weapons and combat styles that KoE has to offer. You can be an expert master of all types of combat or just focus on one. Melee, ranged, mounted or even magical combat is an option. Being a great warrior only depends on you and your skills. If your settlement is growing and you need extra hands to produce and expand, you can go around the kingdoms looking for NPCs to recruit, who, with a couple of coins or some food, will be happy to serve you. Recruit and train them for the tasks that suit you best, such as going into battle, providing guard services, transporting resources, and even doing farm trades. Remember that the way you treat your NPCs is the way they will treat you. If your reputation remains high, you will always have them as allies, otherwise they could betray you or abandon you. Your character represents you in the virtual world of KoE, so we will give you the freedom to create an avatar with which you feel identified. You can virtualize your face to give your character an identity or choose from the wide variety of options that we have for facial, body or cosmetic features such as tattoos and hairstyles. You will also have endless options in skins for your character and their companion pets or NPC's. We will take customization beyond the physical form, molding your character's personality as it acquires the knowledge and skills that you want it to have, without limiting the amount of gameplay that you can access. It will only depend on the time you are willing to invest in KoE. Being a great warrior will not be the only thing you will need in order to raise up in KoE. Your skills, such as knowing how to speak correctly, mastering ancient texts, writing letters with hints of diplomacy, persuading, conspiring, spying and many more, will allow you to earn more money if you are a merchant, do favors that you can later collect, start rumors of war to destabilize a kingdom and surround yourself with important characters who you can use to fulfill your purposes. Imagine how chaotic the world would be without diplomacy and in KoE it's no different, so keep your allies close and your enemies much, much closer. Form strategic alliances, sign trade agreements, make peace treaties (or declare war), trade supplies, and have tea with the lord of neighboring lands to strengthen bonds. War results in victory or defeat, but in peace you will always be a winner, going down the path of diplomacy can be beneficial. Make a clan, gather like-minded people, build your headquarters, then establish embassies in different cities from which to expand your political and commercial influence. Access unique services provided by these instances and protect these places with guards to safekeep your stored wealth. If in real life you can't be the adventurous traveler you dream of, in KoE you have the opportunity to know more than that one supermarket at the end of the street. Magical towns surrounded by steep mountains, vast oceans, ruins that hide secrets, mysterious caves, almost impenetrable fortresses that guard treasures, and many other hidden places to discover await you. Whether by land, sea or air, always carry a good mount with you so that the trip is pleasant. KoE will have a wide variety of creatures to ride and with the option to add armor sets and appearances, being able to improve their statistics with training and use them to compete in races or fight in mounted combat. Additionally, you can attach accessories to your mounts such as carts to increase the load available or weapons so that you can pepper your enemies with deadly built-in weapons. We want to thank you for your support. That's why we've compiled various rewards to fascinate you including game packs. We started with an ambitious goal, to serve as a foundation to grow our project, every step takes us closer to making the ideal game. For those looking for a challenging destination, immerse yourself with us in the waters of the impossible and let's conquer new lands. Follow us on social media where we will be updating the progress of Kingdoms of Ereloth. Arcadius Entertainment was born in October 2021, but the idea took place many years before the official signing as an ambitious project that emerged based on experiences within the world of video games. That was the beginning of this whole adventure. Although we are still a little less than 100 people in the studio, we already have our first video game "Kingdoms Of Ereloth" (or KoE), on the Steam platform; This is a survival RPG inspired by medieval times, which will offer a multiplayer experience and allow players to create alliances to build stories together. One of the goals of the studio in the upcoming years is the opening of different offices, starting from Barcelona-​​Spain, followed by Canada, the United Kingdom and Japan, in order to grow as a studio and expand our operations in key regions of the market. Currently, KoE has been approximately a year since the project was resumed, however, since the first time this idea came to light was in mid-2019, when there were only a couple of people trying to program for the first time, then we could talk about its "gestation" time as far as almost 4 years ago. Because it is a space where you can share ideas with people from all over the world, backed by the reliability that Kickstarter provides, whose prestige has been greatly earned over the time. 📌 Why Early Access? We are an indie company with its first project which is being carried out with the few resources we have. We want the gaming community to be involved throughout its development 📌 Approximately how long is this game going to be in early access? We plan for KoE to stay as early access for approximately two years. During this time, we plan on having constant content updates and fix bugs. 📌 How different will the full version be from the early access version? For the full release we plan to have a considerably large world where it’ll be possible to augment playability which means updates and new content like dungeons, caves, ruins, among others, while also including maritime navigation as a new form of exploration. We plan to renew the combat system where players will be able to dominate diverse types of combat so as to adapt and dominate the style that best suits them, as well as technically improving optimization and reinforce prevention for any cheating and hacking. Even considering all that, our north still lays on molding our playability according to feedback given by the community Meet the team that was built on a dream, together they have stepped up and made KoE a reality. We have different departments that have different budgets. Taking into account this injection of capital, the purpose would be the following:

I've always been drawn to music that blurs boundaries, inspires and elevates the listener, and brings all types of people together. On April 8th I will be joined by an incredible ensemble of BIPOC musicians to share our project titled “Close to Home.” With the help of nine renowned composers we will re-imagine my original R&B tunes for a classical ensemble and perform them at KMFA’s Draylen Mason Studio. A top notch video and sound team will capture the entire show and make a live video compilation. But we need your help to make it happen! My story: I’m a singer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist, and producer based in Austin, Texas. I grew up belonging to different musical worlds—the Christian and gospel music my parents raised me on, the R&B / soul / hip hop I listened to at friends and family’s houses, and the classical music I played and absorbed during my studies at The University of Texas and Yale. You can hear these many influences hear that in the music I’ve created . But to be honest with you, I stepped away from the world of classical music. I never fully felt at home there, or that there was space for me to be myself. Last fall, Austin’s classical station KMFA approached me about contributing to their OffBeat Concert Series . With some motivation from my friend and composer Nathaniel Earl, I saw an opportunity to reclaim a space in the classical world I had left behind--using my unique voice and perspective. For this upcoming project I've enlisted the help of nine composers and arrangers, each with their own take on the music I’ve created, to bring my songs to life. This is the first project of it’s kind here in Austin, and the truth is we can’t do it without your help. Here’s where you come in… The arts have always had patrons–people who believed in the talents and the vision of artists, composers, and creatives. Those patrons used their resources–knowledge, access, and/or funding–to help bring that vision to life. Alongside a great team of creatives and sponsors, we will arrange, rehearse, perform, record and film this music. The arrangements are coming together and they sound amazing! We have rehearsals scheduled with some excellent musicians and tickets to the show are on sale now! Producing an event of this scale is not cheap. The cost of musicians alone for a project of this size is around $6500 . This is the state of the music industry right now. If we want to be committed to paying artists a fair and living wage for this initial project, we will need to secure outside funding. The next step is getting this thing across the finish line! Your support will fund: Musicians' rehearsals and performance time Commission fees for composers (the cost of creating a new arrangement/piece) Production costs (lights, set design, stage manager) Audio engineers for live and recorded sound, plus the final mix Video team to capture the performance Video editor to compile footage and make it look real nice My dream is to one day take this show on the road. We've already gotten an offer to perform at a music festival later this year. Imagine what could happen with a full set of songs and videos? Your support can help make that happen.

The last year and a half was messy. Tired of being what other people wanted me to be, I decided to just be. To let people see my darkness and my light. I lost a job. I lost some friends. I threw out my back and spent close to a month on the floor under the weight of anxiety. Not everyone left though. Those who mattered stayed. To them my honesty was a brave act. A gift. Permission for them to be honest too. Permission for them to be. Time moved on, and so did I. New opportunities. New relationships. And I was present for them all. There really were far better things ahead than any I left behind. This record is an end and a beginning. A death and rebirth. A dream. This is my journey on the night sea. A place dark and uncertain. A beautiful place, alive with possibility. These songs are my honest gift to you. A continuation of my journey. Permission for you to be honest too. Permission for you to be. In January we recorded Gold , the first single for the record, with Andy Prickett up in Orange County. You can hear a rough mix of the track HERE . Andy produced, engineered, and played additional guitar on the track. Bram Inscore played Wurli, Juno, and Bass. Bram also played bass on Beck's Morning Phase, which won a little award back in February :). Kyle Crane played drums on the track. Kyle is currently on tour with Daniel Lanois . He's one of the best, hardest working drummers I know. My good friend Satoshi Noguchi will be mixing and mastering. $13K is the minimum needed to record, mix, and master the remaining songs and finish this project well. That said, the hope is to exceed the goal to allow room to make the project even better in the studio and in mastering. If exceeded significantly, I'd love to record a music video and hit the road for a west coast tour.

title: Jessenation - Recording My New Afrobeats Album
description: Hi, I'm Jesse Emeghara. My stage name is Jessenation. I was born in Nigeria and moved to the U.S. to pursue my music career. I became a U.S. Citizen in 2024 and I am currently based in Chicago. I grew up surrounded by music and video, and Afrobeats became my way of expressing love, joy and resilience. As an independent artist, I've been building this dream of becoming one of the top Afrobeats singer in the world. Music has been a part of my soul to a point where I have been funding my own music that I have created this far from money I earn at my day job. Currently, I am not signed to any recording labels and I don't have any recording contracts. I have been recording in small studios, making videos and content, performing at local venues, and connecting with fans online. As a child, I was introduced to various music genre through my father who listened to all kinds of music ranging from Raggae to Blues, Pop, High-life, and Gospel. I realized that music was my outlet to escape the hardships growing up in a society with challenges such as lack of food, social ammenities including shortage of running water and irregular power supply. Your support will help me create a new album and videos for it since I can no longer financially afford to create new music using funds from my day job. This will help take my singing career to greater heights and put to use my life long experience as a singer artist. Below is the video of "FIL - Fall In Love" from my Album "GRACE" Below is the video of "HIGHGRADE" from my EP "PENG (PEN not GUNS)" Below is the video of my song "Fire Party" from my album "GRACE" Below is the video of my song "Fever Refix" from my album "GRACE" Below I am performing at 2023 Miss Diaspora Nigeria USA in Chicago.

title: Bringing Two New Elise Records To Life
description: Dear friends, I have been working steadily on recording my songs and am getting close to the finish line for not one but TWO full length records!! I need your help getting them from seed to sprout to blossoming trees. The first of these is the twin record to my first full length record, 'A Little Longer', recorded in New York City in 2020 with Robin MacMillan and all the same musicians; the second I have been working on for the past year with Nat Smith and Lake Wilkinson in Nashville, TN, and it is made up of newer songs that you have most likely heard me play in my shows over the past few years, recorded with various combinations of bass, drums, pedal steel, electric guitar, harmony vocals, and keys in addition to my vocals and guitar. This is the strangest and saddest time I have lived through yet. There are two things that are giving me hope and getting me through: my AMAZING music community, and music itself. I believe that music can hold us together when everything else seems to be falling apart. I want to put out this music as soon as possible - every cent helps! Thank you thank you thank you! Love Elise

title: Ry Edwards' faith album: Finding Grace
description: Have you ever felt like life was too much to handle? Ever felt like things couldn't get any worse? Welcome to Finding Grace. What's this album about? "Finding Grace" is a collection of songs chronicling a journey of faith.  It begins from the promise of a new day with the song "Sunrise".  A song about one of those days when the mistakes of the past can't hold you down!  The next track rides the momentum and stays up with the song "Living on my Knees".  This song tells you to "hit the ground running" with the power of prayer with the feel of Tom Cochrane's "Life is a Highway". Sunrise But as you know, life doesn't stay high all the time.  What goes up must come down--and so it is with this album.  We descend into the darkness with a song called "Count the Stars" and see a world that "looks so full, but feels so hollow". Funny how quickly the bottom can drop out of life.  Especially, when it takes so long to climb back!  This album is designed to help people while they're still in places where they can't see the stars because of the tears in their eyes.  It's to help the person who feels like life has been one failure after another, not feel so alone.  It's for the person who wakes up in the morning feeling like a stranger in the bed they're lying in. Count the Stars Thankfully, there's Grace. "Grace" sends out the message that no matter how dire and heart-wrenching and hopeless our situation feels, we don't have to listen to the situation or the critics. God's Grace doesn't evaluate us as a student or a business person.  God's Grace doesn't refer to us as weird or disabled or stupid or naive or even sinner.  EVEN THOUGH, all have sinned--falling short of God's glory--God's grace calls us much better names.  That's the whole point of Grace. Grace means 'My chains are gone!' Grace tells us we are more than our pain.  More than our mistakes.  More than our doubts. God calls us Precious .  God calls us Beautiful! God calls us children of his own family. A fan, and fellow musician, wrote this message to Ry this on October 1st, 2019 about the song Grace: "The very first time I heard you perform "Grace" was at the Union Gospel Mission benefit in Mike Jacobson's garage.  You were playing your blue guitar.  It was simple, solo acoustic.  I loved it.  Actually, that was the first time I heard any of your music.  So, my first impressions all started from the basis of that solo acoustic set. Photo from the actual night in Mike's garage! "Somehow, Grace captured me that day.  Not just the performance, not just the lyrics, not just the delivery, but all of it somehow came together to stick in my mind.  And, to be honest, the phrase, 'You can call me stupid' really stuck, especially followed by, 'but I don't have to listen at all', which was given with such a simple confidence that demonstrates the Grace that is the source of such a response." - Jason Madison Ry's favorite part of the album, however, is what happens after Grace. "For me, Grace is just the starting point," Says Edwards.  "Once you can let go of Shame and Regret, it frees up your hands to grab hold of your Dreams and embrace the Community around you!" Ry reflects that attitude in the songs that come after Grace: Sparrow My Offering Today Paths in the Garden Never miss the beauty... These 3 songs wrap up the project by reminding you of simple beauty in the mundane, the power of offering what you have, and enjoying the gift of life by finding your own unique path. Every new Sunrise brings a new Challenge and a new Opportunity to enjoy.  Let this album help you grieve through your strife and celebrate with you as you resurrect Grace and Beauty in your life. Why Now? 2019 has been a year of grieving for Ry. In February, Ry lost the hearing in his left ear In March, Ry's 2-year IT project was handed off and he was let go due to a corporate re-structuring and in June he and his children and lost nearly everything they owned due to a family member with mental illness lost hearing, lost job, lost all possessions (books, furniture, pictures, kid's toys, etc.) Yet, no matter the challenge, Edwards still says: "We're called to praise God in all circumstances.  In the face of tragic adversity, I can think of no better time for me to put out a record about 'Finding Grace'.  Because right now--that's what I'm searching for." Ry wants to find Grace and Forgiveness for: his own ignorance that led to the hearing loss the corporation's decision to restructure in a way that took away Ry's job opportunity a feeling of betrayal from Ry's mentally ill family member a feeling of betrayal from God himself for the some of the ways he has shown up through these difficulties Thankfully, Jesus reassures us that we who mourn will be blessed with comfort. While working hard on this project, life continues on a roller coaster for Ry and his family.  Yet when he enters the studio, the intensity of emotions, both difficult and beautiful, translate into passion. Edwards says, "You'll hear my tears on this record--from sadness, from joy, and from yearning for the healing that I know will come. "Every song on this record reminds me of the importance of sharing the gospel of Christ, not through platitudes, but with a rich human experience of a life lived through faith.  God gave me these artistic works of poetic and pragmatic lyrical imagery.  God has shaken my heart with melodies and music that I am burdened to share.  Come listen to this album and journey with me from Sunrise through the Valley of Shadows and into a life lived with beautiful promise and hope all under the Grace of God." Click to learn more about Dallas Willard's "keys to the kingdom" Who is Ry Edwards? Ry Edwards is a guitar playing singer songwriter from Minneapolis.  He and his wife, Elisabeth, spent the last 10 years building up a neighborhood community in North Minneapolis.  Through the summer months he entertains up to 25 kids at one time in his tiny Minneapolis yard where the only rules are 1. Be kind and 2. No phones!  : ) In the corporate world Ry has been a change agent for improving ordering processes, international shipping procedures, and creating collaborative team cultures.  All the while, he loves to talk about faith and minister to people at work, in the neighborhood, and even people on his bus commute! But, even so, his favorite way to minister is through music. Ry Edwards performing the Finding Grace Concert Ry has created over 700 songs to support his personal mission to help people feel Human through Faith, Love, and Joy!  The 10 tracks on this CD  have been curated to accompany the existing acoustic " Finding Grace " concert.  After being introduced to the songs at the concert, listeners can enjoy deepening the experience through this masterfully produced record. What does "masterfully produced" mean? Ry's had a long history with recording projects from the self-produced "Mirage" in 2007 to the analog-recorded E.P. of Finding Grace of 2011.  But this is a new undertaking in a whole new arena (Digital ProTools Studio)--complete with an expert gladiator! The gladiator in this project is a multi-instrumentalist and veteran record producer, Matt Patrick, who has worked with the likes of Ami Andersen (Wayfaring) Sara Groves ("All Right Here" and "The Other Side of Something") Jason Gray ("All the Lovely Losers") My producer, Matt Patrick, saving Jason Gray with a loaner guitar pedal board Minneapolis record producer, Matt Patrick, of Minneapolis bands, "Greycoats" and "Fathom Lane", is strongly shaping this record by taking Ry's songs to new heights of production.  Thanks to Matt, you'll hear ethereal sounds that fill the tracks with heavenly tones and heartbeat rhythms. Existing fans of Ry Edwards will get to hear the songs you love with new life nuance breathed into them. Here's what people are saying about Matt's Production: I've always loved Ry's warm and heartfelt vocals, but with the gorgeous layering and the added space and the polish in the supporting sound, really lets Ry's voice shine forth and elevates the whole song!  It's fantastic! - Eric Distad (of " The Faithful Sidekicks ") "Wow. "Back To You" is a beautiful song. And what an amazing arrangement with all the other instruments! I'm so glad that you've found a way to have the songs of your heart realized so fully." - Tim Thompson (aka The Feral Pastor ) "I just listened to "I am ready to wash " and am blown away. What a beautiful song to share!" - Rob Schwanz  (incredibly humble servant leader) Why should I contribute? 1. It takes a community! We're ideally looking for 500 people to invest $30 to help this artist create a beautiful masterpiece!  (of course, you're welcome to contribute more, but we really want to spread the opportunity to more music lovers!) Give $30 + S&H and tell 4 other friends! 2. It's more fulfilling than ordering pizza! I'm always shocked at how often the tab for pizza reaches $30 (see below).  Then you add on the tip for Shipping and Handling.  Just think, instead of ordering pizza, you can help fulfill the teenage dream of an indie musician! 1-Topping + Breadsticks + pop + $10 tip = $40! 3. It isn't just a donation, you get to join the journey ! Hear the music before anyone else! See all of the quirky behind-the-scenes videos in the studio! Get the first looks at the music videos! 4. Oh, and there're are rewards for every level of giving... including the $100 Mystery Club Bonus Rewards! Where does the money go? A good estimate for a radio-quality recording of a single song is $1,000. This includes the 3 steps necessary for making a recording sound good enough quality to be played on the radio. Recording: Actually, playing the song into microphones that record into a system.  This includes any hiring of musicians to add instruments that the producer wants to add to the track.  Adding computer programming elements like synthesizers or pre-recorded instruments that you cannot play.  This is where the biggest expense comes in because every instrument is recorded separately--usually multiple times.  So if you have a 5-minute song with 4 instruments all recording 2 good takes,  It takes an hour with all of the starts and stops--IF they nail it with the first two takes. Then add the time to listen back to the takes and you're at 2 hours.  If it weren't for human error, recording could be a lot cheaper! Mixing: Adjusting the frequencies of all the instruments to create a sonic texture that allows your ear to hear every nuanced part of the song.  How important is this?  When done wrong, it's like when your kid is in the backseat with her headphones turned up loud enough that you can hear the song.  But you hear he singing loud and clear and irritating.  This stage is what led to Ry's hearing loss with a different project back in February.  Please leave mixing to the pros!  (Or at least know to mix at low volumes, with speakers--not earbuds, and take 40 breaks every few hours) Mastering: The Master engineer compresses the sound of the tracks and puts them together on the album so that every song sounds like it is at the same volume--without losing the dynamics of the songs.  This engineers gets the entire album up to standards for national radio play. We have 10 songs on this album.  So, that's $10,000.  Then we have to design the album cover concept and print it.  Marketing is tricky.  Then there are Kickstarter fees logistics and taxes.

Fight. Sleep. Repeat. That's life for 14-year-old SoCal skate-punk Wylie Masterson. Like most teens, Wylie struggles with authority, puberty, and family. But when you’re a transgender male whose body, family, and society insist that you are female, the struggle to break out and live the life you were meant to live becomes a matter of life and death. Born Willow, Wylie wants nothing more than to escape his oppressive SoCal hometown and live the life he was meant to live—as a man, but his overly-religious mother has other plans for her “sweet, innocent daughter.” This is a book for anyone who knows what it's like to struggle to find their place in the world. It is both universal and timeless. While not presuming to speak for the entire transgender community, call me him. provides insight into what it can be like for transgender youth growing up in an environment that seeks to vilify and erase them. It is a book that, I hope, will open minds and change lives. The traditional gatekeepers to publication seem to feel that the time for publishing transgender coming-of-age stories is at a close. I know this because my agent has discussed the project with mainstream publishers and, while loving the writing and the story, they felt the time for coming-of-age transgender novels has come to an end. I get it, mainstream publishers are in this business to make money. I don't begrudge them that. But, let's look at some disappointing numbers: Barnes & Noble stocks 48 books categorized as "Transgender Fiction." Contrast that with a bookshelf of over 2585 YA novels . Amazon lists about 1,000 transgender YA novels , a small subset of over 90,000 young adult novels . "[T]he work isn’t done until every reader has a shelf full of titles that reflect them.” ~ Caleb Roehrig , author of YA thrillers and mysteries Must we wait for the world to once again silence us, push us back into the closet, and re-emerge once more before we are deemed by the mainstream as significant enough to tell our stories? I'll be using the money raised in this campaign to publish and market the book. The money you pledge in exchange for a wide variety of perks will go to things like: Cover Art Editing Formatting Printing costs Shipping PR costs Social Media outreach Kickstarter Fees Once publication is complete, all proceeds from the book sales will be donated to the following organizations that are instrumental in supporting and promoting transgender rights, providing mental health support and resources, and educating others about what it means to be transgender in the U.S: The Trevor Project - Founded in 1998 by the creators of the Academy Award®-winning short film TREVOR, The Trevor Project is the leading national organization providing crisis intervention and suicide prevention services to lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer & questioning (LGBTQ) young people under 25. Human Rights Campaign - the largest national lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender and queer civil rights organization, HRC envisions a world where LGBTQ people are ensured of their basic equal rights, and can be open, honest and safe at home, at work, and in the community. Transgender Law Center - the largest national trans-led organization advocating self-determination for all people. ***Just to be clear, the money you pledge during this campaign goes to the cost to publish and promote the book. You are not donating to these organizations. You are making it possible for this story to be told and distributed . The funds received in the sale of this book will be donated to the organizations above.*** Book trailer edited by KEnagonio .

title: Heart Language
description: Welcome to Heart Language ! How far would you go to follow your heart? Today’s world tells us to lead with our heads instead of our hearts, but what if there's more . What happens when we decide to follow our hearts and find out what the “more” is? Could it change everything? The Way of the Heart I believe that if people gave themselves permission to be who they truly are, peace on earth or what I like to think of as global harmony would be attainable. When we continually sacrifice our true heart’s desires for our society, our family, or our vocation, seeds of resentment begin to build. Dreams that once seemed attainable now seem distant. Hope slowly dies, and despair sets in to be passed on to the next generation. Common refrains like, “I had to go through it, so you do too,” or “This is just the way things are” become our mantra. But it’s not the way of the heart. When your heart is full, you have more to give to others. More time, patience, space, laughter, and willingness to understand. That ripple effect creates better communities, better families, and ultimately, a better world. I always think of my grandmother, Mary T. Even though she lived a simple life, she was one of the richest and most fulfilled people I’ve ever known. I can hear her laugh, a deep-throated cackle, grateful to God for everyone and everything in her life. I can still see her bright blue eyes shining with the light of a six-year-old on her 100th birthday, eating ice cream. Years after her passing, people still talk about how kind she was, how she always made sure everyone was included, and how special she was. She led a whole-hearted life true to her. An open heart not only changes your world, but the lives of those around you. More About the Book What is Heart Language ? Have you ever made a decision from your heart? Maybe you felt called to go somewhere, take a new job, or start a business. Perhaps you just knew something was right for you even though it made no logical sense at the time. Most likely, when you took that chance and followed through on your heart's calling, it turned out better than you could have ever imagined. That's the power of the heart. Heart Language is innate. We are all born connected to our hearts, so instead of learning a new language, it's a remembering, a coming home to who you truly are. When we center in our heart and follow what most lights us up, anything is possible. The world needs more heart. More care. More compassion. More light. More passion. Where could your heart lead you ? My First Heart Decision It all started with a choice and a Chinese word. The choice: do I move to New York City to pursue being a VP by 30 or do I follow my heart and volunteer teach in rural China? The logical choice was NYC. I had interned the summer before at InStyle magazine and everything I had worked for pointed me towards a predictable path after college, but my heart wanted more . I chose to follow the more and moved to rural China three months after graduation. I didn't speak the language, was the second tallest woman in the town, and the only female foreigner. The heart has its own way of speaking, and the day I came to Ningyuan, I listened. The word: 心里话  x īnlǐ huà , which in context means “honestly speaking” or “what's in my heart,” but literally translates as the “heart’s inner language.” I first heard it while speaking with He Ping, the Tai Qi teacher I would later fall in love with despite the few words we knew in each other’s languages. One evening, two months into living in Ningyuan, a small town in rural China, I found myself warming my hands by a coal cooktop. He Ping and I had eaten and there was now a silence between us that came from the shyness of a new crush and the inability of either of us to speak the other’s language. He looked at me, eyes bright and shining with kindness. “I know your heart,” he said, pointing towards mine. Then he tapped on his own heart twice and said, “x īnlǐ huà, ” and gave me a big smile. I was taken aback by the directness and sincerity of those words from a practical stranger, but as I let the phrase sink in, I realized that he did know my heart. There was no language to hide behind, no life stories that could be shared, and yet I felt truly seen for the first time in my life. The Three Primary Purposes of Heart Language This idea of the heart having its own language struck a chord with me, and from that day on, I began exploring the idea of what I call “ Heart Language ,” which is a language that lives within each of us and has three primary purposes: 1) It is our guide to living our truest lives, dreams, and soul's purpose 2) It is a universal language that allows us to deeply connect with everyone and anyone in the world, regardless of background, belief, language, religion, or culture 3) It is our connection to the divine (however you choose to define it) The choice to move to Ningyuan was a decision of the heart. It was a knowing that volunteering after college was on path for me, and that first decision to honor my heart changed everything. It started me on a heart-led path that has taken me all over the world, has guided me towards fulfilling my biggest, boldest Dreams (including writing this book), and most importantly, has helped me support others everyone from individuals and soloprenuers to small businesses, and Fortune 100 and 500 companies, in co-creating a more harmonious world for all. Your Heart's Path In Heart Language , we will begin to explore your own heart’s language and open up to what’s possible for you. Where will your heart lead you? Download Chapter One Here ! Why this Kickstarter Project? I first came up with the idea for Heart Language 18 years ago, and I've been diligently working on the book for the past 3 years. Heart Language has not only positively impacted my life and career, but those of thousands of people around the world. Heart Language has the power to change not only your life, but the world. What will the funds be used for? I have invested more than $12,500 of my personal funds on this project. The money I am raising now will go towards: Publishing Heart Language (costs range from $10K-$30K) Marketing and PR for Heart Language (costs range from $3500-$10K/month) Website creation and upgrades (costs range from $5K-$10K) Book Launch Events (costs range from $5K-$15K) Merchandise and other branded goodies (costs range from $2500-$10K) Other miscellaneous items If you have any questions about this project or Heart Language , feel free to reach out! Thank you so much for your support! It means the world to me.

title: SCP: Eye of the Beholder Season 1
description: Eye of the Beholder is an audio drama based on the SCP Foundation set in a world where a hidden organization is committed to identifying, confining, and researching entities that defy natural rules, known as anomalies or SCPs. Episode one is out now! https://rss.com/podcasts/eye-of-the-beholder/ The Plot: Dr. Vincent Minnow is the head of research at Site-29 , a botanical and mineralogy Foundation facility in Oregon . When it appears that the site is about to shut down due to a lack of funding, the Foundation unexpectedly raises funds and transfers SCP-4051 from Site-17 into its care. Crow , a member of the Caretakers , a broken-off branch of the Serpent's Hand, and a former member of the Chaos Insurgency , tries to find someone with whom they once shared a special relationship during their time in the insurgency after they disappeared one day. When a mission from the Caretaker's founder falls into their lap, they and their friends accept it. However, when an unknown SCP is smuggled into Site-29 , the site devolves into chaos as the unknown SCP causes destruction and spatial alterations. At the same time, an opposing force begins a hostile takeover of the site. Dr. Vincent Minnow and Crow find their paths crossing together, and team up to help retake Site-29 . Artwork by: Who this podcast is for Eye of the Beholder is for those looking for a fresh audio drama narrative experience, delving into the Sci-Fi horror world and community of the SCP Foundation . Eye of the Beholder aims to give old fans of the community a sense of freshness, shared passion, and love for the passion project they hold dear to their hearts, while giving those who are new to the fandom something that will ease them into the community and give them a glimpse into the wonderful world and community project of the SCP Foundation . The Crew Adrian Linford - Creator/Writer/Director/Producer Hello! I am Adrian Linford . I'm a writer, creating a plethora of short stories , poems , and even a movie script (pending buyout). I am 26 years old, getting my MFA in Creative Writing at Arizona State University . Growing up as a bookworm, gamer, and writer, creating compelling narratives is my lifeblood and what drives me to improve every day. “Whenever you are fed up with life, start writing: ink is the great cure for all human ills, as I have found out long ago.”  -Stephen King Jakobi Kendrick - Audio Engineer/Producer/Co-Director Hello hello! I’m Jakobi Kendrick , known as FunSizedJJ , called JJ . I’m a professional Audio Engineer , and I helped put everything together ( Voices , SFX , and Music ) for this project! Check out my website to see what else I’ve done! FunSizedStudios : https://jjk113.wixsite.com/funsizedstudios August Herrenhardt - Music and Composition Hi there! My name is August . I am the composer for the Eye Of The Beholder series of audiobooks and an aspiring content creator who specializes in Minecraft content . I mostly create orchestral music , but I also do electronic , synth , and experimental music as well. I have been making music properly since 2025 (however, my musical knowledge goes many years back, during high school ), which, funnily enough, was supposed to be a one-off thing , but eventually the idea stuck with me, and so I decided to pursue this endeavor . I am very grateful to have been a part of this project because I'm a big fan of SCP content , and to see Eye Of The Beholder gain attention, no matter how big or small, is nothing short of awesome to see! Eye Of The Beholder not only allows me to connect with other SCP fans but also makes me go beyond my comfort zone and try out new musical concepts that I otherwise would not have done in my other music projects . I poured a lot of thought and effort into the music behind Eye Of The Beholder , and I hope that you will enjoy it as much as the audiobook itself!
