title: MakerLength
description: What is MakerLength? Is a stop block build on top of a scale. Basically a positioning system. Watching the video will make more sense. Features Fast Scalable Repeatable Low effort Accurate, usually 2 or 3 thousandths Simple calibration, no micro adjustments Unedited demonstration Explaining MakerLength Early Development Initial design First prototype, 3D printed. First laser cut version, fully functional prototype. Early scale in imperial. More functional prototype, not longer used due complexity Current one is simpler using a miter saw. Stretch goals $100,000 Make it available in aluminum $250,000 Make it available in plastic Timeline 2023 December Begin production, except longer pieces Order more raw materials Order a larger CNC 4' x 4' 2024 January Begin production of longer pieces 2024 March Begin shipping Shipping All shipping cost are not included, we will require payment at the time of shipment. if desired you can send us your own shipping label.

title: G-810N BlueEye Grabber - Find & Retrieve Small Objects
description: Introducing the Vividia G-810N BlueEye Grabber! Tired of losing small items in hard-to-reach areas? Meet the G-810N, the ultimate foreign object retrieval tool. This innovative device not only helps you fish out items from tight spaces with an integrated grabber claw, but also captures crystal-clear pictures and video to help navigate your retrieval. Whether it’s a ring that fell down your sink or a tiny screw in a dark corner, the G-810N combines precision and convenience into one sleek design. It also features a removable magnet ring, making the capture of metallic objects even quicker. Learn more about this innovative product below: Applications This product is, in essence, a semi-rigid probe equipped with a camera, grabber claw, and bright LEDs. It can also be known as a visual grabber, visual retrieval tool, camera assisted retrieval tool, visual pick up tool, or grabber borescope. This unique combination of features makes it suitable for retrieving objects from a variety of areas, such as: Automobile, Aircraft, & Marine Engines Vehicle Compartments Drywall Industrial Machinery HVAC Systems Plumbing & Piping Electrical Panels Telecommunications Equipment Behind Furniture/Tight Spaces And More! Product Blueprint Top Features Device Compatibility Technical Specifications Project Timeline Prototype Drawing Demo Footage What's Included -G-810N BlueEye Grabber -USB-A to USB-C Adapter -U-101 “U2i” USB-A to iOS (USB-C & Lightning) Adapter -Product Manual -Removable Magnet Ring -Cloth Carrying Case Warranty Information Our products are covered by a one-year product quality warranty. This will cover issues such as factory defects & the product not working on arrival. It will not cover any damages caused by improper use, negligence, or intentional destruction by the customer. To learn more about our warranty policy and contact us in case of need, please visit our website - www.oasisscientific.com About Oasis Scientific Oasis Scientific Inc. is committed to making high-quality visual inspection equipment by staying at the forefront of industry innovation. We ensure our customers receive top-notch products at low prices, enhancing the world of visual inspection. We strive to uphold our core values of innovation, quality, professionalism, and integrity. We set ourselves apart from the competition by offering unique products and bringing premium tool performance to an affordable price point. Oasis Scientific Inc. was established in 2009 in Greenville, SC. Our line of products consists of Borescopes, Microscopes, Thermal Cameras, Inspection Cameras, Digital Magnifiers, Veterinary Endoscopes, and other powerful visual inspection tools. Oasis Scientific is our company, while Vividia is our brand of inspection products.

title: LOSS Red Carpet Film Premiere!
description: LOSS was filmed locally in the Greater Seattle Area by independent film maker Jima Tong. After 3 years of hard work and dedication White Eagle Productions cast and crew has put in effort and support towards one another into making this movie a great success. This premiere is to thank and honor those that stood by us through this journey. We appreciate the hard work everyone has given us and want to give them this opportunity to enjoy the fruit of their effort. In order to do so, we seek donations and support from our community to help make the premier a reality. There will be music, networking opportunities and lots of champagne! Please help donate to show support for our local film maker community in Seattle WA. To find more out more about White Eagle Productions please check us out on our official website at whiteeaglepros.com or on Facebook at www.facebook.com/lossfilm . The Red Carpet Event will be located in the heart of Seattle, WA at the McCaw Hall. McCaw Hall Backers that pledge $80, $120 or more will receive either the Premium or the VIP Gift bag depending on how much you pledge. Inside the gift bag includes: Thank you card Bottle of water Lunar charging cable Press Kit of LOSS Malden Travel Tumbler T-Shirt 20" x 24" Poster of LOSS Engraved logo of White Eagle Productions Wine Stopper and Opener Set Gift Bag

WE BRING MOVIES (AND MOVIEMAKERS) TO INDIANAPOLIS! Last year you helped us raise a record amount to bring more filmmakers to Indianapolis for the 10-day Indy Film Fest – and it was awesome! So much so, that we'd love to do it again! Indy Film Fest's mission is to create a shared experience around film. What better way to do that than to bring movie lovers and makers together? We believe magic happens when we connect festival fans (that's YOU!) with filmmakers. During this year's festival, July 13 – 23, 2017, we're aiming for more post-film Q & A sessions, more discussions, and more rubbing elbows with the talented artists who entertain and challenge us. Since 2004, the Indy Film Fest has become one of the Midwest’s most-watched film festivals — featuring the best in independent and innovative film from both award-winning professionals and emerging filmmakers from nearly every state in the country and more than 50 countries around the globe. As a non-profit, all-volunteer organization, we depend on the support of our community to sustain and flourish. FOURTEEN YEARS AND STILL GROWING! 2017 marks our fourteenth annual festival! Your kickstarter donation will go toward filmmaker travel expenses, like flights and hotels, for the 2017 festival. Being an official festival backer means you'll become a part of our community — a community of like-minded film lovers, a community that appreciates a story well told, a character expertly developed, and an experience shared on screen. Whether you contribute $10 or $1,000, you'll receive a variety of perks as our thank you for your support, ranging from screening tickets to premium access to screenings and swag. You become an official member of Indy Film Fest with your donation, meaning the rewards just keep coming, as you gain access to our year-round events and screenings. NEW STRETCH GOAL! Thanks to the contributions from our generous donors, we have hit our initial goal! However, we want to keep pushing for the few remaining days we have. As additional incentive, we're offering a free ticket to an upcoming Roving Cinema screening to every contributor regardless of your donation amount!

facebook.com thefastestseries.tumblr.com twitter.com youtube.com The Fastest is a sci-fi action series in which citizens of the small borough of Tran’s Port, find themselves moving and thinking at light speed after an accident affects random inhabitants in strange ways. OUR GOAL To transform this into a web series and grow The Fastest 
campaign into a television series.  We've written character arcs for 
dozens of fleshed out characters but will be shooting one episode for a 
character that Chris developed and a character that Greg developed. We've
 also extensively developed the history of Tran's Port, its supporting 
characters, and what's to happen in the first several seasons.  Way too 
voluminous to list here so if you're interested contact us and we can 
give you a more fleshed out synopsis: THE FASTEST: Speed Freak by Greg Payton Damian
 Quantus, a young club promoter is struck by lightning and is given the 
gift of hyper-accelerated speed and reflexes.  However, whenever he 
tries to use these new abilities for good he only winds up hurting the 
people he cares about.  Damian is approached by the covert Hermes 
Initiative, a mysterious group within the local Police Department with a
 similar skill set that can help Damian control his destiny if he 
doesn't burn himself out first. THE FASTEST: Minds Racing by Chris Chan Roberson Remmy
 and Rommy are the smartest mammals on the earth.  The twin girls were 
born impossible geniuses but are also aging at a rapid pace.  Can they 
uncover the origins of their abilities before they die from 
hyper-accelerated mental and physical exertion at the age of five? WHAT WE'VE DONE SO FAR Thanks
 to the years of teaching and on-set experience by Chris and Greg, the 
shoot is fully scheduled.  No, it's not one of those "yes we have twenty
 pages for Sunday but we'll find a way to make it work" schedules.  It's
 an achievable, practical schedule that Greg and Chris stand by 110%.  
The crew is in place and the roles have been cast.  We have all the 
equipment we need; taking advantage of the latest in RED, Sony, 
Panasonic and Go-Pro High-Definition camera technology. WHAT WE NEED NEXT Any
 filmmaker with experience will tell you that on any film the two most 
expensive budget items are food and transportation.  The money raised 
here will feed our fearless crew because without food there's no morale 
and without morale there's no movie. The money will also help 
our crew travel to various parts of New York City and beyond to 
realistically create the fictional sixth borough of Tran's Port.  Simply
 put: every dollar you spend will be seen on screen.  The money you 
invest in our film will not go towards processing fees or producer 
salaries, but will pay for someone to record professional quality sound,
 to have professional make up artists, to pay for actual locations 
opposed to redressed NYC apartments, and so forth and so on. WHAT HAPPENS IF WE DON’T REACH OUR GOAL If
 we don't reach our goal, we don't get anything.  Donors pledge the 
amounts, but don't actually pay until the goal is reached and the 
funding deadline has passed.  We want to avoid this so 
SPREAD.THE.WORD. Every dollar helps! When we meet our goal we will begin
 production as well as sending out investor packages. TO KNOW MORE ABOUT THE WORLD OF THE FASTEST...  AND ITS CREATORS; SEE BELOW Rupert Tran, our story’s precursor to the catalyst, was the most prominent globe trekking entrepreneur of the 21st century and fostered the spirit of commerce, travel, science, culture and technology pouring his nearly infinite resources into the creation of New York City's sixth borough, Tran’s Port. Tran’s Port was created to be at the forefront of a new renaissance in the 21st century. Tran’s Port acts as a hub of information and commerce as people from around the world begin to take notice. But upon Rupert Tran's disappearance and presumed demise, an initiative was activated to give the borough some hope of existing in his absence; refining and researching new technologies for the military in the “war against terror”. One evening a fantastic event occurs: an impossibly big lightning storm hits the city and several people are struck by lighting.  Miraculously they survive and are blessed with the power of hyper-active speed, intelligence and reflexes. Some have used it for personal gain, others merely survival.  But all of them pique the interests of several parties that want the people and powers for themselves. GREG AND CHRIS ARE, IN ESSENCE, BIG KIDS Greg and Chris have worked together for nearly a decade on various webseries and online pilots, always with a professional ambition, tempered with a love for visual storytelling and informed by our infamous comic book knowledge and sensibility. But it is the faith in one another, that has maintained their professional relationship. A moment highlighting this faith, was when Greg and Chris showed up to Midtown Comics in New York at the midnight launch of DC's reboot of their universe several months ago.  Several times the long crowd was asked to disperse; the gathering of people waiting were told there weren't enough tickets, creators Geoff Johns and Jim Lee were leaving early, and that there wasn't enough capacity to receive everyone. Regardless, Greg and Chris’ gut told them otherwise and they endured the elements and long wait to get their copy of Flashpoint#5 and Justice League#1.  Chris and Greg have a love for comics that dates back to their childhood.  The Fastest is an homage to all of the wonderful elements that have inspired readers over the years as well as an attack on those elements that are a slap in the face to a fan (reboots, retcons, and retellings of stories).  Greg and Chris are, with The Fastest, tapping into the joy they got at kids opening up a comic book and the excitement of a non-traditional, drama.

*** Content Update *** 1. New gameplay video : https://youtu.be/ptl5CybDHLs Inside Trader is a cross-platform, multiplayer mobile game that features character specific abilities and realistic trading simulation. Players must use their wits to compete against one another by securing the most profit in a game. Character specific abilities allow players to form a strategy to profit from the stock’s rise or decline, or win by sabotaging opponents’ wealth. Watch out though, the more inside trades you make, the more likely you are to get caught! At the core of the Inside Trader game engine is a simulation order processing system. This system simulates real-time buying and selling of financial assets and moves prices according to supply and demand. While prices can be altered by user interaction, there is also a game level buyer and seller that simulates market reactions to macroeconomic events. To achieve the objective of obtaining the most profit in the game, every player must strategically allocate their initial cash to acquire assets or abilities. Asset values fluctuate just like real-world investments; “buy low sell high” is your friend. Abilities can turn the tables in your favor by allowing you to impact the direction and magnitude that the stock will likely move after they are played. Example Stock-Impacting Abilities: Not all characters come with stock-impacting abilities. In developing different play styles in our game, we have created characters that rely on sabotaging others to win. Targeted abilities allows you to inflict damage on a specific player in the game, this could be stealing their stock-impacting abilities or increasing their risk of being caught. Example Targeted Abilities: While Inside Trader is now a working prototype, there is still a lot we have to do before it is ready for the App Store and Google Play.  With the extra budget acquired from Kickstarter backers, we aim to add many new features to the game, many of them involving user customization and upgraded game mechanics. Our ultimate goal is not only to make a game that is fun to play, but also worth playing. 10,000 = Game release with four characters, one for each class. 20,000 = Game release with eight characters, two for each class. 30,000 = Game release with eight characters and build your own character feature. 40,000 = AI bots and single player mode Packages $1 = our love and gratitude $5 or more = our love and gratitude + 100 Poisson Coins $15 or more = our love and gratitude + 150 Poisson Coins + character stickers $50 = our love and gratitude + 500 Poisson Coins + character stickers + digital wallpaper $100 = our love and gratitude + 1000 Poisson Coins + character stickers + digital wallpaper + game soundtrack $200+ = our $100 package plus honorable mention in game credits. Limited Quantity $500 = design a character skin. Our Art Director will work with you to design a skin to be included in the game's release. Limit 5.

The Wolf Evolves Picture a moonlit night in a quiet forest... A lone wolf stands on a ridge, silver moonlight tracing the outline of its form. The image of solitude, power, and beauty is at the heart of this deck’s narrative. The CANIS LUPUS Beta Edition cards blend the characteristics of the wolf while also capturing the energy of the bloodstone to represent courage, vitality, and strength. The deck captures this energy in a minimalist form. Sleek lines, refined geometry, splatters of bloodstone and storytelling in the moonlight. This is not just a deck; it's the rise of the underdog. The Beta will no longer stand behind the Alpha... Refined by Design These CANIS LUPUS Beta Edition Playing Cards are a huge step up from the Alpha, featuring a more customized design than ever before while still keeping things minimal. There are two editions of the Beta deck, each crafted with a purpose: Beta Edition — A pure, minimalist design on printed on Room One's Elite stock for everyday use. Great for Card Magicians, Cardists, Collectors, and literally everyone. Includes a subtle one-way back design and color marking system. Bloodstone Beta Edition — Features foiling and gilding on the cards, with a tuck box wrapped in metallic foil for an elevated card game experience. Printed on Room One's Afflux stock. Includes a subtle one-way back design. Additional Highlights The faces of the cards are done in two recognizable colors: Red and Black. But this isn't just any standard red, it's a deep red... to represent the bloodstone. And to top it all off, each of the cards have subtle one-way face designs for that extra bit of magic. And of course, we can't forget to show you the incredible gilding done on the Bloodstone Edition of these playing cards These editions represent two sides of the same moonlit path: one subtle and strong, the other gleaming and ceremonial. Legacy in Motion This is the second edition in the CANIS LUPUS series from a journey that began with the original CANIS LUPUS Alpha Edition Playing Cards , a bold, amethyst debut that introduced the spirit of the Wolf. That was followed by the refined and luxurious Alpha Lux , which elevated the design with gilding, foil, and finish. Now, the Beta Edition emerges as the next evolution. Bolder , Fiercer , and more Deliberate . As always, the design process starts with an idea, just drawing things out on paper and seeing what fits. For this project, I had tons of idea on how to incorporate another gemstone - the Bloodstone (following the Alpha deck's Amethyst). As you saw, the previous deck featured claw marks, I wanted this to center around another wolf theme, so I thought about the moon and how that could be incorporated. Then lastly, the wolf itself. Here is a snapshot of some of my rough sketches and ideas: As you can clearly see - one my drawing skills are terrible, and two it turned out very similar to my early drawings! Big shoutout to my designer for being able to see and share this vision! The legacy will continue with the CANIS LUPUS Beta Lux... The Cards The most difficult part about making a custom deck of playing cards is figuring out the level of customization that should be integrated without deviating too much from the original design. We really took that into account when designing the cards themselves but took a lot more liberties with the tuck box design and the back design. The tuck box and the back design are fully custom designed by Destino with me as the "Creative Director". The results were absolutely stunning! Tuck Box (Bloodstone Beta) Back Design (Bloodstone Beta) THE CARDS For the card faces, we wanted to add subtleties within the design to integrate the wolf theme. And we accomplished just that! The Court cards carry subtle nods to the Wolf: The Spades - Feature Claw Marks/Scratches The Diamonds - Feature Wolf Fangs The Clubs - Feature Paw Prints The Hearts - Feature the Moon The back design also features secrets for those inclined towards card magic. SUBTLE One-Way FACE DESIGN and BACK DESIGN Featured on both decks, this is an excellent way to determine which way a card is facing or inserted back into the deck. Great for MANY card tricks! SUBTLE Color Marking System The color marking system is an INCREDIBLE feature that assists with many tricks that rely on knowing colors of playing cards. The classic "Out of this World" card trick is a great example. Card Details The CANIS LUPUS Beta Edition and the CANIS LUPUS Bloodstone Beta Edition cards are both printed on different card stocks by Room One . Beta Edition: Printed on Room One Elite Stock - 300gsm Paper. This stock provides a luxurious feel, combining durability and flexibility. Bloodstone Beta Edition: Printed on Room One Afflux Stock - 290gsm Paper. Crafted from high grade paper materials, offering a reliable and consistent experience. This version also features hot red foil on the back design and tuck box as well as red gilded edges to really elevate this edition. ONE LAST SECRET The Bloodstone Beta Edition deck contains a special QR code card that leads to a secret. Will you be able to figure it out and claim the prize?! All of these cards hold a ton of secrets and details; I can't wait to see them in your hands. Add-Ons For those of you who really want to dive deeper into the CANIS LUPUS collection, there will be three add-ons available! Wall Display - Limited to 10 (International Shipping) Proudly display the CANIS LUPUS Playing Cards in your collection. A functional display piece within your home. Home-crafted using Walnut wood with a sleek black plated label. Wall Display Dimensions: Outer Frame - 5.5"(L) x 7"(W) Deck Holds - 3.5"(L) x 2.5"(W) x 0.75"(Depth) Display Stand - Limited to 10 (International Shipping) Give the Alpha Lux deck the attention it deserves by displaying it on a home-crafted Walnut display stand with a beautiful black plated label. Display Stand Dimensions: 5"(L) x 3.5"(W) x 1"(H) Close-Up Pad - Limited to 5 (International Shipping) The Alpha Lux cards need a platform to shine as you show off your skills at the card table. Check out the CANIS LUPUS close-up pad, home-crafted on walnut wood with laser engravings and a well cushioned, velvet pad. Close-Up Pad Dimensions: Outer Board - 17.5"(L) x 12"(W) Inner Pad - 15.5"(L) x 10"(W) Huge shoutout to Mr. Bennie Holcomb for his work phenomenal craftsmanship! Couldn't have been possible without your creativity and passion. Other additional add-ons are available as well, such as the CANIS LUPUS Alpha Edition, and Alpha Lux Playing Cards, along with deck signings. Fulfillment After getting experience with shipping out orders for the Alpha Edition and Alpha Lux decks, the CardMechanic Studio is more ready than ever to fulfill orders. BUT I thought I could use a little bit of help... Room One will be fulfilling all international orders related solely to the Beta Edition cards (Greatly reducing shipping costs outside of the United States). All orders within the US will be packed, shipped, and fulfilled by yours truly. Stretch Goals 125% of Funding Goal => Beta Lux Deck Production It took me FOUR YEARS to release the Lux deck after the original CANIS LUPUS Playing Cards. I don't want to wait another four years to release the Beta Lux cards. Let's make this goal happen and we'll go TO INFINITY AND BEYOND with the next edition. 150% of Funding Goal => Community Card A reallllyyyyyy awesome way to have you more involved in the creative process, would be for you to have your OWN card within the next deck! If we reach this goal in Kickstarter, we'll create a community card together for the Beta Lux version of the CANIS LUPUS deck. Whatever design people want to see, we'll make it happen! 200% of Funding Goal => $750 Magic Contest in 2026 I had a TON of fun doing the first Prestige of the Pack magic contest this past year. I learned a lot throughout the process, what to do, what not to do, and areas where I can make things a lot more fun and entertaining! If we reach this goal in Kickstarter, Prestige of the Pack season 2 will drop in mid-2026, with the biggest prize fund to date... $750 ! My goal since the inception of my YouTube channel has always been to grow the world of magic and inspire others to perform and entertain. Based on the feedback I got from Season 1, that's exactly what this accomplished! Thank You THANK YOU for supporting this project in whatever way you could. Whether it's a purchase, a share, words of encouragement, or any type of support, it's more appreciated than you know! The release of the CANIS LUPUS Alpha Lux deck gave me the encouragement I needed to start the Beta series. It's crazy to just say that the ALPHA LUX DECK IS SOLD OUT! It's because of your support that the Beta Edition cards are coming to fruition and I can't wait to get them in hand just to share with all of you! From the bottom of my heart and soul, THANK YOU! ------ Credits ------ There are some people who I'd like to give a special shoutout to because of the impact they had on this project. Manufacturers: Ocean from Room One ; for helping me through their entire manufacturing process, providing me details on everything, providing me with feedback and deck samples, and EVERYTHING else he's done. Designer: Destino; for working with me on the design, giving me great ideas, and being able to handle my design madness. Craftsman: Bennie Holcomb; for his fantastic work on the display stands, close-up pads, and wall displays. Friends: Armon, Nicole, and Julie; for always providing encouragement, providing feedback, and sharing their enthusiasm for everything I've done. Community: Underground Club Members, the YouTube community, folks across all social media platforms, and members of card communities. Creators: Stu Pedassle, Daniel Huckins, Jeremy Tan, Silas Noyes, KTrickstery, Nerdx52, Card Collector, and more; for helping me promote these cards and having a great impact on the card community. Moving Forward If you made it this far, I want to share something with you. I want this deck to mark this deck as "CardMechanic's turning point". I want to lean in harder, do more for the community, become a better influence, set the gold standard and raise it every time. If this project funds 200% , I will spend ALL my time with content creation, community engagement, and more!

EVERYONE LOVES TO DANCE Dance is core to the very being of so many people, and many more want to learn! Wouldn’t it be amazing if you could learn any dance style at home, in the privacy of your own living room? What if you could build the confidence to go out dancing by simply slipping on a VR or AR headset and learning from a judgment-free virtual instructor? Maybe you just want to shake it out in an all new type of rhythm and dance game that’s never been possible before? Salsa, Swing, Hip Hop, Line Dancing etc. TRIP THE LIGHT makes it all possible! TRIP THE LIGHT is a fully immersive dance game that brings the connection of partner dancing to life. Whether you're a beginner or a seasoned dancer, you can experience authentic ballroom , salsa , swing , hip hop, and line dancing in stunning environments or in your own living room. Dance hand-in-hand with virtual AI partners and instructors in real time, learning techniques while unlocking new levels, dance styles, outfits, accessories and more. Perfect for fitness, fun, and improving your real life dance skills. Reception and Praise TRIP THE LIGHT has received an overwhelmingly positive response! We’ve conducted user research studies and have taken the project on the road to multiple trade shows and conferences including Augmented World Expo , Game Developers Conference and GamesBeat . Hundreds of people have played it, and out of all the data we've collected, one metric stands out: EVERYBODY SMILES! Charlie Fink of Forbes had this to say after trying the game at AWE earlier this year: Let's Dance was the working code name for the project at the time this article was written Full Article: https://www.forbes.com/sites/charliefink/2024/06/26/glasses-gadgets-haptics-and-playing-around-at-awe-2024 We've already built a small fan base of people who really want to see this game come out! Join them in making it a reality! Features Incredible AI powered virtual dance partners This is the true magic of what we're building! Our cutting-edge Partner Dance System lets you reach out and touch your virtual partner. Take their hand and dance with them just like you would in real life. Partners adapt to your skill level, helping you master moves, timing, coordination, and core skills. We use cutting edge AI and Machine Learning techniques to teach our virtual partners how to danced base on motion capture data. Characters We are proud to have a roster of beautiful virtual partner characters in development and at the concept level. What’s really cool about the initial four we've modeled is that they're based on real, talented people who have been or are currently contributing directly to the project. All of them have been kind enough to share their likenesses with us. Miyoung Margolis Miyoung is a professional dancer, dance teacher, choreographer, artist and model. In addition to her likeness and dancing skills, Miyoung has graciously provided use of her dance studio, and access to her amazing dance network. She has donned the mocap suit on numerous occasions and provided the animation for most of the initial salsa moves used in the game and the Machine Learning data. She also coaches Patrick, who really needs it! https://www.instagram.com/mikkukfriend Veronica Lyn Esque Veronica is a video game Art Director and was the first person to help with the project. Working in the games industry in Seattle, Veronica and Patrick crossed paths many times and became friends over their shared love of dance and animation. Veronica provided the first suit we used for motion capture and wore a MoCap suit herself on the first shoots. She also art directed the design of her 3D character model likeness. https://www.verolynesque.com/ Jaz Lund Jaz is a professional singer and musician. She and Patrick met over a decade ago when they were in a band together in Seattle. In addition to sharing her likeness, Jaz has lent her beautiful voice and talent to the project. Jaz is the creative brain and singer of the game’s first fully complete original track “Move Your Body,” and she's also the voice of your dance partner in the current version. Let’s get this thing funded so we can get her back in the studio to record more music and V.O. https://www.instagram.com/jazlynshepard/ Za Thomaier Za is an award-winning professional dancer and dance instructor. Za is our first male character model, and provided some of the earliest MoCap dance animations for the first prototype. He is an absolutely incredible performer and we are honored that he has shared his likeness and skill with us. https://www.instagram.com/zathomaierdance Interactive Tutorials Start with basics of popular dances, then progress to more complex moves and partner routines, all while receiving real-time feedback on your technique. Feel nervous about going out Salsa dancing because you don't know the basics? Learn at home first! Mirror Me Match you partner's movements! Perfect for Line Dancing, Hip Hop, Pop and the latest social media dance crazes. This feature can also be the backbone for challenge modes and exercise routines. We’ve cracked the code on how to show and teach you to move in VR / AR. Full AR Integration Switch to AR mode and bring your virtual instructors and partners into the real-world, turning your living room into a dance studio. No need to worry about crashing into walls! Campaign / Story Mode Level up to encounter ever more talented partners, progress through spectacular venues, become the best dancer in the land and show off your moves! Customizable and Social Experience Personalize your avatar, choose from a variety of music tracks, and select different difficulty levels to match your skill level. Record your performance and share your best moves directly to your linked social media accounts! Our Team Our amazing core team is made up of highly experienced veterans and rising stars! Founder Experience Patrick has over 23 years of experience in the fields of video gaming, VR, AR and related technology. He's worked on top teams at big firms including Microsoft, Meta and Vulcan, as well as startups including Camouflaj, the company behind Iron Man VR, and Presence Labs which had a successful music VR App on the Gear VR. Patrick has shipped dozen of well known video games and multiple gaming hardware projects including Xbox, Kinect and Hololens. He has led successful teams from technical and creative perspectives. Here are just a few of the projects and companies he's contributed to: Rewards Goals Future Steps Why Kickstarter Many reasons! We truly believe in this project and in ourselves. We think you’re going to believe too! Kickstarter allows us to bring our specific vision directly to you. Also, this isn’t the first project Patrick has worked on that had a successful Kickstarter campaign! He worked on a successful game that got its start on Kickstarter back in 2011 called Republique that raised over $555,000. Many successful VR projects have campaigned on Kickstarter, including the headset that started it all, The Oculus Rift! Now twelve years later, the VR / AR industry is booming and there are millions of headsets in people's homes. Kickstarter helped start the VR hardware revolution, it's only natural that it helps with the VR software revolution! We want your input Backers on Kickstarter can get exclusive super early access to the game! We want to give you a preview on your headset ASAP so we can get your feedback and make TRIP THE LIGHT fantastic! What's the money for Right now, we have an absolutely amazing 'pre alpha' version that’s universally praised. It works great and is super fun! The money will be used to finish the project and get it launched into the market as soon as possible. The vast majority of the money goes to the people who make and do all the stuff that it takes to build this thing! If we hit our minimum, we can get the first version of the product launched as quickly as possible, with early access / preview builds shared to backers along the way. If we hit our stretch goals, we can grow the team and bring more incredible dance content and features to you sooner! The remaining spend goes to rent, legal (contracts, copyright, trademark, compliance, patents etc), and the hardware and software we need to do our jobs. THANK YOU FROM THE WHOLE DARK ARTS TEAM AND EXTENDED FAMILY!

1899 DAIHAN In 1899, the Seoul-Incheon railway marked a significant step in Korea's modernization. Our game transports you to this pivotal era, when the Korean Empire was caught between Imperial Japan, Russia, and Western powers. As the Empire teetered on the brink of collapse, forced to become a Japanese protectorate in 1905 and ultimately annexed in 1910, you'll have the chance to shape its destiny. Experience the thrill of preserving and reshaping Korean history, making choices that could change the fate of this struggling nation. Step into a bygone era of ambition and innovation. Take the reins of a national railway company, founded with Korean capital. Your mission? To drive national prosperity through strategic railway expansion. Inspired by real-life historical endeavors, you'll navigate a world shaped by the Korean Empire's bold vision for industrialization. Compete with foreign powers vying for influence, each with their own railway empires. The starting point is set, but the destination is yours to define. Will you forge a new path for Korea, or will you be swallowed by the tides of history? Maximize your economic potential and secure Korea's prosperity. Every turn, you'll balance economic growth with national resilience, shaping the Empire's destiny with each railway track laid. Can you defy the odds, outmaneuver foreign powers, and lead Korea to a future of wealth and strength? Embrace the challenge, and overcome the odds with your strategy, courage, and determination. Join us on this epic journey to rewrite history and experience the thrill of building a stronger, independent Korea. OVERVIEW The game is primarily divided into two phases: Stock Round, Operating Round. Stock Round – Players buy and sell railway company shares, causing stock prices to fluctuate. New companies may also be established. Operating Round – Only established railway companies operate, build tracks and run trains to generate revenue. This system revolves around the transportation and trade of four major resources: rice, coal, gold, and ginseng. These resources were significant exports, and each of them is represented by three production sites on the map. Additionally, each resource is associated with a private company, which manages and participates in the trade of that specific resource. When a corporation acquires a private company, a production token for the corresponding resource is placed at one of the three production sites, along with a railway connection. This production token represents the origin of the resource, from which the transportation route begins. Corporations can generate additional revenue by operating along the railway line, transporting the resource from its production site to its designated collection point, such as a port or border area. This provides an incentive for corporations to expand their operations and maximize profits through efficient transportation. Once a production site is established, however, all corporations that can access the same railway route are allowed to participate in the transportation of that resource. In other words, securing a production site does not grant exclusive transport rights to that corporation. Other corporations may also utilize the railway line to transport resources from the production site to ports or border areas, allowing them to take part in the trade as well. This structure encourages competition among corporations and maximizes the utilization of the railway network. In conclusion, this system is designed to manage the efficient transportation of resources from production sites to collection points, fostering competition among multiple corporations. Corporations strategically use railway routes and production tokens to effectively transport resources and maximize their revenue. Introducing another unique feature of this game: the 'Executive Car' system The Executive Car can be optionally attached when purchasing a train, allowing the train to skip over a selected city or town during its journey. Once attached, it cannot be removed, and it only returns to the supply when the train is retired. Use the Executive Car strategically to add an exciting new layer of depth to your play COMPONENTS Product Specification Development Process Our Kickstarter journey began with a passion for history and strategic gaming. We started by exploring Korea's rich yet tumultuous history, focusing on an era marked by rapid modernization and foreign interference. We created prototypes, rigorously tested them, and refined the gameplay based on feedback from testers. We are now ready to bring this game to life with your support. Backing this project will allow us to produce high-quality components, ensure historical accuracy, and provide an engaging experience for players. Join us in bringing this unique story to the tabletop gaming world! The rulebook for 1899 DAIHAN is undergoing final refinement prior to production. Previews of the rules are available here for download. Preview of Section 10 (Private Companies List) as detailed in the rulebook Backer Rewards Shipping Info Taxes, Customs, and Fees Our logistics partners will make every effort to minimize additional fees upon delivery. However, as these fees are determined by your country’s local customs regulations, taxes, and policies, we cannot confirm all charges. Any additional charges incurred after the initial dispatch of the 1899 DAIHAN will be the responsibility of the recipient. Due to the unpredictable nature, and varying policies for each country and delivery region, we cannot cover these fees in advance or include them in our shipping fees. We invite all backers to double-check their local policies regarding imports from other countries, as the 1899 DAIHAN will be dispatched from its manufacturing country. Changes or reductions of the product's declared value, product type, or product made cannot be made. Shipping to Remote Locations If your shipping address is usually considered a 'remote location' additional delivery charges may be incurred upon dispatch due to our shipping providers’ policies. title: ExoTerra : The Giant Mech Campaign You've Been Waiting For
description: Story ExoTerra is a cooperative campaign game where 1-4 players pilot massive mechs called Jackets, battle cunning enemy pilots, and fight for their place in a new solar system. The freedom of deep deck customization and flexible paths to victory provide the perfect arena for brutal mech combat, as you follow the story of a group of Jacket pilots hailing from the frozen moon of Pelmont in an ongoing sci-fi campaign . Customizing their Jackets and exploiting the weaknesses of rival pilots will give players an edge as they work towards the defeat of the sinister Vega Apex - the driving force behind the occupation of their home world and the struggle of their people.  They must make choices on how to interact with the world around them as each decision has a lasting impact.  Only the bravery of these few pilots can stop the impending doom of mankind. Create a Jacket unit with your friends from 15 different mech options - each with unique decks and play styles! Build your Arsenal with powerful weapons, complex AI-personas, armor, and equipment to play however you like and claim victory. What Is ExoTerra? Key Features Soak up all the intricate details of your behemoth Jacket as you battle on the green plains of Chal, the inky depths of space, and the frozen tundra of Pelmont.  15 unique miniatures - one for each Jacket that players can pilot. Customize your Arsenal by combining cards from your Jacket, Gear, and upgrades from various Vendors in the ExoTerra universe.  Your Jacket provides a kit of abilities to your deck that you can supplement with a plethora of weapons and equipment.  Let your play style dictate which cards you remove or add as you fine-tune your deck.  If you want to pull all of the defensive cards out of a burly frontline Jacket, load it with thrusters, ammo cards, and stealth mods, and pick up a sniper rifle...go for it! Sorties are flexible and provide a great deal of player agency as you choose how best to approach an objective.  The outcomes of a battle are player-driven - Will you use map features to aid in your mission or brute force them?  Will you use the terrain for hit and run tactics, or charge up the middle with a good support Jacket at your back?  The campaign will even introduce random quests and points of interest as you play so there are always options in a fight! Rival pilots will interact with the world and take advantage of the choices you make...or don't make. Which pilots you'll face in a given battle is slightly randomized so you always have a chance of an old rival showing up with new tricks and a chip on their shoulder. Dismantle their Jackets to thwart their strategies! A strong emphasis on cooperative player interaction creates engaging teamplay and eliminates multiplayer solitaire.  You could build your Jacket to support your allies, defend them, or let them fend for themselves as you hunt enemy pilots or take care of locations on the battlefield. Unlock the full potential of your Jacket by using your Components to perform powerful abilities. But be careful - if you push your Hull, Cybernetics, and Chassis too far, you may find yourself in a dire situation or even forced to retreat.  Will you use your Countermeasures to protect your Jacket, or to enhance your strategy? While your mission objectives in a Sortie are clear, the tales told on the battlefield will always vary.  Perhaps you choose to rescue civilians during a fight and an enemy pilot is forever grateful to you for saving their family.  Perhaps your defeat of an enemy causes them to become weary of the war, making them nervous and watchful during a fight.  This could pair perfectly with their ability to lay mines in their wake creating a fun arc for that character.  Playing through a session of ExoTerra will expose players to character-driven plots, environmental storytelling, and rich lore. What's In The Box? What's In The Minis Box? What's In The Expansion? Pledge Tiers Why Back Now? Bringing ExoTerra to the world has been my passion project for 5 years now. Every penny that has gone into the development of this game has been a personal investment. I really need the support of the community to get the project across the finish line and onto your tables!  My goal is to make a game for my fellow fans of mechs and cooperative games.  We've brought ExoTerra to many conventions including Gen Con,  OrcCon, and GamerCon and have gotten it in front of fans of every mech game and anime you can think of (well maybe not EVERY one haha).  The response from play testers has been overwhelmingly positive and ideas from all around have made their way into the project to truly make it the giant mech game you've been waiting for! If you'd like to chat about ExoTerra, hop in a play test or just talk mechs, please feel free to join our Discord Community . Playthroughs How To Play Choose a pilot and Jacket, then build and customize your Arsenal by adding Gear and Vendor cards to your deck.  Each Jacket's starting deck includes two focus areas with unique abilities, but you can always tweak this later based on your preferred play style! Set up your Components - Chassis, Hull, and Cybernetics, your Countermeasures - Armor, Shields, and Thrusters, and power up your reactor to enter the battle.  You can push your luck by spending these resources to perform more powerful abilities, but be careful not to leave yourself too exposed! Quickly set up the Sortie using the map books and placing tokens and units on the battlefield as laid out in the Campaign book.  The enemy Jackets in a battle will be called out, but the pilots in those Jackets will come from a pool of rivals from each player, providing variability and suspense to your games.  You may see a new face or an old nemesis shows up to get revenge for their last defeat, but with new abilities, weapons, and stories. Play cards and perform basic abilities to traverse the battlefield, power up your Jacket, and clash with your deadly rivals.  Players can take turns in any order to allow for strategic planning. Interact with map objectives and work with your allies to increase your chances of success.  Each map is littered with points of interest, supplies, and wreckage that you can choose to use to find the right approach to an objective for your build and your group. Enemy Jacket pilots are dangerous and cunning.  They may have their own personal goals and ambitions, but their prime directive is always to destroy you.  A unique 'macro' system makes enemy activation easy and gives the players all of the information needed to best these opponents.  Activation Tokens are pulled randomly from a bag to determine when and how these enemies, and any Sortie events trigger. Complete mission objectives and destroy the enemy Jackets to claim victory in a Sortie. Enemy Macros can be disabled through clever dismantling of their Jacket Components or by outplaying them entirely. Use the Reputation you gain on the battlefield to gain upgrades, weapons, programs, and equipment for your Arsenal and customize your deck to suite your playstyle.  As you progress through the 3 story arcs in the campaign, your Reputation will earn you better and more powerful Jackets and gear. Rulebook For those of you who like to dive right in, please check out our work-in-progress rulebook. Note - this component has yet to receive any graphic design and still has a great deal of editing, consolidation, and formatting work to be done. Try it! Demo ExoTerra today and let us know what you think about the gameplay mechanics! Stretch Goals Add-Ons Pledge Over Time We are excited to be able to leverage a new Kickstarter feature that allows backers to split their pledge into 3 payments. When you make a pledge, you can choose the "Pledge Over Time" option to split your payments up.  The first charge will occur when the campaign ends successfully with the remaining 2 payments collected each following month until the pledge total is paid in full. We hope this makes it easier for gamers to get games on their tables!  Let us know if you have any questions! Shipping Because shipping costs vary depending on your location and may shift overtime, your shipping costs will be calculated and collected in the pledge manager after the end of our campaign and closer to our ship date.  This includes sales taxes and VAT. You will receive an email from our pledge manager, a few weeks after the campaign to confirm your pledge and add any additional items you would like. In the survey, you will be able to confirm your pledge and add any additional items you would like to get. Later, you will receive an email a few weeks before fulfillment where you will be able to pay for shipping costs and any taxes or duties where applicable. Please be aware that the shipping rate estimates provided in the table above are only approximations for our different pledge levels (including stretch goals). Final charges for shipping and sales tax or VAT (where applicable) will be determined based on actual shipping costs incurred. Unfortunately, import laws prohibit us from shipping games to certain countries. We are not able to ship to the following countries: Russia, Afghanistan, Ukraine, South America, Iran, Iraq, Syria When it’s time to fulfill your pledge, we will send your rewards to one of our international shipping hubs to then distribute to you! These shipping hubs will help streamline our fulfillment process and decrease VAT so you can have faster, cheaper, more reliable shipping. We have hubs in the UK, USA, Canada, China, and Australia. Meet The Team Hey folks, Jared here!  I'm so so so excited to finally be sharing ExoTerra with you!  I've been a board game fanatic for as long as I can remember, with some of my favorites being Xia, Eldritch Horror, and Mage Knight (my very first board game!).  I spent many years making little mods and expansions to these games and decided to take things to the next level when one day I was unable to find a co-op mech campaign game to play with my group.  ExoTerra has been a passion project of mine for over 5 years.  If you can tell from my favorite games, I'm a big fan of variability, emergent narratives, and large room for player creativity.  Those were some of the guiding principals I had when I set out on this project, and I think the team has done an amazing job at making that vision into a reality! I could not have done this alone, and owe everything to the positivity and support of my wife and family.  When things got really hard, they kept me going.  They made sacrifices so that ExoTerra could exist.  Thank you so very much!  I'm also incredibly grateful to all of the folks in the mech community that have supported the project.  From helping with rules language, helping spread the word, and helping balance the game with play testing...you are all critical Components of this Jacket 😉.  A huge thank you to all of the folks on the ExoTerra team creating amazing art, 3d designs, animations, and ensuring that the game is easy to learn, remember, and play! I'm blown away when I see the excitement that ExoTerra stirs in people.  I grin like a fool when play testers use cards and abilities in creative ways that I never intended.  I feel great joy when the game creates interesting stories for players on their own mech adventure.  A huge thank you to everyone who made this possible - and happy piloting! Retailers I'm a huge fan of perusing games and hanging out at my local game shop and would absolutely love seeing ExoTerra on their shelves!  If you are a retailer interested in stocking our game, please fill out the form below.  Please do not pledge to the campaign for stock for your store and we will reach out to you after the completion of the campaign.  Thank you! Partners This project is promoted by Jellop, the ad tech power behind the best Kickstarters with 3,500+ successful Kickstarter projects with over $1B raised between them. Made By Humans ExoTerra does not use AI art.  We've got a great team of artists that have done an amazing job of realizing the vision of ExoTerra's mood and themes.  Our budget for art on the project is ~$30,000 with even more coming in through stretch goals.

Screaming Brittle Siren is one of Michael Knott's best releases from start to finish there is not one bad song on it. Come join us in making this historic event happen.This Alternative Classic will be available to be released on a Limited Edition Double Viny with an Autographed Insert and Limited Edition Digipack CD also Autographed! Unfortunately, in order to properly release this it has to be a Double Vinyl which means higher manufacturing costs. I have tried to keep the costs down where I could. I think in the end it will be a release we will all love to play on our turntables. This is moment we have been waiting for and we can't do this project without you the fans! So please pledge and also spread the word around to your fellow Michael Knott Fans! SPIN Magazine.... Screaming Brittle Siren Named Top Ten in it's Genre in 2022 "It’s nice when an album’s title accurately describes what you hear when you give that album a spin, although “Screaming Brittle Siren” Only describes the loud parts. Not that there aren’t plenty (think grunge with pinches of goth and Blue Öyster Cult) but they’re spelled by the occasional Kronos Quartet-like interlude and threaded through with introspective lyrics"....

My name is Julian. I'm a 44 year-old musician, husband and father, and I'm making an album about being middle-aged. There are plenty of albums about being young and cool. I’ve made a few of them myself! I’ve written songs about finding love, searching for fame, and trying to keep it all together. But this is the first collection of songs I know of that deals with the rest of life: the struggles of parenthood, monotony of marriage, fear of dying - you know, the real stuff . Recording albums is expensive, especially for a middle-aged person with a family. I'm launching this Kickstarter with the belief that there are enough people who like my music to get this album funded. Kickstarter is an all or nothing platform, so I have 60 days to hit the below goals, or I get zilch. Since I live in Los Angeles now, I'm employing some of the world's finest musicians to make these tunes come to life. In the spirit of the classic 1970s songwriter albums I adore, this album features a real Hollywood string section. Take a look at the crew I've lined up: And as a reward for contributing to the campaign, you'll get one-of-a-kind exclusives including: I'll be posting weekly updates throughout the campaign that will include videos journals covering the entire process of making the record: If we raise enough funds, I'll print vinyl copies of this album, as well as a vinyl repress for my NYC concept album, If You Don't Like It, You Can Leave , which celebrates its 10th anniversary this year. Also, backers will get the album as soon as the campaign ends - months before it's available worldwide. This is my first album in four years and, considering how busy the life of a middle-aged dad is, I'm not sure when I'll get to make another. Help me bring this new music into the world, or I'll be forced to pay for the whole thing myself. Nobody wants that. Especially not my wife and kids (see below).

My new album, Make Light of the Dark , is currently in the works. These songs reflect where I am in life right now—what I care most about, both as a writer and as a person. At its core, this record is about relationships : finding real love, losing someone you thought you’d be with forever, and learning what it means to walk alongside another person through life, especially when things get hard. Many of these songs were born from moments of support and doubt, connection and uncertainty, and the choice to stay when it would be easier to leave. The album is also about resilience —about holding on to your drive and inspiration when the road ahead feels unclear. Some songs explore heavier themes like loss, uncertainty, and disability, while others are lighter and more playful, finding humor and perspective in starting over or learning to live with less. The goal of this record isn’t to dwell in darkness, but to face it honestly while still offering hope . I want these songs to feel real and human, leaving listeners encouraged and empowered. Additionally, a portion of the net proceeds from this Kickstarter campaign will also go to the foundation, Fighting Blindness , to help them find a cure and treatment options for retinitis pigmentosa and other inherited eye diseases. Make Light of the Dark is being produced by Bobby Holland , a two-time Grammy–nominated producer, and will be recorded in April in Nashville. Bobby and I both live with retinitis pigmentosa, and that shared experience deeply shapes how we approach this project. Together, we’re focused on creating something honest and lasting—built on real performances and timeless songwriting. This is an independent project , and your support makes it possible. Thank you for believing in my music and for being part of bringing this record to life. With love and light, WE Make Light of the Dark CD My Hometown Listening Party A Song Written Just for You Stories Behind the Songs

WHO ARE YOU? My name is Gordon McKinney- a songwriter, storyteller, producer and singer from Norman, Oklahoma (by way of Southeastern Illinois.) As a young kid, I got hooked on musical theatre. I sang in church. I played drums in school band, took piano lessons, tried my hand at jazz choir ( I am NOT a dancer!) and even thought I could be an actor after spending a summer as an apprentice at the famous Williamstown Theatre Festival. I've always been performing, creating and searching for the best way to share my life's experience with the world. And then, I found the guitar. And my voice. Once I picked up the guitar, I knew it was the best vehicle to share my stories and songs. It doesn't require a full band. No need for amplification. It travels well and can tell it's own story when lovingly played. Still, after 36 years of playing, it challenges me. Pushes me. Supports and encourages me. And, every so often, reminds me that I am still not good enough to stop practicing. I started crafting songs. Songs about the common themes we all experience. Love, loss, hope, humor, family, relationships, successes and failures. Slowly, I moved from third person narratives to first person. I longed to be as vulnerable as possible when sharing my songs. I felt it was necessary to be vulnerable if I was to be honest. To be kind. To grow. To be open and afraid, but still willing to share in hopes that my audience and I could, for a moment, feel less alone in the world. More connected. More seen. Heard. At the age of 48, I decided to finally pursue music full time. To write down my dreams and chase them. To give up on the idea that working hard at what doesn't matter to me IS what matters. That accumulating stuff means success. That a passion is just a hobby. That a backup plan is important. I wanted experiences, travel, truth and the discomfort that accompanies it. So, along with my partner, we booked a UK Tour in 2019. No management. No label. No press or real presence on social media platforms. No agent, no record to support and no one who knew my name in the UK. All I had were songs and a need to know if I could do it. If what I was writing and singing could resonate with people I had little in common with and who did not know my story. After 7 months of cold calls, hundreds of emails, messages, inquiries, begging, pleading and relationship building, I had a tour. A small tour. But, a tour made up of people who heard my songs, believed in them and took a chance that I could fill their pub, venue, theatre or home. That my songs were what they wanted to share with their customers, family and friends. IT. WAS. MAGICAL! I walked away from that initial tour with a confidence I'd never known. In myself, my songs, and in my ability to prove through songwriting that, I have what it takes to make music my livelihood and better yet, my legacy. So we did it again. And again. And again. Each year on my previous tours, I gained fans. I made more friends. My earliest relationships overseas began to feel like family. People started opening their homes. Feeding me. Driving me to gigs. Designing, producing and selling my merchandise. Offering weekends to relax in their gardens. Tours of museums and botanical gardens. Setting up last minute gigs and often helping me carry equipment. And, in 2021 when I got Covid during the tour, I was offered a flat to stay in at no charge during my 10 day quarantine. While I was desperate to make others feel seen, I was actually the one being seen and heard and taken care of. We became a community. The work to put a tour together felt less and less like work and more like a blessing. An opportunity to work towards a quiet, intimate moment that we could all share. And, for an hour, be at ease in the uneasy world in which we live. I am forever grateful, humbled and thankful to my frans (friends/fans) who have consistently eased the burden of touring overseas financially, emotionally and professionally. That's why I am on Kickstarter. At this time, I would like to ease the burden of my frans. And the financial burden of touring on my partner and I. It has been a year of big changes for us and we can no longer do it alone. Nor do I want to rely on the kindness of those who have repeatedly given and given. I want to grow our community and share the opportunity with as many people as we can in order to lighten the load of a few. Asking for and accepting help is a major tenet of my show. In the spirit of following my own advice, I am asking for help. BUT WHAT ARE YOU MAKING? I am recording a live CD of my performance at the Grayston Unity in Halifax, UK during their Festival of Music and Words. I am also filming my travels and other performances so that I can create a 10 minute short film about my experiences. Both the live CD and short will be made available to backers in specific donation tiers. I have been lucky to be invited to their Festival of Music and Words. The Grayston Unity and people of Halifax hold a special place in my heart and in my journey. Owner Michael Ainsworth was kind enough to be the first in the UK to give me a gig in his venue in 2019. Not only that, but he and his patrons have consistently supported, encouraged and helped me gain fans, get more gigs in the UK through their networking and ensured I was fed, housed, looked after and paid. Then, they created a festival and asked me to be a part of it these last couple of years. The Grayston has grown from an 18 seat venue to a capacity of 110. It still feels intimate and warm. Inviting and rich with respectful audiences and some of the very best talent in England. I'm honored to be a part of it. It's the perfect place for me to share my unique blend of songs and storytelling. It's the perfect opportunity for me to finally capture my live performance in a way that properly showcases my ability to draw the crowd into my world. To share the best and worst of myself.  It's in those vulnerable moments that those listening feel less alone. It's in those moments, we all feel seen. Heard WHY DO YOU NEED MONEY? Touring is expensive.  Especially overseas where the US Dollar holds increasingly less value compared to the Pound and the Euro. We decided this year to also open a new market for my music in Ireland. You may not know this, but Ireland is considered one of the most expensive places to travel in Europe.  Choosing to make music my full time job requires a lot of sacrifice financially. Producing a live CD and creating a short film about my travels requires a lot of time, effort, money and people.  No Tour, no CD or film. I've broken the expenses down generally. This is not at all inclusive and does not include taxes, unforseen expenses (i.e. one year the trains were on strike in the UK and we had already purchased tickets and had to find alternate transport), changes in schedule, performance cancelations, illness or acts of God or Zeus. Expenses have been rounded up to account for the USD to the British Pound exchange rate. Trave l: Round trip plane ticket from OKC to Manchester UK- $700 Car rental for Ireland Nov7-19 (does not include petrol)- $1000 Round trip ticket from Manchester, UK to Belfast Ireland- $200 (requires checked bags, guitar expenses, train to destination, etc) Train tickets, cabs in England, Scotland from Oct 17 through November 6- $400 Petrol for transportation given to me by another artist I'm traveling with: $100 These costs do not include my partner"s flight, cab and train transport to join me on the Ireland leg of the tour. TOTAL TRANSPORTATION TO DATE: $2,400.00 Lodging: Manchester Oct 17 & 18- $200 Halifax Oct 19-21-comped London Oct 21 & 22- $250 Hebden Bridge  Oct 23-26 - comped Sheffield Oct 27- comped Oct 28 - Otley -$100 Oct 29- Leeds- $100 Oct 30- York-$100 Oct 31- Skipton- comped Nov 1- Skipton - comped Nov 2-6 Edinburgh- comped Nov 7- 9- Belfast- comped Nov 10-19- Irish coast-comped TOTAL LODGING to date = $850 Food: Meals in UK Oct 17-Nov 6: 2x a day at $7.24 each = $305 (Tesco's meal deal) Meals in Ireland Nov 7-Nov 19: 2x a day at $6.09 each =$160 (Tesco's meal deal) I have not included my partners meals while in Ireland, going to restaurants or buying groceries, spirits, or snacks.  I've only included the cheapest way to eat at the grocery store that provides decent and healthy meal choices for me twice a day. TOTAL MEALS ON TOUR= $465 Live CD Recording, Short Film,  Production and Shipping: Live Recording: I am grateful that the Grayston Unity has offered this service at no charge. I do,     however, believe the engineer deserves to be paid for setting up, soundchecking, mixing, transferring, and tear down. It is a thankless and involved job. I couldn't do it without him. Sound Engineer=$100 CD mixing and mastering: Can't have a good sounding album, especially live, without mixing and mastering.    Cost for an audio engineer: $1500 CD Physical Production: CD with 2 page booklet x 250 copies = $500 Shipping worldwide : Shipment of Physical CDS worldwide =$500 TOTAL CD PRODUCTION AND SHIPPING COST= $2,600 Short Film Production: I'm purposely filming this short documentary on my phone. I like the homemade feel and grittiness. I'll be combining my footage with other video and photos from fans, festival goers and new friends I meet on the road. I'll take you on a journey through the gorgeous English countryside. The gritty cobblestone and cathedrals of Scotland and rolling hills of green in Ireland. I'll bring you backstage before gigs and put you in the audience at my shows. I'll speak to you before and after performances. I'll introduce you to my friends and fans.  I will even share all the joys and difficulties that accompany a long tour in a foreign land. I want you there with me. Camera Condenser Microphone Accessories- $150 Access to the film will be online-$0

Terry Dame & Electric Junkyard Gamelan have been invited to perform at the 16th annual International Recycling Art Festival of Catalonia in Barcelona, Spain this December. We are so excited to have been invited to this amazing multi-disciplinary arts festival, known in Barcelona as DrapArt '11.  This month long festival presents visual arts, music, theater and film all created from or with a focus on recycled objects.  Who could be more committed to the recycled objet than EJG?  We perform on instruments made from other people's cast offs...aka junk that has been transformed into beautiful works of musical art.  So with a bit of your help Electric Junkyard Gamelan will be performing at the festival's opening night celebration on December 16th. It's a long way to Barcelona... This will be our first journey to Europe and that in itself is so EXCITING!  In order to accomplish this we must pack up our arsenal of original musical contraptions and fit them into 8 road boxes to get it all on a plane.  As you probably know airlines now charge for everything...every pound of luggage and every drop of water, I think you even have to pay for your own air sickness bag now!  EJG performs on my original musical instruments created mainly out of found and recycled objects.  Many of these are metal and metal is heavy so as we speak I am redesigning some instruments to be lighter and break down smaller, plotting and scheming on what kind of road cases will make this possible.  It is all part of the preparations and excitement however...it costs a lot of money to fly to Spain. What will your hard earned donations be used for? Your hard earned contributions are greatly needed for airfare to Spain and some new road cases...and maybe some paella for the band. Ah, you still want to see more of what we do?  Check out these links. Live from The Kennedy Center, Washington, DC http://www.kennedy-center.org/explorer/videos/?id=M4744 Excerpts from Joe's Pub, NYC and Time Space Limited, Hudson, NY http://youtu.be/-dr-19JuhII Brooklyn Independent Television feature on EJG http://youtu.be/l_pcR3WJSgI More live from Dixon Place, NYC http://youtu.be/jMZbGpEQDrY

My Dear Elegant Folk ( that's you! ), THANK YOU for stopping by my Kickstarter. Whether you're a friend, a family member, a loyal fan or a complete stranger, I love you for even remotely considering investing in my art. I'll aim to make you proud ... THE PROJECT is simple; I want to record an album -- now . I say now because I am proud to say my wife and I have a baby on the way (and if I don't get to it now , it may be a while before this first-time-daddy gets around to it). I have all the songs written and the people in place to make it happen, now I just need the funds to execute. Cue YOU. THE THEME of this record is contentment. I spent years writing sad songs, then clicked into high reaching motivational mantras. However, this is a record about 
being content with life in the middle. It’s not about being too high, it’s not about being too low -- it’s about being "steady as I go" (the album title!) --  comfortably happy with where I am, in all things. A great place to be. YOUR INVESTMENT can be any amount; high, low or steady as you go. Just know it will help me make and master a 10-song album with producer Cary Samsel (of Samsel and the Skirt ), film a featured music video (with Americonic Films ) and print physical CDs for posterity. YOUR REWARD will vary, depending on the size of your contribution, but think of it as a win-win team effort. You invest now, I go record the album, then we get to enjoy it together. Forever. I CAN'T STRESS THIS ENOUGH: This album cannot and will not happen without you. I have spent years writing these songs but that is the extent of my abilities -- the new music will never see the light of day without the funds to pay the fine recording professionals their humble dues. AGAIN, THANK YOU. Please help spread the word and don't whisper softly the things you want loudly to be. Much love, tyler

As rare a find as an unpeeled butcher cover of (The Beatles) Yesterday and Today."-CMJ Magazine Two Pound Planet started life as Urban Edge with Tom Shaw (Guitar and Vocals) and Jerry Chapman (Bass and Vocals) around 1982. By 1984 Jerry Finley had joined the band (Drums and Vocals).  Finally, by 1986 18 year old keyboardist, Jason Buss joined the band, which recorded its first full-length album titled Bottom of the Stairs. Bottom of the Stairs put the band on the club circuit for the first time. The band started to open for national acts, such as The Romantics and Guadalcanal Diary. In early 1988, Urban Edge caught the attention of producer Mitch Easter (Let's Active, R.E.M.) who agreed to record fresh demos for the group at Mitch’s Drive-In studio in January of 1990. Over the next four months they recorded their finest effort, Songs From the Hydrogen Jukebox.  Released in August of 1990, the album sold well to widespread critical acclaim.  It also charted on college radio, from New York to Portland, OR.  Now called Two Pound Planet, the band showcased for various labels. Alternative Records wound up re-releasing the album with extra material, and updated artwork. A subsequent EP was released- new demos on Whispering Delicious had a new, harder edge with more guitar based songs.  In the spring of 1994, the band rerecorded "Big Bang" and two Shaw penned rockers, "Acid On,"and "Mindblower" at Easter's new studio (Fidelitorium). Now, for the first time fans old and new can listen to the originally released sessions with Mitch Easter, as well as five songs done with Mitch that have not been released commercially. In addition, there is going to be nearly 80 minutes of material that has not been released on cd, or at all from the band’s early days to the end.
