It’s time for a new adventure… The creative journey is a long, winding, and unpredictable road that's led me here, to you. It’s been awhile since the release of “High Heels and Heavy Things”… three years to be exact. The last time I wrote and recorded an album was 2020 - it was a different world, for all of us. Little did I know how heavy things would get. But everything feels a little lighter when you have the best fans and supporters in the world, many of you since Day 1! Whether on Zoom or in person, you’ve listened with your whole heart. You’ve shared the gift of your laughter, you’ve confided in me, and you’ve created a safe, loving harbor all over the world, whether online, at a house concert, or on the road. My community, my music family… You are my magic. I wouldn’t have made it to this point without you. There’s not a day that goes by that I take that support for granted. Are you ready for the next leg of this journey? Yes! Exciting! Where are we heading? This summer, while the rest of the world is swimming and sunbathing, I’ll be heading back into the studio to record my next full-length album with Bobby Holland, a highly respected producer in Nashville (who also happens to be the brains behind some of my favorite records!). This album is the culmination of the most valuable, most soul-baring, most heart-opening moments of my career to date. It showcases my vulnerability like never before, and I cannot wait for you all to hear it. However… However…? What’s stopping you? The highs and lows of my musical journey has schooled me on the harsh realities of the current state of the music industry. Though music (and its surrounding community) brings abundant joy to my life, creating something new and putting a piece of my soul out there once again is incredibly scary. Without a label or any backing, it’s a risk. But with your continued support, I’m ready to take the leap. How can we help? Well… I’ve got top rate musicians that are going to make this album sound absolutely magical. I’ve got inspiration, I’ve got the songs, and I’ve got the courage to share my truth. I’ve got a solid home base and a beautiful community that supports me every step of the way. The only piece I’m missing  - the finances. I need some help raising funds to make this happen. Friends, strangers, and loved ones… making a record is expensive. Especially when you’re an independent artist. My incredible producer. My talented musicians. Mixing the album. Marketing and promotion costs…. These crucial elements all add up, and fast. I need to raise $30,000 to bring this vision to life and share it with the world. Your generosity is crucial to this journey to kicking off, sending us into uncharted, exciting new land. In return, I promise you deep, vulnerable, raw music. I promise you some super groovy incentives as well! Exclusive Zoom listening parties, custom songs, house concerts, VIP access at shows, swanky swag, and of course, endless love and hugs from me, forever. Alright, we’re in… let’s make an album! I love you all. I appreciate whatever you are able to donate and I’m continually humbled by the generosity you’ve shown me over the years... I would not be where I am today without you. I cannot wait to share this amazing record with you.

TELL ME A LITTLE ABOUT YOURSELF Hi! I'm Joanie Leeds and I have the best job on the planet! I write and perform kids music with my band, Joanie Leeds & The Nightlights rocking out at schools, music festivals, performing arts centers, museums & rock music venues across the country. I have released 5 CDs and 1 DVD to date and our songs are heard on many radio stations across the country including Sirius/XM Kids Place Live. Our CDs have been giving top honors and put on various "Best Kids CD" Lists - People Magazine, Parents Magazine, Washington Post, NY Post, Time Out NY Kids... My last Kickstarter funded CD, 'Bandwagon', was chosen as People Magazine's TOP KIDS CD of 2013 WHY AM I PRODUCING THROUGH KICKSTARTER...AGAIN!? Our last 
album, 'Bandwagon' was Kickstarter Funded and once released...it was 
chosen as People Magazines Best Kids CD for 2013 and won The USA 
Songwriting Contest! The album was a huge success because of you!  With 
the funds raised, we were able to pay for most of the costs for the 
album. I was also able to release 14 music videos (I would have never 
been able to do that completely out of pocket) Looking back at the 
process, I realize that it is the BEST way to get our fans involved, 
spread the word about our CD and get everyone excited about the project 
BEFORE it is released. We want to involve fans in what we are doing 
every step along the way and offer personalized products and experiences
 to everyone, no matter how far away you live. For 'Good Egg', we’ve
 started with a minimum goal of $20,000 and we’ve come up with a pretty 
sweet rewards for anyone who pledges within the 30 day campaign. For those of you who aren’t
 familiar with how Kickstarter works, if we don’t reach our goal of 
$20,000, we don’t get ANY of the money (you won't get any of your prizes
 and your credit card is refunded)- That would be so sad for everyone. 
Let's NOT let that happen. Every dollar counts and spreading the word is
 crucial. We can do it! HOW IS THIS ALBUM DIFFERENT FROM ALL OTHER ALBUMS? These are with out a doubt THE best songs I have ever written. While the tunes are totally appropriate for little toddlers, the songs will also strike a chord (literally!) with older kids well into their tween years and beyond.  I challenged myself to write deeper creative lyrics, play on words- even some subject matter chosen by the fans. When it was time to go into the studio, it was a no-brainer to again work with GRAMMY WINNING PRODUCER, Dean Jones. BUT this time, different from all other albums previously recorded, I brought my entire band- THE NIGHTLIGHTS- into the studio. Yup, all 5 of us stayed in a cozy Bed and Breakfast near New Paltz and for 4 days lived and breathed this album. The musicians you see at our live shows are the same ones playing on the tracks. HOLY MOLY, they sound GOOD. WHAT ARE THE SONGS ABOUT? 'Good Egg' is an album that dances around many subjects and genres. I'm never one to stay stuck in one genre because I know kids love em all- (Rock, Pop, Funk, Soul, Ska, GoGo, Gospel...) and frankly, so do I! 'Good Egg' contains songs about Brooklyn hipsters, trips to the doctor, dressing up in costumes, dinosaurs, stories from our travels, staying away from germs (EW!), Drummer Dan (YAY!), the Dads in our lives (fan request) and many other subjects your kids of all ages will relate to. And did I mention we have some GUEST VOCALISTS? The one and only- Mista Cookie Jar is rapping on 'Drummer Dan' and Jacob Stein from The Grammy Nominated band, The Pop Ups is singing a duet with me on 'The World is Your Oyster'. $20,000 IS A LOT OF MONEY. WHERE DOES IT ALL GO? SHOES? No, not shoes. Sadly, $20,000 will not cover all of the costs to make 'Good Egg' but it will cover the majority of the expenses. When an independent artist makes an album, it takes about a year to get it right (and 30K). Here is where all the money goes: Recording, Musicians, Mastering, CD Duplication, Publicity, Photography, Marketing, Graphic Design and Art, Merchandise (T-Shirts, shaky eggs, stickers...), Press Materials, Website Updates & Music Videos & Kickstarter Fees. We do this all on our own. Our stretch goal is $30,000 . If there are any funds above $20,000, they will go directly into the rest of the Good Egg costs and our next DVD 'Joanie Leeds & The Nightlights, Live at Symphony Space' AND more music videos. This is in the works for 2015! OK, SOUNDS GOOD BUT WHAT DO I GET? LOOK TO YOUR RIGHT--------------------------------------------------------------> See all those amazing NEW prizes? These are the many rewards you will get if you help fund our album during the 30 day campaign. From handwritten lyrics to music lessons, private performances or VIP tickets to a NYC concert...there is something for everyone at every price level. *** There are some limited prizes and early bird discounts so jump on it early! ONCE I FUND 'GOOD EGG', THEN WHAT HAPPENS? Each week, once you have contributed, you will automatically be put on the list to receive my Weekly Video Journal where you can see all the behind the scenes footage that goes into producing the album. You'll get an early glimpse of the new songs, you'll step into Dean's studio and I'll even show you how the artwork is coming along. You'll really will be with me every step of the way! Oh, and the earlier you support the album, the more videos you will get in real time. WHEN WILL THE ALBUM BE FINISHED? Our album will be released to the public on June 24th 2014. The CD Release concert will be at Madison Square Kids in NYC at 10:30 AM- but every one of our Kickstarter supporters will receive an advance copy of the music a month before the actual release date, in May 2014. FINAL WORDS? I couldn't even begin to do any of this without you, the amazing fans. You support me in ways I can't even begin to explain. Thank YOU from the bottom of my heart! You are ALL Good Eggs! Dear friends, Thank you so much for visiting this page and reading my stories. My name is Mina Cho, a jazz pianist/composer and the director of IGJI (International Gugak Jazz Institute). I hope that you had a marvelous summer filled with many memorable events and enjoyable moments with your loved ones.  And, I am very excited to share with you a very meaningful project about music, culture, and spirituality and some relevant events this fall. Reflecting my musical journey that had been centered around the subjects of spirituality and its cross-cultural transformation, last year I have founded a non-profit organization called International Gugak Jazz Institute (IGJI) to create a healthy cosmopolitan culture in tandem with Korean traditional music (Gugak), jazz, and various other musical traditions around the world. One of the first achievements of IGJI will be introducing the global audience in Boston to a new cross-cultural musical platform––particularly, through my recent composition Pansori Cantata Gilryungjeon (“The Story of Gilryung”). In this piece, a Western Cantata and a jazz orchestra meet the Korean musical storytelling genre Pansori, wherein the unfulfilled life of a blind Pansori singer named Gilryung is intersected with the death of Christ in her dream. Gilryung’s story about transforming pain into passion resembles our lives in various ways and will speak to our hearts on a profound level. Between September 30, 2019 and October 9, 2019, IGJI will be participating in the “Faith in a City” project that the Somerville Museum presents and hosting various musical events to perform Gilryungjeon. Three Gugak musicians from Korea and one from Boston will be collaborating with various local artists and a jazz orchestra for these events. Accordingly, IGJI is raising $10,000 to cover some of the project expenses (out of the entire expense $17,600); warmly accommodate the participating artists; and proceed with the project. With your financial support and help, the mission of IGJI will be achieved very meaningfully. Your donation will go toward IGJI Pansori Cantata Project. Please note that we are a tax-exempt organization and your donation qualifies as a tax deduction should you care to take it. IGJI의 대표 앙상블 국악재즈소사이어티 멤버들의 본 보스턴 연주 일정과 보스턴 지역 연주자들과의 협연, 국악과 재즈의 창의적인 협력을 위해 만들어질 프로모션 비디오와 초연 앨범 제작 비용 등을 마련하기 위해 이 곳에 펀드레이징 페이지를 열게 되었으니 여러분들의 많은 관심과 사랑 부탁드립니다. 감사합니다! 2019년 9월 6일 보스턴에서 조미나 올림

I love to worship. Music has been a part of my life since I was five years old. I played keyboard at a couple of different conferences when I was eleven, and a couple of years later, songwriting and worship became a huge part of my life. I've had the amazing opportunity to not only lead worship in many places all over the USA, but also, to lead in Israel, Australia and South Africa. All these experiences have been amazing, and have grown my passion for worshipping Jesus. God has used all these experiences to prepare me for this new season that I am about to enter into, and I'm so excited to be able to record an album with the songs He's given me! It's been a dream I've had for a long time to record my own songs, not for fame, but so that I can be a part of bringing people into the presence of God that I enjoy so much when I worship Him. I was so excited when I was approached to do this project and knew the Lord was opening a door for me to minister through worship in this way.  I am also humbled that God has surrounded me with a gifted team of friends and family that are helping make this dream a reality! This is where I need your help!  I can't do any of this without you, the people and family who have watched me grow up and prayed for me and my family for many years. Some of you have spoken things to me that have pointed me in this direction, encouraged me to keep going, or have even let me worship with you. I am so thankful for you and all that you have poured into me with your love for Jesus! Now I need your help to finally see some of the very things you spoke to me happen. This is where YOU come in because I need YOUR help to fund it!! The success of this album is completely dependent on people partnering with me by pledging financial support. Kickstarter is an all-or-nothing fund raising system, so if we don’t raise our total goal by the end of the 30 day campaign, then we don’t get any of the money pledged and no money is taken from our backers. I would be so humbled if you would join me in this new adventure by pledging whatever God has put on your heart. When you do that, you will be a part of this season of my life and the ministry that comes from it. Every little bit helps, but if you aren't able to give financially, I would be honored and blessed if you would join this project as a prayer supporter for me. Please pray for this project, and for all that God has for me in the days to come. My heart is to simply bless Jesus by following His path for me and bringing people to worship and love Him with all their hearts too. I hope that I will see many of you as I continue to lead worship in various places around the United States and other places in the world, I thank you in advance for being a part of this team as a backer of the project. The incentives that we have for joining are pretty cool too! Thanks for checking out my page and I hope that God really blesses you through this album and that you experience Him in an awesome new way! *Below is an explanation of the costs included in the “total goal”. I know this is a large sum of money, but in the recording process, every penny counts! *

Production, Studio, and Recording Cost - $7000 -Pre-production, engineering, equipment needs, studio fees, musician fees, producer fee, etc... *Post Production Cost - $1500 -Mixing, mastering, album graphic design, and CD duplication *Kickstarter Cost - $1,000 -Kickstarter processing fees, Kickstarter incentive fees, shipping fees **Total Campaign Cost - $9500 **Any extra money raised through this campaign will go straight into the promotion and release of this album. There are many expenses that aren’t listed above that any extra funds will go towards funding.  Here is how we would spend any extra funds! ! TIER 1 - $9500 -Fund the album as planned TIER 2 - $11,500 -This would enable me to add 2 more songs to the album!

We are members of BFAMFAPhD. In 2014, we published Artists Report Back , making headlines with our call for cultural equity in the arts, and enabling us to give a keynote at the National Endowment for the Arts. Artists Report Back (2014) received national attention Since then, we have turned our attention to art school classrooms. For the past five years we have been trying ideas out in our classes and learning what works and what doesn’t — in classes which range widely from Foundations to BFA and MFA thesis classes. We have compiled our favorite activities and assignments in one book, website, and game. With your help, we can publish and distribute our resources from the classroom to the living room. Our workshop for Jeff Kasper's class at Pratt Institute Whether you're a student, teacher, curator, artist, or someone who just wants to learn new ways to collaborate, we're hoping our project provides entry points that you can adapt to your context. Ways of Being open source website with free downloads, and interactive card game, in progress What Makes Ways of Being Different? Our approach connects art to economy. Ways of Being is for anyone who wants to make artworks that reflect the conditions of their own production. For example, our group agreements and social-identity reflection activities bring awareness of positionality and shared intentions into the classroom. "Asset Mapping" - as it is called in community organizing - allows us to incorporate what students already know into our syllabi and to share existing skills. Our "critique menu" allows students to think through a range of structures for feedback that aim to support and facilitate their growth. Images from our workshops over the past five years. We teach deep listening, delegation, and collaboration. We sit together in silence to practice “slowing down” and being present for whatever arises. Last of all, we bring in a politicized professional practice into everyday assignments and activities that goes beyond a focus on the circulation of art projects. Website with free downloads, in progress. Our project is called Ways of Being . It includes an interactive website with freely downloadable content, including PDFs of activities and assignments, a series of videos, and a card game. Videos for our website, in progress, including interviews with Dave McKenzie, Paul Ramirez Jonas, Linda Goode Bryant, Elka Krajewska, João Enxuto, and Erica Love. What Will Your Contribution Support? Ways of Being will be published by Punctum Books, and will go to print this winter in tandem with a series of public programs at Hauser & Wirth Publishers. Why Kickstarter? We are running a Kickstarter campaign so that you can help us: - make our open source website with freely downloadable content - pay our computer engineer - pay our graphic designer - pay our copyeditor - pay our interviewees - refine our website's videos and interviews with contemporary artists. - print books and send them to you - and offer free public workshops in NYC After five years of workshops and classes, trying out our assignments and activities where we teach (at the School of Visual Arts, the University of Hartford, at Cooper Union, and at UMass Amherst), as well as at invited workshops at a lot of places (Malmö Art Academy, the Creative Time Summit, The Maryland Institute College of Art, Cornell University, College Art Association, CUE Foundation, Elizabeth Foundation for the Arts, Spaceworks, and Open Engagement, Teacher’s College), we are ready to share our activities and assignments with you. Test print of our book, in progress. Please pre-order a book now, and consider giving more if you can. This will support the production of our website and videos and all of our freely downloadable content. Thank you! About BFAMFAPhD Caroline Woolard, Susan Jahoda, and Emilio Martinez Poppe Ways of Being is a contribution to BFAMFAPhD made by Susan Jahoda and Caroline Woolard with support from Fellow Emilio Martinez Poppe. Susan Jahoda is a Professor in Studio Arts at the University of Amherst, MA; Emilio Martinez Poppe is the Program Manager at Fourth Arts Block (FABnyc) in New York, NY; Caroline Woolard is an Assistant Professor of Sculpture at The University of Hartford, CT. BFAMFAPhD is a collective that employs visual and performing art, policy reports, and teaching tools to advocate for cultural equity in the United States. The work of the collective is to bring people together to analyze and reimagine relationships of power in the arts. BFAMFAPhD received critical acclaim for Artists Report Back (2014), which was presented as the 50th anniversary keynote at the National Endowment for the Arts and was exhibited at the Brooklyn Museum, the Museum of Art and Design, Gallery 400 in Chicago, Cornell University, and the Cleveland Institute of Art. Their work has been reviewed in The Atlantic, the New York Times, the Washington Post, the New Yorker, Andrew Sullivan’s The Dish, WNYC, and Hyperallergic, and they have been supported by residencies and fellowships at the Queens Museum, Triangle Arts Association, NEWINC, and by Pratt Fine Arts, Project Third, where they are in residence right now. Not contributing directly to Ways of Being are BFAMFAPhD collective members Agnes Szanyi, a Doctoral Student at The New School for Social Research in New York, NY and Vicky Virgin, a Research Associate with the Mayor's Office for Economic Opportunity in New York, NY.

Each time I visit I wonder: What are these flashing lights? What are these twinkles of dreams and fantasy? This dizzy spell that is Las Vegas. The Book: American Vista is a book sculpture made through the eyes of a 20-year-old queer person that aims to counteract the exhausted trope of the road trip belonging to the male gaze. It is an object that is modeled after vintage playboy magazines– just with slightly different imagery. Featuring photographs I took of the American Southwest, collages, and writing by Henry David Thoreau and myself. It features two gatefolds; one in the beginning and one in the end. It also has several “centerfolds” or foldouts starring National Park landscapes collaged with found stripper cards from Vegas.  In addition there are acetate pages that flip back and forth from two different pages. One side it lays on top of a blank sheet of paper and the other on top of an image— in a way of morphing the two contrasting landscapes. By including different textures, papers, foldouts, and flaps I encourage the viewer to flip, fold, and play with the piece as they wish- just be gentle with her. About (The Project): The work juxtaposes my first time out West—10 years old as a little girl, on a family vacation, watching men handing my father stripper cards on the Las Vegas Strip—with my return ten years later as a young, queer adult in 2016. I traveled with a friend to the Southwest of America through Colorado, New Mexico, Arizona, California, Nevada and Utah. I was observing the same landscape from a different lens; without my family and exploring my newly discovered identity as a genderfluid queer individual. The stripper cards reminded me of the billboards on highways- advertising everything from women to national parks and monuments. When I read portions of Walden by David Henry Thoreau along our trip, I stopped on the word "Vista" and realized the way I'd defined it in my head had always been more of a visual- a vanishing point or a light at the end of the tunnel. I asked my friend what it meant to her and then started asking people along the way, friends, and family, what the word meant to them. Oxford defines Vista as: a pleasing view, especially one seen through a long, narrow opening. a mental view of a succession of remembered or anticipated events. mid 17th century: from Italian, literally ‘view’, from visto ‘seen’, past participle of vedere ‘see’, from Latin videre . I noticed how “vista” was used by advertisements to conjure images of escaping your reality—streets, towns, and hotels used the word “vista” to help people escape. I’m now using the word  to help people look at reality. American Vista questions man’s attempts to build up and over Nature, remaking it as a white capitalist dreamscape. Seeking to play with this gaze, I’ve merged my photographs from the road trip with stripper cards that I collected on the Las Vegas Strip. I aim to reassemble these elements and examine the extent of how much man has manufactured: not just advertisements and buildings but words, monuments and land itself. These manufactured parts make up the real American Dream: a waking nightmare of a flawless facade. This is the true American Vista . About Me: I am A.(Annie) Grey, a queer nonbinary multidisciplinary artist based in Brooklyn, NY. My work is community-based and an ongoing conversation involving nature, a sociological exploration of fear and trauma, and questioning societal norms. I encourage people to play with the pieces I create! I have exhibited the original collages at The SVA Gallery in their 2017 Mentor show, The Living Gallery in 2019, ARE YOU STILL WATCHING that I also co-curated, and the Ornithology Jazz Club in November 2023. American Vista will be printed at Conveyor Studio in Clifton, New Jersey. All photographs in the book were taken with Kodak Portra film. If I reach the funding goal, the book will be able to be printed. Due to the complexity of this book sculpture, time, and it being self published, I will need support financially. The books design has been completed, and this kickstarter was created to cover the printing costs only. I aim to sell 100 copies of American Vista in hopes to print more and have a book release party this summer to celebrate its finality after working on it for 8 years. This book has been created by A. Grey, and edited by A.Grey, Ali Di Luccia, and Genevieve Fournier. American Vista is fully self-published and cannot be printed without your help! Specs: Size: 8.5x11 Acetate cover 52 color pages 4 acetate pages 2 gatefolds 12 foldout pages

header image: Covers from four issues of 3LBE magazine with artwork by Rew X. Three-Lobed Burning Eye (3LBE) magazine has published stirring original horror, dark fantasy, and science fiction for 25 years. Last year we grew significantly in our first-ever campaign with your support (thank you!), adding more authors and more fearsome tales. Now we need your help to continue. We absolutely love sharing new stories, and we want to keep doing so until all the stars go dark and only whispers remain. Stories published in 3LBE have gone on to year’s-best anthologies and received many accolades. More tales are still to come! Your backer support is essential to our publication. This funding goal pays for another year of the magazine: it makes sure our authors are paid at pro rates, keeps the stories online for free reading, and covers multiple technical costs. If we achieve stretch goals, we’ll be able to grow even more—release more issues, increase our staff, and bring more writers to the magazine. Speculative fiction is experiencing an incredible resurgence. As it widens in subgenres, deepens in subject and narrative, more and more voices too often unheard are telling stories from countless backgrounds and experiences. We wouldn’t have it any other way! We want to share as many stories as possible that ignite the blood, sing with the crows, and defy the world. Help us continue publishing new stories that terrify, beguile, and astound; along with inspiring cover art and dramatic recordings. We’ve put together some exciting rewards as well! Note: Physical add-on rewards can only be added to physical rewards (not to digital rewards). So we have created a new 3LBE Subscription + T-shirt reward for those who would like both. New Fiction Eugen Bacon, Clay McLeod Chapman, Kristi DeMeester, AI Jiang, Rachael K. Jones, Gwendolyn Kiste, John Langan, Premee Mohamed, Hailey Piper, Keith Rosson, Nisi Shawl, Molly Tanzer, and Wendy N. Wagner. We’ve asked several authors to write new stories for our 2026 releases: Eugen Bacon Clay McLeod Chapman Kristi DeMeester Rachael K. Jones Ai Jiang Gwendolyn Kiste John Langan Premee Mohamed Hailey Piper Keith Rosson Nisi Shawl Molly Tanzer Wendy N. Wagner …with more on the way! How the Funding Will Be Used Our fundraising goal of $10,000 will maintain 3LBE magazine for another year. This goal will pay for: 3 issues x 6 stories = 18 stories, in online and ebook formats (~100,000 words) Audio readings of 2 stories in each issue Print anthology every year (18 stories, 40+ interior new illustrations, new cover art) $.08 per word pro payment to authors $25 honorarium for audio readers 3 months active submission system Keep all the stories up at the website This publishing schedule is new for us. It was made possible by backer support from our first Kickstarter exactly one year ago. So let us howl a tremendous thank you to everyone who helped us bloom in our 25th year. In previous years we published 12 stories per year at much less pay. We don’t want to go back, and would love to do this for another 25 years! Our funding goal will pay for the same as above in 2026, along with some budget for website hosting, software, and backer rewards creation. (It does not include any pay for the editor.) We welcome diverse voices, characters, and points of view; and encourage authors of every race, creed, gender, orientation, and ability. We believe that stories can inspire growth, change perspectives, and are essential components of every culture. We have a strict “no AI” policy regarding stories, audio, artwork, and every creative aspect of our publications. 2026 Stretch Goals $12,500: Add a few stories and move to a quarterly release schedule (4 issues x 5 stories = 20 stories/year), and extend our submission windows. $15,000: Add more stories to each issue (4 issues x 6 stories = 24 stories/year), and stay open for submissions all year. $20,000: Move to an every-other-month release schedule (6 issues x 4 stories = 24 stories/year), raise author pay to $.10 per word, and hire more first readers and audio readers. We have more stretch goals planned, and are excited to go there if you want to see more! There are several expenses that are not a part of our initial goal, and any additional funds will go toward: book design, illustration, printing, shipping, audio editing, ebook formatting, domain registration, website development, software, rewards production, Kickstarter fees and taxes. If any amount remains, the editor is paid last. About 3LBE Magazine Three-Lobed Burning Eye magazine is a digital magazine of speculative fiction, bringing you stories of horror, wonder, and the weird. 3LBE launched in 1999 as one of the first online magazines, and introduced several authors. Its stories have gone on to be featured in year’s-best anthologies, and several reward reading lists. New issues are published thrice yearly in digital format, with a print anthology now every year. About the Editor Andrew S. Fuller is an author whose fiction has appeared in several magazines, anthologies, a few short films, and the collection Constellations of Ruin (2023, Trepidatio Publishing). In 1999, he started Three-Lobed Burning Eye magazine, and has worked with many inspiring writers. He lives in the Pacific Northwest, near several rivers and extinct(?) volcanoes, where he reads from a tall pile of books and talks to crows who have not yet talked back. Fulfillment Rewards and subscriptions will be fulfilled directly by Three-Lobed Burning Eye magazine. Some autographed books may be shipped from author representatives.
