title: Categories
description:
YOU MADE US REACH OUR FUNDING GOAL FOR THE FILMING!
Every additional budget we raise will help us to visualize the interactions of our heart with ourselves, each other and this planet in ways no one has ever seen before! A small group of acclaimed scientists and doctors disprove the century-old idea we all take for granted: that our heart is a pump. Instead, they found that it stops the blood flow, creates vortices and sends more information to the brain than vice versa. Our emotions, broadcast via our hearts throughout our bodies and far beyond, influence our brains capacity to be creative and solve problems. Last but not least it synchronizes us to the ‘heartbeat’ of our beautiful planet earth. The planet that many cultures deem to be a living, intelligent being. Could it be that not with our brilliant brains, even enhanced with artificial intelligence, but rather with our hearts we will find solutions to the heart-breaking health, social and other problems we are facing worldwide? In this film, we explore our human life from before the first heartbeat to beyond the last heartbeat. We weave together studies from cutting-edge science, the wisdom of a Sufi master, an ancient myth about our planet and intimate personal experiences from people of all walks of life, to show you why this mysterious pulsing organ is the key to all we ever longed for. Created by a team of filmmakers who have worked on Oscar nominated and Sundance winning films, Dawn of Hearts is a poetic science documentary that will forever change the way you see your heart, yourself and life. There are only two things that could prevent us from having paradise on this planet. Our belief that more mind and technology is what we need. And our conditioning to ignore most of what we feel, meaning what our heart tells us. THE GROUND-BREAKING STORY Pulsing impressions of life, maybe memories, maybe a life to come ... holding hands, a gentle kiss, giving birth, the first steps in life, tears of heartbreak, looking to the stars, war and destruction, growing old, entering a hospital … impressions dancing to the eternal rhythms of our hearts. We begin our journey where sadly to many of us end up. In a hospital waiting for her heart surgery an elderly woman tells us how she feels. Asking the surgeon about the role our heart has the answer is what we all expected: Our heart is a pump. But as we visit Li, the young Ph.D. student and film his last experiment we become witness of an unbelievable scenery; blood moves without a heart pumping it. He is investigating the effect that infrared light has on the blood flow. Prof. Pollack, Li’s mentor and the world’s leading water expert, has published a ground-breaking book on water that helps explain this mind-boggling observation. He shares the tentative conclusions from their research with us "It is astonishing to think that it's not just your heart that's driving everything but it could be also light energy." A century ago Rudolf Steiner, the founder of Waldorf education, biodynamic agriculture and anthroposophical medicine made a provocative statement. For humanity to evolve three 'things' need to happen. One of them is that we have to realize that our heart is not a pump. His teachers sometimes called him Doubting Thomas because he had such a hard time accepting authority figures. Especially if the answer to “Why?” was “Because someone said so.” Nearly 40 years ago, in his early twenties, Thomas Cowan, a medical doctor from San Francisco stumbled upon Steiner’s work. He did not understand it at first, but today he can explain in easy to understand concepts how our heart works and what it’s real purpose is. As we continue to paint an new portrait of this pulsing mystery in our chest, we visit the renowned Institute of Heartmath. Dr. McCraty has spearheaded cutting-edge studies that were published in peer-reviewed scientific journals. His mind-bending research shows that our hearts energetic field is many times stronger than that of our brains. A mother’s brainwave can synchronize to the baby’s heartbeat. Our heart has it’s own memory and sends more information to our brain than vice versa. Uplifting emotions help our brain to solve problems, be more creative and make good decisions. Via our heart we can access information beyond the confines of time and space and last but not least his studies show that we are truly connected. To each other and this planet, via our hearts. But the scientific approach to investigating reality is just one path. Using your own consciousness to explore the nature of existence is another.
“As the heart begins to awaken, we begin to experience that this whole life is animate. Nothing is random, nothing is purely accidental. Everything is part of our own, if I may say so, individually designed curriculum of the heart.” Kabir Helminksi, a Suf master and Rumi translator who has vast personal experience of the mystical dimensions of the life explains how our heart is the instrument that connects us to an aspect of reality that our minds cannot access. While these subject matter experts paint a picture of our hearts that will redefine how we see and treat our hearts we believe that we are all important puzzle pieces of this breath-taking and challenging mystery called existence. And so we’ll talk to people from all walks of life, a young couple in love, fresh parents, kids in school and a sweet older couple to discover the magic of our hearts. WHY WE NEED TO MAKE THIS MOVIE I share a wish with everyone that I met throughout my life: To live in a world where we all live in a peaceful, healthy environment and have a fulfilled life. But this is far from the world we have today …
Many people today believe that if we invent and use more technologies we’ll soon have the live we all long for. Yet despite ever increasing technification, seemingly more and more people struggle with war, poverty, loneliness, chronic disease, and other heart-breaking situations. We believe that we humans are able to solve all of our current problems and create the world we long for. And based on the research, discoveries, and anecdotal evidence we will show you in this film we suggest that this starts by realizing the true purpose and potential of our hearts. But we might not have much time left.
Some of the most notable thinkers of our times are concerned that artificial intelligence might be our last invention, meaning the end of humanity as we know it. What will happen with us once we start modifying our brains with technology?
Maybe now is our last chance to find the real purpose of our hearts before we are convinced that evolution means becoming AI augmented humachines. LET'S IGNITE OUR FULL POTENTIAL We hope to inspire teachers worldwide to change our educational system, which is nearly exclusively focused on training our minds, to equally facilitate the full potential of their student's hearts. Towards that goal, we offer all high schools around the world in low GDP countries FREE streamings/downloads of this documentary for classroom screenings. If you like our vision and mission, please share it with those people who you think might like it too! Your pledge is tax-deductible. This documentary is a fiscally sponsored project of the The Film Collaborative, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. In accordance with IRS regulations, The Film Collaborative issues tax receipts for all donations of $250 and over. Kickstarter will take their fee and the credit card processing fee out before The Film Collaborative receives the funds. The Film Collaborative issues tax receipts on the amount they receive. YOU ARE INVITED This is the story of our hearts and without your input we are missing an important puzzle piece. If you like our vision we invite you to sign up as a VIP (Very Inspiring Person) crew member, even if you don't financially support this campaign. You will have access to confidential behind the scenes material. And you can be involved in the decision making process for many elements of this film! Here are some very early drafts for potential movie posters. Which one is your favorite so far? Want to submit your idea for a poster? We'd love to see it! Become a crew member and you can upload it. ABOUT THE DIRECTOR At age 13 I was determined to become a theoretical physicist. Back then it seemed to be the only way I could discover and understand the deepest mysteries of life. Then I realized that in order to understand life completely I would have to study it all. From math and music to physics to psychology and everything else. It was hopeless to ever discover the secrets of life. Feeling disconnected from life and depressed I fell into a deep darkness. It took me many years to find my path to not just cope with all the unpleasant emotions, but to end up living my life like I would have never dreamt before. No, life is not easy and without pain, but most of the time it is an incredible magic gift that I can experience, primarily through my heart. This is my debut as a filmmaker. Throughout diverse careers, I gained creative skills (12+ years as a sound designer in the German movie industry), solid analytical (passed German naturopath exam) and business skills (3 years as a product manager with $1+ Mio./yr revenue responsibility). And I enjoy learning and improving my work with the feedback of others. Over the last 23 years, I looked into various paradigms, always seeking a deeper understanding and experience of life. Since 14+ years my specific interest is emotion and the heart. In March 2017 I co-created the online event 'The Heart Revolution' presenting best-selling and award-winning speakers about the various aspects of our hearts. Let’s have a Skype call if you have questions or want to get to know me before you support the production of this movie! Or if you want to get involved beyond this crowdfunding campaign. CAN WE PRODUCE THIS MOVIE? The team of Dawn of Hearts consists of professionals that have been involved in the production of Oscar nominated and Sundance winning films. Additionally we are excited to being supported by Sundance's Creative Distribution Initiative and honored to be featured on their curated Kickstarter page. We are well prepared to finish filming until March and start the post-production in April to deliver your Kickstarter special edition by May/June 2018. With your support the world will see this movie in Summer 2018 and discover the truth about their hearts. MEET THE FILMMAKERS Benedikt Just - Writer & Director - has worked as a sound designer and supervisor in the German movie industry for over 12 years. During that time he worked on award-winning documentaries and films as well as one major Hollywood production. For the movie Underworld, he was part of the team that got a Golden Reel Award nomination for best sound design. This is his debut as a writer/director. Daniel B. Gold - Director of Photography - Has over 20 years of cinematography experience in feature documentaries including working with Oscar winning directors. His movies Blue Vinyl and Everything's Cool, where he directed and filmed were both nominated for the Sundance Grand Jury Prize.  He won the Sundance Cinematography Award in 2002 for Blue Vinyl. Passionate about the art of film he also taught classes in digital cinematography and visual literacy. In July 2017 Netflix is showing the 4 part documentary 'Daughters of Destiny', where he was DP and consulting producer. Alexandra Wedenig - Editor - Alexandra is an award winning filmmaker and professional editor with more than a decade of experience. Her feature film ‘Reflection of Maya Rose’ is has won awards at festivals on multiple continents. Her background involves studying directing at the Vienna film school and she runs her own production company where she writes, directs, produces and edits short films that are screened at festivals around the world. Tobias N. Siebert - Producer - Tobias studied Production and Media Economics at the Munich State Academy for Film and Television from which he graduated with honors. Tobias is partner of BASIS BERLIN Postproduktion and CEO & Producer of BASIS BERLIN Filmproduktion. For THE STORY OF THE WEEPING CAMEL, he received an Oscar Nomination for Best Documentary. The film sold in more than 60 countries. Tobias N. Siebert is a member of the German documentary alliance, the German Producers Association and a Documentary Campus and EAVE Alumni. Thank You & Acknowledgements We want to thank many people who made this campaign possible. So, in alphabetical order: David M. Boussou for his wonderful creative commons footage Jay Carpio for his wonderful creative commons footage Liz Manashil, our mentor from the Sundance Creative Distribution Initiative Lynnette Gryseels, our mentor from The Film Collaborative Nadine & Daniel, for your constant emotional support Rufus Blackwell , for his generous offer to use his awesome mirror worlds footage Tim Williams for his wonderful creative commons footage Unsplash for their wonderful platform with the best photographers worldwide

title: MADE CLEAN MOVIE
description: MADE CLEAN — A Film That’s More Than a Movie This isn’t just a movie. It’s a movement. MADE CLEAN is a powerful, redemptive story about a mother and maid who spends her life cleaning up everyone else’s messes, while secretly fighting to keep herself clean from addiction and hold on to her teenage daughter. It’s raw, real, and ultimately filled with hope, healing, and redemption . As Psalm 51 says: “Create in me a clean heart, O God…” That’s the heart of this film. We believe in shining light in dark places, because real change starts when truth and grace collide. Why We’re Making This Film MADE CLEAN is the first feature film from Cinema Country , a new kind of movie studio with a mission: to make films that fuel impact. Each Cinema Country production is built not only to entertain, but also to support real causes like addiction, obesity, poverty, mental illnesses, etc. that bring hope and resources to families and communities in need. This story is for anyone who’s ever fallen and fought to get back up again. It’s for the mothers, the daughters, and the families who believe that redemption is still possible. But making films for impact isn't where Cinema Country stops. It's a new kind of model altogether, a methodology that involves soft-equity partnerships in creating a share program that allows us to film movies on a $3 million dollar scale , yet only costing us under $250k in hard equity. Providing a new route where everyone involved in the production of the film shares in the revenue of the movie! We're out to make a splash in the film industry and lead by example in wise financial acumen and operations. The days of unethical, wasteful and greedy business is over...at least for us and our house. How You Can Help We’ve already raised $100,000 , and we’re now reaching out to you, our community of believers, dreamers, and doers. Please help us raise the minimum $88,000 to move this film into production. Your support will go directly toward bringing this story to life and helping to launch Cinema Country, from filming and post-production to outreach materials that connect audiences with practical recovery and family support resources. Be Part of the Movement When you back MADE CLEAN , you’re not just funding a film, you’re joining a movement that brings hope where it’s needed most. Together, we can help others find their way back to light, love, and a clean heart. Join us. Share the story. Be the spark that helps make MADE CLEAN a reality. Whether you are able to pledge or not, our biggest tool in the shed is public support, so PLEASE SHARE with anyone who might be interested in helping! Click the link to dive deeper into the story of Made Clean. https://drive.google.com/file/d/15b0wZqFd54g_C2uiLeQkRQvXmuXBxdcc/view?usp=drive_link

title: Categories
description: Origin story. As a filmmaker and content producer with over 20 years of experience in the tv and film industries, I wanted to create something personal and unique in a style that's wholly specific to me. Fandom is sharing your love through the lens of the self and what better way to share my love (and gratitude) for comic shops, than to create a film designed to re-ignite people's love with a comic shop's potential. My very first comic shop was called Quest Comics. I found it in the phone book and though there were a few options near Morrow, GA, it was the closest to my grandparents house. It was off of Lake Harbin Road and less than a 10-minute drive. When I walked in for the first time it was a little over-whelming. Wall to wall comic books and geeky sundries were what I found walking through the door. More than that, I discovered a community that welcomed the happily-literate and overly Star Trek obsessed young geek where, unlike the new rural town my parents had moved me to, I wasn't afraid to be who I was. The spectacular thing about comic shops is that they still do that for people of all ages. They're a truly unique and important part of our communities which this film celebrates from start to finish. Shopping for Superman, was developed for the uninitiated viewer whose impression of the modern comic book shop might still be mired in inaccurate preconceptions which is one of the first and most exciting challenges of telling this story; re-shaping the broader narrative. With a deliberate attempt to avoid the common tropes and trappings of similarly-themed films we have used the guiding light of elevating nerdy materials above the sophomoric in what has commonly been called "geek-chic". There's plenty of nerdy good fun to be had but plenty of amazing art and storytelling too. 10 years. 30 comic shops. 50 interviews. Pre-production began in late 2014 in Los Angeles, CA with local shop visits and pre-interviews in person and over the phone. Everything was self-funded with nothing more than a Sony DSLR, a wireless lavalier microphone, and a not-so-great LED lighting panel I picked up on Amazon. Since that time, some truly amazing people stepped up and sat down with me to share their first-hand experience as retail shop owners, comics industry professionals, and big nerds in the entertainment industry (Todd Stashwick, we're looking at you). Our previous GoFundMe campaign raised over $26,000 which helped to cover the costs of travel, hiring, and equipment needs, including production, storage, and editing needs. It was a shoestring budget but having a 9 to 5 helped pick up a lot of the slack over the years. A long road. Moving four times, dealing with a pandemic, freelancing and loss in the family really slowed things down and left me wondering if we would ever make this happen. Hard work and the support of an incredible team kept this film on track and now we're... Almost there. With principal photography wrapped and over an hour of the film already edited, it's time to bring in skilled artists and craftspeople to do some heavy lifting, and that means raising money to pay them what they're worth. Motion Graphics - Including credits, lower thirds, and visual elements that keep this thing looking gorgeous. Color Correction - Tashi Trieu (our colorist) literally wrote the book on digital color correction. So it's going to look great. Original Score - We're talking music that's tailor-made for this film with an artist who has the chops. Antonio Teoli is one of the best in the business and we couldn't be happier to have him on board. Sound Design/Mixing - Sound is just as important as picture and we want ensure you have the best experience with both! Lamps Lampadinha has an ear for these things and loves making sound nice and crisp. Festival Submissions - Our goal is to put this in front of film lovers and sales reps to get it seen by the largest audience we can. Early-bird deadlines are rapidly approaching and your dollars will help us get Shopping for Superman seen and screened in the 2025 festival circuit! Legal Funds - We're dealing with a lot of intellectual properties and want to make sure that everyone's work is protected and that we're doing this by the book. Marketing/Promotional Materials - It's not just enough to make a film, you have to let people know. While we have a small budget, our hope has always been to take advantage of the network of shops that this film supports to spread the word. Digital marketing, posters, stickers, and even postcards are another great way to help generate interest. Subtitling/Translations - Comic shops are for everyone and their story is no exception. We want to help anyone and everyone experience this story and subtitling in English, Spanish, and French is our first step. Comic shops. Serious business. Shopping for Superman , covers the over 50-year history of comic book shops and shows audiences how these stores came to be, why they're important, and the ways they've changed the world. As champions for childhood literacy, safe spaces for marginalized peoples, and badly needed social hubs; comic shops play an important role in our collective culture and deserve our support. Featuring interviews with comics retailers from across the U.S., industry creatives, publishers, and distributors in a never-before-seen feature-length documentary, Shopping for Superman, is a one-of-a-kind experience you'll have to see to believe. A little background. Comic shops may only be a little over 50 years old but in that time, they've helped to completely re-shape the nature and quality of graphic storytelling, push back on the censorship of recursive culture wars, and create life-changing communities that give life lines to marginalized identities and combine the tools for self-discovery and understanding with art and literature. Comic shops are one of the few ways we show new generations what we value. They act as a societal mirror with the stories they make available and the characters they present but also the proof that we value reading and the ideals these shops promote within their walls. Doing good and striving to be better. These stores are run by real people selling physical products made by actual artists and craftspeople amid a digital age where genuine face-to-face human interaction is on the decline. The goal of telling their story is to reignite an interest in what they offer and to ensure we keep them a part of our communities for a long time to come. Who am I? I'm, Wes Eastin , an award-winning director, editor, and producer of digital video, television, and film production who has received grants and support from groups such as Creative Capital of New York, Flux Projects, and the InLight Richmond Festival. Several art exhibits I’ve collaborated on have been featured in such places as the Public Art Review Magazine, the Huffington Post, the Atlanta Journal-Constitution, and Art Papers Magazine . These works are held in the permanent collections of the Museum of Contemporary Art of Georgia and the Columbus Museum of American Art . In addition to the work I’ve done in television and film, my own works have been featured on: The Independent Film Channel (IFC), CBS Sports , and Funny or Die. What's in store for you. Get it? Well, we get it. Not everyone wants a warm handshake and a little thanks for their hard-earned scratch. We have you covered. Digital Copy of the Film - You'll receive a link to view the film online. It'll never expire and is yours to enjoy via any web-browser you like! Onscreen Thanks - You get your name in the credits as one of the people who helped make this film possible. We couldn't finish it without you. You've earned it! Physical Copy of the Film - You get a signed Blu-ray of the documentary. We're limiting these to just 100 so be sure you claim yours ASAP! Deluxe Pack - You'll receive a physical copy of the film, a full-size poster, and a sticker pack. Executive Producer - Want to be a Hollywood big shot? Well, now's your chance! Purchase this reward and you'll be added to the credits, our IMDB page, and poster as our executive producer. It's a win-win! Want to be the only one? Buy all three! Would You Like to Know More? Want to know more about us? Check out our IMDB page to learn a little more about our team and where VIP contributors will be featured for all of time!

title: Star Wars.. The Fallen
description: Hello and thank you for taking the time to review this amazing adventure that I, my crew, and (hopefully) you are about to take. I am creating my 3rd and FINAL Star Wars short film, in hopes of attracting Disney Studios to hire me to create a Live action TV series. Before you laugh, look at the production value of my first 2 films and I hope that you will agree that it could happen... People win the lottery everyday, so why not us? Having funded my first 2 films myself at a price tag of about 15 thousand per short, you'll see exactly why I need your help this time. So please watch the video and you will see and hear everything you need to know about the subject matter and nature of our film. From the bottom of my heart, I thank you for taking the time to review this and if you decide to donate, I want to give you a HUGE thank you for helping us achieve this goal. God Bless and, "May the Force be with you .... Always..."

title: Hardy's Home Improvements
description: LOGLINE: An ex-Marine starts working at an unassuming hardware store that turns out to be a Pandora’s box of inexplicable phenomena and unforgettable coworkers, including a jaded actress, a tough-as-nails lesbian, and their recently divorced boss who spend their days fighting (or giving into) the gravitational pull of Hardy’s Home Improvements. WRITER'S STATEMENT: I used to work as an electrical associate at a big box hardware store in Burbank, California.  After work, I would come home and do voice practice and film self-tape auditions.  I began screenwriting because I was frustrated with the quality of the scripts I had been seeing in my auditions.  I wrote about my bizarre experiences at work and the memorable people I worked with: a variety of characters who were larger-than-life.  Real people with real problems who were puppeteered by the invisible hand of a corporation.  The sphere of Hardy's is synonymous with the illusion of the "American Dream".  When one gets sucked too deep into Hardy's, one's hope for a better life swiftly disintegrates, and degrades exponentially the longer one stays there. For some of the employees, Hardy's is the best option for them.  For others, it is a temporary job on the way to something better.  I gained perspective during my time at the hardware store.  I learned how blessed I was with the privileges I had.  I was touched and inspired by the people I met there, the majority of whom were endlessly supportive in making sure I got out and followed my dreams.  To me, these folks--not the "influencers" or Hollywood stars--are the heart of Los Angeles and their voices should be amplified in the socially influential Hollywood industry.  Needless to say, this is more important now than ever. Our series opens just as newbie Francisco Vasquez, our ex-Marine, arrives to pull Monica Lopez, the jaded actress, out of the quicksand and remind her there is more to life than the electrical aisle. Francisco is the perfect foil to his new electrical coworker Monica.  They clash immediately upon meeting, but they'll soon realize they’ve got a lot to learn from one another.  We are excited for you to see the way their friendship buds once this story is fleshed out into a series.  For this proof of concept, we'll follow Francisco’s first day at Hardy’s: a combination of common workplace initiations and frustrating customer interactions that culminate in an outrageous hazing ritual (that we unfortunately don't yet have the budget for). Francisco is thrown into the shark tank to learn the ways of Hardy’s, an unusually challenging workplace to navigate on account of its Magical Realist proclivities (which you'll see more of in the series). He is whipped into shape by Monica and the Assistant Store Manager and Head of Electrical Jaime Favela. Jaime in turn takes out his mid-life frustrations on Cameo (Cuh-MAY-oh) Gomez, the tough, lesbian Tools Manager, who challenges Jaime's machismo principles and serves as the de facto Theft Prevention manager. Our color palette will evoke the feel of summer in the San Fernando Valley—warm neutrals evocative of the "Timberland brown" California hills paired with pops of bright, saturated color through our set design.  This all changes in Magical Realist moments as the fluorescent tube lights of the store glow bluer and spookier.  As Jaime would describe it, a transition from a warm, sunny 2700 Kelvin to a cold, blue 5000 Kelvin. The tone will be a blend of workplace comedy satirizing the corporate environment with moments of Magical Realism.  We want to bring the heartfelt truth that comes out of satirical comedy, like Community and The Office UK . Though our story is set in the 2020s, our style will nod to the era of 90s LA. We want to call to mind LA Chicano and cholo culture part of our ode to Latinidad. The different characters represent different aspects of Latinidad and the complexities of our culture. Francisco, the child of immigrants, represents a newer generation of Latinos who strive to make a better life for themselves upon the sacrifices their parents and ancestors made. Monica marks a liminal space as a mixed-ethnicity Latina, at odds with her identity. She is unsure of where she fits in and grows to understand the privilege that comes with her whiteness, yet she yearns for belonging. Cameo represents the style and strength of cholo culture, an act of resilience against the white cultural mainstream. Jaime represents a generation of immigrant Latinos with an older world view and the work ethic that the “American Dream” demands. CHRISTINA HÉLÈNE BRAA ~ [writer/producer/actor] is a performer and screenwriter who has spent the last several years writing while touring as a musical theater actor. Her onscreen work has been featured in the LA Shorts Festival , Tribeca , and CIFF .  She was in the original cast of the Broadway National Tour of Peter Pan from 2023-2024 and in 2025 played Miss Argentina in the cast of Beetlejuice aboard the Norwegian Viva cruise ship.  She began developing Hardy’s Home Improvements in 2021 while working as an electrical associate at a big box hardware store in Burbank, CA.  She will be playing Monica Lopez. RORY KRAMER ~[director] Decorated writer, director, and producer, with an emphasis on comedic directing. Past projects include Reddit, TIDE, BMW, Atlantic, Republic, FUJIFILM, Amazon Prime, Youtube Premium, etc . Their narrative work has also received high honors at various film festivals, most recently Raindance 2025 , Just For Laughs 2025 (Grand Jury Winner) , and has won an Emmy for Best Scripted Web-Series . Tonally, their work can be described as absurd with a sincere center. Most recently, their global campaign for FUJIFILM won the American Advertising Award for Best Series in 2023 . They are currently a participant in the Disney 2024 Directing program & the NBC Launch 2024-26 Directing Program , with seven episodes of television under their belt at 28. On the development side, they’re currently taking out a show with the Wolper Organization and recently entered a Deal with Will Arnett’s Electric Avenue . JESSE POSEY~ [actor] Jesse is an actor known for his portrayal of Chris Perez in Netflix's Selena: The Series as well as his performances in NCIS , Station 19 , Intermedium , and Screamboat . He will be playing the role of Francisco Vasquez. MORGAN RUTTER~ [producer] Morgan is a queer award winning filmmaker based in New York and Los Angeles. They graduated from the University of Southern California with a BFA in Acting. With experience as an Associate Producer on Emmy nominated TV shows like LET'S MAKE A DEAL (CBS) and PASSWORD (NBC) , she has a solid background in fast paced television production. They have produced multiple narrative short films, including the award winning DION & DENVER, ASHES ASHES WE ALL FALL DOWN , and the upcoming short film MATCHES . She associate produced her first feature film, D(E)AD in 2024. As an actor, she is a member of the Actors' Equity Association and a short film she starred in, THE COST OF CONSIDERATION (ADJUSTED FOR INFLATION ), was nominated for Best Short Film at the Atlanta Shortsfest 2025 . They are currently in post-production on their directorial debut, AS SHE SLEEPS , a psychological thriller about the terrors lurking in one's own mind. Most of our budget will go towards paying the cast and the crew equitable day rates. We aim to make our production rewarding for everyone involved.  The rest of our budget will be focused toward the location cost (which can get pricey due to insurance costs and compensation for store employees), equipment rentals, and post-production fees so that we can create a beautiful final product. Even if you cannot make a financial donation, we would greatly appreciate other forms of support: Emailing this page to friends Facilitating introductions to potential funders or collaborators Offering in-kind donations — i.e., food, costumes, etc. We will be providing further updates on this page and on our Hardy's social media page, @hardyshomeimprovements on Insta.  Give us a follow if you haven't already!

title: DEMON TWINK - Help Finish A Queer Horror Short Film
description: Help Bring a Queer Horror Film to Life! Demon Twink is a queer horror short film inspired by the idolization of youth, beauty, and sex in the queer male community. We’ve completed principal photography, and now we’re reaching out to the queer & horror communities to help us complete post-production so that this vision can come to screens everywhere. With your support, we’ll finalize the editing, color grading, VFX, sound and score, to create a uniquely terrifying yet mesmerizing cinematic experience. THE STORY When a depressed queer man named Dante is offered eternal youth via a mysterious glory hole, his wish is granted - but with a deadly price. Dante must kill every full moon in order to maintain his youth. But after 30 years of meaningless sex and violence, one unsuspecting hookup leads to a chance at a deeper connection. Will Dante decide to sever this new connection and stay young? Or, will he take a chance at true love and change back to his true form? THE IMPORTANCE Demon Twink explores a taboo subject within the queer male community not often dissected. We put sex, beauty, and youth up on a pedestal while ignoring our yearnings for deeper connection. This film's goal is to spark new conversation and show viewers that being vulnerable with ourselves and others is the key to combat feeling incomplete. Rarely do we see queer characters existing in a purely queer world, especially in horror. In this setting, we're able to explore more nuanced issues within the queer community and bring light to what has been left in darkness for so long. But— this film was not just made for queer people. With social media now so engrained into society, many of us have fell victim to constantly comparing ourselves to others and preferring to live in a fantasy world rather than facing our own realities. THE STYLE Demon Twink is inspired by the surrealist, haunting tone of David Lynch films mixed with the artistic, colorful violence from Dario Argento films. The director, Steven George, worked hard to establish a unique aesthetic and create images that would stay imprinted in your mind even after the film ends. THE IMPACT Despite its genre of horror, Demon Twink strives to leave its audience with a positive message to walk away with and make viewers feel like they are not alone in their struggles. The sometimes graphic images shown in the film are not without purpose, they show us that working through our pain can yield beautiful results and help heal the world around you. THE FUTURE This short film serves as a proof of concept for a queer horror feature which deals with similar themes, moral quandaries, visual style, and tone. Steven George, the writer/director behind Demon Twink , has crafted a narrative that taps into even more areas of fear queer people face, while still intertwining deeper meaning and a positive message underneath. We believe that now is the time for queer horror to prosper as an established genre. With your help, through the success of this project, we can open the door for generations of new queer filmmakers to make their mark and uncover more of our community's intricacies through the mode of horror filmmaking. Why We Need Your Support The film has come a long way — from script to shoot — but bringing this story to life means raising more funds, especially for post-production. The funds we raise will go toward: VFX : While the raw footage from the shoot is striking, we need visual effects artists to seamlessly blend the practical effects into the world of the film. Sound Design & Mixing : Sound is integral to filmmaking, particularly in the horror genre. In order to create the right emotional tone, we need to record foley, mix the existing sound, and add original scoring and composition. Color Grading : The surreal and stylized atmosphere of this film requires expert color grading to have the desired impact on audiences. Film Festival Fees : Bringing a film to screens around the world costs a lot of money! Submission fees, travel expenses, and marketing costs are all necessities for bringing this film to a cinema near you. We are looking to raise $15,000 for these post-production necessities, and with your help, we’ll be able to add that final layer of polish, creating a short film that’s ready for festivals and beyond! Rewards for Backers We want to make it worth your while! Here are some rewards that backers can look forward to: $25 - "Contributor": Receive a social media shout out from the official Demon Twink Instagram. $50 - "Fan": Receive immediate access to Behind The Scenes content and Digital Assets - and feel like you're part of the action. $150 - "Friend": Receive an original Demon Twink poster signed by writer/director Steven George and Producer Chris Stanley after we reach our funding goal, plus receive immediate access to a digital copy of the script. $500 - "Supporter": Participate in a LIVE virtual meet-and-greet one-on-one with writer/director Steven George and Producer Chris Stanley (@StanChris)! $2,500 - "Associate Producer": Receive an Associate Producer credit, plus all previous rewards! $5,000 - "Executive Producer": Receive an Executive Producer credit, plus all previous rewards. MEET THE TEAM We’re a dedicated team of queer horror fans and filmmakers, each bringing years of experience and love for the genre. STEVEN GEORGE (he/him) - WRITER / DIRECTOR Steven George is a queer screenwriter and director based in Los Angeles, originally adopted from Russia. He began his career producing music videos, digital content, and virtual reality short horror films, which gained millions of views on social media. From there he found success in reality television, climbing the ladder and working as an associate producer. Steven’s main goal in the entertainment industry is to write and direct queer horror films and popularize the genre. With his work, Steven hopes to create a fantastical sense of reality that mystifies and enlightens his audiences while striving to give proper visibility to characters of all backgrounds, beliefs, and identities. His previous films can be viewed on his YouTube Channel called Rainbow Moon . CHRIS STANLEY (he/him) - PRODUCER Chris Stanley, (known as StanChris) is a YouTuber, writer, producer, and social media influencer famous for his engaging LGBTQ+ content. Born on April 18, 2000, in the U.S., he has gained a substantial following on TikTok and Instagram, where he shares comedic and body-positive videos. StanChris started his social media journey in 2017, focusing on LGBTQ+ themes and has over 850,000 TikTok followers. A passionate longboarder, he advocates for the LGBTQ+ community and emphasizes confidence and body positivity. His debut short film “ Second Thought ” is currently in the festival circuit and has already won 1 award. JAMES CANELLOS (he/him) - PRODUCER James Canellos is a Freelance Producer and Production Coordinator who really hopes those reading this know how grateful he is that you're taking the time to consider our story. This Queens, NY native started his filmmaking education at Frank Sinatra School of the Arts and has continued producing in NYC and LA since. His produced Shorts featured in the Bushwick Film Festival, Cinequest, 30 Under 30 Film Festival, Netflix funded programs, Cannes Short Film Corner and the Tribeca Film Festival. Along with producing he’s Production Coordinated for clients such as Mercedes BMW, Musicians, Mizuno, Food Network, A&E, Netflix & MrBeast. James has produced (3) narrative features and is excited to have a queer horror short under his belt. LIVIAN SAPHIR (she/her) - PRODUCER / CASTING DIRECTOR / STORY EDITOR Livian Saphir is a transfeminine producer with a lifetime love of horror and a talent for amplifying creative voices, finding untapped depths in works of true passion. Originally coming from the music industry where she worked as an audio engineer with Grammy Award-winning songwriters, Livian later rediscovered her childhood passion for film and prides herself on her successful career jump, thanks to her broad skillset of directing, story editing, videography, audio/video editing, and more. Her favorite part of producing is helping shape a project to have a solid through-line of strong central themes that resonate with their creators and audiences. In her spare time, Livian collects VHS tapes and was proud to cast her own CRT television set in the film. OLIVIA CORDELL (she/her) - PRODUCER Olivia Cordell is a queer Asian-American horror filmmaker currently living and working in London, UK. Olivia started her career on TikTok and YouTube, where she built a passionate fan base of over 160,000 followers for her Sweet & Spooky brand. Her family-friendly murder mystery webseries, Dead Best Friend, has accumulated over a million views. She has written and produced a number of short and feature-length horror films, with credits including Waldgeist (Amazon Prime), Self-Isolation (Short), and her directorial debut, FRAME (currently in post-production). She is an alumna of the University of Southern California and the University of Westminster’s MA Business of Film programme in partnership with the BFI. Her goal is to elevate underrepresented voices within the independent film community and to diversify the horror genre. FINAL WORDS We’re excited to bring this film to life! Thank you for supporting independent horror. We hope you can see the potential of this project as much as we can. With your help, Demon Twink will come to a screen or festival near you as soon as possible!

title: ORMUS JP
description: Ormus JP: In the limitless expanse of the universe, where the whispers of ancient truths resonate, a tale of exploration and enlightenment unfolds. This is the journey of a young seeker named Lyra, whose pursuit of existence's mysteries propels her beyond the stars. Under the watchful gaze of celestial constellations, Lyra's keen intellect and insatiable curiosity reflect the legacy of the great philosopher-kings. Inspired by the timeless wisdom of figures like Walter Russell, she sets forth to decode the universe's enigmas, guided by the sacred symbols woven into the very fabric of creation. As Lyra immerses herself in cosmic consciousness, she encounters enigmatic beings and ethereal entities, each offering a fragment of the puzzle she seeks. Accompanied by loyal companions, she traverses distant galaxies and explores ancient ruins, unearthing the hidden truths that lie dormant within the cosmos. Embracing the scientific method inspired by Francis Bacon, Lyra employs meticulous observation and empirical experimentation to unlock the mysteries of nature and unveil the universal laws that govern existence. Yet, as her journey unfolds into the unknown, Lyra realizes that the essence of the cosmos is not merely in its secrets, but in the connections forged between all living beings. With every revelation, she draws closer to the ultimate truth: that love, compassion, and empathy are the forces binding the universe together. Throughout her quest, Lyra experiences a spectrum of emotions, from despair to wonder and joy. She confronts hatred and darkness but also discovers the transformative power of love and happiness. These feelings are woven into the narrative, enriching gameplay and immersing players in a profound emotional journey. Ormus JP:  is not just a game; it’s an exploration of the human experience. Players will feel the weight of despair, the warmth of love, the thrill of discovery, and the awe of cosmic wonder alongside Lyra. Join her on this epic quest to illuminate the mysteries of the cosmos and forge a brighter future for all beings across realms and dimensions.

title: ⚔️ Charity Quest: Turf Wars ⚔️ Solution to volunteer deserts
description: Charity Quest ™ is a free volunteering app that connects local volunteers to the charities in their communities, 100% free of charge. We're seeking funds on Kickstarter to add Turf Wars® , a gamified expansion pack that addresses the critical problem of volunteer deserts in communities that need the most help. Charity Quest Hello Kickstarter, my name is Frank DiCola! I'm a software developer with a big love for gaming, community service, and my country. Let’s talk a little bit about why I built Charity Quest, how it succeeded, how it failed , and why we need your help to make it better. I fell in love with my college town. When I finished my Master's degree at Stevens Institute of Technology in Software Engineering, I decided to remain in Hoboken, NJ! I lived with my older brother (also a Stevens grad) and had a few local friends, but most of the Stevens crew had scattered to distant parts of the U.S. as work and life forced them to travel away. As a result, those post-grad years were lonely, and I felt a real call to serve my community so I could get to know my city better. I could see that we had a small issue with homelessness locally, and was aware of a homeless shelter in our city. But I also wondered - what else was out there? At the time, I was going into Manhattan in search of volunteer opportunities since I didn't know how many were just around the corner. There's beardless Frank on the right in 2018! How naive he was... Without a centralized directory of local charities, most locals were in the dark about how to volunteer. I launched Charity Quest on May 15th of 2020 to serve as a “one stop shopping” mobile app for community service. Here I am volunteering at the Hoboken Community Center Pantry in 2019. From left: Me, with the Executive Director Toni Tomarazzo, Councilwoman Tiffanie Fisher, and Aaron Lewit of happy memory. The HCC Pantry was the first local charity to use the Charity Quest mobile app for their volunteer recruitment and management! Not only does the Charity Quest mobile app show you all the nearby charities looking for volunteers, it also has their volunteer shift info like times, locations, and the number of other volunteers that are already signed up. How much do I charge for this service? Absolutely nothing. Why is that? Because, as the executive director of a homeless shelter once told me: "Free fits within our budget!" Local charities get to use our back-end tools to post their “quests” to the network, view volunteer contact info, and reach out to volunteers. Since 2020 we've grown organically throughout Northeast New Jersey (and a tiny bit into New York City). We've grown a lot over the past five years! The network now has more than 14,000 volunteers serving at 50+ local charities . They're serving up thousands of hot meals, bags of groceries, hygiene kits, and baby supplies for single mothers. They are helping tutor kids after school, clean up their neighborhoods, and even move homeless families into their own apartments. As more charities joined our little network, my problem was solved! I figured out where I could volunteer in my area and connected lots of volunteers and charities to each other. Time to kick up my feet and relax, right? Not exactly. As I grew the Charity Quest network to its current size of 50+ local charities and 14,000+ local volunteers, I began to notice a troubling trend. The areas of greatest need around me (that is to say, areas with the highest poverty) were also the areas with the lowest number of active volunteers . That’s because volunteering is a bit of a luxury hobby. If you have time during business hours to pitch in around town, that’s great! But the poorest Americans among us, who are bouncing between two or three different jobs, just can’t afford to spend that kind of time. This is how a community enters the death spiral known as a volunteer desert . In a volunteer desert, local charities eventually have to close, because the skeleton crew running the organization can’t operate without a deep bench of local volunteers. ...which decreases the amount of social services (food, education, legal assistance) available in that neighborhood.... ...which increases the strain on the remaining organizations in the area. But it doesn’t necessarily increase the volunteer pool, because there weren’t that many to begin with. The people who do have time to volunteer reach their burnout point incredibly fast. They get overworked and decide they can’t commit anymore. The local charity gets one step closer to closing down . Like real water in an actual desert, the last few drops evaporate and fade away, never to return. We need your help to reverse this trend, bring life back to volunteer deserts, and help the poorest communities around us. We need the Turf Wars expansion to the Charity Quest mobile app. Ready to make a pledge? Kickstarter will only charge your card if we reach our funding goal, and you have until the end of the campaign to adjust your contribution. Take a look at the Pledge Levels section below and pick the pledge tier that works for you. Pledge Levels There are many ways to support our campaign! Seed Pledge Tier - Follow our campaign and receive exclusive backer updates! Wood Pledge Tier - Show your support and join the Charity Quest community! Stone Pledge Tier - You'll receive a unique digital weapon your character can equip as soon as Neighborhood Wars launches. You'll also get to join us in our developer Discord and provide feedback to the development team and view sneak peaks at what we're working on! Bronze Pledge Tier - Secure a lifetime subscription to Charity Quest, ensuring you'll never miss a second of that sweet Neighborhood Wars action! Collector Pledge Tier - As a token of your incredible generosity, we will send you a rare collector's coin with the Charity Quest emblem on it. We say coin, but it's more of a medallion because this thing is MASSIVE. Knight Pledger Tier - Join the wonderful world of game design by helping the Charity Quest team design one of the items players can acquire in Turf Wars. Whether it's a shimmering set of armor, a trusty carpenter's hammer, a deadly dagger, or something incredibly stupid, whatever you design is going in the final game! Wizard Pledge Tier - Join the wonderful world of game design by helping the Charity Quest team design a Spell that players can use to dramatically turn the tide of Turf Wars. Will you allow players to rain hailstones down on their foes with the devastating "O, Hail No" spell? Perhaps you'll allow them to rebuild their home base with the "Crafty Corgis" spell? Whatever you design is going into the game! Merchant Pledge Tier -Secure a discounted 2-year sponsorship on the Charity Quest app. Your business will be listed as a Gold Sponsor, inspiring love from all of the grateful volunteers in our network! Royal Pledge Tier - We will move Heaven and Earth to bring Charity Quest to your part of the country! Your region will start with zero volunteers and zero charities, and eventually blossom into a thriving network for charities and volunteers! You'll receive in-app credit as the benevolent warlord of your town, earning the eternal adoration of volunteers nationwide. If you select this tier, the CEO of the company will work with you to bring our free volunteer management platform to the town of your choosing. Be prepared to help facilitate this effort (couch surfing, anybody?) in any way you are able! Wow, that was a lot! Thank you for pledging to our project! We can't do this without you. Now let's hear from some volunteers and service leaders in Hudson County, NJ who love the app. Testimonials Charity Quest is already operating in four counties of New Jersey, and many of the organizations we serve are in volunteer deserts. We spoke with Carol Deribeprey, the Communication Specialist & Organizer at Dvine Konektion food pantry in West New York, NJ, to get her thoughts on Charity Quest: Carol is a huge fan! West New York has the demographics of a volunteer desert, and recruiting volunteers has always been a struggle. In this short video, Carol describes how the Charity Quest app has brought the next generation of volunteers into service. It isn't just staff members that love the app! Volunteers appreciate that fact that it's now easy and fast to sign up to volunteer locally. Sakura Helm is a local college student: David Ford has been a resident for years, but his shy demeanor kept him from volunteering. Our mobile app gave him the confidence to step into a world of volunteering. Mark Bloomberg lives in a town that doesn't have any charities, so he really appreciated our map-based approach: We're aiming to raise $10,000 to build the Turf Wars expansion pack. But what happens if we raise more than that? Let's take a look at some of the Stretch Goals we have planned for this campaign: Stretch Goals We're thrilled that we get to work on this project. But we want to be up front and remind everyone that some of our more ambitious ideas will require more funding than our initial goal. Now that we have reached our initial funding goal, we have revealed our first Stretch Goal: Summoning Stones! Keep sharing the campaign, so we can reveal them all! From this point on we will solely be discussing the Turf Wars expansion pack! Project Timeline April 2025 - October 2025 Design and Development November 2025 - March 2026 Closed Beta Testing March 2026 - May 2026 Open Beta June 2026 - October 2026 Hudson County Launch October 2026 - December 2026 Feedback from Hudson County, Design Refinement January 2027 - October 2027 Design and Development of Nationally Scalable Game October 2027 - December 2027 Launch in all active regions Turf Wars Charity Quest™: Turf Wars® is an expansion pack that builds upon our free volunteering app. We're creating a neighborhood-based "meta-game" for volunteer events and charitable donations that will act as a motivation engine to address the critical issue of volunteer deserts in areas of great need. Turf Wars will add a territory system that pits your town against neighboring towns in a volunteer contest to save your city and leave your enemies in shambles! Sketch of three Territories in Hudson County: Hoboken, Jersey City, and The Port Imperial Alliance. Each Territory has a Tower that must be defended by the residents of that Kingdom. We will add a special button in our app that lets volunteers toggle between the normal map view, and the Turf Wars view. Before your very eyes, your peaceful community will morph into a fantasy medieval battlefield! This volunteer is attacking an enemy Tower by volunteering far from their hometown. Prototype artwork, final results are subject to change. Turf Wars is simple: you want to destroy the enemy's Tower, while keeping your Tower intact until the end of the Battle. A single Turf War will last for a predetermined amount of time, typically two weeks. During the Battle Phase , volunteers can complete any of the following actions: Volunteer at / donate to a charity in their home neighborhood, which will add Health to the Tower in that neighborhood Volunteer at / donate to a charity in another neighborhood, which will remove Health from the Tower in that neighborhood After they successfully volunteer (e.g. are marked Present by Staff using our app) or donate through our mobile app, they see an animation of their character either attacking an enemy Tower, or repairing their home Tower. This volunteer is defending their home Tower by volunteering in their hometown. Prototype artwork, final results are subject to change. At the end of the Turf War, any players with a Tower still standing will receive a bundle of Valor Points (VP) during the Repair Phase . Players who attacked an enemy Tower at least once during the Turf War will also get a reward if that Tower was eventually destroyed. If your Tower got destroyed, you will get a minuscule consolation prize of Valor Points. Players can then spend their Valor Points on Weapons, Armor, Spells, and cosmetic items for their avatar! Destroyed Towers have time to heal during the Repair Phase , and then a new Declaration Phase begins, which starts the loop over again. Then it's on to the next Turf War! How will you play? Will you stay on defense and volunteer locally to keep your Tower from getting destroyed? Will you blaze a trail forward into neighboring towns, volunteering at new charities, meeting new people, and dealing massive damage to their Tower in the process? You can do both, actually! The choice is totally up to you. The technology for making this happen is already in place! We have data on whether volunteers have completed a service event (a quest) because the staff at each charity takes attendance through our app . That data contains information on where the charity is physically located. When we put all that together, it gives us a "battle log" that might read something like: If you read the battle log above, you'll notice a few interesting things. As mentioned above, Turf Wars is a motivation engine to encourage people in our volunteer network to alter their habits in two key ways. We want to discourage no-show volunteers, while rewarding volunteers who serve at charities with few helpers. Notice that Frank foolishly damaged his own Tower by failing to show up to a volunteer shift he signed up for. He also completely annihilated the Jersey City tower by volunteering outside of his home territory and landed a "critical strike" by volunteering for a quest that had less than 2 other volunteers signed up. Look out for a future update here on Kickstarter all about the Motivation Engine behind Turf Wars, and how it will help solve the critical problem of volunteer deserts. Weapons So... the Turf War is over! You have bravely volunteered many times in your local community, and also in some nearby towns. You have vanquished your foes, and you now have some Valor Points to spend before the next Turf War. Let's buy weapons! Weapons are items your character can hold in their hand to help them deal more damage, or heal more health, when volunteering. Attack is a modifier applied to your volunteerism abroad (attacking a Tower). Support is a modifier applied to your volunteerism at home (healing your Tower). Some Weapons, like the Wooden Stick above, are really intended for going on offense and they don't help you much on defense. Many are multi-purpose (like the Wooden Hammer ), and some are purely for support, such as the Tattered Flag . Once you purchase a Weapon, it's yours forever and can be equipped at any time! Weapons can't ever break or be destroyed once you have them. They give volunteers a sense of permanent progression as they dig deeper into their own communities and serve the poor with greater love. If you have a fun idea for a Weapon, remember that you can help us design one if you back this project at the Knight Pledge level! Now let's take a look at some other actions players can take besides volunteering that affect the outcome of the Turf War... Spells What medieval fantasy game is complete without magical Spells? The Turf Wars expansion will add the ability for players to turn the tides of battle with well-timed fiery attacks, last minute heals, and dazzling defenses. For now, we're using artwork in the public domain as a placeholder. We plan to use the money to hire freelancers to create custom artwork, preferably in the style of the Victorian Romanticists. Players can cast any Spell in their Inventory to tip the scales of the current Turf War. Many Spells will heal your Tower, and others will deal damage to an enemy Tower. Look out for more details on Spells in a future update, right here on Kickstarter! We had a lot of fun designing these, and we want you to join in the fun! Remember that everyone who backs our campaign at the Wizard Pledge gets to design their own Spell. What will you create? Summons Dealing damage by casting a Spell is pretty cool, but wouldn't it be even better to send troops to battle to do your work for you? That's where Summons come in! Summoned units make your neighborhood's battlefield crackle with life. During an active Turf War, you might see a fierce Red Dragon flying toward your Tower while a battalion of your Noble Knights gallop toward your foes. The Red Dragon moves in real time so players have a chance to react to it. Here, it is just two hours away from the Hoboken Tower. When it reaches Hoboken, it will begin attacking the Tower. This Tower is already on fire, but its health total can go negative, all but ensuring the defenders will never repair it before the end of the Turf War. Summons are special kinds of Spells that add monsters to the map . The summoner chooses a target (e.g. "Attack the Castle of Bayonne!") and the monster will then begin traveling to their target in real time. Some Spells require you to bring your phone to a natural site in your community in order to cast the Spell. Examples of these "Summoning Stones" are places like a reservoir, trail, forest, park, monument, or historic landmark. Defenders in the region will need to decide between attacking invading units , or healing their Tower . Look out for more details on Summons in a future update, right here on Kickstarter! Motivators Charity Quest: Turf Wars is all about building on the neighborhood volunteering app we created and adding a key layer of gamification to motivate the kind of behavior we want to see in the world. We plan to offer daily and weekly missions that will reward players with bonus Valor Points if they volunteer at charities who are in dire need of help. We're also planning a Bounty Board that highlights these opportunities the moment a player logs in, to encourage more people to choose those shifts. Groups already exist in Charity Quest as "Guilds", so we're planning to offer a special bonus to any volunteers who complete a quest with a fellow guild-mate. Volunteering with your guild-mates will result in a Guild Strike! Look out for a future update here on Kickstarter all about the Motivation Engine behind Turf Wars, and how it will help solve the critical problem of volunteer deserts. Territories Charity Quest: Turf Wars will be constantly evolving as more charities across the country adopt our free volunteer management platform. Right now there are only ten Territories, but one day there will be hundreds, perhaps over a thousand! A Territory is any patch of land (a city, or a part of a county) where there is at least one charity in our network. It is represented by a banner and a title that is either the name of the city, or a fun creative name that blends many little towns together. Hoboken has many charities in our network, so it stands alone as a Territory even though it is dwarfed by neighboring Jersey City in terms of population. The Port Imperial Alliance is a fantasized amalgamation of many towns in north Hudson County: Weehawken, West New York, Union City, Guttenberg, and North Bergen. Players must declare for a single Territory before a Turf War begins, and they will be fighting for that Territory for the entire duration of the battle. Here's the flag of Hoboken, the first town added to Charity Quest: Each Territory has a flag and their own color. We'll do our best to make sure neighboring Territories have distinct colors that contrast nicely with each other. Currently, the majority of the Territories players can declare for are in northeastern New Jersey and Greater New York , but more Territories will be added as more charities join. For balance purposes, areas like Hoboken that are densely packed with charities stand alone as their own Territories. That's because Hoboken has 7 different charities all in one square mile! On the flip side, small towns can be merged together into one combined Territory if they have very few charities, such as the Port Imperial Alliance. Here's a look at some of the other Territories in Hudson County, New Jersey: Some Territories will retain their actual municipal names (like Jersey City) and others will adopt silly fantasy versions (like Bayonne becoming the Bay of Yonne). Why bother handcrafting these Territories? Because our goal is to make sure that the results of the Turf War are based on player behavior, instead of being pre-determined by factors outside of the players' control. What if there are no charities in your town using Charity Quest yet? Are you still allowed to compete in Turf Wars? Absolutely . Players without a Territory to call home can declare for The Outlanders , a roving band of marauders who have no home base , and can only attack. At this point, a huge majority of the United States is considered The Outlands, a vast uncharted wilderness that must be explored! The Outlanders faction ensures that no one is ever left out of Turf Wars, even if the charities in their area have yet to adopt our platform. Speaking of which... Players who live in the Outlands can add their town as a Territory by persuading at least one local charity in their area to use our free volunteer management tool. Once that charity joins the network, civilization will gloriously arrive at this formerly barren hellscape! As a thank you, the player who founded the Territory will permanently gain the title of Lord or Lady of the Keep , visible to all other players. They will help us design the banner for this new Territory, and their name will always be listed as the Founder of the Territory. In a future update here on Kickstarter, we'll break down the process of how we design Territories , what goes into making each of their banners, and how the map will evolve as our non-profit network grows. Our Team Charity Quest would not be the thriving network it is today without the help of many freelancers, volunteers, advisors, and supporters. I want to extend my gratitude to everyone who has labored over the years to help me with this wildly ambitious idea. I also wanted to include this section so you can see that I'm not creating Turf Wars totally alone without any help. Without further ado, here's our awesome Charity Quest team! I started programming the mobile app for Charity Quest in October 2019. Since then, we released a website + admin dashboard, recruited 14,000+ volunteers, and added 50 non-profits to the network! My role at Charity Quest is to recruit new charities, support our existing non-profit clients, guide the strategic vision for the company, and guide the ongoing software development of the mobile app + backend tools. Team members are shown below along with icons to indicate their favorite type of volunteer service! We want to extend a special thank you to the advisors who have consulted with us during this process. Bruce Blondina has been a wellspring of knowledge and support since we bumped into each other by chance at a nonprofit event in Hoboken. Our mutual love of our alma mater (Stevens) and volunteering brought us together. Bruce believes in the Charity Quest project, and has 27+ years of nonprofit management under his belt. Jacob Spizziri provided technical consulting to usher Charity Quest out of the prototype era and into a framework that could scale nationwide. The app owes him a great debt, and his taciturn coaching has made me a better software developer. Rocco Vaccone is a software engineer at Xonar Technology, and went to my alma mater. He was instrumental in growing the team to include more student volunteers from Stevens Institute of Technology, and also helped with the Charity Quest website! Sam Jacobs created our Kickstarter video pro-bono because he believes in the Charity Quest project and has seen the power of its volunteer recruitment ability firsthand. We can't thank him enough, and highly recommend his video services to all who live near Southern New Jersey! Charity Quest is a mobile app with a companion website for admin features. Our dedicated software developers help maintain the website, the mobile app, the backend service, and handle custom data queries for larger charities. Yishu (Ben) Song joins us remotely for the West Coast and boasts a wealth of experience in Vue 3 development. He has been instrumental in cleaning up legacy code and implementing new features! Phillip Anerine has done an excellent job working on the admin features of the website that most users never see. These tools are used by our charity partners, and are the true strength of the network. After all, if charities don't find the system useful, why would they continue posting quests for volunteers to easily sign up for? Josh Blecherman originally joined to help us with fundraising, but found his niche in the data engineering department given his extensive work experience in that field. He is helping us clean up legacy data from the early days of the Charity Quest protoype! We've had a lot of help with this Kickstarter from our incredible Fundraising and Marketing team! Roma Razdan, Laina Haymes, and Sarah Solano have led the way during our Kickstarter campaign by reaching out to the press, influencers, local politicians, and businesses. Nate Jensen is a Stevens student who has helped us film early drafts of the Kickstarter video, as well as stakeholder interviews with staff & volunteers. Aasis Jaura is a Stevens student who created our updated logo along with its super cool animation. Then he created mockup animations with original illustrations of the attack / defense sequences that make up the bulk of the Turf Wars gameplay loop. Laura O'Brien helps to spearhead our outreach to new partners, content creators, and corporations. Rebecca Bang is a Stevens student who creates social media graphics, and edits our Instagram Reels. We're blessed to work with not one, but two wonderful teams of seniors who attend my alma mater, Stevens Institute of Technology . These student teams were assigned to Charity Quest as part of their capstone projects for their final year of school. Our Computer Science team is adding donation capabilities to our volunteering app, and our Business & Technology team has been helping with this Kickstarter + conducting market research for us. Though we will have to say goodbye to these bright smiling faces in June, we look forward to continuing this partnership by engaging two new teams in the Fall of 2025! We will seek students from the Game Design program to provide additional help with the Turf Wars expansion pack, while the new Business students will conduct outreach to charities across the Tri-State area. (NY, NJ, CT) Thank you for reading our entire Kickstarter story! If you made it this far, you probably think Charity Quest is awesome. You can be a valued member of our team by pledging money toward our campaign, sharing it with your friends, and introducing us to additional charities that are interested in our free platform. Please pledge to our campaign, and share it with your network today!

title: Christmas Ornament Games - Garland Games
description: Games Pledge Levels Meet the Team Gameplay Videos Reviews Shipping Info About Garland Games Garland Games is an independent game studio that creates family games designed to fit perfectly into the magic of the holiday season. Each Garland Games product begins as a beautifully crafted Christmas ornament game, a collectible ornament that doubles as a game box with different games that families can enjoy together. Our goal is to help build new Christmas traditions through games that are easy to learn, beautifully designed, and light enough to play anytime during the holidays. As creators of family Christmas games , holiday keepsakes , and interactive ornament collectibles , we focus on making games for all ages that spark connection and encourage meaningful moments. These ornaments don’t just hang on the tree, they bring families together with gameplay that kids, parents, and grandparents can enjoy in just a few minutes. Whether you’re looking for Christmas gift ideas , new family traditions , or holiday games for kids and adults , Garland Games specializes in experiences that are simple, warm, and memory-making. Garland Games is focused on creating a Christmas games for families that want more than decorations, they want keepsakes that become part of their yearly traditions. Our games are perfect for stocking stuffers , Christmas Eve activities , classroom gifts , holiday party favors , and anyone searching for a light to mid-weight family game that fits right into the festive season. If you love supporting independent creators , discovering new Christmas traditions , or collecting interactive holiday ornaments , Garland Games is proud to design heartfelt, charming, and easy-to-play games that help families create memories year after year. Join us as we continue to bring new family games to life through our unique line of Christmas ornament games.

title: Epipocalypse: Bug Detectives, A Medical Card Game
description: Epipocalypse: Bug Detectives is a medical educational card game that provides a more enjoyable and engaging way to learn infectious disease than simple flash cards or tables and charts. Learn the clues to infectious disease diagnoses using a fun, endlessly replayable game that tests your knowledge beyond basic trivia-style questions. Play alone or with friends either competitively or collaboratively. Who is this game for? Epipocalypse was designed by an infectious disease doc with a passion for educating healthcare professionals, students, and anyone interested in learning infectious disease epidemiology . Game play is simple and requires no preexisting medical knowledge so Epipocalypse: Bug Detectives can be picked up by anyone interested in learning more about medicine (though the knowledge does help). Game Explanation In Epipocalypse, players draft a team of scientists to combat the rising tide of infectious disease. Players match scientists to infections to score points in a race against either each other or themselves (too many errors and they're out!) Different scientists look for specific disease traits and can be scored with multiple infections allowing for combination scoring. In competitive play, up to 4 players (or teams) can race to identify the most infections over 10 rounds. In the solo campaign, players test their knowledge and skills against 66 increasingly difficult levels. Game play is easy to learn, and games are quick to play. Scientist Cards Scientists are the heroes of Epipocalypse and include a diverse collection of clinical and epidemiological researchers who are looking for different types of infection (e.g., those affecting the heart or lungs for example) or the keys to their transmission (e.g., geographic distribution, vectors, reservoirs, & more). Match a symbol the scientist is looking for with individual traits on Infection Cards to score points. Infection Cards Infections are the enemy in Epipocalypse and the means to victory. Each Infection Card has up to 19 unique traits that can be paired with a multitude of Scientist Cards to score points. Traits include the systems they infect, their geographic distribution, their means of transmission (i.e., vectors), places they like to hide (i.e., reservoirs), their organism type (e.g., fungi, parasites, etc), whether they are vaccine preventable, and more. What's In The Box? How To Play Epipocalypse is easy to learn! A single round in the competitive mode plays as follows: Solo Mode: In the solo campaign, it is you alone against the germy hordes. There is no set number of rounds; instead, you play until you finish the Mission that Home Office sent you on or you don’t come back (i.e., you accumulate more than 6 failed searches & relocations). Each named mission pairs you with a hand of Scientist Cards. Missions slowly increase in difficulty over time. Complete enough missions to get bonuses to help you along on your campaign! Watch the 6:54 minute "How to Play" video to quickly learn how to play Epipocalypse's "multiplayer versus" mode. An additional short walk through for Epipocalypse's solo campaign mode is also available. Free PDFs of the Rule Book and a Print & Play of the most recent prototype can also be found at the links below. Rule Book Print & Play Try It Out! Become A Scientist! Reviews "Just as much fun as Pharmageddon. An engaging way to learn the epidemiology of infectious diseases with friends!" -- Matt G, ID Fellow "Epipocalypse: is smart, fast, and keeps infections fun." -- Soubhi A, Scientist "A fun and accessible teaching game that mixes Infectious Diseases, Epidemiology and Global Health. 10/10 would recommend!" -- Alan K, Microbe Wrangler "Epipocalypse: Bug Detectives transforms the slog of memorizing infectious diseases into a clever card game where players hunt for “horses” and spot elusive “zebras.” It’s a fun, strategy-filled way to lock in pathogens, vectors, and diagnoses — turning study time into something you’ll actually look forward to." -- IDStewardship.com Stretch Goals Add Ons Game Budget & Estimated Shipping Rates Game Timeline About the Creator For those still interested in reading more, I'll quickly tell you a bit about myself and how Epipocalypse: Bug Detectives came about. I am an Infectious Diseases-trained physician from California. During residency and fellowship I spent countless hours studying, some of which were enjoyable and some of which were less so. I made plenty of charts and even a phone app (Micropedia for iOS!), but always felt that the fight between body and bug was ripe for the gamification of learning. After serving in the Air Force for a few years and now working in academic medicine a few more, I finally decided to bring one of these ideas to life. Pharmageddon: Bugs vs Drugs was born in 2023, and after a successful Kickstarter was shipped to backers 12 months later. Demand for the game continued, Lab Beagle Games was created in 2024, and more ideas were put through the play testing works. Epipocalypse is the latest. It is arguably the prequel to Pharmageddon: Bugs vs Drugs, and I am happy to continue to create small, new ways to make learning infectious diseases a little bit better.
