title: Categories
description:
Hopefully you already watched the video all the way through to the hilarious outtakes at the end and you're ready to do this, but in case you didn't... Hi! I'm Crys Matthews. I sing and write songs—love songs, life songs, songs about getting through life whole enough to want to love and be loved. Welcome to my Kickstarter! I am so excited to share my newest music with you. I can say that, hands down, these are the best songs I've written to date. Producing an album is an expensive undertaking, but producing two projects simultaneously... well, you can just imagine. [Insert a massive Star Wars–type scrolling list of the various expenses: studio time, paying the producer and engineer, having both projects mixed and mastered, paying the musicians, paying the designers, paying the replicators to have it packed and shipped, paying to have it publicized so we're not the only ones who know how awesome this music is, etc.] Some folks might say, patience, grasshopper—just do one at a time. Well, about that... In 2016, I wrote a song called The Imagineers and entered it into NPR's Tiny Desk Contest. It didn't win, but it got referred to as "the unofficial anthem of the Tiny Desk Contest." It's a song about not ever giving up on your dreams. It says, "We are Imagineers, the ones who still believe in magic, the ones who rise above our fears." I knew the song was something special and that it would be the title track for my next release. Every other song on this album has that same theme of determination and hope. What I didn't expect was to write another song for this year's Tiny Desk Contest that was just as special. Battle Hymn for an Army of Lovers (you can hear a live version below) follows the folk tradition of music as social commentary, as do the other songs on the EP. One of my favorite lines from the title track says, "So don't let four years change your heart. We were not born hating each other — now is not the time to start." Bob Dylan was right when he said, "the times they are a-changin'," and that's why these songs cannot wait. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Off English So if you've made it this far, you're either one of The Imagineers, a proud member of the Army of Lovers, or both (note to self: there should be a Facebook quiz for this). Either way, I'm glad you're here! I hope that means you'll join me in bringing these songs to life. With Kickstarter, it's all or nothing; the project gets funded only if we meet our goal—that means I can't do it without you, and we can't do it without reinforcements (a.k.a. some of your friends, family, co-workers, etc.)! We have only a small window to reach our goal. Remember, pledge early—don't wait! And share often! AND don't forget to keep an eye out for project updates so you can see all of the fruits of your generosity! Because this is such a big endeavor I'd definitely prefer to finish in the OMG-YOU-TOTALLY-CRUSHED-IT category! #NoSandbagging Thanks again! PS—For those of you who have been waiting, these are the songs I'll be releasing (not necessarily in this order): The Imagineers,  Unraveling, By My Side, All-Nighter, Fall All the Way, Looking Glass, One Chance, Anchor, Two Hearts, Off of My Lips, Pittsburgh, Missing You, Shatterproof, Battle Hymn for an Army of Lovers,
 American History XIX, One and the Same, Paris is Burning, We Must Be Free

title: Categories
description: Stretch Goals - #HereWeGo "The reason for living to giving." -TR Once Eugenia Hunts for Gemstones reaches the original goal of $6000 we move from supplementing print costs to gifting books to kids in San Diego, CA. I've set 3 Stretch Goals. I hope to reach the top tier of raising an additional $10K by March 28th. Reaching this top tier level would allow for 730 books to be gifted into the San Diego community. How you can help meet the STRETCH GOALS. Pre-Order your copies. Choose one of the philanthropic options Pledge any amount without a reward. The inspiration. The Eugenia books are based on true stories told by my grandmother and my family. These stories have been in the making for over a hundred years. Lifestyles were very different then. Bringing my grandmother's adventures to life through words and beautiful illustrations will give today's reader a glimpse into a little history, but kept in a modern look and feel. Telling these stories will help my grandma's memory live on...and if you have ever loved a grandma, you know the importance of sharing her life with the next generation. P.S. That is an illustration of her home and a photo of us sitting on the front porch of her home. XO, Jasmine About the author. Hi, I'm Jasmine. I have had this story and many others written in my head for many years. I put them to paper not too long ago, found an illustrator that does beautiful work, and am now launching my first children's book. I have enjoyed writing from a young age and always loved reading. The library was one of my favorite places in school, I loved the crinkled plastic covers that protected the books, and using the Dewey Decimal system to locate books. The act of checking out a book felt important and I was proud to carry as many books as the librarian would let me. My goal as a children's book author is to deliver memorable stories packaged in a book that looks beautiful and feels special when a child holds it. Why I'm looking for your support. Creating this Kickstarter will help launch this first book and grow an audience for the upcoming books for the Eugenia 's Books collection. I am depending on this campaign to (literally) kick-start my children's book author journey. ALL PRE-ORDER BUNDLES SHIPPING TO THE USA RECEIVE FREE SHIPPING. About the book. Eugenia Hunts for Gemstones Eugenia is a curious little girl who loves to learn. She looks  forward to trying new things and sharing them with her friends like  you. In this book, you will spend the day hunting for gemstones with her and Mama. You will explore the desert, make salad sandwiches, share Eugenia’s favorite pie, and learn how to tumble gemstones. The top 3 favorite pre-order bundles. Book Specs. 10.50″ x 10.50″ A large size for tiny hands to easily hold and perfect for reading in bed before bedtime. Hardcover with Spot Print A raised shiny area on the cover that makes the book look and feel extra special. First Eugenia Book Get the first title of the Eugenia Book collection coming soon. 24 Full Color Illustrated Pages Beautifully illustrated pages that bring the story to life. About Eugenia Eugenia is a curious, imaginative, and playful little girl. She is always brave enough to try new things, (even if she is scared). Eugenia is friendly to everyone she meets and loves to learn about new adventures through reading books and then trying them out for herself. About Mama Mama is an adventurous, smart, and independent mom. She loves her  daughter, Eugenia. She has a calm sense about her. She is mysterious and  loves nature. Mama guides Eugenia to be the best she can be at everything new she wants to learn. Mama knows the more Eugenia reads, the more she will grow. Where you can find us. Website: https://eugeniasbooks.com/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/eugeniasbooks/

title: Papa Possum's First Little Book of Satires
description:
The Project "Libellus•Primus•Saturarum - First Little Book of Satires" is the first book by American political satirist Papa Possum. Fourteen original satires in verse with accompanying illustrations and notations are contained in its pages.  The book, started in 2010 as a side project to the Papa Possum blog , is almost complete. If you have not yet read the Papa Possum satires, then consider this project an introduction. If you have read them, and if their message of Liberty and Classical Liberalism resonates with you, then join the campaign. The Remaining Work An estimated 2 - 3 months are needed to complete the book.  Approximately 40 references remain which need research, notation, and citation.  When these references are completed, the book will go through its final proofing and editing.  When the final proofing and editing is completed, the book will go to an on-demand publisher. When the finished book is uploaded to the on-demand publisher, the delivery of rewards will begin. The Rewards The rewards in this campaign are based on the book, the blog , and the nature of artistic political satire.  The rewards stack, so a reward at a specific level includes all previous upgraded rewards.  An upgrade is a change, not an addition, to a previous reward: e.g. an upgrade to a signed and numbered copy of paperback edition removes the unsigned copy of the paperback edition from the list of stacked rewards. Tangible rewards, other than copies of the book, include a Papa Possum Collector's Card, an Artisan Vellum Bookplate, a print of  a particular Single Satire which will appear in the book, and the Accompanying Illustration to that particular Single Satire.  A sample of each of these is included in its own following section. The Collector's Card The Papa Possum Collector's Card is a double sided, color, 3.5" x 2" card.  The card is signed, numbered, and laminated. The Artisan Vellum Bookplate The Artisan Vellum Bookplate is printed on a sheet of 3" x 4" genuine 
artisan vellum.  For rewards which include it, the book plate is signed,
 numbered, and affixed inside a copy of the hardcover edition of the book. The Illustrations Some of the rewards include a print of a specially commissioned illustration which appears in the book.  In the following section on the satires, each entry includes a cropped, watermarked sample region of the full illustration. The Satires Classic satire is more social commentary and artistic ridicule than outright comedy.  The Papa Possum satires hold to this style and sensibility.  The satires to be featured in the book first appeared on the Papa Possum blog from late 2008 to mid 2011.  A synopsis of each satire is listed below. Each synopsis includes a cropped, watermarked sample region of its full illustration. Each synopsis is titled with a link to the full text of the satire.  The synopses are listed in the order in which the satires will appear in the book.  If you are new to the Papa Possum satires, then take some time to read what the book will feature.  Enjoy, or not. Now In The Center Ring President Obama's dependence on his teleprompter is presented as the main attraction in the D.C. three ring circus. Chopenhange President Obama's 2008 campaign slogan and promises are presented as the pitch to an Old West traveling snake oil show. Marx The Angry Munchkin German philosopher Karl Marx is cast as a Grinch-like misanthropist who devises a master plan to extinguish all mankind. Little Billy Pipebomb A recap of the life, times, and claims to infamy of unconvicted domestic terrorist Bill Ayers. George Bush's Fault An exposition of some of the more ridiculous things for which President George W. Bush has been blamed, and an identification of one thing for which he actually is to blame. Old Sun Tzu The age of American politics no longer ending "at the water's edge" is juxtaposed against relevant advice contained in Chinese philosopher Sun Tzu's "The Art of War". Georgie Sorgie The January 20th, 2009 transition of the office of President from George W. Bush to Barack Obama is cast as the coronation of Dread King George Soros. Brother Al's Traveling Ecopalypse Show A recap of the rise of Vice President Al Gore to the position of Holy Prophet of the World Church of Eco-Marxism. Happy Birthday, O Dear Leader The first installment in what was to become a Papa Possum yearly tradition, a birthday poem for Dear Leader Barack Obama. Leviathan Thomas Hobbes wrote  "Leviathan", his treatise on State power. In a nod to him, Constitutional Lawyer Mark Levin popularized the term "Leviathan" when referring to our federal seat of power. In a nod to both, Papa Possum's "Leviathan" casts our federal seat of power as a malevolent being of insatiable appetite. Historia Dea Stili The force of History is reimagined as a goddess who is can never be obscured despite the unending attempts of the powerful. Happy Birthday, O Dear Leader, Year Two The second installment in the Papa Possum yearly tradition of a birthday poem for Dear Leader Barack Obama in which D.C. is cast as the decadent French court at Versailles. The Bones of Bolívar The unbelievable but true story of how, in a cynical ploy to retain power, Venezuelan dictator Hugo Chávez exhumed the grave of South American hero Simón Bolívar. An Accounting Originally a final paper in a class on Ethical Individualism, this piece is an exposition of the major philosophical schools of thought which were taught in that class, each of which are either accepted or rejected by the author of the Papa Possum satires. The Book Layout All editions of the book are formatted as 6" x 
9" U.S. Trade.  The satires in the paperback and hardcover editions are 
rendered in a specially licensed, distressed font.  Each satire is 
paired with a specially commission illustration.  To provide context, 
each satire is also notated with end notes and works cited.  The 
majority of cited works are available online. Conclusion: The Making of The Book You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 I'm Rick Poach, the author of 
the Papa Possum satires. In 2008, I started writing classic political 
satire in verse and posting it to a blog . 
 It wasn't long before I had enough satires to fill a little book.  I 
knew that there was no publisher that would accept a manuscript of 
political satire in verse, especially from an unknown blogger, so I knew
 that I had to publish the book myself.  So, I started learning the 
LaTeX typesetting language, I commissioned an illustrator, and I delved 
into legals and licensing.  Soon, the first collection of Papa Possum 
satires, "Libellus•Primus•Saturarum - First Little Book of Satires", 
started to take shape.  As I was working on the book, I realized that, 
as time passes, the popular context which gives political satires its 
edge is often forgotten.  So, I decided to add that context.  It turned 
out that adding the context was a huge project.  However, I'm happy to 
say that the first little book of satires is almost finished.  To finish
 the first little book of satires, I started this Kickstarter campaign. 
 The rewards are based on the blog and the book.  Many of the rewards 
are signed and numbered to be collectibles.  So, if you've ever stopped 
by the Papa Possum blog and liked what you've read, if you've ever felt that the everyman 
character of Papa Possum spoke for you, if you think there's a place in 
our political discourse for classic satire in verse, then join me in 
this campaign.  Thank you.

title: Healing The Cancer In Our Genes
description: Modern science is beginning to reveal something remarkable: our genes may not be fixed destinies. Through the field of epigenetics, researchers are discovering how lifestyle, environment, and even emotional states can influence how genes behave. Healing The Cancer In Our Genes explores this fascinating intersection between science, spirituality, and human biology . In this book, the author presents an unconventional yet thought-provoking perspective: that our genetic expression may be influenced by the choices we make, the lives we live, and the spiritual frameworks we adopt. Rather than viewing disease purely through a biological lens, the book invites readers to consider a broader understanding of health one that integrates epigenetics, emotional wellbeing, lifestyle, and spiritual awareness . Through this exploration, readers are encouraged to rethink the relationship between their inner world and physical health . At the heart of the book is a new concept called Holographic Medicine . This approach seeks to combine: • modern genetic science • lifestyle medicine • emotional wellbeing • spiritual philosophy Instead of focusing solely on treatment, this perspective explores how individuals might take a more active role in influencing their overall health. The idea is not to replace traditional medicine, but to expand the conversation about healing and inspire new ways of thinking about human biology. Today, millions of people are searching for answers about how lifestyle, stress, environment, and beliefs may affect long-term health. This book aims to: • explain complex ideas in epigenetics in an accessible way • explore the deeper connections between lifestyle and disease • inspire readers to take greater responsibility for their wellbeing • open discussion about the future of integrative medicine It is written for readers who are curious about the intersection of science, consciousness, and health . The mission behind this project is to encourage both patients and healthcare professionals to consider how lifestyle choices and personal awareness may influence long-term health outcomes. The book aims to spark curiosity, conversation, and further research into how genetics, epigenetics, and human behavior interact. It is not presented as a final answer but as an invitation to explore new possibilities in human health and healing. We invite you to follow the development of this project and join the conversation around epigenetics, health, and human potential. Your support helps bring new ideas into the world. Together, we can explore a deeper understanding of the relationship between our genes, our choices, and our health.

title: Wisdom Riot
description: Wisdom Riot: A Revolution of Thought , is a roadmap for navigating modern life, offering real solutions to improve personal well-being, strengthen relationships, and drive meaningful political change. From the mind of the 9th great-grandnephew of Benjamin Franklin, this book channels a legacy of revolutionary thought into a modern call for action. Rejecting passive acceptance of a broken system, it calls on individuals to become active agents of transformative, collective action. At its heart is a call for ethical evolution and structural reform, including the pursuit of constitutional amendments that embed justice, equity, and sustainability into the fabric of governance. This is not a self-help book filled with inspirational platitudes. Wisdom Riot is book of solutions to create a more unified world, and how we fight to keep it. One step at a time. One connection at a time. Welcome to the revolution. Welcome to the Wisdom Riot From philosophers pondering in their studies to revolutionaries rallying in the streets, the desire to improve our collective existence has been a constant companion to human progress. Today, as we stand at the crossroads of unprecedented global challenges and extraordinary human potential, that desire burns brighter than ever. They say you have to hate the world enough to change it yet love it enough to consider it worth changing. It's easy to hate something and complain about it. It's something else to actually talk about solutions and make them happen. While we have seen improvement in the world from previous generations there remains many broken systems that need changed for the better. Change starts with a revolution of thought. What we need is a Wisdom Riot . Have you ever looked at a broken system and wanted to do something about it but weren't sure where to start? Yes, you can protest. Yes, you can boycott companies. Yes, you can joke through satire and maybe you can influence but what can bring about the most change? This book is not a critique of society's ills or a utopian fantasy of what could be. Instead, it is a call to action, a roadmap for change, and most importantly, an invitation for you to join a revolution. Not a revolution of violence, but a revolution of thought, action, and compassion that begins with you and ripples outward to encompass your community and, ultimately, the world. The goal is to plant seeds of wisdom through education, to feed the roots of change. This change could happen anywhere, in any country but I would like to see this change begin in the United States of America yet be successful enough to inspire change to the rest of the world. Who will fix the broken system? I will. You will. We will, together. My Uncle, Benjamin Franklin Who am I? Why listen to me? When it comes to changing the world and fixing the Constitution of the United States, I guess you can say it literally runs in my family. Benjamin Franklin (1706-1790) was an inventor and one of the Founding Fathers of the United States of America. He's the only man to sign the three key documents in the birth of the United States: the Declaration of Independence , the Treaty of Paris , and the Constitution . It also turns out that he's, my uncle. On my mother's side, If you go back several generations, you come to John Franklin Sr. (1690 - 1756). John was the brother of "THE" Benjamin Franklin. He is my 9th Great Grand Uncle. For me, this is interesting family tree trivia yet there are thousands of descendants out there. Why even mention it here? The Constitution served to create a new society and separate it from a country ruled by a King. A system of elected officials and three branches of government, a legislature branch to write the laws, an executive branch to enforce the laws and judicial branch to judge the laws. Sounds simple enough, but there's a lot of things the Constitution wasn't prepared to do in 1787, and that is care for its people properly. The founding fathers were smart enough to put a few ways to make it better through amendments but fell short of quick fixes early that would have made the country even stronger now. We can't change the past, but we can change the future by actions we take now. Benjamin Franklin was a revolutionary whose contributions to the country's foundation is well studied. He wanted a constitution that worked for people and made life better for all. The constitution wasn't perfect when it was ratified, nor is it perfect now. He knew in 1787 that a number of things could bring down the country if left unchecked but hoped future generations could make it better. We need a revolution of thought. I'm no politician but I do care about people. I want to live in a better world. I want to help build a better world for future generations. I want to inspire change that helps us all. That is why I'm here. Why This Book? Why Now? What makes this book different from the many other self-help guides or societal critiques out there? Anyone can identify a problem, but few will figure out solutions to solve them. We live in an age that future historians might well label as pivotal, a time when humanity faces existential threats ranging from social inequality, war, peace, climate change, from technological disruption to global pandemics. This book is born out of the conviction that there's no time like the present to try and save the world. It's a product of years spent observing, analyzing, and most importantly, problem-solving. I am at heart, a problem solver and the problem we're tackling here is nothing less than the future of our world. The change will start with the United States Constitution. Additional amendments designed to help all of us. So many talk about the problems in the world, few offer solutions. This book is meant to offer those solutions. If you're tired of how bad things are in the world and looking for a way out, you're in the right place. It won't be easy. It won't be an overnight fix, but together, we can make a difference. This is what Wisdom Riot is all about. Feel free to ask me any questions about this project and I'll include what I can in the FAQ section of this campaign.

title: Looking Forward - Inspiring perspectives, compelling themes
description:
What LF is and what we want to do Looking Forward brings
you exciting stories of inspiring people around music, art, fashion, and
humanity. By doing so LF forwards perspectives that matter and gives cultural
lifestyle a voice in this world. LF publishes in print and online. We have been testing the
concept since June 2010 and are ready to extend our publishing options. The
newly developed website explains the concept and enables readers to interact. For one of the past issues we developed an interactive iPad
App that is fully functional. We are ready to make an even better pilot
version, which we want to make available for free download on the iOS market. How we will do it (with your support) The pilot of LF's website has been launched on January 11th! www.looking-forward-magazine.com .
Starting early in February we will also welcome two 4th year
University interns, who will support us in the start up phase/introduction of the
new online platform and gear up to collect material especially for the tablet
version of the magazine. An outsourced designer/programmer will develop the iPad App. The
material we collect is not only intended to be published online, but also in
print and for the tablet version. With focus on the tablet version we will create the ultimate
interactive experience utilizing all the magical tablet functions. How
the funds will be used The funds will be used to pay for: 1)  
The website design and programming 2)  
The App design and programming 3)   Annual fee to have the App available for
download on the iOS market. In case of overshooting our goal we intend to use the funds to
print a recent issue. We do intend to make a living from LF in the future. How far along is the project? We are ready and fully tested. All we need is temporary
liquidity to make it a reality. The Core Team We are asking for quite a bit, so here is a proper team introduction for those of you who are interested! Francesca (Founder/Creative Director) Looking Forward is her
brainchild. Already back in high school she has always been very outspoken and
interested in life and all its facets. Friends have called her high Priestess
of compassion, the moral conscience of this world or the tolerant warrior for
the weak and humble (thank you Toni). One month prior to graduating from
university she registered her business with the chamber of commerce. She is involved in
assisting three independent businesses aside her own, and also has a daughter. Julien (Digital Editor) The name’s Julien, with an “e”
because he is French. He was born and grew up near Paris. He just loves this
city, but then again, who doesn’t? And they say Parisians are
arrogant…Flashlights on cameras always take him by surprise and he doesn’t
especially like to travel. Yet somehow he’s ended up visiting about fifty
cities in a dozen countries… Go figure. The thing is he can’t leave his
apartment without a music-playing device of some sort and his headphones; when
he’s all equipped however, he can go a long way, so perhaps that’s why…Over the
years, he has lived and worked in France, England (London baby!) and the
Netherlands. What else? He just likes to take the time; time to hang out alone
or with friends, watch a nice DVD, play around with one of those graphic design
programs or look up stuff on the internet about music, sports, advertising,
communication, promotional campaigns, you name it: He’s just interested in
what’s happening. And once in a while, he’s down for good old night out! Alex (Strategic Advisor) Alexander Schroeder, is always up
for conversations about intriguing and controversial topics concerning this
world we live in, as long as it comes with a cold beer. His interest for the
world is most likely a result of his global citizenship. Alex was born in
Istanbul as a South African, who grew up in Germany, studied and worked in the
Netherlands and lived in China for a little while. If he had the time he would
just start walking earth in order to meet and talk to every person on this
planet before his passing. Probably this is how he got hooked with the LF team.
On a professional level, Alex graduated in Communications, worked as a Brand
Consultant in Shanghai and a Copywriter/Strategic Assistant in Rotterdam for
graphic design studios. Next to working for LF, Alex sings and writes; is a
hobby beat boxer, a backpacker pilgrim for life, monkey expert, movie crazy,
World Cup visitor and many other things he doesn’t want to bore our readers
with here. Let’s just say he appreciates the little great details in things and
the versatile finesse of life. Keep it crunschie! Charli (Feature Editor) Charli (short for Charlotte)
describes herself as a slightly eccentric humanitarian and a non-conformist
with a penchant of books, shoes, traveling, meeting interesting people and
having brilliant conversations. (Not in any particular order). Highly
empathetic yet equally opinionated, she’s known to comfort the disturbed while
disturbing the comfortable. Born and raised in rural South Africa, she moved to
the sleek streets of Johannesburg to finish her studies before landing in the Netherlands
over ten years ago. She likes to challenge herself and does not believe in the
word NO. If she is not writing and editing for LF, she’s heading her own
production company Balushi Media Productions that among others conceptualized
FACE OF HOLLAND model search competition. She also co-founded Khaya Foundation,
a non-profit set out to alleviate generational poverty in South Africa through
education. Meghan (Editor) Meghan MacKinnon is a 21st century
renaissance woman. The fun loving five foot three brunette from Nova Scotia,
Canada lives for her family, friends, traveling, reading, eating things she
hasn’t cooked, and all things sarcastic. This little lady has big dreams.
Taking inspiration from her mother, she likes to work hard, follow her dreams,
and makes sure she’s nice while she’s doing it. She has always loved being
creative, initially perusing a career in music back home in Canada, which is
now only relived through random late night karaoke. Although her heart will
always be with her family, in Canada, she has made Holland her second home over
the years. After a two-year detour studying Psychology in Madrid, she returned
to NL where she worked as a Color Consultant for Farrow & Ball, before
joining the LF team, where she is looking forward to pursuing her creative and
literary passions working for something she truly believes in. Meghan is always
up for an adventure! If money and time allowed she would travel the globe with
Jaime, stopping by to visit her family in Canada, Spain and South Africa. Jalane (Correspondent) Mr. Aparicio is a special case. He is
loud, bossy, straight forward, and stubborn. Yet somehow he get’s everything he
plans for done. Any and everybody know him in his city…and maybe that’s why.
Besides contributing to Looking Forward he is one of the front guys of Hip-hop collective L4, hence has deep knowledge of the entire music world and business
attached. Besides the previous mentioned attributes he is also a father, and
enjoys dancing with his daughter in the living room from Ronald Isley’s to Kanye
West’s tracks. As deep as his taste for music, is his thirst for documentaries
of any kind. He can spend any free minute in front of his laptop watching stuff
on Boxee. As the man he is…hard on the outside, but all gushy and warm inside. EMAIL: info@looking-forward-magazine.com Relevant Links: https://www.facebook.com/LookingForwardMagazine http://vimeo.com/lookingforwardmagazine/videos Pilot version is live! www.looking-forward-magazine.com

title: Talk of the Tavern
description:
I am Travis Sivart, creator and host of Talk of the Tavern,
live weekly radio show with a pub-like atmosphere since September of 2012. the first episode aired on October 1st, 2012 as Sounds of Steam. I do this out of my home and cover all costs out of my own pocket, and love every minute of it! Talk of the Tavern is your at-home,
interactive, weekly GeekCon. All blended with wit, sarcasm and a pub-like
atmosphere. It is done in the style of the great offenders, such as; Lenny
Bruce, Wanda Sykes, Sam Kinnison, Richard Pryor, Robin Williams, Redd Foxx , George
Carlin, Chris Rock, Lisa Lampenelli and others. If you are easily offended, you will find we will keep you busy without even trying. I have gathered an amazing group of people from diverse
ethnic, religious, and political backgrounds to laugh and talk about fun topics
or serious issues. We laugh a lot and include our live listeners in the conversation
via a Facebook event page, Twitter, or even email. We have covered topics ranging from silly and comedic (such
as conspiracies, cryptozoology, Fairy NaughTEA Parties, "Stress
relief", etc) to serious and hard hitting, (such as Feminism, Gay Lesbian and
Transgendered people in our community, racism, as well as the psychology and sociology
of human nature), all with a drink in our hands. Talk of the Tavern is a community oriented show. We have
supported countless authors, artists, artisans, musicians… the list goes on. Now,
we need your help. Listener support is invaluable, and it doesn't matter how
much you can give, just that we all work together to continue to get the show
we all deserve. I want to continue to revolutionize modern radio and how we
interact and communicate with each other, creating community and camaraderie. So, take a look around and choose one of the awesome reward
levels, or just contribute a flat amount. I can’t do this without great people,
people like you. And it wouldn't be worth doing without you. And most of all…
thank you. Thank you for your support, and for sharing this Kickstarter campaign with others. Previously I had a great setup with high speed internet. I moved and now I don't. That is the short version. I spent the last 6 months "borrowing" internet from very gracious friends (mostly just one, thank you and I love you dearly) who would have me over, feed me, let me sit on my computer in their living room for five hours and run the show. Now, I need a permanent solution. My new home only has satellite internet, which comes with up to a twenty second delay when on Skype, and that is without video or running constant streams to my radio station servers. I am going to train my co-hosts, Ed Summers and Daphne Dewell, to run the technical and software side of things. And to get a decent signal so I can join in the show without the delay, I am going to get a signal booster installed in my home. The money are seeking is enough to get the hardware and software my co-hosts need to efficiently run the technical side of the show, replace worn equipment of my own, get a zBoost TRIO Xtreme REACH for my home studio, cover the fees associated with a Kickstarter campaign, the rewards offered, shipping, and other business costs such as the website, business cards (and other minor office supplies), and other annual fees. $10 - Just Slip Us the Tip: ...and we will toast to your good health on an upcoming episode of Talk of the Tavern. We will deliver this as soon as the live show is up and running, probably sometime in April. $25 - S ay Cheese: We will send you an autographed 8" x 10" color photo of single Team Tavern member. You may choose from Travis Sivart, Kat Taylor of Katastrophe Labs, Kevin Crew, Matthew "The Correllbow" Correll, or Ed Summers. It will be inscribed personally to you, and you can display it in any room of your home or work. We will get these out as soon as the Kickstarter campaign funds, probably sometime in April. Note the shipping costs, especially if you are outside of the US. $50 - Book Nook: A DRM free digital copy of; Aetheric Elements: The Rise of a Steampunk Reality by Travis I. Sivart, Steampunk For Simpletons: A Fun Primer For Folks Who Aren't Sure What Steampunk Is All About by Travis I. Sivart and Wendy L. Callahan, and the Aetheric Artifacts bundle (4 books, the Chronos Clock, The Daemon Device, The Engima Engine, & The Gilded Gun) by Wendy L. Callahan, Mrs. Bumblebottom and the Chatterbots by Daphne Dewell, and The Completely Inappropriate Tales of Gandersnitch the Goblin by Robert Turk.  Plus more may be added along the way. We will deliver these via email soon after the Kickstarter campaign funds, probably sometime in April. ***NOTE - We may add more eBooks to this package. If someone has already chosen this reward level we will not be able to change the reward description to the left. Instead we will note it in this description and in a Kickstarter Update to backers. $75 - Do You Hear Voices? : Get a 30 second or less voice recording of a single Team Tavern member. Your choices are Travis Sivart, Gabrielle Riel of Radio Riel (who will even help you refine the message), Gandersnitch the Goblin, Kevin Crew, Kat Taylor of Katastrophe Labs, or Matthew "The Correllbow" Correll of The State of the Nerd. It will be in mp3 format. This can be for voice mail message, ring tone, or promoting you! Messages are subject to approval. $100 - Bar Pack: You get two Talk of the Tavern Mug Shots (mug shaped shot glasses), a metal engraved Talk of the Tavern Flask, two Talk of the Tavern Bar Towels, and four Talk of the Tavern copper tags (can be used as a keychain, luggage tag, or whatever). We will get these out as soon as the Kickstarter campaign funds, probably sometime in April. Note the shipping costs, especially if you are outside of the US. $150 - Pick a Topic: (Limited to 40 rewards) You pick a topic for an episode, and we will discuss it for a show. Topics must be approved on a case by case basis and the date the topic airs will be subject to availability. We will plan these and place them on our calendar over the next year, fulfilling all topics by April of 2016. $250 - Co-Host the Show: (Limited to 25) We bring you on the show to hang out with us for a full episode. You must have access to Skype (with mic and headset), a Monday night free, and ready to have a good time. Dates are between April 2015 and April 2016 and subject to availability. $500 - Sponsor a Show: (Limited to 70) You get a 15 minute pre-recorded interview, and once per hour for that episode we will talk about your product or service for a minimum of a minute, (3 times total). Dates are subject to availability and will air between April 2015 and April 2016. The interview can be done via phone or Skype. $1,000 - All of the Above!: Just what it says, you get everything listed in any reward of lesser value than this one. It's a bargain, and only takes one click! All restrictions, including shipping costs, mentioned above apply here also. $10,000 - The Tavern Comes to You!: You get everything listed above, and I will fly anywhere in the US and do the show from your home or business, provided you have high speed internet available. All restrictions, including shipping costs, mentioned above apply here also. If you live outside the US, then add $2000 to this, and I will grab my passport!

title: Where Are You From? An Illustrated Book of Poems
description: Neal the Earthling reading the poem "Where Are You From?" You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Our Project - A Book of Illustrated Poems Written on Earth but, out of this world, "Where Are You From?" is the story of one Earthling's journey to find home. It starts in reflection, detection of childhood trauma responsible for lifelong drama. A sensitive creature born, identity torn by lifelong scorn. We follow this creature becoming hollow, lost and alone, confused and abused, lacking rhyme or reason, for committing treason, season after season. Until realizing an open mind kept in time, shows signs of hope, light breaking through, giving clues to end the blues. Will this Earthling find home, or be forever unknown? Discover the cost of being lost. Flip through pages, life’s stages, depicted with one-of-a-kind illustrations and poetic verses from undiscovered universes. We hope this story will make you feel less alone and help you discover home. Sneak Peak Lyn, the artist, holding a mockup of the book. A look into what to expect from the book and its illustrations. Each poem does not start with a new story. The book is one continuous story, written with many poems and one illustration storyline. It's dark, raw, uplifting....it's relatable. Book Details: -174 pages -34 poems -160+ illustrated pages in color -Hardcover or Paperback -9x6.5in large Book Mockup Pages 2 & 3 Pages 4 & 5 Page 11 & 12 Page 13 & 14 Page 23 & 24 Page 25 & 26 Page 27 & 28 Our plan For this book to be published we need your help. The poems have been completely written and over 100 pages have been illustrated. We are using Kickstarter as a "pre-order" system so that we can receive the funds before we order the books. This guarantees that the money we put into publishing, ordering stickers, and packaging goes to use. If we do not reach our goal we personally can not afford to make the books. This is a passion project. We don't expect to make a huge profit from this. We want to get it out into the world to share because we think this book will be inspiring and a tool for others. This book will tug at your heartstrings. It will make you smile, it may even make you cry. It will make you think and most importantly it will make you feel. Our biggest hope is that it will make you feel less alone. Note From the Author I’ve always had trouble fitting in, which was my one true sin. Lyn the Alien could relate to my troubled state, a deadly fate. Trying to be someone while being no-one. One connected with a self that had been left on a shelf, all by myself. If fate would have its way, day after day, life would have no say. Led astray, far away, trapped in the past of shadows cast. Depressed, repressed, pressed into action, more as reaction, never any satisfaction. Looking for purpose, I left behind everything. My life seemed meaningless, successful by the measure of some, but truly successful to no one. Money, power, climbing a tower, to what ends, it never ends. Training for sustaining, instability, crumbling ability, of all our utility. When all is said and done, we are one. One amongst stars... Rewards We want to give the most we can to our supporters! So each reward comes with more than just a book. We will be producing high-quality vinyl sticker packs for those who order a hard copy, an option to buy a signed copy, prints, original drawings by the illustrator, Lyn, and original poems not included in the book by Neal. First few rewards include these three stickers. Past Experience Lyn Sweet is the illustrator for this book. She is also behind the marketing and producing of rewards. She has a successfully funded Kickstarter project earlier this year called "Things Unseen - An interactive, intergalactic photo book" (Check it out here: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/lynsweetart/things-unseen-an-interactive-intergalactic-photobo ) She has been freelancing her artwork for about a decade and has been producing stickers, pins, patches, books, t-shirts and more for about 6 years. She is familiar with the companies they will be ordering the stickers, t-shirts, and books from and understands the turnaround time. She is also well versed in shipping prices and how to package books properly to ensure safe arrival to the buyer. Because of her experience, you can feel reassured that this project will be created at the highest standard, on a decent timeline, and with ease.

title: STEM SLAM: Science Weekends for Curious Kids
description: STEM SLAM was designed by high school science teacher and instructional coach Dr. Ramsey Musallam . Frustrated with the lack of opportunities for scientific exploration present in the traditional classroom environment, in 2008 Ramsey began hosting summer science camps for Bay Area youth designed to transcend traditional curriculum and expose students to the wonders of inquiry. STEM SLAM weekends have emerged from Ramsey's original ethos, but aim to provide young minds with a chance to explore a specific area of interest during the regular school year. After a weekend of cutting edge exploration removed from paradigm of traditional schooling, participants will return to their respective classrooms with a kit of materials and associated lesson plans in hand ready to empower their peers and teacher(s). The connection between the excitement of a STEM SLAM weekend, and the immediate sharing of a student's experience with their classroom teacher(s), is integral to STEM SLAM'S mission: To  promote a love for science in all young minds that transcends the classroom, while simultaneously helping to empower and transform the traditional classroom environment. Program Specifics Fundraising Rational: Initial funds will go towards securing a consistent location, weekend facilitators, various high-end materials spanning a diverse range of activities/STEM content domains, participant take-home kits, and a low-participation fee to attract a high number of curious participants. Time Frame: Four self-contained weekend "slams" 1/month, that may be taken in sequence, or individually. Weekend "slams" will occur every month during the normally scheduled school year (September - June). Tentative Weekend Topics ( note : topics are flexible and will likely change and develop as resources are gathered and feedback is obtained) Weekend 1: Circuits Weekend 2: Robotics Weekend 3: Biotechnology Weekend 4: Forensics Content : STEM SLAM activities include, but are not limited to: building and soldering circuit boards. designing remote control devices for land and air robots. creating robotic drone air shows. programming autonomous robots. prototyping and designing wearable technology. exploring the chemistry of explosives. leveraging chemistry to solve mysteries. Sample Weekend Scope and Sequence Day 1: Circuit Construction Part 1. Exploring electrical resistance using conductive and non-conductive dough and ink. Part 2. Soldering as a method of building circuit boards. Part 3. Construction of a circuit board that uses conductive and non-conductive materials to play musical tones of varying frequencies. Part 4. Musical instrument invention using constructed circuit board and re-purposed conductive and non-conductive materials. Day 2: Circuit Application Part 1. Development of robot remote control circuit board that repurposes conductive and non-conductive materials. Part 2. Construction and connection of robot to developed circuit board. Part 3. Control of robot using circuit board to accomplish a variety of tasks. Part 4. Task invention that leverages developed remote control and autonomous robotic functions. Program Sustainability: Each STEM SLAM participant will be given a kit that includes a myriad of materials and activity guides to share with their peers and teachers. Follow up between the STEM SLAM staff and classroom teachers will occur, and subsequent classroom adventures and creations will be shared with the world via stemslam.org. More information: http://www.stemslam.org

title: Categories
description: 3 years ago we brought you the award-winning Roadie 2 & Roadie Bass. These products received massive support from our buyers and triers and since then we have focused on using all that we’ve learned to push things to the next level for Roadie 3. Tune faster than ever Roadie 3 is our new flagship automatic instrument tuner.  It helps you find your sound fast with quicker rotation and enhanced accuracy to keep your tunings consistently dead-on. Roadie 3 is built with carefully tooled and proprietary audio algorithms. Its next-generation vibration detection delivers improved tuning accuracy and enhanced noise immunity. If it has guitar pegs, Roadie 3 can tune it Roadie 3 can tune almost all instruments with geared pegs in just seconds. Like what you ask? How about electric guitars, acoustic guitars, classical guitars with nylon strings, pedal steel guitars, ukuleles, mandolins, banjos, to name a few. We have also seen musicians tune their lap steel guitars, bouzouki, mountain dulcimer, santoor, cigar box guitar, qanun with geared pegs, violins with planetary pegs, and many other exotic instruments using Roadie 3's predecessor! Others have even created custom instruments such as the charango, the guitalele, the bandurria, and the baglama. No matter the number of strings, just keep the lowest string above 27 hertz. This is the lowest note Roadie 3 will be able to detect. Restring & tune effortlessly We have completely revamped and sped up how Roadie 3 helps you change strings. Roadie 3 will now very quickly wind your new string to tension with a motor speed of 120 RPM and then tune it up, all in one smooth step! Watch it in action You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 When placed on the tuning peg, Roadie 3 will listen to the vibrations of your guitar string, analyze its pitch, and automatically turn the peg to get your string into perfect tune. Watch it in action: You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 No matter your experience level, tuning is no fun and it takes time away from playing. Roadie 3 will help you save time, explore more music, and truly enjoy tuning your instrument. We wanted Roadie 3 to be all about the details. Here are some of the little things that show our fellow musicians that we use what we make and listen to our fans. Designed by musicians for musicians, the app has a beautiful, minimal, and intuitive interface. It allows you to tune almost any string instrument on the go and stay up to date with the latest Roadie upgrades. It’s like your Roadie 3 found its soul mate! The App can : Refine your advanced tuning settings: change the reference pitch, tune with a capo, and modify the desired frequency to the cent. Receive the latest firmware updates and feature improvements for your Roadie 3. Create and store a profile for each of your instruments (electric, acoustic, classical, 7 and 12-string guitars, ukuleles, bass, mandolins, banjos, etc.) Download a whole bunch of new tunings from our curated tunings library. Access music news, our latest updates, and tips on how to improve your playing. Create custom instruments such as a charango, guitalele, bandurria, baglama or anything else with geared pegs. Turn off and on Roadie’s beep and vibration function depending on your needs. Band Industries came out of a need for its two co-founders to tune less and play more. Our company’s first product, the Roadie Tuner, was launched in 2013 and garnered worldwide attention and accolades including the TechCrunch Disrupt 2014 Audience Choice Award. Not ones to sit by when they could be innovating, we followed Roadie up with Roadie 2 in 2017 and then Roadie Bass in 2018. Bands began touring and recording using our tools which was pretty powerful to see. Since then and while we were hard at work on Roadie 3, we have released several interesting apps for musicians: Roadie Tuner, Roadie Music Recorder, Roadie Bass Tuner, and Ukulele by Roadie. We will keep up on all these projects but it is now finally time for our next milestone with the release of Roadie 3! By some chance of luck, fate, or both we have assembled a group of music-loving professionals who have wonderfully complementary talents. Due to an additional unfathomable plot twist, we all also really like one-another so plan to keep working on fun projects together to benefit the musical community. People also seem to like what we make together so that's always nice too. That's us at work (from home) You might be wondering why a brand like Roadie with products already in the market would still need to turn to Kickstarter to launch a new version of their product? The most important benefit we get from being on Kickstarter is the feedback that we receive from this community of curious, passionate, and progressive musicians. When we share our designs, features, and progress with the Kickstarter backers we get perspective far greater than the sum of our own. This is the best way we have found to create something that will be impactful for a great many musicians. When we first started off, there were so many tuners out there and getting the word out seemed as hard as inventing Roadie! We truly do not believe we would have gotten this far without the support and close communication of our Kickstarter backers. Our team has never been more ready to deliver something next level. We are once again asking for your passion, critique, and pledge in order to help us bring Roadie 3 to life. Press For press inquiries, please reach out at pr@bandindustries.com Warranty & Shipping We will be shipping worldwide! Free shipping to the US. All our Roadie products come with a 30-day money-back guarantee and a 1-year warranty.

title: Sonant Sound API
description: User-Friendly front-end: https://www.sonant.co Computer-Friendly back-end: https://www.sonant.io Sonant, Who and What...? What? Sonant is a platform independent language translator and synthesizer. It offers the robustness of Amazon Polly and Google Cloud Speech, but with no steep learning curve. Who? My name's Andy, and I'm a full stack developer. Recently, a few of my co-workers from the EU arrived in the US and we've been teaching each other our languages. As developers are wont to do, instead of doing these language tasks, we argued on the best way to plan said tasks. True to programmer form, we made no group progress, but individually we all came up with some really good ideas. At home, after relating the day's experience to my better half, she asked why I couldn't just magic up a tool to meet everyone's requirements (not to dishearten her, without her I'd be content living off Ramen and pop-tarts, and would probably bathe less). Her distance from the situation gave very good insight; why not build a tool that each of these people could use? When, Why? When? Well, now. The alpha build meets nearly all the preliminary requirements: An authentication layer Storage Fairly simple SSR front end Fairly complex back end A data layer All that's missing is a few key phonetics, funding to cover hosting, domains, and some of the required services. Why? This is a cool project. Once complete, this tool will be able to speak any character or word in any (available) language. You can build note card apps to help learn language, build anonymous translation tools that don't store personal data, build a multi-language collaboration app, build a multi-language auto-translating chat app, all with an easy to install, one-and-done service with gracious free and hobby tiers, and fair enterprise pricing.

title: Spend Ninja
description: Money is Personal. Your Financial Tools Should Be Too. We all have a vision of a rich life. Maybe it’s the freedom to take risks without living paycheck to paycheck, the security of knowing your bills are covered, or the ability to say yes to experiences without guilt. But for too long, financial apps have been one-size-fits-all—rigid budgets, outdated templates, and unrealistic expectations of a stable, predictable income. That’s not real life. That’s not your life. We built Spend Ninja to give people a financial tool that actually understands them. We’re here to strip away the stress, guilt, and shame around money and replace it with clarity, control, confidence, and maybe even a little fun. 👋 Who We Are We’re a team of real people from the USA, Mexico, Brazil, Estonia and beyond—people who have faced financial stress firsthand. Some of us grew up in households where money was tight. Others navigated the instability of freelance work. Some of us took the traditional 9-to-5 route. We took our knowledge, our concerns, and our wishes and put them into an app. We believe that financial well-being isn’t about cutting out your daily coffee run or obsessing over spreadsheets. It’s about having a system that adapts to you , not the other way around. We are a team who have spent years working in FinTech companies, startups, and even as freelancers. We have poured our expertise into Spend Ninja; making managing money feel human again. 💡 The Origin Story In 2015, our founder realized that traditional budgeting apps were not made for freelancers, gig workers, and small business owners. As a freelance technical consult himself, Johnathon soon realized he would have to create his own system for managing his money. So he came up with a spreadsheet that he could use to track his income and expenses on a daily basis. Using a cashflow runway (similar to what businesses use), Johnathon could now see ahead into his financial future. By using this runway method, he would have days, weeks, or even months to correct his spending or win more contracts to cover his personal expenses. Fast-forward 10 years and we are; we have moved away from the spreadsheet and into an app! 💰 Why We Care Because money stress is real. Because financial literacy isn’t something we’re all taught, and it’s costing people their dreams, their mental health, and their futures. Because we want to contribute to a generation that feels in control of their financial decisions, not intimidated by them. Every day, our team studies the behaviors, struggles, and habits of different financial personas to make Spend Ninja truly inclusive. Whether you’re a gig worker, a startup founder, a content creator, or someone navigating an unstable job market, we see you —and we’re building for you. 🛠️ What We're Building Spend Ninja is more than a budgeting app—it’s a personal finance companion designed to remove the guesswork from managing money. Here’s what makes it different: ✅ A Financial Forecast, Not Just a Budget – Spend Ninja predicts your bank balance for the next 90 days, giving you real insight into your financial future. ✅ No More Surprises – Our intelligent system detects recurring expenses, subscription charges, and variable income trends so you always know where your money is going. ✅ Built for the Real World – Unlike traditional apps that assume steady paychecks, Spend Ninja adapts to changing income patterns, side hustles, and gig work. ✅ Smart Spending Buckets – Organize your money the way you think about it, whether it’s saving for rent, travel, or investing in your dreams. ✅ Seamless Bank Integration – Securely sync your accounts to get a full-picture view of your finances, powered by Plaid and Finicity. Personal Wallets Your money should work the way you do. Create multiple wallets to organize your finances however you like: Keep track of different income sources separately Set savings goals for each wallet (travel, rent, emergency fund, etc.) See spending patterns across different areas of your life Get personalized 90-day financial forecasts for each wallet Shared Wallet Easily manage shared expenses with others: Perfect for couples, roommates, or families See real-time balance updates for everyone involved Track shared expenses and split costs effortlessly Keep a clear, shared transaction history Get financial forecasts for group expenses Business Wallet Ideal for freelancers, entrepreneurs, and gig workers who need to keep business and personal finances separate: Track business income and expenses in one place Get automatic spending reports to simplify bookkeeping See 90-day forecasts for business revenue and costs Categorize transactions to prepare for tax season Core Features for All Wallets 90-day financial predictions – Know what’s coming before it happens Automatic transaction categorization – No more manual sorting Real-time balance updates – Always stay in the know Smart spending alerts – Catch unusual spending before it catches you Custom budgeting options – Organize money in a way that makes sense to you Secure bank connections – Sync accounts safely through Plaid and Finicity Visual spending insights – Clear, easy-to-understand analytics Easy exports for taxes and accounting – No more scrambling at tax time Why Spend Ninja Stands Out 3 Ways We Make Tracking Your Spending Fun Budget Balancing Quests Embark on a journey to master the art of budgeting. Complete daily spending challenges to earn ninja stars and unlock budgeting achievements. The more precise your budget, the more stars you can earn! Bill Payment Adventure Turn paying bills into a fun challenge! Earn Ninja belts to level-up and ninja weapons like swords; by consistently paying bills on time. The more bills you conquer, the greater your financial hero status! Financial Curveball Challenges Prepare for the unexpected! Encounter surprise financial events in a gamified simulation. Successfully navigate these challenges to earn resilience badges and fortify your financial strategy. 🌎 The Impact We Can Create Together Financial stress is real, and too many people feel lost when managing money. We’re here to change that—by building tools that empower, not intimidate. Together, we can push back against a system that prioritizes spending over understanding. By joining this journey, you're helping create a world where financial conversations are open, honest, and judgment-free. 🚀 Why Kickstarter? Your support helps us take Spend Ninja to the next level. Here’s what your backing will fund: Smarter AI Forecasting – More accurate predictions for your financial future. Stronger Infrastructure – Secure, seamless bank integrations and a scalable platform. Exceptional Support – Real help from real people when you need it. Feature Expansion – Automated savings, deeper insights, and more. Kickstarter isn’t just about funding—it’s about building a community of people who believe in a better way to manage money. People like you . 📅 Timeline & Milestones March 2025: Beta testing with early adopters April 2025: Launch of core features and web application June 2025: Release of advanced forecasting features September 2025: Mobile app launch 🎁 Exclusive for Kickstarter Backers We want our earliest supporters to be part of our journey from day one. That’s why Kickstarter backers will get: 🔹 1-Year or Lifetime Access to Spend Ninja before anyone else 🔹 Early Feature Access – Be the first to test new tools and improvements 🔹 Influence Product Development – Share feedback directly with our team 🔹 Exclusive Community Access – Join our members-only Slack group 🔹 A Direct Line to Our Team – Get insights, updates, and behind-the-scenes access Join Us in Redefining Personal Finance With your support, we can make financial clarity accessible to everyone —not just the lucky few. Thank you for considering backing our project. We hope that very soon Spend Ninja can help you take control of your financial future and achieve the peace of mind you deserve.

title: Categories
description: My goal is simple: to better the lives of those living with celiac disease through a mobile app called Bluesponge. Below I'll talk about who I am, why I'm doing this, the features of the app, how it will benefit the celiac community, where the funding will be going and the target date of launch. If you have any questions, I'll be checking the comments section frequently...perhaps even obsessively ;-) Thank you. Why the name Bluesponge? Fair question. When you have celiac disease, you need to be ungodly careful of cross-contamination, especially in the kitchen. One tiny crumb is all it takes to set off the autoimmune response. So in our home, we always had two sponges at our sink. A pink or yellow one for general use. And a blue sponge that was never to touch any gluten. Whenever we had guests, we'd always say "Don't touch the blue sponge!" And a mobile app name was born. Scott Hayes, web developer & digital entrepreneur. But within the celiac and gluten-free communities I'm known as Gluten Dude , a popular, passionate and very outspoken celiac disease advocate. I was diagnosed with celiac disease in 2008 and I launched my blog in 2011. In the 8 years since, I've written over 700 articles, received over 33,000 comments and it's been visited over 9 million times. I've helped a lot of people along the way, both publicly and privately and will continue to do so via the mobile app. I've got a wonderful, spirited following on my blog and also on social media (over 50k followers). Here are a few kudos from my fellow celiacs: "Thank you from the bottom of my heart. You changed the journey for me." "You make me smile, you make me cry, and you make me mad. But most of all, you make me grateful that I don’t have to figure this all out on my own." "You were a life line for me when I was bewildered, brain foggy and...sick. Thank you." "I literally bawled my eyes out reading your blog because someone finally was being honest and understood and wrote the truth. So thank you so much!" "I Googled “f**k celiac” and found my way to your blog. Thanks for blazing the trail. My life is better for your efforts." "Your latest post is why my son wants to be Gluten Dude when he grows up." >> More testimonials here I've also written a book that has sold very well (4.5 stars on Amazon) about the trials and tribulations of living with celiac disease. Yep...that's me on the cover below. My point here is not to brag. It's to show that I have a great connection with the celiac community, I'm a trusted voice and I know I can help people live better lives thru this mobile app. Celiac disease is a serious autoimmune disease, affecting 1 in every 133 people. When someone with celiac disease eats gluten, it sets off the autoimmune response and damages the villi of the small intestine (see image below). When the villi are damaged, the body cannot absorb nutrients and becomes malnourished, which can lead to a bevy of health issues, including osteoporosis, infertility, anemia, other autoimmune diseases -- it can even lead to the start of neurological diseases and certain cancers. There is NO CURE for celiac disease and the only treatment is a 100% gluten-free diet FOR LIFE. A mobile app built specifically to help and empower those with celiac disease and anybody else who eats gluten-free for health reasons. The idea of the app is to create a close-knit community like I have on my blog where people come to find safe places to eat, to discover, to learn, to share, and to connect, all to make their lives easier, better and healthier. Keep on reading to see what specific problems the app will solve. Ah...the million dollar question. Let's tackle them one at a time. PROBLEM > EATING OUT SAFELY: Eating out is one of the more challenging and stressful parts of having celiac disease. One crumb is all it takes to set off the autoimmune response. Everything that is fun and spontaneous about eating out can be overshadowed by the anxiety of whether you can get through your meal unscathed. There is currently a well-known restaurant directory app on the market geared toward the gluten-free community. But the problem is that most of the listings are not 100% gluten-free, putting the health of the community at risk. SOLUTION: A directory of only 100% gluten-free restaurants, guaranteeing a safe meal, and allowing the community to eat out without fear. Each listing will include a description of the establishment, pictures, reviews, ratings, directions, etc. The user will be able to search by their current location or another location. There will also be a way for the establishment to connect with their audience directly (via notifications, beacon technology, etc.) The app will definitely encompass the US and Canada and if I can raise enough funds here on Kickstarter, it will be world wide. PROBLEM > FINDING GLUTEN-FREE BEER: Beer, sadly, is not gluten-free. The most popular "gluten-free" beers on the market today are actually "gluten-removed" and are not safe for those with celiac disease. There are a handful of amazing 100% gluten-free beers out there nowadays, but because their distribution is limited, they can be really difficult to find. I can't tell you how many stores I've walked into fingers crossed, only to come out empty handed. SOLUTION: This app will show the stores, bars and restaurants where people can purchase gluten-free beer. The user will be able to search by their current location, another location and/or by brand.  I have a good relationship with the gluten-free beer companies and they will be sending me updated data on a monthly basis, which will be loaded into the app. PROBLEM > FINDING A VARIETY OF MEALS TO COOK: While keeping a kitchen safe from cross-contamination is challenging enough, finding the time to plan and cook meals adds to the mix. Many people with celiac disease tend to come back to the same meals over and over again. Variety is the spice of life and someone with celiac disease should not have to settle for less. SOLUTION: Recipes and meal plans. We'll cull thousands of recipes across the web from some of our favorite celiacs and (with permission and credit/link to their website) add them to the app. The user will be able to search by meal type (breakfast/lunch/dinner/dessert), by other allergens (nut/egg/dairy/etc.) and by time of preparation. We'll also offer 7 day meal plans to make it simple to plan your week in advance. PROBLEM > CELIAC CAN BE AN ISOLATING DISEASE: Celiac disease is a very misunderstood disease and can be quite isolating. I've received so many emails over the years from people whose own friends and family don't take their disease seriously; who don't get invited out to eat because they're "too difficult"; who are bullied at school simply because they can't eat gluten through no fault of their own. And most of the time, there is no one there to turn to, to listen to you, to understand you. SOLUTION: Live chat. Nobody understands the celiac journey quite like the people who live it. This app will connect people in the celiac community, allowing them to have both private one-on-one and group conversations, with each other and with me. I'll be a very active member on the app. Users will even have the option of providing their zip code, allowing people to search for others by location. Imagine traveling to a new town and not knowing anybody. Pull up the app, search your zip code and make a new friend! PROBLEM > GETTING BAD ADVICE OR NO ADVICE AT ALL: The internet is littered with falsehoods, outright lies and dangerous information regarding celiac disease and eating gluten-free. People are looking for answers to their problems, quickly and accurately, and they are not sure where to turn or who to trust, putting their health in jeopardy. SOLUTION: A forum run by the most trusted voices in the celiac community, including me. I'll create a number of topics where people can chime in with questions, answers and advice. The forum will be heavily moderated so only the right information is getting out there. There are too many celiac Facebook groups that post dangerous information and scare tactics. This forum will stick to the facts, allowing people to get the answers they need...and deserve. Two words: Research & Development Research: I have already accumulated a list of over 1,500 dedicated gluten-free establishments, thanks to the amazing celiac community chiming in. But there is much research to be done still. I will need to hire two people full time to 1) Keep searching for any and all establishments that we don't have already; 2) Call all of the establishments to verify they are indeed 100% gluten-free and ask the owners to send a brief description of their establishment plus a photo of their choice; and 3) Organize all of the data so it can be easily exported to the app. This will be an ongoing process from the time we start until the day we launch. So the estimated cost for research is $12,000. Development: I have spoken to a large number of mobile app development companies and have chosen one that stood head and shoulders above the rest in all aspects (business, design, development, deployment). Their final estimate is between $25,000 and $38,000, dependent on several variables. Based on the above, the final budget to build the mobile app is between $37,000 and $50,000. The more funds I can raise thru Kickstarter, the more resources I can put into the app, which will result in a wider database of gluten-free establishments and a quicker launch time. If the goal is reached, about 9% of all funds received gets taken off the top in both Kickstarter and credit card fees. One last note. With Kickstarter, it's an all-or-nothing deal. If the goal is not reached in the 30 day time-frame, your pledge will be returned to you. If it is reached, your credit card will be charged at the end of the project and we're off to the races. I am going to push hard for an end of December launch. It would be a nice way to start the new year for the celiac community.
