title: McQuaidFrame: Vertical Crop Guides for Your Camera
description: What Is McQuaidFrame? McQuaidFrame is a Screen Protector PLUS . Each screen has markings printed for vertical 9:16 and square 1:1 crops allowing you to properly frame your shots in the moment and not in the edit bay. Printed Vertical and Square Markings I'm Denver McQuaid, a LA based VFX & Motion Graphics artist who's been creating social media videos on Instagram and Musical.ly with over 39,000 followers. I always struggled knowing what my square and vertical crops were on set. I needed a solution but there weren't any. So we made it. Crystal Clear Glass Design Square and Vertical Crops McQuaidFrame comes in several designs for a range of cameras. Each version has been hand measured for precise markings to benefit you the most while filming. Cameras are not a one size fits all cookie-cutter world and neither is McQuaidFrame . Reuse or Recycle Our high quality tempered glass is made to last, if you want it to. Tempered Glass Material, Prototype Design If you're more of a ' use it when I need it' kind of person that's totally cool! McQuaidFrame is 100% recyclable. Yay Nature! Why Did We Make It? With social media platforms like Instagram, Snapchat, Musical.ly, and Facebook favoring vertical videos and ads, the way we record must adapt. If you've ever tried to crop a normal video into a vertical sequence you know the problems you can face; cutting off most of the video or adding those dreadful black bars. Vertical screens cut off upwards of 60% of your normal video The Pros Need It, so You Do Too I had the idea came while interning on a large commercial shoot in Los Angeles, California. Our cast and crew exceeded 200 talented members including an Oscar Winning Cinematographer . So what was the problem? The final commercial needed to work horizontally and vertically for Instagram but the crew had no way of telling where their vertical crops were. They shot extra wide as a safe "buffer" for vertical instead of getting the exact shots they wanted. McQuaidFrame fixes that. What Cameras are Supported? We currently have select models but plan to expand to a more robust line of cameras with your contributions. Sony: a6000/a6300/a6500 ( Stretch Goal $20,000 : a7 series including the new a7 iii) Canon: 5D mark iii, 5D mark IV, Rebel T7i, T6i, T6s, T5i, T4i, (800D, 750D, 700D, 80D, 70D), 7D mark ii, m3 ( Stretch Goal $25,000: Canon Cinema Series) Panasonic: GH5, GH5s ( Stretch Goal $17,500: GH4) Stretch Goal $32,000: Introduce a Nikon line What if my camera isn't listed? We are adding new camera models to the line up as we progress forward. If we exceed our campaign goal , the extra money will be used to make more camera lines. Let us know what camera model(s) you have and we can let you know what's next in development! We look forward to expanding our product line as the company grows. The Team What You Get Rewards because we love you <3 Why do you Need my Money? Creating a product that has never been done before is a bit resource intensive. My partner and I have invested to get the ball rolling and prototyping various designs. Your contributions help us bring McQuaidFrame to life in a much bigger scale. The goal is to expand to dozens of different models for each camera out there. Look! A millennial with a check book! The most popular cameras get priority treatment but we want to branch out to other models that have their own faithful user base. We need enough capital to foot the production bill before the new lines can be available for purchase. Why Kickstarter? Kickstarter provides several benefits for us. First being an online outlet for us to reach potential contributors like yourself on a global scale. Second, Kickstarter has developed its own name brand, sense of quality, and spunky start up vibe that people have come to know and relate to. Basically if the project is on Kickstarter, it must be serious. And dammit we're serious.

title: Categories
description: How We Got Started When Maddox wanted to sign up for the 2015 football season, our mother was worried that he would get a head injury because of news articles she had recently read. Eventually, we made a deal with her where we would build something to monitor head hit impacts. In exchange, our mother would allow Maddox to play football. With the help of Arduino classes and meet-ups at San Jose TechShop, we designed our prototype and quickly realized that many players like Maddox could benefit from this. We spent most of 2015 and 2016 developing our design. In 2006, we did a Kickstarter campaign for our 1st generation of the design. We fine-tuned the design in 2017 for 2nd generation design, and have made first batch of initial production. Feel free to watch the HD version of the video below (on Youtube). What is the Aegis of Soteria Product The Sensor Based on research done by the Department of Physics and Astronomy at University of Rochester, the Department of Neuroscience at Columbia University, and the Department of Organismal Biology and Anatomy at University of Chicago (see this research paper here ), an evolution of g-force from hit impacts happens in milliseconds and the threshold of a concussion happens around 200g (see figure below from above quoted paper): The AoS400 sensor is a specially designed sensor module that can be easily attached to the helmet, with a customized accelerometer chip that can measure +/- 400g. When a player absorbs an impact in a potentially dangerous way (currently set at 50% of concussion threshold, using both Severity index and Head Injury Criterion), the sensor lights up to alert the player. The Mobile Application The impact data can be downloaded to smartphones on the sideline, using Bluetooth Low Energy, so coaches and parents can monitor the behavior. Coaches and parents can add players to their smartphone app (with permission granted), so they can monitor any given player's situation. Each player also has a designated guardian with whom he/she share such information and alarms. The Cloud-Based Data Storage and More The data is also uploaded to the database on AWS cloud. Not only can parents remotely monitor players' situation, records of their hit impact can be used by medical staff to diagnose the players' symptoms. If we collect enough players' data over a long enough period of time, with enough volunteers' permission, we hope to collaborate with engineering and medical schools from universities to build a model that can better predict how hit impacts will lead to long-term brain damage. With such a prediction model, we will be able to perform real-time predictions and preventions. Patent We have filed US patent earlier this year regarding our cloud-based approach Where We Are We have manufactured the first batch of initial production (see this link on where all those components are coming from). This is our production site: And here is our products: We have built the Android version of the app, and plan to port to iSO down the road. How You Can Help We are young entrepreneurs, but we have big dreams and we are growing our skills to make it happen. We need your help to transfer our prototype into production. We pledge to keep you updated on our progress on our website . If you are in the bay area, we are happy to meet you to get your advice and help. We have authorized adults with thorough experience in semiconductor industries to help us make sure everything is running along smoothly with no complications.

description: JoyDuo is the world's smallest hub and stand ever designed that works with 13, 15, 16-inch MacBook Pro 2017~2019 to massively expand its connectivity for higher efficiency and productivity while sturdily elevating it to a more comfortable height and keeping your desk clean and tidy. JoyDuo's outstanding advantages over other MacBook Pro Hub : Last much longer - Interlocking design hides the plugs from scratches and snaps Super small and portable - Weighs only 3.2oz and fits on the palm of your hand No overheat at all - Does not cover any vents Fast Internet Connection - Includes a 1000mbps Gigabit Ethernet port JoyDuo is the world’s first hub with a groundbreaking interlocking mechanism that after disengaging, snap tightly into the USB-C ports on both sides of your MacBook Pro to add a plenitude of ports while charging it at max. 100W and moreover, to sturdily elevate it for a more comfortable typing and viewing experience and better heat ventilation. It interlocks the same way as a puzzle cube that packs down small while hiding the plugs inside, enabling you to enjoy easy access to all your favorite ports anytime, anywhere. It is exclusively designed to work with MacBook Pro, various models from 13-inch to 16-inch released in 2017~2019. JoyDuo allows you to simultaneously connect Thunderbolt 3 USB-C, USB-C, USB-A, HDMI, Ethernet, and 3.5mm audio while keeping your MacBook Pro fast-charged at max. 100W. It saves your money, helps you stay organized and transforms your laptop into a fully-fledged work and entertainment station. All the most used ports are located in the back of the JoyDuo so the plugged devices and cables are hidden away from your sight to keep your desk clutter-free. JoyDuo attaches to your MacBook Pro so securely that there is no flaky connection even when all the ports are occupied. The surfaces on the JoyDuo that are in contact with your laptop and the desk are coated with fine silicon to keep scratches and wobbles away. Your MacBook Pro stay put on JoyDuo when you are locked into an intense online battles or hammering out an essay. There are two ports on JoyDuo for you to establish connections to the Thunderbolt 3 on your MacBook Pro . Vastly superior functions of power delivery, data transfer and video output can be achieved through the connections to these two ports. They can deliver power in both directions. They are capable of charging your MacBook Pro at up to 100W or alternatively, can charge a device at up to 15W. They can be used to connect to a dock or display and charge your MacBook Pro simultaneously so you will never have to worry about power drain. They transfer data at blazing-fast speed of up to 40Gb/s, which is eight times faster than USB 3. You can copy tons of HD video, photos or songs in less than one minute. They enable your MacBook Pro to support up to 5K@60Hz perfect high-resolution display, allowing you to enjoy immersive visual experience on multiple monitors of Macbook / Mac / iMac. You can also plug into display using the 4K@30Hz HDMI port on JoyDuo to get a stunning image on TV / Screen / Projector. JoyDuo features one USB-C and three USB-A 3.0 to connect to a variety of USB peripheral devices such as flash drive, hard drive, keyboard, mouse, printer, camera, smartphone, MP3 player and more. They allow data transmission between your MacBook Pro and the devices at up to 5Gb/s while offering max. 10W (5V/2A) power output to the devices. (USB-C is a charging port only.) JoyDuo is equipped with a Gigabit Ethernet port that allows your MacBook Pro to benefit from more stable and faster Internet connection up to 1000Mbps. The JoyDuo comes with a 3.5mm headphone jack. Just plug in your headphones or speakers and you're ready to bask in a paramount sound effect. JoyDuo combines a highly functional Type-C hub with a laptop stand and is exclusively designed for MacBook Pro 2017/2018/2019. It offers three USB 3.0, HDMI, RJ45, Type-C (data transmission) and two Type-C (Thunderbolt 3) connections. It supports 100W power delivery, high-resolution 4K@30Hz HDMI output, full-performance Thunderbolt 3 (video, data & power) and Gigabit RJ45 Ethernet connections. It is small, stylish and portable. We are JoyDuo team, we wish to portray our products in the most sincere and simple way and bring brand new user experiences to the customers with superior designs. JoyDuo features a unique interlocking mechanism specially designed for MacBook Pro to work as a hub and stand integrated into one, to expand connections as well as saving space. The test shows that the WiFi signal is full bar and the connection is strong and unaffected after JoyDuo is plugged onto the MacBook Pro. description: Stretch Goals $120k Flooding-Mesh Protocol : the Duo can join as a node and also be a Wi-Fi gateway for the whole mesh network . Please check our Update #4 for more information and how to help us & our backers to reach the goal. $50K Python Support : we will add a Python interpreter $100K Dev Apps : for both Android & iOS, you will be able to send Arduino / JavaScript / Python code to Duo via Wi-Fi or Particle Cloud Introduction The Duo is a thumb-size development board designed to simplify the process of building Internet of Things (IoT) products. The Duo provides everything you need—Wi-Fi, BLE and a powerful Cloud backend, all in a compact form factor that makes it ideal for your first prototype, a finished product, and everything in between. We’re also introducing the RBLink, an expansion board for the Duo that allows you to attach additional sensors and modules without any soldering. You'll have all the tools you need to get your prototype up and running in no time. Wi-Fi + BLE The Duo is your Bluetooth gateway to the Internet of Things. It allows you to communicate with your projects both over the web and with your phone, enabling a wide range of cool interactions. Would you like to build a front door lock that can be opened using a phone and whose settings can be changed wirelessly to give access to additional people? Maybe connected lights that turn on as your phone comes in range but can also be controlled when you’re miles away from home? Wi-Fi + BLE also means you can do make your wearable products extra interesting by adding things like beacons and data collection. Oh and robots! Whether you’re in the same room or halfway around the world, the Duo gives you all the connectivity you’ll need to control, interact, monitor, and play with your awesome new creations. Wi-Fi & BLE Bridge Demo RedBear Duo connects with eight BLE Peripherals as BLE Central and iPhone via Wi-Fi, background information available on our website. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Features There are a few things that are important to all connected devices—here are a few details: Connectivity: How does the Duo communicate with the outside world? The Duo contains both Wi-Fi and BLE capabilities. This means your project can communicate locally with Bluetooth enabled devices and can also connect to your local Wi-Fi network to interact with anything else on the web. The Duo is built around the Broadcom BCM43438, a Wi-Fi 802.11b/g/n plus Bluetooth 4.1 (Dual Mode) combined chipset. They share the same 2.4GHz antenna and can run at the same time. This gives you the flexibility to utilize the most suitable wireless technology(s) for your project. Development: How do I program the Duo? The Duo gives you four development platforms/languages to choose from: Arduino IDE - The most popular development platform used by makers, it's easy to learn and comes with plenty of references guides. Particle Web IDE, Dev and CLI - A development platform that allows you to program your Duo online and wirelessly.  You can be miles away and still be able to do your tweaking of your Duo. JavaScript - Yes. If you know JavaScript already, you are ready to go! We are porting an open source JavaScript interpreter for microcontroller to the Duo. You could control all the I/Os, Wi-Fi and BLE connections with JavaScript only and without the need of compilation. Broadcom WICED SDK - An advanced development platform where you can do low-level tweaking.  With the WICED SDK, Duo can support Classic Bluetooth which you can build audio or streaming data devices. If you have Apple MFi license, you can even develop your Apple HomeKit project with Duo and WICED. We will also provide sample projects and sample code for both Android and iOS on our website and our GitHub page. Live Programming Demo No special effects: Arduino IDE, JavaScript and Particle WebIDE. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Cloud: What about the Cloud? Thanks to our partner, Particle , every Duo will come out of the box with built-in Cloud service .  With just a few clicks you will be connected to the Cloud seamlessly. Not only that, the Cloud service is FREE for the lifetime of your Duo*.  You can push any changes and data via Cloud right from the start. Add-Ons: How do I use the Duo with modules such as LED lights or sensors? With the RedBear RBLink you can easily attach modules from the Seeed’s Grove System to your project. No need to pull out your soldering iron--just attach your sensors and actuators with jumper wires to the RBLink and you’re ready to go.  Looking to attach your own sensor or peripheral?  The Duo is breadboard and solder friendly, so you’re never limited in what you can connect to the web. Partners We don't work alone.  We connect with other IoT companies to bring Maker community together and better user experiences. BlueKitchen - We are collaborating with BlueKitchen to integrate their BTstack with the Duo. BTstack is BlueKitchen's implementation of the official Bluetooth stack. It is a dual-mode Bluetooth (BLE + Bluetooth Classic) stack, with small memory footprint and available as ANSI-C source code. BTstack is free for non-commercial use. Blynk - You will enjoy 50% discount on a single purchase of Blynk paid services when they're live. Blynk is a the most user-friendly mobile app constructor for the Internet of Your Things, working seamlessly with the Duo. Blynk helps you simply snap together an amazing interface from various widgets, upload the example code to the Duo and enjoy seeing first results in under 5 minutes! It works perfectly for newbie makers and saves tons of time for those who know how to code already. Particle - You can use all Particle online tools and cloud messaging service with Duo free of charge* for development and prototyping. The Duo is pin and sketch compatible with Particle's Photon, you could run your existing code with no/little change or simply add in the BLE code if you want BLE functions. In addition, all shields/add-ons for Photon should work with the Duo too. Particle offers a suite of software tools to help you prototype, scale, and manage your Internet of Things products. These tools make your Duo cloud ready right out of the box. (* Inclusion of the RedBear Duo in commercial products is not covered by these terms and will require a $2/device fee to Particle unless otherwise negotiated.) Seeed - Our integration partner on the RBLink board and the creators of the Grove System to keep prototyping quick and solder-less. Seeed will also manufacture all our Duo and RBLink boards. Seeed is a hardware innovation platform for makers to grow inspirations into differentiating products.  Seeed helps makers to productize their ideas/prototypes using their in-house engineering, supply chain management and agile manufacture forces. Apple HomeKit Support You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 The Broadcom WICED SDK supports HomeKit and makes it easy to integrate instant connectivity with HomeKit into product prototypes. (**MFi license is required) Open Source Software : All libraries and code by us will be open sourced, we have already published some early code and information for beta testers on our GitHub page . Design : The final hardware design of the Duo will be made publicly available. Hardware Duo STMicroelectronics STM32F205 ARM Cortex-M3 @120MHz, 128 KB SRAM and 1MB Flash Broadcom BCM43438 Wi-Fi 802.11n (2.4GHz only) + Bluetooth 4.1 (Dual Mode) combo chip On-board 16 Mbit (2 MB) SPI Flash Integrated chip antenna with the option to connect external antenna 18 I/O pins RGB status LED Small single-sided PCB for easy mounting on other PCB boards Duo comes with headers soldered 20.5mm x 39mm RBLink Running ST-LINK/V2 Broadcom WICED SDK for Internet of Things compatible Apple MFi authentication coprocessor support (**MFi license is required) USB MSD interface - enabling programming Duo by drag and drop of bin file USB CDC Virtual Serial port STM32 ST-LINK Utility software compatible 8x Seeed Grove compatible connectors 53.5mm x 53.5mm Grove Starter Kit Grove Base Shield Button Buzzer Rotary Angle Sensor Temperature Sensor Light Sensor Chainable RGB LED 3-Axis Digital Accelerometer (±1.5g) 4 Digit Display Vibration Motor User's Manual Rewards You are able to pledge for a variety of combinations of rewards. We'll be delivering a small first batch of rewards in January with the rest to follow one month later. The Duo is already tested and functional and we are confident that the first batch of boards will be ready to ship to backers in January 2016. Extra Pledge If you're interested in getting more of an item, or creating a combination of boards not listed in the rewards section you can make a larger pledge to get additional quantity of items. Simply add the extra corresponding costs to your total pledge amount: Duo - add $22 each RBLink - add $11 each Grove Starter Kit - add $43 each Early Bird pledges will have priority in terms of delivery. We will try to include all pledged items in one shipment, but the extra pledged items are subject to production availability.  If we need to make more than one shipment, we will bear the shipping cost of all additional shipments. Shipping & Delivery We have tried to keep this very simple and affordable. We will ship ANY QUANTITY, ANYWHERE in the world for  $5! That’s right, any quantity! After the campaign has ended, we will send you a survey form to fill in the delivery and contact information. The shipping address can be different from your billing address. RedBear or RedBearLab We are the same team that developed nine IoT boards under the name RedBearLab . We are changing our name from RedBearLab to RedBear and we will slowly migrate to a new domain redbear.cc. We’ve decided to go on Kickstarter to help bring RedBear to more makers all over the world. We are also excited about the feedback and activeness of the Kickstarter community. Thank You Graphics and video: Ernest Chong & Seadog Yu Background music: http://www.bensound.com All our partners. A special thanks to the Particle team! Everyone who helped us with our campaign. Last but not least, our supporters and backers. ---------- Apple and HomeKit are trademarks of Apple Inc., registered in the U.S. and other countries. Broadcom and WICED are among the trademarks of Broadcom Corporation and/or its affiliates in the United States, certain other countries and/or the EU. Any other trademarks or trade names mentioned are the property of their respective owners.

title: My Brother's CD Album and Tour
description: I want to make my brother's dream possible. He is a magnificent artist and back in Ecuador there are very few opportunities for artists. Even in that reality, he and his friend Daniel have met some goals! They produced their first single with a great producer, it was even mastered in L.A! It was top 10 for a few weeks and top 40 for many weeks on ecuadorian radios. You can find the song on iTunes, Spotify, Apple Music and more. They also made a lyric video: http://bit.ly/gyd-dejenmedormir. The song is called 'Dejenme Dormir'. But this time has shown us a hard reality: some radios ask for expensive “gifts” to play the song in their stations, promoters ask for so much money. Private companies don’t support music, there is no money for music. People who can make differences with their money don’t invest on art and there are no labels looking for any artist. And this kind of things might be able to destroy an honest entrepreneurship. So let's be kind of the label! :D are you in? You won't regret! I have prepared this campaign for you all with a lot of fun rewards you could earn if we build this together! There is a serious project behind this, there is a dream, there is good material and there is you and I! The challenge is to gather funds not only for their next hit. But for their first entire album and tour! If we collect enough money we are going to produce the CD Album. The album will cost like 30 thousand dollars. And then come the videos, the promoters, the marketing campaign and the tour! Lets go on tour with my brother and Daniel! Let’s record their album! We are going to have so much fun!

description: Welcome (or Welcome Back!) to my whimsical world of botanical drawing! I’m Kelly Barone, the artist behind Whimsy by Kelly and a teacher who believes with all my heart that everyone can draw. Over the years I’ve shared that belief with thousands of students through retreats, classes, workshops, and my previous book each one designed to make drawing flowers and plants simple, joyful, and accessible to all. Because of the amazing support for my first Kickstarter, Drawing Botanicals came to life and found its way into homes and studios all over the world. That book gathered together years of teaching experience and showed that with the right guidance, anyone can put pencil to paper and create something beautiful. Now I’m bringing you Drawing MORE Botanicals , a new volume that adds 90 brand new flowers to explore as well as a handful of more IN DEPTH tutorials for when you want to work on something more advanced Every single flower comes with clear step by step instructions as well as a video demonstration (that can be accessed by QR code and also by link) so you can learn in the way that works best for you. I know that everyone learns differently, which is why I have built this book to meet you where you are, whether you prefer to follow along visually, read detailed directions, or jump right into practice pages. You can watch one of the videos from my last book here: This book is already 95% written and prepared, with just some final tweaks and finishing touches and then I'll be having them printed locally right here in Buffalo NY. That means that they will be in my hands speedy quick and shipped in time for the holidays, so it will be in YOUR hands and ready to gift for Christmas. The Kickstarter goal will cover my initial printing of the book, through a local printer and allow me to provide you with some amazing rewards. Whether for yourself, a creative friend, or someone who has always wanted to try drawing but never believed they could, this book will open the door to a world of creativity and botanical beauty. Together, let’s keep growing this joyful community of makers, one flower at a time. A note about: Funding & Stretch Goals You may notice that my goal is not an even "100" number. That IS intentional. I calculated exactly what it will take to responsibly bring Drawing MORE Botanicals to life, including printing costs using a local printer, Kickstarter fees, taxes and the minimum print run required. That brought me to my estimated goal. It is the true baseline needed to make sure every backer receives their book. Once we hit that, every dollar beyond will make the project even better. Here is what you can look forward to unlocking together: $5,375 Funded The book goes to print. Every backer at the book tier or higher is guaranteed their copy. ACHIEVED!!!! $7,500 Printing Upgrade We will move to even thicker, higher-quality paper to make the book a joy to use and will also include embossing on the cover page. ACHIEVED!!!! $9,500 Bonus Botanical Lesson I will add an extra 10 flowers & another tutorial to the book, available for every backer at no additional cost. ACHEIVED!!! $10,500 Signed Bookplates Every backer receiving a book will also receive a hand-signed adhesive bookplate. This is a personal thank you that makes your copy unique. $15,000 Community Celebration We will celebrate together with an exclusive live online drawing workshop just for backers. This will be a thank you event to mark how far we have come.

title: Categories
description: THE FISHERPOETS ANTHOLOGY: ANCHORED IN DEEP WATER ( Scroll DOWN for photos of the cover artwork ) "[Fisherpoets] might wax about the versatility of a deck bucket or of romance in rubber boots, but they also describe a livelihood that can kill those who pursue it." - NY Times, March 3, 2009 Commercial fishing is an industry in the midst of extreme change. Many of the traditional fisheries of the 20th century have already disappeared due to conflicts over allocation, the degradation of habitat and the advent of technology. Many of the old techniques and methods are gone or are fast drifting out with the tide. The FisherPoets Gathering, an annual event at the end of each February in Astoria, Oregon for the past 17 years, has been a way for fishermen themselves to chronicle these changes and the attending issues and the stories they inspire. The Gathering brings together scores of writers, poets and musicians each year to perform their work celebrating the commercial fishing industry throughout the United States and abroad on the stages of Astoria's taverns, restaurants, museums and art galleries. Covered by such respected publications as the NY Times and Smithsonian magazine, the Gathering continues to entertain and attract audiences because the world it describes is a mythical place for so many people. Creating an anthology of the writings of fishermen and women is to create a unique and significant record of commercial fishing's history and culture. It is one important way to preserve their voices. Patrick Dixon and Chelsea Stephen have edited and designed Anchored in Deep Water: The Fisherpoets Anthology , seven books of original poetry, songs and stories written by commercial fishermen and women who have performed at the Fisherpoets Gathering. This is the first comprehensive collection of fisherpoetry in over a decade. While this printed anthology can only provide a glimpse of the spoken word performances at the event itself, the books catalogue a rich history of the event and of the commercial fishing industry itself. The books are thematically organized: Every Boat Has a Wave deals with risk and survival at sea; Illusions of Separateness deals with the politics and environment of the fishing world; Making Waves is filled with stories by and about women in the fishery; Gathering chronicles the community and camaraderie inherent in commercial fishing; Family Dynamic speaks to the family issues commercial fishing inspires; For the Love of Fish chronicles the reasons fishermen go to sea; and the final book, Mending Holes , which is still in the works, is about the history of commercial fishing. Nearly 40 writers are represented in the anthology, from Massachusetts, Rhode Island and Maine on the east coast to Alaska, Washington, Oregon and California in the west. One poet hails from Hawaii, another from Japan. Several of the writers have (different) stories or poems in multiple books. Each book is 50+ pages long, with original cover designs created by Portland, Oregon artist Chelsea Stephen and photographs by fisher poet and photographer Patrick Dixon. The books will be made available singly or as a complete, "boxed" (more like a sleeve) set. We are seeking funding for the printing and distribution costs of 300 copies of each book (that's 2100 books) as well as 200 sleeves. We are offering fine-art, archival prints as rewards with the books for larger donations. Every Boat Has a Wave: Risk and Survival Illusions of Separateness: Politics & the Environment Making Waves: Women in the industry Gathering: Community and Camaraderie Family Dynamic: Family Issues in the commercial fishing world For the Love of Fish: The Work of Commercial Fishing Mending Holes: The History of commercial fishing

title: The Japanese Domestic Market Library
description: I am a professional Japanese translator and Japanese Car enthusiast. After building a 20B powered FC3S model Mazda RX-7 in 2012, I set my sights on the Eunos Cosmo. I learned Japanese, then I tackled both the Wiring Diagram book and the massive 888 page Workshop Manual. While doing so, I began seeing all of the flaws in books digitized with traditional scanners and even dedicated book scanners. Add in Japanese Kanji, plus poor Photoshop "corrections" and it becomes a nightmare quickly. So I designed my own imaging equipment that eliminates all of these issues. The goal is to create a library of English-language resources for vehicles imported from other countries. Imagine you have a Nissan Skyline and you want a second key just in case you accidentally lock your keys in the car. What do you do? Dealerships in North America are unable to assist because they do not have access to Japanese resources, and most Locksmiths are powerless due to being unable to reference a Japanese Market vehicle in their North American databases. This is where I come in. Cross-referencing and sourcing parts and information between Japanese and North American markets was a necessity with my RX-7. Over the next decade, my reputation for finding "impossible" parts and information grew to where I am sought out worldwide regarding anything from common items such a spare key for a Mazda Familia GT-X, all the way to sourcing transmission filters for the Eunos Cosmo 20B Type E-CCS. With more Japanese Market vehicles being imported everyday, the need for for accurate information to operate and correctly maintain them will only grow. And what better way to accomplish this than with a library?

title: Stormborn: The Complete Trilogy Deluxe Hardcover Omnibus
description: Do you love ... Found family and unbreakable idealism? Ruthless and cunning antagonists? A rebellion rising through darkness? Then dive into the Stormborn Trilogy! An echo of war. A fractured alliance. A looming prophecy… The attack on Ballern caught more than the Skyborn rebels unprepared. Mordair’s sinister agenda has been revealed, leaving countless shattered lives in its wake. He seeks a pact with the Children of the Dark Fire, a secret society that has manipulated the royal line for centuries. And their pact will break the foundation of the alliance. Archibald and Lady Katherine have split their forces between Karn, Ballern, and the East, leaving them vulnerable to Mordair's ruthless strikes. Karn prepares to fight beside the Stormborn, leading the Ancoran rebels, Jacob and Alice, to a nightmarish place known as the Dead Woods. There, the land itself is poisoned, making the air toxic to every creature who dares enter. Secrets lie hidden in an ancient control center, a glimmer of hope in the crushing darkness. But everything comes at a price. The forests will burn. The skies will darken. Amid it all, the Stormborn will rise. THE BOOK Gilded silver edges, die cut dust jacket, gorgeous matte finish, and a foil stamped colored leather cover all protected by a stunning slipcase. Beautifully designed by Murphy Rae with a meticulously illustrated map by Misty Beee, interior art by Enggar Adirasa, and Stormborn emblem by Sean Long. Map artwork headers on the first page of each chapter. Map printed on the opening end papers. Signed by the author. I'm so excited about that die cut dust jacket! What exactly is it? Well, where the map has been "burned away" in the artwork on the dust jacket, that will actually be die cut to reveal the leather and foil cover underneath! It's an amazing effect, and I cannot wait to see it on one of my books. Only 300 sets of this edition will ever be produced! One of four interior illustrations by Enggar Adirasa To celebrate the finale of the Steamborn series, we've collected the final trilogy in a single 6"x9" 980 page omnibus that includes Stormborn, Stormforged, and Stormsworn. If you missed the previous Steamborn special editions, you can grab them in the add-ons, or snag the Deluxe Edition Set of 3 reward! Slipcase, metallic edges, ribbon bookmark, color end papers, and chapter spread examples from Skyborn. Stormborn will maintain these high quality standards. THE REWARDS Stormborn Deluxe Edition Hardcover Gilded silver edges, die cut dust jacket, gorgeous matte finish, and a two-color foil stamped colored leather cover all protected by a stunning slipcase. Signed by the author. Sprayed edges, color end papers, foiling, and chapter header examples from Steamborn. Stormborn will maintain, and surpass, these high quality standards. Deluxe Edition Set of 3 Hardcovers (Steamborn, Skyborn, and Stormborn Special Editions) Complete the set with the first and second trilogy special edition hardcovers. Steamborn features a slipcase, foil stamped dust jacket, foil stamped leather cover, detailed sprayed edges featuring map sections, plus a full cover map printed on the end papers. Skyborn includes many of those same features, with a slipcase, stunning red foil accents, and gilded edges. All signed by the author. Pledge Over Time has been enabled for all pledges over $125.00 USD if you'd like to break up your pledge in installments! THE STRETCH GOALS Officially, we have none. We're going all in on this edition from day one. This includes gilded edges, slipcase, ribbon bookmark, color end papers, four bound pieces of black and white artwork by Enggar Adirasa, custom chapter headers, and more. THE JOURNEY It's been over a decade now since I started writing the Steamborn series, and what a journey it has been. Nine books—three trilogies—born from my love of Studio Ghibli movies, steampunk, and post apocalyptic adventures. I set out to write a story I would have enjoyed when I was younger, and I'm thrilled it resonated with so many readers and listeners. I wouldn't be here without the support of every one of you. Which brings us here, to this monstrous omnibus and a way to celebrate the conclusion of the Steamborn saga. Thank you. THE ADD ONS Maps signed by the author. This is a gallery-quality giclée art print on 100% cotton rag archival paper, printed with archival inks. Available in 8x10 or 12x15. Steamborn map by Misty Beee. Please note, as these are giclée art prints, they do have the usual border of archival paper around the image (approximately one inch in width) so the map graphic is smaller than the full measurement. By popular demand , we recently had slipcases manufactured for the original Steamborn Special Edition Omnibus! If you backed the omnibus without a slipcase in a previous campaign, and want your editions of Steamborn, Skyborn, and Stormborn to match, this is the way. Manufactured to the same standards as the Skyborn slipcase by the same printer. 2 inches (5 cm) wide with 3D emblem, neck ribbon, and embossed text on backside which reads "Through the black, we ride once more." Steamsworn medals. Reminiscent of the medals worn by Charles and the Steamsworn, show your allegiance with this raised 3D medal with an antique silver finish, displayed in a velour box. Emblem design by Sean Long. Did you miss the previous Eric R. Asher campaigns? Interested in grabbing some additional treasures while you're here? Ellie's Park (Cozy Fantasy, Mundane Magic, Fae, Low Stakes, Tasty Treats, Found Family) Mason Dixon: Monster Hunter (Cryptids, Ozark Folklore Monsters, Wildlife Rescue, Educated Sasquatch, Secret Agency, Urban Fantasy) VESIK (Post-Modern Magik, Necromancers, Reluctant Hero, Warrior Fae, High Stakes, Urban Fantasy) Recommended for ages 17+ due to graphic violence and language. THE LETTERED EDITION There will be a maximum of 26 of these, one for each letter of the English alphabet. They will be hand-lettered by me and will include a certificate of authenticity (COA). The book and the COA will also be signed by me. THE AUDIOBOOKS If you're an audiobook fan, don't miss the completed series narrated by Saskia Maarleveld! Available exclusively on Audible, here . Saskia poured some serious heart into this story, and it makes the audio shine. I've been lucky enough to work with some amazing narrators in my career, and she has an ability to bring a story to another level. THE AUTHOR For those of you asking, "But who is this guy?" I'm the author of over 30 titles, including the Vesik urban fantasy series, The Theme Park at the End of the World cozy fantasy, and the steampunk series, Steamborn. I'm currently living in St. Louis, Missouri, where the local folklore often finds its way into my Mason Dixon: Monster Hunter cryptid series. SHIPPING I was able to keep US shipping relatively low by taking advantage of cubic rates. Due to the sheer size and weight of this tome, international shipping is significantly higher than my usual campaigns. The rates here are slightly lower than the actual shipping rates as I'm trying to offset some of the increased costs for backers outside the United States. ~A NOTE ON SHIPPING AND TAXES~ *Shipping fees do not include VAT, customs, or taxes assessed by your state/country. Taxes and fees, including any applicable sales tax, will be collected through the Kickstarter pledge manager AFTER the campaign ends. Base shipping rates. THE TEAM No AI. Every word, every piece of art, and every bit of editing is done by artists and pros in the publishing field (me, my editor, and my team of artists). Cover design and typography: Murphy Rae . Since 2014, Murphy Rae has been designing high-quality book covers for self-published and traditionally published authors. Fel, Stormborn, and Steamsworn emblem: Sean Long . Prolific Midwest illustrator and instructor. Illustrated map: Misty Beee . An artistic cartographer living in the region of Alsace in France and specialized in high quality hand drawn fantasy maps. Interior art: Enggar Adirasa. Illustrator specializing in card games, table top games, mobile games, book covers, and more.

title: She-Preachers:Seven Snapshots of Women & Spiritual Authority
description: She-Preachers: Seven Snapshots of Women and Spiritual Authority NOTE: If I exceed the target funding and reach a stretch goal of $15,000, I will create a 40-day devotional download highlighting snippets of writing from women preachers, which will be delivered to backers of all tiers. WHY THIS? WHY NOW? Why prescribe bite-sized history for leaders who are burned out, exhausted, questioning their beliefs, overwhelmed, or uncertain how best to lead in and through disorientation? Sometimes, we need the uncanny strangeness of historical voices who encountered some similar dynamics to what you're facing this week. And yet, they're removed just enough to be startling, different, fresh - but practical. As I've spent time in the words of diverse women preachers and leaders who've lived and led over the past several hundred years, I've been awe-bound, humbled, encouraged, surprised, and equipped. But I also know you are tired; busy; some of you can barely crack open a book. Your brain is fried from trauma or long Covid or burnout, and you do not have the mental energy to plow through old vocabulary to find the beautiful, bracing wisdom buried in two-hundred-year-old autobiographies. Maybe you need the Hebrews 11-esque testimony and examples of women who practiced public habits of preaching in ways that angered saloon owners, pastors, corrupt politicians, traffickers, those with high social standing and wealth, those without high standing or wealth, pimps, the Klan, occupiers, mobs, misogynists, and sometimes their own families. What you need is a side-door: an accessible way into the testimonies, jaw-dropping stories, and warm faith of women who: Who preached before anyone would ordain them Who prayed their justice work forward Who snipped their bonnet strings and pinned them in place so they'd break away if a mob tried to strangle them for public preaching Whose travel was delayed due to at-will segregation policies on trains Who hobbled over 10 kilometers a day, feet painful from being partially bound when young, visiting villages, praying, and planting churches Who went down and proclaimed God's truth in a coal mine, ankle-deep in coal mud. ("She-preachers," as Pam Durso would remind us, is an old pejorative term a la the 1600s, when some of the early women preachers in England were Quakers and...yes, Baptist.) Lack of status, resources, and at times official recognition by church bodies could not extinguish the spiritual authority evident in their intercession, preaching, writing, or ministry. While men were still debating whether or not women have souls and whether or not it was worthwhile to teach women to read , God was calling women to preach scripture publicly to audiences of both women and men - and women were responding. For some women, it took decades to ease into their calling. For many, their calling caught fire as testimony to sanctification. Some spent time in jail; many faced hostile crowds; some were dismissed on the basis of both sex and race. But as women relented to that pressing sense of call, as they began to stand up and speak up, the Holy Spirit showed up - and they began bearing witness to their obligation to answer to an authority that transcended any mortal walking on earth. There is a difference between recognized status and spiritual authority - as leaders of the Civil Rights movement knew so well; as Mary Dyer, Quaker preacher who was hanged in Boston in 1660 knew so well. With a great deal of fasting and prayer, often enduring physical discomfort, these women sowed grace that grew fruit in the short-term and the long-term. It is not a stretch to say that without the ongoing, enduring preaching of women from around the world, religious freedom would’ve remained stifled far longer; the abolitionist movement would have been weaker; legislative action to protect vulnerable women in England would not have accelerated; the suffragist movement would have taken even longer; people who found themselves in war zones would have suffered far more; and the fallout from physical and substance abuse in rural areas would have gone unconfronted. Technology prioritizes the living, but the living have been through a lot of disorientation. So once again: maybe you need the Hebrews 11-esque testimonies of women who preached in ways that angered saloon owners, pastors, corrupt politicians, traffickers, those with high social standing and wealth, those without high standing or wealth, pimps, the Klan, occupiers, mobs, misogynists, and sometimes their own families. These women angered these entities - and many more - because they were radically focused on God’s truth in scripture; they could not abide distance between their spirits and the Spirit of God if they ignored the pressing conviction to preach; and they would not accept human misery as something an individual deserved who “brought it on themselves,” or as inevitable - as something God predestined or the scope of which was too great to tackle. Their holy boldness fostered moments like the first time a group of small-town Methodists met for racially integrated worship. Their holy boldness helped provoke the uprising of a violent anti-Salvation Army group known as the Skeleton Army. Their holy boldness stopped invasive physical exams of women even suspected to be prostitutes. Their holy boldness told a famous evangelist he needed to be sanctified - and prayed for him to receive it. Their holy boldness prayed for the car tires to last to their next scheduled preaching stop. Their holy boldness helped turn away an attempted Klan bribe at a camp meeting. Their holy boldness met the spiritual needs of people under occupation. Their holy boldness revealed the trafficking of young girls from England. Their holy boldness turned down prestige or ease. Their holy boldness stepped foot on the soil of the nation that had just dropped atomic bombs on their homeland. Their holy boldness advocated for women’s ordination decades before it happened. Their holy boldness resulted in rotten egg and dung being thrown at them, their hotels being threatened, physical assault, and incarceration. Their holy boldness stood up to - and prayed down - a bullying sheriff attempting to shut down a tent meeting. “The world was not worthy of them…” One preacher became very ill and almost died; she had a vivid dream of Death coming for her. In her dream, she argued with Death; Death argued back. Then, she told Death , "No, YOU DARE NOT, I have work to do!" And she got better. Will you join me in amplifying these bold, diverse, brave, anointed voices? Their examples - and their prayers - stand behind us, while shining a light ahead of us. If She-Preachers is fully funded, you'll have your copy in hand before the July daylilies bloom. If funding exceeds the target, I'll create additional materials. Because I have no interest in muting women out of history books, out of classrooms, out of lecture halls. I do, however, have a great deal of interest in amplifying them. And I intend to do so: their testimonies and sermons turned towns, cities, regions, and nations upside-down. Spiritual authority does that. “For in obedience to the will of the Lord God I came, and in his will I abide faithful to the death.” - Quaker preacher Mary Dyer, before her execution in Boston, 1660 I believe their testimonies and examples will fortify seasoned leaders, students, pastors, and the coffee and cookie committee with reader-friendly glimpses engaging the remarkable spiritual authority found in women who lived before us. HOW IS THE BOOK ORGANIZED? I invite you to enter the book wherever your interest lands : wherever you encounter a compelling point of entry, like flipping through a photo album and getting absorbed by a particular image. (If you are sick, or your focus is fractured, this approach enhances accessibility: flip through it and sink in somewhere.) WHICH WOMEN ARE INCLUDED? Out of thousands of women preachers, it’s difficult to highlight only seven! But I think that number keeps this volume accessible. I'm making the final decision on one or two. She-Preachers: Seven Snapshots of Women and Spiritual Authority will amplify the voices and experiences of women from different centuries, nationalities, denominations, races, and backgrounds. I tend to favor featuring women who may not make the "women's history month" bulletin board display every year. Early backers will unlock a supplemental bonus profile. (This book is intended to engage church members or pastors; to browse the kinds of research I've shared and the women I've amplified, I invite you to visit the first season of my weekly FireFall newsletters . Just keep in mind this book is for lay-level engagement, a slightly different style than what you'll find at the link.) UPDATE: Here is a tentative outline, subject to change, I-reserve-the-right-to-change-at-any-time. Yes, there are more than seven, but that's my problem. Introduction: Oral Theologian Afua Kuma’s Jesus of the Deep Forest A snapshot of one woman’s chiefly praise No Typical Vocation: Catholic Order Foundress Mary Ward, 1585-1645 A snapshot of bold authenticity No Mother of Monsters: Quaker Preacher Mary Dyer, executed in Boston, 1611?-1660 A snapshot of non-violent witness No Muted Call: Methodist Preacher Mary Barritt Taft (1772-1851) A snapshot of enthusiastic refusal No Unjust Terms: AME Zion Preacher Rev. Julia A.J. Foote (1823-1900 or 1901) A snapshot of prophetic presence No Cheap Comfort: Anglican Activist Josephine Butler (1828-1906) A snapshot of prayer, speaking truth, and power No Common Will: Methodist Evangelist Amanda Berry Smith (1837-1915) A snapshot of holy tenacity No Hand-Me-Down Faith: Presbyterian Elder Rev. Khaalyát’ (Tillie) Kinnon Paul Tamaree (1864-1955) A snapshot of indigenous voice No Pristine Suitcase: Holiness Preachers Minnie Shelhamer (1867-1902), Rev. Emma Irick (1888-1984), the Booth women, & Rev. Lucia Carmen Garcia de Costa (1903-1984) Snapshots of proclamation on-the-go (sometimes, jailed) No Pain-Free Pilgrimage: Pentecostal Preacher Wong Yen (Mary) Yeung (1888-1971) A snapshot of chronic pain and church planting No Cheap Consecration: Anglican Elder Rev. Dr. (Florence) Li Tim-Oi (1907-1992) A snapshot of faith in the dark night Conclusion: The men who testified to the anointing of God on women who preached with spiritual authority IS THIS AN ACADEMIC HISTORICAL BOOK? While respectfully handling the unique place each of these women occupies in broader movements and moments, this is not academic history book - much as I appreciate them, learn from them, and glean from them. This book is meant to bridge "library archive" life and "gas station" life (see below). I delight in pulling people into stories that will shape them and equip them. I especially enjoy engaging people who think they don't like history or who assume the best wisdom comes from something new. She-Preachers will engage readers through accessible storytelling that highlights and honors the voices of women, while opening space to explore the ways their witness continues to press into today. But She-Preachers is not a free-for-all that will make historians weep into their soup: Stories matter when they honor facts, context, evidence, artifacts, oral history, art, letters, photos, food, place, documents, songs, traditions, architecture, eras, and realities of the real-life people we're talking about. It can be challenging even for career historians not to read some implicit assumptions backward into history. (How easy is it for those with dental care and anesthesia to fully appreciate life without them, for instance?) (For nerds who care: I have an M.A. in Theological Studies, which was heavy on church history, historical theology, and sacramental theology, with some anthropology and contextual theology. I'm relying as much as possible on primary sources, backed up with close-proximity secondary sources. I may pull from some supporting academic research if it happens to flesh out a particular point that's still shaped for accessibility.) WHO ARE YOU AND WHY ARE YOU WRITING SHE-PREACHERS ? I'm Elizabeth Glass Turner. I'm an American writer and editor who remembers library card catalogs and misses the sound of their soft thud. I've pastored a small rural church through revitalization, worked with international students in campus ministry, helped shape the transition of a global Methodist non-profit into a new era of leadership, edited a faith-based online magazine, and served as a spiritual director for cohorts of online MDiv contextual ministries students for United Theological Seminary. For several years, I've helped leaders find, shape, and sharpen their voices. I've developed, line edited, or copy edited full-length manuscripts for experienced authors and new writers alike. I've worked on titles like Live Anointed: How the Holy Spirit Sanctifies Men and Women to Lead Together by Katie Lance, This Holy Calling , a collaborative devotional volume by women in ministry for women in ministry, the original printing of Saints Alive! Thirty Days of Pilgrimage with the Saints by Maxie Dunnam, and more. I contributed entries to the latest edition of The Historical Dictionary of Methodism . I've also contributed an essay to The Philosophy of Sherlock Holmes edited by Phil Tallon, a chapter to The Sound of Revival: Seven Powerful Prophetic Proclamations , edited by Kelcy Steel, and a chapter to The Journey of Grief: Traversing Our Sorrows Together edited by Tom Fuerst, Chad Foster, and Jonathan Powers. I'm also delightfully credited as "book doula" for Live Anointed by Katie Lance. Last Year, I launched FireFall , a newsletter amplifying and connecting the voices of women in church leadership across denominational silos, around the world, and throughout church history. In the process, I built a Zotero library with over 3,000 references and counting. WHY KICKSTARTER? I appreciate traditional publishing; it's also gone through quite a time the past fifteen years or so. I may link up with traditional publishing at some point. But right now, I could spend a large chunk of time on social media, querying agents, parlaying with publishers, and then still doing a lot of my own promotion and marketing at the back-end anyway - or, leveraging my experience helping authors navigate self-publishing, I can crowdfund this project, with Kickstarter funding my time that I would otherwise need to invest other work and funding the creation of this resource. ("Other work" is fine, good, and honorable. But I feel compelled to invest in this, if I am able, and I want to invite you to join me.) And I love the idea of what band Over the Rhine's Bergquist and Detweiler refer to as a "barn-raising" for creatives. It might be fun to collaborate with a traditional publisher. But I know it will be beautifully meaningful to construct this book one supporter at a time: look at what you helped print, cut, glue, and package! If I exceed the target funding and reach a stretch goal of $15,000, I will create a 40-day devotional download drawing from the writing of women preachers which will be delivered to backers of all tiers. (If you're stuck at the airport, feel free to keep reading for: postures on advocating for women in ministry; how we engage history; and why I'm like a yarn-bomber.) Social Media Throw-Downs about Women in Pastoral Ministry: Aiming the Puck Where Your Teammate Will Be If charged social media engagement ever feels like a carousel (forward motion - but a closed circuit that never actually arrives anywhere), maybe engaging history feels like a Ferris wheel: you're still traveling through cycles, but with every rotation, you gain perspective as the carriage rises and you see the panorama below. It can be extraordinarily difficult to chart a direction without being pulled off into current event controversy. Sometimes it's important to engage those and put some energy into addressing them. But it's valuable to return to a posture of "aiming the puck where your teammate will be," in classic hockey lingo. To me, engaging with phenomenally inspiring women from history helps to set a positive vision of what I'm aiming for. If I choose just two characteristics from these women to try to practice and emulate - but I try to incorporate those into my life over the course of a year - my leadership will change as a result. My perspective will change. And my boldness will change. I will have more resting clarity, less noise. Resetting the center of gravity from reactionary response to positive vision is easier when you have real-life examples from other times and places who have something very relevant and pertinent to say to you. If We Teach the Bass Line as the Whole Song: Engaging People Who Think They Don't Like History I've always loved engaging people who think they don't like history. Usually, they learned history as fragmented bits of dates and facts with little sense of story - macro, or micro. Sometimes, they learned history the way people learn a bass line in four-part harmony. The bass line is there, but it's only part of the song. But what if, for years, we primarily taught and learned and studied the bass part? What if the bass line was the element of old music most discussed? What if we thought the bass line was the song? We're going to study music: here are the famous bassists we'll learn about this semester. I don't want to erase the bass line. I want to include and celebrate the other parts of the song that got buried behind the sheet music filing cabinet - so to speak. All the parts of the song together. The Gap Between the Archives and the Gas Station - and the Image of "Yarn Bombing" I'm like one of those people who "yarn-bomb" public spaces: I love gleaning colorful fibers from historical accounts and sharing those time-worn stitches in ways that are true to their pattern - but in ways that also engage and delight passersby who see a familiar streetlight in a whole new way. Because there's a gap between library archives and people stopping at a gas station, as it were. History isn't boring, but there's a big divide between what's held in the archives and what seems relevant to the random person filling up their vehicle at a gas station. My nerd-heart would love the average citizen to enjoy crumbling autobiographies or academic works that dive into tightly defined scope: "Four Women Preachers from Monroe County All Named Emma, and Their Homiletical Approach to the Book of Isaiah, 1810-1820" - that kind of thing, and apologies if you were just sitting down to write a journal article with that exact title. Actually, my hypothetical sounds pretty delightful to me. But it's unfair to ask the level-headed people around me to dive too deeply into "thus verily did I fly unto the bosom of my Saviour, and there repose" kind of sentences. So if I can, figuratively speaking, yarn-bomb your day with the colorful fibers of history so that you see everyday, walk-the-dog life a bit differently, then I've accomplished what I want to do. You don't have to learn to knit or sneak out in the night to wrap streetlights in yarn, so to speak; you can just enjoy the added perspective on your world.

title: Publishing Tis the Night of Christmas
description: I have two (2) main factors that are driving this project. 1 - Each holiday season we all get the opportunity to enjoy elves, reindeer, Santa and that quirky writer who happens to run into the one that got away at a cabin in Vermont during the holidays. The current programming that surrounds us does not incorporate the birth of Jesus very well, so here is a chance to share a little bit of hope and love. 2 - I am ready to start my next career as a full-time writer and content creator. Your support will help to fund this project by helping me get an illustrator, web designer and help me establish my business to share more content and create a space for more creators to come together and share our talents with the world. Please note that in the 25 years of working on this project, in the last few months, I did use AI to generate images for illustrations and to improve some stanzas. I am claiming the text as my own, but due to the nature and stigma around AI, I wanted to disclose that I used the tools to improve the product. Your funding will replace the AI produced artwork with human created pieces so that at the time of publication, this will be humans at every level. At the end of the day, the Bible provided the characters and details, and the cadence and rhythm comes from the original poem. I am only responsible for the reimagining and providing a Biblical focus.

title: Vision Therapy: Insight Out
description: Since November 1999, I have been involved in Vision Therapy. What started as a second job and an opportunity to make a few extra bucks, morphed into a passion, a mission, a calling, and a career. I am living, breathing proof of someone who loves their work! I wake up everyday excited for to see the patients who trust me to help them and I am grateful for the opportunity beyond what any written word could describe! Since I started as a Vision Therapist some 17 years ago many things have changed, mostly for the positive. Our profession has grown, our knowledge base has widened, and our understanding of the visual implications across many populations is more vast than it has ever been. Whomever coined the phrase "or eyes are our windows to the world", truly was ahead of their time. My goal for this project is to write a book which offers parents, as well as current and potential patients, a window into how Vision Therapy looks from my side of the table. Planned chapters include helping new and current patients understand what to expect from Vision Therapy, an in-depth explanation on how to become a Vision Therapy success, remembering to base decisions solely on needs and struggles of the patient, ideas for managing the constant drumbeat of those detractors, some of my favorite experiences in the therapy room, and so much more! While written as a complete project, this book will also be arranged as a quick reference guide to parents and patients alike as they navigate the waters of choosing what is the best course of treatment for themselves or their children. Vision Therapy is magnificent, and it's benefits far reaching.  The more we work to demystify and educate, the more powerful our message becomes. See the world of Vision Therapy through my eyes. The pinnacles, the pitfalls, and the potential to change a life! By sharing my perspective, my understandings, and my experiences in the Vision Therapy room, I hope to contribute to that positive message!

title: Tales From OmniPark
description: From 1977 to 2003, OmniPark thrilled, astonished and educated guests of all ages. ...or did it? While some cynics claim our beloved Texas theme park never truly existed, 19 of today's greatest weird-fiction authors remain convinced it did — and we've written all-new original stories to prove it! Brian Evenson, Gemma Files, Ben Thomas, Jesse Bullington and Orrin Grey Join Brian Evenson, Gemma Files, Jesse Bullington, Orrin Grey and many others as we journey back to the Seven Realms: The Realm of Life — a primeval jungle zoo, aquarium, and riverboat ride The Realm of the Cell — a microscopic encounter with protozoan predators The Realm of the Deep — a strange marine voyage aboard a wondrous submarine The Realm of the Stars — a space station charting the far reaches of the cosmos The Realm of Man — a face-to-face encounter with humanity's distant ancestors The Realm of the Particle — a reality-warping adventure at the quantum scale The Realm of Time — a 19th-century mansion housing a powerful time machine 1986 OmniPark Guidemap Each story in this anthology reimagines OmniPark folklore in unique, startling ways. I (Ben Thomas, editor) have hand-selected each one of these exclusive tales, and have worked one-on-one with the authors to refine every character and scene. In these stories you'll meet sentient sea creatures, time-traveling siblings, creeping cosmic horrors, mad inventors — and a full cast of equally memorable characters. You'll travel to alternate universes, distant solar systems, ocean trenches, paradoxical dimensions , and dozens more realms of the fantastic. Along the way, you'll discover for yourself why OmniPark remains vibrant in our thoughts and memories — and alive and well in our hearts. In the interest of full transparency, click here to download the complete budgetary spreadsheet for this project.

title: Categories
description: Maps matter. Take a look, for instance, at the beauty of modern Champagne today, and you can fairly argue that its current embrace of terroirs and transparency came in large part from the rediscovery of Louis Larmat's extraordinary 1940s maps. Larmat did what only places like Burgundy were known for doing: depicting the region not as a monolith but as a diversity of small places. And indeed, the current popularity of Burgundy comes in significant part from maps like those of Pitiot and Poupon, which made the climats and terroir concrete and real, something you could hang on your wall. So why not Beaujolais? In my case, file it under "Everyone needs a hobby." Or rather, everyone needed a COVID pastime. For me — in addition to bottling dirty martinis for the neighbors — it was a return to mapmaking. I'd studied cartography in the early 2000s, and since I couldn't travel for much of 2020, or even leave the house for a few months, here was a perfect way to mentally embrace places I couldn't go in person. My fascination with Beaujolais was stoked by my work on THE NEW FRENCH WINE , although it's been there in one form or another for at least two decades. But in the course of my reporting and research, I discovered an entire alternate history of the region — one that undermined its simple reputation and made a clear case for its greatness. The "bedtime story" version of Beaujolais, as I put it in the book, starts with Philip the Bold banishing "disloyal" gamay in 1395, leading to centuries of ignominy. That's true on its face, in that his decree was a spark that fueled longstanding tension between the Beaujolais and parts north — not just the heart of Burgundy but the neighboring Mâconnais. Roll forward to the mid-20th century, and Beaujolais reasonably had taken on the countenance of a rustic country hollow. The past reveals a different tale: Beaujolais was a prosperous region through the 19th century and even into the 20th — producing wines that rivaled Burgundy in quality.  (Meantime the Côte d'Or was growing more gamay than pinot noir in the mid-19th century). It was shipping millions of liters of wine both north to Paris — overland, then via the Loire — and south to Lyon. Perhaps the most visible evidence of this wine culture was captured in 1874, when a Lyonnais engineer, Antoine Budker, drafted a map of the Beaujolais (plus the Mâconnais, and Chalonnaise), depicting individual lieux-dits, or crus, ranked in first through fifth place. This built on an earlier map Budker had sketched in 1869, and crucially factored in results from a recent competition in the Mâconnais, held under the auspices of the regional agricultural council. This work was quietly revolutionary; its methodology mirrored the 1861 classification undertaken by the Beaune agricultural committee, which effectively was the genesis of the system of crus used today in Burgundy. Here, a similar committee endorsed a similar ranking, in a region considered distinctly worthy of the effort. These classifications not only created a hierarchy of quality, but also established Beaujolais as a place worthy of hierarchies as notable as that of Bordeaux in 1855. Was this a one-off anomaly?  It was not. In 1893, Victor Vermorel collaborated with the agronomist Réné Danguy to publish Les Vins du Beaujolais, du Mâconnais, et Châlonnais . Their book ranked hundreds of specific lieux-dits for each of the regions. And Vermorel was no fringe character: He was the most prominent person in Beaujolais at the time — inventor of the portable sulfur sprayer, an industrialist who employed hundreds in his factory in Villefranche-sur-Sâone, one of the region's most prominent citizens. (His factory would go on to build early automobiles and even an airplane prototype, and he would soon co-author the seven-volume Ampelographie , the definitive reference text on grape varieties for a century.) Vermorel harnessed Budker's work, but also added local classifications where relevant — C.L. alongside A.B. — to highlight nuances that might have been overlooked by the Mâcon committee, as well as folding in a more recent ranking from an exposition that year. Even amid the destruction of phylloxera, the value of the terroir was clear. The details in both works are extraordinary — a level of precision not again applied to Beaujolais for more than a century. Yet they affirm some of the things we've come to know in recent decades: the importance of places like the Côte du Py in Morgon; Poncié and Chapelle des Bois in Fleurie; Les Capitans in Juliénas. And they reveal far more: important lieux-dits in the southern Beaujolais, essential terroirs in villages outside the crus, like Vaux and Leynes, and so many other significant places. It's telling that these classifications have been rediscovered in the past few years as Beaujolais has reaffirmed itself as a place worthy of precise devotion. But a classification is one thing. To drive home the point, you need a map. And so began my project — first to transform the classifications into viable data, then to build a detailed map, one that embraced not only what now is known as cru Beaujolais (with its complicated creation) but great terroirs throughout the region. As happens with side projects, this one sat for a while — I had this small book to publish — but the time has come. So I'm pleased to introduce THE CLASSIFICATIONS OF BEAUJOLAIS , a full-scale, full-color map of all the crus documented by Budker and Vermorel. Now, why share this on Kickstarter, especially when I'm an avowed skeptic of self-publishing?  For one thing, because this is a labor of love. But also, I have deep faith in the community of like-minded lovers of Beaujolais. Are we as manifest as I suspect we are? Also, I wanted to make this project just a bit more open-source. So you'll see that different tiers of support come with additional formats for this info. There are some limited opportunities to get digital copies of the map, as well as access to the data I've compiled and researched — including a nifty printed booklet with the listed crus; databases; even a GIS shapefile. My hope is that these will make the information more accessible, and lead to more discovery of Beaujolais terroir. [Music: French Kiff Beats, "Freestyle." Video photography © Susannah Ireland.]

title: FaceTheCamera — AI Teleprompter for Confident Business Video
description: 🚀 FaceTheCamera — The AI Teleprompter for Business Communication Speaking clearly and naturally on camera is difficult — especially when the message matters. Whether you’re pitching investors, leading a team, selling to clients, recruiting talent, or building your personal brand , talking on video has become a core part of professional communication. FaceTheCamera helps founders, executives, sales teams, marketers, recruiters, and  content professionals deliver clear, confident, and credible on-camera messages — without memorizing lines or sounding scripted. Use it for the full spectrum of business communication: Investor & fundraising pitches CEO updates & C-level communication Sales outreach & client presentations Marketing explainers & product demos Recruiting messages & employer branding Internal training & team updates Personal branding content With script reading, real-time AI eye-contact correction, and a suite of supporting features, you can present naturally and deliver polished, professional video messages every time. And as 2026 approaches, there’s no better moment to strengthen your on-camera presence and elevate your business communication for the year ahead. ⚠️ The Problems for Business Professionals Recording a business video—whether it’s an investor pitch, client update, or internal announcement—should be simple. This helps you emphasize the most important points naturally, without breaking your flow or sounding scripted. 🚀 Solution 6: Expression Ease — Present with Confidence and Approachability AI softens tense facial expressions and stabilizes micro-movements, helping you appear relaxed, calm, and trustworthy. Ideal for leadership messages, investor communication, and any client-facing presentation where confidence matters.
