title: Percussa micro Super Signal Processor Eurorack Module (mSSP)
description: The Percussa micro Super Signal Processor (mSSP) Eurorack Module is a powerful Eurorack DSP module based on one of the fastest ARM processors available, the quad core Cortex A17 at 1.8GHz, and high end AKM audio converters offering 8 in / 4 out with up to 192kHz sample rate and 32-bit resolution, DC coupled. The mSSP comes with 50 pre-made patches covering a wide variety of synthesis techniques such as wavetable, granular, subtractive and modal synthesis , supporting polyphony and multi-timbrality , and a Mac and Windows software editor to support creating and editing your own patches . Explore some of the video demos below. Scroll down for more demos. The mSSP comes with 50 modular synthesis patches offering polyphonic wavetable, granular, sampling and traditional synthesis techniques, multi-channel audio processing and much more, all in only 26HP of rack space and for only $549. The mSSP will also come with a Mac and Windows editor allowing the user to create and edit patches. The editor will connect with the mSSP hardware for real-time updates to patches and will support the SSP as well. Mac and Windows mSSP /SSP Editor Software Mockup example module editor (envelope generator) on the mSSP example module editor (envelope generator) on the mSSP Percussa mSSP functional prototype Percussa mSSP functional prototype Key features and specifications Audio and Control Voltage I/O 8 inputs via high end AKM ADC with 32 bit resolution supporting sample rates of 192kHz, 96kHz and 48kHz (AK5558) 4 outputs via high end AKM DAC with 32 bit resolution supporting sample rates of 192kHz, 96kHz and 48kHz (AK4454) DC coupled inputs and outputs, eurorack voltage compliant (-5V to +5V) All inputs and outputs support any signal type (audio, control, gate, trigger, etc) Low-noise power supplies and op-amp stages, featuring 0.1% resistors DSP Processing Quad core CPU running at 1.8 GHz per core with NEON SIMD parallel processing 32-bit floating point ADC/DAC input and output with 64-bit DSP algorithms Capable of running multiple DSP modules in parallel on multiple cores Synthesis Engine Mac and Windows editor software for full patch creation and editing Preloaded with 50 modular synthesis patches covering a wide range of synthesis techniques and audio processing methods (half of the patches are chosen by Percussa with the other half to be voted on by backers) Custom DSP software with more than 25 built-in synth modules such as wavetable oscillators, granulars, samplers, filters and effects Patches can use combinations of many fundamental DSP modules allowing multichannel and multivoice architectures Supports loading your own WAV files into patch modules via USB flash drives (e.g. load your own samples in the wavetable oscillators, granular processors, samplers, etc) Modify, store and recall patch module parameters as presets (limited only by size of the USB stick) Storage and recall of presets and patches with the press of a button 100+ pre-recorded samples / wavetables with royalty free license for (m)SSP users Supports loading synthesis patches created on the SSP by Percussa or the SSP community Development Kit / VST support Support to develop and load your own Linux/ARM VST plugins (requires linux knowledge and compiling code for Linux/ARM) Example Quad VCA VST plugin code written in C++ using the JUCE framework already available for download on github and ready to go, including documentation in the SSP forum Display, Graphics and User Interface 320x240 pixel high contrast full colour IPS display (wide angle visibility) 4x High quality Aluminium Machined Knobs 4x Endless and Click-less High Quality Encoders 8x Industrial Temperature high quality Switches Shallow menu system (no menu diving) Large fonts for parameter display for instant readability Signal input / output Oscilloscope features Connectivity 2 USB host ports supporting USB MIDI (MPE) controllers and USB flash drives (for storing samples, patches, presets and for loading software updates) Support for USB ethernet adapters for developers (see instructions in SSP forum) Serial linux console connector on back of PCB On-board Storage 4 GB flash 2 GB RAM Dimensions and Front Panel 26HP width, 3U in height 50mm depth behind front panel (estimated) 3mm thickness CNC machined aluminium front panel, brushed and black anodized Passive aluminium heatsink Power 800mA @ +12V and 100mA @ -12V draw (estimated) Standard 10-pin eurorack power connector Premium low impedance eurorack power cable included Demo Videos The demo videos below show two patches running on the mSSP functional prototype: 4-voice sequencing with per voice step sequencer, microtonal quantizer (jazz scale from the SCALA archive), sine osc, envelope, bus routing + global stereo delay & reverb and frequency modulation of all sine oscillators via the input module. This patch contains 23 modules total. We use a make noise STO and function to frequency modulate the sine oscillators in this 4-voice patch. Various parameters in the patch are adjusted using the #mssp encoders, such as attack time for the envelopes and clock division factor for the step sequencers. 8-voice wavetable synthesis patch, controlled via MIDI, with each voice consisting of a wavetable oscillator with 3D morphing, ADSR envelope, input modulation, MIDI module, and a bus module. The patch also has a delay and reverb module before going to the output module where the signals are routed to the output channels. granular patch with input (start/length) modulation, and a delay and reverb processor, right before the output module. Sample used for the granular demo is choir sings -Magnificat- 140928.wav by klankbeeld / capella ducis on freesound.org. All sound was recorded on the Percussa SSP, using its built-in multichannel recorder, and was then synced to video afterwards. Why this powerful Eurorack module? The mSSP comes with one of the most powerful ARM processors you can get in a eurorack module: a quad core A17 at 1.8GHz, which provides over 20,000 DMIPS of processing power. The A17 processor we use in the SSP and the mSSP is about 40x more powerful than the Cortex M processors popular in other eurorack modules. By putting more processing power into the user’s hands, we empower them to create better sound and do more within a smaller eurorack system: Run more sophisticated and computationally expensive DSP algorithms Run DSP algorithms at higher sample rates, supporting up to 192kHz, with 32-bit resolution Run more DSP algorithms in parallel, enabling more voices and more channels (e.g. 8 voice granular with 200 grains per voice) Easy to use The mSSP will come with a Mac / Windows software editor allowing patch creation and full editing. Besides that the mSSP will be preloaded with 50 modular synthesis patches which lets users easily create powerful sounds: Modular synthesis patches can be switched on the fly using the up/down button Parameters of every synth module in the patch can be changed via parameter pages Samples/wavetables can automatically be loaded into patch modules Changes in parameter settings can easily be stored in a preset and recalled at any time Ability to change the internal routings between modules in patches using toggle settings Ability to mute/unmute a module in a patch Patches come with visual overview of input and output jacks which makes it instantly clear how the patches can be integrated in your rack Percussa mSSP orthographic 3D render Percussa mSSP 3D Render Percussa mSSP 3D Render Overview of included synthesis patches Below is a preliminary list of patches (subject to change) that will be included with the mSSP. Of the 50 patches we will provide, 25 patches will be based on suggestions and votings of the mSSP backers. So you get a chance to suggest patches and vote on them! 8 voice wavetable synthesis with 3D morphing, with 2 oscillators per voice (16 oscillators total), with MIDI support, XYZ modulation, envelopes and delay/reverb processors 8 voice granular synthesis with 200 grains per voice (live input and samples supported) 32 oscillator additive synthesis 8 SVF bank (modeled state variable filters) 4 voice physical modeling membrane synthesis 4 voice karplus strong (string synthesis) physical modeling patch 8 channel delay and reverb processor 8 channel comb filter processor 8 channel mixer with morphable levels 8 channel signal follower 4 voice modal synthesis (seperate exciter sections, filter bank and delay/reverb processors) 4 channel algorithmic pattern/curve generator 8 voice multichannel sample player with envelopes (up to 8 channels per player) 4 channel 4-operator FM with envelopes 4 channel physical modeling resonator ... Any patch that has been created on the SSP, can be loaded onto the mSSP as well. So besides the patches we will develop you will have the chance to load patches from the SSP community. Workflow of the Percussa mSSP Module The UI of the Percussa SSP will use the same philosophy of the Percussa SSP, where only one “level” of pages is used, to avoid menu diving. There are 2 pages on the mSSP: Global : to switch patches, make high level (macro) parameter changes to the patch, change signal routings in patches via simple toggle settings, and to save/recall presets (parameter settings of patches) Module : to make changes to the parameters of the individual modules used in the patch as well as to load samples or other files User Interface Design of the Percussa mSSP (Top left) Change parameters of synth modules (Top right) Load different WAV file into Sampler (Bottom left) Switcher module to change internal routings in patch (Bottom right) Switch presets/patch 2D Graphical User Interface Mockups of the mSSP showing 4 different screens (Top left) Switch modules in patch (Top right) Input output overview of a patch (Bottom left) Visualization ADSR envelope (Bottom right) Select start and length of sampler 2D Graphical User Interface Mockups of the mSSP showing 4 different screens (Left) Visualisation input scope (Right) Visualisation 3D Wavetable Oscillator 2D Graphical User Interface Mockups of the mSSP showing 2 different screens What is the difference between the mSSP and SSP? Both the mSSP and SSP are equally powerful in terms of DSP power and sound quality, since they share the same CPU as well as the same family of high end audio converters. The table below shows the differences between the two modules in detail: Comparison table between mSSP and SSP Appendix - DSP modules to be used in the mSSP patches The mSSP software will use the DSP engine we developed for the SSP, featuring more than 25 different synthesis modules. The patches shipped with the mSSP use combinations of these modules, and through the menu system of the mSSP it will be possible to change the parameters of the modules used in the patches. wavetable oscillator with 3D morphing, stacking, loading of arbitrary WAV files and realtime recutting, etc granular processor with all the usual controls including start and length of grain selection window, duration, pitch, randomization, grain trigger freq, nr of grains, etc. comb filter waveshaper SVF (modeled) envelope generator with log/exp stages physical modeling membrane percussion module envelope follower reverb and delay processors apreggiator of arbitrary signals with clock divider step sequencer sampler module supporting up to 8 channels per sample and loading of 8 samples per module with start/length, crossfading, etc. simple delay line LFO (also usable as normal oscillator) microtonal quantizer with SCALA support noise generator with variable freq DC generator VST loader module quad VCA (VST module) output and input modules (with gain/bias per channel on the input module) MIDI module with MPE support bit crusher bus module for summing and routing signals signal switcher (audio rate) Selection of upcoming modules and features on the SSP: looper module time stretching for the sampler module live input and loadable (WAV) windows for the granular processor Additional features and modules requested by SSP users are described in the Percussa SSP forum. Since we shipped the SSP we have averaged 1 software update every 1-2 weeks. What is the status of the project? The Percussa mSSP module is currently a functional prototype, based on the Percussa SSP, which was successfully funded on Kickstarter in September 2017, with first units delivered in June 2018. Here is what we’ve already done development wise for the mSSP: Designed and manufactured mSSP front panel prototypes Designed and manufactured a prototype PCB for mechanical fitting and testing Connected switches, encoders and display to our prototype PCB for UI testing and connected the prototype PCB to the SSP’s expansion connectors so we can develop for the mSSP, on the SSP platform Updated the SSP’s software and drivers to show graphics on the mSSP’s display Designed and tested the first demo patches for the mSSP featuring wavetable and granular synthesis Percussa mSSP functional prototype Objectives The mSSP module will be based on the carrier PCB design of the SSP, but needs several hardware engineering changes and optimizations in order to produce it at a lower price and to make it fit within 26HP of eurorack space: We need to redesign the PCB of the SSP to make it smaller. To do so, we’ll need to split it into multiple PCBs which will be stacked on top of each other (audio and USB I/O, audio converters, CPU and flash/RAM module) the ARM system on module which we used on the SSP will be replaced with a module we will design and cost optimize in-house, featuring the exact same processor as on the SSP. We’ll design a custom passive heatsink for the mSSP just like we did for the SSP, keeping the CPU cool, and letting us run it at maximum speed. The audio I/O section of the mSSP will be based on the SSP’s I/O section but will have less channels, so we will be redesigning the that part of the PCB.  We will be using the same high quality components from the SSP so there is no difference in sound quality. The display of the mSSP will be connected via a different bus than the wide format display of the SSP which means the display section of the PCB will be redesigned. Besides these hardware modifications, there is also the following to be done software wise: we need to build a UI specifically for the mSSP that sits on top of our existing SSP software code base. we need to develop the Mac/Windows patch editor software supporting the mSSP and SSP we need to develop the patch collection for which part of the patches is listed above we need to add additional features and modules to the existing SSP software to support the patch collection we'll be developing Percussa mSSP functional prototype Timeline Dec - Ordering parts Jan - PCB design + order front panels Feb - First prototype assembly and testing Mar - Software engineering + patch development Apr - Second (final) prototype assembly and testing May - Final Software and Hardware testing & Manufacturing June - Assembly and shipping Normal Backers Taxes and Duties If you are located outside the United States, please note that import duties, taxes or VAT is not included in the pledges. When your shipment clears customs, you (the backer) might be liable for paying import duties or taxes to the customs office in your country. If you are located in California, please note that sales tax is not included in the listed pledges, and depends on the county / city we are shipping to. At the time of shipment, we will calculate the correct CA sales tax and send you an email with a secure payment link asking you to pay the correct sales tax amount for the address we are shipping to. Outside California, within the United States, sales tax is the responsibility of the backer. The HS code for customs classification of the module will be 9209.94.80 00 (parts for musical instruments for which the sound is produced electronically). Acknowledgments This kickstarter project would not have happened without the support of our SSP users and community, as well as close friends in the electronic music and music technology communities, and enthousiastic eurorack users. You know who you are. Hard at work building patches for the mSSP

title: World’s 1st AI-Exclusive Smartwatch
description: World’s 1st AI-Exclusive Smartwatch Three years ago, AI was still costly and clumsy. We could see the future—but could not yet reach it. So we waited. We refined through the quiet winters of technology, believing through iteration after iteration— believing that AI would one day feel light, that a watch could be more than notifications and step counts. That it could understand what you say, remember what you think, and walk with you through work and life. Three years on, models have gained warmth, and the technology has finally matured. And now, we’ve turned what was once an expensive dream— into a silent flower upon your wrist. The world’s first truly AI‑exclusive watch is no sudden miracle. It is the end of a three‑year wait— for the tech to grow ready, for the cost to grow gentle, and most of all, for you. Introducing Defiant: The AI Watch That Grows With You Meet Defiant, the world's first pure AI watch. It starts with four powerful, ready-now features designed for your mind and style: 1. Flash Note : Lift your wrist and speak—ideas are instantly transcribed, no button needed. 2. A Living Canvas : A stunning 2.2" OLED display with AI dynamic wallpapers. Wear it also as a cyber-fashion accessory. 3. Aura : Your voice-activated AI companion for tasks and answers. Early-bird backers can priority-customize it. 4. Real-Time Translate : Break language barriers with live conversation translation. 5. Meet Minutes – Your AI Meeting Secretary Turn any discussion into an organized, actionable record—effortlessly. · Multi-Speaker Intelligence : Automatically identifies and distinguishes between different speakers in a meeting, creating clear, attributed transcripts. · Smart Summarization & Action Items : After the meeting, AI instantly generates a structured summary and extracts clear decisions, key points, and to-dos , assigning them within the transcript. · One-Click Share : Seamlessly share the full minutes or just the action items with your team directly from your wrist. This is just the beginning. Defiant evolves with you, based on your feedback. We will release new features every 2-3 days. For example: “Text Sarah: Running 10 minutes late.” — Just say it, and Aura will send the message, no phone needed. Back Defiant today—and own a watch that gets smarter, more personal, and more powerful every week.

title: One Field Live: Launch!
description: What if you had an event you could attend every month - an event where you and your friends were able to present their projects to the world, and connect with each other, both in person and online, with a loving and joyful community energy? What if you were able to be part of an online community that had access to the recordings of this event, so the teachings, and the teachings of spiritual coaches and masters stretching back generations (through my access to some very powerful archives) were all there for you as your constant listening and viewing companions? What if you would be able to post, chat and share in this energetically contained community - and it wouldn't be Facebook, Instagram or (name your poison!). And, if you are a USM student, what if you could work your process and support others with ongoing online trios? What if this same monthly live/virtual hybrid event, in addition to bringing us all (finally) together in person, also gave you the opportunity to share your writing, music, art, and of course your speaking, coaching and teaching mastery - giving you a professional, high-quality video recording you could offer to your own students and clients as a product, or gift the world with your messaging and content creation? What if your paid membership in this online community supported a continual focus and effort on growing an audience for these events and your own offerings - an audience that would be able to purchase these offerings, watch your teachings and view your creative mastery - and spread the word so more and more like-minded seekers just like yourself could discover and join? What if this membership allowed you to have access to not only continually increasing trainings on how to make tech easier in your life as a content creator, how to enroll and build your audience and create greater impact, but also be able to hire One Field to produce your events and help you created your content at greatly reduced prices so your return on investment is much higher? Welcome to One Field Live . If this vision has landed in your heart, please help us meet the launch goal by pre-paying for a membership either as a participant or as a teacher, artist or content creator - or truly letting this magnificent journey take flight by gracing us all as an angel investor or board member! Explore our first launch event Sunday April 16, 2023 , where the joy was electric as we all gathered in person, enjoyed 15 extraordinary speakers, and LOVE LOVE LOVED each other and honored each other's gifts with immense love and respect. Live and recorded, this event went out to hundreds of people who were hungry for more! Let this vision arrive and take root in your Soul, and may it blossom forwards as your gift of love to our One Community! NEW: ONE FIELD IDEAL SCENE AND LIVING VISION! MEMBERSHIP DETAILS I will build this platform on Heartbeat.chat, which is like a social media site but with a closed membership. You can watch a 5 minute demo of the platform here. It is like Facebook in that it stays open, you can bookmark it anywhere like Facebook, Instagram or any site, and you can get notifications for the events, new recordings, new online classes, new videos. A digital marketing campaign will bring a ton of new people in who will see your coaching, your workshops, your art, writing and music, and you speaking at our events. The first event will be the weekend following the successful funding of the campaign as a celebration event! There many inspiring choices for membership! For $29 a month (the Joyful Participant level), you can attend the events both live and online, join the trios, and enjoy the platform. You will also be able to get discounted media services from One Field, if needed. You will also be able to post about your own projects and services to your community. Sharing your gifts is what we are all about! (This will also start you as a member with a regular $29 monthly membership fee that will begin 1/3/6 months - depending on your reward choice - after our first event). For $79 a month (the Artist: Speaker & Creator level), you can be a speaker at the events, which will get you a professional, two camera edited recording of your share you can use to create a pilot, post on your website, use as a marketing tool, etc. You can speak once every two months, but preference will be given for those who have not shared. You are guaranteed to have a spot for at least twice in a six month period. You will also be able to have your share posted in the platform, and receive discounted media services from One Field, as needed. And of course, you will have full access to the community platform. (This will also start you as a member with a regular $79 monthly membership fee that will begin 1/3/6 months - depending on your reward choice - after our first event). Finally, there is an opportunity to support at the Angel Investor or Board Member levels. The benefits for joining at these levels will appeal to the savvy content creator who will recognize the advantage of being able to not only have their content featured in a larger way on a platform with predicted exponential growth, but to use the speaker opportunities of being recorded professionally in front of a hybrid audience to create a pilot course, a mini-course, an enrollment workshop, or content to divide into tons of content for marketing and social media clips. Your support at these levels also gives you a better deal on the membership, with your content featured more prominently in the outreach and marketing for our platform - and with the Board level, to create a role as an advisor, mentor and a larger stakeholder role - you will create your role as one of the faces on One Field, to the degree you are called. We have three amazing Board Members now who have been financial supporters in the development of this campaign with gifts over $1000, and some equally incredible Angels who have already offered their support with loans and participation at a highly committed and energetic level. How are we creating enrollment? This is my favorite question. This will be done by a combination of continual outreach on social media, our growing email list, word of mouth, sharing on podcasts and speaker engagements, affiliate connections, and most importantly, paid marketing as guided by our network of experts. EVENT DETAILS Once a month, our live event will occur in North Hollywood at the following address: 11525 Chandler Blvd. North Hollywood, CA 91601 We have the capacity for 25 people at each event, and for over 100 online. We also livestream the event to increase our audience, but only One Field members can join and participate on Zoom, and watch the archived recording, and interact in the community, as well as join the trios and connect with the benefits. The event will be two hours, with a half hour meet and greet before, a 15 minute break halfway through, and a half an hour after the speaker event. Then, our trios will begin after all the speakers & artists are finished, and after all the abundant time for socializing. The trios will be two hours, with 25 minutes for each round and 5 minutes feedback - 30 minutes will also include preparation and large group sharing at the end. ADDITIONAL BENEFITS AND SUPPORT - A MODERN DAY "SALON". "A salon is a gathering of people held by a host. These gatherings often consciously followed Horace 's definition of the aims of poetry , "either to please or to educate". Salons in the tradition of the French literary and philosophical movements of the 17th and 18th centuries are still being carried on today." - from Wikipedia This is a community for artists, coaches, health and wellbeing teachers, spiritual masters, artists, writers and musicians (if you are a USM student, you will recognize the similarity where we presented our Year Two projects!) . Our foundations are the SRF, MSIA/USM, Yoga, 3 Principles/Spiritual Psychology and coaching communities, (especially as connected to Steve Chandler, & Carolyn Freyer-Jones). It is also a professional community; One Field professional services will be offered at deep discounts for those who wish to create online media, workshop events, virtual offerings, websites, graphics, and marketing and enrollment tools. This is also a way of me evolving my business into a community, which has always been my intention from the beginning - and to keep this beautiful workshop and recording space...and stay here in LA! The most exciting part may be the resources you will find on the platform! I have access, in my over 10 years supporting various organizations with their permission, to exquisite archival material, as well as the powerful work of my clients, who will have the option to use this platform to host their own products and services, both free and paid. However, I will personally be posting mini-courses on how to self-film, solve technology issues, create enrollment for your offerings, and everything I know about creating content both cost & time effectively. The platform will be largely self-guided so that our membership fees stay low. Like so many business models, you can rely on community to help you, as well as using the pre-created guide resources in the platform. So, at first there will not be a lot of tech support, other than some smart videos on navigating the platform I will create. It seems sometimes that we are really scattered to the four winds. Are we on Facebook? Do we connect by email lists? Do we stay connected by some of the same events and organizations? What about all the different membership sites, platforms and events we are all creating? And: why can't we share what we are doing professionally? I love hearing about your workshops and professional services, and want to support you!! Finally: a home for us all, created by One Field, and a super supportive group that has been in the very center of supporting our community, with all things online and technical. I welcome you, and love you all! Love, Light, Joy! Shannon Lloyd ANGELS AND BOARD MEMBERS HAVE ARRIVED! These generous Souls have leapt in with their support for me, and are Seeds for this new launch. My undying gratitude for their generosity and vision! BOARD MEMBERS (some identities revealed pending permission) Anonymous Anonymous Ayman Samman ANGELS Richard Kean Mark Cote Clay Cutts Rachelle Rigby Kirk Souder Mike Jackson-Polek Inely Cesna Vivek Munjal Lane Carlson Wendy Stretten
