The goal is to build momentum, collect real-world testimonials, and polish the game experience before the main launch. Phase 2 – Kickstarter Launch and Early Traction (Month 3-5) Official Kickstarter Campaign: Launching the campaign with polished product tiers, professional branding, and early access bonuses. This phase is about setting the tone for what’s to come, laying the groundwork for long-term success. Game Night Rollout: Hosting the first game nights, collecting UGC, and capturing family stories to fuel the campaign. This is also when we’ll coordinate with our early influencers, partners, and institutions to create a synchronized wave of social media buzz for the official launch. Backend Sales Offers: Building sales funnels at these events to convert attendees into long-term customers and secure early momentum. We’re also stockpiling influencer content for a synchronized big game night and website launch, targeting 5 key influencers and 5 key podcasts to release content simultaneously. Phase 3 – Full Production and Fulfillment (Month 6-8) Final Production Run: Using Kickstarter funds to finalize the full production run, including expanded card sets, upgraded components, and polished packaging. Digital Infrastructure: Initiating the development of the Touchdown Tots app, with features for score tracking, family learning tools, and digital expansion packs. Shipping and Fulfillment: Coordinating seamless delivery for backers, ensuring a premium unboxing experience that matches the quality and excitement of the game itself. Phase 4 – National Rollout and Institutional Growth (Month 9-12) Touchdown Tots Across America Tour: Hitting the road for a nationwide RV tour, spreading the message through grassroots community events and corporate partnerships, starting on the East Coast. This phase is about creating a lasting presence in the market and building community momentum. Institutional Outreach: Leveraging game nights to secure long-term contracts with corporations, schools, and community organizations, incorporating CSR through family engagement. We aim to have professional athletes and high-visibility partners greenlighting the product at this stage. Phase 5 – Long-Term Growth and Scaling (Year 2+) App Development and Expansion: Completing the Touchdown Tots app, adding new languages (e.g., Spanish), and building an online academy with a subscription model. This will extend the ecosystem beyond the physical board game and create ongoing revenue streams. New Sports and Product Lines: Using the same model to expand into basketball, soccer, and other sports, each with its own board game and learning ecosystem. Ongoing Community Engagement: Hosting monthly game nights, speaking engagements, and global expansion efforts to keep the momentum strong. Why Your Support Matters: Your support isn’t just helping us produce a game – it’s funding a full-scale movement to reconnect families, inspire learning, and build a lasting legacy. This funding will cover everything from venue rentals and marketing to production and fulfillment, making it possible for us to kickstart the Touchdown Tots journey. What’s the Budget? Strategically focusing on high-impact areas: Here’s a rough breakdown: • Revamping the Full Catalog: Professional photography, design updates, and polished packaging to reflect the full ecosystem. • Website Development: Creating a seamless, consumer-friendly digital experience. • Influencer and Podcast Outreach: Securing 5 key influencers and 5 key podcasts to create synchronized, high-impact launch waves. • Event Spaces: Booking at least 3 high-yield venues in advance. • Videographer for Events: Capturing high-quality UGC for ongoing marketing. • Liaison Support: Potentially hiring 1-2 liaisons for outreach and community management. • Digital Tools and Subscriptions: Covering annual fees for essential tools (Adobe, Canva, QuickBooks, Opus, etc.). • Miscellaneous Costs: Kickstarter fees, taxes, and buffer funds to handle unexpected expenses. Total Goal: $15,000 (with a high yield on the backend to maintain flexibility and growth potential). Why Do We Care? We believe that every child deserves the chance to learn, grow, and connect with their family in meaningful ways. Touchdown Tots isn’t just a board game, it’s a catalyst for building confident learners, intentional parents, and lifelong bonds. By backing this project, you’re not just buying a game, you’re joining a community that values family, education, and the power of play. You’re helping us change the way families learn together, one touchdown at a time. Join the Team. Build the Future. Let’s create a world where every game night builds a brighter tomorrow. Thank you for believing in our mission.

title: Categories
description:
The Todalo Shakers (Eric & Suzy Thompson, WB Reid, Frannie Leopold and Matt Weiner) are getting ready to launch our third CD, celebrating our 20th anniversary as a band. We’re very excited about the sound of this new recording, a big step up from the other two (which were recorded direct to stereo in our living rooms). We were fortunate to record at Megasonic Studios in Oakland, with the wonderful recording engineer Jeremy Goody. Now we’re finalizing the artwork (by the esteemed artist and fiddler Howard Rains) and preparing to send it to the manufacturer. Our $6,000 Kickstarter goal includes the costs of recording, mastering, publishing royalties, artwork, design, and manufacturing. You can help bring the new Todalo Shakers album into the world, by pre-purchasing your own copy, and if you want to be an even bigger help, we’re offering some cool rewards for higher levels of support. We thank you! What's a Todalo? or, Who Are The Todalo Shakers? Todalo Shakers by my side! (at KPFA) The Todalo Shakers is a "super-group" of veteran acoustic musicians whose roots in the old music run long and deep.  Together, they create jug band party  blues and old time rags & stomps which sound like they are lifted right out of 1928 in Memphis, Tennessee. The band features lots of harmony vocal arrangements, making for a rich and funky palette of vocal blends and a great feeling of camaraderie; the exuberance and fondness that the musicians feel for their material and for each other is unmistakable. Mix in hot ragtime tunes on fiddle and mandolin plus virtuoso bass slap solos, and the party really gets going! Suzy Thompson is nationally known for her bluesy fiddling and powerful vocals; the fiddler of choice for Jim Kweskin, Geoff Muldaur and Del Rey, she has also worked with Maria Muldaur, Dave Alvin and Peter Rowan. Eric Thompson (playing mostly mandolin in the Todalo Shakers) began exploring the country blues in the 1960s with Mother McCree's Uptown Jug Champions (with Jerry Garcia) and more recently in his work with such artists as David Nelson, David Grisman and Jody Stecher. The Thompsons are based in Berkeley, California.  Guitarist/vocalist Frannie Leopold (Mendocino) has toured and recorded the Delta Sisters and the Balkan Kafe Orchestra, and is a perennial favorite instructor at many West Coast music camps including Lark in the Morning and California Coast Music Camp. Bruce "WB" Reid (Seattle) sings and plays a rare vintage 6-string banjo-guitar (affectionately known as "the enforcer") with the Todalo Shakers. WB spent part of his twenties hanging out in San Diego with bluesman Sam Chatmon, of the legendary string band the Mississippi Sheiks. He has toured and recorded with the Tallboys (Seattle's hottest oldtime string band), with the Carolina Jug Stompers and with his wife, Bonnie Zahnow. Upright bassist Matt Weiner (Seattle) boasts a distinguished resume including stints with the Asylum Street Spankers, Hot Club of Cowtown, Wayne Horvitz, Danny Barnes and Becky Kilgore. His slap bass solos are always a highlight of a Todalo Shakers performance! No, Really: What's a Todalo? No one really knows! First of all, it's pronounced "TOE-dah-low." It's closely related to "diddy-wah-diddy", and crops up occasionally in obscure old blues songs: "I'm satisfied, satisfied, my todalo shaker by my side" is a line from the Memphis Jug Band classic "You May Leave, But This Will Bring You Back" and references to "todalo" turn up in songs by Mississippi John Hurt, Eubie Blake, and Bessie Smith. A few of the places the Todalo Shakers have played: Berkeley Old Time Convention Portland Old Time Gathering The Barn in Gilroy, CA Santa Fe Bluegrass and Old Time Festival Freight & Salvage, Berkeley, CA Live Oak Music Festival
Festival of American Fiddle Tunes Seattle Folklore Society The Ugly Mug, Soquel, CA And catch us during our March, 2019 Northern California Tour 3/14 Woodside 3/15 Walnut Creek 3/16 Cotati 3/17 Half Moon Bay (afternoon) 3/18 Santa Cruz 3/19 Oakland 3/20 Marin County 3/22 Mendocino

title: Categories
description: Who are we? Trendafilka is a New Orleans-based vocal ensemble that studies and performs both traditional (village style) and modern (choral style) folk songs from areas of Eastern Europe - the Balkans, the Baltics, the Caucasus, and the Eurasian Steppe - that hold rich cultural traditions of polyphonic singing. We're excited to make and release our second album, and share our arrangements of 12 beautiful songs from Bulgaria, Ukraine, Greece, Latvia & Lithuania with the world! But we can't do it without you! Why This Project? This (hopefully) upcoming album is very special to us, and we really want to release it out to the world. We have been singing together since 2016, and first released a Zoom-recorder-demo in 2017, then our first studio album (" Under, Over ") in 2019.  After that we were excited keep pushing ahead, learning new music, and growing as an ensemble... Our self-titled zoom-recorder demo, and our first studio album But then came 2020. We had been accustomed to singing together weekly, but suddenly we could no longer gather in intimate living room spaces and share song. The pandemic forced us to pause, to rest, to lie fallow. When we simply couldn't wait any longer to begin growing again, we learned to reach out to new teachers via previously-unimagined methods and find creative ways of continuing to grow our skills and resources, individually and together. By the time it finally felt safe enough to begin singing with each other indoors again, we all had a lot of energy and dedication built up, and a great appreciation for what a special group this is and what a gift it is to be able to sing together and share our music with wider communities. We decided it was time to record a second album. Getting ready to record at Esplanade Studios this past March We spent this past Fall and Winter working really hard, meeting multiple times a week to sing and plan, applying for grants to help get us into the studio as soon as possible, and working with teachers both in-person and via Zoom (thank you to all our teachers for sharing your vast knowledge and giving great personal feedback to get us ready!) . A few days after the Spring Equinox, we went into Esplanade Studios for three days with Grammy award-winning engineer Misha Kachkachishvili (who is Georgian!) to record our second album. Even listening to just the rough bounces at the end of each day, we were SO excited with how they are sounding, and can’t wait to share these special songs with the world! (Below: Sneak peak of us listening to one of the raw songs right after recording!) You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 HOWEVER, we spent all our available resources just on recording... and that doesn’t leave anything for mixing, mastering, and putting these songs out--and it turns out that all costs a lot! As independent musicians, we don't have a label fronting the funds necessary to plan, record and release a studio album, especially as costs continue to rise, so we are here asking humbly for your support so that these songs can be finished and spread to the world. Your contribution will help us cover the remaining costs of recording, mixing, mastering, making physical media (CDs, cassettes, and vinyl), and promoting the album. We really love this music and know you will, too. Thank you for your support in helping us get it out to the world! (Below: An emotional cheers on the steps of Esplanade Studios at the end of our last day of recording. We had such an amazing weekend together and no one wanted to leave. As the last line of the Greek song from the album, sung here, says: "κρίμα να χωριστύμε - It's a shame for us to part"!) You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 About Our Goal Our goal is based on the ACTUAL COSTS of recording, mixing, and mastering for both CD/cassette and vinyl; artwork and photography; duplication for a small run of CDs, cassettes and vinyl; video production to support the album's release; shipping fees; and paying the Kickstarter and credit card fees to run this campaign. All of these costs have gone up since we made our last album, but we're committed to putting out a really beautiful album that does service to the songs, with your help. Kickstarter is all or nothing , so if we don't make the full goal by the time our campaign finishes on June 30th, your cards will not be charged and we will not receive any of the funding. But, with your help, we believe we can do it! Thank you for your support in donating, sharing the campaign with your networks, and believing, as we do, that this project deserves to happen! Thank you for checking out our project and for supporting our second album! Благодаря! Дякую! Ευχαριστώ! Paldies! Ačiū! About Trendafilka Trendafilka is a modern exploration of European polyphonic traditions. Founded in New Orleans in 2016, the group grew out of a common desire for group singing as well as a collective fascination of culture and ancient traditions. Trendafilka, named after the Balkan wild rose, draws repertoire from areas of Eastern Europe - the Balkans, the Baltics, the Caucasus, and the Eurasian Steppe - that hold rich cultural traditions of polyphonic singing. Polyphony, from Greek polyphōnos ('many voices') is a style of music that has been found throughout the world since ancient times. Predating Western harmony, polyphonic music is archaic, yet still present to this day, and nearly-universal, occurring on nearly every continent on Earth. The polyphonic textures of Trendafilka's repertoire are often referred to as 'textured' and 'weaving,' as the style of singing is non-hierarchical: all voices represent relatively independent and equally important melodic lines within the song, rather than assigning some voices to dominate while others support. A "lawful coexistence" of vocal lines, polyphonic music requires the performer as well as listener to perceive multiple independent melodies as they simultaneously occur. In Trendafilka's performances, songs from Bulgaria, Serbia, Greece, Latvia, Lithuania, Ukraine, Georgia, and the Roma diaspora are represented in 3, 4, and 5 distinct vocal lines. Dissonances and harmonies weave and swell throughout each song as common human themes are explored: grief, love, loss, jokes, and magic. Shifting between styles with ease, the singers work to blend their nine unique voices into one unified sound, creating a musical tapestry greater than the sum of its original parts.

title: Categories
description: Best Kept Secret This project is really special to me not only because it's my first, but because it contains so much of my personal life. This album is my way of sending a message to the Hip-Hop culture that I love so much, That it's ok to be yourself and express your true feelings in your music, not just the ones that you think are going to generate revenue. On this album I touch on many situation that I myself have lived through and the average person can relate to. Dear Opportunity : Is a detailed letter written out of frustration, because of the lack of limelight on the talented Hip Hop scene in Baltimore. Tears: This song revisits a relationship with a woman that I let go bad due to my immaturity  and one on one convo with my brother that I lost in 1999 Loyalty: A song where I address close friends that turned on me once I decided to change my life and join the military. To My Unborn Child: A father to son talk to my unborn son Zion informing him on the ways of the world and what to expect upon his arrival Plus many more...................... All donations go toward the recording, duplicating, packaging. promotion and distrubution of my first album. I would like thank everyone in advance for your love and support! GOD BLESS!

title: Love Needs Love Recording
description: Elizabeth Tomboulian at Kitano Jazz NYC A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step. This is far from my first step, but it's a huge leap for me. I dipped into my retirement funds to invest about $10K in this project. My vision encompasses forging connections with a global audience who can be uplifted through the music, and promoting the message that LOVE WINS. All it needs is more love. I learned that love changes everything in 2005 in a lecture at UNT with Kweethai Neill . My all-or-nothing goal is $12K to cover: refining the 11 tracks I've recorded, (final vocal recording, mixing, mastering), photography, design, marketing (professional press and radio campaigns), licensing (to pay the composers), duplication, and distribution.  (See Risks and Challenges.) After traipsing through folk, blues, jazz, and pop for many years,  I met my husband Lee and dove head-first into the music of South America and singing scat horn unisons on Circo North/South Convergence and Circo Return to Whenever . I set my career on the back burner and worked at UNT in publishing to provide greater stability to raise a family.  Our move to Wisconsin set me on a track toward Quantum Energetics Structured Therapy and Quantum Sound , where I've learned the power of sound to catalyze wellness. Since we moved to the NYC/NJ area, I produced Lee's Imaginarium solo piano recording and his latest, Interference by the Weekly Reeders. Lee's music is brilliant, and has provided myriad opportunities for me to build my vocal instrument and learn more about music. I've dreamed of recording songs with strong melodies and stories, with my arrangements, for a long time. I began compiling the repertoire and working on arrangements in 2015. After the Orlando shootings in 2016, Stevie Wonder's Love's in Need of Love Today came to mind, and I knew I had my concept for this recording: Love Needs Love. Since then, I've continued to fine-tune the songs, with the unwavering support of Roseanna Vitro , my friend and producer. Lee has been on-board from the beginning, devoting many hours in collaboration.

title: Categories
description: Edge of Morning: Native Voices Speak for the Bears Ears, is an anthology of potent words and story by fifteen native writers who champion the protection of the Bears Ears area. "The necessity of being a voice for the earth is built into our ways of life. We do it for the future of all people but also for the future of our land, flora and fauna." --Cutcha Risling Baldy, Edge of Morning contributor Hardcover Book - Cover Art Detail from "Peaceful Eve" by Shonto Begay (painting image courtesy Modern West Fine Art) Bears Ears. It is a land carrying the past of over thirteen native tribes. It is a land of cultural, spiritual, and ecological significance that records the continuous presence of people for more than ten thousand years. It is a land in critical need of protection. Edge of Morning contributor Martie Simmons (Ho-Chunk) tells us that "to this day, it’s a matter of Land Grab Roulette, no different from broken treaties, a gamble for whose tribe will be next." But this land is not commodity, for contributor Cutcha Risling Baldy (Hupa). "The old stories say we are made of the earth. The earth is like us, living. We are responsible for the earth. The earth is responsible for us. It is not just that land is sacred, it is that land protects, provides and nurtures us, so we should, and it is imperative that we do, the same." Desecration of Bears Ears cultural sites, of the land, scar more than rock and dirt. Contributor Lloyd Lee (Navajo) shows how: "The land is a physical, emotional, psychological, and spiritual presence for the people.  The land is the core of what it means to be human and Native.  Its vitality, energy, and power is reflected in the Native peoples’ narratives." A coalition of five tribal governments —Navajo, Hopi, Ute Indian Tribe, Ute Mountain Ute, and Zuni—has come together to seek protection for the Bears Ears lands in Southern Utah, a place they call both “home” and “soul.” The area’s hundreds of thousands of cultural sites and incomparable wilderness are threatened daily by reoccurring vandalism and looting, careless off-road vehicle use, and devastating intrusion by mining and drilling. Tribal and Federal Group with Bears Ears Buttes - Photo Courtesy of Tim Peterson “We are talking about healing…Ruining the integrity of these lands forever compromises our ability to heal.” —Bears Ears Inter-Tribal Coalition Bears Ears is located in Southern Utah and encompasses 1.9 million acres of ancestral land on the Colorado Plateau. Its diverse topography and unique land forms range in elevation from 3,700 feet to over 11,300 feet. Named for two distinctive buttes, the Bears Ears is bordered by the Colorado River on the west, the San Juan River and Navajo Nation on the south, and the White Mesa Ute Reservation on the East. Abajo Panorama - Photo Courtesy of Tim Peterson “We are a spiritual people. However, our holy practices happen right here on earth, not in a church, but in special places like Bears Ears.” —Bears Ears Inter-Tribal Coalition The anthology will be edited by Jacqueline Keeler of the Navajo and Yankton Dakota tribes and published by Torrey House Press, a nonprofit literary press promoting conservation through literature. The book will be created in collaboration with representatives from the Bears Ears Inter-Tribal Coalition as well as conservation groups, including the Grand Canyon Trust, Utah Diné Bikéyah, and Southern Utah Wilderness Alliance. These groups will use the published book to galvanize support for protecting the area and educate leaders and readers about its natural and cultural significance and the tremendous need to protect this and other sacred landscapes. Edge of Morning will also serve as a tool for other native tribes and coalitions in their efforts to protect sacred places. The Dollhouse - Photo Courtesy of Tom Till The Bears Ears Inter-Tribal Coalition , backed by the support of twenty-one other tribes and pueblos, is petitioning the Obama administration to designate 1.9 million acres as a national monument. The designation would protect the landscape and its significant cultural history, thus offering future generations a connection with ancestral land. If accepted, the proposal will be the first instance of collaborative management between tribes and federal government and bring together both western science and traditional knowledge. If the monument is not designated, the movement to protect the Bears Ears will continue. Either outcome calls for native voices to tell the story of this important area. “We can still hear the songs and prayers of our ancestors on every mesa and in every canyon.” —Malcolm Lehi, Ute Mountain Ute Crane Petroglyph photo credit: Josh Ewing WHAT YOUR GIFT WILL DO: Funding for Edge of Morning will finance printing, marketing and publicity, national distribution to all retail sales channels, editorial support, shipping, contributor coordination by the project editor, and gratis copies delivered to decision makers in Washington, as well as to Bears Ears Inter-Tribal Coalition, Grand Canyon Trust, Utah Diné Bikéyah, and other tribal and conservation groups to help in the fight for Bears Ears. Torrey House Press is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit so all donations are tax deductible. Funds received in excess of goal will finance events that bring anthology contributors together for public readings and discussions. This book will be used to raise awareness and mobilize support for effective protection during the critical management planning process. Edge of Morning will serve as a model and inspiration for other native groups to protect other sacred landscapes. The anthology will be printed as an elegant hardcover book comprised of an introduction, a map, and 15 essays and poems for a total of approximately 150 pages. Torrey House Press has secured a contract with project editor, writer and journalist Jacqueline Keeler (Navajo, Yankton Dakota), who is contacting native writers, scholars, and activists including Linda Hogan, Orlando White, Simon Ortiz, Jim Enote, and Navajo Poet Laureate Luci Tapahonso, as well as undiscovered native writers. Torrey House Press will coordinate cover design and produce early marketing materials including catalogs, online book metadata, and sales kits to help these native voices reach the widest possible audience. The project editor will collect and compile the poems and essays, and Torrey House Press will provide editorial direction and services to ensure a high quality, literary anthology. After editing, the complete manuscript will be sent to a designer for interior layout. Torrey House will have 150 galley copies printed and will mail them to bookseller trade magazines, booksellers, sales reps, and media. Finished books will be printed in November of 2016. Torrey House Press will send copies to Kickstarter contributors, submit the book to award contests, and provide copies to tribal leaders, conservation groups, and elected officials. Torrey House Press will arrange for readings and events with contributors around the country, including possible participation in Bioneers in conjunction with the Grand Canyon Trust. STRETCH GOALS This campaign will cover essential editing, production, and marketing costs, but additional gift will allow us to bring contributors together for events, print additional copies to donate to tribal and rural libraries, and record readings and events. Give today to make this important book of native voices a reality.

title: Your Mom Is An Artist
description: Why have nearly 100% of moms who have read the manuscript for "Your Mom Is An Artist" found themselves somewhere between misty eyes and full on bawling? I believe it's because this book says exactly what all moms need to hear: That they are artists! That their work is immeasurably valuable! That what they create as a mom is far more beautiful than anything made by any other kind of artist in this world! Not only does it communicate these truths, but it causes mom's to look at their own creative processes as far more than just mundane, everyday "mom tasks." Instead, each task is ultimately one more brush stroke towards their great masterpieces! Written for: my magnificent wife along with all other mom artists, especially those who have ever been made to feel that their calling as a mother is somehow lesser than any of the more highly esteemed callings in our society. Written to: my beautiful children, Rowan and Marlee, and to any person who might need to know what a wonderful artist their mom is. Written about: both my wife (throughout the book) as well as my own mom (in the end) - two of the most masterful artists whose incredible creative processes I’ve had the privilege of experiencing first hand. Timeline: Manuscript complete! Illustrations currently in the works - my goal is to finish before end of Kickstarter (03/19) Publishing/printing process to be completed by 04/17 (first edition prints to be shipped on or before this date) Official book launch through Amazon on 4/21 - more than 2 1/2 weeks before Mother’s Day! Funding For: •  🎨 Illustrations •  📚 ISBN registration •  ©️ Copyright registration •  📦 Production and shipping of rewards •  💳 Kickstarter fees and taxes Reward Tiers: Hardcover Copy: This tier includes 1 hardcover copy of Your Mom Is An Artist - signed and numbered (as first additions) with a special hand written message from the author/illustrator. Backers have the option to select up to 100 additional copies of the book under the "add-on's" section. Also included in this tier is 1 digital copy of the book. Digital Copy: This tier includes a digital copy of the Your Mom Is An Artist, which will be available for backers to download shortly after illustrations are complete and pages are properly formatted into digital form. Pledge Without A Reward: For anyone who is not looking to add a book to their collection at this time but still want to support this project on any level, this is the tier for you! Add an amount and know that you've helped to make this dream come true :) Payment and Shipping: How Payment Works: You will only be charged if the campaign successfully funds on March 19. Kickstarter will automatically collect payment at that time using the payment method you provide at checkout. If the campaign is unsuccessful, backers will not be charged. Shipping: Shipping will be charged separately at checkout. Only available in the United States, the cost to ship one hardcover book is $6 ($2 for each additional book). THANK YOU! I am sincerely thankful to all who are able to support me in making this dream of mine come true! Whether you pledge $1 or you buy 100 copies of Your Mom Is An Artist, you are helping to get these books into the hands of countless moms who need to know how valuable they are. You are also making it possible for me to create more books like this one - and yes! There is much more to come :) UPDATES:

title: Categories
description: A picture book by Naoshi that follows the short but endearing life of Tako, filled with humor, joy, and merry friends. ◆ “Why make a picture book about Tako?” I began drawing “Tako” (Japanese for octopus) in 2012. Fascinated by their rounded form and blank expression, I started wondering how they spent their days with their eight arms and legs. They must be able to do so much at once! The more I thought about them, the more I became immersed in the world of Tako. After learning that an octopus' lifespan can be as short as one to two years (*depending on the species), I was struck by their strength to live out their lives in earnest, despite their fate. I was reminded that “time,” which had seemed infinite, is in fact very precious. "TAKO KNOWS" reflects my desire to cherish and enjoy the present moment to the fullest! TAKO ENJOYING WORK! TAKO ENJOYING EXERCISE! TAKO ENJOYING ART! TAKO ENJOYING NATURE! TAKO ENJOYING THE SEASONS! I hope that anyone who reads this book is able to spend their days filled with joy and humor, just like Tako! TAKO KNOWS that your life is filled with brightness! Language: English Size: 6”x 7” (15.2x17.8cm) Details: Hardcover / 92P / Full Color (88 interior pages and 4 inner/outer cover pages) Publisher: Overcup Press , the publisher of my first picture book, “ ICE CREAM WORK ” that came out in 2015. My strong desire to deliver “TAKO KNOWS” to as many people as possible resonated with them, and they kindly agreed to support its publication. *The art and writing for all pages have been completed. After a final review of the layout and text, etc. The files should be ready for print submission in June. ・I will create a 6"x14" (15.2ｘ35.5cm) custom art piece with colored sand based on your request. Please check the video! ◆ “When will I receive my custom artwork?” Since my schedule is full through the end of December 2023 with the “TAKO KNOWS” picture book and merchandise production, as well events including exhibitions and signings, production of the custom art is expected to begin in January 2024 , starting with the first order received. Please note that creating custom artwork takes time, and your patience is greatly appreciated. This reward is not recommended for those who need to receive the artwork in a rush. ・I created this 18"x24" (45.7ｘ60.9cm) artwork with colored sand! The residents of Tako City are enjoying THE GREAT TAKO WAVE! ・Everyone who places an order between May 13 and 15 (the first three days of the campaign) will receive a free sticker sheet ! Add-ons are items that you can purchase in addition to what you already receive with your reward. All items listed below are available as add-ons. ◆Picture Book ”TAKO KNOWS” ($20) ◆Art Print ($25) / Size：8ｘ10 inch (20.3ｘ25.4cm) This is a reproduction of my original SUNAE (sand art), and it has an original texture. ◆SUNAE (Sand Art) DIY Kit ($15) Peel off the stickers and sprinkle sand to make SUNAE (Sand Art)! ◆Sticker Sheet ($5) ◆Washi Tape ($5) ◆T-shirts ($30) Size：S / M / L / XL (100% cotton) ・Unisex fit with a crew neck collar and tightly knit jersey. Hand-printed in Los Angeles. 【Obento Box】 【THE GREAT TAKO WAVE】 You're helping get 1,000 hardcover books of “TAKO KNOWS” into the hands of backers like yourself, bookstores, aquariums and galleries+shops! The money raised through this Kickstarter will go towards book printing, merchandise production, and shipping costs. Around 10% of the proceeds will go to Kickstarter fees. *Kickstarter will only charge backers at the end of the campaign if the project is successfully funded. June 2023　Kickstarter campaign ends. Book sent to print. October 2023　Receive books from printer. (TBD) November 2023　Ship books and rewards to backers. Naoshi is a Japanese SUNAE (sand art) artist based in Los Angeles.  All artworks are made from hundreds of thousands of grains of shiny colorful sand! I started my career as a SUNAE artist in 2004, and since 2007, I've continued to exhibit works in Japan, the United States, Italy, France, England, Germany, China, Korea, Taiwan, Thailand, and other countries. A turning point in my life came in 2012 when I fell off a ladder at home and suffered a serious injury that shattered my back. I was bedridden in the hospital and unable to move as I wanted. Although I felt completely helpless, I became determined to try all the things I had wanted to do, because if my life could change so drastically in the blink of an eye, why should I wait any longer to challenge myself? In 2014, I left the company I had worked at for many years and relocated to Los Angeles, where I continue to take on new challenges to this day. The main theme of “TAKO KNOWS”, which is to live and enjoy the present moment to the fullest, comes from this experience. It would mean so much to me if this picture book about Tako's precious and lovely days reaches the hands and hearts of as many people as possible! Thank you for your support!

title: Categories
description: Killing Our Gods: Essays On Religion, Christianity, And Video Games is a collection of criticism on the bizarre intersections between the divine and technological. While video games sometimes get characterized as an especially profane or childish medium, its hard limits and “little worlds” lend themselves to a curious existentialism. So many games are about defying fate, upending the holy structures that game before, or even just fighting evil monks and priests. Originally a column on the website Uppercut, Killing Our Gods explores the contradictions, contrivances, and cutting insights of this modern medium and its divine discontents. This project will include: All 13 original Killing Our Gods columns, edited with brand new introductions and commentary. Other previous writings that fit the Killing Our Gods model. New essays exclusive to this book, including writings on Mass Effect, Silent Hill, Baldur’s Gate 3, and more. Unique formatting, cover art & illustrations NOTE: The above photo is not representative of the final cover design, colors, font, or format of the final book and is meant entirely as a placeholder. THE FIRST NEW ESSAY Looking for some proof of concept of the new work that will be included in this project? Grace has shared the first new essay for this book on her website and you can read it here ! About Us Grace Benfell (she/her) is a terminally unemployable and forever cranky freelance writer who has written for Paste Magazine, GameSpot, Vice, Bulletpoints Monthly, [lock-on], among many others. She writes fiction about fucked up mormons and guilty women and non-fiction about killing gods, electronic dysphorias, and virtual environmentalism. She creates for herself and for freaks like her. She is a proud member of the National Writer’s Union/Freelance Solidarity Project. She lives in Chicago with her wife and two rabbits. Gina Louise Fowler (she/they) is a book artist, writer, and all-around chaotic creative, who also works as a full-time barista. She makes work about gender, history, language, and politics, and can occasionally be found dreaming up fictional fantasy worlds. Her work has been exhibited internationally and can be found in collections across the country, including The Metropolitan Museum of Art, Bainbridge Island Museum of Art, The University of Miami Special Collections and Baylor University Libraries. She lives in Brooklyn, NY. Gina and Grace first met in Germany through a set of convoluted acquaintances. Afterwards, they both served in the same LDS mission and attended a study abroad together. After they both came out and moved across the country, Gina reached out to Grace on Instagram to collaborate on a book project together. This project is a culmination of each other’s work and an outgrowth of their friendship. The Story of Killing Our Gods Killing Our Gods originated when Grace shot off a tweet about wanting to work on a column about religion and spirituality in RPGs. Uppercut editor Ty Galiz-Rowe offered Grace a spot, an opportunity for which she has ever been grateful. Grace continued to write Killing Our Gods every month, until life circumstances conspired to put the project on hiatus. The column occupied a unique place in games writing, somewhere between creative non-fiction and materialist-minded critique. Every Killing Our Gods column was prefaced with the following phrase: “Killing Our Gods is a monthly column from Grace Benfell about Christianity, religion, and role-playing through a queer, Marxist, and lapsed Mormon lens.” While these elements inform all of Grace’s work, it was in Killing Our Gods where those influences found their most sincere expression. This book provides an opportunity for Grace to write more essays in this vein, but at a more ambitious scope and in a wider timeframe. A book affords space for deeper, more complex criticism and the time needed to pull it off. From a publishing standpoint, this will be the first essay collection published by The Puzzled Press and the first major collaboration done by Gina under this imprint. This is an opportunity for gamers, book people, and special collections to come together and support independent writers, artists, and non-traditional publishing. As always, Gina is looking for opportunities to expand the boundaries of what is possible with the book form, while making work that is as accessible as possible. Funding Breakdown As writers and artists, one of the most consistent barriers that we face is the undervaluing of our time and labor. On the other hand, we are both passionate about making work that is accessible to the widest possible audience. We had worked hard to develop and plan a project that can achieve both of these goals, and we are also committed to being transparent with you about where the funding from this campaign will go. Our initial goal is $6000 , which is truly the minimum that we believe we can produce this project for, without compromising the value of our labor or the quality of the final product. If we reach that goal, the money will be broken down as follows: The initial funding breakdown will scale up related to the increased costs (i.e. more support from you means more books that we need to make and more materials for the initial production run), however, we do have two stretch goals that we believe will elevate this project! Timeline Producing a book at any scale is a large undertaking and we have been doing a lot of work leading up to this project to be as prepared as possible. That said, the most time-consuming element of this project is that every. single. book. will be handbound. The timeline below is reflective of this, and represents the time that we believe it will take to produce the books as rewards for this project. That means that backing this Kickstarter is the way to guarantee that you can have this book in your hands at the earliest possible date!

title: Categories
description: THE FIRST PROOF IS IN We received the first proof from the printer and it looks terrific. No changes necessary, so we put in an order for all of the first editions. They should be here by Tuesday and will be in the mail to you a few days after that. It's a beauty. Stay tuned for more sneak peaks... Our Story Since 2008, Nowhere has been devoted to longform, immersive literary travel writing—that doesn't just tell a story, but takes you to a place. We don't run top-10 lists. We don't review spas. We published more than 1,000 travel stories from 65 different countries exclusively online—until last year when a banner Kickstarter campaign helped us launch our first Print Annual. Kickstarter supporters will receive a special "First Edition" cover and a travel planner in the back of the book. After four print runs, last year's Print Annual made its way to mailboxes, back pockets and coffee tables around the world. We were incredibly honored when Bill Bryson selected one of the stories for the 2016 Best American Travel Writing anthology. The annual is printed in the U.S. with color images and a durable matte cover than can travel the world with you. Kickstarter supporters will receive a special "First Edition" cover, a travel planner in the back of the book and an Acknowledgements page listing all Kickstarter supporters who donate $50 or more before November 27. In the 2017 Annual: Drew Bratcher writes a "Letter from Istanbul" Jeannine Ouellette follows her childhood through the American West Josh Weiner crafts a portrait of Kosovan Jews Richard Hague floats Kentucky's Big Bone River Douglas Despres photographs the Standing Rock Sioux protest Nick Flynn scavenges for collages in Zanzibar Colleen Khachatourians gets lost in Saigon Kali VanBaale grows up in drought-plagued Iowa Corinna Cook discovers life and death on an Alaskan ferry Bill Bryson selected a story from last year's Print Annual for the 2016 Best American Travel Writing anthology. With your support, the journal will be printed in early December. Early-release issues will be sent to Kickstarter supporters before Christmas, along with other rewards. (See below for reward choices and details.) For almost a decade, we have published stories based on their quality, efficiency, detail and artfulness. The destination is secondary. What we like is the writing, photos, narrative skips and jumps and scraps of detail most other travel magazines would leave out. We like to put the reader in the setting. Publishing in print form means you can now take the stories with you anywhere. We hope you support this project and become a part of the Nowhere journey. Rewards! First Edition Kickstarter Print Annual 2017 Nowhere Lit Kit Nowhere Passport Wallet Flower Prints Nowhere in the Catskills Retreat

title: Troll Under the Bridge – A WWII Fantasy Adventure for All
description: Set during the height of World War II, Troll Under the Bridge follows brothers Tye (10) and Dash (6) Dansbury as they’re evacuated from bomb-ravaged London to Alston , a quiet town tucked into the mountains of Northern England. But Alston is older than the war. Older than the coal mines. Older than anyone remembers. Strange things stir beneath its crumbling bridge. Missing sheep. Whispered runes. A story too old to be told aloud. At the heart of it is a troll—neither protector nor monster, but something in between. Exiled. Observing. Waiting. And it just saw them. Historical Authenticity : Carefully researched WWII-era England setting, accurately depicted landscapes, and integration of real English folklore (including historical "troll graves"). Rich, Diverse Characters : From the courageous brothers facing wartime fears, their diverse, loving family, to unforgettable side characters like mysterious charm-makers and misunderstood landowners, every character is deeply developed and relatable. Thoughtful Fantasy : Magic in "Troll Under the Bridge" is subtle, meaningful, and nuanced—anchored in historical reality but still sparking wonder and imagination. Emotional Depth : Themes of family, friendship, courage, loss, and the complexity of morality provide layers of emotional resonance that readers young and old will find deeply moving. What It Means One of the characters — a boy in the forest — is inspired by Zane , my youngest son’s real-life best friend. Zane is bravely fighting terminal brain cancer, and this story ensures his light will never fade. What Makes This Story Special Historical Authenticity: Deeply researched 1940s England setting, complete with real details about WWII evacuations and northern mining towns. Grounded Fantasy: Magic in this world is quiet, broken, and fading. It's not showy—it’s spiritual and old. A Different Kind of Magic : This isn’t a wand-waving world. Magic is broken, buried, and fading—but it’s still there, if you know where to look. Emotional Resonance: At its heart, this is a story about grief, courage, and brotherhood. The weight of promises. This is a fantasy that honors the reality of childhood in a broken world. Memorable Characters: A racially mixed family, a battle-scarred miner, a misunderstood landowner, and a wild-eyed drunk who just might be telling the truth. What Your Support Will Do Print full-color copies of both the YA novel and the children’s picture book Fund professional illustrations and consistent character design Create high-quality storyboards and a map of Alston Produce a pitch-ready animated pilot for YouTube or Amazon (if stretch goals are met) Offer unique rewards like stickers, art prints, original music, and more Digital + Audio Stretch Goal — Unlocked at $55,000 If we pass our initial goal, we’ll make Troll Under the Bridge even more accessible. This stretch goal unlocks: A professionally produced audiobook of the YA novel—featuring immersive narration, character voices, and original sound design A fully formatted Kindle edition of both books, complete with embedded art and accessibility features for readers on the go Every backer at the $15 level and up will receive the digital editions, and all $35+ backers will receive the full audiobook as part of their reward. This is about bringing the story to more families and more kids , no matter how they prefer to experience it. Super Stretch Goal — Animated Pilot Episode (at $110,000) If we reach this milestone, we’ll bring Troll Under the Bridge to life in motion. Our team will create a 15-minute animated pilot episode , adapted from the opening chapters of the novel and drawn in the signature storybook style that defines the project. Expect layered sound, a haunting score, and a deeply faithful tone—not a cartoon, but a cinematic fantasy short. This pilot will be used to pitch the series to streaming platforms, schools, and libraries. It’s the next step in building a world meant to grow—book by book, child by child. 🔹 $250+ backers are credited as Patrons 🔹 $1,000+ backers help shape the pilot and appear in the lore Help us make magic move. Bringing the World to Life Bringing the World to Life Funds raised will support: Professional illustration of characters, key scenes, and historically accurate maps of Langmere. High-quality storyboarding to maintain visual consistency. Detailed development of unique, emotionally resonant artwork for critical story moments, such as the evocative first meeting with the troll and poignant flashbacks to wartime London. Concept Artwork Tye and Dash fine a lost Teddy Bear on the Exodus train. Different Concepts of How I Wanted the Family to Appear. These make it easy to print out and paste on my wall to stare at for hours. Join the Adventure! Your support is essential in bringing Troll Under the Bridge to life. Let's create something truly magical together—an unforgettable adventure waiting to be shared. Back this project and help launch a world where forgotten magic, ancient creatures, and brave children intersect. This isn’t just a book—it’s the start of something bigger. New Contributor I am excited to announce an addition to the story. I would like to welcome Shelley to the world of Troll Under the Bridge. She will serve as internal editor, brain wrangler, and guiding star. Shelley S brings over 30 years of experience in education, with a rich background that includes teaching, literacy coaching, and special education support. A passionate advocate for student growth, she has dedicated her career to empowering both learners and educators.  As an avid reader, Shelley finds joy in sharing her love of books and is especially passionate about inspiring a lifelong love of reading in children of all ages.

title: Slock - Wearable Alarm Clock. Stop Snoozing.
description: The Slock uses a Force Sensitive Resistor to detect up to 40 pounds of weight evenly across it's surface, so it won't stop vibrating until you walk around! This is the cure to snoozing. 57 percent	of	Americans are	snoozers, according	to	a	sleep	study	by	health	innovation	company	Withings,	with	results	showing	that we	spend	a	total	of	three	and	a	half months	of	our	lives	hitting	the	snooze button . Source When you let yourself snooze, your body thinks, “False alarm! I guess I didn’t need to do anything, because we’re not getting up after all,” and settles in. When that loud alarm clock goes off a second time, Rafael Pelayo, MD, a sleep specialist at the Stanford University Sleep Medicine Center, says that your body and brain are taken by surprise, resulting in that groggy, fuzzy-headed feeling called sleep inertia. This type of sleep inertia can persist for up to two to four hours , research has found. Source The Slock's Vibration Motors are far quieter than a traditional alarm clock, allowing you to wake up before your significant other without disturbing them! Wake up early and make them breakfast, or get to work and let him/her sleep in! This a demo of a beta version of the app. We have a few exciting features we will be adding before the Slock is shipped. We are going to add a timer mode so that you can easily nap with the Slock, and we are going to add controls which let you set the vibration level! We are going to build an Android app after a successful funding campaign. We currently have the color choices of Black, Pink, and Blue, but we are going to add more with Stretch Goals later on in the Kickstarter campaign. The Slock fits US Shoe Size 6-12, Euro 36-43, and UK 4-10 comfortably.

Nothing about the Modular Wrench system is off-the-shelf. Every handle, extension, and wrench end is engineered to replace the conventional tools that we've used for years. These are 100% custom manufactured to demanding standards from only the finest of raw materials. The tooling, templates, and specialized machining fixtures are just as unique and customized to production as are the tools in your new Modular Moto Wrench Tool Set. Strong Enough? We've added a short video demonstration to the 1st update (Updates Tab). Bench testing of these handles, inserts, and extensions with my full body weight throughout development using a large bolt chucked in a vise, assured that these new tools are strong. Testing strength was not one of the questions that I expected to come up, else detailed testing results would have been included in that first video. You'll find a quick, low-budget video that I did this week trying to twist and break large bolts and new tools. I'm proud to say that the tools stood up to the torture of 153 lbs/ft of torque as you'll see and my breaker bar configuration stood up to 102.1 lbs/ft of torque. These are 4 ~ 5 times what most 6mm, 8mm, and 10mm threaded fasteners are rated for - you'll break most machines that you're working on before my tools. I do have to report though, that the 1/4" Hex to 3/8" Square adapter bit did yield at about 115 lbs/ft - The RRR Modular Moto Wrenches and inserts passed with flying colors. Please check out the 1st Updates tab for a link to the strength testing video. Get yours now and store one under the seat, in a tankbag, a tailbag, a pocket, a kitchen drawer, or in your glovebox!
