title: Megamark: The Robot That Can Do Everything*
description: The Choitek Megamark is an advanced full-size humanoid mobile manipulator robot platform capable of performing virtually any human-size task, such as watering plants, cleaning rooms, feeding pets, making meals, delivering coffee, playing piano, laser sword dueling and more, built for students, artists, educators, hobbyists and researchers. Features / Specs of the Megamark: The Megamark boasts a rugged all-metal aluminum body, cross-platform laptop-for-a-face, expandable open hardware, compatible open software, and adaptable end effectors. The Choitek Megamark combines especially well with 3D printers for designing custom grippers. Batteries: The Megamark is powered by two 12V 8AH SLA batteries. Under normal load, these batteries are expected to last roughly 8 hours, and can be recharged by a standard 12V SLA charger. Processor : The Megamark is powered by an Arduino Mega 2560, which connects to the robot's laptop-for-a-face with a standard USB A-to-B serial cable. Construction: The base, arms, and chest can be attached and detached for easy transportation and storage. There are plenty of mounting holes to add custom sensors and electronics. How to use the Megamark: The Megamark is intended to be an educational and experimental robotics research platform. Although prior robotics engineering experience is not required, a willingness to learn programming and basic electronics is highly recommended. The Megamark will work especially well as an educational robotics platform to teach and inspire students into STEM. Students learn both the hardware and software sides of engineering by designing the robot's grippers and programming the robot through its laptop-for-a-face to do various tasks: First, dream up a task you want the robot to do. Feel free to dream big! Robotics is still a growing frontier in technology and we want you to be a part of it! Need a robot movie actor for a WWII film? You bet! Second, customize your robot by adding sensors and attachments. Every gripper is designed to perform a different and unique task; learn about hardware design by building your own or 3D printing the ones we've already designed and tested ! Grabbing ducks? No problem! Finally, use the robot's laptop-for-a-face (not included) to program the robot in your favorite language. Currently, Python 2.7 and Arduino are supported. We are in the process of adding support to more languages such as Unity, Processing, and RobotC. Arduino-based controller makes programming the Megamark a snap! Stretch Goals: If we hit $18,000, we will deliver two extra sets of 3D-printed grippers to each backer that gives at least $1,300. If we hit the full $20,000, we will deliver four extra sets of 3D-printed grippers to each backer that gives at least $1,300. For both stretch goals, backers can choose from the designs below (or wait until we have more designs available): Each gripper is designed to perform a unique task. *BIG FAT DISCLAIMER: PLEASE READ!* We'll be completely open and honest with you: although the Megamark is technically capable of doing virtually any human task, the Megamark does not do any task particularly well yet. DO NOT USE THE MEGAMARK ROBOT TO PERFORM CRITICAL OR DANGEROUS TASKS! UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCES WILL CHOITEK BE RESPONSIBLE IF A MEGAMARK ROBOT ATTEMPTS TO TAKE OVER THE WORLD, SO ALWAYS USE UNDER DIRECT HUMAN SUPERVISION AT ALL TIMES! (*Otherwise, you might end up like this plant.*) More things you can do with the Megamark! Deliver coffee to your coworkers! Hand out goodies at events! Robot: What is my purpose? Scientist: YOU PASS BUTTER. Special thanks: We couldn't have done it without all of the amazing people, places and things we've worked with over the course of the past four years, so a special thank you to all of the following schools, organizations, programs and initiatives that have helped propel our project into the future: You are all awesome - thank you!

title: Categories
description: Making music is a lot like starting a business. More often than not, what ends up being more important than the idea or the funding is the team itself. This is even more difficult in music than it is in business. It's hard to bring together all the right people, let alone find them. We aim to fix that. As musicians who have spent countless months looking for people to collaborate with, we want to make that easier for everybody. Noted is about more than posting and browsing content. It's about an experience that brings people together. People with shared musical interests in a setting where everyone gets to collaborate and benefit from the process. Noted will be a community. Noted will revolutionize how local music works. What do we need the money for? So far, the Noted team has been self-funded. The Noted team is raising funds so that we can expand our server space, pay rent for space to work in, and get our application's development completed and maintained on various mobile operating systems. While we are doing all of the design and initial development within our team, we need to go outside our team's abilities for some of the cross-platform development. We aren't stopping here. We have brought Noted this far, and find it to be an amazing product, but there are always ways to improve. We will continuously update Noted with new features that we hope will excite and engage people who love music. Most importantly, we will continue to develop a new way to find your city's music. Although we can create the world's best way for musicians to meet and get together, it takes YOU to make it worthwhile. Every new user brings value to the network, and allows us to better tailor Noted to the people who use it. We want to share Noted with the world, especially once we finish development, but we can't do it alone. The rest is up to you. Help us create the future of your city's music.

title: Categories
description: "Lele Goes Out to Play" is a bilingual (English, traditional Chinese & Pinyin) book about a boy's adventures in Hong Kong with his monkey! The playful story helps young children develop everyday social language. We really hope our readers will enjoy learning Chinese while going on everyday adventures with Lele and Monkey. "Lele Goes Out to Play" is a 10x10 hardcover picture book with 32 full-color pages of vibrant illustrations that showcase his hometown. Check out a few pages here. This book is written with both native and non-native speakers in mind!  Each page contains Chinese characters, Pinyin (phonetic spelling based on Mandarin pronunciation), and English! Ebook (PDF) Perfect to engage your little one on the go, in line at the store, waiting for appointments... the possibilities are endless. Bilingual activity sheets (PDF) Color, draw and match in Mandarin (with Pinyin) or English with Lele and Monkey! Perfect for your little one at home. Includes 5 pages of the following activities: - Shadow matching - Counting & number recognition - Line drawing - Maze solving - Item search - Coloring Magnetic playbook Extend the fun with Lele and Monkey by playing with them in this magnetic book (8.5x11" when folded)! Featuring 20 magnets, recreate or get creative with two scenes from the book. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Interactive* & printable bilingual poster (PDF) A full color 11.5x8" printable poster of Lele's playground. Objects are labeled with bilingual text. Click* on the name of objects to hear how they are pronounced and used in a sentence! *To interact with the poster, Adobe Acrobat Reader on a computer is required. Not tablet/smartphone compatible. Audio Reading of Book (MP3) Take a moment for yourselves as your kids listen to this dramatized reading of "Lele Goes Out to Play". Hi, I’m Tiffany! My husband, Tim, and I are parents to two wonderful and adopted children — Lele and Meimei. I believe in the power of reading and finding joy in books, but I have not been very successful in finding books where my kids can see themselves as the main character in the story. This inspired me to create books for other children and parents like myself who may be wanting to normalize the idea of Asian kids in books. Why Bilingual? As an American Born Chinese mom, I struggled with teaching my kids Chinese but eventually stumbled upon self-published bilingual books. I found them to be such a great resource in helping me read and teach Chinese to my kids. Since there are not that many on the market, I decided to create one in hopes that it will help other parents who are learning or trying to teach the Chinese language as a non-native speaker. Language This first book will be in English, Traditional Chinese (TC) with Pinyin. Growing up in a Taiwanese American home and now living in Hong Kong, TC has always been the more practical version of Chinese to learn. It is not easy to find books with both TC and Pinyin so I decided to create one. If there is enough interest (ie we reach our stretch goals), I can open up options for Cantonese-Jyutping and Simplified Chinese-Pinyin. I hope you'll support this book to bring more diversity into children's literature! September - Kickstarter Completes & Files sent to printers November - Printing Completes & Books Shipped to the U.S. January - Books Shipped to Backers for the New Year! Thank you for your support!!!

title: Categories
description: The ABCs of Grief is a children’s book that walks through each letter of the alphabet to unpack different aspects of the grief experience. Grief is messy, confusing, and overwhelming for all ages—but especially young children who are trying to make sense of the big changes and losses happening around them. Grief is something EVERYONE will face at some point in their lives. Yet, grief can be difficult to navigate without having supports in place. This book is exactly that, a gentle support, holding your hand and validating the experience each step of the way. As a Certified Child Life Specialist and a bereaved mother , grief has played a major role in my life over the last 15+ years . I spent 14 years working in the hospital setting, including 8 years at Johns Hopkins Medicine children’s hospitals, and nearly 2 years in private practice supporting grieving children and families. In my practice and at home, books have been a huge source of comfort, validation, education, and an invaluable avenue for further expressive exploration for children. High-quality books can absolutely make a life-long impact for children. I feel passionate that this is one of those books that will deeply impact grieving children and families, giving them concrete tools and language to navigate these difficult experiences. Please help me place this book in as many homes, classrooms, libraries, grief centers, and hospitals as possible. For Children facing grief related to: death, moving, illness, traumatic medical experiences, new diagnoses, natural and manmade disasters, big changes with school or other activities, feeling ‘different’ from others, abuse, immigration, foster care, absent or distant caregivers, and more. Also perfect for: Parents, Schools, Libraries, Hospitals, Therapy Offices, Grief Centers and More. The books will be: Hardcover,  Offset Printed,  8x10 book on high-quality paper, printed end-pages. The ABCs of Grief is written, edited, illustrated, designed, and READY TO PRINT. My first book, Forever Connected, was published without Kickstarter. So, you may be asking what is different this time. Why the need for Kickstarter? I am determined to offer an offset print, which gives you the highest quality book possible. Without Kickstarter, my little business would not have the capacity to fund such a high-quality print, which requires an order of 1000+ books minimum. I want to give you a chance to secure your own copy of The ABCs of Grief in the first print run AND give you the opportunity to take part in some incredibly meaningful rewards! So where is the fundraising money going exactly? Below is the breakdown of the costs for this project. Jessica Correnti has spent the last 15+ years working with grieving children within her professional role as a Certified Child Life Specialist (CCLS) at several different children's hospitals and within her private practice, Kids Grief Support. Jessica has a bachelors in education of young children with a minor in psychology and a dual masters in healthcare administration and community health. Jessica lives in Baltimore, MD with her husband and children. During her free time, Jessica can be found enjoying time outdoors with her family, drinking coffee, trying to catch the perfect sunrise/sunset, and likely exploring house hunting apps for her dream beach house. Follow her on Instagram @kidsgriefsupport. Rachel Nieman has been living in a creativity filled world since she has a kid. Constantly doodling as though she was born with a pencil and a brush in her hand, it was a no brainer for her to pursue art as her career. After receiving her bachelor of Fine Arts Degree from the University of Cincinnati, Rachel moved to Huston, Texas where she set up shop drawing, painting, and creating to her heart's content. Rachel's work features hand-painted illustrations and pattern using watercolor and gouache. She is primarily inspired by her imagination and travels. In her free time, you will find Rachel traveling and hiking with her husband Ricky, reading a book, or taking a walk with her pup-assistant Frankie. Follow Rachel on Instagram @artbynieman. DOUBLE OUR GOAL: 14k Raised 50 books donated to hospitals/grief centers. QUADRUPLE OUR GOAL: 28k Raised 100 books donated to hospitals/grief centers. 5x OUR GOAL: 35k Raised add an additional 1,000 books to the print run (print 2k books). 8x OUR GOAL: 60k Raised, illustrations for book 2 in the series paid for. Step 1: Decide which reward tier you'd like. Step 2: Create a Kickstarter account and determine your payment method. Step 3: The countdown begins! At the end of 30 days, if the campaign was a success, your account will be charged and your reward will be on its way. (If the campaign does not meet its goal, you will not be charged) Step 4: Spread the word! Facebook and Instagram posts, TikToks, Threads, Pins, Texts, Emails, anything! The more people we can reach, the more likely this book is to be placed where it's so very needed.

title: Launch an Aber Stoat Books Vending Machine in North County
description: Project story I’m expanding my established online bookstore into the physical world with custom book vending machines that stock up to 280 books each, arranged in four rows with 14-16 rotating selections of hand-picked fantasy, romance, and thriller titles. Each machine costs around $7,000 before professional wrapping with eye-catching custom designs, stocking with fresh books, and expert installation/transport to ensure they’re placed perfectly and securely. The estimated start-up funds needed are $18,000-$20,000. I have set the goal at $10,000 to cover the cost of the first machine, design wrapping, and freight charges. Any funds raised over the goal will be applied to the starting inventory and additional machines for additional locations. Why I care: Reading these genres has been my escape and passion for years; sweeping worlds, heart-racing tension, steamy connections, and I want to make them instantly accessible to more people. North County San Diego has vibrant communities, coffee shops, shopping centers, and local businesses full of potential readers who might not make it to a traditional bookstore but would love a quick, exciting find during their day. This isn’t just vending; it’s about sparking joy, inspiring new readers, and building a community network in which local spots host these machines to add a unique, literary touch to their spaces. How we’ll make it happen: Funds will cover the first machines, custom wraps, book inventory, professional moving/installation, and initial outreach to businesses. Hosts (cafes, malls, boutiques) contact me to partner; no cost to them beyond providing the space, while I handle maintenance, restocking, and curation. We’ll begin at high-traffic spots like the Shoppes at Carlsbad and expand across North County as demand grows. Who I am: I’m Mandy, a Carlsbad-based bookmonger running an online bookstore focused on fantasy, romance, and thrillers. Established in January 2024, I’ve built a loyal following through curated selections and passionate recommendations. Now I’m ready to take it offline also, blending my love of books with innovative vending to bring stories to where people already are. Back this project to help launch the first wave and turn North County into a hotspot for spontaneous literary adventures!

title: OLIFT Magazine
description: BUT IF YOU'RE WONDERING "WHY A PRINT MAGAZINE IN 2015,"... Two words: credibility and timeliness. Our goal is to secure additional funding to help take the magazine from a startup phase to a sustainable publication. We want to make it easier to access the magazine, we want to raise awareness about weightlifting, and we want to attract quality advertisers. WHERE YOUR DONATIONS WILL GO OLIFT needs $20,000. The breakdown: 1. The 2015 Summer and Fall Issues ($12,000) 2. Kickstarter rewards & postage ($6,000) 3. Ongoing event coverage & promotion ($2,000) With this traction, we are confident that we will attract several advertisers and subscribers to become a sustainable media company. BECOME AN ADVERTISING PARTNER For detailed OLIFT advertising information, click here to see our media card. WHAT YOU GET FOR DONATING Before we go any further, we want you guys to know that we are not just a magazine, we support the local weightlifting community. With that said, we will provide a 10% of the subscription profits as donations to a weightlifting club each time we print an issue. OLIFT Magazine - Summer 2015 PAPERLESS - Digital copies of our Limited Edition 80 page Summer 2015 Issue are on offer for those pledging $10. Designed so well and packed with premium weightlifting content. Don't forget to download a copy of The O ~ It’s FREE, as always! OLIFT Magazine - Summer 2015, Digital Copy The O - Issue #3 Summer 2015 The first 200 backers will be rewarded with the Summer 2015 Issue delivered ANYWHERE in the world for just $20. OLIFT "RAISE THE BAR" T-Shirt in Heather Red OLIFT "RAISE THE BAR" T-Shirt in Black INDIVIDUALLY SPONSORED PAGE - A $250 donation earns you a dedicated page in the Summer 2015 issue - 50 characters acknowledging you at the bottom of a page ***All perks, including the SUMMER 2015 ISSUE of OLIFT Magazine, will be shipped September 1, 2015. Delivery dates will vary based on location. ENDORSEMENTS BOB TAKANO, USA Weightlifting Hall of Fame - " The sport of weightlifting needs a regularly scheduled publication to keep athletes and coaches involved and to provide a gateway for newcomers.  OLIFT magazine is just that.  We need to support OLIFT for the sport and for ourselves!" HARVEY NEWTON, 1984 USA Olympic Team Coach - “ OLIFT remains the finest lifting publication available in this country at the time,” says Coach Newton. “Lifters and coaches need to support this venture and keep it going. ” Harvey Newton was USA Weightlifting’s first national coach, executive director, and a lead instructor for coaching certification courses. His website is www.newton-sports.com. WIL FLEMING, All-American Athlete & Co-Owner of Force Fitness and Performance - "OLift magazine is going to continue to be part of the explosive growth of Olympic Weightlifting in the United States. Their mission is to educate and expose great ideas to weightlifters and as such are an extremely valuable part of the community of weightlifting in the United States and abroad!" Wil is the co-host of The Performance Podcast . JIM SCHMITZ, US Olympic Team Coach 1980, 88, & 92, USAW President 1988-96 - "I think OLIFT Magazine is the best pure Olympic Style Weightlifting magazine to come along in a long time.  It has great articles about lifters, coaches, training and competing.  You don’t need to be a competitive weightlifter, anyone who does snatches and clean and jerks, pulls, squats and any and all lifts that will improve your strength, power, and athleticism would benefit from reading the very informative and interesting articles and should subscribe to OLIFT.  OLIFT is a great source of Olympic Lifting information, it’s beautifully and professionally designed and laid out." BECOME AN ADVERTISING PARTNER For detailed OLIFT advertising information, click here to see our media card. - For Press Release: PDF , https://goo.gl/6HAkui - For High Resolutions Photos: https://goo.gl/8JNBQy - Contact us for Press/Distributions at: anton@theoliftmag.com OUR TEAM Behind OLIFT is a team of weightlifters with a variety of professional skill sets: Anton Jefferson - OLIFT Founder/Publisher/CEO, is a nationally-competitive weightlifter and coach with a plethora of experiences in print, design, journalism, communications and advertising. Anton has dedicated his life to Olympic weightlifting and luckily, he knows how to build and manage a magazine. Elliot Bauer - Co-Founder & CFO, is the creator of Olystats.com , one of the most revolutionary web resources in the weightlifting world. He’s also just completed his Juris Doctorate at UC Davis School of Law and he has worked with California-based tech startups. Taylor Chiu - Co-Founder & CIO, from olympicweightliftingguru.com , has over a decade of Olympic weightlifting experience. He is a former Marine and an entrepreneur in many different industries. He wrote a book on weightlifting and he’s well qualified to be on the editorial team for OLIFT Magazine. Mindy Houser , our Editor-in-Chief, is an almost-Masters weightlifter, wife, and mother of 3. She depends on strong coffee to power her through juggling a startup fitness business and renovating her home at the same time. Mindy is a grammar geek and she enjoys reading and learning about new weightlifting concepts and philosophies. Michelle Mok , is our Creative Director with a BFA in Visual Communications from the Illinois Institute of Art in Chicago. She's a weightlifter and a meticulous designer who is always open to learning something new. She's also the resident photographer for Sayre Park Weightlifting Club; check out some of her photos, graphics and videos on her Facebook page . We have a growing team of renowned writers, designers, and photographers who share a common passion for weightlifting and want to spread weightlifting to the world!

title: Don the Moron Book & Doll
description: Book Cover This holiday season, I set about to make a fun gift for my family and friends. I wrote a rhyming children's parody loosely inspired by the "Elf on the Shelf" books. The focus of Don the Moron is to stop the President from tweeting! The book is 30 pages (11" x 8.5") with beautiful illustrations in true children's book fashion. Some samples are below. Book Sample Book Sample 2 Book Sample 3 In keeping with the theme, I designed a doll which would accompany the books and serve as a fun little game. I quickly realized that making these dolls is not cheap. In order to complete the project, I need help! I can't manufacture these dolls unless I will be making about 1,000 of them. I hope you enjoy the Don the Moron previews and will support this project! Quick Doll Facts: 8-10 inches in height Molded plastic head with plush body (think Woody from Toy Story) Anatomically accurate:  extremely small hands & terrible hair Navy suit, red tie, and Presidential American Flag pin Help make this book a reality!!!

title: Categories
description: For the past two years, Life & Thyme has been publishing stories on a weekly basis, covering food culture and the people behind it, exclusively on our website . These are stories about real people that work tirelessly in order to feed us . We have interviewed chefs, bakers, restaurateurs, farmers, coffee roasters, cocktail makers, handcrafted apron makers, all sorts of purveyors, or even grandmothers that contribute a masterful skill to the food, beverage, and hospitality industry from around the globe—whether it be in a professional kitchen or someone's home.  Our content is typically long-form with an editorial sensibility and focused on people, rather than the latest trend on a plate. Storytelling is our mantra, while also executing original photography and filmmaking on an online platform dedicated to a variety of content related to food and its lifestyle. Through a global network of hand-selected and talented contributors, our stories and content go beyond our home state of California and into other culinary destinations such as New York, Nashville, Austin, Portland, Chicago, Mexico, Colombia, Rwanda, Scotland, Taiwan, Turkey, Japan, India, and many others. We are constantly looking to collaborate with talented creatives in order to showcase their work and knack for storytelling. Where there is good food, there is culture and stories to be told. The Next Chapter: Life & Thyme going to print. There will always be something special about a beautifully designed print publication. Whether it’s the smell of ink on paper or simply the opportunity to shift our gaze away from a screen, print offers a certain tactile sensation that an online experience simply never can. So we want to extend Life & Thyme’s timeless culinary stories into the real world, by creating a quarterly print magazine that offers fresh and new content about the people that drive the food world forward. The Life & Thyme Magazine will be printed in full-color on a high quality paper stock, run between 96 to 120 pages, and is projected to publish on a quarterly basis across the globe. We need your help to make this dream a reality. In order to bring this dream project to life, we are asking for your help! By pledging—and being the first to pre-order a copy of Issue 1—you are helping us fund print production and the creation of stories for issue 1 of the Life & Thyme Magazine. Even a dollar short of our goal, we lose it all. Every little bit counts and we hope you can join us in being the voice for those that feed us . What people in the industry are saying " I discovered Life & Thyme through a good foodie friend.  He described the site appropriately as the most beautiful, romantic and cinematic content on food and beverage on the web. We've since had the great fortune to work together, be part of their story (and one of their stories), and continue to be inspired and energized by the work they do and what they contribute to the landscape of makers." —David Kaplan Proprietors LLC. (HoneyCut, Death & Co., NiteCap NYC) "Life & Thyme is bringing respect back to the craft of food writing. They remind me of reading the New Yorker. It's a collection of essays that speak to and about a core group of craftsmen who spend their life trying to perfect details. It's not critique, it's not gossip, it's the observance of their slice of life." —David Nayfeld Chef, Eleven Madison Park, Fifty Seven "Life & Thyme really captures the true essence of the kitchen.  Their incredible ability to harness the raw emotion and passion that goes into what we do, leaves me speechless. I look forward to working with them in the future.....as often as possible!" —Bruce Kalman Chef, Union Restaurant, CHOPPED Winner, Knife Fight Winner " I was most impressed with their approach.  Life and Thyme showed up, were a fly on the wall, asked questions and observed.  What they did then, and do best, is to find a story and tell it.  The content is varied, interesting, artistic, attractive and relevant.  Im a huge fan of the site and cant wait to see what they do next." —Greg Daniels Chef, Salt Air, Perry St. The Rewards. Aside from being the first to pre-order Issue 1 of the Life & Thyme magazine (by pledging at least $25 USD), we have included many other great rewards to choose from. Below are some of our favorites: ISSUE ZERO In order to prove the concept and to show a prototype, we created a 64 page ISSUE ZERO of what the Life & Thyme Magazine will look like and feel like in printed form. An incredibly limited print run was made for this issue which includes some of our favorite stories from lifeandthyme.com. Such stories include: a Chef Portrait on Ludo Lefebvre, Hay Rosie Ice Cream Co. out of Brooklyn, Kimisagara Market out of Rwanda, Guerrilla Tacos out of LA, a trip to the Santa Monica farmers market with a Michelin-starred chef, a photo essay on Taipei's night markets, and other great stories. Starting at the $50 pledge , you can get your hands on a digital copy of Issue Zero. However, we have only TWO printed copies to give away. They will never be sold and they're reserved for any two incredibly generous $1500 pledgers. We're not kidding on the limited print run. The L&T Team will even sign them to show our deepest appreciation! L&T FARMERS MARKET TOTE BAG We love taking tote bags to the farmers market, so we'd love for you to have one too! Starting at the $75 pledge , we will throw in an exclusive L&T Tote Bag. EXCLUSIVE PHOTO PRINTS Photography plays a big role over at Life & Thyme. We work with talented photographers in order to produce our stories and we're giving away a limited quantity of the following photos for certain reward tiers: by L&T Photographer Josh Telles by L&T Founder Antonio Diaz by L&T Photographer Alicia Cho

title: Categories
description:
Hearth Magazine What is Hearth? It seems like a simple
question but when you sit back and really dive into the complexities, it
becomes a multifaceted question. No two people can answer that question the
same because is different for everyone that picks up a copy or strolls through
our online journal. To us, Hearth Magazine is a platform for
dreamers to tell their stories. It’s an opportunity to share their passions
with other like-minded people who appreciate the time, skill and determination
it takes to follow their dreams and become successful. We are supporters of the
little guy, the mom and pop shops around the world that depend on their dream
to support their family. We believe in buying local and supporting our friends
and neighbors. We also believe in the value of art and creativity. The Art of Felt | Volume 3 Support Hearth What makes the creative world work is that we
help support each other both through sales and also moral support. As a
community of dreamers, we are a shoulder to cry on when the dark days come and
the first to shout ‘Hooray!’ on the good days. Although we may not know each
other in person, there is a kinship, a connection felt between us. In order to continue to foster these creative
relationships, we need a little help. The Hearth team is blown away by the
amazing level of support that we receive from our friends, both new and old. As
artists and dreamers, we are always looking for new ways to help share stories
and photographs with the world. We want to be able to continue to grow and
expand and we need your help and support to make that dream a reality. Photographic Essay of Ballet Dancers | Volume 4 An Authentic Experience, a Photographic Essay | Volume 4 A Photographic Travel Essay of Paris | Volume 4 Where the money is going For this campaign, we are hoping to raise the
desperately needed capital to ensure Hearth continues for a long time to come.
One of our biggest challenges has been the high price of printing. Because our
printing standards are high and we use a high quality paper, the cost for a
small printing run is expensive. Most of the money we raise will be used to
help build up a printing fund so that we can order more magazines and bring the
cost down for our supporters. Times are tough for everyone and we
understand that better than most. We’re certainly not asking for much, but just
imagine if every person that follows us on Instagram or Facebook, or that reads
the journal regularly or purchased a copy donated $1, do you know the impact
that would have? It would be huge! Fatty Sundays, an Artisan Study | Volume 4 Our Rewards Our rewards are somewhat special for this project. Part of the thing that makes Hearth great is the community that we have built. We have so many wonderful artisans that have been a part of Hearth Magazine from the very beginning. When they found out that we were doing another kickstarter project, they were eager  to help out in any way they could. All of the artists have been or will be featured in our magazine or online journal. They believe in the community we are building and they want to be a part of it. Alfies Studio | 4 Pack Camp Collection Postcards with Envelopes. Shrimp Salad Circus | Limited Edition 8 X 10 Salt Print Harvest Beard, a Smith & Cox company | Harvest Beard Shaving Mug. title: Sweet/Nothing Oracle Deck
description: Sweet/Nothing Oracle Deck The Sweet/Nothing Oracle is a 77-card deck with accompanying poetry collection and guidebook. This simple, impactful deck is minimally and tastefully designed to spark intuition and deepen reflection. Similar to tarot, oracle decks can be useful tools for self-reflection. Unlike tarot, oracle decks don’t follow a specific structure. The Sweet/Nothing Oracle is a first-of-it's kind word-based deck derived from a collection of short-form poems. Each card features a single word and number corresponding to its foundation poem: There are countless beautifully illustrated tarot and oracle decks in the world, and I’ve found inspiration and deep self-reflection while using them. But I’ve also longed for a deck that allows the reader to interpret words instead. This is that deck. The individual words of The Sweet/Nothing Oracle can be interpreted in many ways: Through their literal definitions, through each reader’s unique life experience, and through the context of their foundation poems. These layers of meaning offer boundless opportunities for self-reflection, creativity, and divination. This deck is a valuable addition to any collection, especially if: You find yourself in a moment of transition wondering how you can weather the change in your life. You’re feeling confused or unfocused, unsure of the direction you’re taking or how to move forward. You find it helpful to have inspiration for daily meditation, mantra, or journaling. You’re want to deepen your relationship with your intuition and inner-wisdom/inner-knowing. You love words and find great meaning in them. You’re looking for a tool that can help offer creative inspiration when you’re feeling stuck in your art or other projects. Deck Design The front of each card features a different word typed on my 1930s-era Remington Noiseless portable typewriter, and the common backs feature "Golden Gate," an original constellation painting by Washington-based artist Christina Miglino : The Sweet/Nothing Name When I first began sharing the concept of this deck with friends and family, I described the cards as “sweet nothings from the universe,” and the idea stuck. There’s something so simple yet so powerful about a single word on a card. A solitary word becomes a beacon. Sweet/Nothing Oracle Guidebook I'm currently writing the Sweet/Nothing Oracle Guidebook, but I've already sorted out the big details for this unique compliment to my deck. The 128-page Sweet/Nothing guidebook will measure 5" x 8", perfect bound with rounded outer edges, and will include: Poems: 33 poems, with 77 bolded words to denote the oracle cards in the context of their written whole. Guide: Keywords, ideas, and guidance for each of the 77 oracle cards. How might a person interpret the word "tremble" when it pops up in a reading? What are some ways to interpret words like you might interpret images? A helpful resource if you get stuck or want to explore a word in more depth. Spreads: Ideas for how to use your oracle cards, including information on asking good questions, oracle specific spreads & ideas, and special oracle-tarot hybrids for those who want to blend words with imagery for particularly powerful readings. Journal: Approximately 40 pages of space to track your readings, writing, and other thoughts. Open space for your thoughts, prayers, and any sweet nothings you want to send back into the universe. Guidebook mock-up. What your Pledge Means Your pledge will allow me print the first edition of the Sweet/Nothing Oracle: A 500 set run of decks, guidebooks and keepsake bags. Money raised will go to printing, design and fulfillment costs. Sets not spoken for to fulfill Kickstarter pledges will be sold online and wholesaled & self-distributed to shops. Reaching my goal will mean I'll be able to bring this first-of-its kind oracle to others for self-reflection, divination, creativity and healing. It's a big task, but I know I can do it with your support. Your pledge today will make this dream a reality. Rewards Each Sweet/Nothing Oracle set will include: 77 2.75" x 4.75" cards printed on 130-pound dull cover stock with satin aqueous coating with deep, quarter-inch rounded corners. The cards are sturdy, smudge-resistant, and beautiful to handle. 5" x 8" softbound guidebook with rounded edges, featuring Sweet/Nothing poems, keywords & interpretative information, sample spreads and journal. Muslin pouch. FREE shipping in the United States. Wholesale Set This level includes 10 sets of Sweet/Nothing Oracle decks (including guidebooks and bags), retail info kit, and Kickstarter exclusive free shipping in the US. Limited Edition Sweet/Nothing Oracle These special decks feature hand-painted gold edges, sampled below: These edges are beautifully shiny but still stand up well to wear and shuffling. Each deck will be numbered and lovingly painted by me. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Only 76 decks are available at this level. The 77th is saved for: Collector's Edition Sweet/Nothing Oracle This set includes everything in the Limited Edition level, plus the original "Golden Gate" painting by Christina Miglino, and a one-of-a-kind hand bound art book typed by me on the 1930s-era Remington Noiseless portable typewriter I used to design the oracle cards themselves: Notes Everything pictured is a work in progress. These designs are meant to give you an idea of what the final deck will look like, but there will likely be small changes and improvements as the project and guidebook are finalized. Project backers will receive updates on these choices and progress toward fulfillment. The Sweet/Nothing Oracle isn't expected to ship until February 2018. If I'm able to fulfill the project earlier I will, but I want to provide the highest-quality deck and book I can while being respectful of your pledge. Music in video by Sarah Goodin , used with permission. Decks and guidebooks will be printed in the USA-- right here in Bellingham, Washington-- by LithtexNW . "Golden Gate" constellation painting by Christina Miglino . About the Author My name is Sara Holodnick (aka Sara Galactica ). I'm a writer and multimedia artist based in Bellingham, best known locally for my work as co-founder of Good Time Girls Tours . I utilize inventive and unexpected mediums such as original cocktails, walking tours and divination decks to tell stories. In addition to my work as a writer, cocktail developer and artist, I help others examine their own stories through tarot and oracle readings. It is my sincere honor to bring this deck to life with your help.

title: Choke Zine - Next Generation Photography and Arts Journal
description: “We can spend our lives letting the world tell us who we are. Sane or insane. Saints or sex addicts. Heroes or victims. Letting history tell us how good or bad we are. Letting our past decide our future. Or we can decide for ourselves. And maybe it's our job to invent something better.” Choke stands for change, and we believe the future belongs to everyone - no exclusions. We have a conscientious approach to the content we provide. Whether its environmental issues, social change, political movements or developments in the arts or cultural events; we remain resourceful and meticulous throughout. Choke is an open discussion, a fight for the underdog and a thought from outside the box. Our Contributors, Photographers, Writers and Talents, are game changers and frontrunners. Challenging the conventional, we bring our readers/followers exciting subject matter, awe inspiring imagery and unique design. We believe that each issue should feel like a collectors item. Therefore each issue is curated with a set theme from which we commission 6 unique Photographers to produce a 6-8 page story for our zine. Along with the main body of work, we would like to feature 3 key interviews with people that we feel are important to the theme of the issue. This could be anyone from environmental activists to musicians, politicians or tech innovators! When we go to print and launch the issue we want to do something a little different and hold an exhibition for Artists. This would be a great opportunity to sell the Zine and showcase exhibited works from the Contributors.

title: Categories
description: The goal of this kickstarter campaign is to bring information on how to build a functioning scientific and artistic glass blowing lathe to everyone. I plan on building a website containing a bill of materials, schematics, construction and operation videos, as well as a forum for discussion on all types of self built lathes, commercial lathes and lathe work. The monetary goal covers parts, construction, video and editing, as well as forum and website fees. For the cost of one lathe, everyone can have one! In September 2016 I took a class from Micah Evans and Stephan Pierce at the Pilchuck Glass School. I looked around for a lathe to fool around on and found my options limited. The cheapest domestic lathe comes in at $12,000. Import lathes are much cheaper but they seem to get mixed reviews. There are some good used lathes around but you are limited to what is available at the time, and they may require work. Size was also a consideration. Many people don't have room for a large floor lathe. As I watched Stephan work on the lathe I thought I might be able to build something smaller and cheaper that could act as an entry level lathe for myself and others. My designs quickly progressed to the one you see here. I was able to source almost everything online, and the construction of the lathe requires only basic fabrication skills. The best part of all? Parts come in less than $2k! My first thought was to bring the lathe to market but it's still a work in progress. My second thought was to go open source and let the community help. This is the perfect DIY solution to a coveted glass tool. I'm not the only one to build their own lathe, and this certainly isn't the only design. There is some information online but it is very limited. I drew my inspiration from what's out there, but there is a big vacuum I hope to fill with this information. I have added rewards that help make the construction go a little easier for some. Note: The $850 reward level for "The Kit" is not a complete lathe kit, it is only the things you would have to fabricate. You will still need to buy the frame, chucks, etc. Stock specs: Radial swing: 9.75"  (more than a Gator!) Max distance between spindles: 24" - or as long as you want. The lathe bed is easily extended for tens of dollars, depending on how far you want to go. Spindle bore: 20mm stock - possible to bore chucks to 30mm .25" cold rolled steel ways Heavy duty frame made from 8020 aluminum extrusion Thank you everyone for your support

title: DIY Light-Up Paper Bow Ties
description: A light-up paper bow tie you put together yourself. My new light-up paper bow tie kits come in packs of 10, so they're perfect for parties, events and education. The flashiest bow tie ever. The super quick version: The detailed version: Ta-Da! Each kit makes 10 bow ties that come in three assorted colors and designs. All kits come with color changing Party Flash LED lights. Choose from these color combinations: The "Neon" Kit DIY Light-Up Bow Ties "Neon" Color Combination: Orange, Yellow, and Hot Pink The "Elegance" Kit DIY Light-Up Bow Ties "Elegance" Color Combination: Gold, Rose Gold, and Silver The "Blues" Kit DIY Light-Up Bow Ties "Blues" Color Combination: Blue, White, and Teal The "Formal" Kit DIY Light-Up Bow Ties "Formal" Color Combination: White, Black, Red And... They're back! Last year I launched TechnoChic DIY Light-Up Paper Flower Kits on Kickstarter . Now, I'm bringing them back and they're better than ever - NOW Light-Up Paper Flower Pins come with PARTY FLASH LED's! Mix and match the flower petals to create your design. Thread the petals, attach the battery and pin, and wear! Detailed printed instructions come with each kit. I'll also make an instructional video to watch online. The "Neon" Kit DIY Light-Up Paper Flowers "Neon" Color Combination: Orange, Yellow, and Hot Pink The "Elegance" Kit DIY Light-Up Paper Flowers "Elegance" Color Combination: Gold, Rose Gold, and Silver The "Blues" Kit DIY Light-Up Paper Flower Pins "Blues" Color Combination: Blue, White, and Teal For too long, creators have identified with a certain type of skill set - either hard skills like soldering and robotics or soft skills like knitting and sewing. These two groups rarely crossed, and that same line has often been drawn between genders. TechnoChic shatters these conventions by providing a resource for crafters to explore technology, and techies to explore craft. Incorporating electronics and engineering principals into crafts (where most women fall in love with making things) could ignite a major shift in balancing the gender gap in STEAM fields. TechnoChic DIY tech-craft kits provide a path to discover a love of technology through easy and fun craft projects that focus on small positive experiences that can be built upon easily. The most common question after building a TechnoChic project is "Wow - what can I build next?" Special thanks to these blogs for featuring my project! :) Make: Mario The Maker Magician GeekDad The Coding Train Hyper Crafty Craft Box Girls 30 Minute Crafts ClearBags The Geekly News Want to see your blog here? Please help spread the word about this campaign! Want to hear more from TechnoChic? Sign up for updates & coupons. Photo Booths. Fun props. Saturdays. Craft all day and party all night. Parties & Corporate Events. A quick hands-on activity makes a great ice breaker! Formal Events. A flashy look for the Droids Ball Maker Spaces & Maker Clubs. Welcome new members with quick and fun activity! Maker Faire. Wear your flair to Maker Faire. Night Life. Make a new tradition! I have worked hard to find quality materials and capable businesses to help me complete this project. The most expensive piece of the project is overcoming the high costs of custom cutting and stamping dies, and running them on the manufacturing equipment: professional die cutting and foil stamping presses. This type of process is expensive to set up, then the more you buy the cheaper it gets per piece. These are the dies I had made with the help of my last Kickstarter project - aren't they pretty? I feel so lucky to be working with such cool manufacturing processes, and I love sharing my adventure with my backers! Top: Foil Stamping Die, Bottom: Steel Rule Cutting Die Breakdown of money: I've done all the math to figure out how much every piece of this project will cost. Here's a breakdown of the costs by category: Special Thanks! Matt Palasz Meredith Hutchinson Hartley Sam Hartley Lorelei Hartley Amber Chartier Lauren Hegdahl Jason & Paige Kim Wong Jennifer L. Wendel Charles Haase Michael Consentino Gabby, Ashley, Anne & Bert

title: New Friends
description: Loneliness is an invisible epidemic, affecting millions of Americans and carrying profound consequences for mental, emotional, and physical health. Studies show that chronic loneliness increases the risk of heart disease, weakens the immune system, and disrupts sleep patterns—ultimately diminishing overall well-being and quality of life. In a world where 50% of Americans admit to feeling lonely and social media intensifying feelings of isolation, the need for a solution has never been greater. New Friends is the app designed to actively combat this crisis by providing a platform exclusively for building genuine, platonic friendships. Unlike existing platforms, New Friends removes the connotations of dating, focusing solely on fostering meaningful human connections. By connecting people based on shared interests, values, and goals, the app empowers users to create relationships that truly enrich their lives. What sets New Friends apart is its inclusive and sustainable model. With a "pay-what-you-can" subscription system, the app ensures accessibility for everyone, giving users the freedom to contribute based on the value they derive from their experiences. This community-driven approach allows some to use the app for free while others support it voluntarily, creating a balance that prioritizes connection over profit. Your investment in New Friends is an investment in a solution to one of the most pressing challenges of our time. Together, we can build a platform that not only fights loneliness but fosters community, love, and a sense of belonging for millions. Join us in changing lives, one connection at a time.

title: Categories
description: After the success of Roadie 3, trusted by over 60,000 musicians and rated 4.5 stars across 2,000+ Amazon reviews, we knew we had to come back with something even better. So we listened. Closely. Every comment, suggestion, and request from our community helped shape what came next. Roadie 4 is the result: our most advanced, precise, and reliable tuner yet. Built from the ground up with your feedback at the core. We’re proud of the trust musicians have placed in us, and we can't wait to earn it again. Join us early and be part of the next evolution in music tech. ‎What It Is The Fastest, Smartest and Most Reliable Automatic Tuner We've Ever Made Roadie 4 is our most powerful and user-friendly tuner to date, built to make tuning faster, more precise, and effortless. With cutting-edge vibration detection, completely revamped audio circuitry and algorithms, a high-torque motor, and improved resistance to background noise, Roadie 4 delivers flawless, reliable tuning, whether you're at home, on stage, or in the studio. Watch this demo **This video is 100% genuine and has no edits. Roadie 4 in a nutshell: Automatically gets you in tune in seconds with incredible precision Compatible with almost ALL string instruments with geared pegs Reliable tuning, even in noisy environments Switch effortlessly between 150+ built-in tunings Intuitive interface with a large, full-color screen Integrated string winder for fast and easy restringing Built-in metronome with optional beep and vibration modes Long-lasting battery that stays powered for months Bluetooth 5.0 enabled for smooth syncing Keep scrolling for full specs! ‎‎‎ Compatibility Fits More Pegs. Tunes More Instruments. Roadie has always supported a wide range of instruments, but with a newly redesigned peg connector, Roadie 4 now fits both smaller and larger pegs more comfortably. Its rubberized texture grips tuning pegs more securely and turns smoothly with no slipping, no shaking, just steady, quiet tuning. It works with any instrument that uses geared pegs, including electric guitars with 6, 7, 8 or 12 strings, acoustic and classical guitars, ukuleles, mandolins, banjos, bouzoukis, lap steels and much more. Whatever you play, Roadie 4 is ready to tune it with speed and precision. Bass guitars, however, have heavier strings and require more torque than Roadie 4 is designed to deliver. That’s why we created Roadie Bass, a separate tuner specifically built to handle the needs of bass instruments. It’s already available on the market, and you can order it directly through our website. How It Works Roadie 4 uses a built-in contact microphone and advanced audio processing to detect string vibrations, analyze pitch, and automatically turn the tuning peg with incredible precision. No focus needed. Just hold it in place and trust it to get the job done right every time. We completely revamped the audio circuit and tuning algorithm, making Roadie 4 noticeably faster, more accurate, and more reliable than ever before. Tune Your Instrument in 3 Simple Steps: [New!] Auto-Detect By default, Roadie 4 tunes from the lowest to the highest string. But with the new auto-detect feature, you can pluck any string and Roadie 4 will instantly recognize and tune it. It beeps and vibrates when the string is in tune. What's New Winder & Metronome Restring, Tune, and Track. All in One. Roadie 4 features a built-in winder with three speed settings, giving you full control over how you restring your instrument. Whether you prefer a fast wind or a slower, more controlled approach, the winder makes changing strings smoother than ever. And beyond the dedicated winder feature, Roadie 4 also streamlines the process from within the tuner itself. When you're replacing a string, you can now wind it and immediately tune it in one smooth step. It’s a fast, fluid experience that keeps you focused on your music, not the setup. Watch it in Action We’ve reinvented the way you restring your instrument. Roadie 4 automates both winding and tuning in one smooth, seamless step, no more manual effort, just a fast process that gets you back to playing in seconds. But here's where it gets even smarter: with the companion Roadie Tuner app, you can now log each string change. Ever found a string you loved but couldn’t remember the brand or gauge later? Or wished you had a reminder to change your strings before a gig or session? The app helps you track what you used, when you changed it, and when it's time to swap again, so your instrument always sounds its best. Play in Perfect Time, With Beep or Vibration Roadie 4’s built-in metronome has been upgraded to give you full control over tempo, time signature, and now rhythm. Set your BPM, choose your beat count, and add rhythmic subdivisions like eighth notes or triplets to match your style. With vibration mode, you can feel the rhythm instead of just hearing it, perfect for practicing quietly or playing in a loud environment. Who Is It For Tuning can be a hassle, no matter your skill level. Whether you're just starting out, a seasoned player, or someone who works with instruments every day, Roadie 4 is made for you. It takes the frustration out of tuning so you can focus on what really matters: playing. See How It Works For You Professionals Roadie 4 delivers pro-level accuracy, even in noisy environments. Customize tunings, set your own temperaments, adjust reference pitch, and use the Tune Up feature for longer-lasting stability. Perfect for stage and studio. Beginners Learning an instrument is already tough. Tuning should not be. Roadie 4 makes it easy with a clear, beginner-friendly interface that guides you through the process. It gets you in perfect tune fast while helping you naturally develop your ear over time. Amateur Players Explore new sounds and grow your skills. Roadie 4 gives you instant access to over 150 alternate tunings, helping you discover new genres, techniques, and creative directions as you improve. Multi-Instrumentalists Playing more than one instrument? Roadie 4 has you covered. Save custom setups for each one and switch between them in seconds. From guitar and ukulele to mandolin and beyond, it keeps your whole collection ready to play. Guitar Shops Need to keep multiple instruments ready for customers? Roadie 4 lets you tune them fast without setting up full profiles. The built-in winder also makes restringing quicker and easier so everything stays ready to go. Teachers Tuning for a group of students? Roadie 4 speeds up the process so you can focus more on teaching while your students focus more on playing. Customization Tune Your Way. Customize Everything. Roadie 4 is packed with advanced features designed for musicians who like things just right. Whether you want faster tuning, ultra-precise adjustments, or custom setups tailored to your instrument and sound, Roadie 4 gives you real control. Every detail is customizable, so it works exactly the way you want it to. Set Roadie to “Tune Up” With the optional Tune Up setting, Roadie 4 finishes all tunings by tightening the peg and locking in your tuning even more securely. Many players swear by this method, while others prefer the default. The choice is yours. Create Custom Tunings Whether you're tuning to tempered or alternate tunings, Roadie 4 makes it easier than ever. You can adjust each string in cents to match your ear or specific setup. If you’ve fitted your guitar with a custom nut or bridge to compensate for intonation, you can create tunings tailored to that exact configuration. For floating bridge guitars like a Floyd Rose, follow the recommended string order (Low E, High E, B, A, G, D) to maintain equal tension across the bridge. Save your custom tuning once and access it anytime with just a tap. Tune with a Capo Using a capo? No problem. Just tell Roadie which fret it's on, keep the capo on, and your preset tunings will adjust automatically so Roadie tunes for the key you're playing in. Adjust Your Reference Pitch Not everyone tunes to A=440 Hz. Roadie 4 lets you shift the reference pitch anywhere between 420 Hz and 460 Hz, in 1 Hz increments (perfect for historical pieces, orchestral settings, or matching fixed-pitch instruments). Fast or Higher Accuracy Tuning Roadie 4 comes set to "Fast Tuning" by default: quick, reliable, and accurate enough for everyday playing. But when you need extra precision, like during recording sessions, just switch to "High Accuracy" mode. It fine-tunes each note with even greater detail, giving you a perfectly polished result. [NEW] Quick Tune: Your Shortcuts to Tuning By popular demand from our Roadie users, we’ve introduced Quick Tune, a feature designed to get you tuning faster than ever. When enabled, your Roadie 4 will automatically open to your last tuned instrument or a favorite as soon as you power it on, so you're ready to go immediately. And now, Roadie 4 also comes preloaded with a few common instrument profiles, so you can start tuning right out of the box. While we still recommend creating full instrument profiles for precision and advanced features, you now have a shortcut when speed matters. So whether you're picking up where you left off, testing a guitar in a store, or helping a friend tune in a pinch, these quick-access options save you time. Product Design Made for Musicians. Built to Perform. After years of careful design, real-world testing, and input from our community, Roadie 4 delivers professional-grade performance in a tool that feels effortless to use. Every detail, from the sturdy build and non-slip grip to the vivid full-color display, has been crafted for comfort, speed and style. Roadie’s product team evaluating design prototypes Design Features: Clear, Intuitive Display Front-facing, full-color screen with large graphics that stay visible while tuning Sleek and Ergonomic Compact form that fits comfortably in your hand or pocket Non-Slip Grip Gives you confident handling, even during quick tune-ups Precision Scroll Wheel Smooth navigation with tactile feedback and hyper scroll for faster menu access Bold, Modern Look Striking black, gray, and red design with clean, sharp accents Roadie 4 isn't just a tuner, it’s a precision tool made to match your craft. Tech Specs The App Roadie 4 connects to a free companion app for deeper customization and updates The free Roadie app unlocks the full potential of your tuner, giving you advanced features, personalization, and ongoing updates, all in one place. Based on user feedback, we also redesigned the interface for a smoother, more intuitive experience. With the app, you can: Create and save profiles for each of your instruments: guitars, ukuleles, mandolins, banjos, and more Add unique instruments like a charango, guitalele, bandurria, or baglama with geared pegs [NEW!] Log your string changes to track what you used, when you changed them, and when it's time to swap again Download from a growing library of curated tunings or create your own custom ones Toggle vibration and beep notifications on or off to suit your environment Receive firmware updates with the latest features and performance enhancements for Roadie 4 Reviews & Awards From groundbreaking tech to critically acclaimed tuners, we’ve set the bar. Roadie 4 is here to raise it. Through The Years A journey of continuous innovation, inspired by musicians' feedback Roadie 4 is the culmination of 10+ years of technical innovation in music accessories. Project Timeline Meet The Team Back in 2013, two music-loving engineers, Hassane Slaibi and Bassam Jalgha, set out to solve a problem: musicians were spending too much time tuning and not enough time playing. So they built the first Roadie Tuner. It was smart and automatic and earned global attention, including the TechCrunch Disrupt 2014 Audience Choice Award. Since then, we’ve kept building: Roadie 2, Roadie Bass, Roadie 3 and Roadie Coach, each one smarter, faster, and more intuitive. Along the way, bands started recording and touring with our tools, and our community kept growing. Now it’s time for Roadie 4, our most advanced tuner yet, powered by the smartest tuning technology we’ve ever built. Behind it all is a small, passionate team of musicians and makers who love what they do, and each other. We’re still tuning, still dreaming, and still creating tools to help you do what you love most: make music. Why Kickstarter You might be wondering why a brand like Roadie, already established with products on the market, would return to Kickstarter to launch the next version. The biggest advantage of being on Kickstarter is the invaluable feedback we receive from this amazing community of curious, passionate, and forward-thinking musicians. By sharing our concepts, features, and development updates, we gain insights that go far beyond our internal perspective. It’s the most effective way we’ve found to create something that truly resonates with musicians everywhere. When we were just getting started, the tuner market was crowded, and spreading the word felt almost as challenging as inventing Roadie itself. We genuinely believe we wouldn’t be where we are today without the incredible support and open dialogue we've had with our Kickstarter backers. Now, our team is more prepared than ever to launch something groundbreaking. And once again, we’re turning to you, your passion, feedback, and support, to help us make Roadie 4 a reality. Warranty & Shipping We will be shipping worldwide! Free shipping to the US. All our Roadie products come with a 30-day money-back guarantee and a 1-year warranty. Taxes & duties included in all our prices, worldwide. Spread the word, boost the launch, and earn commissions as an affiliate This project is boosted by BackerCrew

The Stepper promotes normal bio-mechanical walking patterns throughout your power walking routine. Because of its unique application to your body, you can increase your  cardio output, build muscle tone and increase flexibility like no other product.
