title: OTR-250 FPV Frame
description:
OTR250 Built to tilt We love flying drones. Our desire to innovate and advance to the next level has driven us to look at modern
designs and try to figure out new ways to improve on them. Our objective is clear. Contribute to the world-wide drone community a design which gives
the beginner or advanced flyer the tools to make them feel right at home in their local club or
on the track!  But we need your help! The costs of making the molds and getting the initial builds done are substantial. Particularly because we've made it a point to use all US based companies for our materials and manufacturing. We could cut costs by going overseas, but we'd rather keep the quality and resources local. Some of the R&D can be seen here: URBAN WORKSHOP We have teamed up with manufacturers around the US who are dedicated to delivering top-notch quality products to the RC world. From the materials selected, to the machines used to CNC the parts, all of it revolves around you, the pilot. We want to build another round of alpha prototypes to ensure design specs, and then produce about 200 (or more) for sale and distribution. The final design will accommodate the Connex ProSight system. We've also lightened the frame considerably from the prototypes shown in the video and made a few other spec changes that will certainly make this latest design shine! SPECS: Size : 250 (249.5mm) Width : 237mm Height : 90mm Weight : 165grams (With Body) FEATURES: This quad frame is full of unique features and lots of flexibility in the design.  Some of those features include: Up to 40 degrees of tilt in the limbs in 10 degree increments. (Done to give you more specific settings for your flight controller as opposed to guessing the degree of tilt) Multiple camera style ready. From your older board cameras to cameras that have their own mounts such as the Connex ProSight, there are many ways you can mount your camera and it's all already included in the kit. The camera can also be tilted to near any angle you could want and it's easily adjustable. Easy access to VTX and batteries. No more having to wedge your fingers into the frame to change the VTX. There is a lot of room to mount your VTX securely under the frame where it's still well protected. Sleek aerodynamic body means less resistance and more efficient runs for your motors and batteries. Vertical offset of the rotors front to back means more efficient use of your motors thrust without causing prop wash front to back. Internally mounted equipment means better protection for your electronic equipment and less worries about prop strikes on exposed wires and electronics. Made in the USA! We pride ourselves for making quality products that are made in the US. A handsome frame is always nice to look at! Particularly when it comes with nice features like multiple camera mounting options. Either for larger board cameras or the Foxeer style cameras. Board camera options Connex or Foxeer options OTR250 Kit reward: Kit comes with; Frame hardware, nuts and bolts (Not shown) Frame components (shown below) Battery strap (Not shown) 2 options for FPV camera mounting (Shown below) 1 Clear body (Shown below) 1 Manual (Not shown) Exploded parts of the kit Action Camera Mount reward: For those who want to record their flight from the birds-eye-view, the action cam mount option is a great way to get it all on video! Attach your favorite video recorder (Mobius, Go-Pro, etc) to the mount and record all the action! Kit includes: Frame hardware, nuts and bolts (Not shown) Frame components (shown below) 1 Manual (Not shown) Exploded parts of the kit T-Shirt reward: Show off an awesome design and let your buddies know what you fly! This exclusive design will only be offered as a reward during this campaign. Once gone, they are gone for good! Here's a glimpse of the design for the back of the shirt! OTR250 Exclusive t-shirt design

title: Empathy: Hurt People Hurt People
description: The Story A man is late for work. That is all we know about him. He rushes into a coffee shop, collides with a woman on her way out, spills her latte across her white shirt, and keeps walking. No apology. No glance back. He doesn't even register that it happened. She does. What follows is a chain reaction that moves invisibly through six strangers over the course of a single day. A near miss at a crosswalk. A snapped word in an office. A stolen table at lunch. A cold dismissal in a retail store. Each person carrying their hurt forward to the next, never knowing where it came from or where it is going. By the time the cycle returns to where it started, the woman who was hurt that morning has become the very thing that hurt her. She almost stops it. She turns back, opens her mouth to apologize. But the stranger is already walking away. Too late. Hurt people hurt people. The cycle never ends. See more at IMDb or Lucani Entertainment . Why This Film From Mike Wood, Writer and Producer This story came to me after a routine visit to a local grocery store. A stranger nearly hit me with a shopping cart, kept walking, and paid no mind to the fact that I had to jump out of their way. My mood changed, then dissipated, after I imagined how much my anger could ripple to other people in the store. I decided to let it go. By the time I made it to my car, the story line came to me. I wanted to do this film to show people that we have a choice. We live in a world that seems to choose anger over empathy. We move through our days wrapped in our own emotional weather, completely unaware that we are affecting the people around us. The person who just snapped at you was probably hurt by someone else five minutes ago. The person you just snapped at will carry that forward to someone else. Empathy is not a lecture about being kinder. It is an observation about what actually happens. About how the cycle works. About why it is so hard to break even when we can see it happening. I wanted to make this film because I believe in the power of quiet stories. Stories that don't explain themselves. Stories that trust the audience to feel what they need to feel and take away what they need to take away. This is one of those stories. What Your Support Makes Possible Your support helps us: 🎬 Hire an exceptional cast to bring these seven characters to life 📍 Shoot on location across Southern California over three days 🎵 Score and mix an original ambient soundtrack that carries the emotional weight of the film 🎨 Complete professional post-production including color correction and sound design 🏆 Submit to film festivals including Sundance, SXSW, and Tribeca 🎭 Host a premiere screening in Southern California for our backers, cast, and crew 📦 Fulfill all backer rewards with care and on time Meet the Team We are a passionate team making a film about something we believe matters. Mike Wood, Writer and Producer Mike Wood is a professional writer, digital marketer, and published author. He holds a law degree and has built a career at the intersection of writing, marketing, and storytelling. He is the author of two published books and a regular contributor to notable business and marketing publications. Mike's professional background in content strategy and narrative communication informs his approach to screenwriting. Empathy is his first produced screenplay under his production company Lucani Entertainment , born from a real-world observation about how carelessness travels invisibly between strangers. Sonia Lowman, Director and Producer Sonia Lowman is an award-winning filmmaker with over 20 years of experience in storytelling for international nonprofits and social impact media. She holds a Master's Degree from the London School of Economics and serves as the principal filmmaker for International Medical Corps, a humanitarian relief organization operating in over 30 countries affected by war and disaster. Her directorial credits include Black Boys (NBC Peacock, 2020), executive produced by NFL player Malcolm Jenkins and featuring an original song by Ben Harper, selected for over 13 film festivals including the American Black Film Festival, Bentonville Film Festival, and Heartland Film Festival; Teach Us All (Netflix, 2017), acquired and distributed by Ava DuVernay's ARRAY; War and Grace (2020), narrated and executive produced by Sienna Miller; and Indomitable (2024), also narrated by Sienna Miller with original music by Ben Harper. Sonia's proven ability to direct socially conscious, emotionally grounded stories for major platforms, combined with her extensive festival experience and industry relationships, makes her the ideal director to bring Empathy to life. Her work can be see at her film company Rakhma Productions . Pre-Production Stills Final Words From Mike Wood If you have ever hurt someone without meaning to, this film is for you. If you have ever been hurt by someone who kept walking, this film is for you. If you have ever stood in the middle of a crowded street feeling completely alone, this film is for you. We are all in this chain. Every single one of us. The only question is whether we choose to stop. Thank you for being part of this story.

title: Categories
description: SYNOPSIS Juan lives in Antofagasta, a small city between desert and sea from the north of Chile, but he dreams with big cities. When an opportunity for studying in New York City appears, Juan doesn't think twice: he travels and falls in love with the huge buildings, the people from all over the world, the places that movies show over and over again. However, when his mother Magdalena starts having serious health problems back in Antofagasta, Juan's life in New York will stop being the perfect one. WHO ARE WE? We are a group of professional filmmakers and actors who came together to create a compelling feature film. We are all young people trying to go to the next level in our different professions. We are mostly immigrants from different parts in the world who are following our dreams. And, most important, we are film lovers that want to create, communicate and develop our skills. WHY CROWDFUNDING? A film requieres lots of things. Passionate and talented people, organization, places, props, equipment... and money. "Antofagasta, New York" is a low budget feature film, but still needs financial support. Your help will be used in the following aspects: -Renting/ Permits for locations (depending if they are interior or exterior). You can't make a film without a context. In "Antofagasta, New York", these two cities are the universe where the action takes place. However, interiors (like rooms, cafés, etc) have to be rented, and exteriors (streets, parks, neighborhoods), have to be shot with a permit from the government. -Equipment Rental. There are films shot in a cellphone today, that's true. But was it a normal cellphone?... No. It had special lenses, special handheld mount, special storage... So we want to rent a professional camera (and lenses), that can give us the best possible images of New York and Antofagasta. Besides, there are other important expenses (hardrives for the footage, sound equipment, electric equipment), that will be possible to have with your support. -Production Design. Once you have a location, you have to dress it. That is important to the consistency of the film: what colors are you going to use, what props are important for the characters and the story, what kind of environment you need to create for a specific moment. Think about what you love in your favorite film... isn't it related to the world that shows you? That's Production Design. For further information about "Antofagasta, New York" visit our website http://www.antofagastanewyork.com/ and our Facebook site . SINOPSIS Juan vive en Antofagasta, una pequeña ciudad entre el desierto y el mar en el Norte de Chile, mas sueña con vivir en una metrópolis. Cuando recibe una oportunidad de estudiar en Nueva York, Juan no lo piensa dos veces: viaja y se enamora de los inmensos edificios, la gente de todo el mundo, los lugares que las películas muestran una y otra vez. Sin embargo, cuando la madre de Juan, Magdalena, comienza a sufrir graves problemas de salud en Antofagasta, la vida de Juan en Nueva York dejará de ser perfecta. ¿QUIÉNES SOMOS? Somos un grupo de profesionales en el cine que se unió con el objetivo de crear un buen largometraje. Somos jóvenes intentando dar nuestro siguiente paso en nuestras respectivas profesiones. Somos mayormente inmigrantes de distintas partes del mundo que estamos siguiendo nuestros sueños. Y, sobre todo, somos amantes del cine que queremos crear, comunicar y desarrollar nuestras habilidades. ¿POR QUÉ CROWDFUNDING? Una película requiere muchas cosas. Gente talentosa y apasionada, organización, locaciones, objetos, equipos... y dinero.  "Antofagasta, New York" es una película de bajo presupuesto, pero que necesita apoyo financiero. Tu ayuda permitirá llevar a cabo lo siguiente: -Arrendar/ Permisos para locaciones (dependiendo si son interiores o exteriores). No puedes hacer una película sin un contexto. En "Antofagasta, New York", estas dos ciudades serán el universo en que las acciones tendrán lugar. Sin embargo, interiores (como habitaciones, cafés, etc) deberán ser arrendados, y exteriores (calles, parques, barrios), tendrán que grabarse con su debido permiso gubernamental. -Arriendo de equipos. Hoy hay largometrajes grabados con celulares, es verdad. ¿Pero eran celulares normales?... No. Tenían lentes especiales, una montura de cámara-en-mano, almacenamiento especial... Por lo mismo, queremos arrendar una cámara (y lentes) profesionales, que nos permitan las mejores imágenes posibles de New York y Antofagasta. Además, hay otros gastos importantes (hardrives para guardar el footage, equipo de sonido, equipo eléctrico), que serán posibles gracias a tu ayuda. -Dirección de Arte. Una vez que tienes una locación, tienes que vestirla. Eso es importante en la consistencia de la película: qué colores usarás primordialmente, qué objetos serán importantes para los personajes y la historia, qué tipo de ambiente tienes que crear para un momento específico. Piensa en lo que más amas en tu película favorita... ¿Acaso no está relacionado con el mundo que te muestra? Eso es parte de la Dirección de Arte. Para más información sobre "Antofagasta, New York" visita nuestra página web http://www.antofagastanewyork.com/ y nuestro sitio de Facebook .

title: Categories
description: Baracutanga ( meaning "people dancing" ) is a much loved, Latin flavored band based in Albuquerque, New Mexico. Baracutanga does just that: they make people dance.  The band consists of 7 hard-working musicians, four from South America and three from the United States, who compose songs that speak to racial injustice, breaking down borders - both geographical and social - and building bridges between south and north.  Baracutanga‘s live shows are joyous, intense and explosive, propelled by the sounds of Andean flutes, charango, accordion, vibes, horns, guitar, bass, drums, vocal harmonies, and an array of percussion and they are often joined on stage, by Afro-Peruvian and Latin dancers. If you ask the band why dancing matters you'll hear them tell you that dancing is freedom. Six Members of Baracutanga from left to right, Micah, Tito, Jackie, Calin, Casey and David The band started 13 years ago as an informal assembly and has slowly, methodically cultivated a musical subculture and a loyal following that is more of a community than a traditional band-audience relationship.  After 13 years of playing in New Mexico and the Southwestern US , Baracutanga is finally realizing a dream and traveling as a group to Bolivia during Carnival, to reconnect with the band's musical heritage and rediscover their sound. We are Albuquerque based filmmakers raising money to follow the band with a film crew so that we can capture their journey and share it with their fans and followers from all over the world. The documentary will be a kinetic mix of D.A Pennebaker's "Don't Look Back" and Wim Wenders' "Buena Vista Social Club". It will capture a snapshot in time of this remarkable collective's evolution. We will follow these seven very different members on their journey back to their musical roots. Filmmakers, David and Nikelle Garcia By backing this project, you will be supporting this band in their journey to break down borders and heal through music.  Going to a Baracutanga concert in person is pure joy and inclusion and we hope to bring this energy and healing to a wider audience. You will also receive great rewards from exclusive updates about the journey from Burque to Bolivia, private concerts and screenings and even associate and executive producer credits in the film.

description: UPDATE: GOAL STRETCH ADD-ON PACKAGE! WE MADE IT!!  Your support has been AMAZING - a HUGE THANK YOU to everyone!  Meeting our goal has allowed us to create BRAND NEW STRETCH ADD-ON REWARDS, which include a first-edition NOSFERATU REMIX POSTER designed by Matt Eskew and a NOSFERATU LOGO HAT - with the option of having the poster signed by Doug Jones and David Lee Fisher! *If you'd like to add one of these items to your current reward, please click the "mange your pledge" button and add the amount (either $35 or $50). Then please send us a message by clicking on the "ask a question" button at the bottom of the page, and let us know which one you'd like to receive! If you haven't pledged yet, you can get these rewards on the right side of the page at the $35 and $50 levels. The Final Push:  A Special Message Directly From Doug Jones! You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 By combining imagery from the original horror classic with cutting edge filmmaking technology, we will resurrect the screen’s first and most monstrous vampire back from the grave! The original, Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror , was the first vampire tale ever filmed, and is widely considered the cornerstone of horror cinema. Shot in 1921 and directed by F.W.Murnau, the film was an unauthorized adaptation of Bram Stoker's Dracula . Upon its release, Stoker's widow ordered all copies of the film burned. Fortunately a single print survived. Actor Max Shrek's iconic performance as the terrifying Count Orlok along with universal themes of fear, sacrifice, and good's triumph over evil is still as haunting as it was over ninety-years ago. The film is highly revered not only by film buffs, but modern horror fans worldwide. Max Shrek as the original Count Orlok Our new vision of Orlok will be played by legendary makeup FX actor, Doug Jones; famous for his roles such as Abe Sapien in HellBoy , the Silver Surfer in Fantastic 4: Rise of the Silver Surfer, the Fauno and Pale Man in Pan's Labyrinth , The Gentlemen in Buffy the Vampire Slayer, and currently as the alien Cochise on Falling Skies . Doug Jones as the new vision of Count Orlok - Makeup FX by Spectral Motion The many iconic faces of Doug Jones Nosferatu is not only a frighteningly well crafted horror tale, but immensely important in the evolution of modern cinema. OUR GOAL is to produce a feature-length, faithful homage to this classic masterpiece through use of an updated story structure, plot pacing, visual effects, and importantly sound and music. We believe this “fresh take on an old tale” will bring something new to devoted fans, and excite an entire new generation of audiences as well. Our team has a lot of experience creating a movie like this, as we've already done it before on our previous film The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari. For that project we created a custom technique never before seen in the history of filmmaking. By scanning a vintage print, we were able to shoot our actors on green-screen, then layer them into digital sets recreated from the original film's imagery. Imagery from the classic film comes together to create a new scene. The illusion was that modern day actors were seamlessly performing within the world of the 1920's film. Now with music, sound and dialog. Werner Krauss and Daamen J. Krall as Dr. Caligari. We coined this technique a " visual remix. "  Nosferatu's production will use this same revolutionary process, but to a much higher degree of quality due to great advances in software, cameras and computer power; allowing us higher cinematic quality and far more dynamic sets and locations. _________________________________________________________ Doug Jones on the set of Dr. Caligari. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Demo track of Eban Schletter's original Nosferatu score: For a detailed look into how we made the The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari, and see some of the same techniques we'll be using to create Nosferatu, watch the making-of special feature from the DVD release of Caligari here: You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Special Edition NOSFERATU Blu-Ray The DOUG JONES AUTOGRAPHED edition of the NOSFERATU BLU-RAY NOSFERATU REMIX POSTER COUNT ORLOCK SCULPTURE REWARDS You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Interview with Nosferatu sculpture artist Matt Levin Funds raised on our Kickstarter campaign will go for pre-production, production, post-production and distribution for the film. A pre-production crew must break down the script, schedule the shoot, and secure agreements for the studio, crew, cast, and equipment. Production involves shooting on a sound stage with camera crew, cast, lights, power etc. Post-production will be the most intensive period, and will take roughly 9-10 months of digitally creating the film. Distribution funds will go for logistics and legal for securing a distribution deal. Son of a NASA engineer, David first joined video game giant Sega working on titles such as Sonic and Doom . Later he founded his own video game studio where he won Best Story and Best Director at ACM Siggraph for MetalWerks - a sci-fi adventure that pioneered live action combined with CG environments and characters. After creating animated content for Apple, EA and Sony Playstation, he later moved into filmmaking winning a Sundance Selection at Triggerstreet.com , and Best Film & Director at the Los Angeles Instant Film Festival. For The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari he won Best Visual Effects, Best Cinematography and Audience Choice awards at the Screamfest Film Festival, premiered at the prestigious Sitiges Film Festival in Catalonia Spain, and won the Saturn Award from the the Academy of Science Fiction, Fantasy and Horror Films for Best DVD of the Year. Caligari was featured in the New York Times, Wired Magazine, Reelzchannel, and American Cinematographer. David has also worked extensively with Marvel Comics legend Stan Lee and Pow! Entertainment to develop animated television properties for studios such as Disney and Cartoon Network. Doug Jones has acted in countless feature films and is legendary for his portrayals of creatures, aliens and monsters. His most famous roles include The Gentlemen in the Emmy Award winning Hush episode of Buffy the Vampire Slayer, as well as The Fauno and Pale Man in the Oscar winning Pan’s Labyrinth, Abe Sapien in the Hellboy films, and the Silver Surfer in The Rise of the Silver Surfer. Doug also played the part of Cesare in The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari, and  is currently filming on the hit TV series Falling Skies. Entering the industry in 1987, Elizalde quickly established his career as a makeup designer, sculptor and one of the top animatronic engineers in his field. He has since worked on over 65 feature films, including Hellboy, Men in Black 1 and 2, A.I. Artificial Intelligence, Fantastic Four and Hellboy II: The Golden Army. He is also the founder of Spectral Motion which is recognized as one of the leading makeup and creature effects studios in the industry. Mike received his first Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences nomination in the makeup category for his work on Hellboy II: The Golden Army, for which he also received his second Saturn Award nomination from the Academy of Science Fiction, Fantasy and Horror Films. He received a Saturn Award for "Best Makeup" in 2004 for his work in the original Hellboy film. Paula produced the Cabinet of Dr. Caligari. She began her styling career designing the 10th Anniversary of MTV for Michael Jackson, and soon after as Costume Designer for television shows such as The Academy Awards, The Primetime Emmy Awards, Mr. Show with Bob Odenkirk & David Cross, The Sarah Silverman Program, Fashion Rocks, Movies Rock, The American Music Awards, Billboard Awards and many more. Paula has also moved into designing for motion pictures including Rock of Ages with Tom Cruise, and Quentin Tarantino's  D’Jango Unchained. She has worked with many stars from Stage and Screen Including; Tom Cruise, Leonardo DiCaprio, Mary J. Blige, Catherine Zeta Jones, Hugh Jackman, Rhianna, Jenny McCarthy, Dennis Hopper, Ben Stiller, Mike Meyers, Samuel L. Jackson, Jack Black, Julianne Hough and Cheryl Burke Chris Duddy was the cinematographer for The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari. Chris has worked in the movie business for over 25 years. His experience includes working at Industrial Light & Magic filming visual effects sequences for The Abyss, Total Recall and Terminator 2 which won the Oscar for visual effects. He then continued his work with James Cameron on True Lies, T-2 3D, and Titanic where he filmed the famous ship sinking sequence - which also won an Oscar. Chris was the cinematographer for Kevin Costner’s feature Thirteen Days, and is currently shooting the hit CBS show, Scorpion. Eban is a prolific composer and songwriter who has written music for countless television shows including Spongebob Squarepants, Mr. Show with Bob and David and the Academy Awards.  His feature film scores include The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari and New Line Cinema’s Dumb and Dumberer: When Harry Met Lloyd.  He has also maintained a steady output of solo albums which explore a broad range of musical genres. Nosferatu's Production Designer, Matt Eskew is a 19 year veteran working in Motion Picture, Television and Video Game industries. Matt started his career working for David Fisher at Frontline Industries. Since then, Matt has gone on to work for several high-end production studios such as Sony PlayStation and DreamWorks Animation where he worked on such films as Madagascar, Madagascar 2: ESCAPE TO AFRICA, HOW TO TRAIN YOUR DRAGON and MEGAMIND.

title: Categories
description:
ABOUT THE FILM We all break sometimes. What happens next can be extraordinary. This feature film, inspired by true events, follows a life transformed by the deep, soulful, power and beauty that is the art of flamenco. Elle, a high-tech executive whose life is torn apart by tragedy, in a moment of chance, experiences the power and beauty of raw emtions expressed through flamenco. Intrigued, she goes on a journey that takes her from despair and grief, to acceptance of the ups and downs of life. Inspired by art, music and dance, she discovers her voice and learns to integrate life’s pains and its joys, finding the strength to embrace, even celebrate the messiness of it all. SOBRE LA PELÍCULA Todos en algún momento tenemos un punto de ruptura. Lo que pasa después puede ser extraordinario Este largometraje, inspirado en hechos reales, sigue una vida transformada por el profundo, emotivo, poder y belleza que es el arte del flamenco. Elle, una ejecutiva de alta tecnología cuya vida está desgarrada por la tragedia, en un momento de azar se ve expuesta al poder y la belleza de las emociones al desnudo, expresadas a través del flamenco. Se despierta su curiosidad, y emprende un viaje que le lleva desde la desesperación y el dolor, a la aceptación de los avatares de la vida. Inspirada por el arte, la música y el baile, descubre su voz y aprende a integrar los dolores de la vida y sus alegrías, encontrando la fuerza para abrazar lo lioso que es todo. WHY WE WROTE THE STORY Finding our way forward from the things in life that hurt to the depths of our being can be a rough and lonely road. The reclaiming of self is the good fight. It takes tremendous courage, determination and inspiration.  This story brings us along that road, to where shared human experiences meet across cultures in the transformative expression of music, art, dance, and community to celebrate this life we all share. We want to bring awareness to the quiet fight, to the struggle inside the heart and mind that can be so easily masked behind busy lives and isolation. We want to share this story that demonstrates so clearly that pain can be integrated into the fabric of our being and honored as part of who we are. POR QUÉ ESCRIBIMOS LA HISTORIA Encontrar la manera de superar las cosas en la vida que nos duelen hasta lo más profundo de nuestro ser, puede resultar ser un camino tortuoso y solitario. Reclamar la propia identidad es la buena lucha. Lleva un valor tremendo, determinación e inspiración. Esta historia nos lleva a lo largo de ese camino, en donde experiencias humanas comunes se encuentran a través de culturas en la expresión transformativa de la música, arte, danza, y comunidad, para celebrar esta vida que todos compartimos. Queremos sensibilizar a la lucha silenciosa, a la pugna dentro del corazón y la mente que puede ser tan fácilmente enmascarada detrás de vidas ajetreadas y el aislamiento. Queremos compartir esta historia que demuestra tan claramente que el dolor puede ser integrado en la fábrica de nuestro ser, y honrado como parte de lo que somos. WHY WE NEED YOUR HELP Seriously, it takes a lot to make a movie, even with lots of volunteers and crew/talent working way below their normal rates. Thanks to all of them, we have completed 70% of filming on our own. Now we need help, not an easy thing to ask for and something we don't take lightly.  But we are determined to complete this film and we’re asking you to help make that happen. Your help will allow us to: 1.	Complete filming (30% left to shoot)
•	Camera rentals, lighting equipment rentals, location permits, studio rentals, theater rental, crew, cast, and H20 for everyone. 2.	Launch Post Production. Bring on an editor who understands the unique challenges of editing dance, complete the soundtrack and sound design, color correction and mastering. Our Goal - $20,000 – and when we hit that goal don’t stop! More money means better locations, more cameras, special effects, more talent and the opportunity to capture an even deeper experience of Flamenco in the film by filming in Spain – if we can take a small crew to Spain, we will be able to more authentically share with the audience the roots of Flamenco as thriving today in Andalusia.  Meeting our stretch goal of $40,000 would allow this rich and exciting aspect of Flamenco to be well represented in the film. POR QUÉ NECESITAMOS SU AYUDA Seriamente, hace falta mucho para hacer una película, incluso  con muchos voluntarios y un equipo y talento trabajando muy por debajo de sus tarifas habituales. Gracias a todos ellos, hemos completado el 70% de la filmación nosotros mismos. Ahora necesitamos ayuda, algo no fácil de pedir y que nos tomamos muy en serio. Pero estamos obstinados en acabar esta película, y te pedimos que nos ayudes a conseguirlo. Tu ayuda nos permitirá: 1.	Completar la película (queda el 30% de rodaje) · Alquiler de las cámaras, alquiler del equipo de iluminación, permisos de rodaje, alquiler de estudios, alquiler de teatros, equipo de rodaje, actores, y H20??? para todos los participantes. 2.	Puesta en marcha de la post-producción. Traer un editor que entienda los retos singulares que plantea el editar baile, finalizar la banda sonora y el diseño de sonido, correcciones de color y pasterización. Nuestra meta: $20,000 – y cuando alcancemos esta meta, ¡no parar! Más dinero implica mejores emplazamientos para filmar las escenas que nos faltan, más cámaras, efectos especiales. Más talento y la oportunidad de capturar una experiencia aún más profunda del flamenco en la película al filmar en España – si podemos llevar un pequeño equipo de rodaje a España, podremos compartir auténticamente con el público las raíces del flamenco tal y como florecen hoy en Andalucía. El alcanzar nuestra ulterior meta  de $40,000 permitiría que este rico y emocionante aspecto del flamenco estuviese bien representado en la película. Exclusive Finding Compás T-Shirt Design for Donors Only! *design by Adrian Arias THE CREATIVE TEAM Kathy Douglas SCREENWRITER/DIRECTOR Kathy is Co-Founder of Wise Women Circles, a woman owned and operated Production Company. Kathy’s interest in filmmaking goes back 20 years, when she directed her first documentary. The Song Within Sedona was her first publicly released documentary, followed by the documentary NURSES If Florence Could See Us Now distributed nationally and internationally.  Kathy is executive strategist for the film Death Makes Life Possible by Marilyn Schlitz and Deepak Chopra and works with Specialty Studios / The Video Project distributing documentary films that inspire consciousness and social change. www.wisewomencircles.com Sara Moncada PRODUCER/ACTOR/DANCER Sara is co-founder of Wise Women Circles, a woman owned and operated Production Company. She has many years of experience in the arts as a producer and performer in film and dance both nationally and internationally. Among her other projects, Sara produced the highly successful documentary NURSES If Florence Could See Us Now. She is the Program Director for Sewam American Indian Dance, an organization that specializes in sharing the music, dance and arts of Native American culture across the country in both education and theatrical arenas. www.wisewomencircles.com Scott Mason Co-PRODUCER Scott is the co-founder of the bi-coastal media collective, Oct28 Productions, and a key producer and creative contributor with Torres Studios in the Bay Area.  In addition to writing, directing and producing short format video content for businesses and organizations, he produces feature length films and creates web and television series, pilots and pitches. www.oct28productions.com Marlon Torres CINEMATOGRAPHER Marlon is a highly sought after cinematographer who works nationally and internationally bringing his keen eye to the art and science of filmmaking. His work ranges from Independent films to corporate work. You can see his reel at vimeo.com/56274509 . He is the owner of Torres Studios www.torresstudios.com La Tania ARTISTIC DIRECTOR/CHOREOGRAPHER/DANCER/ACTOR La Tania is an award winning dancer and choreographer. She has a long and distinguished history as a dancer, choreographer and producer.  She was introduced to Flamenco at age 2 when her mother became a Flamenco dancer. In addition to developing the choreography for many of the dances in the film, she has contributed to the screenplay sharing extraordinary aspects of her life to the richness of the film. www.lataniaflamenco.com Eddie Madril ASSISTANT DIRECTOR Eddie is a dancer, filmmaker, radio show host, playwright, performer, producer, and college professor. He has experience both in front of and behind the camera on many projects over many years. He was nominated for the prestigious Isadora Duncan Dance Award as a soloist for his hoop dance presentation in his 2008 original play "SunDagger Solstice." He has been recognized by KQED as an American Indian Bay Area Local Hero and his film projects have been featured in the American Indian Film Festivals in San Francisco, Oklahoma and North Dakota. For over ten years Eddie has been a member of the Board of Directors for Friendship House of American Indians Inc. and currently acts as Vice President of the Board. www.sewamdance.com THE CAST/CREW We are so honored and grateful to have drawn so much talent to this project. While there is not room for bios on them all, we encourage you to checkout their websites to learn more about the extraordinary artists that have come together for this inspiring project. Farruquito - Dancer/Chorographer/Actor
Juan Manuel Fernández Montoya 'Farruquito' is a resident of Southern Spain and is arguably one of the most famous gypsy flamenco dancers in the world. He is the son of Flamenco singer 'El Moreno' and dancer 'La Farruca' and is grandson of the great 'Farruco'.  He was born into flamenco “puro” making his début on the international scene on Broadway at the age of five. Since then he has devoted his entirety to the preservation and innovation of his beloved art form. http://www.farruquito.eu/en/ La Tania - Artistic Director/Dancer/Chorographer/Actor La Tania is an award winning dancer and choreographer born in  France and raised in Spain. Now settled in Oakland, her awards include the Guggenheim Fellowship, Fellowships from the National Endowments for the Arts, Choreographers Fellowship from The California Arts Council and two Isadora Duncan Dance Awards. She founded La Tania Baile Flamenco a company dedicated to preserving and promoting flamenco dance in the Bay Area. http://www.lataniaflamenco.com José Luis Rodríguez - Composer / Sound Design / Guitarist 
José Luis Rodríguez is an award-winning flamenco guitarist, composer, and performer whose original compositions and performances blend traditional and experimental instrumentation delivered with an emotional brio that is without parallel. Rodriguez has recorded several classic and experimental albums, accompanied important figures in flamenco song and dance, and composed original scores for dance, film, and performance art installations. Among his most notable works are compositions created for Ballet Cristina Hoyos, Ballet Flamenco de Andalucía, Belen Maya, Juan Carlos Lerida, Ramon Oller and the 2015 collaboration with LA Philharmonic and Maestro Gustavo Dudamel in collaboration with Antonio Canales, Farruquito and Siudy Garrido (artistic director) in Amor Brujo. http://www.joseluisrodriguez.us/#! Jesús Montoya – Singer
Jesús Montoya is from Seville, Spain. Born into the one of the oldest Gypsy families. Jesus was steeped in flamenco purity and power, earning him the name “ El Rey Del Fandangos Grande” at age 7 and by age 9 he had started his professional singing career. He was the featured singer for the legendary Fernanda Romero, Isabel Vargas, Andres Marin, Pepe Rios,La Cipri & Manuela Salazar, Juan Ogalla, Alejandro Granados, Isabel Lopez, Omayra Amaya, Ciro, Juaquin Ruiz, Esrella Moreno, La Truco, Carmela Greco, Pepa Montes, Manolete, Jose Galvan and many more. In addition to his Spanish and European touring credentials, he has toured tirelessly with major flamenco companies throughout the United States and Canada to sold out audiences. http://jesusmontoya.net FEATURED DANCERS (In alphabetical order) Fanny Ara, Lakshmi Basile, Melissa Cruz, Marina Elana, Farruquito, Christina Hall, Clara Rodriguez, La Tania, Carola Zertuche MUSICIANS (In alphabetical order) Roberto Aguilar, Jose Manuel Blanco "Grillo", Cliff Eberhardt, David McLean, Jesus Montoya, Yuli Norrish, Clara Rodriguez, Jose Luis Rodriguez, La Tota, Roberto Zamora DANCERS We have over 30 Flamenco dancers from the San Francisco Bay area participating in the film. ACTORS (In alphabetical order) Roberto Aguilar, Nikki Chase, Karina Cornelio, Farruquito, Michael French, Joe Galambos
Joelle Goncalves, Carlos Hailey, Karl-Heinz Teuber, Baily Hopkins, Lea Kobell, La Tania, Lindsay Mason, David McGinn, Sara Moncada, Chris Morrell, Janessa Olsen, Lollie Ortiz, Kunal Prasad, Puneet Prasad. THE TEAM (In alphabetical order) Mathew Antaky, theatrical lighting, Stephen Berke camera, Alec Boehm, camera / DP fill in, Lena Chavez, hair and makeup, Kathy Douglas, screenwriter and director, Greg Landau, production sound manager, Eddie Madril, assistant director, Jacob Martinez , lighting/grip, Scott Mason, co-producer, Sara Moncada, producer, Claudia Ponce, hair and makeup, Rachel Poulos, wardrobe, Conrad Scheepers, sound, Laina Thomas lighting/grip, Marlon Torres, director of photography, Renick Turley, camera. FINDING Compás - One's Inner Harmony

title: Categories
description: PROJECT STORY La Noche Lleva Espuelas ( The Night Wears Spurs ) is a supernatural Mexican western-giallo — a collision of genres where the haunting suspense of Italian giallo meets the grit of spaghetti westerns, wrapped in the supernatural shadows of Mexican folklore. In the arid borderlands of northern Mexico, a respected charro named Manuel lives a quiet life devoted to his wife, Eva, and his horses. One night, he witnesses Eva’s brutal abduction and murder at the hands of men working for the local patrón — a politician entangled in corruption, narco-financing, and human trafficking. Manuel tries to save her, but is killed and buried in his black, silver-embroidered suit, his spurs silenced forever. But the dead do not always rest. Doña Cata, an elderly ranch worker and keeper of ancient Mexica knowledge, performs a ritual deep in the desert to restore balance. The earth opens, a black horse returns, and Manuel rides again — no longer a man, but a silent judgment. One by one, those responsible fall under his unrelenting vengeance. Every echo of his spurs signals that justice is coming. Visually, La Noche Lleva Espuelas is a fever dream: the heat and dust of the Mexican north, the sharp shadows of giallo lighting, and the romantic tragedy of the western. It’s a story about love and loss, about a land silenced by power and fear, and about what rises from the desert when justice is denied. We are currently in pre-production , assembling our creative team and preparing everything from costume and set design to camera tests and location scouting. Your support will help us take the crucial steps that bring this vision to life. This is more than a film — it’s a haunting ride into the heart of the border, where the silence finally learns to bleed. WHY THIS FILM MATTERS The north of Mexico is a land of beauty and brutality — a place where desert winds carry both ancient legends and modern tragedies. La Noche Lleva Espuelas draws on this landscape to tell a story rooted in reality: the silenced crimes against women, the corruption that shields the powerful, and the resilience of those who remain to remember. This is not just a revenge story — it’s a tale woven from Mexican folklore, where the dead ride again and the desert itself can be an accomplice in justice. The imagery of the charro in black, the echo of spurs in the night, and the shadows of giallo-style horror are not just stylistic choices; they are symbols of resistance, memory, and cultural identity. As Mexican filmmakers and storytellers, we believe in telling our own stories — ones that honor our landscapes, our traditions, and our struggles. With La Noche Lleva Espuelas , we are bringing to the screen a genre-bending film that speaks to both international audiences and the communities whose stories are too often overlooked. By supporting this project, you are not only helping to make an independent film — you are helping to preserve a piece of cultural heritage, to amplify voices from the border, and to ensure that the silence of impunity is broken.

title: Categories
description: UPDATE! With just under a week to go, Upper Michigan native and NFL Network analyst Steve Mariucci has arrived to rally the team and push us forward. Lets Go Northbound! We can't wait to introduce actress Molly Leland (Greys Anatomy, Law & Order:SVU, Ozark) in NORTHBOUND: Season 3, but we need your pledges to bring her to Michigan and bring our grassroots project to the next level. Watch her testimonial below! Actor and Podcast pioneer Dan Klass will join our cast as Dr. Stephen Trepanier, one of the key figures behind the cataclysmic event that forms the foundations of the entire series. NORTHBOUND is a post-apocalyptic web series set in a North American wilderness soon after a mysterious event called The Cataclysm killed millions in a single day. Seasons 1 & 2 of the series are free to view exclusively on Seeka TV , and through their apps on Roku , Apple TV , iOS , Google Play , and Amazon . From the beginning, our team has devoted itself to producing NORTHBOUND in Michigan's Upper Peninsula, and aspire to make a lasting cultural and economic impact on the region. When complete, all 3 seasons of NORTHBOUND will serve as the prelude to a feature film titled NORTHSTAR . Taken as a whole, THE NORTHSTAR SAGA tells the story of a father, his comatose daughter, and a world-altering mystery lying at the heart of The Cataclysm. This journey will take father and daughter across a new America, where they will work to rebuild their lives under the looming threat of an authoritarian regime that is building strength in the East. Promotional photo by NORTHBOUND 3 cinematographer Michael Johns, featuring Alex's team of Infinity Group guerrillas Since premiering on Seeka TV in October 2017, NORTHBOUND has consistently topped the 10 most-viewed shows on the site. Thanks to a tremendous outpouring of support from the Kickstarter Community in 2016 and our creative family in Michigan, we have reached a crucial turning point. Season 3 will close out the NORTHBOUND era, and lead directly into the opening scenes of NORTHSTAR . There has never been a better time for YOU to come on board. Season 1 begins 3 Months after The Cataclysm and follows a former marine named Alex as he retreats with his comatose daughter Crystal into a post-apocalyptic wilderness. Over the course of this first year, Alex encounters two emergent groups: a militia nicknamed The Firewalkers , and a much graver threat called Allied Command North America . Alex soon discovers The Firewalkers are supplying Allied Command with comatose "sleepers", much like his daughter, for a still unknown purpose. After narrowly surviving these events, Alex and Crystal are saved by The Infinity Group , a small band of refugees led by a Minister named Wallace. Season 2 resumes 18 Months after The Cataclysm and finds Alex taking on more leadership within the ranks of Wallace's Infinity Group . His growing bond with the Minister is tested when he helps rescue a sleeper from Allied Command captivity. Blaming The Firewalkers for this incident, Allied Command coerces the militia leaders to retrieve their lost asset at all costs. Outnumbered and outgunned on two flanks, The Infinity Group is caught in the middle of a power struggle that could ignite into a violent turf-war. Season 3 picks up a few days following Season 2’s cliffhanger ending .  Reeling from an assassination attempt, Alex ponders whether to go off on his own with Crystal, or lead an Infinity Group offensive to snuff The Firewalkers out once and for all. Allied Command however, has decided to take a firmer hand in the region, forcing all three factions into a decisive clash that will end the NORTHBOUND series and open the door to NORTHSTAR . NORTHBOUND is produced entirely in Michigan’s Upper Peninsula, a geologically striking region that borders Wisconsin and America’s Great Lakes. Series co-creators Seth and Nathan Anderson grew up in the area and decided to craft a futuristic survival story around the forests, mines, and lakes that inspired their creative development. Each NORTHBOUND episode opens with an image of Upper Michigan; a primal landscape that will continue to define the ongoing NORTHSTAR SAGA. As co-creator Seth Anderson puts it, "Michigan is the project. You can't separate NORTHBOUND from where it's made. When you stand in the forests here, you are surrounded by eons of history, which is both inspiring and humbling. It felt like the right place for a story about starting over, and the proper backdrop for a new mythology." NORTHSTAR SAGA co-creators (L-R) Jason Hagen, Seth Anderson, and Nathan Anderson in front of the historic Braumart Theatre in downtown Iron Mountain, Michigan NORTHBOUND is the brainchild of brothers Seth and Nathan Anderson , and producer/writer Jason Hagen . The trio has made films together for over a decade under the banner, Lullskull Ltd. After cutting their teeth with a series of shorts, they decided to make their first feature film concept, NORTHSTAR in their hometown of Iron Mountain, Michigan. Soon after the Andersons began researching the feasibility of mounting a major motion picture production in Northern Michigan, executive producer Gary M. Bettman joined the team, bringing his 20 years of experience as a feature film producer to the project. NORTHBOUND Co-Creator Nathan Anderson and Executive Producer Gary Bettman discuss Season 2 at the 2018 Moving Parts Film Festival in Los Angeles with moderator Monica Quinn Season 1 of NORTHBOUND came about after word spread of the plans to make NORTHSTAR in the area. A local group of creatives, led by NORTHBOUND actor Nate Alwine approached the team about making some videos that could serve as a "proof of concept" for potential investors. The result was a promotional video that evolved into full-fledged episodes in 2015. This new project, now called NORTHBOUND, was exclusively acquired by Minneapolis-based distributor Seeka TV in 2017, and later sponsored by national beverage brand Rip It Energy Fuel . Since then, this early collaboration between Lullskull Ltd. and a core team of talented individuals based in Upper Michigan has solidified into the current braintrust behind Seasons 1-3. The current NORTHBOUND creative family at a September 2018 screening event in Upper Michigan In addition to a talented regional cast that expanded in Season 2, the final season will add Los Angeles-based actor Dan Klass , New York-based actress Molly Leland , and Upper Peninsula-based actress Alisha Mondloch . Dan, Molly and Ali will debut in Season 3 as a set of characters that will go on to play pivotal roles in NORTHSTAR , and their presence will only help to further bridge the gap between the web series and feature film . Since 2014, our team has grown from 5 to nearly 50. Many of these individuals work on a volunteer basis and demonstrate a can-do spirit that has overcome many of the obstacles that typically keep projects like NORTHBOUND from happening outside of Hollywood. The result is something that wasn't possible until recently: an ongoing media project with a potential worldwide reach that is produced efficiently outside of a major media market. Your contribution can only help us cement our commitment to making films in Upper Michigan, thereby opening the potential for new creative industry jobs that would not exist otherwise. Our journey began with a feature film idea that led the three of us back home to Midwest. Along the way, we found the community we needed to help us get even closer to that dream. Now with Season 3, NORTHSTAR is within view, and we've set 3 goals: First is creative. Season 3 is the final piece of a storytelling puzzle connecting Season 1 & 2 to NORTHSTAR . It's now or never. We are ready to build this world out, deepen the dramatic intrigue, and take the action to another level. Second is technical. As we demonstrated in Season 2 , every penny we raise will go towards widening the scope of our production. With your pledge, we can put more resources into key departments, such as camera, lighting, sound, and visual effects, not to mention logistical support, such as on-set catering, transportation, and sanitation. All of the work we do now with Season 3 will only help us better prepare for NORTHSTAR . Third is our audience. After raising our visibility with Season 2, our team was able to form product placement partnerships with regional and national brands such as Stormy Kromer , Dickies Medical , NcSTAR and Damascus Worldwide . Along with major sponsor Rip It Energy Fuel , and newly-minted 2019 sponsor True North Outpost , NORTHBOUND will continue to pioneer innovative product sponsorship models. Your resources will also enable us to submit our series to more festivals, which helps us raise our profile in an evolving media landscape that is hungry for original content. In the end, a growing audience number is the most tangible way we can demonstrate our project is viable to co-production companies that can take THE NORTHSTAR SAGA to another level. How your contributions will be utilized to make NORTHBOUND: Season 3 A breakdown of our forward production strategy going into 2021. As with any large goal, we are breaking things down into manageable steps that are ambitious, yet realistic enough for us all to realize today . Season 1 was made on the pooled resources of individual team members. We doubled our resources for Season 2 which was produced for $15,000, thanks chiefly to a successful Kickstarter campaign. Now, with careful creative and logistical planning, the third iteration of NORTHBOUND has been budgeted at a minimum amount of $20,000 . This target will insure that our team can meet core production and marketing requirements to produce the final 4 episodes of NORTHBOUND . If we raise more than $20,000 during the course of this 30 day campaign, we’ve set a stretch-goal of $25,000 , which unlocks more resources into each department, increases cast & crew compensation, can account for additional shoot days, and will expand both festival and marketing outreach possibilities. NORTHBOUND Co-creators Jason Hagen (Left) and Seth Anderson (Right) at a 2017 filmmaking workshop held for High School students in Dickinson County As you consider donating, know that your contribution is an encouragement to filmmakers that are working hard to craft new stories in a landscape dominated by sequels and remakes. Your contribution affirms that we are now in a place to affordably produce trailblazing work outside of established systems that for too long have dominated what gets made, and how it gets seen. Your contribution supports our long-term commitment to make Michigan a new destination for creative employment. Finally, with you behind us, we can inspire future generations to dream without fear and use their gifts to give back to their communities. From our family to yours, THANK YOU .

title: Re;MATCH - The Puzzle Fighter Board Game
description: Ready... FIGHT! *solo & co-op mode materials will be digital downloads by default, and physically included with new art and custom boards if we hit the 500k stretch goal. 🕹️How To Play Watch the 3 minute How To Play video here Solo & Co-op Mode: In solo & co-op mode, each playable character will come with their own Boss board that you and a friend can challenge! Each character's boss board contains a simplified move list. On the Boss's turn, they will clear the bottom row of 3 marbles, then resolve an attack based on those 3 marbles cleared. Each player as well as the boss will only use a single health bar instead of 3 separate health bar, meaning there are no broken buttons and short circuiting in this mode. These boss boards will be released digitally as free printable files. But if we hit the $500k stretch goal, not only will I print the boss boards to physically include them in the game, we'll also create new boss mode art for every character! 🥊Meet the Fighters Each playable character comes in their own tuckbox which holds all of their unique components 🔴Standard Edition The Acrylic Standees are the only difference between Kickstarter and my webstore post release 🔵Collector's Edition! Check out the Re;ACT Artbook Here! Re;MATCH's will be similar. 🟡Super Turbo Edition!!! 🟢Optional Add Ons All of the videos reviews and how to plays use ACRYLIC marbles. This add on gets you an extra pack of 36 marbles that are translucent glass! (You only need 33 to play, but it comes with 3 extra backups) Check out the Re;ACT Artbook Here! Re;MATCH's artbook will be similar. 📈Stretch Goals Why Back Now? REAL Reviews As board game crowdfunding increasingly rely on paid videos, I am very proud to say all of the below reviews were done without any compensation! The Cardboard Herald : "Even though I don't do a lot of 1 v 1 combative games, this has been a hit in my household, and I'm looking forward to the final version!" Click to watch the review! Space-Biff! (Dan Thurot): "T he fighters in Re;MATCH are sharply crafted - and I say this despite playing a prototype, with all the small tweaks and imbalances that always entails." Click to read the review! Kohdok : "The setup is vibrant and inviting while the mechanics click easily. I was able to play this game effortlessly with somebody not used to this style of games!" Click to watch the review! The AV Club: " Not only does Re;MATCH have an original concept and tactical nuances, it does something that even many great board games fail to do: it very much respects the player’s time." Click to read the review! Liege of Games : "This is a head-to-head dueler unlike ANYTHING you have in your collection." Click to watch the review! Cardboard East : "As a Super Puzzle Fighter II Turbo analog, Re;MATCH is everything I wanted it to be." Click to watch the review! What real users are saying on Board Game Geek! You can try Re;MATCH with ALL 8 characters on TTS now! Full Playthroughs You can find many more playthrough videos on my channel here! Tabletop Simulator FREE TO PLAY on Tabletop Simulator NOW! My Other Games Re;ACT - The Arts of War is my first original IP board game. Similar to Re;MATCH, it is also a highly asymmetric 1 v 1 fighting game where every character is a different type of artist with shonen battle manga inspired super powers. Unlike Re;MATCH however, instead of MATCH ing marbles, Re;ACT is all about playing cards to a visualized REACT ion chain inspired by classic TCG stack mechanics. POND is my second original IP board game all about FROGS featuring art by my good friend PondHQ! POND is a 2 to 4 player deck builder war game where you'll deploy and command your small army of frog meeples across the board and wage dice combat. Similar to Re;ACT and Re;MATCH, each player selects an asymmetric frog faction, each with their own unique starting deck of cards and a unique faction ability. It is currently in the middle of production and is expected to begin fulfillment in April. Genshin Tarot is my most polished fan game. Created through the HoYoCREATORS Program, it features original fan art from over 100 different artists, and is a 1 to 4 player solo/co-op deck upgrading game. Each player builds a unique starting deck with 4 random characters. Each character has 2 unique cards, and every card in your deck can then be upgraded into a more powerful version of itself. History & Timeline History of Re;MATCH (it's long): 2018 - Ming designs the first prototype called Orb Strikers 2018 - Ming Enters Orb Strikers to Board Game Design Contest 2019 - A Publisher signs the design and renames it "SENTO" 2020 - SENTO is demoed at PAX & other conventions 2021 - SENTO is put on hold due to publisher's financial struggles 2023 - Ming Publishes his first original IP game, "Re;ACT" 2024 - Ming buys back SENTO and renames it "Re;MATCH" 2025 - Re;MATCH is demo'd PAX, GenCon, and Origins Photo of Re;MATCH from 2018 at ProtoATL Photo of Re;MATCH from 2019 at ProtoATL Re;MATCH was originaly called SENTO and demoed at PAX South & East in 2020 Re;MATCH re-debuting at Pax East in 2025 Timeline Going Forward: MAR 2026 - Re;MATCH Launches on Kickstarter! MARCH 14 - Re;MATCH Online Tournament #1 APR 2026 - Pledge Manager Opens on Backerkit JUN 2026 - Re;MATCH Online Tournament #2 JUL 2026 - Manufacturing Begins DEC 2026 - Re;MATCH Begins Fulfillment Worldwide! This timeline will need to be extended if we unlock stretch goal characters due to the extra time needed to develop those characters. Shipping Because of how much variance there is between the weight of orders and the wide variety of destinations, shipping costs will be collected after the campaign in the pledge manager. I will be fulfilling from 6 Shipping hubs in: USA (PSI Delivers), China (Send From China), Canada (TBD), United Kingdom (Spiral Galaxy Games), France (PTS Operations), and Australia (ARMARLO) Estimated Shipping Cost for 1 KS Standard Edition / Super Turbo Edition Pledge: USA / China - $14 / $18 United Kingdom / Canada - $18 / $24 Mexico - $28 / $34 EU Zone 1 - $18 / $24 EU Zone 2 - $20 / $26 Rest of EU - $30 / $39 Australia & NZ - $32 / $38 Asia Zone 1 - $16 / $22 Asia Zone 2 - $26 / $34 Rest of Asia - $58 / $66 South America - $68 Rest of the World - $ 88 Asia Zone 1: Hong Kong, Macau Asia Zone 2: Japan, South Korea, Malaysia, Singapore, Philippines, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam EU Zone 1: France, Belgium, Switzerland EU Zone 2: Netherlands, Germany, Spain, Portugal, Italy, Poland, Denmark Who is Brother Ming? I (Ming) am a first generation Chinese American immigrant. I moved from China to the US in 4th grade, and got my US citizenship 2 decades later. In 2019, my love of game design was combined with my love of art when I created Anna's Roundtable together with the Fire Emblem fan zine community, commissioning over 200 different artists to create a massive and gorgeous Fire Emblem fan game with 100% original fan art. In 2023, I launched Re;ACT - The Arts of War on Kickstarter, a board game about Artists with Shonen battle manga inspired super powers. This led to me quitting my job to pursue board games full time. I am proud to be one of very few Asian owned publishers in America making complex hobby board games, and I hope the pave the way for more PoC creators to self publish heavier games while actively try to work with marginalized creators. I am also VERY passionate about supporting human artists. I love seeing and experiencing art. From food to music, painting to writing, and of course, video and board games, the best creations also make me admire the people who make them. Meet the Team NO AI ART!!! I, Ming, AKA Brother Ming, and everyone associated with Brother Ming Games, unequivocally denounce the use of AI Art. There is no ethical use of Generative AI to create art. We strongly disapprove of any board game publisher who use Image Generation tools in their process. Board games only have 2 components : written rules and printed physical components. The visuals in board games carry much more weight than in video games. To use AI art in board games is to treat them as nothing more than a product to be shoveled out for the sake of profit. SAY NO TO AI ART IN GAMES The Firefighter from Re;MATCH says NO AI ART (illustrator: @PsyOptima) The Painter from Re;ACT says NO AI ART (illustrator: @loxtix) Fan Art Gallery To support human artists , I went on a mission to commission 100 artists to create art of Re;MATCH characters! Not all of them are shown here! More will be shown via updates and you can find them across social media with the hashtag #ReMatchPuzzleFighter on Twitter , Bluesky , and Instagram . description: Introducing GLYDR Our hands are often overloaded when we play games while our feet are at rest. By moving key actions to your feet with GLYDR, you’re expanding the tools at your disposal during your most intense gaming moments. Expand Your Gaming Toolkit Extra buttons on controllers, keyboards, and mice don’t actually solve the problem of repetitive overuse of your hands and fingers, and often those extra buttons don’t expand your options, they simply move them around. Whether it’s one favorite action or sixteen, every player that uses GLYDR is gaining options they can use in tandem with control schemes they already use. More Than Traditional Pedals Foot-based controllers and pedals currently on the market are particularly suited for one purpose, like single click pedals for single, non-analog actions. Rudder pedals offer a great experience for flight sims, but uses outside of flight sim are limited since the rudders aren't designed to be bidirectional in tilt. Car pedals only go in one direction, limiting uses outside of racing games. GLYDR's versatility is one of its greatest strengths! You'll find improvements in any game you connect to it! *You can still totally use GLYDR for stenography, racing, or flying. Set Up In Minutes Getting GLYDR going in your setup is a snap and will only take a few minutes from unboxing to getting your feet in your favorite games. Step 1: Connect GLYDR via USB or Bluetooth Step 2: Open the Configurator to choose or create a control profile Step 3: Fire up your favorite game Our configurator empowers you not only to select finely-tuned pre-made profiles for the best games and most popular genres, but to create their own profiles to suit their specific needs. You can then share their profiles (think Steam configurations) with the world! We want to build a community of other players who are curious and excited to get the most out of GLYDR. The configurator also allows you to customize individual deadzones per pedal, creating an experience tailor-made for your specific feet. Hard to Reach Actions Made Simple Keyboard reaches and frustrating team communication are a thing of the past with GLYDR, a device perfect for accessing important but awkwardly-situated functions while you play. Mute teammates Push-to-talk Screenshots Gain an Edge on Your Competition GLYDR provides a tactical advantage while playing a vast variety of games spanning all genres. In intense first-person shooters, GLYDR can boost your mobility by moving crouching, peeking, and sprinting to your feet, giving you the hand focus you need to do everything else. Your Gaming Library is #FeetLegal GLYDR is fully compatible out of the box with your entire PC library* and can be reconfigured on the fly with our app. Certain console experiences are compatible as long as they offer keyboard and mouse controls. More official compatibility with consoles is a priority for our team, as is MacOS and mobile compatibility moving forward. If you're into legal console emulation through your PC, GLYDR will work with your setup to turn your classic console games #feetlegal. *There are like...tens of thousands of PC games. We cannot immediately identify any reason GLYDR wouldn't work with whichever PC game you have in mind, but we have also not tested every single game. Take the Friction Out of VR Virtual reality is an amazing way to experience games and digital experiences, but clearing space and standing up to move around isn't always something you want to do. With GLYDR, dusting off your VR headset is a no-brainer because you can play where you are! Just set up GLYDR with Bluetooth on your VR device of choice and play any game that allows for seated play! Don't trip all over yourself to play VR, just get your feet in the game! GLYDR Goes To Work! GLYDR can be configured to move your mouse cursor, not only unlocking even more options in games, but also creating opportunities outside of games to improve productivity and efficiency. That's right, GLYDR can be used with countless apps on your PC desktop. Create profiles for scrubbing video with your feet. Music creation in your digital suite is now superpowered with your feet, allowing more efficient recording setups. Scene switching while you're streaming is also a snap with GLYDR, meaning you can stay focused and connected with your chat and no-look all those cool transitions with your feet. Every GLYDR comes with the necessary USB cable to set up the device if you choose not to connect with Bluetooth. The software needed to configure the device and set custom profiles will be available via desktop or mobile app. Product Details Every GLYDR is built to last with high quality materials needed to withstand the rigors of intense, consistent gameplay over the span of a few years. The pads on each pedal are soft enough for playing with socks or bare feet, with textured grips for control when you need it the most. For playing with shoes, the pads can be cleaned with an alcohol solution and gentle cloth as needed. Our cofounder Rick Tett has been iterating on GLYDR for years, going through multiple variations and testing the product through all of these cycles. Here are some shots of just a few of our working prototypes: GLYDR has five working patents covering the United States, China, Korea, and Japan, with more patents pending. Meet the Team Based in Dallas, Texas, the GLYDR team is made up of curious, innovative folks who know the next frontier in gaming is incorporating our feet! Rick Tett - Inventor, CTO, & Cofounder Rick got the idea to build GLYDR after watching nephew zoom around on a hoverboard in the physical world and thought "the simple forward and backward foot motion would work in the digital world, too!" Rick built the first GLYDR prototype as a way to locomote in virtual reality and the concept evolved from there to incorporate general video gaming. Peter Bartnik - CEO & Cofounder Peter comes to us from Down Under and is awaiting the next great Aussie Rules Football video game so he can dominate with GLYDR. Peter's decades of consumer product experience ensures GLYDR is going to hit the market with quality and our customers in mind at all times. John Warren - CMO & Cofounder John is a thirteen year games industry veteran, having tackled the publishing and media worlds previously as one of the founders of Fanbyte.com. Before that, he made games and continues his journey deeper into the games space by helping bring GLYDR to market. He recently discovered he doesn't actually stink at Apex Legends if he uses GLYDR for sprinting and sliding. Our Advisors We have an advisory team including Jordan Reiss , who saw an exit with gaming hardware company Astro Gaming to Skullcandy and then Logitech, Adam Rymer , an entertainment and games industry veteran who ran Optic Gaming, and Jinwon Lee , a manufacturing specialist with vast experience in wearable and consumer technology. Shipping Estimates Please note that the pledge amount does not contain taxes or shipping. If applicable, backers will be responsible for VAT/Customs tax for their respective countries. These costs will only be charged once the campaign is complete and we are ready to ship your package, to ensure that we can offer the best and most accurate rates. We will send you a survey where you will be able to provide your address and pay for your shipping costs. We will also let you know if any taxes are due, depending on your location. This project is boosted by BackerCrew!

description: The fourth installment of our Dr. Livingston’s Anatomy Jigsaw Puzzle line features larger-than-life puzzles built to showcase the Human Heart, Brain, Eye, Hands, and Feet. These unique puzzles were created to dynamically capture parts of human life at work. The puzzle pieces are designed to outline and highlight the stunning art, created by a Certified Medical Illustrator. Mesa Schumacher is a Certified Medical Illustrator who studied at Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine.  She has worked in scientific and medical visualization for over ten years and loves problems that wrinkle her brain. See more of Mesa's work at www.mesaschumacher.com All puzzle dimensions, piece counts, and piece shapes are subject to change. In this volume, you’ll assemble an accurate cross-cut section of the heart that gives you a detailed interior view from brachiocephalic artery to descending aorta. Focusing on the powerhouse of our cardiovascular system, this puzzle shows what it takes to pump 2,000 gallons of blood daily throughout our bodies. See the atria and ventricles that contract and relax to circulate oxygen and other nutrients. Follow the pulmonary vessels that transport carbon dioxide and other waste products to be replenished or discarded. Note the pericardium that protects and preserves our heart. All puzzle dimensions, piece counts, and piece shapes are subject to change. In this volume, you’ll assemble an accurate cross-cut section of the head that gives you a detailed interior view from corpus callosum to spinal cord. Focusing on the hub of our central nervous system, this puzzle uncovers the astounding organ that gives rise to our inner experience and takes in sensory data. See the cerebrum that enables us to reason, the cerebellum that allows us to explore the world, and the brain stem that tells our hearts to beat. Trace the brain’s lobes and hemispheres; locate its deep structures and encasing bones. Track the woven nerves from which we perceive our surroundings, the blood vessels that keep us nourished, and the protective tissues that keep our brains intact. All puzzle dimensions, piece counts, and piece shapes are subject to change. In this volume, you’ll assemble an accurate cross-cut section of the eye that gives you a detailed interior view from the lateral rectus muscle to the cornea. Focusing on our windows to the world, this puzzle illuminates the many specialized parts that empower us to see. Look through the lens that, along with the cornea, allows light into the eye and focuses its rays onto the retina, a nerve layer that contains photosensitive cells. Observe the iris and pupil that act like a camera’s diaphragm and aperture to adjust to our surroundings and control the amount of light we take in. Marvel at the macula that gives detailed clarity to our sight. Find the optical nerve, the sum of more than a million fibers that transmit impulses from the retina to the visual cortex. Track the extraocular muscles that rotate our eyes and expand our field of vision. When finished assembling these exquisite pieces, you will be in awe of your accomplishment and your body’s complexity. All puzzle dimensions, piece counts, and piece shapes are subject to change. In this volume, you’ll assemble an accurate cross-cut section of the hands that gives you a detailed interior view from ulnar artery to distal phalanx. Focusing on our prehensile appendages, this puzzle displays the numerous intricate systems that enable us to grasp our surroundings and connect with our kin. Count the bones that gird our great dexterity. Trace blood vessels as they weave throughout the wrist and onward. Track the median and other nerves that convey crucial tactile information. See the hinge joints at the fingers’ knuckles that give us a powerful grip and the ligaments that limit our extension. Note the flexor tendons that allow us to straighten our digits and the abductor pollicis that helps move our opposable thumbs. All puzzle dimensions, piece counts, and piece shapes are subject to change. In this volume, you’ll assemble an accurate cross-cut section of the feet that gives you a detailed interior view from tibia to distal phalanx. Focusing on our body’s foundation, this puzzle illustrates the rigid yet flexible structures upon which we stand, walk, run, climb, and jump. See the bones that combine to keep us upright and the articulated joints that provide us with an impressive range of motion and bipedal balance. See the talus’ mortise and tenon joint with the tibia that enables us to bend and flex. Note the thick Achilles tendon that springs us into action and the flexor retinaculum and other ligaments that bind everything together. Follow blood vessels like the tarsal artery that supply these weight-bearing wonders. The puzzle boxes of this series are designed to look like vintage medical text books. They open in a book style, with a magnetic closure. Inside each box, an anatomy guide details what you’re seeing. Shipping costs for each reward level and country are shown within the pledge levels. These will be charged with your pledge as soon as the campaign ends. These shipping charges are all subsidized from the actual cost, to make it cheaper for you to get a copy of the puzzles. All puzzles pledged for during the campaign will be shipping from US, EU, UK, Canada, and Australia through several fulfillment centers, ensuring that we have "Friendly Shipping" to all countries in these regions. Sales Tax and VAT are included in these costs , which may result in higher shipping costs in some regions. PLEASE NOTE: Please note that any pledges not confirmed within 6 months from the time the campaign begins actual fulfillment may need to be sent outside of our normal fulfillment process, and so will incur increased costs, customs fees, and/or may receive the closest available product (if a limited print run is no longer available). No guarantees can be made concerning pledges not confirmed more than 6 months after the shipping begins. Our complete Human Body series is available for purchase and shipping now . Assemble each of the 7 volumes independently, or build the complete set to form a 10’ tall human. Over 4300 pieces total! We just released a 100 piece, 4’ tall floor puzzle for kids. The giant sturdy pieces and included anatomy guide will engage their hands and spark their curiosity. Available now in the U.S. and coming soon internationally! Play through the fundamentals of chemistry and biology with our science games ! They’re designed with the gamer in mind, and have received recommendations by the NSTA and an endorsement by the Journal of Cell Science.

title: Starground | Multiplayer Automation & Roguelite
description: Starground is a multiplayer top-down automation and roguelite game. All progression trees are completely independent from each other, so you can play through in a truly flexible way. Spend all your time automating, dungeoning , or maybe a bit of both. There's no right way to play the game, and any path you take will let you win. If you want to play together , invite your friends to work on the same things, or divide and conquer each part of the game. Play the demo here! Join the Discord here! Starground is coming to PC through Steam Early Access in 2024. Multiplayer Co-operative multiplayer with as many friends as you'd like! Starground was built up with multiplayer capability from the very beginning. Experience what Starground has to offer as a full co-operative experience with as many friends as you'd like. If multiplayer isn't your thing though, you can play alone in single player too! Automation Automate resource collection and manufacturing! Automation is a core pillar of what Starground is. Work your way through a research tree filled with all sorts of unique machines and solutions to your logistical needs. Start humble by mining resources by hand and smelting manually, but soon build up to a formidable factory that can tackle anything you can throw at it. Dungeoning Fight enemies and grab loot in procedural dungeons! If you're not into automation or need to wait for your factory, then venture to other places in the solar system to explore and crawl your way through a variety of dungeons. Battle enemies, avoid obstacles, kill bosses, and reap the rewards of your effort. Take your loot from dungeons and make yourself stronger. Modular Weapons Create custom weapons with different components! Create your own unique weapons by using different components found in dungeons or craft your own. Make a spear, dual-bladed sword, or maybe a gun on a stick. The choice is yours, and variety is near endless! Each component has a different stat that effects the overall performance of a given weapon. Stretch Goals The Team Why Kickstarter Indie game development is hard, and trying to do that while also making ends meet is a herculean task. We chose Kickstarter so that we can help make our passion our full time jobs and continue to make games for people to enjoy. Kickstarter is the way for us to financially support ourselves while we spend the necessary time in order to get our projects off the ground and self-sustaining.
