Shut Up and Sit Down take a look at Cascadia: Alpine Lakes ! Steph and Michael teach and play Cascadia: Alpine Lakes on All The Games with Steph! Amanda plays Cascadia: Alpine Lakes solo on stream! Randy and Amanda play Cascadia: Alpine Lakes ! Catch the Tantrum House playthrough from October 16th! Watch Banz play Cascadia: Alpine Lakes on Twitch! BJ and Jeremy play Cascadia: Alpine Lakes on Board Game Gumbo! What People Are Saying Amy and Maggie from ThinkerThemer and Richard from Rahdo Runs Through give their thoughts on the game! Sir Thecos previews and reviews Cascadia: Alpine Lakes ! See what Alex thinks of Cascadia: Alpine Lakes in his Board Game Co review! Check out what Alicia and Dwayne of BlackBoardGaming have to say about Cascadia: Alpine Lakes ! See what Sara thinks of Cascadia: Alpine Lakes on Let's Table It! Kimberly at Tabletop Tolson previews Cascadia: Alpine Lakes ! David and Ryan take a look at Cascadia: Alpine Lakes in their Man vs Meeple preview! Learn how to play en français with ProfesseurBoardGames! RollingReggie check out Cascadia: Alpine Lakes! Jenna previews the game on The Board Game Garden! Tantrum House Reviews Cascadia: Alpine Lakes! Brian Chandler reviews Cascadia: Alpine Lakes on Colorblind Games Read Jonathan Liu's review of Cascadia: Alpine Lakes on Geekdad! Check out what Andy Matthews has to say about the game on Meeple Mountain! Does Cascadia: Alpine Lakes stack up? Find out in Eric's review on What's Eric Playing? Upgrades We have put a lot of time and effort into the design and development of Cascadia: Alpine Lakes . We believe that we are presenting you with a complete and refined game that is fantastic, right out of the box. However, without stretch goals, we have decided to partner with AEG early and make a larger initial print run in order to add lots of automatic upgrades that would normally only be possible with campaign stretch goals! Below are some of the upgrades that you will receive with the KS Edition of Cascadia: Alpine Lakes . These upgrades will make each game feel more premium and will give you the best possible gaming experience! Pledge Levels Interested in Calico, Knitting Circle, Cascadia, Cascadia: Landmarks, Verdant, Fit to Print, Nocturne, Point Salad, Point City, Point Galaxy, Deep Dive, Propolis, or Cascadia: Rolling ? Get the Kickstarter Editions of these games in our post-campaign pledge manager with free promos, special bundle prices, and shipping discounts! ***These pledge level prices are set well below the MSRP of the game, when it reaches retail distribution in the future. In the past, our games have been $50+ MSRP when they reach retail, and these KS Editions of Cascadia: Alpine Lakes also include additional free content that will not be included in the retail edition. We value your contribution and want to provide you with outstanding value for your early support!*** Shipping We know that part of the challenge of backing a Kickstarter campaign is that there is a considerable cost associated with shipping games from our manufacturing partners directly to your door. We would like to help with some of that burden, so we are offering a large discount off of our quoted shipping rates to all of our backers! During our previous campaigns, we have subsidized over $250,000 in backer shipping charges to help pitch in, and we’re continuing with that trend here. Shipping will be charged in our post-campaign pledge manager. Using the pledge manager, you will be able to confirm your address and finalize your order. We have included shipping estimates for some common geographic areas in the chart below. Please note that these are estimates and the actual costs of shipping could change based on many circumstances. Because of the uncertainty of tariffs, we may need to add a small charge for tariffs for US backers at the time of shipping. We always do our best to keep shipping as low as possible, but many events beyond our control could always significantly shift prices. The games will ship next year and there is always uncertainty about how much global shipping could rise. We are dedicated to getting you the best possible value for shipping and are committed to shouldering a significant portion of the burden of shipping the game to you. We will provide customs-friendly shipping to the USA, Canada, the UK, the EU, China, and Australia. Please also note that due to strict tax laws, some sales will be subject to sales tax and VAT, where applicable. Taxes will be collected in our pledge manager after the campaign ends. Backing for additional copies of the games, or other games in our catalog, will provide lower per-unit shipping costs - so getting an extra copy for a friend or family member is a great value! We will also have copies of other Flatout Games CoLab titles available in our post-campaign pledge manager so you can take advantage of shipping discounts for multiple items! Did you miss out on one of our previous games? This is a great place to pick up the KS Editions of the games, not available through standard distribution! Meet the Team Flatout Games is a board game design collaborative founded in 2017 by members Molly Johnson, Robert Melvin, and Shawn Stankewich. As a design collaborative, they have several award-winning designs under their belt, including the 2019 Dice Tower Family Game of the Year and Spiel des Jahres Recommended Point Salad (2019, AEG); Truffle Shuffle (2020, AEG); TEN (2021, AEG); Point City (2023, Flatout Games); Point Galaxy (2025, Flatout Games); Propolis (2025, Flatout Games); Deep Dive (2023, Flatout Games); Verdant (2022, Flatout Games); Satchel Quest (2026, Weird City Games); Public Market (2021, Talon Strikes Studios); Dollars to Donuts (2021, Crafty Games); and Abstract Academy (2021, Crafty Games). They have also been developers on all Flatout Games published titles. What’s this ‘CoLab’ all about? In early 2019 Flatout Games opened its doors to create the Flatout Games CoLab. This CoLab is an opportunity for the founding members of Flatout Games to team up with awesome established and emerging folks in the board game industry and make cool things together. Since 2019, the CoLab has brought you Calico, Cascadia, Verdant, Fit to Print, Point City and Deep Dive , Nocturne , Cascadia: Rolling, Knitting Circle, and Point Galaxy and Propolis, with a combined total of over 70,000 backers! Our approach is to build the best possible experiences by involving everyone in the entire process. We strive to build passion in each of our collaborators through shared efforts and shared profits. The publication of Cascadia: Alpine Lakes is a passion project for everyone involved, and we are all part of an interconnected team - we take risks and are rewarded together. We hope you will join us on our journey! Let’s meet the Cascadia: Alpine Lakes team! Dylan Mangini, our graphic designer, makes amazing modern board game apparel as well. If you’re looking for some sweet new threads, check out www.teebletop.com ! You can check out more of Beth Sobel’s fantastic work at www.bethsobel.com ! Flatout Games is focused on creating innovative and elegant game experiences. We love puzzly games with simple rules, high replayability, and great tension! Players’ options and decisions should be complex, rules should not be. We love products that are immaculately detailed, and designed with clarity and simplicity at the forefront Thank You! We would like to take this opportunity to say thank you to people who have helped make Cascadia: Alpine Lakes the game it is today! We could not have done it without the help of these amazing supporters: Marleen Arenivar, Kimberley Bamburak, Nicolas Bongiu, Haley Shae Brown, Kaliope Bush, Max Clendenning, Jeremy Davis, Karen Dong, Patrick Hayden, Tami Hurlburt, David Iezzi, Carla Kopp, Nick Kopp, Charlotte Kyle, Emma Larkins, Peter McPherson, Tylor Murray, Andromeda Pendragon, Aaron Russin, Nicholas Trahan, Cody Thompson, Ilya Ushakov, Emily Vincent, Josh Williams, Kyndra Williams, Amanda Wong, John Zinser. Arcane Comics, Blue Highway Games, Playtest Northwest, The UnPub, and Zephyr Workshop. All of the reviewers and previewers who checked out Cascadia: Alpine Lakes and helped us to spread the word! Thank you so much for taking time to learn more about Cascadia: Alpine Lakes ! We hope you will join us on the journey to making it a success!

title: Hinterlight
description: What is Hinterlight? A tome of gothic roleplay with 120+ full-color, art-heavy pages. Within, you will find… A COLLABORATIVE WORLDBUILDING RITUAL. A deck of cards, four(ish) willing compatriots and the gentle guidance of our play-tools are all you need to mold a fae dreamscape stranger than any solitary mind could conceive. Assemble a band of ludicrously gloomy ne’er-do-wells. Recall and map the haunted forest that claimed your miserable lives. Bind yourselves to the noble faerie who preside over the grim ritual of your resurrection. It's a wicked fever-dream of manufactured memories, tragic tales and enticing possibilities that come together to create a world rich in fae melodrama & ready for deep exploration. PROCEDURES FOR RESONANT ROLEPLAY. After the ritual is complete, use our simple card-based system for play not only aesthetically but thematically gothic. Fetch A Candle is a Together We Go game less concerned with the risks of failure than with what success will cost you. Wherever you may go, you carry your choices with you. When they grow too heavy, you must choose: Surrender or Persevere? Whether you elect a GM to guide you in your further adventures or choose to share that burden in perpetuity, Hinterlight has a robust toolkit for quick and easy campaign prep. Savor a morsel of the ritual's power for yourself: Quickstart Available Now! CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD NOW--FREE UNTIL THE CAMPAIGN ENDS The Ritual The Hymn of the Hollow Flame is a spell to help the dead remember how to live again. Entertain the noble fae with the details of your recent tragedy, and they shall unwind the gruesome demise that’s waiting at the end of it. Draw cards to rekindle lost memories. Consult the hymn to interpret these visions. As they leak back in through the cracks in your skulls, you will learn not only of your own sad fates but also of this strange world you’ve come to be lost in… Wherein we draw cards to learn WHO YOU WERE. Reassemble the meager pile of tragedies you were made of. Recount every thoughtless act that drove you into the faeries' arms. The suits of your cards reveal what the fae wish to know about you. Answer their inquiries, no matter how personal. Set down your portrait for posterity by marking your effigy. Account for what few talents and personal effects you can yet lay claim to. Wherein we build cards into a pattern to help recall THE DWELL, mother of all forests. Turn your mind back to that time spent wandering amidst her shadows and the grisly end you found out there. Behold again her mighty boughs and twisting roots. Physically assemble a patchwork map of your tragic journey through the empire of the noble fae. Once, it shone brighter than any built by mankind. Now all is gone to rot. What dregs are left strewn about the Dwell have since been claimed by the wretched, the untamed and the damned. Surely, you met their ilk along the road to your death? Tell all. We must know how bad things have gotten if we are to reclaim them. Wherein we come home to RHYME , the ancient seat of faerie power. It, too, sags under the weight of neglect and decay. Some few noble fae yet linger. It is to them you must swear your second lives. Build Rhyme's pattern to seal your fate and make your vows. Learn first and foremost the manner of creature you have bound yourself to. Use your deck to reveal their habits, dreams and secrets. Spy out the corners they claim for their own and mark them on Rhyme's legend. Once they’ve set your hearts back to beating, you'll call the banners, fill the halls and set sure again each crumbling brick. It is what you were reborn to do. Wherein we feel the presence of THE ABSENT CROWN , the lost ruler whose influence still stains every corner of the Dwell and keeps this land bound to its rotten past. What gift shall they impart as they render their unseen blessing to this unholy resurrection? As the Hymn comes to a close, crystalize your new selves into something of practical use to your new masters. Then draw a single card, that reshapes everything and sets the stage for further play! Stitch together a record of your past, present, and future doom! The Hymn is no-prep, GM-less, and radically collaborative. Everyone comes to the text together and shares the responsibilities of curating your group’s experience. Continuing play with Fetch A Candle provides a veritable feast of tools at your disposal to make the preparatory work a breeze, with or without a GM. Including: Tables to quickly generate Noble Fae, their private chambers, their strange servants, their uncanny pets, the many rites and feasts that they observe, the daily tragedies that befall them, and More™. Simple GM rituals to expand collaborative locations into one of Hinterlight’s unique adventure types: Haunts, Delves, & Knots. Procedures for courtly intrigue, hypnagogic travel, and quiet moments of growth. A Mystery Map to track the web of intrigue weighing Rhyme down and to set the hidden history of Rhyme’s long decline in stone as your players reveal it bit by bit. Hundreds of combinatory relics, rites, creatures, moments and threats. Intuitive phases of play to help you balance time spent amidst the intrigues of the faeries’ court and the perils of their ruined empire. Pre-gen adventures to showcase the types of play that make Hinterlight shine bright! If you cannot commit to further explorations at the ritual’s close, use these tools to populate Rhyme & the Dwell with an absurd knot of interconnected faeries, tug at the strings of their fascinations, secrets and affiliations, then watch the madcap (and often lethal) fallout of your ill-advised meddling. The Book Just a mock-up, of course. We're printing Hinterlight as a 7x10 softcover. Deluxe tier backers will also receive maps, gatefold character sheets, custom-backed playing cards, an altar cloth, and a few other physical goodies that other folks can buy as add-ons for a few extra bucks. Testimonials & Media "A storytelling seance that makes a first impression out of your character's last words. After just two minutes of play, we had already created a creature fascinating enough to build a setting around. After five minutes, we had crafted a wonderfully cinematic final moment for our doomed adventurers (RIP to the Harvesters of the Pyrite Scale)!" – Sean Ireland, Co-Creator of Triangle Agency "Gorgeous art with evocative world-building tools?  Sign me up!  But to be honest, they had me at 'collage'!"  - Kyle Latino, aka Map Crow "Immaculate, numinous darkness." - Will Jobst, Good Luck Press "Hinterlight will inspire you to draw out a weird and tragic macabre tale you did not know you had. If you like the fae, poetry, or some good Type Two Fun, gather your bravest friends to play Hinterlight." - Superdillin "I love that the tone of Hinterlight is so tightly woven into the gameplay itself." - Will Chesney, Writer and Designer "Hinterlight is a ghastly, gothic delight. Each step of your journey will be fraught with collaborative decision-making, reverberating consequences, and an inevitable ending for each of you. One from which neither characters nor players will emerge entirely the same..." - Ed Spence, Warcradle Studios Hinterlight on the One Shot Podcast! Listen to Episode 2! Listen to Episode 3! Behind the scenes: Art of Hinterlight Fetch A Candle Once the Hymn has gone silent, your song must find a new home. Fetch A Candle is a Together We Go game that explores the soft tragedies and difficult choices you will face in your deathless service to the noble fae. Its card-&-pattern mechanics are dead simple and foster a ceremonial grandeur in play, even after you've left the ritual behind. Feel the unbearable weight of the past. Every corner of The Dwell reflects and manifests your own tortured past, as well as the terrible history of those you’ve sworn to serve. Phase play allows for quiet moments to confront these terrible burdens. What you find there may give you what you need to dispel this land's terrible curse. When death is off the table, what is left to despair? As your characters gain strength, they also risk losing themselves. With every false death, a bit more of your soul lost to the spell. With every return, a bit more of the fae sinks in to fill the gap. Hold yourself to nobler standards. The noble fae do not measure their servants by brawn, reflex or sacrosanctity. Your new shape will be carved from subtler things: authority, memory, patience, grace. Things that will be of some use to you in places where the knife fails. Fetch A Candle's four Humours force you to consider every action and moment of growth in the light of fae standards. Make yourselves in their image, or pay the cost of insolence. Many of TWG's base procedures have been tweaked to resonate with Hinterlight's gothic sensibilities. Ill-gotten gains, foolish mistakes and horrid secrets all weigh equally on sojourners of the Dwell and each fetch their own lurid price back in the safety of the court. Those skills and tools that resonate with you become enshrined in the sanctum of your self. Those that fail you fade and are fed to the flames of your growing strength. When the spell of your resurrection falters, you must surrender to the Hymn and return, for a while, to incorporeality. There, you will see things that only lost lights can see. Hear things no ear of flesh could prick itself to. Commune with things too old to recognize life as a form of thinking. Fetch A Candle is in alpha-test as of this Kickstarter, but we'll share a sneak peek soon! See? They're Having a Good Time! Meet the Team Tyler Lamb Lead Writer & Designer Robbi Burns Artist/Co-Designer Kelsey Jirikils Co-Designer/Administrative Lead Richard Ruane Editor Charlie Ferguson-Avery Trailer/Additional Art & Design Tony V, Plus One EXP Publishing and Fulfillment Shipping Estimates US Shipping will be $8-20 International will be $18-40

title: Skyless Blind - a stylized cosmic horror exploration game
description: Play the Beta Download the beta for Windows PC Skyless Blind is now fully funded! Thank you all so much for making this dream a reality, this means everything to me and I'm so grateful for how much support and enthusiasm our little community has shown. The boundary between universes is falling, one dying world spills into another... Introducing part one of the official OneShot X Skyless Blind crossover update, available July 15th to Kickstarter backers with the "Beta update access" reward tier or above! Drill across the universal barrier and collect OneShot themed items, face corrupted robot enemies, eat pancakes, ride a roomba, and more! If Skyless Blind is fully funded, part two will follow shortly after the end of the campaign. It'll include an Alula cloak, a Niko plush you can combine with the roomba familiar, and more! Also, I just want to say that everyone who backs the game will have their name listed in the credits! (I forgot to put this in the rewards description, sorry!) Overview Skyless Blind is a stylized cosmic horror game set in a shattered cosmos. Explore the atmospheric ruins of incomprehensibly ancient worlds, meet mysterious characters and eldritch entities, unearth reality-bending artifacts, and uncover the dark truth behind the weeping stars of a broken universe in this Yume Nikki -like RPGMaker horror game. Vibrant pixel art, environmental visual effects, ambient sound design and music work together to bring to life the immersive and emotive universe of Skyless Blind. Travel through a nexus of portals to surreal alien worlds, uncover the intricate and thematic mythos of bygone cosmic societies, collect mystifying artifacts and carve out a home for yourself under shattered starlight. Gameplay Skyless Blind is about exploration and mystery, first and foremost. Inspired by and expanding upon the design philosophy of the cult classic indie game Yume Nikki , Skyless is structured as a series of open worlds rich in secrets and oddities to discover, connected through a nexus of interweaving portals. As you travel across the galaxy you’ll encounter new portals to deeper and darker worlds, collectable items to unlock new abilities or customize your appearance, friendly characters and hostile creatures, and fragments of lore to piece together the expansive mythos of the universe. Mysterious ancient homes of bygone civilizations are scattered throughout the Blind, calling to be reclaimed and refurnished with trophies and artifacts collected from your travels. Cryptic puzzles and arcane mechanisms lay still in desolate wastes, silently guarding forgotten knowledge of eons past. Hidden away in the furthest and darkest reaches of space lie secret worlds of untold horrors, awaiting only the most determined of explorers. Gameplay features include: Expansive worlds filled with a variety of atmospheric environments, enigmatic characters and intricate lore Treasures of a bygone age locked away within hidden chests Shattered masks of reality-bending power, waiting to be reforged Collectable cloaks to personalize your appearance Collectable music tapes to be played in your home, expanding the original soundtrack through exploration Hostile alien creatures to be avoided or confronted with optional combat designed to be simple, satisfying, and non-intrusive Complex secret endgame mechanics hidden in the darkest, most distant of worlds And more! Story To the few who still survive among the starlit ruins, the collapse of cosmic civilization is remembered as the " God Calamity." In a single moment, countless planets and stars were torn asunder by fingers of an unseen force—fingers that still cradle the mangled fragments in the sky, frozen against the flow of gravity. The universe had shattered like a pane of glass, and something beyond a veil of darkness is preventing it from healing. What caused this calamity, and why does the universe refuse to heal? The truth is in fragments—but the pieces are all out there, waiting to be put back together. Search for answers among the broken stars. Piece together the hidden history of an unfathomably ancient universe. Take back the skies that once belonged to your precursors. Follow the journey of the anaklyte across eons—a starry-eyed civilization of biomechanical aliens that refused submission to a malicious cosmos.  Learn of their struggle to persist and connect the stars within a dark forest universe, their discovery of hidden physics within the deepest layers of reality, their mysterious origins and their connection to an eldritch entity known as the God Machine . Many strange characters wander the the remnants of their bygone civilizations, all with unique stories to tell. Meet a psychovian witch who leads you on a quest to retrieve a lost artifact from a four-dimensional fissure , befriend the surreal plant-like stellacyte who grows throughout the ruins of a circumstellar particle accelerator , debate ethical philosophy with the last god of the Hyphysicalist Constructancy , and more. Inspirations and origins The largest inspiration of the gameplay design philosophy is Yume Nikki and its many fangames. Yume Nikki was always so enthralling to me because it truly felt like you never knew what you might find, it captured the feeling of exploration perfectly. There were secrets around every corner, and a lingering sense that there were always deeper layers to the world around you. My goal is to capture and enhance that experience, adding mechanics that compliment exploration without getting in the way. And with an expansive mythos behind the world, my hope is that every discovery will feel like unlocking a piece of a larger puzzle. The more you explore, the more you'll understand. Everything is designed around discovery. The world of Skyless pulls inspiration from more sources than I can recall. I've been slowly building this mythos since I was in high school, and so many fragments of my favorite art and ideas have found their way into it in one way or another. The largest influence for the art style would definitely be Hyper Light Drifter by Heart Machine, which is the game that inspired me to start making pixel art in the first place. Some of the largest inspirations for the setting, tone, and concepts would have to be the Remembrance of Earth's Past trilogy by Liu Cixin, the many writings of H.P. Lovecraft , 2001: A Space Odyssey by Arthur C. Clarke, All Tomorrows by C.M. Kösemen, and the lore of Bionicle , funnily enough. More abstractly, I take a lot of inspiration from ideas relating to theoretical physics, astronomy, speculative astrobiology, and philosophy. I also pull from a lot of mythological and religious sources. Don't get the wrong idea though, this isn't a religious game, even if it does use religious parallels to explore other ideas. The long and short of it is that I've been inspired by a lot of whacky stuff. The story of Skyless Blind is a deeply personal one that explores many interwoven themes and ideas that are incredibly important to me. The main idea that I'd like to convey, however, is that purpose and beauty can still be found in a broken world. Skyless is a cosmic horror game, but I like to think of it as " optimistic cosmic horror. " Even in a malicious cosmos, the indomitable human spirit, or the indomitable anaklyte spirit, can persist against all odds. This next part may be heavy to read for some, please keep that in mind. I believe this is important to discuss to help people understand where the message of the game is coming from and why it's so important to me. The story of Skyless is deeply inspired by my personal experiences with tragedy, and the event that motivated it more than anything was losing my sister to a car crash, a little over a year and a half ago. When I got the call telling me the news, I don't think I quite felt or registered what I had heard at first. Shortly after it hit me all at once, and I quite literally felt as though the entire universe had shattered before my eyes. Before then, I was building Skyless casually as a hobby. After this event I decided I would dedicate myself to it entirely. The story of a broken universe, the themes of loss and tragedy, those were set in motion long ago. But after this experience I felt as though I suddenly understood what I was making so much more. It's strange how that worked out, and if I was a spiritual person I'd say I've always known that I was meant to tell this story, I just didn't know why until the time was right. This event drastically influenced the direction of the story and the emphasis of these ideas, and my ultimate goal is to convey to anyone with their own experience of tragedy that there is still beauty and purpose to be found in a broken world, even if you have to make it yourself. I hope my sister would be proud if she could see this. To finish this game I need to finish making the eight main worlds in the nexus, including their unique environments, mechanics, collectables, lore, add multiple new ownable home locations, add new story events and characters, finish some secret stuff I don't want to spoil, and add a lot of polish. I estimate that this will take me about a year of non-stop development, and I'm incredibly excited for it. About two thirds of the base budget will go towards paying my rent, bills, and keeping me fed while I work on the game for the next year. With the exception of my incredibly talented musician and a technical wizard who just recently started helping with a couple programing tasks, Skyless is entirely made by me, which means my living expenses will be the primary budget concern. About 15% will go towards taxes and Kickstarter fees, and the rest will serve as commission payment for the aforementioned musical savant and coding wizard, who have been gracious enough to help in their free time so far. Thank you If you supported this game thank you so, so much. I genuinely don't have the words to express how sincerely grateful I am to have had the opportunity to make it this far. I'm so deeply passionate about this game and it means everything to have a chance to make my dream a reality, to share this world and tell this story. I've poured so much of my soul into this project and I'm beyond excited to have the chance to pour the rest into it. Whoever you are, whether you've experienced your own calamity or not, I hope you can find a little bit of peace among the starlit ruins. I'd also like to thank a few amazing people who have been so incredibly gracious with their time and effort. They've done amazing work in areas of the game I absolutely don't have the necessary skill for, and I couldn't have made it this far without them. Forgotten Dawn is an incredibly talented musician who specializes in ambient and atmospheric music. He's one of my oldest friends, and has been making the incredibly beautiful soundtrack of Skyless from the start. I couldn't imagine working with any other musician on this project, please go check him out. V!RMSKVLL is an insanely talented programer from the Skyless Discord community and has been an incredible help these past couple of months. They really know their way around the back end of RPG Maker, and they've created an amazing menu overhaul system for Skyless. And special thanks to NightMargin , GIR , and CHI XU for providing their experience, time, and relentless support in helping me get this game off the ground. I don’t know where this project would be without them, and I'm incredibly grateful to have such amazing people to help me start my journey in game development. If you'd like to follow the development of Skyless Blind anywhere outside of Kickstarter, please consider joining the Discord server, or following me on Twitter .

title: Categories
description: Over the past 30 years, many people around the world have discovered new meaning to the Scriptures through the long and faithful work of Eugene Peterson in his work "The Message." Our hope is to bring these texts to a new generation of readers through setting them to music through this project in partnership with The Eugene Peterson Center for Christian Imagination. Latifah Alattas, Terrian, and Jessica Fox recording in 2022 The Porter's Gate and The Eugene Peterson Center will gather a group of roughly 25 songwriters, musicians, and scholars to study The Message together; then we will take passages from this and write songs inspired by or directly from the text. We intend to write and record these songs between May and September of 2023. Jon Guerra and Tristen Davenport recording in 2023 We do need help to fund this project. Our hope is that people who already know and love The Message will be enthusiastic about seeing this project come to life, as well as those who have already been listeners of The Porter's Gate Worship Project. Help us spread the word! To bring this project to life, we'll need to raise at least $36,500 with kickstarter. We also hope that if we can raise more than this, that we can write and record even more music and create additional resources like live videos and other performance opportunities. Pledges given through kickstarter are not tax-deductible; but for those who would prefer to make a tax-deductible gift, you can do so easily at https://actintl.givingfuel.com/the-porters-gate . Orlando Palmer (IAMSON) recording in 2022

title: Categories
description: Plus, a very thoughtful and generous write-up about the project from former NPR music producer, Paul Schomer, can be found here: http://www.florfilms.com/radiocrowdfund/nice-bison-caleb-w-cliff-marcellus-hall/ * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * "Embracing the Grey" has been carefully coming into being over the last 2 years.  There are still 2 more songs to be recorded and the album needs to be mastered as well. Therefore, with this money, these 2 songs will be recorded, the album completed, and then professionally mastered. It will also cover necessary recording equipment costs. * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * At multiple points over the last 20 years I have attempted to record an album, yet for various reasons it was never formalized.  During this time there was also the matter of musical maturation and coming to understand just what my music was a vehicle for.  This album's song topics range from breaking through social boundaries to reconnect with each other, recognizing that our thoughts paint the story that is our life, and that love is much bigger than something that you share with just one other person - it is what connects us all. Through these convictions, I see music as something that transcends all differences and allows us to connect at a deeper level. Over the last 2 and a half years I have also been researching the healing effects of music at Lesley University's self-designed master's program.  This expanded on what I innately understood about music in a profound way and this research influenced the album greatly.  Working on this degree also opened my eyes to how important community is and how, in my own opinion, that we need to start meeting our neighbors once more.  In the US, we are growing apart and it is causing the cultural fabric to unravel and suffer. In light of this, another intention of this album is to encourage people to move past their differences, find each other again and seek the potential that is constantly available in life. Lastly, a close friend of mine had been pestering me for many years to make an album.  So, in January 2011 I finally started recording "Traces of It All," the first song on Embracing the Grey .  Later on that year I learned that this same friend had fought through one of life's sudden, intense battles. I was just happy to know he was alive.  It was at this point that I vowed to myself that I would finish this album.  For me, at the heart of my best music are the people that have made my life as amazing as it is and I never want to forget this. Through all of this, and though it has taken me some time, I now understand that my music is for you, and you, and for everyone to recognize that they are bigger than what they think they are.  We are only limited by the limitations that we place on ourselves.  I think it is time that we embrace it all and challenge ourselves because we can do more - if we do it together. Thank you, Caleb Here are two tracks, nearly complete, from the forthcoming album. hear more songs and learn more about my music & art here: http://sonotropic.weebly.com/

title: Categories
description: I put out my last solo record in 2018. Life, other projects, burnout, and loss shored up the dam that all of my expression would live behind between now and then. Writing anything was a near-impossible task. Then, after a singular event turned my entire world on its head this spring, songs began to reveal themselves again. Over the last 2 years, I'd been asking close friends to sit in with me during shows, one-by-one, and then two-by-two, etc. Very slowly, the live show went from being just me and another vocalist or two on stage, to a full-blown 8-person phalanx. So, we decided to take the current 'line-up' into a studio to put these songs to tape. We'll begin to document this process and these songs on Orcas Island in October. We intend to take the project to many different studios around the Seattle area, but to begin in a place as haunted with magic and whimsy as Orcas, well.. what better place to commence the artistic endeavor~ We're gonna take our time with it and let it become what it needs to be for all of us (but mostly me). We'll keep you posted to be sure. This promises to be a record and an experience that will mark a triumphant moment for everyone involved. Come with us, if you please.
