description: "NOTE: On Kickstarter, once a campaign passes its Kickstarter target , as we have, it means funds WILL transact after the campaign (yay!). While this is good news for all - we're now hopeful to raise funds needed toward - licensing music, clearing archival material, and adding to the film's educational content and outreach. With the help of Windham Hill Records' artists, we've added cool new Rewards. Please check 'em out, and thanks!" - Tal Windham Hill Records and its artists produced acoustic music with folk, classical, and jazz influences, yet ... the style and sound couldn't be typecast. It proved to be one of the few times in music history when the name of a record label became a genre unto itself. It was music and mood. It bridged the eras of vinyl and cd. And it came at the precise time when it was most needed. The early 1980's in Palo Alto, CA represented the cusp of the Digital Revolution. Life was getting faster, and contemporary music was getting louder - punk had rebelled against corporate rock with electric guitars pounding out block chords, and lyrics being shouted in angry voices; the loud blaring beats of disco dominated dance clubs. A carpenter named Will Ackerman began writing and recording original compositions for solo guitar. His cousin (and fellow carpenter) Alex De Grassi did the same. Will and Alex were also disenfranchised by corporate rock and pop radio - but they took a gentler, more modest approach, with pastoral ambience and introspective mood, and kept their focus squarely on their music. Windham Hill Records was born. Will Ackerman, playing soulfully Windham Hill Records began making and releasing music that spoke to the soul and our humanity. The sounds reflected meticulous care. The melodies unfolded, like a lotus. The sounds were true, and timeless. Will Ackerman, making music, 50 years later, in his Vermont studio Will and Alex, two acoustic guitarists, began their musical journey. Alex De Grassi, the first to join Will Ackerman on the new Windham Hill Records label Soon after, Will met a wonderfully talented like-minded man who played piano. George Winston joined Windham Hill Records. Will Ackerman (guitar) with George Winston (piano). "Winston's music seemed to hang, suspended in space, each note dropping like rain on a placid lake." - John Diliberto Will wanted to spread the word - and share the unique sound. At first, he did this by loading his Volvo station wagon with his catalog and selling what he could to bookstores and health food stores across the California coast. Windham Hill Records' artists posing on the California coast There was interest in the music. It became quickly evident to Will that he had tapped into something, sonically and societally. Additional talented musicians like Liz Story, Darol Anger, Michael Hedges, and Phil Aaberg joined the label. Barbara Higbie, Mike Marshall, and Michael Manring joined as well. These musicians were not seeking to be rock stars. None were fixated on image or pomp. They were artists who had musical stories to tell. They wanted only for audiences to listen. Windham Hill Records became a brand. A young Barbara Higbie Windham Hill Records went on to become one of the most successful independent record labels on the planet. It produced 27 Gold and Platinum Records in the US and beyond. It changed how audiences listened to albums, and what they got out of them. For Will, it was gratifying that they had produced a sound which defied classification. Their music charted as soft jazz ... contemporary jazz ... New Age ... and in fact, California-based Tower Records gave Windham Hill Records -- its own section in their stores . The music of Windham Hill Records was embraced in almost a reverent way. In an era of accelerating technology and increasing demands on time, it was music that could quiet the mind and soothe the soul. Therapists and medical providers still use it today. And - 50 years later - their artists are still performing and touring, and many are also teaching. The artists - and the label - have thriving fan bases worldwide. Barbara Higbie at the keyboard today Welcome to the Kickstarter campaign for A QUIET REVOLUTION: The Story of Windham Hill Records. Acclaimed filmmaker Tal Skloot, working with much of the talent and team that made it happen, is telling the story of Windham Hill Records - the music, its influence, and its enduring impact. Just as Windham Hill Records has always remained true to itself and its independent stylings, Tal and his team have launched this campaign on Kickstarter to help them tell the story truthfully and independently. "The music produced by Windham Hill Records and its artists is relevant to today's world. I believe that the more frenetic our pace, the more complicated and corporatized our world is, the more that music, like that of Windham Hill Records, provides welcome and important counterbalance. Please check out our Rewards - we've got some good ones. Thanks for your support." - Tal Skloot === BY VIRTUE OF A FISCAL SPONSORSHIP -- -- through Filmmakers Collaborative (SF), pledges made to this campaign are tax-deductible , to the amount and extent allowable by law. See section on "Fiscal Sponsorship" - very bottom of page. === ABOUT THE FILM The film will be a mix of present-day interviews, archival footage, video and stills, music, discussion, history, and behind-the-scenes stories. It will explore the founding and genesis of the label (Will Ackerman's first recording was made on a lark with a $300 investment from friends). It will speak to the music itself, and how it both dovetailed with - and was set against - the societal changes prevalent in the moment. Musicians interviewed for the film will be asked to play a few pieces from the early Windham Hill Records collection, which will be captured up close, with the camera focused on small details, and expressive visuals that tie to the emotions evoked by the pieces. Among the Windham Hill Records artists interviewed for this film, are Philip Aaberg, Will Ackerman, Darol Anger, Alex De Grassi, Barbara Higbie, Michael Manring, Mike Marshall, G.E. Stinson, Liz Story, and George Winston (archival interview). For more about these artists, scroll down to the section entitled: FEATURING ... Others interviewed for the film - including journalists and historians - will theorize as to the underpinnings of the label's popularity, why it was so embraced, and how its popularity has endured to this day. Here's a short message from Will Ackerman: Throughout the film, archival footage will seamlessly integrate with present day footage to connect the past with the present, reflecting the eras spanning the growth of the label, and ultimately the continued timeliness and relevance of Windham Hill Records today. "My vision for the film's added impact is to have audiences reflect on how they've embraced change over the years while maintaining meaning, focus, and introspection. I hope to tie the messages of the film to our collective ability to meet challenges and still remain conscious of personal growth along the way. I plan to develop discussion guides and workshops facilitating conversation on personal development." - Tal Skloot WINDHAM HILL'S PLACE IN MUSIC HISTORY Windham Hill Records occupies a special and significant place in music history. Some of that has been driven by its priorities, innovation, teamwork, and unwavering commitment to its artistry and style. Early on, Will Ackerman began using recording studios (primarily in the SF Bay Area) that prioritized high fidelity. He wanted the nuances of the music to be heard and for the finished product to please even the most discriminating audiophiles. George Winston (on the far left) and Mike Marshall (on the far right) flanking Darol Anger, photographer Irene Young, and Pierre Bensusan. Ackerman also championed catalog "samplers" - which featured his personal favorite tracks on recently released albums of the label's artists. This inspired a multitude of themed compilation albums. Windham Hill Records' holiday-themed releases were very popular Windham Hill Records was aways more concerned with music than image. Even though they had some good-looking folks performing on the label (see below), it was years before an artist's face appeared on one of its album covers. Montreux, with Darol Anger, Mike Marshall, Michael Manring, Tom Miller and Barbara Higbie Probably the most unique aspect of Windham Hill Records was that it became a brand unto itself, and a team. Concert tours were booked as "An Evening with Windham Hill" and everyone wanted to play with everyone else. Guitarist Michael Hedges (L) with bassist Michael Manring (R); the two played together often And probably the most important aspect of Windham Hill Records was that it provided a sound and ethos to help heal a turbulent and distracted society. The music posited a sound not built on anger or dissonance, but contemplation, introspection, and harmony. Audiences today embrace Windham Hill Records as much as ever. Three of George Winston's Gold and Platinum albums MUSIC AND MOOD Music has long been linked to mood. Certain tones, frequencies, and styles have been shown to help people manage stress, aid in their ability to relax, and enhance positive emotion. Physiologically speaking, listening to certain music can relax our sympathetic nervous system, lower our heart rate and lessen blood pressure. Music therapy is an art and science that uses music to heal and alleviate pain. Acoustic music reliant on keyboards and strings like much of Windham Hill Records' albums, help the body and brain absorb frequencies linked to pleasure of a carefree nature, as might be depicted in open air and natural beauty. Windham Hill Records' artists have spoken to their cognizance of this. Many have claimed to use pleasant scenery, outdoor landscapes, and positive recollections from their past in crafting their compositions. And countless Windham Hill Records fans have commented on how listening to the music stimulated their imagination, and oftentimes when they close their eyes, they craft their own visuals to accompany the sounds. Rhythms, vibrations, and simplicity converge to serve as a calming antithesis to the many stresses prevalent in our daily modern grind.  It's fitting that among Windham Hill Records' most popular releases were its regular summer and winter solstice recordings, reflecting nature's balance and the changing of seasons. FEATURING ... (listed alphabetically) Philip Aaberg - among the most technically gifted of the Windham Hill pianists. Before joining the label, he was a journeyman keyboardist for Peter Gabriel, Elvin Bishop, toured with Tom Johnston of the Doobie Brothers, and others. He hails from Montana, where the endless plains stretched to the horizon. Philip Aaberg, in the endless plains of Montana Will Ackerman - founded Windham Hill Records in Palo Alto in 1975. His initial profession was carpentry and building - and he borrowed the name Windham Hill (a place from his adolescence in Vermont) from his building company so that his original business card read, “Windham Hill Builders/Records/Music (BMI)." His impact on instrumental music over the past 40 years is virtually unparalleled. Will Ackerman, at home in Vermont Darol Anger - a fiddler who has helped guide the evolution of contemporary string bands through his involvement with numerous pathbreaking ensembles, such as his Republic of Strings, the Turtle Island String Quartet, Montreux, and others. He envisions a great nation of string players, a floating intergenerational orchestra that plays music scooped from backyards, garages and kitchens in every continent. Alex De Grassi - aguitarist whose deft, finger-style approach to intricate compositions is unmatched. His 1978 recording Turning: Turning Back is cited by Acoustic Guitar magazine as among its top 10 essential finger-style guitar recordings. The Grammy-nominated The Water Garden (1998) is considered a classic of the genre. "Alex de Grassi is a treasure - his technical wizardry as well as his vibrant and poetic music making make him one of the most distinctive steel-string guitarists performing today.” - New York Guitar Festival Director David Spelman. Michael Hedges (1953 - 1997) - creative guitarist who wrote nearly exclusively in alternate tunings. Mastered the technique of slap harmonics (created by slapping the strings over a harmonic node). Will Ackerman heard him perform in a small theater in Palo Alto and signed him on the spot to a recording contract with Windham Hill Records - on a napkin. In an interview, he once said he saw himself as a composer who played guitar, rather than a guitarist who composed music. Barbara Higbie - the first female instrumentalist on the Windham Hill Records label. She has played with Santana, Bonnie Raitt, the Kronos Quartet, Milton Nascimento, and Spyro Gyra, among others. She jokes: "I was working hard on creating my own style of music somewhere between classical music, West African, bluegrass, and Bartók ." Will Ackerman heard one of her early compositions on a Darol Anger album and said: “I want to make a whole album of that!” Michael Manring - he's hailed by many as the world’s leading solo bassist. Manring collaborated frequently with Michael Hedges, who died tragically young. He has a diminutive frame, which belies the intricacy and depth of his bass-playing, and it creates the illusion that you're hearing multiple instruments when he plays bass. Mike Marshall - guitarist and mandolinist who played bluegrass before joining Windham Hill and went on to form his Modern Mandolin Quartet afterward. He is a prolific performer with a deep connection to music education. He once said: "Sometimes a whisper is the best way to get a person's attention." G.E. Stinson - innovative guitarist and founding member of new age/electronic musical group Shadowfax which brought strong Asian and Native American influences to their music. He later entered the Los Angeles underground music community to refine his 'extended technique' and 'frequency manipulation.' Liz Story - a student of classical piano since childhood, Liz met Bill Evans at NYC's The Bottom Line in 1976 and was inspired to begin composing jazz and contemporary music. She has been compared to Copeland, Chopin, Debussy, Keith Jarrett, and Chick Corea, and has released ten full-length albums and wrote or arranged many songs that were released in Windham Hill collections. George Winston (1949 - 2023) - touched the hearts of generations with his many acclaimed solo acoustic piano compositions. Spent his early days in Montana, Mississippi and Florida, and later life living in the San Francisco Bay. His appreciation and love for America’s beautiful landscapes and natural seasons shaped his singular instrumental folk sound. His music is evocative and offers us all a chance to take a step back from our perpetually busy lives to let our minds adventurously wander. Here's a sampling of his well-known composition Autumn: FILMMAKER COMMENTARY Says Tal: "It's special when a filmmaker is able to honestly claim that a film they're producing and/or directing is a "passion project." For me, A QUIET REVOLUTION: The Story of Windham Hill Records, is just that. I grew up in the heyday of the label and was and still am a big fan of the artists. I am also a musician. And I can say that I have been greatly influenced by the unique stylings and sounds of Windham Hill. It's helped me appreciate beauty, taught me to relax, taught me to listen, and it's had a definitive impact on me as an artist, and as a human being. In fact, my father, who passed away when I was young, owned a toy store and used to play Windham Hill Records as his customers shopped for their kids. I can think of no greater honor than to work with Will and others in telling the story of Windham Hill Records. Thank you for supporting the effort and helping this film become a reality." MEET THE TEAM Tal Skloot    Director Tal Skloot is an award-winning documentary filmmaker and editor with a credit list of over twenty-five feature-length documentaries that have won multiple Emmy awards and other recognitions. His work is broadcast nationally on PBS and on multiple streaming platforms. His credits include 4 WHEEL BOB , about wheelchair-hiker Bob Coomber and his attempt to cross the Sierra Nevada and FREEWAY PHILHARMONIC , which follows freelance classical musicians struggling to find a permanent orchestra chair. Both films aired nationally on PBS and screened at numerous film festivals. Tal has also worked on major narrative feature films and television series released by 20th Century Fox, Lucasfilm Ltd., New Line Cinema, Lionsgate and Orion Pictures. Asali Echols    Associate Producer Asali Echols is a Bay Area filmmaker and film editor, whose work has screened in festivals across the country and worldwide. Her directing credits include the animated documentary The Violin Upstairs (2019) and the award-winning short documentary Lion on the Mat (2021). She's edited award-winning feature documentaries, including 2e: Twice Exceptional (2015), Navajo Math Circles (2016), and High as a Kite (2020) . She helped produce the Simons Foundation's 32-part oral history series Science Lives , and edited and produced the You Got to Move short video series about activism in the South. Derek Knowles   Cinematographer Derek Knowles is a filmmaker and cinematographer working in creative non-fiction. His work has been supported by the Tribeca Film Institute, Kartemquin Films, the Berkeley Film Foundation, and California Humanities, it's been featured as a Vimeo Staff pick and on Short of the Week, and has appeared in The New York Times, The New Yorker, The Atlantic, PBS, Frontline, and Aeon , as well as festivals around the world. ABOUT THE REWARDS ALL REWARDS "CHOICES" (like t-shirt size) are made AFTER the campaign; delivery dates are approximate; if you'd like a combination or quantity not offered on the page, click the round image of Tal (top R), send a message and we'll work it out. MOST REWARDS also receive many lower Rewards - check the "items included" at the bottom of the Rewards boxes. EVERY REWARD AT $20 OR MORE includes an advance streaming of the film. EVERY REWARD AT $30 OR MORE includes stream and download, with bonuses. EVERY REWARD AT $95 OR MORE also includes YOUR NAME in the film's closing credits, as part of a scroll, under: " The Producers Wish to Thank ..." Here's a little extra detail on some of the special personalized Rewards, with a few photos: BARBARA HIGBIE CDs - $360 In addition to many of the lower Rewards, you'll receive 3 personally hand-signed and dedicated CDs from Windham Hills Records artist Barbara Higbie. Limited quantity available. LIZ STORY CDs - $360 In addition to many of the lower Rewards, you'll receive 3 personally hand-signed and dedicated CDs from Windham Hills Records artist Liz Story. Limited quantity available. MICHAEL MANRING 1 ON 1 BASS GUITAR LESSON/CHAT - $525 In addition to many of the lower Rewards, you'll receive a private 1-hour bass guitar lesson and/or chat session (virtual) with Michael Manring. Limited quantity available. ALEX DE GRASSI 1 ON 1 GUITAR LESSON/CHAT - $550 In addition to many of the lower Rewards, you'll receive a private 1-hour guitar lesson and/or chat session (virtual) with Alex De Grassi. Limited quantity available. WILL'S GRAB BAG - $600 This is a unique grab bag assortment of music (both CDs and vinyl), mementos, and memorabilia from Windham Hill Records founder Will Ackerman. Includes a personal, hand-signed note from Will. VERY limited quantity. It's very much an "insider" Reward emblematic of Windham Hills Records' history. (Note: The record/clock pictured is a 1 of a kind item, so the first to select this reward level will receive that in their Grab Bag.) HANDMADE COLLECTIBLE LIGHT BOX - $700 In addition to many of the lower Rewards, you'll receive a very special limited-edition handmade collectible, lightbox, inspired by Windham Hill Records, and produced by ElliotFanDesign. Each lightbox is carefully crafted in Kensington, California; it is designed to be enjoyed in both the illuminated "lightbox" state as well as in the more traditional "shadowbox" state, with the LEDs turned off (see below). Limited quantity available. Accompanied by a Certificate of Authenticity. Hand-numbered. Earliest orders get lowest numbers. ONE-OF-A-KIND RECORDING BY PHIL AABERG - $1,000 Talk about unique and one of a kind! Phil Aaberg will make a custom LIVE recording in his new recording studio The Mill of any piece you request composed or recorded by him. YOU will have the only copy of this personalized version of that recording. With personal letter, signed dedication, certificate of authenticity, and Zoom call. Says Phil: "Each time I play one of my compositions, it's different. Some of the pieces I've recorded have never been played live, so a recording of that would be especially rare. This would be the first thing I record in my new studio which will be ready soon, and whoever chooses this would have their choice of which piano I'd use: the 7' Yamaha S6, the 9'4" Mason Hamlin, or the "Concert Upright" Meister which I've played since I was 12. I’m happy to speak about the piece you’ve chosen." WILL ACKERMAN VERMONT TOUR AND DINNER FOR 2 - $9,000 --- SOLD OUT! In addition to many of the lower Rewards, you'll receive a very special trip you'll NEVER forget - you and a guest will visit Will Ackerman’s Imaginary Road recording studio in Vermont. Includes a detailed tour by Will, lots of great music, shooting the breeze about awesome music history, and you'll see special artifacts, hear behind-the-scenes stories, and partake in great conversation. Also includes dinner with Will. (Travel not included.) And while you're in Vermont, there are, of course, many other sites to see. BUDGET, TIMELINE, & USE OF PROCEEDS WHY KICKSTARTER We came to Kickstarter so that we could tell the story of Windham Hill Records truthfully and independently - with all its inspirational music and timeless history. Windham Hill itself was independent, and it's no surprise that they've been supportive of their story being told independently. We also came to Kickstarter to interact with a community that would appreciate it. What better place for us than Kickstarter, where an independent film that speaks of inspiration, accomplishment, and history can seek a like-minded community for appreciation -- and support. QUESTIONS If you've got questions, we're pleased to respond. At the top of the page on the right there is a small round image of Tal - click that, then click CONTACT ME to send us a message. FISCAL SPONSOR Filmmakers Collaborative SF is a nonprofit media organization with a mission to produce and support films that catalyze positive change for public benefit. By virtue of a fiscal sponsorship through Filmmakers Collaborative (SF), pledges made to this campaign are tax-deductible , to the amount and extent allowable by law.

title: Categories
description: WE DID IT!  Here are our stretch goals!  Continue reading below for more. EASY PIECES THE COMIC I've been writing and drawing my whole life.  Something happened in late 2010 while reading the wonderful comic Love & Rockets.  I began kicking myself for never before playing with the amazing medium that is comics, and I kept kicking myself until I developed my own comic Easy Pieces, which can be seen here: easypiecescomic.com The Direct Result of Being Off-Balance, Every Piece Aware EASY PIECES THE ANIMATION It's been a great ride and I've seen a lot of success with the comic, one of the most notable being my acceptance into Act-I-Vate Comix , the best webcomics hub out there.   There's still a long road ahead of me, as the comic itself is still in act 1.  Last year while working on the comic, I was Director of Animation for a short film called Ouverture .  The short has been well received, premiering at The NY Museum of Modern Art's Film Festival and moving across the country in the some of the best film festivals out there. I want the same for Easy Pieces.  I want to create an animated short that will move people, will get them interested in the comic, and hopefully explode a few brains.  Fans of the comic will see some familiar faces and concepts in the animation (as well as things they've never seen before), and future fans will have a unique opportunity to enter the world of Easy Pieces! There is a magic to animation that this work begs to embrace. The Comics Beat reviews Easy Pieces "It’s like reading Walt Whitman in haiku form presented with powerhouse design and detail." -The Comics Beat CONCEPTS Easy Pieces is about duality.  It explores why humans can love and hate something simultaneously, and how our gut and brain can dispense radically different advice.  Dealing with that human condition is the main character of the comic, Neil.  He eventually meets some folks from the mysterious Jupiter Organization, and perhaps his problems have all been solved.  Or, perhaps not. ABOUT ME: I have my MFA in Drawing from SUNY Purchase and am very lucky to be working in the design, art, film and animation fields. You can see my work here: www.neildvorak.com 1/4 of "Ocre Piece" BUDGETING...HEY WAIT, DIDN'T I SEE THIS PROJECT LAST WEEK ASKING FOR $18,000!?  HOW COME IT'S ONLY $11,000 NOW.  THAT'S 61% LESS MONEY...DOES THAT MEAN THIS ANIMATION IS GOING TO BE 61% AS GOOD AS TH- Okay, okay, yes.  Yes.  I have relaunched the project for less money.  In this section I will show you exactly how you get the same quality of animation for less.  (The very short answer: it will take slightly more time.)  The longer answer: The original budget was as follows: In short, I am removing the animation budget altogether.  How is an animation produced with no animation budget, you ask?  The $7,000 that would have gone to pay an animator to work full time 60+ hours a week for 2 to 3 months to get the lionshare of the drawings done is now gone.  I will now draw every single frame myself, in addition to overseeing and keyframing.  I will draw 20 frames everyday, 6 days a week for one year.  This is only 9 months slower than the original timeline, and for a 5 to 6 minute animation with nearly 6 to 8 thousand drawings it's a pretty fast turnaround.  I hope you agree! New Budget: -NOTE: Any amount over 11,000 collected will go to enhance the post-production facilities.  This means more special effects, better sound and compositing, and more live musicians on the score!-

title: Categories
description: “We’re not friends. We’re friendly" We are seeking to raise $7,200 to film the pilot episode of "Old Heads," a bold, character-driven comedy inspired by shows like "Louie," "The Larry Sanders Show," and "Curb Your Enthusiasm." This show takes place in the gritty and vibrant world of New York City's stand-up comedy scene. Created by comedians Guy Knoll and Oscar Salem, who also star as heightened versions of themselves, "Old Heads" draws on their extensive experience in the comedy circuit to depict the chaotic, neurotic, and hilariously authentic life of a downtown NYC comic. Filmed in the heart of the East Village, the series features a dynamic cast of actual comedians, capturing the unfiltered energy that characterizes the scene. This is not your typical polished comedy—expect sweaty greenrooms, envy, and inflated egos. It's raw, real, and extraordinarily funny. We’re assembling a veteran comedy crew , led by award-winning director Alex Helmer , fresh off producing the upcoming feature A Christmas Party (co-written by Guy). We’ve got our A-team back, including: ● Spencer Bruttig (DP) ● Casey Schwartz (Production Design) ● Travis Persick (Gaffer) ● Brendan Kimmet (1st AD) ● Isabella Bahl (Script Supervisor) ● Valentina Quintero (2nd AD) ...and more pros who believe in this show’s potential. We plan to shoot in May 2025 , but we need your help to get this off the ground. Your donation helps pay for: ● Equipment rentals ● Crew wages ● Insurance, permit, post-production ●And maybe enough bagels to keep the comedians from revolting

title: Categories
description: Director's Statement My retired friends ask me why, after having a long and successful legal career,  I am spending my time on this "mishegoss.*" They tell me to take it easy, take my family on a cruise or  to Disneyland.  What I tell them is: I love movies and I now have time to write and direct a movie that I would like to see--and make it with a talented group of filmmakers.  What could be better? (Although arguing a case before the U.S. Supreme Court was pretty cool.) Now, please keep reading to discover more about Deadly Resurrection and why you should support the making of this film. * mishegoss is yiddish for "a foolish fixation' or "a whacky, irrational, absurd belief." What's It All About? Deadly Resurrection is a modern love story gone wrong. It is about a young man, Joel, who is haunted by the spirit of his dead wife, and Becky, a girl he found on Tinder.  Lonely, yet still grieving, Joel invites Becky over to his house for "Netflix and Chill." But he does not want to watch a movie--or even to have sex.  What he wants is to transfer the spirit of his dead wife into the body of the beguiling  Becky.  Despite Becky's fondness for Joel, he pursues his plan, only to realize that maybe he should have left well enough alone.  Is it too late to undo what he's done?  And if he can undo what he has wrought, will there be repercussions? What Inspired You? The film was inspired by a joke posted on Facebook by the director's son: "If you are feeling lonely, watch a scary movie at home." So we set out to make a horror film about watching a scary movie at home--a film that will cause you to dig your fingernails into your friend's arm--and, if you watch it alone,  make you worry about some  "stranger" stalking you in your own home. image by Freepik Who Influenced You? Our aim is to capture the gripping suspense reminiscent of Alfred Hitchcock's Frenzy , the chilling horror evoked by Ari Aster's Hereditary , the thought-provoking themes found in Jordan Peele's Get Out , and the captivating storytelling of Stephen King's Pet Sematary . While we humbly acknowledge that we are not yet on par with the likes of Hitchcock, Aster, Peele, or King, we firmly believe that this movie will be truly exceptional. We are proud to announce that the script has earned third place in the esteemed International Horror and Sci-Fi Film Festival. However, we invite you to be the ultimate judge by supporting us in financing this incredible project. The opening scene from Hitchcock's Frenzy. Why Do You Need Help to Make It? We are self-funding at least half the cost of making this movie, but we want and need the help of a larger community of film lovers to make Deadly Resurrection the best it can possibly be. To accomplish this goal we will need funds to: Acquire top-notch equipment, including camera lenses and lighting, to enhance the visual quality of the film. Facilitate comprehensive post-production processes such as editing, color grading, sound mixing, and original music composition, to deliver a polished final product. Effectively market Deadly Resurrection and submit it to prestigious film festivals across the globe, expanding its reach and visibility. Compensate highly skilled professionals at competitive rates, ensuring that we assemble the finest team to bring this project to life. By joining our endeavor and contributing to the funding, you become an integral part of creating a cinematic masterpiece that will captivate audiences worldwide. Image by macrovector on Freepik The Writer and Director Stan Eisenhammer is a writer and Sundance-trained director. His scripts have been finalists in short screenplay competitions at the Vail, Beverley Hills, Omaha, Shriekfest, and Portland Comedy Film Festivals among others. His script for Deadly Resurrection earned a third-place award at the International Horror and Sci-Fi Film Festival.  His short film Pornography has been shown at film festivals from Portland, Oregon, to Kurdistan in Northern Iraq. The Cinematographer Stan Miskiewicz has been a visual artist for over 35 years. His career has taken him from Chicago to San Francisco, Berlin and London. He has  worked on such major motion pictures as Lost in Space and Final Fantasy the Spirit Within, . Stan's  vast experience makes him uniquely qualified to collaborate with producers and directors to bring their visions to life by capturing  the best visuals for the project. The Actors Joel Eisenhammer is an accomplished actor and comedian whose roles have ranged from a transgender middle schooler to a grieving widower. He has been seen In New York City in the off-Broadway play And/Or and as far away as Northern Iraq in the short film Pornography at the Kurdistan International Film Festival. Magdalena Conway is an award winning actress of both stage and screen. She has been seen in Contessa, Black Out, It Came from Somewhere, and Book of Face s. She has won  the "Best Young Actress" award at X World Festival in Rome, italy and was nominated  for "Outstanding Supporting Role" at MLC Awards.

title: Categories
description: Hey Everyone!! We're thrilled to be sharing this project with you and hope that with your help we can bring it to reality! Let It Go is a 2D Animated/Live-Action Music Video based on the song of the same title performed by the talented Chapel Hill based rapper Speedy Sprag . (Listen To The Full Song & Virginia Is For Lovers Album on Spotify , Apple Music , and YouTube !) This music video tells the story of a heartbroken college student desperately clinging to the remaining threads of a toxic relationship with his girlfriend. "We really wanted to bring a strong story to this music video. Something that pushed deeper into the beautiful lyrics Sprag had already performed. By creating this as a hybrid of mediums we feel the animation will heighten the character's emotional conflict. " - Jeremiah Something special happens when two people come together who are both passionate about their craft. We believe the combination of Sprag's musical ear and Jeremiah's artistic eye are doing exactly that. "I love music. Music brings words to life . But animation just does something that music can't do. With beats, melodies, and poetry I can paint a picture with words. But animation takes that and brings life to it. " - Sprag The Animation The main reason for this Kickstarter. Our vision for this Music Video is to produce high-quality 2D Hand Drawn Animation in a visual style that is unique to this film. Since the pandemic, 2D Animation has begun to make a resurgence and we want to be a part of pushing this amazing medium back into the forefront of the animation industry. Crafting every single frame by hand is a trait distinctive to this medium and we believe this hand-crafted feel will transport the viewer into a new world . One only animation can offer. Above is our proof of concept animation. This is meant to demonstrate the level of quality we're trying to reach with this music video. Clean lineart, fully colored animation, digitally painted backgrounds, and selective lighting. "Stylistically speaking, I can't hide the fact that my influence growing up was superhero comic books . With this film, we're looking to mesh the graphic lineart style of comics with the simpler and cleaner aesthetic of classic animated films. Think Prince of Egypt meets Invincible ." - Jeremiah The Process It all starts with a good idea . Or in this case a song. Speedy Sprag doing what he does best. "What's special about this song was my vulnerability. I was going through a very difficult situation and all that emotion came out. It was also special because it was one of the first pop songs I've made. Generally, pop songs are just meant to be catchy. But with this, I wanted the song to also have meaning when you looked closer at the lyrics ." - Sprag "I remember listening to the entire [Virginia Is For Lovers] album for the first time. And one song stuck out to me . As I sat there listening to the hypnotic beat and lyrics of "Let It Go" bumping through my headphones, images started to pop into my head. I quickly jotted the ideas down and made some sketches of Sprag climbing a mountain. From there the rest of the story just started to flow." - Jeremiah An early thumbnail sketch. Originally, our idea for this story was to make it completely animated. But since this is our first experience with crowdfunding we recognize that route would be much more expensive to fund. Instead, we chose to make the film a hybrid of animation and live-action. Not only is this cheaper to make but we found that this method would make for a much stronger story. So, what does the process of making this music video look like? First, it starts with an animatic . An animatic is a preliminary rough-cut version of the movie that helps us get a sense of the musical timing and compositional layouts of both the animated & live-action sequences we'll be creating. Which are crucial to any kind of film, especially an animated music video . Fortunately, Jeremiah has already put together an animatic and figured out which scenes will be animated and which will need to be filmed in live-action. Let It Go will require roughly 2 minutes and 15 seconds of animation and 1 minute and 15 seconds of live-action footage! The next steps of the process are what this Kickstarter will help us with. The sequences that need to be animated will be sent to production ( meaning Jeremiah will be at his drafting table passionately slaving over hundreds of drawings ). During production, each shot will go through the same perfected process. First, they will be roughly animated, then inbetweened, cleaned up, and finally colored. With animation, you're only limited to what you can imagine. That's what makes this art form so beautiful. For the live-action sequences the process is a bit simpler . We anticipate it will only take one full day's work to set up and shoot all the footage we need. With another bonus being that it will be filmed on UNC Campus! When all this work is done, the post-production process is all that's left. Compositing, visual effects,  color correction, and sound/video editing are the last final touches that will bring it all together. Sounds simple right? We think so! The Budget If we could do this project for free then it would already be done. That's how passionate we are about this. But unfortunately, if you didn't know, Animation is timely and expensive . Very expensive . That's why 60% of this campaign is going directly to the animation production and 10% towards the live-action sequences of the music video. And of course, we would be remiss if we didn't account for the Kickstarter Fees , Taxes , and Miscellaneous costs that quickly add up. Lastly, the remainder of our budget is for you guys! We want to bring you all the best rewards we can offer. The cost of producing and shipping these rewards have all been carefully calculated and are presented in this pretty pie chart below ↓ The Rewards The Crew Tar Heels stand up! We pride ourselves on being a team of artists and filmmakers comprised of UNC alumni, students, and locals! Go Heels!!! Originally from Chesterfield Virginia, Michael Spragley , better known as "Speedy Sprag" is a Division One Track & Field Athlete at the University of North Carolina Chapel Hill. Sprag is currently pursuing his Bachelor's Degree and will be Graduating in May with a Degree in Business and Media. Not only does he run track , he also makes tracks! While he's not running miles or competing, he spends his time producing and recording songs in his bedroom . He also creates his own music videos and has amassed over One Million Plays on Spotify & Apple Music combined . He has a bright future ahead of him on and off the track! "I hope when people see this Kickstarter and the Music Video they see that it is possible to achieve their dreams. Whatever it is, you can do it. We're not super special people, we are just two dudes who have an idea going out and actually doing something about it. So I hope that inspires more creators like us." Director , Animator , Story Artist , and Filmmaker , Jeremiah Derby is the epitome of a passionate indie creator. Since the day he was able to pick up a pencil, he discovered one of the greatest loves of his life, drawing . Through countless hours of reading comics, watching animated films, scouring artbooks, studying art from master storytellers, and applying techniques in his sketchbooks, Jeremiah learned what it took to create beautiful animation and craft soul-touching stories. "In a way, this project is full circle for me which is exciting. Music was something I wanted to pursue when I was younger but animation had my heart . I even started a Christian Rap Group during my middle school days ( my first video on YouTube was actually me rapping with this group of friends ). With this story, I hope the work Sprag and I have done on the music video will do more than entertain. I want it to leave a lasting impression on the viewer." In 2022, Jeremiah graduated from the University of Chapel Hill with his Bachelor's Degree in Studio Art . During his Senior year as a student, he proudly led the Carolina Animator's Anonymous Club as Club President . And oh yes, Jeremiah managed all of this while being a student-athlete competing Division One for both the Tar Heel's Wrestling and Boxing Teams. Go Heels! Stretch Goals Stretch Goals! The next best thing after hitting our funding goal is creating more rewards for you guys! Goal #2= $13,500 - Every Backer receives Virginia Is For Lovers Stickers Goal #3= $15,000 - Every Backer receives a Special Edition Art Print Goal #4 = $20,000 - A Crazy Special Surprise! Will be revealed after Goal #3 is unlocked!

title: Ariadne's Book of Legends - D&D beyond 20th level!
description: What's Inside You reached 20th level. What now? Your character has reached 20th level. What now ? In a normal game, you would retire the hero to a life of glory, for D&D lacks engaging high-level content. You might never even have reached these heights, fearing the balancing nightmare that are the final levels of our dearest RPG. There are no bosses that can stand against a party this powerful, nor any further milestones to reach... Well, we come to fix just that! Reach 25th level with any class, fight storied monsters that go beyond CR 30, and personalize your legend with heroic paths, 10th-level spells, and mythical martial techniques. Ariadne's Book of Legends brings you everything you need to level up your table to its final form! Rival the Very Gods 20th level is the absolute limit for 99% of heroes, but that hardly describes you . Through hardship and talent, you’ve risen to legendary status . How, you ask? 10th-Level Spells Do you favor casters ? We have created a plethora of 10th-Level Spells , arcanisms capable of reshaping the world at your command, available to both players… and Legendary Foes. These potent feats of magic can be combat-oriented, such as the mighty Mass Foresight , a 10th-level spell of the Divination school, which gives the entire party a glimpse into the future. A Legendary Warlock casting Cloak of Barbatos 10th-level spells also unlock great story potential. Call of Sheol , our 10th-level Necromancy spell, is one such example. With a proper offering and a powerful summoning ritual, an entire army of undead rises from the shadowy plane of death. Ghosts, zombies, and revenants, led by death knights and vampires, all at your beck and call. Conjure black holes from the depths of the howling void, crack the crust of the earth with surging volcanoes, or petrify entire armies with a pulsating curse. Reality is what you decree . Mythical Techniques For those who prefer martials, we’ve designed a variety of Mythical Techniques , storied skills for the mightiest warriors. By channeling your soul into your strikes, you’ll be able to keep your high-level martial character on even footing with casters, even at this tier of play. A Legendary Barbarian using Durandal's Unbreakable Stance Perform Barbaric Leaps , jumping from one end of the battlefield to the other and pulverizing those unlucky enough to occupy your landing spot. Imbue your blade with Divine Severance and slash through the very fabric of reality, separating meaning from words, breaking bonds, demonic and divine, and even shattering the connection of a God with their followers. Harness the spite of a thousand betrayals and carve a Soul Wound unto a Lich, making it incapable of returning to its phylactery. Deflect an ancient dragon’s breath weapon, throw your hammer beyond the horizon, and topple towers with a singular punch. The battlefield shakes beneath your feet . Heroic Paths Something for everyone, no matter your preferred class: Heroic Paths , powerful and customizable perks that build upon each other like a skill tree, for your beloved character to reach their maximum potential. A Legendary Paladin that has chosen the Path of the Herald Perhaps your paladin has gone the route of the Combat Master , able to stop most blows with her blade and mastering the art of riposte. Perhaps she has gone the way of the Fated , however, chosen by a higher power—or some unknown force—to prevail, destiny favoring her every act. Or, she might otherwise have taken the path of the Regal , her very presence inspiring worship, an aura of greatness aiding those who fight beside her. Pick and choose from a wide variety of legendary perks, and build up your arsenal through a skill-tree progression path. No two legends are the same ! New Half-Caster: The Shaper “The elemental forces of the universe are chaotic in nature. To think they can be controlled is madness. Shapers are those who understand this fundamental truth and call upon the elements to aid them regardless, knowing they might not answer. A Shaper expects nothing, but acts anyway.” Shapers are half-casters powered by the elements : they’ve no use for Gods or wizardly tomes. Their magic is borne of willpower , a stubbornness rigid enough to impose itself over the laws of nature. They are a Wisdom-based class , with the option to choose between Strength and Dexterity for their secondary statistic. The chaotic elements do not respond to reverence, but to tenacity—your elemental power stems from the forces of mind, body, and soul. A Shaper of the Flamecallers Shapers can take several elemental paths : Flamecallers use controlled yet chaotic flames to burn their enemies to ash; Windchasers float above the battlefield with whimsy and fury in equal measure. Waterdancers move through enemies with the grace of a river and the intensity of an ocean storm, while Earthbreakers are unmoving, brash juggernauts, stone clinging to their forms in unbreakable barriers. Finally, some relinquish the elements altogether, instead focusing their power on the laws of reality; the Wordbearers , their commands greater than those of any other Shaper. New Astral Martial: The Starblade “When doom fell upon the higher planes, no radiant archangels or graceful deities were left to pick up the pieces. That thankless task befell the Starblades, heirs of the broken army that faced the voidborn. Like any survivors, they made use of all at their disposal. In their case, it was the pulsing energy of nebulae, the force of gravity, and the violence of comets.” Starblades are non-spellcasting , yet profoundly magical martials . Capable of harnessing the primordial forces that inhabit the astral sea, they strike with ignited blades, push enemies and bolster the mobility of allies with gravity manipulation, and even act as living siege engines by summoning and launching miniature celestial bodies. They are Dexterity or Strength-based , with their more magical features depending on their secondary statistic: Intelligence. All in all, they are a martial that does a lot more than strike repeatedly! This Starblade follows the Path of the Comet Starblades have three subclasses available, called Astral Paths , each focusing on a specific type of cosmic power. The Path of the Nebula is a damage dealer by design, specializing in harnessing the raw elemental blitz of celestial bodies. The Path of the Singularity , on the other hand, focuses on battlefield control and utility, manipulating gravity to hinder opponents and aid themselves and their allies. Finally, the Path of the Comet focuses on the violence and speed of travelling celestial bodies, and is intended for a devastating ranged playstyle. Face Impossible Foes For the Dungeon Masters , receive a collection of legendary foes to pit against your overpowered players! They come with a comprehensive guide on how to use them to maximum effect, including tips on managing stages , building tension , setting up engaging victory conditions , and much more. Many will include unique , interactive , and thoughtfully designed lairs , every bit as epic as their owners! Avoid the fun-killing slog and become the conductor of the great opera that is D&D beyond 20th level! The Grand Alchemist will not go down easily - Attack on Castle Blackfume The Hermit, shard of an Elder Deity, awaits worthy heroes upon his lair–the Scar of Divinity, where the Dead Gods fought the last battle against the void. This elusive creature guards not only the rift but also heralds a grand test; should you succeed, his divine powers may become yours, so long as you defend Existence from the rampage of the Enemy. Many heroes of the past have given their lives to defend reality, yet none have sacrificed more than the void huntress herself, Scáthach–she who wields a God. Her weapon has seen the blood of a thousand agents of Eternity, the Enemy, yet at some point during her service, the cursed weapon she carries has finally taken over her mind. Today, it is not Scáthach who fights, but her scythe: Abaddon, the God of Doom. Can you foil this ancient foe, and perhaps rescue the heroine in his thrall? There are villains so terrible among the Cosmos, their names remain etched beside those of the voidborn.  The Grand Alchemist is one of these beings. An amoral scientist, an ambitious fiend, a horrid abomination. Upon his walking laboratory-fortress, Paracelsion traverses the fields of Hell with impunity, his long list of atrocities a roadmap of progress to him, as he inches ever closer to his true goal: alchemical ascension. With his network of corruption stretching ever further, no great power will stop him. Will you? A New System That Delivers Discover the Legendary Point System , an overhaul of high-level D&D that promotes speedier turns, key tactical decisions, and cinematic moments. The Legendary Point System is designed to streamline your game experience by offering: Smoother turns for both players and monsters An adjustable challenge level Easy-to-learn mechanics with plenty of tactical depth Be careful! Star incoming! - The Trial of the Hermit Both Characters and Foes will have access to a new and scarce resource: Legendary Points , which will be used to perform incredible feats of strength, resist incoming attacks, and take action even outside one’s turn. Our design objective for this system is to enhance high-level D&D combat , iron out its quirks, and create a framework for better, more balanced legendary encounters, without the slog commonly associated with this tier of play. The Ending Your Campaign Deserves Give your campaign a grand finale worthy of the stories you've been weaving for years! Though each Legendary Foe in this book has their own lore and mechanics, we’ve included ways to adapt these incredible challenges to your own story , putting the face of your BBEG over the archetypes these CR 30+ monsters represent. The mechanics, stories, and notes within Ariadne’s Book of Legends are all written and designed with a single purpose: creating legendary showdowns. This supplement will give you the ability to orchestrate a conclusion to be remembered by you and your players for years to come! Free Previews Begin your legendary journey early! Enjoy these pre-release playtest options, including all the key design elements we want to incorporate into the final product. Don’t forget to leave some feedback . Every comment helps us improve Ariadne’s Book of Legends! Through The Maze by Karina Castillo and Mateo Ledgard! The incredible theme song of Ariadne's Book of Legends. Tiers Take a look at all the different ways you can support the project, and all the goodies you can fetch for your party by doing so! When you find something you like, pick it from the sidebar to pledge at that tier. The Team Ariadne’s Codex of Strings was started by two friends, writers, and forever DMs in the middle of an early-20s crisis. What should we do with our lives ? title: Categories
description: The Game In this GMless and solo-able game , you play as the residents of one of the home-ships that house the remnants of humanity. You are its Lamplighters : the unions of pilot and mech that each home-ship maintains to protect their interests. Build your mech with parts gained both from your own home-ship’s research and from other factions. 8 Frames and more than 100 parts give you a lot to work with! Engage in deep, tactical combat against other Lamplighters as you seek to carry out whatever mission your Home-Ship needs to survive. Experience life between missions through downtime events. Build up your home-ship and lead it to greatness. Deal or fight with 24 factions , including: INFINITY : A wealthy, absolutely ruthless home-ship that has been accumulating secondary ships and colonies since the second generation. Neon Witch Network : A tiny coven of operatives procuring worrying amounts of Titan materiel for inscrutable purposes under the noses of other Factions. The Virtuous Collective : A set of 4 home-ships who have split into a series of quarreling houses with a noble veneer over a vicious mecha-dueling culture. Reverie : Uncannily-happy, psychically-linked missionaries who rent themselves out as mercenaries in exchange for proselytization rights. Uncover the Titans: The dead gods at the edge of the stars, now ripe for harvest and plunder for humanity’s war against itself. What dark mecha and psychic secrets might they yet reveal? How to Play You'll construct your mech by placing parts on a grid. When combat arrives, you'll find yourself using that same Grid as not only a record of what's contained on your mech, but a set of hit locations! (You can read more about that here .) When it's time to embark upon missions, you'll determine what happens using a hexflower-based mission generator . (You can read more about that here .) Between missions, in addition to dealing with various events and finding out how some factions liked how you dealt with them, you’ll construct your home-ship by adding structures to it. These can give your pilots more equipment or enable more  (You can read more about that here .) And who knows? You might even find yourselves in the possession of one of the mighty Titan Frames : unique mechs that play by their own rules. Reward Tiers You step into the command center of the SELKIE, the central homeship of the BINARY STAR faction. Strange lights, buttons, and switches litter the various control panels. It looks much cleaner here than most of the other homeships you’ve been on. Your focus is on the Commander, the de facto leader of BINARY STAR’s fleet of Sparks. Almost as soon as you walk in, he begins ushering you through further hallways and down elevators without a word. Months of training to be a pilot — a Lamplighter — and you’re going right back into the mech hangar. He stops, and before you and the other newly minted pilots are shiny and only slightly busted Frames. “Welcome to your new home, Lamplighters.” He addresses you all, and gestures back to the frames. “I know you’ve been training for this, and now it’s time for your first mission. We’ve got a settlement on the moon Litany, and the kick starter of their main generator has been sabotaged by INFINITY. We need you to scare them off and fix it. Are you capable of this?” Murmurs spread among the other pilots. Weren’t you supposed to be allied with INFINITY? He doesn’t address this. MECH HANGAR (just the zines) “Pick your Frame for the mission. This will be your second skin while you’re out there, so make sure you’re comfortable in it.” At this tier, you'll get all 4 supplement zines (detailed below). If you're happy with your existing digital copy of Celestial Bodies and just want the new stuff, this one's for you! LIGHT (digital) “Want to stay fast and evasive, do you? LN-VIGIL provides solid mid-range battlefield support, while LF-THORN's the fastest mech in the hangar. Just watch out for flak.” At this tier, you’ll receive a digital (PDF) copy of Celestial Bodies. (Estimated final pagecount: 350-400 pages.) You can also get the LIGHT+ tier, which will include digital copies of all of the supplement zines described below! MEDIUM (softcover) “Staying balanced? Good thinking. MF-BANDIT can threaten enemies at any range, while MN-FORTUNE is a crack sniper.” At this tier, you’ll receive the 6" x 9" softcover of Celestial Bodies, as well as a digital (PDF) copy. (Estimated final pagecount: 350-400 pages.) HEAVY (hardcover) “Need more firepower? Probably wise. HF-CARNAGE is a terrifying mid-to-close-range brawler, while HN-GRIDLOCK is a long range terror of electronic warfare.” At this tier, you’ll receive the 6" x 9" hardcover of Celestial Bodies, as well as a digital (PDF) copy. (Estimated final pagecount: 350-400 pages.) ULTRA (the collection) “None of these are big enough for you? Say no more. UF-CASTLE is a full-auto specialist gun platform, while UN-BLITZ is a dedicated missile platform.” At this tier, you’ll receive both the hardcover of Celestial Bodies from HEAVY and physical copies of the four supplement zines (described below), as well as digital (PDF) copies of everything. (You can also get these supplement zines as an add-on! Select it after you select your reward.) Supplement Zines In addition to the book described above in digital, softcover, and hardcover versions, we have a series of four supplement zines available, covering extra content that will not be in the primary book : Allegiance: A zine written by Binary and Charlotte that details ways to play as the various factions in the book, including playing without a home-ship or with a fleet of home-ships. Cosmic Powerstomp: A zine written by Kyle Tam that details the calamity as the result of a miraculous resource that ruined Earth and outlines the kinds of realities that result from that. Keep the Lamp Lit: A zine written by Binary and Charlotte that details life on home-ships, including procedures to determine the things Lamplighters get up to in their free time. Synodic//Sidereal: A zine written by Hipólita that details the calamity as an ongoing struggle between imperial Earth and an ever-expanding network of colonies and describes the kinds of realities that result from that. These are included in the LIGHT+ digital-only tier , while physical and digital copies are included in the ULTRA tier. You can also add digital or physical copies of these zines onto everything as an add-on! (Make sure to select it after choosing a reward.) Stretch Goals $40,000: A set of sticker sheets featuring the eight base frames will also be sent to anyone backing at the MEDIUM, HEAVY, and ULTRA tiers. $50,000: All of our collaborators get paid more. $60,000: A fifth zine written by Snow ! ???: To be revealed when we hit $50,000. Fulfillment Digital fulfillment will be handled by BackerKit. Physical fulfillment will be handled by Space Penguin Ink, LLC, shipping all orders from the USA. Shipping fees will be charged after the campaign closes via BackerKit. All efforts will be made to keep EU / UK / ROW shipping costs as low as possible, but we can't guarantee there will be no import fees. Import fees are the responsibility of the backer. We will offer tracked shipping for as many regions as possible. We are using weight-based shipping, and you will be charged shipping via BackerKit for the total weight of your pledge and any add-ons. United States: $11 – 15 Canada: $16 – 26 United Kingdom: $16 – 26 EU: $16 – 26 US Territories and ROW: $23 – 38 Unfortunately we cannot ship to the following countries at the current time: Afghanistan, Belarus, Brunei Darussalam, Lao People's Democratic Republic, Myanmar, Niue, Russian Federation, Syrian Arab Republic, Yemen. The Team Binary Star and Charlotte Laskowski - Design, writing, layout, and illustration Binary and Charlotte are the core team behind the Terrestrial and Titan Editions of Celestial Bodies. You might also know Binary from games like APOCALYPSE FRAME and Valiant Horizon , while you might also know Charlotte from games like Daisy Chainsaw and Rosethorn Keep . You can find Binary and Charlotte here and here . Roz Leahy - Editing Roz is a game designer, editor and massive transsexual living in Edinburgh. When she's not working on TTRPGs ( Mothership , Warhammer 40,000: Wrath and Glory ) she teaches creative game design workshops to children! You can find her here . Kyle Tam - Supplement Writing (Cosmic Powerstomp) Kyle is a writer, game designer, and full-time complainer from the Philippines. Her games include the IGDN Honorable Mention MORIAH , Primadonna from PlusOneEXP, and Forsaken from Afterthought Committee. She has also written for games like Pathfinder , Tangled Blessings , Killing Time , and now Celestial Bodies! You can find her here . Hipólita - Supplement Writing (Synodic//Sidereal) Hipólita is a trans TTRPG designer, writer, editor, translator and consultant from Argentina. Her work focuses on the displaced, dispossessed and marginalized, characters inhabiting historical contradictions and the fraught legacies of colonialism and revolution. You can find her here .

description: Thanks to all Kickstarter backers who funded the Smash Stick! If you discover the Smash Stick after the project ends, you can sign up to be notified when the Smash Stick is available for purchase on the Alt Lab Controllers website. FEATURED ON: AN ARCADE STICK WITH ANALOG CONTROLS The Smash Stick allows you do subtle inputs that aren't possible on a standard digital joystick. It's ergonomically designed to reduce strain on your hands and wrists. Plus, it's compatible with the following consoles: Nintendo 64, GameCube, Wii, Wii-U, Switch, PC, PlayStation 3, PlayStation 4* *Requires Brook Super Converter. Sold Separately . UNIQUE FEATURES: Our analog joystick can read subtle inputs giving you analog control. Similar to how an analog stick on a controller works, you can gradually control the amount of input. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Use it to enjoy thousands of games that previously required a controller to play. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 The analog joystick is great for playing the Super Smash Bros series! You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Additional Info: 100% analog - The analog joystick can perform all angles/values just like a controller's analog stick. No modifiers required. Octagon Gate - Easily angle your attacks with the joystick's octagon-shaped gate ("notches"). Aligned for "Shield Drops" - The bottom-corner "notches" of the joystick are aligned to perform "shield drops" in Super Smash Bros. Melee. No modding required. Hall Effect Technology - The joystick uses non-contacting analog sensors so your joystick's performance does not degrade over time. The Smash Stick is ergonomically designed to reduce strain on your hands and offer a comfortable gaming experience. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Home-row layout - The Smash Stick uses a home-row layout, so you don't have to shift your hand while playing. Shaped for your hands - The Smash Stick's button layout is curved to keep your wrists straight and minimize your finger movement during gameplay. Button cushions - The Smash Stick uses modified Sanwa Denshi buttons that absorbs impact, speeds up your inputs, and are almost silent while playing. Raised button caps - Inspired by ergonomic keyboards for typists, certain buttons are raised higher than others to further reduce finger movement during gameplay. Using our Remap Software , you can completely customize your controls and save up to 9 unique profiles onto the Smash Stick. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 Configure your joystick, buttons, and triggers settings. Save Up to 9 unique profiles to your Smash Stick. Use the 3-way rocker switch to switch between your saved profiles on the fly. Able to import and export profiles to share with others. Able to define custom angles when mapping ADC buttons. Able to define custom values for the "soft" pull of the triggers. Use the Remap Software to keep your controller up-to-date with regular firmware updates from us. Software Overview: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gfWDphBxw_0 The Smash Stick works on the following consoles: Nintendo 64, GameCube, Wii, Wii-U, Switch, PC, and PlayStation 3. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 The Smash Stick comes with 3 Detachable Cables: Nintendo 64, GameCube, and USB. Simply plug the cable into your console and the Smash Stick automatically detects what console is plugged in. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 *Please note: the Smash Stick is a 3rd party controller designed by Alt Lab Controllers. We cannot guarantee full game support across all consoles. Not charged until August 29: Even if you pledge on day 1, you won't be charged until the END of our Kickstarter campaign on Saturday, August 29 . Receive exclusive Kickstarter perks: Being a Kickstarter backer means you'll receive all 3 console cables + stretch goal items bundled with your Smash Stick AND receive FREE shipping. Kickstarter backers come first: You'll be the first to own a Smash Stick before anyone else. We won't start any future production-runs until every Kickstarter backer receives their Smash Stick. We listen: We have always been about open communication and transparency. We are prompt answering any of your questions or concerns, and value backer feedback. Because we need you: We've been developing the Smash Stick for the past 6 years, but we can't get it out to your hands without your support. Backers who pledge $249 or higher will receive all Smash Stick - Retail Bundle stretch goals earned during the campaign! (additional components and other special rewards). Pledge $1 or more to send some love to the Alt Lab team! You'll also receive access to the "Pledge Manager" after the campaign -- just in case you want to order anything later! Pledge $249 or more to get: 1 Retail Smash Stick 1 Detachable Nintendo 64 Cable 1 Detachable GameCube Cable 1 Detachable USB Cable Smash Stick Stretch Goals (Earned During Campaign) Extra Smash Stick retail bundles are available for $249 each, just add the extra cost into your pledge. *Please note: the product image is a prototype. The final product will appear different due to us switching to an injection mold construction for mass production. Pledge $495 or more to get: 1 Smash Stick Retail Bundle (everything listed above) 1 Beta Smash Stick Receive a limited edition beta build Smash Stick 6 months before release. Beta Smash Stick ships November 2020. [Limited Quantity] 1 Beta Nintendo 64 Cable 1 Beta GameCube Cable 1 Beta USB Cable Smash Stick Stretch Goals (Earned During Campaign) *LIMITED QUANTITY - 150 If we reach the following funding goals, backers who pledged $249 or more will receive the following benefits for FREE! If the Smash Stick is fully funded within 48 hours of launch , Smash Stick backers will receive a free clear acrylic panel + Kickstarter exclusive artwork ( will be shown in a future KS update ). A clear acrylic panel allows you to install your own custom artwork onto your Smash Stick! Below are some examples of the creative artwork that you can create and install for your Smash Stick using a clear panel. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 *(Artwork shown was created by our Discord member, Genki-JAM . The e xample artwork was made for personal use and does not represent the official artwork that will be shipped with the Smash Stick.) If we we reach $80,000 in funding, ALL backers will have their Smash Sticks shipped for FREE! If we reach $85,000 in funding, all backers who pledged $249 or more will receive a swappable octagon gate and square gate for FREE. You'll need an HTML5 capable browser to see this content. Play Replay with sound Play with sound 00:00 00:00 We have partnered with ButterCade , an arcade parts maker from the fighting game community, to bring you an injection molded version of his BatBall Top ! The BatBall Top is a custom bat top that sits at the same height as a ball top! This allows the joystick to retain its small throw/travel distance while giving you more control! Simply add the price of each add-on to your pledge! Detachable Joystick Shaft: $40 Additional Retail Smash Stick Bundle: $249 Additional Octagon Gate: $4.99 Additional Square Gate: $4.99 Shipping is FREE for backers, due to us reaching our stretch goal! We are using international fulfillment centers to ensure your packages arrive safe and on time as every Smash Stick is professionally packed and shipped. Smash Sticks will be shipped from international fulfillment centers local to backers so that backers avoid VAT. How it Works You will receive an email from our pledge manager, BackerKit, after the campaign. Using the pledge manager, you will be able to confirm your rewards and address. We have already created functional Smash Stick prototypes, we just need your support so we can start production. Here is our timeline once we reach our funding goal: 2020 July - August: Our Kickstarter September: Kickstarter Funds Received / Parts Ordered / Tooling Process Starts (Joystick / Gates) November: Beta Smash Stick Production Run / Beta Smash Sticks ship 2021 January: Beta Feedback Implemented February: Parts Ordered / Retail Smash Stick Tooling Process Starts March: Retail Smash Stick Production Starts May: Retail Smash Sticks Ship We are a three man team composed of a mechanical engineer, software engineer, and exercise physiologist. We created the Smash Stick so people can continue playing the games they love with a high quality controller that’s both analog and ergonomic. We’ve been working on the Smash Stick for the past 6 years, doing all the R&D to make this happen. We'd like to thank YOU for being a part of this 6-year journey with us. We've been working on the Smash Stick throughout this time as a labor of love. This is our way of giving back to the community and we want to thank you for supporting us. Sincerely, Alt Lab Controllers If you'd like to receive updates before they make it to Kickstarter, please join our Discord using the link below.
