title: Categories
description:
The GameKid Is it ergonomic ? No. Maybe it’s just the nostalgia (okay, definitely) but there is something special about this simple shape that barely fits in your pocket. The GameKid is not built like a fragile modern handheld that tries to jam too many parts into the smallest space possible. It feels like you could run it over with a tank. It’s the same with the controls, nothing flimsy or floaty; our button action is straight out of the 80’s. Features Compatible with Raspberry Pi A+ , B+ and 2 Configured for RetroPie to emulate all your favorite consoles and Pi games. D-Pad, 4 Front Buttons, Start/Select/Option, and 2 Trigger buttons Built in Stereo Speakers and a Headphone Jack 2500mAh Battery (lasts ~5 hours with an A+ Pi), rechargeable by Micro USB port HDMI out! Connect to a TV and add USB controllers for a portable multiplayer game console! Functional Raspberry Pi mini computer! Just add a mouse and keyboard. Stretch Goals! We got funded! on to our first stretch goal! $35,000 - GameKid Raspberry Pi Store! Our biggest hope with the GameKid is to bring new cool games to the Raspberry Pi. We want to encourage that even more by giving Indie Devs a place to distribute Pi games easily.The store will launch right from the RetroPie EmulationStation menu and be browseable over the net with a wi-fi or lan connection. Rewards SoundTrack Legendary composer Alex Mauer is making a soundtrack for the GameKid. Why does a game console have it’s own soundtrack? Because it’s that AWESOME. Alex’s Credits include cool indie game soundtracks like Starr Mazer , and the movie Motivational Growth . He also wrote the first album to be released on a NES cart, VegaVox . The GameKid soundtrack will be a NES rom included with the RetroPie build, and also as downloadable mp3’s. Check out the first track below! GameKid Figure This adorable 1.5” figure is designed by CobraMode ! Cobra is the artist working with us on our upcoming game, Upslion Circuit . In fact, this GameKid is actually a character in the Upsilon Circuit universe! In fact, you can find GameKid creatures in Legend of Dungeon and Tiny Plumbers . This little guy is 3D printed in glow in the dark PLA, so if you paint him, his screen can really glow! (Also Includes the Soundtrack!) GameKid 3D Print Kit (GKP13-K) A limited edition 3D Printed GameKid Kit. For those of you who have a Raspberry Pi sitting around with nothing to do... this is it's new home! We'll have our custom RetroPie image available for download with instructions on how to get it all setup. Raspberry Pi installation is easy as pie, just plug it in and screw it back together, no soldering. (Includes: Soundtrack) GameKid 3D Print (GKP13) A limited edition 3D Printed GameKid. A Raspberry Pi 2, and a 16gig SD card preconfigured with RetroPie. (Includes: Soundtrack) Glow GameKid 3D Print (GKP13) A limited edition 3D Printed Glow in the Dark GameKid. (Includes: Soundtrack + Raspberry Pi 2 + 16gig SD card) Custom Print Material (GKP13) This limited edition printed GameKid can be printed out of nearly anything. Have a look at some of your options here . Pick up to 3 materials for the Front, Back, Backplate, Screenplate and Buttons!
If you have a question about whats possible with this tier, send us a message. (Includes: Soundtrack + Raspberry Pi 2 + 16gig SD card) Ben Weatherall Custom (GKP13) Ben Weatherall makes art for cool indie games! You might know Ben’s art from ‘ Crossy Road ’ or ‘ Deck War ’. Ben's going to design cool decals for these custom colored, 3D printed, one of a kind GameKids. (Includes: Soundtrack + Raspberry Pi 2 + 16gig SD card) Custom Design Print (GKP??)Lets make something amazing. Cat Ears? Robot Antenna? Functional GameKid that looks like the videogame character? Work with us to design a completely customized GameKid… Not sure if we can handle your idea? Send us a message and we’ll figure something out :D(Includes: Soundtrack + Raspberry Pi 2 + 16gig SD card) Steel GameKid (GK-ULTRA) We found out we could get our GameKid shell made in stainless steel... Honestly we don't think anyone will get this. BUT WOULDN'T IT BE COOL?!?! We like to call this the "Actually run it over with a Tank" version. (Includes: Soundtrack + Raspberry Pi 2 + 16gig SD card) Extras + $10 to any GameKid = Micro USB Wall Charger (we figure most people have boxes of these and don't need another one) + $15 it Any GameKid = Glow in the Dark GameKid Figure! We want more Games for the Raspberry Pi! Raspberry Pi’s are everywhere, and they are awesome… What they need though, is more indie games! We want to bring more games to the GameKid, and to the Raspberry Pi. To encourage more devs to try out the platform we’re hosting the GameKid Raspberry Jam on itch.io from 9/13 to 9/20 and you can be sure Robot Loves Kitty will be taking part and livestreaming the whole time! When we’re done there will be a new handful of games ready to be launched right from the RetroPie menu! About the Brothers When we were young, we bought our first game console together with our paper route jobs. It was the Nintendo Entertainment System Challenge Set, it came with Super Mario Bros. 3. It changed our lives forever. Calvin and his wife Alix founded Robot Loves Kitty , an indie videogame company. Last time they ran a Kickstarter, they were living in a treehouse . For Christmas last year, Calvin stuffed a Raspberry Pi into an old handheld console as a present to Scott. This led to a conversation about bringing the joy of handheld retro games to everyone. We were so compelled by the GameKid idea that Scott quit his job to come work for Robot Loves Kitty as lead hardware developer.

title: Categories
description: It’s 1998. Manhattan is underwater. The sky fell years ago. And the world ends on New Years. Happy celebration. Happy ‘99. We’re trying to fight it. The Sears corporation built Edge City around their secret headquarters in Nebraska. It collapsed a decade ago, revealing their haunting secret project beneath the surface, and ever since Edge City has grown down and out, collecting more and more souls as it goes. And we’ve been in the alleyway shadows trying to crack in, break in, and smash everything we can. Anything to learn the truth they hide. Anything to find the keys that’ll give us more time. But we need your help. We have the team, The Mouse, The Magical Girl, The Mom, each with our own unique sets of skills. But without you there will be a fraction more work to do and we'll fail. We all need to do our part. And this is your part. Build your computer and help us slip their firewalls, crack their code, and discover what they're hiding at the bottom of Edge City. This is the cyberpunk game at the end of the world. Full of 90’s cyberpunk bullshit and bursting with all the trappings of a personalized, handheld hacking device. Happy99 is a solo/co-op hacking game where you build your computer out of components, placing them on a grid for strategic optimization, so you can battle against the God Machine (GM) that is the Sears Supercomputer. Between assaults we live our lives and do what we can to prep, including espionage, breaking and entering, and dodging the Sears Security. The game uses 2d6 modified by computer components and skills to accomplish tasks and overcome obstacles. The hacking is inspired by the likes of Battleship. Head-to-head, grid-based challenges against stacked odds where you’ll need to team up and use each character’s unique abilities to overcome. Inside the game are several pre-generated characters with their own lives here in Edge City. You alone, or with friends, will take on their roles, build your computer and try to uncover the secrets at the bottom of Edge City, the mile-long hole in the middle of America where Sears are untouchable by mortal hands…but not digital ones. You can find the demo for the PvP-focused rules on the itch page right now. - https://selkie.itch.io/happy99-demo This will show you how the computer building basics work, how to play the game in general, and introduce you to the aesthetics of Happy99. The full game will be fleshed out with much more build options, the pre-gens, world building, and so on. Quotes from play testers: “90's hacking so authentic you can see the yellowing computer plastic" (Jared Wright) THE BOOK Happy99 is a zine presented in a unique format. The full color, 48-page zine comes in a DVD case along with a full soundtrack and CD-booklet with additional rules to continue play. The packaging is a focus for us on this project. We’re aiming to deliver something unlike a typical zine and really give you a physical object from Edge City to proudly display to your players. We’re budgeting for 500 zines printed through PrintNinja, each one will be packaged in the DVD case and come with a physical CD of the original soundtrack. TIERS For this campaign, we’re keeping it simple. We want to deliver the exact experience we want with zero fluff. If you back you can either get: The Digital Package - $25 Includes a PDF of the zine, PDF of the CD booklet, and mp3 files of the OST. The Physical Package - $50 The DVD case with the zine, the CD booklet, and the CD of the OST, along with the PDFs and mp3 files. SHIPPING All shipping information will be collected after the fact on our store. This allows us to save all the shipping hassles for when we have zines and cases printed, without the risk of shipping costs changing from the time of the Kickstarter. Snow’s fulfilled ever Kickstarter of hers using this method and is doing the fulfillment for this one as well. Beyond the initial scope of the project, we hope to stretch a little further and really flesh out Happy99 into something a little more special. At 10k, Char will write an introductory module for Happy99 that can be used to start a campaign and set the vibes of the setting. At 12k, Jess Levine will write an additional module separate from the zine, with a climactic God Machine and the consequences of defeating it. At 15k we create some custom stickers to add to every physical order, allowing you to deck out your actual PC with designs from Charlotte. At 18k we include prints of each pregen character that double as character sheets, designed by Charlotte. And finally, we've been plunking away at the world building of Edge City for a while now. We know what it looks like, we know what's in it. We want to bring that to life with a foldout map included in each order of Happy99. At 20k, we'll be able to pay Char for the art and design of such a momentous task. THE TEAM Creator/Artist - Charlotte Charlotte is fairly new to creating TTRPGs but has been playing them for almost a decade now. She has been drawing since time immemorial and loves bright, neon colors and loose lines.  She and her art can be found on Twitter. Musician/Fulfillment - Snow Snow is a trans writer and semi-known quantity in TTRPGs. They have written and fulfilled several Kickstarters and have at least one award to show for her efforts. They’re mostly known for .dungeon. Wizards & the Wastes, and My Body is a Cage, but also a few sleeper-hits like Red Snow and 6e. Editing - Roz Leahy Roz has been making rpgs for over a decade. She has worked on Warhammer 40,000: Wrath and Glory, Mothership, a bunch of Mothership 3pp books, and her own little osr zines. Her gender identity is Dade Murphy in that latex one piece. https://rozleahy.carrd.co Stretch Goal Writer - Jess Levine Jess Levine (i.e. jess from online ) is an author and TTRPG designer, whose work primarily concerns two themes: scifi and lesbians. She is the creator of I Have the High Ground, PLANET FIST, and the CRIT Awards 2023 Best GMless Game of the Year going rogue 2e.

description: What Is Tessellation? Tessellation is a Jazz record with 25 collaborators from around the world.  Its artists hail from Turkey, Iran, Tunisia, Israel, India, China, and the United States. Tessellation is deeply inspired by Pan-Arabic and Persian musical systems and Maqam-inspired approaches to melody. Tessellation is premised on futurism, and combines traditional, ancient instrumentation with state-of-the-art practices in synthesis, cutting-edge electronics, and a contemporary tone that pays homage to its musical origins while striving to create something new and unheard. The result is a musical record executed by an international ensemble whose members span the entire globe .  Their varied skills, backgrounds, and cultures are the tilework of a vast and joyful Tessellation, a testament to the beauty of the varied human spirit. Tessellation was released on all streaming services on May 17, 2024 .  This Kickstarter campaign is to raise funds for a luxuriant, high-fidelity vinyl pressing.  You can stream the album below, or anywhere else music is found. A Globe-Spanning Collaboration Tessellation is composed, arranged, and produced by Keegan Tawa, who resides in Philadelphia in the United States.  In studying the Duduk, a traditional, double-reeded Armenian wind instrument, Tawa became fascinated by Arabic and Persian temperament systems and musical approaches.  Tawa began to work with a variety of musicians from overseas, inviting both their technical skills and compositional influences into his pieces.  As this body of work expanded over the years, it drew in Turkish, Carnatic, and Chinese musicians as well. Each of these artists imbues Tessellation with both their incredible technical abilities and the ethos of their musical culture. Tessellation embraces and spotlights the unique styles, dialects, and improvisational approaches of each of its musicians, weaving a tapestry of wide-ranging musical expression enabled by the power of modern technology. In this way, Tessellation is a piece of science fiction: bringing together a round table of geographically disparate musicians to join their traditional styles into something sincerely modern, all on the wings of technology - something that has become possible only recently in the long musical story of our species. The Astounding Art of Linda Zhang Tessellation is deeply inspired by recurring geometrical patterns and polyhedral shapes.  With these influences in mind, Philadelphia artist Linda Zhang has developed an incredible pair of works that depict the character of the record with perfect clarity. Linda blends Islamic and Arabic geometric design with traditional  Chinese watercolor to create a detailed piece of artwork that embodies Tessellation's spirit of cultural interplay.  The piece is finished with her traditional Chinese seal stamp. The outer sleeve of the record will be Linda's colored work: Tessellation 03 , in Chinese Watercolor.  The golden stellated regions and central rhombus will be inlaid with gold foil.  The sleeve itself will be printed with a luxurious matte finish on heavy, 24-point cardboard. The inner sleeve containing the vinyl record will be Linda's black and white linework: Tessellation 02 , white gel pen on black paper, digitally traced. The vinyl disc itself will be white marbleized with gold, mirroring the contrast of gold paint and watercolor paper on the outer sleeve.  The platter will be a heavyweight, 180g disc, crafted using high-fidelity direct-metal mastering. An Early Look A live rendition of Twelve Freedoms, the second of eight songs on Tessellation. Secrets in the Sleeve Like Tawa's previous record, Startide , the vinyl sleeve of Tessellation will contain encoded messages leading to the secret location of a buried treasure . Startide's burial site has been discovered by four different independent explorers. Tessellation 's treasure hunt will be significantly more advanced in difficulty, both crypotlogically and physically. It will necessitate collaboration and teamwork between rivaling teams of treasure hunters, unlike Startide's hunt, which rewarded only the first adventurer to unearth its treasure. Startide's treasure site Previous Successes and Lessons Learned Tessellation is our second vinyl Kickstarter campaign. Our first campaign for Startide was a success and taught us a lot about the reality of pressing vinyl and distributing it globally. The final 26 Startide shipments :') Despite a successful fulfillment, Startide's delivery timeline was delayed.  This was partially a result of the fact that Startide was released during the height of the Covid-19 lockdown, when the prices of shipping were doubled in cost and our pressing plant's staff was cut nearly in half. In approaching Tessellation , we have arranged a far more conservative series of cost estimates which account for a much higher degree of unpredictability in cost.  Additionally, we have created estimation curves for not one - but three different iterations of the record: a basic pressing (green), an upgraded pressing (blue), and our objective: the luxe pressing (orange).  In the case of an unanticipated cost spike, we will be able to dynamically pivot into pressing a version of the record that accommodates the funding we have available. X-Axis: number of records.  Y-Axis: Cost with Safety Multiplier.Vertical red bars represent the minimum run sizes for the pressing type. The Collaboration Map

description: I'm setting out on a quest to venture into dismantling how I have mostly chosen to perceive myself as for most of my life: not a "real" musician. I've stunted my own creativity and growth out of the fear that I could not succeed, but I very recently that I don't care anymore about whatever "success". means. I'm finally letting my creativity flow without judgement and perfectionism commanding my every move — and it turns out that I'm really enjoying it, despite how scary it can be. I am a musician. I am a songwriter that now has the guts to follow a dream that has always been kept neatly tucked in a dark closet, only taken out when nobody was watching. I don't want to limit the one dream I have always had in my heart since I can remember having dreams at all. I'm ready to start, and am hoping you'll start it with me. The entire recording process is expensive, and so is paying the incredible musicians to record with me. l've set a goal of $10,000 to cover expenses of recording/editing/mixing/mastering 11-12 songs, paying Josh Rilko, Oliver Bates Craven, and Geoff Saunders (all from Sierra Ferrell!), as well as making some excellent merch and pressed albums to give back to all of the supporters who are willing to show up here and support this quest. Any contribution that you can make is perfect just as it is. Thank you so, so much for even coming here to read this far, let alone consider helping me on this journey. Leading with love, Victoria PS: Lyrics art, stickers, and trucker hat designs are all created by the incredible artists Julia Evered!!! And the album cover will be designed by Steph Garibay. So thankful to have these amazing artists as my friends 🥰

description: STRETCH GOAL ANNOUNCEMENT If we can get to a funding level of $35,000 , the members of Aunt Bettys will return to the studio to re-record six Aunt Bettys songs. They will be joined by several guest contributors, who will provide vocals and additional instrumentation. So far we're excited to announce that Michele Palmer, Bill Campbell, Steve Hindalong, and Matt Slocum are all onboard, and we'll have more to share in the days ahead. The Stretch Goal will cover studio costs, mixing and mastering. Every Kickstarter backer will receive a digital download, so we hope you'll join us and make this crazy dream happen! ORIGINAL CAMPAIGN DETAILS The Aunt Bettys was a brief glimpse of what could have been for Michael Knott. Growing up in the Orange County music scene, Michael fronted the bands Lifesavers, L.S. Underground, and Bomb Bay Babies, while also managing to build a respectable catalog of solo releases like Rocket & A Bomb , Strip Cycle , and Screaming Brittle Siren . Michael formed the Aunt Bettys in the mid-'90s with long-time collaborator Brian Doidge. As Doidge recalled, "Michael and I started talking about getting a record deal ... so Michael got a drummer [Chuck Cummings, who had drummed on previous Knott releases] and I put an ad out and went through a ton of guitarists until we found Andrew Carter and started Aunt Betty's Ford." It was a whirlwind ride for the Bettys who attracted the interest of several major labels, before signing to Elektra with a big cash advance and the promise of their marketing muscle. The band shortened their name to Aunt Bettys (after a legal threat from Ford Motor Co.), and produced an amazing album of blistering alternative rock, with nods to contemporaries like Foo Fighters and Jane's Addiction. Lyrically the album was a continuation of Knott's strong story-telling, with tales of sex, drugs, and alien abductions. Also included were reinterpreted takes on Knott classics "Rocket & A Bomb" and "Kitty Courtesy." Despite all of the promise of the album, turmoil within the band resulted in the label quickly dropping them. The album was released with little promotional effort from the label. The band later independently released a follow-up collection of demos called Ford Supersonic . The Kickstarter Campaign The Aunt Bettys album has lived on as one of the most celebrated releases of Knott's career. We have had the pleasure on working with Mike Knott on prior reissues of his albums The Grape Prophet and Strip Cycle , and we've now decided to take on a deluxe reissue of this album as well, with the direct involvement of Knott's family and band. We will reissue the album on vinyl, in two stunning color variants. Each will be pressed on heavy 180 gram vinyl in a deluxe gatefold package, with liner notes and never-before-seen photos and memorabilia. We will reissue on glass-mastered CD, in a digipak with a full booklet, with the same attention to detail afforded the vinyl. For those still rollin' the streets in their '80s Camaro, we have a cassette option, with a large fold-out J Card (magnifying glass not included). All production will be handled by trusted partners that we have worked with multiple times for great, consistent results. Reward mockups (artwork subject to change) The iconic album artwork paintings are lost to time, as confirmed by artist Tim Okamura. We have chosen to work with another renowned painter Stephen Cefalo , who will be creating two brand-new 12x24 oil paintings for the release. The new artwork will be inspired by and an homage to the original art, designed specifically for the larger vinyl format. Like Okamura, Cefalo is a celebrated painter with a background in surreal life-like paintings. He has also done work in comics and as a staff artist for Nickelodeon, so he brings experience relevant to the unique demands of this project. And some fun facts for those in and around the Mike Knott scene: Stephen is fan of Knott's work in the late '80s and early '90s, and is excited to be involved in the project. He went to high school with Blaster the Rocket Man, and painted the cover to their album The Monster Who Ate Jesus in his younger years. We have definitely heard him name Fluffy Luvs You as among his all-time favorite albums. Stephen Cefalo example work. See more at stephencefalo.com We will be working with Christopher Colbert to remaster the album, and hope to obtain the original master tapes for the highest quality results. (Warner Bros. maintains meticulous master archives and will begin the search and restoration process at the conclusion of the Kickstarter. Although we expect to have access to the master tapes, we will ultimately use the best available audio sources.) Goal & Stretch Goal This is the most expensive project we have undertaken. There are several reasons for this. First, the cost of manufacturing continues to rise every year, as does shipping. As a major label release, we also have to license from Warner Bros. directly, and with certain quantity commitments. But given how special this album is to us, and we believe for many others too, we hope you will join us in making this a reality! You will hopefully find that our pledge tiers are reasonably priced for a Kickstarter, and we have included (for the first time ever) a transparency chart of where these expenses will go. We do have a very special stretch goal planned as well, and we will share these details if and when we hit our primary goal! (We will also keep you informed with any other announcements we have throughout the process!) Shipping We ship our vinyl orders using high quality Mighty Music Mailers. These rugged boxes have transported thousands of our records safely to homes all around the world. Some oversized orders may need to ship in alternate packaging, but we will do our best to protect your rewards. Please note that we are not responsible for minor damage to your rewards in transit, but we do ask you to contact us if you encounter any issues. International Backers: We are happy to ship worldwide! For all international orders, we will invoice you when we are ready to ship . We use a shipping partner that typically saves 30-40% below retail rates, and we will include insurance with all international orders for peace of mind. If you would like to know a ballpark of what shipping might cost to your country, send us a direct message and we can give you an estimate.

description:
I NEED YOUR HELP Other than my
desire to put out another album as soon as possible, one of the real
goals of “The Color Scheme” is to give you an even deeper, richer
and more stimulating listening experience than you had with my debut,
“Chokecherry Jam”. “Chokecherry” was mostly recorded in four
days with almost no rehearsal and heavy emphasis on the “Jam”. While
this more spontaneous and organic technique was hugely fulfilling and made for
what many think to be a wonderful album, “The Color Scheme” takes
a much more acutely focused approach. The songs are catchy and sharp
with a wide range of topics from politics, love and death to more
abstract themes and the interesting blending of genres that I've
already become known for. In addition, a much larger amount of focus
and time has/will be spent on achieving the uniquely perfect tone and
composition of each individual instrument for each part of every song. Really
tap into... the whole... “color scheme” of the thing. GET IT? ;)
All in all, the end result should be a “colorful”, entertaining
and hopefully emotionally moving record unlike anything you've ever
heard! I just need your help. To create the
kind of top-notch, quality record that I hope to release, I need more
studio time than I can afford. 10K is my Kickstarter "goal" but it is still onIy a fraction of what I need. So help me make this happen and give as
much as you can. I vow to give you all that I have with
this new record. Thank you! Others that are already involved in the making of "The Color Scheme": Producer: Jason Burt Multi-Instrumentalist: Nik Lee Cover Art: Joonbug Lenworth McIntosh Mastering Engineer: Brian Lucey

description: All 10 Stretch Goals Achieved! Hundreds of amazing backers pledged double our original base goal. Now Kate Wolf’s Live In Mendocino album will be 20 tracks! Kate’s family is very grateful...we couldn’t have done it without your support. Thank you. After the Campaign is Over, The Album Will Still Be Available Visit KateWolf.com/Mendocino_Kickstarter Introduction Kate Wolf had a great affinity for the California coast, and she frequently played in Mendocino area venues prior to becoming a nationally known folk singer and songwriter ( biography ). Since her passing in 1986, Kate’s family has collaborated on a number of projects to keep her music and memory alive. Now we are excited to present a new collection of songs recorded at Mendocino concerts from 1979 to 1982. Please pledge to help bring this album to life on Kate's original Owl Records label! The Album Live In Mendocino features eight songs that haven’t appeared on a prior Kate Wolf album: three by Kate and five by others. The album also contains a live version of Across The Great Divide , one of Kate’s most beloved songs. The CD booklet includes lyrics for every song and many previously unpublished photographs. The Songs The album will contain at least 10 songs; up to 20 if we meet our stretch goals! (See details below.) Click song titles to hear excerpts. 10 Songs for the Base Kickstarter Goal 1. Golden Harmony Kate Wolf lyrics 2. Links In The Chain Kate Wolf lyrics 3. You And I Together Kate Wolf lyrics 4. Lonesome Lyn Hardy 5. Rain Song Mike Gwinn 6. Scott's Creek Bluff Utah Phillips 7. 20/20 Vision (and Walking Around Blind) Joe Allison & Milton Estes 8. 2-4-D Antonia Lamb 9. Across The Great Divide Kate Wolf 10. The Trumpet Vine Kate Wolf 10 More Songs for the Stretch Goals (all written by Kate Wolf. lyrics ) 11. Safe At Anchor 12. The Lilac & The Apple 13. Back Roads 14. She Rises Like The Dolphin 15. Everybody's Looking For The Same Thing Kate Wolf & Hugh Shacklett 16. Great Love Of My Life 17. The Heart 18. Early Morning Melody 19. I Don't Know Why 20. Riding In The Country The Recordings The town of Mendocino in the 1970s The songs are from seven concerts recorded at various Mendocino County, California venues by Nicholas Wilson, whose photos of Kate appear in the album. Perhaps you saw Kate at The Well or Crown Hall in the city of Mendocino or at Greenwood Oasis in Elk? When Nick told Kate’s family that he had recorded many of these shows, we were excited about the prospect of releasing them. Nick's 15 hours of tapes were made directly from the soundboard at each concert, and he painstakingly digitized nearly 200 song performances. We then turned to Nina Gerber, Kate’s long time accompanist, who has compiled two other posthumous Kate Wolf collections. Her deep and intimate familiarity with Kate’s music, along with a desire for quality that matched Kate’s, resulted in a selection of 20 tracks that we feel beautifully represent Kate. If you’d like to learn more about the process, visit the Kate Wolf website. The Performers Nina Gerber, Kate Wolf & Ford James at Mendocino's Crown Hall in 1982 Nina Gerber appears on every track (except the a cappella The Lilac & The Apple ). In addition to playing mandolin, harmonica, and guitar on various tracks, she also sings harmony vocal. Ford James played bass and sang with Kate and Nina for years, including Kate’s show on Austin City Limits. Rick Byars played with Kate early on in her career: he’s on bass and harmony vocals on this album. These fine musicians were the backbone of Kate’s band during these Mendocino years. Stretch Goals to Double The Album! The $10,000 base goal reflects the costs for a 10-song album. Through a series of stretch goals, we have can double the album to 20 tracks. Each of the ten $1,000 stretch goals covers the additional costs for mastering, CD booklet pages, publishing royalties, performer fees, etc. for one song. We hope you’ll help us reach all ten stretch goals so we can release a music-packed 76-minute album! Rewards The Basics Choose a reward level and complete the pledge process.  If you would like additional items, please see Add-Ons below.  You can always change your reward level before the campaign closes. About the Mendocino “Groovy” Album T-Shirts Designed by Kate’s granddaughter, Bonnie Kate Wolf, we think the “Live In Mendocino” album t-shirt is really, ahem, “groovy”. The image is 12” in diameter and incorporates the original LP label artwork from Owl Records, which Kate started in 1976. Our screening process dyes the fabric rather than placing a layer of plastic ink on top of the shirt fabric, for a much softer feel. The black, 100% cotton shirts are soft too, and will come in women’s and men’s sizes S, M, L, XL and 2X. This design is now also available in a 3" vinyl sticker! "Groovy" Album T-Shirt designed by Kate's granddaughter, Bonnie Kate Wolf About Digital Downloads Instead of a physical disc, we'll send a CD-size sticker featuring the album cover.  A unique code on the removable backing can be used one time to download the album (in MP3 or FLAC format). If you like, you can order extra digital album stickers (see Add-Ons below) and give a download code to a friend. The Survey After the campaign closes, we'll send a survey requesting your shipping address, shirt sizes, and anything else applicable. Add-Ons Want an extra album , video , songbook , t-shirt or sticker for yourself or a friend? You can add these items by increasing your pledge by the amount(s) shown above. If you have already pledged and want to add items, simply select Manage your pledge and increase your pledge amount by the total of the items you wish to add. Thank You & Please Share! Please share this campaign with your friends and help us reach our goal. - Thank you for your support!  - Kate's Family L to R: Max Wolf, Kate Wolf, Hannah Wolf, Nina Gerber, Kate Wolf Photo credit: all photos are by Nicholas Wilson. Used by permission. Please visit nwilsonphoto.com

description: I have a new single coming out, "Let Me Live Again," with amazing remixes by Wawa, Frankie Knuckles, Tony Moran and Warren Rigg, Craig C, Bill Bennett and Tyler Nelson, and J-C.  I am raising money to shoot a video that will knock your socks off and be just as amazing as the song and remixes. I've also been collaborating on a 2013 "The Way I Am" calendar with acclaimed photographer, Aaron Cobbett , that needs to be manufactured so we can get it to you, and we need some funds to do just that.  This will be coming out in tandem with my new full length album, "The Way I Am," with collaborations by Nervo, Chris Willis, Wawa, RedTop, Count De Money, Razor N Guido, Jochen Simms, Chris Castagno and Ron Schrader. This has been a labor of love for me and my management, Peace Bisquit, and we've put all of this together ourselves.  Right now we just need your help to see it thru!!  I've been at this for 30 years, and now more then ever it's really tough out there for artists.  It takes a village and your support, whatever you can give, is very greatly appreciated!! Colton peacebisquit.com/artists/colton-ford/ facebook.com/ColtonFordMusic twitter.com/ColtonFordMusic
