description: Catalyst: Duro Wicks & Chicago Hip Hop is an independent documentary film about how Duro “Shame Love Tempo” Wicks fell in love with Hip Hop and helped give it a home on the Chicago music scene of the early 1990’s. It examines what made Hip Hop blow up in Chicago so much later than in other cities, how a tiny 100 watt college station became Chicago’s Hip Hop radio powerhouse, why it was so difficult for a young Black entrepreneur to get a party started, and a time when social, political and economic conditions ultimately proved to make Duro’s dream unsustainable, even though it was more successful than anyone ever imagined it could be. Duro Wicks on stage at The Lower Links in 1992 The Chicago Reader called Duro Wicks “one of the godfathers of the Wicker Park hip hop scene.” The Chicago Tribune called him “a large man with a charismatic presence… at the center of the kinetic circus.” Duro describes himself as “The Catalyst” when talking about how he created a place for a generation of kids to show up and prove. “Before I did my own thing, I had nowhere to go,” says Duro “so my whole thing was let’s do our own shows.” Duro fell in love with hip hop long before it was easily accessible in Chicago, so he wanted to create a place for people with similar passions to gather. After several false starts, he finally got some traction running the first weekly Hip Hop open mic night in the city at a basement club called The Lower Links . It brought in hundreds of kids every week from all over the city and suburbs, which was unheard of in the segregated Chicago of early '90's. After a brief, but wildly popular run, the club ended up being a victim of its own success when neighbors and other business owners in Wrigleyville, unhappy with the growing number of Black kids hanging out in the street every week, took action and got the club shut down. A flyer from the final Lower Links $3 Sunday Sunday Hip Hop Show Thirty years later, the story of Duro's wild rollercoaster ride of music, passion, culture, and Chicago history is finally being told. The story is told by Duro himself, with the help of recollections from many of the MC’s, DJ’s, graffiti artists, B-Boys, and members of the hip hop community who were there with him. Their stories are woven together with archival footage and still images to tell the vibrant history of the early days of Chicago Hip Hop. Duro in the early 90's with his band He Who Walks Three Ways The filmmaker behind the project is Duro’s high school friend Dave Steck , an award-winning, twice Emmy nominated member of the Producers Guild of America and a veteran of underground music scenes. When Duro first told Dave about his journey as a Hip Hop pioneer in Chicago, Dave knew that it had to be captured and preserved as part of Chicago’s musical history, so they began to collaborate as co-producers to get the story told. They both will share in the success of the film, but with Duro's increasingly serious health issues, the guys are trying to get the film done as soon as possible and could really use your help. Duro and Dave There are still a few more conversations to be had and stories to be heard, but so far the film has interviewed Duro, Anthony “DJ Tone B. Nimble” Fields, John “JP Chill” Schauer, Lori "DJ Rapture" Branch, PJ "Black Fist" Khan, Andrew “Gravity” Kirkland, Scott "Ski One" Styles, Omar “TherapyBeats” Johnson, Chris “Cool-Out Chris” Hawkins, Howard Bailey, Rob McKay, Amina Norman, Mary Datcher, Rick Wojcik, Mario Smith, Leigh Jones, and Yung Savolainen. Filming with Duro There’s an incredibly talented team working behind the scenes of the film as well. Besides Dave and Duro producing, they’ve been joined by the award-winning documentary editor Patty Schumann, the Emmy nominated sound mixer Thomas Ryan, and the legendary Chicago Hip Hop pioneer Dug Infinite, who is providing music and beats. They are also working closely with Sara Chapman and Media Burn Archive to source authentic archival footage. An example of restoring and preserving old archival footage. If all goes well, the shooting will be wrapped up by this summer, the editing completed this Fall, and the film ready to screen in January. The hope is to premiere it in Chicago as a benefit for Duro’s medical expenses. The WGBL custom sticker and t-shirt design

description: Help us bring Cucalorus 27 back in person! 27 years ago, a fearless band of filmmakers called Twinkle Doon held "An Evening of Celluloid Art" at Water Street Restaurant in downtown Wilmington. Today we support emerging and innovative artists through an annual film festival (that's Cucalorus!), two regional film festivals, a residency program, a community cinema, an outdoor film series, and youth education programs. We need your help as we plot and scheme for this fall's 27th annual Cucalorus Film Festival. The pandemic has taken a big toll on all of us, and we're cooking up a celebration that has us back together, November 10-14, 2021 in Wilmington, NC. We need YOU to make it all possible!! While our clown alter-egos work in a magical virus-free circus, we need help bringing in the pioneering filmmakers and visionary artists that make the Cucalorus experience such a mind-bending creative journey. Your pledge travels around the world picking up trailblazers in film and comedy, so get those dollar bills flyin’! This festival only flourishes because of the 27 years of continued support from our loyal Cucalorus family. Help us create powerful and heartening experiences for all the cultural travelers out there looking to collaborate, share, and learn as we all reconnect. And we'll toss you a Cucalorus bumper sticker, a custom Cucalorus 27 Freaker, a vintage screenprint, or your smiling face on the big screen! Cucalorus loves you! See you in November you sweet, silly humans! Dan Brawley is the Chief Instigating Officer of the Cucalorus Festival, an international celebration of filmmaking, performance and technology in Wilmington, North Carolina founded in 1994 by Twinkle Doon. Cucalorus screens more than 300 films with a range of multi-disciplinary programs focusing on dance, social justice, cocktails, entrepreneurship, and performance. Cucalorus was one of the “25 Coolest Film Festivals in the World, 2015” according to MovieMaker Magazine. Brawley was elected three times as the President of the Film Festival Alliance, an independent non-profit organization that develops and fosters collaboration among mission-driven film festivals around the world. Brawley was born in Wilmington, North Carolina, attended New Hanover High School, and graduated cum laude from Duke University, where he was an Academic All-American on the varsity golf team. He is also active as a performing artist and community organizer. Rachel Taylor is the Managing Director of the Cucalorus Film Foundation, overseeing the foundation’s operations and development departments. Key responsibilities include financial management, staff and volunteer development, grant writing and management, corporate and individual giving, and earned revenue cultivation. She was previously the Programming Director, during which time Cucalorus launched the Lumbee Film Festival, expanded the Popup Cinema program, and enhanced the workshop and performance components of the annual Cucalorus Film Festival. Rachel came to Wilmington from nonprofit arts management in Boston, where she worked at the Huntington Theatre, the Handel and Haydn Society, and Company One. Rachel received her Bachelor of Science in theatre and business from Skidmore College. She earned her Master of Public Administration from the University of North Carolina with a focus on nonprofit management. Rachel is also a 500-hour Yoga Alliance certified instructor and currently teaches at Longwave Yoga. She serves on the Board of Directors for Yoga Village, an organization dedicated to bringing yoga to underserved populations throughout our community. CB Johnson is the Operations Manager for Cucalorus and oversees the volunteer, Pop-Up and Drive-In Cinema programs as well as other operational, behind-the-scenes aspects of the Cucalorus Festival. CB began working at Cucalorus at Cucalorus in 2017, and when he’s not setting up, striking or managing venues and productions, CB is an active musician and songwriter, performing and recording in Wilmington, NC and the southeastern US. CB earned a BS of Naval Architecture from the US Naval Academy. He has learned live event production through experience, his service as a US Marine (the school of hard knocks and busted knuckles), and his work as a local REALTOR (currently with the Property Shop International Realty). In his spare time, CB enjoys hanging out with his kids, surfing, yoga, exploring the universal field of awareness, and practicing telepathy. Anna Ray Smith is the Programming Coordinator for the Cucalorus Film Foundation. As a proud Laurinburg native and independent filmmaker, she has a deep-rooted passion for the art of storytelling and the North Carolina film industry. A former educator, she spent 2.5 years teaching English and North American Culture in public schools in Spain. While there, she produced two short films in Madrid, one of which, “Chueca”, a story documenting the life of trans sex workers, went on to have a successful international film festival run. Anna holds dual Bachelor’s degrees in Journalism and Hispanic Studies from East Carolina University, and her Masters of Fine Arts in Creative Producing from the School of Filmmaking at the University of North Carolina School of the Arts. In her free time, you can find her at any coffee shop writing or sharpening her Spanish skills, traveling, or enjoying long walks with her rescued American Dingo mix, Honey. Marika Adair is the Communications Coordinator for the Cucalorus Film Foundation. She graduated from the University of North Carolina Wilmington in 2019 and has worked in film festivals for the past four years. She was the Festival Manager at the Double Exposure Investigative Film Festival in Washington DC and held many positions at UNCW’s Visions Film Festival & Conference, which is where her love of film festivals originated. She has also worked in extras casting for projects in Wilmington, NC including Hightown Season 2 and Blumhouse’s The Black Phone. Born in Montana and raised in the Brushy Mountains of North Carolina, Marika appreciates and experiences nature at every moment and plans to travel the world in a converted van. Marika spends her free time practicing witchcraft, contemplating her existence, and occasionally creating autobiographical short films. She is a proud bisexual and gender fluid, going by the pronouns she/her and they/them. Mark Ray is the Summer Festival Assistant at the Cucalorus Film Festival. Mark is a student at the University of North Carolina Wilmington, studying film studies and staying involved as a resident assistant.

Stretch Goal #2 The new target is $12,500. When we break that goal, each person donating $75 or more will receive a DVD of the film!! This is in addition to all the other rewards we have already listed. It's an amazing opportunity to add this to your video library. What will we do with the extra money? We'll use it to document the premier in audio and video recording as well as still photographs. Then we'll use that to get presenters interested in booking the show in a theater near you! Stretch Goal #1 A limited edition poster of the image above for the premier, signed by Erika and Eleonor and the band! Everyone will be entered in a lottery for 10 limited edition posters suitable for framing. The more you pledge, the more entries you will receive! EXAMPLES: Pledge up to $50 for 1 entry Pledge $50 - $100 for 2 entries $100 - $200 for 4 entries $200 - $400 for 6 entries $400 - $600 for 8 entries $600-$1000 for 10 entries LIVE Premier Performance of WUNDERKAMMERN: The Secret Life of Objects

. Film By Erika Suderburg Music By Eleonor Sandresky Presented by Tribeca New Music - 2017 Festival at DiMenna Center, NYC on May 24, 2017! Our Goal: To create a partnership with you good people to create a film event that will travel the world, bringing the quirky joy of Wunderkammern to all Live! To do this, Eleonor is remaking the movie soundtrack for an ensemble of 8 plus conductor. This has already consummed one Yaddo residency and many weeks of 2016 in the re-imagining of the score, choosing and sourcing the technology and adapting it to the project so that the group can play the complete, 92 minute soundtrack from the film live, in synchronization with a screening of the film for an audience that hopefully includes you! Eleonor is feverishly working to complete the adapted score that matches her composed music with the film. Once she has a complete score, she will then create parts for this ensemble of 8 plus conductor. She is simultaneously gridding the film to sync up the sound perfectly with the picture for purposes of live performance. This grid will allow her to see where the beats and bars are in connection with the picture, and then she will have a conductor score with all that information. It may also be desirable to create a click track for all the players to use. And from all of this, she, with the musicians and the wonderful folks at Tribeca New Music will premier the work! Tribeca New Music is a not-for-profit organization that promotes a bold new classical music infused with American pop culture.  Our core group of professional artists engages the public with compelling performances that stimulate and inspire, offering new and recent works with passion and integrity. The goal is to forge new links between musicians and a growing audience of adventurous music lovers through concerts, commissions, competitions, educational initiatives, dialogue, and our annual Tribeca New Music Festival. About the film: Wunderkammern: The Secret Life of Objects examines acquisition, objects and what a contemporary Wunderkammern might reveal about desire, knowledge, materiality and memory. Fabricated out of objects contained in an existent fifty-year-old Cabinet of Wonders, Wunderkammern: The Secret Life of Objects leaves no object untouched, no dust mite comfortable and no item microscopically unexamined. Organized alphabetically for sanity’s sake the film activates a series of associations, arcane, eccentric, playful and expansive. Ever unfolding, yet carefully constructed to facilitate your perusal, Wunderkammern: The Secret Life of Objects takes you on tangents yet to be imagined, springs coils tightly wound, unnerves the inanimate and vitalizes cultural detritus. You will look at the objects that surround you with a newfound appreciation, animist suspicion, perspectival acumen and a regained sense of wonder. We invite you to get lost with us.

description: WELCOME! ⭐️ Overview/How to Play 🥇⚔️ Become one of the multi-verse's top champions and duke it out in a series of fast-paced mini-games to win powerful, stackable treasure ... and bragging rights. Then, use your hard-earned loot to take down monsters and earn victory points. When the game ends, the player with the most victory points from defeated monsters claims ultimate triumph! In each game you will play a set number of rounds based on the number of players. Each round has 3 main phases as outlined below: Rules 📜 NOTE: Rules in the link may not be final. Please expect that some adjustments and edits will be made to the rulebook before the final production. Pledge Tiers + What You Get 🎲🎲🎲 *NOTE: Images above are renders and the physical product might be slightly different! Stretch Goals ⬅️ ➡️ Add-ons 👑 Note: The Holographic Foil Champion Cards are already included with the Deluxe Game Pledge and All-In Pledge , and both the Plush and Dice Tray are included with the All-In Pledge . If you’ve pledged at these levels, there’s no need to select these items as add-ons—unless you’d like extras! Why Back Now? 🤷‍♂️ Get it cheaper! Kickstarter backers get a special discounted price (20% off MSRP) — because you believed in Get Stacked before it was cool. Kickstarter exclusive stuff you can't get later. We are only making enough deluxe edition games and add-on items to send to backers and/or hit our manufacturing MOQs. Without you, the game won't happen! We are a first time indie publisher, and manufacturing is only possible with your support! We can't wait to get Get Stacked on your tables. Shape the game. The more backers we get, the more awesome extras we can afford — and as a backer, you’re part of the crew shaping the expansion pack and other exclusive cards. Media 📺 Note: The game preview copy is a prototype that was made by an on demand print shop! Accuracy of treasure stacking and game materials will be largely improved in the final game version! Shipping 📦📬 SHIPPING - Shipping is not included in your pledge. After the campaign ends, you'll pay the actual shipping cost via the pledge manager. Below is a quoted estimate what you will be charged based on your region: *Notes: Shipping costs for certain regions may decrease if we have enough demand to open a local hub during the campaign. Also, if you live in a remote or rural area of certain countries the actual shipping cost could be outside the given range. All '$' are in USD. TAXES - There are two types of taxes related to shipping out the games. Customs Duty Taxes: Friendly shipping regions will not require additional customs payments. For regions outside these hubs, local duties may apply and will be your responsibility to pay. VAT and Regional Taxes: VAT and similar taxes are not included in your pledge. Since these taxes are dependent on your exact location, we do not have a cost estimate for these taxes. These will be collected through the pledge manager based on your shipping address. For the UK and EU, we are required to collect VAT on the total pledge value and shipping costs. *NOTE ON TARIFFS: Our games will be manufactured in China and shipped to the U.S. for fulfillment. As you may know, U.S. import tariffs have seen a whirlwind of changes, which will impact the cost of bringing games into the country. Our priority is always to protect and respect our backers. We’re committed to absorbing these costs as much as possible. However, if tariffs increase significantly and remain at a level that makes it unsustainable for us to cover them entirely, we may need to slightly adjust shipping costs—likely by just a few dollars—compared to the original estimates. This potential change would only affect games fulfilled from the U.S. hub. Backers whose orders are shipped from a separate hub will not be impacted. Unfortunately, we CANNOT ship to the following countries at this time: Russia, Belarus, Afghanistan, Ukraine, Iran, Iraq, Syria, Congo, Eritrea, Guinea-Bissau, Haiti, Ivory Coast, Liberia, Myanmar, Somalia, Sudan. Due to high rates of packages going missing in some countries, we will not re-send games if they go missing to the following countries: Pakistan, Brazil, and Nigeria. Israel may have additional fees due to dangerous handling being imposed by couriers to the region Timeline 🗓️ What to Expect After the Campaign (AKA: How to Get Your Stuff!) A few weeks after the campaign ends, you’ll receive an email from us via BackerKit. This will take you to the pledge manager, where you can confirm your pledge and add any extra items you’d like. Then, a few weeks before fulfillment begins, you’ll get another email from us via BackerKit asking you to pay for shipping, as well as any taxes or duties (if needed). Taking care of this promptly will help avoid any delays in getting your rewards. Keep an eye on your inbox, and we will give reminders and updates here as well! Please note that we can only guarantee delivery for pledges that are confirmed within the specified deadlines. Meet the Team 👏 Moon Crossing Games is an indie board game publisher founded by two childhood best friends, Matt and Cole. Moon Crossing is a combo of the street names we grew up on. From kindergarten to college, we spent virtually every weekend at each other's homes, so we felt the name was a perfect tribute to remind us of the fun and games of our childhood. Here's a photo of us, 20 years apart! (Yes, we're still smiling—proof that even 3 a.m. arguments over card layouts couldn’t break us. 😉) When not working on Get Stacked, you’ll find Matt playing the drums wherever he can and Cole relaxing at the beach. Get Stacked is our debut game and combines our love for fantasy, fun, and fast-paced party gameplay. We’ve poured our hearts into every moment of its creation and hope to complete more projects to carve out a niche for crafting unforgettable, fantasy-inspired party games that bring people together! We are REALLY proud of the game and think that you will love it. Partners 🤝 Champion, Monster, and Treasure Art + Logo and Box Design was created by: @CharlotteN_Art . We CANNOT express how amazing Charlotte was to work with, and how crucial her vision was for the Get Stacked Multiverse.

description: Riftblade in 30 Seconds Draft spells and shape a strategy that evolves every turn as you grow your spellbook with each move. Every turn in Riftblade is a fast-paced tactical challenge as you marshal your spells and Riftstones to create a single, powerful chaining attack , proving your mastery and conquering your foes! Reward Tiers How to Play Take a look at our eight-minute How To Play video to get a feel for the flow of the game! You can also read the rules on our website in English, French, and German! What You Get A Living Card Game Riftblade launches with two exciting expansions to take your game even further — Voidspawn and Weavings . After an initial Spring delivery, Riftblade becomes a living game with new content released every quarter for both tabletop and mobile. Every release will add exciting new mechanics to explore and master, keeping each battle fresh and unique for years to come. Learn More Jasper William Cartwright gives it to you straight in less than 5 minutes with a quick but comprehensive review of Riftblade. Kevin Parr from Dicecreamsandwich gives his own personal take on Riftblade. Spice8Rack gives a great technical review of the mechanics within Riftblade. This Dad Reads gives a full overview of the game and his thoughts on how it stands out among deckbuilders! Riftblade Mobile Ready for a truly dynamic experience where you control the very rules of magic? Beta access for Riftblade Mobile opens in October! Be one of our early backers to get guaranteed early access. Action-Oriented: Get your fix of deep, strategic game play in quick, 15-minute matches , perfect for your commute or coffee break. Challenge the World: Test your skills against players from every corner of the globe in our competitive ranked mode . Climb the ladder from a humble Apprentice to the legendary rank of Riftblade Vanguard. Do you have what it takes to reach the top and claim your place among the elite? Reap Exclusive Rewards: With every victory, you'll earn experience and unlock a treasure trove of rewards . Show off your achievements with stunning alternate card art and unique player avatars and skins. Take Fate into Your Hands. Before each ranked match, you and your opponent will enter the Riftforge , a pre-game phase where you use Fate Shards to alter the very fabric of reality. Will you bid to increase both players’ starting health, giving you an edge against aggressive decks? Or will you boost the number of available Riftstones, ramping up each turn’s intensity to the max? The choices you make in the Riftforge will dramatically shape your strategy and create a unique experience every single game. Never Pay to Win. No matter the outcome in the Riftforge , players will always begin the match on an even battleground . But how you alter the state of that Battlefield is up to you. An Ever-Evolving Battlefield. The Riftforge features hundreds of unique combinations of game-altering effects, that will rotate every month to keep gameplay fresh and exciting. There's always a new strategy to discover and master on your path to becoming a Vanguard. Add-Ons Stretch Goals Gameplay Videos Dive into Spice and Jasper’s epic best-of-three matchup and see who is the superior Riftblade! Only have time for a single video? Check out the epic conclusion in the last match where Jasper and Spice8Rack throw in powers from both Voidspawn and Weavings expansions! Project Timeline Shipping Info Unfortunately, we are currently unable to ship to: Afghanistan, Belarus, Brazil, Bolivia, Chile, Congo, Cuba, Eritrea, Ivory Coast, Guinea-Bissau, Haiti, Iran, Iraq, Israel, Liberia, North Korea, Peru, Russia, Sudan, Syria, Ukraine, Venezuela, and Yemen. * If your shipping region is not shown, please email contact@thegambitfoundry.com to ensure we can ship to you via the 'rest of world' option. Retailer Information Retailer pledges will be available to Brick and Mortar stores in North America and Europe. To be put on the list for retailer pledges, please read the information about pledges and fill out the Google form here: https://forms.gle/NEuvFP8ing5FcKRBA For any immediate questions not answered on the form, please email contact@thegambitfoundry.com Who We Are Why We Are Crowdfunding Riftblade and its first two expansions are rules-and-art-complete, honed and tested in both thousands of simulations and hundreds of in-person matches by enthusiastic players at Origins, PAX, GenCon, Tabletop Simulator, and beyond. The digital game is in alpha testing now, and we are preparing to shift to our Beta phase shortly after the close of this campaign. We have ambitious plans for this game series that go well beyond just this campaign, and we need your help to launch the game with a bang; Kickstarter helps to provide a vital infusion for us to invest in this game's longevity, including things like tournament play and events, stronger retail support, exciting digital modes, and more. Thank You The Gambit Foundry is an independent game publishing company. Our dedicated team of artisans, designers, and artificers is driven by an obsession with creating high-quality games that are quick to teach, easy to set up, and full of innovative mechanics that keep you coming back for more.

description: About the Game: As an action-platformer, Maiden of the Dark is inspired by Game Boy classics of the same genre, like Operation C, Return of the Ninja, and Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back. Make your way through 10 levels filled with danger and deadly enemies. Ranging from rocky mountains, dark forests, spiked caves, burning villages, and rapid waters, you're in for a difficult task. Not only will you need to master enemy patterns and intimidating bosses, but you'll need to perfect the art of platforming as you jump from ledge to ledge. Your adventure is set to a heavy-metal-inspired original soundtrack that will have your Game Boy bleeding from the speakers. The Story: Deep within the rural lands of Japan lies a village in desperation. Overtaken by an evil warlord named Zankoku and his two shoguns, the village resorts to using the power of dark magic. They gather around an ancient scroll and summon the Maiden of the Dark. The spirit only has 10 hours of darkness to rid the village and the surrounding areas of this menacing warlord and his army. Once the Sun rises, the Maiden will lose all powers and vanish, leaving the village to the rule of Zankoku. In the cover of the night, the Maiden of the Dark wields the sharpest katana that cuts through any man or demon. Her quest to save the village starts on the outskirts of the forest as she draws near a scouting patrol. The wind whispers of her arrival as her katana shines in the dark. Blood will soon spill... Characters: The Maiden - You'll be playing as her! An ancient spirit who is powered by the night. She is summoned by the village to protect them against invaders who wish to harm them. Her blade knows no limits. Yoroi - A shogun tactician who values impenetrable defense over firepower. He lets his enemies tire out before coming down on them with his massive broadsword. Find his weak points to begin his demise. Shiruku - Quick and nimble. She strikes with her iron claws without warning. As a shogun for Zankoku, she is arrogant and despises weak soldiers. She is relentless, but her onslaught can’t last forever. Zankoku - A warlord who rose to power with his ruthless intimidation and violence. Only those closest to him know his true power. Teixeira - The embodiment of love and affection. Find him for a special reward. The Team: Maiden of the Dark was created by four individuals who share a passion for video games. TigerChainsaw, is the creator of Maiden of the Dark, writing the plot, dialogue, and character designs. He also crafted the level layouts, play-tested, and outlined the gameplay as well as all marketing materials, including video, and these very retro-style magazine ads that you see! RetroRoo, is the programmer. He makes the game function from the menus to the combat, while stomping out bugs, programming enemy patterns, and polishing all aspects of the gameplay. PixelChunga is the pixel artist, carefully crafting the sprites of everything you see in the game, from the environments, enemies, weapons, and even the Maiden herself. Last, but not least, there is OGM, the musical composer of the game. Tailoring every level and cut scene with music, the Maiden of the Dark comes with a highly unique and killer heavy-metal inspired soundtrack. The Goal: Maiden of the Dark is the first video game created by TigerChainsaw Studios. A lot of time, money, and effort have been invested in the project. It has been nothing short of exhilarating as I've gotten to live out a lifelong dream of creating a video game. I'm so happy with how it has turned out, and now the goal is to share it with as many retro gamers as possible. Physical media matters. I've been a game collector for a long time, and there's no feeling that can replace opening up a new game, putting the disc/cartridge into a console, and powering it on. It's sadly a feeling that new generations are experiencing less and less. I want to create physical video games for retro game lovers. The goal of $10,000 is to cover the costs of labor and manufacturing for Maiden of the Dark. Having paid five different talents to work on this project, including a concept artist, pixel artist, musical composer, a developer, and a manufacturing company, the money will go toward those expenses plus the 5% that Kickstarter will receive. With enough money, we can turn this dream game into a physical reality, complete with a cartridge, manual, and game box. This cartridge will be compatible with the original Game Boy, Game Boy Pocket, Game Boy Color, Game Boy Advance, Game Boy Advance SP, and new retro handhelds that play original Game Boy games. Stretch Goals If funding reaches $12,000, we will port it to PC/Steam! If funding reaches $15,000, we will add color to Maiden of the Dark! If funding reaches $20,000, we will have a Nintendo Switch port of Maiden of the Dark! Timeline: Maiden of the Dark is close to being finished. It's very far in the development phase, with only a few aspects that need to be polished and implemented. This means that the game will be ready for physical production once the Kickstarter ends (successfully). With the game exiting the development phase, it will take approximately 60 days for manufacturing of the cartridge, manual, and game box, along with any other bonus goodies that come along with the tier that you select to support. This means that with enough backers, Maiden of the Dark will be in your hands very soon! About The Studio: Founded in 2024, TigerChainsaw Studios was designed to create physical retro games for retro gamers. The studio head, TigerChainsaw, is a retro gaming enthusiast, collector, and YouTuber. Each game is crafted to revive the nostalgia of the 80s and 90s for gamers. With a focus on what makes video games great...FUN! TigerChainsaw Studios is dedicated to bringing new high-quality experiences to old consoles. Try The Demo - NOW Our team is proud of our work, and we're excited to have gamers play it. Try out a digital demo of Maiden of the Dark, and see for yourself what all the fun is about! Tips on how to play: Your best attacks are calculated. Attack with precision! Running and jumping blindly from platform to platform will spell your doom! Watch your enemies before striking. Some enemies can only be killed by shurikens; use them wisely. Download it below and try it on a Game Boy Emulator! https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/lxuhiohoeedfdhweqsbng/Maiden-of-the-Dark-Official-Demo.gb?rlkey=o7wfi9rc3a2707yvq6ap5ahnc&st=7565jpe1&dl=0 Watch the Trailers: Reveal Trailer Story Trailer Physical Trailer Kickstarter Launch Trailer What YouTubers Are Saying Maiden of the Dark has been previewed and played by GameCube Galaxy.

description: About Blocksworld Welcome to Blocksworld, a fun 3D-building game for the whole family! Drag and snap building blocks together to make anything from cute characters to mighty machines. Blocksworld is a lighthearted build-and-play system for kids and grownups alike that brings the imaginative play of toy blocks to your gaming device, allowing you to bring your digital creations to life. Snap together colorful 3D blocks or apply "action-blocks" to create anything you can imagine - from crazy characters to drivable cars, space rockets and planes, animals, tanks and giant robots, monsters and dinosaurs, and much more - and then bring your creations to life and play with them! Simple drag-and-drop controls make it easy to add interactivity and animations to anything you make. Press or tap "Play" and get ready to smile as your creations walk and talk, jump, drive, fly, shoot lasers, fire rockets, or even explode. CREATE anything you can imagine with easy-to-pick-up creation tools that offer a remarkable amount of possibilities: snap-together resizable 3D blocks, including more than 200 different shapes, colors, characters, and more! PLAY with what you’ve built or any of the creations other Blocksworld users around the world have shared with the community. LEARN. The building blocks is encourages 3D modeling and understanding of the action-blocks facilitates game design and programming. SHARE. Share your own creations with the Blocksworld community and earn a place on the leaderboards and visit and admire other players' challenges and constructs in shared virtual showcases. Technology The Leading Platform for Children and Young Adults 3D Player-Generated Content At its core, Blocksworld is a visual programming language that is very accessible and same time very powerful enough to bring your creation live on any platform. You can build a live dinosaur, transform robots, and player-controlled mini-games, your imagination is only a limit. We are encouraging your curiosity to learn new building amd game creation techniques using action-blocks, no-code visual programming language. The Goal Our goal of $25,000 will allow us to bring back Blocksworld to the original platform - Apple iOS and add some online functionality. To cover the release on the other platforms we need stretch goals, they are listed below. The build will be delivered in premium format (via digital download key) with infinite free blocks including existing premium sets and modified previously known as "licensed" blocks. We will also include unreleased blocks, textures, and sets that exist in the game and were turned off for some reason once have stable revival builds.  Depending on the reward tier, we will offer early access to the builds via Apple Testflight (on the appropriate way for other platforms).  We also would like to highlight backers in the game and reward them with exclusive Blocksters and its cosmetic items, offer future discounts for DLC, licensed blocks, premium subscriptions, etc., and have more surprise offers for the early backers. Stretch Goals We are hoping to exceed our goal with the help of the community and in order to bring you an exciting experience. When our stretch goals are reached, prepare yourself for what is to come including new platforms, Spatial and Mixed Reality, multiplayer, and more: Valve Steam revival and full Steam Deck support (Don't forget to wishlist Blocksword page ) Release to the community an archive of legacy art files and videos with old logos, mockups, etc. All existing in-game tutorials return to the game New platform - Google Android Play Store New platform - Apple Mac Store Old worlds and models are accessible so you can claim your old worlds - the community's most requested feature. There are 38M user-generated worlds and 3M+ user-created models and bringing back the old world requires much more effort, which is why it is the stretch goal. Unlocked unreleased blocks and assets that exist in-game to be included in the game for all platforms Steam ARM CPU support - because is a thing! We believe in the future of spatial computing and Mixed Reality and we want Blocksworld to play a special role there. Check out the Meta Quest 3 previews of the very early prototype what we are building: New platforms - VR / MR support - Meta Quest 2/Pro/3 New platform - Apple Vision Pro spatial computing platform Added TiltFive - holographic display support to the Steam version Better in-game help and documentation More integrated in-game tutorials New platforms - Nintendo Switch/Switch2 Highly curated Switch-only server (because we want kids to be safe) Better video-sharing options for vertical videos/reels and other formats for YouTube, Instagram and TikTok Even More integrated in-game tutorials Peer to Peer multiplayer support for building - Yes. you could connect with each other and build together! Moddable Steam version - community's second most requested feature (use your old tunes, old menu, old-fashioned colors, The Old Sky from 2013-2017, whatever) Yes, we want to port Blocksworld so you can play the game inside Roblox! Here is the deal: Roblox has many features we would like to see in Blocksworld: collaborative interactions, voice chat, monetization, and most importantly - a huge kids audience. But this audience is mostly browsing content, not creating new worlds (as it's pretty hard) - we want to encourage them to create and learn programming with friends and immediately see the results. New platform - Roblox ! Legacy (early) BoldAI Blocksworld playable builds - a highly requested feature by the community. It takes some effort to bring them to modern devices but we will do our best if we reach that goal. New Integrated music and sound synth - so you can compose and share worlds with your own melodies Procedural Vehicle Engine technology - cars and mechanisms were a huge part of Blocksworld and having a variety of engine sounds is very important New platform - Sony PlayStation + PSVR2 New platform - Microsoft Xbox New Storefront - Epic Game Store New Storefront - GOG More play modes - not only mini-games. We will keep those as a surprise! Lots of new sets, blocks, and features - will be revealed once we reach the goal. Why we need your help? We have a lot of experience developing and porting games across platforms and operating games in different mediums: from 2D to Virtual Reality but to be completely honest, revival wasn't an original plan for Blocksworld IP and the technology we acquired. However, the idea of the revival Kickstarter was warmly met by the Blocksworld community, so we need your help here, the fans and members of the community: our relationship with you can be closer than ever with Kickstarter and we could attract a new audience, who had never seen the game back then. We want to ensure your voice is heard and we deliver on what you want. Not only can you help us make this game a reality but you can play a role in shaping the ongoing development. Blocksworld is more than a game, it's a UGC (User Generated Content) platform and has to be moderated to be safe for everyone, especially children. When we reach our target goal we’ll invest every dollar raised into the revival of Blocksworld to update current platforms, add new platforms, improve first-time user experience, and implement hot community requests. We have a lot of things planned to keep you informed so stay tuned! The Developers Fortell Games, Inc. is a new company formed by experienced game industry veterans (ex-Beatshapers), based in Santa Monica, CA with a research and development studio in Kyiv, Ukraine. Here is selected works below: Merch Store Our rewards are exclusively digital but made an official Blocksworld merchandise store . There are several designs to choose from for the true fans. Congratulations on reaching this far ❤! We deeply appreciate your interest in Blocksworld. Your support means the world to us, and we're eagerly looking forward to turning our dream game into a reality for you to enjoy. Social media Unable to donate, but still want to help us?  Spread the word! Please share/like this page on Twitter/X, Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, Facebook, or any social media platform you prefer. Wishlist on Steam Please visit and wishlist Blocksword page on Steam, this will help us to gain visibility on the Steam platform.

title: Pursue: A Strategy Board Game w/ Chance and Trivia!
description: I love games and knowledge, and I wanted to create something fun that could bring people together post the craziness of 2020.  I really connected well with new friends by playing chess with them and talking about what news we all saw at one of the few bar/restaurants still open. We questioned the world while having fun....we researched things, we learned new facts, and we battled for a winner on the games we played. I love trivia and strategy.  I think a healthy mind seeks knowledge, and that the pursuit of wisdom should be an unending quest.  I think the greatest games embody this, and combine it with variables for which a player cannot always account. Additionally, I think the best games are easy to learn, but hard to master.  "Pursue" is designed and explained in the rules book so that it can be opened, understood, setup, and started in about 10 minutes.  Anyone aged 13 and older should be able to start having fun quickly the first time they play, and then want to play it again and again. In my game "Pursue",  I have created a hexagonal board made up of 91 hexagons.  I combined this with three different colored dice (1 for the number of cards to draw to start a round, 1 for the bonus color for the round, and 1 for the number of turns in the round), and added two decks of 4 color suited cards that offer bonus game movements and provide Trivia conjointly with QR codes to cite sources.  The tokens on the board fall into 2 categories: player tokens and money tokens.  The 7 player tokens, which remain on the board at all times, improve in capabilities and movements, while the money tokens are utilized to stymie one's opponent. While the mechanics of the game remain the same, the library of knowledge in Trivia can and should grow.  The first two decks of cards cover the following topics:  1. US Government Programs, 2. Conspiracies, 3. Read, Seen, or Heard, 4. Jesus, 5. Big Pharma, 6. Experiments, 7. Money, and 8. Charities.  As the game grows, I plan to release more decks of cards with new topics...1 deck which is already completed! So what is the goal of this Kickstarter?  I want to grow this game as a company, share the joy of a new fun game, and deliver information and knowledge to people to share with their friends while connected to them in person...in real life...not on a social media platform, or a cold computer screen.  With the goal met, I will be able to order at least 500 copies of the game, and begin to sell them online, direct to consumer at www.PursueYourGame.com, in person at festivals, open market places, and perhaps one day in stores too. Thank you for reading my story.  I welcome your questions, and I hope you will join me on this journey with a pledge!

title: Categories
description: Thank you so much for your interest in this project. Chupi Goes Cowtipping is the first of a series of casual games that star the characters from my online comic strip Robots-N-Aliens .  In this game Chupi is sent to investigate cows.  We found out in the comic strip that Chupi is actually vegetarian so instead of eating the cows he does the next logical thing.  He tips them.  Some earth folk told me that there is no such thing as cow tipping. Chupi and I beg to differ. The first version of this game is directed towards the iPad.  Once this version is complete I will have the codebase to create the game for other devices. I'll be able to finish the graphics and programming, set up the leader board and find music. Not to mention we can prove all dem earth folk wrong! I will be able to focus and get the game completed in the next couple of months.  I have a personal goal of the game being complete before I have to give my sister a kidney.  We're not sure when that is going to be, but it would be nice to have this project out the door by then.  I will, however, be ready to work on the rewards as soon as funding is achieved and sending them out as soon as they are ready.   I will be diligent in keeping you informed.

title: Categories
description: CARDS: I Think Therefore I Question... I Think Therefore I Question is more than just words on paper. It's more than manufactured questions generated by co-workers in a think tank. This deck is an expression my cumulative lifetime of experiences, interactions, and friendships amassed on a beautifully designed deck of cards. 60 cards, with 60 thoughtful if not eccentric questions crafted to mine entertaining, unexpected answers, interesting conversations, and a whole lot of fun. And just for the record: the questions in this deck are thoughtful and sincere. I don't move through the world presenting crass topics in the interest of shock value. Enter with an open spirit and you will learn a little something about yourself, too. Received final prototypes in the mail today. The update is more elegant and we've included a color coding system that will facilitate another level of play. "When somebody tells you about a past life regression or fortune teller experience, what runs through your mind? Are you interested in exploring the supernatural? " Have you ever asked yourself, “Is it just me?” when it comes to the way you think or feel about things? That is where this deck comes in. Standard icebreakers like “If you were ice cream, what flavor would you be?” have their place in the world, but not in this deck. There is no reason why every encounter, even the short ones, shouldn't bring you a little closer to the meaning of life, or at least closer to the human you are speaking with.  A good question guides conversations towards mutual illumination. I've focused this deck on human experiences to garner dialogue about the choices we make and why,  to illuminate what shapes us, and to highlight our intersections. Think of this deck as a facilitator who makes you a student of someone else’s life. This question deck liberates you to ask questions you wouldn't normally ask, and to do so with bold confidence because you are the messenger, and the questions flow through you. You can make a genuine effort by asking deep and thoughtful questions. People appreciate and admire initiative. Let's spread the good habit of meaningful conversation. "What value is inherent in a question?" You have to understand somebody in order to connect with them. Is there anything in my life that I am so certain of that I can not doubt away? I've been at this question thing quite awhile. When I graduated high school, my best friend's mother casually said, “I’m going to miss you. You ask interesting questions.”  Legend has it that when I was born, slapped, and laid on my mother's chest, I looked up at her and asked, "Mom, which was the biggest moment for you: finding out you were pregnant with me, or birthing me today?" Only a few questions away from shortening your distance from another person Thank you... ...for checking out my project. I'm thrilled to invite you to join me in realizing I Think Therefore I Question .  I feel that my life as an inquisitive spirit has been leading up to this moment - I've wanted to create something like this for a very long time. Our production budget is $15,000. Manufacturing requires volume in order to get a product to market at a reasonable retail price. So, my target production is 1,500 decks. In addition to printing, Kickstarter funds will also be used to pay for the illustrating talents of Nery Orellana, packaging, and shipping. There is a small contingency in the budget to cover any last minute hiccups. After reviewing samples from many manufacturers, I am happy to be working with Shuffled Ink . They are proudly family owned & operated since 1999, and have produced millions of decks of custom playing cards, board games, and tarot cards for individuals and companies around the world. They have production facilities in both Orlando, Florida, and Nantong, China. They are attentive and friendly, and follow up on their promises.  I was seeking an environmentally-friendly ink product, so I asked Charles (my contact and owner of Shuffled Ink ) if we could use a soy ink instead of the usual petroleum-based one. Charles went out of his way for a couple of weeks to do the sourcing to make that possible. Their products are certified safe and are high quality. I love how this deck looks and feels. Please join me in realizing a vision to create great dialogue and delve deeper into what unites us.  You are just a few questions away from a deeper understanding of what makes us all human. Are we, as human beings, driven to create meaning in our lives? HOW TO USE THIS DECK. Each deck will contain 60 cards. The subjects and tone are random. Some cards will ask a specific question like: "How you ever been swindled by anybody? If so, how did that experience shape you moving forward?" Other cards will contain a thought or a story. Just roll with both styles. Here are options for using the cards: Bring the deck to dinner parties, or host dinner parties yourself. Use the cards to get one conversation going around the table. Take it to conventions. Pluck a random card to use as an opener in a business Zoom meeting. Whip out a card in an elevator or in line at the market. Give a single card to someone you'd like to meet at a cafe, along with a cup of coffee. Memorize your favorites and feel confident at parties. Just one card is good for numerous gatherings. Take them on a long flight to make good use of that time. Be bold: just walk up to somebody and ask, "Can I ask you a question?" Many of the deck's questions are comprised of stories, so it makes good beach reading--and, you never know who you are going to meet! Beautifully-designed items always make good gifts. Give this deck along with a notebook to enliven your parents' or grandparents' stories. The cards work well with friends, family, business colleagues, and strangers. ADDITIONAL PLAY SUGGESTIONS . There are 10 actionable cards in this deck that have single words on them. Shuffle these cards and lay them facedown on the table. Each player is invited to pick one card. At any point in the game they are welcome to play that card. Safe - This card gives you a chance to answer without group contribution. Share - This card lets you call anyone to answer question. Pass - This card lets you pass the question to the next player (but you have to explain why you're passing it). Next - This card allows you to return the card to the middle of deck and pick another. Elaborate - This card lets you force deeper answers by laying this card down in front of an individual. Community - Lay this card down to get the group to discuss. Black - This card asks everyone to write down a secret and put it in a pile to be read when game ends. Each player randomly picks one of the action cards. The card you draw may not be the card you think you would've chosen on your own, but trust that it’s the card that will serve you best. The black card remains in the shuffled deck. U THINK THEREFORE U RULE: There are no rules how to play the deck. Once you are familiar with the deck you will come up with your own methods of play. I would strongly suggest that you don't allow single-word answers.  Address short responses with the wondrous tools, "Why?," "Why not?," and "How?". HERE'S THE THING. Questions are magical, but you have to cast the spell by employing them. WHAT'S THE BIG TAKEAWAY HERE? “Have fun always.” “Whoever said talk is cheap was not conversing with you.” - Founder, Cooper Bates IN CLOSING The idea has been claimed, the platform created, the game designed, the art realized, prototypes received, friends consulted, and game played. We now need backers, believers, and support to bring I Think Therefore I Question to life. I've included a handy pie chart of the budget. I am asking for support to cover, illustration and design, website, printing and shipping. I am not taking a fee. THE SPECS Each deck will come in a sturdy two-piece box. Each box will contain 60 question cards, an additional 10 action cards, and one instruction card. I've specifically chosen this card stock for the best combination of durability along with the texture. Card size: 3.5 x 5 inches Card stock: heavy 340 gsm, linen finish Ink: non-petroleum, from soy Box: rigid, two-piece box with semi-gloss finish FAQ Q. How Much is Shipping? A. We have found a fulfillment center to handle the bulk of the shipping and handling labor and thus have determined the following shipping costs as “Ballpark” numbers.  I will do me best to insure reasonable rates. United States. 9.00 + 2.50 per additional deck Canada $12.00 +$2.50 per additional deck Europe $13.00 $2.50 per additional deck Asia $13.00 $2.50 per additional deck Australia $13.00 $2.50 per additional deck Q. Are there categories or themes for this deck? A. Short answer: No. Long answer: When we engage in conversation with friends or new acquaintances, we don't naturally gravitate towards themes or categories. A good conversation is organic and not organized around a pre-designed theme. Card to card, this deck is full of the same surprises, and moves from one topic to another. Q. How many cards are in a deck? A. There are 60 question cards. There are also ten surprise action cards and one intro card, which equal a total of 71 cards. Q. Will you be selling the deck anywhere besides Kickstarter? A. Once the kickstarter campaign ends, we will be selling the deck on our website: IThinkCards.com. We're negotiating with various distributors and boutique retailers. Kickstarter pricing will not apply. Q. Can I play this game with my 10 year-old? A. We do not consider any of the questions in this deck to be vulgar or racy, but we would recommend a PG rating or ages 14 and up. Q. Do you have more questions or decks coming out in the future? A. Yes, we plan on releasing expansion decks and other games as well.  In fact, we already have two more decks, a board game, and other surprises in queue. Q. Do you really think another conversation deck is what the world needs right now? A. The mission of every question in this deck is simple: to create connection. Connection is love. And what does the world need now?:  " ...love, sweet love. " Q. Will this deck cure cancer? A. Highly unlikely, but you might someday. FIRST KICKSTARTER? Glad you are visiting. This is an amazing platform and it's so easy to support. Scroll through the awards on the top right side. Pick an award level. The amount will go into queue on your credit card, which will only be charged if we have met our fundraising goal by the time the campaign ends. At that point, if we have succeeded in meeting our goal, we will move right into production. A few months later, you will get a your reward(s) in the mail. REACTIONS FROM OTHERS "For me, the questions are a gateway to moral dilemmas and situations that hone in on who I am and how complex life and choices can be, as well as a chance to discover the other person(s) way of thinking about life. These cards provide ‘food for thought’ for someone open to self-discovery and a great way to discover someone else. " Birgitte Hellsten "This deck actually erodes my shyness. I actually look forward to talking with people." - Tracey Lynn R.N. "This is so you. Like a card deck that gets to holds a part of your inquisitive spirit." - Grace Kim, business strategy consultant. "These questions make me think about things I've never thought about before" - Josie, student, UCSB "You say that they are conversation cards, but the cards elicit philosophical, emotional, and personal conversations through introspection, deep listening, and patience.  These cards are about so much more than conversation." Rachel Hedgepath.  Educator "This is pure, unadulterated Cooper. You've given us a chance to dream of having the magic you have." - Claudia Vizcarra, communications director High quality vinyl stickers. Sticks to many surfaces.  Remove easily without residue. MADE POSSIBLE BY Creator - My name is Cooper Bates. I'm a writer, entrepreneur, and storyteller, who is deeply fascinated with people and our human condition. I co-founded the world's first designer breath mint company: www.hintmint.com , and currently tour my award wining solo show, blackwheniwasaboy.com . Nery Orellana - A versatile graphic designer and artist with over 30 years of experience who lives and works in Los Angeles, California. Born in Guatemala’s Caribbean coast, Nery discovered his passion for art and illustration as a child when his 3rd Grade teacher neglected to teach anything but art for the whole year. Nery believes that interesting things happen when the creative impulse is cultivated with curiosity, freedom and intensity. Trained and worked with legendary designer Saul Bass for five years, Nery has worked in a range of art forms, including painting, mural making, illustration, ceramics, block printing, and photography. See www.neryorellana.com . Joe Nankin - Music Video and Commercial Director in California whose work includes videos for M83, Baths, Purity Ring and The xx. See http://www.joenankin.com . Niazayre Bates - Scenic artist and costumer, recently graduated from Ithaca College with a BFA in Theatrical Production & Design.  Contributed to this project as lead graphic designer and editor of the Kickstarter page. See niazayre.com . Lina Parades - Deck Editior - Lina is an independent Philanthropic/Nonprofit Consultant and Certified Professional Coach Feedback Advisors/Video Cast/Photography Subjects - Hulah Al-Khamis, Niazayre Bates, Zayden Bates, Salil Bhayani, Una Blue, Shelley Cline, Harley Cross, Josie Bakken, Greg and Rachel Hedgepath, Birgitte Hellsten, Shauna Howard, Jessica and Kamala Horwitz, Owen Maher, Sandra McNeill, Emile Mojave, Amber Vicari, Claudia Vizcarra, and so many others. Thank you for always being open to advise me, console me, and put up with my dumb questions to get to the gold.

title: Casey Page's First Album in 20 Years
description: My Story Hi friends, I'm Casey Page. 👋 🌼 Over twenty years ago, I was a teenager with a dream and a guitar, releasing my first album and playing over 100 shows across the US. My dad was a folk musician who told me that great artists write their own songs - so that's exactly what I set out to do. Singing my songs in the early years. 🎶 My plan was simple: move to Nashville and make it as a singer/songwriter. Well, I did move to Nashville...only to return home two years later, feeling defeated. Life took some winding turns after that. I became a high school English teacher (that lasted a year), went to seminary, and eventually found my calling in ministry as a worship pastor. But through all these changes, songwriting never left my heart. Writing a song - crafting a melody and lyrics seemingly out of nowhere - remained one of the greatest thrills of my life. A few years ago, something shifted. I joined an online songwriting community called Artist And , started writing again, and found the courage to perform for audiences in Southwest Florida and Nashville. My confidence as an artist slowly returned. Playing live shows in the past few years. 🎶 Two years ago, I decided it was finally time to make a new album. My first album in twenty years. For the longest time, I wondered what my music would sound like now, as a thirty-something. What stories and themes would I write about today? What kind of artist would I be after twenty years of life? Well, I finally found out. I now have seven carefully-curated songs that represent my life journey and express the musical style that's been waiting in my bones for years. These songs come from someone who's learned that dreams don't ever really die - they just wait for you to be ready to pick them up again. I can't wait to share these songs and stories with you. With your support, this music won’t be just mine - but it will be our creation, together. Here's an acoustic preview of "New Tree Growin' from the Old, Dead Root" – the full band version will be released in the Spring of 2026. About This Project This album traces the journey of a human life: birth, childhood, growing up, discovering who you are, loving, losing, and looking ahead to a life beyond. The songs are written from my experience, but they explore themes that exist in all our stories. My hope is that they will be good companions as you navigate your own unique path. Three of the songs were inspired by my dad, Bruce, who passed away in 2022 from Lewy Body Dementia. Pictures of my dad Bruce, my mom Marcy, and me over the years. 💛 My dad loved all kinds of music - everything from Bob Dylan to Phantom of the Opera to Chopin. He started accompanying me on the piano when I was four, me standing on the piano bench beside him, belting out Amy Grant songs. He taught me to play guitar and encouraged me to start writing songs when I was thirteen. In my early performing years, he and my mom drove me to gigs from Virginia to Texas, cheering me on all along the way. With this album, I am finally releasing my voice…but I’m also honoring his. Here I am playing it like me, Dad. Love you. The Music I call my style warm and wild indie-folk. 🌱 My sound blends folk, Americana, and rock, with Celtic and orchestral notes. Think Alanis Morissette meets Mumford & Sons meets Fleetwood Mac, with layered harmonies reminiscent of Enya. You can trace my musical sensibilities back to the music that I was surrounded with growing up: Peter Paul & Mary, the Beatles, Amy Grant, old show tunes by George Gershwin and Irving Berlin, broadway musicals, Disney movies, and classical piano music. I've already recorded my vocals and guitar with producer Cheyenne Medders in his forest studio in North Georgia. With your support, we'll bring in session musicians to give each song its full arrangement. Recording my vocals and guitar with producer Cheyenne Medders in North Georgia. 🌳 These songs are ready to find their complete voice—they just need the right musicians and finishing touches to help tell their stories. How You Can Help Here's how we can bring this album to life together: The $8,500 goal covers session musicians, mixing, and mastering - the final steps to give these songs their full sound. This also includes reward fulfillment costs and Kickstarter's platform fees. Budget: Producer - $2550 Session Musicians - $3000 Mastering - $800 T-shirts, tote bags, and other rewards and shipping materials - approx. $1000 Kickstarter Fees - estimated $650 Taxes - estimated $500 TOTAL: $8,500 Our stretch goal of $10,500 would help cover CD manufacturing costs, which would make a huge difference. Release Plan Here's My Timeline: Recording session musicians, mixing, and mastering: January-April 2026 CD Production: Summer 2026 I’ll begin releasing the songs online in April 2026 using the “waterfall method.” This means that I will release the songs as singles on all the streaming platforms one at a time, giving 6-8 weeks of space for each song to be heard and shared before the next one drops. As backers, you'll receive your digital downloads in May 2026. Physical CDs ship by August 2026. Meanwhile, everyone else will hear these songs as singles starting in April 2026, with the full album not releasing until May 2027. You get the complete musical experience a full year before the general public! All backers will receive an invitation to an exclusive livestream concert in late summer/early fall 2026 to celebrate the completion of this journey together. How Kickstarter Works Kickstarter is an all-or-nothing funding platform. This means if we don't reach $8,500 in 30 days, no one gets charged and I don't receive any money . But when we succeed together, we'll have everything needed to complete this album. I've created reward tiers that tell the story of this project —from digital downloads, limited-edition merch, and memorial tree plantings to personal Zoom sessions, custom songs, and house concerts. Each reward connects to themes from the album: memory, growth, connection, and coming back alive to your dreams. My illustration for "New Tree Growin' from the Old, Dead Root," our first single. Featured on limited-edition t-shirts, tote bags, stickers, and magnets. Want to contribute a different amount? You can pledge any dollar amount without selecting a reward using the 'Make a pledge without a reward' option above the reward tiers. For specific questions about this Kickstarter, feel free to reach out to me at: casey@caseypage.com Every contribution matters and is so appreciated. If you can't support financially, you can still help tremendously by sharing this campaign with friends or on social media. I’m so grateful that you would want to be part of bringing this music to life. Bonus Track & Stories Latest Single: "Don't Give Up On Yourself" My newest release – not on this album, but part of the journey. Written in the month after losing my dad, this song became my own reminder to keep reaching for my dreams. Behind the Album Podcast I'm sharing the stories behind each song – the memories, the moments that sparked them, and what they mean to me. Dive deeper into the music with live performances and hear how this album came to life. Listen on Spotify or watch the latest episode below. Let's Make This Album Together Twenty years ago, I thought my dream was over when I left Nashville. But here's what I’m learning: dead dreams have incredible ways of coming back alive. This album isn't just about making music; it's about living fully and authentically— what my dad would call playing your life like you. When you support this project, you're not just funding an album. You're joining a story about second chances, honoring the legacy of those who've loved us, and the power of community to make dreams real. Your tree planted in someone's memory, your name in the credits, your presence at a house concert—these aren't just rewards, they're ways you become part of this journey. We have 30 days to make this happen. Choose a reward that speaks to you, or pledge any amount that feels right. Every share, every conversation about this music, helps build the community that makes it possible. In May 2026, when you're listening to these songs for the first time, you'll know you helped bring them to life. And in late summer/early fall 2026, all backers will be invited to an exclusive livestream celebration where we'll enjoy the album together before the world hears it. We doing this together, and that makes all the difference. Thank you so much for being here. 💛 Casey

title: Categories
description: First of all, we'd like for you to listen in on how a couple of the songs are sounding! Here are some unmastered (rough-cut) song samples from the new album so you can get an idea of how things are shaping up already: "Loving Wish" "Put Down" Now, a note from Josh: This is exciting. Really, really exciting. The album is finally ready to be finished. And what a ride this has been... I started recording these songs in 2013 from my bedroom, but it didn't take long to realize they needed a full-band recording. The problem was that I couldn't get that sound in my bedroom. Something needed to improve. That's why it was a huge blessing when the Nehemiah Foundation for Cultural Renewal said they would record my next album, and at no cost to me. My previous obstacles when recording from my bedroom: 1. I have only one microphone to record with. 2. I don't own a drum set, nor a bass guitar. 3. My knowledge on audio mixing is limited. 4. My old computer can only handle so much; the poor guy has been worked to death. By recording at the Nehemiah Foundation, not only were ALL of those needs met, but I learned a lot of useful things, and I made some great friends. Michael and Justus manning the controls in the studio. I received a lot of extra help along the way. At certain points during recording, I would try to play a drum part, or a vocal take, and realize, "I can't do this!" And then someone would step in and play the part that I could not. Phil Hodges, a musician at the NFfCR, even arranged a string section for one of the songs! In that regard, In Clover is the most collaborative album I have ever made, and I'm incredibly happy about it. This is the album I have dreamed about making, because there were no limitations this time. I mean, we got to use a lap steel guitar. Seriously. My hope is that this album will not only be more accessible, but be something encouraging and emotionally helpful for the listener. In fact, that's my main goal for this entire project. I'm looking forward to hearing the final product, and I certainly hope that the music finds you well. Michael in his element. And a note from Michael, producer and president of the NFfCR: I met Josh about a year ago. Justus (our VP of Operations) kept telling me I had to hear this album, Practice Shots, by Fiery Crash. So after a bit of prodding from Justus (who couldn't stop listening to it), I finally checked it out. I told Justus that I liked it, and I felt like the band had made some really good recording and writing choices, and I was curious what they would be able to do with more recording options. Except Fiery Crash isn't really a band. It's basically just Josh Jackson. Upon further reflection, I realized why I was impressed. The record did have the intimate and personal "bedroom ballad" feel you would expect from a single singer-songwriter effort, but it had avoided the self-indulgence and emotional narrowness that plagues so many artistic products of a single mind. Recording on the Korg synthesizer. Let me reiterate how rare this is in a lone artist. When you are working by yourself, you have no one to give you any feedback—good or bad. There is no one shaking his head in the corner. Or nodding it. So it's very easy for singer-songwriters to succumb completely to either paralyzing insecurities (e.g., Elliott Smith) or an obnoxious lack of self-editing (e.g., Sufjan Stevens). Josh Jackson somehow avoided both extremes. How a single person was able to achieve such a feat, I didn't know. But I wanted to meet him. And work with him, if he would let us. So Justus and I agreed we should invite him to the studio for an initial meeting in preparation for a future project. "Great," said Justus. "So... you need to call his father." Oh, yeah. When I first met him, Josh was in high school . Having heard his music, I had completely forgotten he was of the age where his parents needed to approve of the kinds of people he associated with. So I called Jim, and immediately liked the Jacksons. Meeting his mother and sisters later just confirmed that even more. Good, solid southern people. I say all of this because so much of what we do here at the Foundation relies on communities. Art doesn't come from a vacuum. When Josh came to record at the Foundation, he lived with us, and ate with us, and played with my kids. We were already part of the same family. I knew that pretty much as soon as I talked to Jim. And that was an early indication that this could work. Anyway, let's get to the music. Recording on the log cabin's baby grand. Josh played nearly everything on this album, most of which was recorded in about a week, with finishing touches (and songs) added in a few additional trips. And unlike most of the artists (especially solo artists) I have ever worked with, Josh already had pretty much every lick and tone already planned—he told me exactly what he wanted a part to sound like, then he played it. Most of the time, he got what he wanted in a take or two. It was amazing to see. The mixing process was similar. I have never gotten such specific notes on mixing. Like he already had the whole song (sound and all) in his head, and he just needed me to click everything in place. The song book. And the songs. When we recorded the vocals for "Steeples," I had never heard the lyrics before.  When he sang, "I'm trying to answer you, dear brother of mine," I couldn't hold back the tears. I lowered my head below the monitoring window so Josh couldn't see me crying from the tracking room. I had never before heard a song like this. It effectively rebuked agnosticism, but it did so with a vulnerable tenderness—the kind of tenderness that rebukes more fiercely than any mere apologetic. It convicts the listener of both kinds of hard-heartedness—the unbeliever's hard-heartedness toward God, and the believer's hard-heartedness toward the unbeliever. Recording vocals. Not all of these songs are explicitly about religion or God or restrictively "Christian" things, though. And I really appreciate that. It is another sign of Josh's maturity. He writes about whatever is going on in his life as a Christian would write about it. It's real , in other words. Some of these songs are about teenage isolation and longing, as you would expect. Some are about girls, as you might further expect. But what you don't expect is the palpable absence of angst . These are the kinds of songs a teenager would write if he knew already what I found out much later in my life. I thought when I was a teen that I would feel more confident and be more sure as an adult. I found out later I was wrong—that the longing doesn't go away. Neither does the more than occasional feeling of isolation, embarrassment, and self-loathing. The only thing that goes away is that futile bitterness and vanity we like to call angst. The Seagull taking a break after a long day of recording. In those songs, like "Loving Wish," where he expresses his doubts and feelings of unworthiness, the lack of resolution is not despair. It is patience. I don't mean to say the songs are resigned, or even sedate. You listen to "The Divorce" and you feel that the zeal and fire of youth is still burning in Josh. But he isn't using up his fire to complain about his own circumstances. He's pleading with others. Pleading for the sake of others. Tracking drums. But he's also talking to himself—telling himself the right things (like in "Put Down," where he tells himself, "Put down the phone. It's okay to be alone."). It's this self-awareness without self-consciousness that really sets him apart. Honestly, I don't understand how Josh exists. Being with him is an easy pleasure. He is respectful, charming, kind-spirited, and patient. He has a dry sense of humor. He goes to bed early, rises early, and drinks milk every day. You would almost forget that he was a special kind of genius. All that said, I am very excited to share In Clover with the rest of the world. It is the product of a genuinely godly and disciplined young man with an extraordinary gift for music. It has been the utmost joy to work with and for him. A final note from Justus (watch out, he's really boring): Being the logistics guy, I'd like to break down a few of the nitty gritty details! First of all, be checking in with us, because we are currently designing the artwork for stickers, posters, and t-shirts , and we will be posting them here for you to check out! Trust me, they're looking super fly. (That's a thing, right?) Next, were you wondering what exactly we need $6,000 for? Good! I'd be happy to tell you. The proceeds of this kickstarter will fund the following: Mastering —the final polishing of an album! A really fantastic group of audio engineers called Chicago Mastering will make sure this album sounds great in every listening environment, make sure the tracks match in volume, and give the tones of each song a final polish. Duplication —It's not easy or cheap to create several hundred CDs from scratch, but our friends over at Disc Makers will press several hundred CDs (depending on how many y'all want!), print a design on the CDs, print the CD cases, and print the insert booklet (lyrics, liner notes, etc.). Distribution —Next we'll turn to our cohorts at CD Baby . They'll make sure this album gets the pro treatment, publishing it in its entirety to all those places you like to find music at, like iTunes, Spotify, and Amazon.com. Merchandise —We'll be ordering up a batch of stickers, posters, and t-shirts from some folks we have worked with before, and so far they have not let us down. The t-shirts will be extremely comfortable, 100% cotton American Apparel tees. Marketing —We want as many people to hear this amazing album as possible, and with your help, we can give this album the kind of push into the world it deserves. Shipping —We didn't want to bog you down with shipping details, so we just included all the shipping costs of this project in the goal amount. Enjoy a hassle-free checkout, and we'll make sure you get your stuff delivered to your door. Studio Costs —At the Nehemiah Foundation for Cultural Renewal , we are determined to never take a dime from the artists that work with us. We know the amount of time, sweat, and money they have already invested in their craft, and we wholeheartedly serve at no cost to them. That's why we recorded this album with Josh absolutely for free! However, if this project raises more than is needed to cover all of the above (and miscellaneous) costs, Josh has volunteered all the additional proceeds to be invested back into the Nehemiah Foundation. It's his way of maintaining our spirit and practice of paying forward.

title: Categories
description: Updates We've reached our all-or-nothing goal and hit our stretch goal of $12,500, so we'll mix an awesome extra bonus track available to everyone throw in an extra special sticker with a design by Sam Bartlett into every mailing It's not over yet! Many great rewards still available and any money raised will be a HUGE help for us. As you can see in our budget Update, our total project budget is $18,000 , so we're far from paying off the full cost of this endeavor. If we manage to make it to $15,000 or 300 backers , we'll find something extra special to do! Thank you *so* much. We're astounded, astonished, and humbled. More about the project.... The Stringrays is on the verge of releasing a full length album, Ticket to Nowhere. And we mean the BIG Stringrays... Rodney Miller aka New England Chestnut / NEA Master Fiddler / NH Artist Laureate (fiddle) Sam Bartlett aka Stuntologist-in-Chief (mandolin) Max Newman aka Nimble McNimblyfingers (guitar) Stuart Kenney aka Dr. Deep (upright bass and banjo) Mark “Pokey” Hellenberg aka the drumminest drummer what ever drummed (drums and, yes, banjo uke) We’ve worked for almost a year-and-a-half on this project, recording with folkie friend (and, uh, Grammy Award winning) engineer Charlie Pilzer in Takoma Park, MD.
