We have the tracks recorded, but it still needs to mixing, mastering, artwork, and printing. What's the music going to sound like? We are aiming to make your favorite album of the year – an album that you will treasure, that will make you feel things, and that could distract you enough to unwittingly drive cross-country if you left your car stereo on repeat. We wanted to create music that would capture the thrilling, spontaneous energy and joy we get playing with each other. We're almost there. What is the money used for? The money will be used to complete the mixing, mastering, and printing an economical first run of the recording. It will also help us secure some fabulous hand-drawn artwork from artist Ben Belcher, who illustrated our first recording as well as some graphic design to make it all come together. Any additional money with go into recouping the costs of recording in the studio, which will allow us to more quickly focus on our next project. Bonus Items Limited hand-numbered edition. The first 200 backers getting physical albums will receive a limited, hand-numbered edition in a special, first-edition package. This is personal music, so we thought this would be a nice way to showcase that. Bonus tracks. Probably the most exciting bonus item is two extra tracks which no one else buying this album later will have access to. The A-side is a Rodney tune called Discovery Bay which might as well be titled “Superfun x 10!” and the B-side is probably our most requested track ever -- Stuart’s banjo tune Wandering Soul -- done up in the biggest band sound we could muster. These tracks both have special somethings going on that you’ll just have to listen to discover. Sticker. We’ll give you a cute sticker with Stringrays art, perfect for water bottles and instrument cases. Other stuff. See the Rewards section. Stringrays Zine Issue #0, one-on-one sessions, call in to the band on-stage, a tune written AND recorded by the band for you or someone you know, etc. Errata Although the text of the $500 level says "(4 available)" we are actually making 5 available. If you have any questions, concerns, or want to share your enthusiasm, just contact stringraysmusic@gmail.com Can't beat that! (Thanks to Sonya Sones for the photos.)

title: Categories
description: I'm making a brand spankin' new record! My name is Kalyn Fay and I am an indigenous (Cherokee) folk musician from Tulsa, OK. I am working on finishing up my second, full-length record, Good Company, and I could really use your help . You see, I released my first record, Bible Belt, in 2016 with the help of family, friends, and Horton Records, a Tulsa-based non-profit label. I didn't know much of what I was doing then and it was first attempt at recording anything, much less an entire album. Since then, I have lived a lot of life, performed all over the U.S., and feel seasoned as a musician and artist. All of this combined means the record I am making is a very true depiction of me. THE MAKING OF THE RECORD This record was written over the course of 2016-2018 by yours truly. Good Company was influenced by the landscape of Oklahoma, the relational ties to the people in Oklahoma, and the need to leave and return to my home state. It is, for lack of better words, my love letter to the place I have known best. The record was recorded to tape in 3 days time at Fellowship Hall Sound, located in Little Rock, AR, and engineered (mixing and tracking) by Jason Weinheimer. The record is produced by Jesse Aycock, who also contributed guitar, piano, and vocals to the album. Other contributors to this record are John Fullbright, Jared Tyler, Cooper Waugh, Paddy Ryan, Bo Hallford, Lauren Barth, Rachel La Vonne, Matt Magerkurth, and Olivia McGraw. This album will be mastered by Carl Saff in Chicago, IL and released through Horton Records in Tulsa, OK. WHY IT'S IMPORTANT TO ME This record is important to me because, like I said before, is about Oklahoma and the people in it. This record was made with so much love, embedded in friendship, laughter, and kindness. I got to play and record with people I consider true and dear friends, people I love and cherish in a mighty way. It's a memory I hold dear and I hope you get to cherish, too. You might be thinking that these are all very normal, sentimental feelings to have after recording. That might be true, but this is especially poignant because, you see, I have finally moved away from my beloved Oklahoma, away from all my Tulsa friends and family. I am now residing in Arkansas, pursuing a graduate degree in Printmaking. So, that's why this is so important! It was made with friends and musicians who cared enough for me and my music to make this memory alongside me before I left. It was made in GOOD COMPANY . THE CRUCIAL PART: MONEY If you don't already know, recording is EXPENSIVE. The whole process. You've got studio fees, engineering fees, the cost of CD replication, paying the musicians, merchandise, press, etc. There are so many things that go into it and it all adds up. And, being completely honest with you all, I don't have enough of it on my own to finish this record , especially not while I am in grad school. I am willing to work for you and with you, though, to make this happen! $10,000 is a steep bill, so I have lots of rewards listed, ranging from digital downloads and unreleased tracks, to private shows and printmaking parties. Heck, you tell me what you want and we'll make it happen because I want this record to see the light of day and I hope you do, too. Rest assured that this record will be released in January of 2019 and all the money will go towards the musicians, the fees for studio and engineering, the replication costs, and press for the record. WHAT YOU CAN DO TO HELP Donate and share this link! Lastly, THANK YOU SO MUCH, FROM THE BOTTOM OF MY HEART. There aren't enough thank-you's in the world to tell you all how grateful I am for you all. - Kalyn Fay

title: Categories
description: J. S. Bach’s music is a pinnacle of Western Art, and his organ works are its crowning glory. This new Compact Disc takes you to the perfect locale to appreciate them: the Gothic majesty of the Princeton Chapel and the stunning IV / 137 rank N. P. Mander / E. M. Skinner pipe organ . University Organist Eric Plutz plays SEVEN  Bach masterworks with this ingenious artistic frame: One Prelude / Fantasia / Toccata & Fugue on each of the diatonic scale degrees: The Gamut of A – B – C – D – E – F and G , followed by a thrilling encore to create a full sonic experience. The full CD Program is Prelude  & Fugue in A major , BWV 536 Prelude & Fugue in B minor , BWV 544 Passacaglia & Fugue in C minor , BWV 582 Prelude & Fugue in D major , BWV 532 Prelude & Fugue in E minor ("Cathedral"), BWV 533 Toccata & Fugue in F major , BWV 540 Fantasia & Fugue G minor , BWV 542 ENCORE - Sinfonia from Cantata 29 (arr.  M. Dupre), BWV 29/1 Your donation to this project helps in three ways: Showcasing Eric’s mastery and honoring his long tenure at both Princeton University and the Westminster Choir College Covering his artist’s fee and the CD production costs Any additional monies raised will go to support Eric’s VIERNE PROJECT , his recording of the Six Organ Symphonies on noted organs around the US Personnel, Timeline and Costs In addition to Producer Mark Dirksen and the artist we are privileged to have John Baker and his Affetto Records team providing technical and marketing support.  John is also well-known in the Princeton community, and brings both not only many decades of recording expertise but also his own personal connection to the Princeton Chapel: As a boy he snuck into the Chapel during a recording session by no less than E. Power Biggs! He and Eric have collaborated regularly on recording there – no one knows the Chapel’s acoustics and instrument better. We will record the program as soon as the University permits access, and with luck and vigor hope to have the disc in your hands by Christmas 2020. Our goal of $13,500 represents the production costs of the discs (we are ordering 500) and includes an appropriate Artist’s Fee for Eric. A complete budget for B A C H The Gamut from A to G is available upon request , and Mark Dirksen welcomes your comments, questions and suggestions at BachGamutCD@gmail.com . Thank you for your support!

title: Categories
description: A Passion for the Planet I’m Geoff Hudson. I’m a composer and for the past two years I’ve been writing A Passion for the Planet , an hour-long work for chorus and orchestra that focuses on the pains and perils of climate change. Two years ago, I was reading an essay in which climate scientists were lamenting their difficulties in capturing the public’s attention. One of them was quoted as saying, “People won’t really respond to the climate crisis until they connect with it on an emotional level.” Geoffrey Hudson, composer of A Passion for the Planet And that made a lightbulb go on in my head. Music is a powerful way to form emotional connections. Perhaps we can harness that power to confront climate change. A Passion for the Planet starts in a place of beauty. The opening movement is a beautiful, spacious celebration of the wonders of creation. It moves from reverence, to gratitude, to revelry, before closing with some resonant “alleluias”. After that, things get darker, as the music looks at what is already happening all around us. That journey into darkness reaches a climax with a musical depiction of the infamous “hockey stick” graphs. Temperatures rise; oceans rise; extinction rates increase… The world flies out of control. What have you done with what was given you? And then a children’s choir stands up and sings words by the Irish poet Theo Dorgan, “What have you done with what was given you? What have you done with the blue, beautiful world?” It’s like an arrow into the listeners’ hearts. And that’s a turning point in this piece. I don’t want to leave listeners in that dark place. Over the last two years, when I tell people what I’m working on, a bunch of people have said, “Oh, you’re writing a requiem for the planet.” And I say, “No. It’s not time for that yet. We don’t have much time left – the IPCC report gave us twelve years – but it’s not over yet. We all need to roll up our sleeves and get serious about turning this around.” And A Passion for the Planet is about breaking through the defenses and avoidance mechanisms that people have built up around this horrific threat. conductor Tony Thornton The last three movements of the Passion revolve around hope, beginning with a wonderful thought from David Orr: “Hope is a verb with its sleeves rolled up.” The piece ends with everyone singing; performers and audience members join together to sing a chorale tune. We’re all in this together. It’s bigger any of us as individuals. The only way to succeed is for all of us to roll up our sleeves. baritone Dashon Burton I’m very fortunate in having some wonderful collaborators for the premiere. In June, Tony Thornton will be leading the Illuminati Vocal Arts Ensemble in the first performance of A Passion for the Planet . I’ve been at the early rehearsals; the beauty of their sound just slays me. We have two fabulous soloists, baritone Dashon Burton and soprano Alisa Pearson, and we’ll be joined by a chamber orchestra of 12 players. I’m so excited to hear the first performance. And if you’re in driving distance of western Massachusetts on June 15th, I urge you to join us. soprano Alisa Pearson The reason I’m here on Kickstarter today is to ask for your help in making a recording of this piece. Our goal is to get this music out into the world – to have groups across the US and internationally perform it. Having a good recording will be tremendously helpful in that process – allowing conductors to easily gauge what it is they’re signing up for. Please be generous. Budget Everything we raise towards our goal of $7,650 will go to fund the recording of A Passion for the Planet . The money will go to Hybrid Vigor Music, the Massachusetts-based not-for-profit that has championed A Passion for the Planet , which will use the money to pay the musicians, recording engineers, and Kickstarter. Anything raised beyond our goal will support our campaign to encourage other groups across the United States and internationally to perform A Passion for the Planet . Here’s what that budget looks like. The Timeline We have a recording date scheduled in June 2019. Post-production work will take place over the summer. We should have a finished recording by August 2019.

title: Categories
description: My name is Michael Chidester. I started HEMA in 2001, and since 2010 I've been leading a project called Wiktenauer . Wiktenauer grew out of a desire to set the text of our fencing manuals free from the page and put it into the hands of fencers. In 2019, I started a venture called HEMA Bookshelf with a different objective: to put the page, and the whole book, into your hands as well—specifically, the 1480s fencing treatise of Philippo di Vadi , titled De Arte Gladiatoria Dimicandi ("On the Art of Swordsmanship"), extensively illustrated and lavishly illuminated. Background The J. Paul Getty Museum's manuscript of Fior di Battaglia (left) alongside the HEMA Bookshelf facsimile (right). There’s something magical about holding a Medieval book in your hands. It’s a tangible piece of history produced by craftsmen of another age—parchmenters or papermakers, scribes or printers, rubricators, illuminators, tanners, smiths, metalworkers, and binders, each applying their arts to contribute to something that might be soon discarded, or might last for a thousand years (sometimes more). The content of such a book starts with what they wrote and drew in it, which tells us what the person who designed or ordered the book thought was important, but it goes far beyond that—we can learn about how it was intended to be used based on its size and shape and the materials used to create it, and we can learn how it was actually used based on the damage it suffered and the marks left by readers. Notes and doodles in the margins, pages pasted in or torn out, even the crayon scribbles of children—these tell us about the readers, and their relationship to the book. At various times, I have had the privilege of handling several 15th and 16th century fencing manuscripts, as well as copies of most of the printed fencing treatises published before 1600, and I wish everyone in HEMA could have that same chance. It's a way of connecting to history that's hard to describe. Comparing the Orbis Pictus facsimile of the Krakow Gladiatoria to the New Haven Gladiatoria at the Yale Center for British Art. In my facsimiles, I try to capture some of this magic and create something that is a bit more than just a leather-bound book. Each facsimile is a thoroughly-modern creation, made by 21st century artisans using 21st century techniques, but it was built based on something old and worn and full of stories. Furthermore, where manuscript facsimiles are generally quite expensive (from the Orbis Pictus Gladiatoria which cost ~$900, to a $19,000 facsimile of the Maciejowski Bible that I got to study in the Morgan Library's reading room once), my objective is to make these as inexpensive as possible so as many people as possible can access them. So far, I've done the Copenhagen Talhoffer manuscript , the Getty's Fiore manuscript , the Munich Lecküchner manuscript , the 1570 treatise of Joachim Meyer , the Bauman Fechtbuch , and the 1606 treatise of Salvator Fabris , with the Goliath Fechtbuch currently in production and the 1539 treatise of Fabian von Auerswald in the pipeline next. The first five HEMA Bookshelf facsimiles; left to right: Talhoffer, Bauman, Meyer, Fiore, and Lecküchner. A Vadi Facsimile For my eighth facsimile project, I want to do another illuminated manuscript, and I've chosen the treatise of Philippo di Vadi, which has been gaining in popularity over the past several years and has finally become available in high-resolution scans in the past year. The Biblioteca Nazionale Centrale "Vittoria Emanuele II" di Roma has graciously agreed to license the scans, so we're off to the races. Dedication to Guidobaldo, including the heraldry of Urbino and extensive, worn illumination (ff. Iv-1r). Vadi's treatise, titled De Arte Gladiatoria Dimicandi ("On the Art of Swordsmanship"), was dedicated to Guidobaldo da Montefeltro (1472-1508), the young Duke of Urbino, and first appears in a library catalog executed between 1482 and 1487. As a preteen and young teenager in this time period, Guidobaldo would have received fencing training as part of his education, and this timing suggests that Vadi may have been his fencing teacher. Armored sword and unarmored spear (ff. 27v-28r). The treatise consists of a dedication in Latin and Italian (ff. 1r-3v), 16 chapters of poetry in Italian outlining a theory of fencing (ff. 3v-14r), and a series of individual techniques of the unarmored two-handed sword, poleax and sword in armor, and unarmored spear and dagger (ff. 15r-42v). These each have a brief couplet describing them as well as a colored illustration with occasional illumination. Some techniques in this section are clearly copied from a ca. 1410 treatise of earlier fencing master Fiore de'i Liberi , but others are unique. The cover of the original Vadi manuscript as it currently exists in Rome. For this replica, the cleaned-up scans will be printed on paper coated to simulate the original parchment (sadly, using genuine parchment would increase the cost of the book by an order of magnitude) and sewn using the correct quire structure . It will be bound in brown kid skin with gold-foiled tooling to match the original. I will once again be turning to Grimm Book Bindery of Madison, WI to do the actual book construction. HEMA Bookshelf's Goliath Fight Book facsimile (left) compared to a photograph of the original manuscript's cover (right). (If you have any religious or ethical objections to animal leathers, Grimm has also done synthetic leather bindings for us in the past. Reach out after you place your order and I will make arrangements for you.) Add-on: Metallic inks (+$125) As with my first illuminated manuscript—the Getty Museum's manuscript of Fior di Battaglia—there is also an option to have the facsimile printed with gold and silver ink to fully capture the beauty of its original illuminations (which have been damaged and corroded over the centuries). Gold ink used in HEMA Bookshelf's Getty Fiore facsimile. The paper selection we're using this time should take the ink even better than the one we used for the Getty facsimile, for a truly beautiful result. Add-on: Commentary volume (+$60) For those interested in Vadi's teachings and not just his pretty book, I will be publishing a commentary volume to serve as a companion. Renowned scholar Greg Mele has offered a fully-revised version of his translation of the text, and I will be authoring a transcription to go with it. Several leading experts will also be contributing chapters, including not only Greg but Stephen Fratus, Connor Kemp-Cowell, J.D. Wiest, Guy Windsor, Isobel Zijlstra, and me, Michael Chidester. This will be substantially similar to the Talhoffer, Leckuechner, and Bauman commentary volumes that we've already released and the Goliath and Auerswald commentaries that we've announced and are in progress. I'm running out of standard linen color options for these (we've used red, green, and blue, Goliath is grey and Auerswald will be black), so I think this one will end up tan. Add-on: Complete Philippo Vadi Longsword Course with Guy Windsor (+$250) Take your understanding of Vadi's treatise from page to practice with this comprehensive digital course. As a special bonus to complement your facsimile edition, gain FULL ACCESS to Guy Windsor's authoritative Philippo Vadi Longsword Course (regular value: $530). This exclusive online training program transforms the historical text you'll hold in your hands into living martial arts knowledge. What You'll Receive: Lifetime access to 75+ detailed video lessons covering Vadi's complete longsword system Step-by-step instruction on all 25 longsword plays directly from the manuscript Expert guidance on footwork, guards, strikes, and tactical applications Downloadable training materials to support your practice Perfect for practitioners of all experience levels, from complete beginners to seasoned HEMA instructors. Guy Windsor's 25+ years of expertise brings clarity to Vadi's sophisticated 15th-century fighting system through methodical, accessible instruction. "The perfect complement to the facsimile edition—see the techniques on the page, then learn to execute them with precision and understanding under expert guidance." This perk seamlessly bridges historical scholarship with practical martial application, creating a complete Vadi experience that connects you to this remarkable 550-year martial tradition. Only available during the primary funding period. No shipping charges until the books are ready As a final note, given the chaos on the international stage at the moment, I'm not going to plan that shipping costs will remain static over the next nine months. The way things are going, I wouldn't be shocked if the USPS were privatized by then, so counting on cheap Media Mail rates for domestic book shipping might be right out. Shipping will not be collected as part of the crowdfunding campaign, and instead I'll calculate your shipping costs and bill you when the books are finished and ready to ship next January. This will result in more fair prices than if I try to estimate now without knowing the exact parameters of the book and have to add on extra to allow for price increases.

title: Mindful Wanders - A Children's Book About Travel
description: Hi everyone! Thank you for visiting our Kickstarter! This book has been in the works for quite some time and we are so thrilled to bring it to life and first officially share it with you through this platform! The story What if kids could learn at any age that their minds have a magical power called mindful wandering? The ability to recognize that at any moment they can use their imagination to take them to new places and spaces all around the world. Planting a seed for the future when one day they might choose to manifest and bring to life their own real travels there. Visualizing the beauty and feeling the excitement that exploring a new place can bring. When using your magical mind each and every new place is only a mindful dream away. Mindful Wanders: Let Your Magic Mind Wander and Travel the World was written to help children dream about and imagine traveling to different places through mindful wandering. A key element that your mind can take you here, your mind can take you there, your mind can take you anywhere and everywhere. Let the mind wonder as we wander through different cultures and dreamy highlights of exciting destinations around the world. This book is meant to be an escape from the ordinary, while in the end a reminder that beauty and fun are always found within you no matter where you are. The idea... The idea to write children’s books, particularly about travel, came to the author Michelle Zivkov, one day during her own real-life mindful wandering around the world adventures while visiting a night market in Bangkok, Thailand. As she explored the market, taking in all the sights and sounds, she noticed a cart with fried bugs on a stick. She thought to herself…’Oh, how my students would love to hear about this place and this experience!’. She could see the looks on their faces. That is when a lightbulb went off and the idea to write children’s books to share her adventures was born. During this time, Michelle was a second-grade teacher on a sabbatical of explorations and adventures around the globe. As an elementary school teacher, writing would often be modeled from the teacher’s viewpoint and her own experiences. Always an avid traveler, she was known to share stories with her students about her journeys to different faraway places and spaces. She hopes that through the story of Mindful Wanders, where she shares bits of inspiration from some of her favorite places visited, children can uncover their own dreams of travel and adventures. Planting the seed of imagination and exploration to help inspire children to explore the world and one day manifest their own travels through the visualization practice of mindful wandering. The inspiration... Many of the illustrations found in 'Mindful Wanders' were inspired by the author's own adventures and experiences from each destination as seen below. Are you curious about which places are highlighted in the book? Check out some of the sneak peeks below! Inside the book... Take a peek inside the book and wonder as you wander around the world with us! :) What the reviews say... The creators About the Author Michelle Zivkov is a former elementary school teacher with a passion for mindful adventures and travel. With a master's degree in early childhood education specializing in literacy and years of experience as a classroom teacher, she has always loved children's books. Based between California and Bali, but often found in Europe or other parts of the world, she has visited 48 countries and counting. Coming from a European family, travel has been an important part of her life for as long as she can remember. Also, a certified yoga instructor trained in Bali and India, she has spent significant time living abroad. These experiences have inspired her to share her passion for mindfulness, travel, and adventure with children around the world. Michelle, also known as @sunsetsnsunshine on her travel blog and Instagram, is seen here exploring the famous Nine Arches bridge in Sri Lanka. The author during her first visit to Bali, Indonesia, her favorite place of inspiration. The author, Michelle Zivkov, during an elementary school visit sharing a mindfulness program along with her first book, Mindful Wonders: A Book About Mindfulness Using the Wonders of Nature. About the Illustrator Maria Biktimirova aka Manka Kasha is a University teacher born and raised in Russia. She has a postgrad degree in English Language and Literature, and is reluctantly trying to work on her PhD in her free-from-drawing time. When she is not teaching her students (who are absolutely the best), or drawing, she prefers to read, listen to cast recordings of some musicals, or binge-watch TV-shows. (Also, be sure to check out her very own ‘Small Knight’ picture book series! ) Known for her dreamy illustrations, Manka shares loads of inspiration and magic on her Instagram @manka_kasha Past Projects Michelle and Manka's first book, Mindful Wonders: A Book About Mindfulness Using the Wonders of Nature is a guided meditation book written to inspire mindfulness and meditation in young children. Launched on Earth Day in 2020, the book was given a prestigious Kirkus Star review and chosen as one of the Top Indie Books of 2021 by Kirkus Reviews! 'Mindful Wonders' helps to calm the mind and connect readers to their breath and the beauty found throughout nature in the world around them. Using imaginative language and illustrations, this book encourages children to visualize and connect all the natural wonders of nature to their own inner peace and self-love. A favorite of many teachers and parents, this book not only helps to relax a child's mind but is great for readers of all ages. How you can help... Are you interested in helping us bring this book to life? Self-publishing a book is a huge endeavor. From when the idea first sparks to all of the costs associated with illustrations, editing, formatting, printing, shipping, marketing, professional reviews, and the list goes on...getting a book to market and especially getting it all done right can be quite the task. Check out the many ways to support this project as seen below for special offers only available through this Kickstarter! Thank you in advance for all of your support and love! Thank you!! A note from the author... Words cannot quite express how grateful I am to each and every person for their love and support along the way in my journey as an author. Thank you so much to everyone who has been following and supporting my work in one way or another. The why for this book, as well as the last, was to help inspire children and people everywhere. I hope that in the story of why and how this book came to life, you can find some inspiration to help me spread the joy of mindfulness, adventure, and travel! I hope you'll consider adding Mindful Wanders: Let Your Magic Mind Wander and Travel the World as a book in your home or classroom, and sharing information about it with others. If you'd like to back and help support this project, just click on the green "Back this Project" button and select one or more of the reward tiers. Thank you! Keep in touch! Instagram: @mindfulwondersbooks Facebook: www.facebook.com/mindfulwondersbook

title: JOHN CARTER OF MARS® THE AUDIO SERIES w/5e RPG & Woola Plush
description: Sign up for detailed updates at www.johncarterrises.com ! INTRODUCTION “A warrior may change his metal, but not his heart.” — saying on Barsoom (Mars) John Carter, science fiction’s original and greatest hero, returns in a multi-cast audio drama starring Sean Patrick Flanery of Young Indiana Jones and The Boondock Saints in the title role, and co-starring Wasé Chief (Kevin Costner's Horizon ), Tim Russ ( Star Trek: Voyager ), Alisha Mullally ( 13 Reasons Why ), Ray Porter ( Zack Snyder's Justice League ), Katy Manning ( Doctor Who ), Bruce Boxleitner ( Babylon 5, TRON ), and Ian McDiarmid (the Star Wars saga). We're also offering a role-playing game supplement compatible with both the Fifth Edition of the world's most popular RPG, OSR ("Old School Revival") rulesets, and Pathfinder Second Edition rules, a digital download of the audio drama, a double CD set with Frank Frazetta art, a folded map of Barsoom®, limited-edition novels published in English and native Barsoomian (Martian), and the first-ever stuffed plush of John Carter’s loyal companion, Woola ™! A MOVIE FOR THE MIND'S EYE Produced by Pocket Universe Productions and authorized by Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc. , John Carter of Mars®: The Audio Series is the first multi-cast dramatic audio adaptation of the science fiction novels by Edgar Rice Burroughs, the creator of Tarzan ®. The series will commence with the classic tale that started it all, A Princess of Mars ®, which Disney adapted into their 2012 feature film, John Carter . It tells of John Carter’s arrival on Mars (Barsoom) and his adventure with the strong, resourceful, and beautiful Martian princess, Dejah Thoris®. This is the John Carter you’ve been waiting for—a cinematic experience told in audio, with a cast of top-tier talent and lush soundscapes that will whisk you away to the red planet where you will be immersed in John Carter’s legendary adventures. It’s a movie for your ears—and your imagination. “We couldn't be more pleased to be working with Pocket Universe Productions to create a John Carter of Mars audio dramatic series,” says Jim Sullos, President of Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc. “Edgar Rice Burroughs' immortal hero has never before been adapted to this medium, and an incredibly talented team will be producing it.” THE CAST Sean Patrick Flanery , the star of Young Indiana Jones , The Boondock Saints , and the recent film Nefarious , among many other features and TV shows, plays the swashbuckling hero of John Carter of Mars: The Audio Series . He also serves as an Executive Producer. The incredible Wasé Chief will portray the Princess of Mars herself, Dejah Thoris . Wasé is an Oglala Lakota woman, model, actress, writer, and artist. She can currently be seen in Kevin Costner’s series of Horizon films . Legendary for his chilling portrayal of the Emperor in George Lucas's STAR WARS saga, Ian McDiarmid has thrilled audiences worldwide on stage and screen, winning the Tony and Olivier awards . He voices the Strange Man in the Atmosphere Factory , the mysterious engineer who teaches John Carter the deepest secrets of Mars. Bruce Boxleitner , Captain John Sheridan in Babylon 5 , Tron in TRON , and the CIA operative "Scarecrow" in Scarecrow and Mrs. King , brings his long and storied career to Mars to voice the role of Kantos Kan , the Red Martian soldier who becomes John Carter's comrade in battle. Tim Russ , well-known for his role as the Vulcan Tuvok on Star Trek: Voyager, stars as the Thark warrior and friend of John Carter, Tars Tarkas . His credits in film include Karma , 5th Passenger , Live Free or Die Hard , Spaceballs , and television series regular roles for The Highwayman , The People Next Door , Samantha Who , and iCarly . Alisha Mullally , who voices the kind yet fierce Sola , broke out as an actress playing the young Juliette Binoche in Bee Season . She has had a multifaceted career starring in hit shows like Netflix's 13 Reasons Why while also voicing roles in Pixar's Elemental, the animated series Transformers: EarthSpark , and even giving voice to Taylor Swift’s famous cat, Olivia. Currently, Alisha plays Ivy in this season of Chicago Fire . Katy Manning 's long career as an actress has spanned three countries over fifty-five years. While starring in the British ITV series Man at the Top , Katy was chosen from several hundred hopefuls to play Jo Grant in Doctor Who with the unforgettable Jon Pertwee as the Third Doctor. She plays the cruel Green Martian matriarch, Sarkoja. The amazingly versatile Ray Porter will lend his voice to the tyrannical Thark Tal Hajus . Ray has had a long career in film and TV, most recently playing Hickman in Rebel Moon and the DC Comics supervillain Darkseid in Zack Snyder's Justice League . A superstar in the world of audiobooks, Ray voiced multiple roles in Audible's award-winning The Sandman audio drama adaptation from writer-director Dirk Maggs. Shepherding you into the world of John Carter is our announcer Peter Berkrot . Best known for his work in Showtime's Brotherhood and the comedy classic Caddyshack , Peter has voiced nearly 700 audiobooks, in addition to being a regular VO contributor to Frontline on PBS. You've heard him in the audio dramas The X-Files: Cold Cases/Stolen Lives , Elfquest , and EC Comics Presents . . . The Vault of Horror ! Other cast names to be revealed in time - sign up for updates at johncarterrises.com ! THE LEGEND OF EDGAR RICE BURROUGHS “Edgar Rice Burroughs was, and is, the most influential writer, bar none, of our century.” – Ray Bradbury When Edgar Rice Burroughs wrote the John Carter of Mars novels, he invented a genre that inspired storytellers from comic creator Alex Raymond ( Flash Gordon ) to blockbuster filmmaker George Lucas ( Star Wars ) to contemporary authors like George R.R. Martin ( Game of Thrones ). Cherished by generations of readers (and gamers) around the world, Burroughs’ grand Martian saga continues to reverberate in the popular imagination and remains one of the most beloved properties in science fiction and fantasy. These adventures have been told in countless books, comics, games, and most recently in film with Disney’s 2012 feature John Carter —but now, for the first time ever, as a multi-cast audio drama. Stretch Goal #8 - $30,000: An original Tars Tarkas audio story by Matthew Stover (Revenge of the Sith novelization) read by Lance Roger Axt - REACHED ! Stretch Goal #9 - $35,000: Tars Tarkas portrait by Daren Bader* – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #10 - $40,000: Behind-the-scenes documentary – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #11 - $42,500: Midnight Matinees MP3: “The Horror of Walker Point” – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #12 - $45,000: John Carter portrait by Daren Bader* – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #13 - $47,500: Midnight Matinees MP3: "The Statement of Randolph Carter" – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #14 - $50,000: Michael Whelan bookmark for physical backers – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #15 - $55,000: " Edgar Rice Burroughs Speaks" audio piece, performed by Lance Roger Axt – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #16 - $60,000: Edgar Rice Burroughs autobiographical audio article, performed by Lance Roger Axt – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #17 - $65,000: Audio short story that expands world – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #18 - $70,000: Interview with Llana Jane Burroughs , ERB's great granddaughter, about her family's legacy – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #19 - $75,000: Edgar Rice Burroughs on how he became a writer , read by Lance Roger Axt – REACHED ! Stretch Goal #20 - $80,000: Michael Whelan A Princess of Mars art print for physical backers – REACHED ! *NOTE: The new and original portrait art by Daren Bader replaces classic art for the signed physical print pledges. REWARDS $?? - Pledge any amount to show your support and receive our gratitude! $25 - Digital Download of A Princess of Mars Audio Drama $39 - PDF-only RPG supplement $49 - (a) 5e/OSR/Pathfinder Second Edition RPG Supplement (print & PDF) OR (b) Double CD Set with Frank Frazetta cover $99 - A Princess of Mars Novel in Martian and English and a Foldable Map of Barsoom $99 - Double CD set, RPG supplement (print & PDF) and Foldable Map of Barsoom $149 - Woola Stuffed Plush and ALL Lower Tier Rewards $199 - Autographed Art of Dejah Thoris and Tars Tarkas by Starring Actors $229 - Early Bird Special - Sean Patrick Flanery Signed Art (ends Mon. 8/5 at 11:59 PM PST) $249 - Sean Patrick Flanery Autographed Art of John Carter $499 - Sponsoring Producer (credit in production) $999 - 30-minute Video Call with Cast and Crew (subject to talent availability) $2,499 - Have a Martian Character named after YOU $4.999 - Associate Producer (credit in production) $9,999 - Producer (credit in production) AUDIO DRAMA ON DIGITAL & CD The audio drama of A Princess of Mars is slated for a feature-length runtime. Stretch goals will expand it with surprise additional content. Available as a Digital Download and a limited-edition Double CD set featuring exclusive liner notes and the iconic Frank Frazetta artwork, courtesy of Frazetta Girls . ROLE-PLAYING ON MARS Have you ever wanted to take your role-playing campaign to Mars? In this limited edition 32-page book, John Carter, Warlord of Mars , gamemasters can lead their group to create their own legend on the red planet, encountering John Carter, Dejah Thoris, the Tharks, and the other characters and creatures of Barsoom. Art from the classic comics run of John Carter, Warlord of Mars illustrates the grand adventure! The RPG supplement has been developed for the Fifth Edition of "the world’s most popular role-playing game," Pathfinder® Second Edition , and also OSR ("Old School Revival") rulesets that make it easy to adapt for use in any game system. Guidelines will also be included on how to incorporate the supplement with the Modiphius John Carter of Mars: Adventures on the Dying World of Barsoom RPG system. A PDF will be included with all RPG pledges. We are also offering physical and digital bundles of Modiphius Entertainment’s John Carter of Mars: Adventures on the Dying World of Barsoom tabletop role-playing game as add-ons so you can expand your campaign with more lore and adventure! Don’t miss out—supplies of the Modiphius RPG are limited and will not be reprinted! A PRINCESS OF MARS NOVELS IN BARSOOMIAN AND ENGLISH AND MAP OF BARSOOM Recently our team made a grand discovery in John Carter’s tomb, finding a manuscript of A Princess of Mars printed in the Helium alphabet of Martian Barsoomian! Pledge and you will own a book that is sure to become a rare collectible, along with a matching English edition for Earth readers, and a folded poster map of Barsoom for geographers! (Covers and map not final.) To fill out your $99 pledge, chose either the Double CD set or the RPG supplement. PLUSH WOOLA Fans of John Carter of Mars have been dreaming of cuddling up with their own cute calot for decades. Dream no longer—now the adorably fierce Martian frog-dog is available as a stuffed plush for all ages! Working in cooperation with Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc., we took the original design from Edgar Rice Burroughs’ son, the artist John Coleman Burroughs, and gave it a modern update, perfect for an all-ages companion . AUTOGRAPHED PRINTS Want a treasured keepsake from the production? Sean Patrick Flanery will autograph a character portrait of John Carter by acclaimed fantasy artist Daren Bader . Wasé Chief (Dejah Thoris) and Tim Russ (Tars Tarkas) will also sign their character portraits by Daren Bader at the listed pledge levels. Check out Daren's study of Dejah below. (Art is a concept sketch and not final.) ADD-ONS After the campaign is over, we are planning to offer for purchase additional Woola plushes, books, maps, RPG supplements, and CD sets in the pledge manager! MODIPHIUS RPG BUNDLES In collaboration with Modiphius® Entertainment, we will be selling special bundles of their critically acclaimed role-playing game John Carter of Mars: Adventures on the Dying World of Barsoom , in both physical and digital format! Digital Bundle – $50: Core Rulebook, John Carter of Mars Quickstart, Supplemental Rulebook (Jeddak of Jeddaks Era), Supplemental Rulebook (Prince of Helium), Supplemental Rulebook (Dotar Sojat Era), Players Guide, Character Sheets, Barsoom and Korad Maps, Landscape and Location Card Deck, Character and Token Deck, and Map Tiles. Small Print Bundle – $60: Core Rulebook, Players Guide (print and PDF), Notebook, Phantoms of Mars Campaign (PDF), and Supplemental Rulebook (Prince of Helium). Big Print Bundle – $145: Core Rulebook, Players Guide, Toolkit, Notebook, Phantoms of Mars Campaign (PDF), Legacy Map, Location Deck, Character and Token Deck, one of each Dice Set, Map Tiles, and all PDF items in the Digital Bundle. Backers will be provided codes to receive bundles and pay for shipping of physical goods from the Modiphius web store after, and separate of, this Kickstarter . Note that physical copies of the game in the Small Bundles and Big Bundles are available in limited supply. Once they’re gone, they’re GONE. MEET THE TEAM An exceptional team of collaborators has come together to make John Carter of Mars®: The Audio Series a reality. LANCE ROGER AXT (Producer, Pocket Universe Productions) is the co-founder and Producing Director of Pocket Universe. Past productions have included The X-Files audio drama revival with David Duchovny and Gillian Anderson, and Locke & Key both for Audible Studios. He most recently produced and co-adapted an adaptation of the lost silent film London After Midnight . Check out the Pocket Universe website at pocketuniverseproductions.com . MICHAEL KOGGE (Writer/Director) is a screenwriter and #1 bestselling author. He has written projects for Lucasfilm, Warner Bros., HBO, and Oscar-nominated actors, and has two television series in active development. In addition to writing games for Disney Interactive, he penned the YA novels of the recent STAR WARS sequel trilogy, with his novel of The Last Jedi reaching #1 on the UK book charts. Find him online at michaelkogge.com or @michaelkogge on Twitter and @michaelkogge.bsky.social on BlueSky. JOSEPH FERRANTE (Producer) has served as a Worldwide Interactive Creative Manager at Warner Bros. where he led the execution of over 200 digital marketing campaigns, including Christopher Nolan's The Dark Knigh t and the Blade Runner DVD and Blu-Ray release. He is also a full-time novelist and screenwriter who has had several screenplays optioned. Find him on LinkedIn and on Instagram @josephferrantewriter . KELBY THWAITS (Co-Producer, Sound Production Supervisor) is a filmmaker, composer, sound designer and media artist with work in film, television, video games and live theatre. He has produced for Spike TV, SyFy Channel, VH1, MTV, Disney, Sony Online Entertainment, EA Games, Yahoo!, Funny or Die, Microsoft, Bungie Studios, and RED 5 Studios. He is also the Director of Graduate Programs in Film Television and New Media at Mount Saint Mary's University's Hollywood Studio Campus. Find him on LinkedIn and Instagram . CHARLES BUNCE (Co-Producer, Sound Designer) is a Los Angeles-based filmmaker recognized for his work on science fiction and fantasy films that captivate audiences with their imaginative storytelling. In addition to his work in film, he has recorded multiple albums for musical artists and is an AVID-certified Pro Tools instructor. Charles also serves as the Chair of the Film, Media & Communication Department at Mount Saint Mary’s University, where he mentors the next generation of creative talent. Find him on LinkedIn . JACK BOWMAN (Co-Producer) is an audio drama veteran with almost 20 years of experience. He has produced or directed notable titles such as The Springheel Saga and Audible UK’s Murder on the Orient Express . He also co-scripted the New York Times -bestselling Expeditionary Force: Homefront for Podium Publishing. Under his AudioMarvels® banner, Jack recently produced and directed the adaptation of the lost silent film London After Midnight and was a 2023 UK APA Audio Drama and Fiction Producer nominee. Find him on LinkedIn and audiomarvels.com . SEAN PATRICK FLANERY (Executive Producer, Actor) is an actor, writer, director, and published author who has been a leading man on big and small screens for the past three decades, starting with his starring role in the George Lucas television series The Young Indiana Jones Chronicles . After that breakout hit, Sean followed it up with mesmerizing performances in Powder , The Boondock Saints , Saw 3D , Dexter , The Boys , and Born a Champion . In 2016, Sean released his first novel, Jane Two , to wide critical acclaim. His recent directorial debut, Frank and Penelope , debuted at the prestigious Cannes Film Festival. Finally, for 2023, Sean reunited with writers/directors Chuck Konzelman and Cary Solomon for the hit film, Nefarious , in which he plays the title character. Find him @spflanery on Instagram or @seanflanery on Twitter. ZACHARY MARSH (Composer) is currently scoring projects for Disney, Tonies, and Yoto. His recent credits include the film guts which premiered at Sundance, the pilot Halfrican American which premiered at SXSW, the original series Pretzel and the Puppies (Apple TV+), and See Us Coming Together for Sesame Street (HBO Max). He is also the music director of Skyship Entertainment where he writes and produces songs for Super Simple Songs and produces the podcast Super Simple Imagination Time , which charted #1 in Kids and Family on Apple Podcasts. Find him on his website . MARSHALL DILLON (Art Director) is an accomplished art designer, letterer, editor, and writer with over 30 years of experience in the comic book industry. He has worked with top publishers and is known for his meticulous attention to detail and innovative storytelling techniques. He is thrilled to be art directing John Carter of Mars®: The Audio Series , blending his creative talents and passion for storytelling to bring this iconic saga to life in an immersive new format. Find him on LinkedIn . DAREN BADER (Illustrator) is a 30-year veteran of the video game industry, best known as the Art Director for the Red Dead Redemption franchise. He is also a long-time illustrator for Magic: The Gathering , Dungeons & Dragons , and other games, as well as numerous book covers in the fantasy and science fiction genres. Follow him on Instagram . HENRY G. FRANKE III (Consulting Producer) is a longstanding Edgar Rice Burroughs fan who has written about Burroughs and his work with book introductions, forewords, articles, and interviews. He has spoken about Burroughs at numerous conventions and fan gatherings. He has served as the Official Editor of the Edgar Rice Burroughs Amateur Press Association and as editor of the nonprofit literary society, The Burroughs Bibliophiles, which publishes The Burroughs Bulletin and The Gridley Wave . Henry is also the founder of Dark Sun Press. Find him on LinkedIn . GILBERT ISLA (RPG Lead Editor) has served in one capacity or another as writer, editor, consultant, system designer, playtester, and layout designer for many RPG companies dating from the late 1990s to the current day. White Wolf, Pelgrane Press, Goodman Games, Stiff Whiskers Press, Shield of Faith Studios, Bloat Games, Evil DM Productions, Cannon Otter Studios, Shinobi 27 Games, and Evil Hat are a few companies in his portfolio of works. STEVE BEAN (RPG Designer) has been designing, writing, developing, and publishing in the tabletop game industry for a dozen years. He is best known as a writer for the Dungeon Crawl Classics role-playing game, for which he has a score of professional credits. As a publisher, Steve has produced Fleet Action Jan-Ken , a one-page children's educational game that teaches World War II naval warfare strategy, and the fast-play car combat miniatures game AUTO-DESTRUCT-O-RAMA ! Steve has also written non-fiction articles for Modern War magazine, published by Strategy & Tactics Press. JOAN TROYER (RPG Designer) is from Goshen, Indiana. She enjoys running Dungeon Crawl Classics RPG and has two adventures in layout for publication by Thick Skull Publishing and Shield of Faith Studios. Joan has also authored articles for Gongfarmers Almanac 2018 & 2019. She serves on the Road Crew Counsel 2024 planning for the Goodman Games Road Crew 2025 with other writers, game designers, and GMs. JEFF MEJIA (RPG Designer) is from Northern California. He has been a gamer for the last 40 years and has written articles for Knights of the Dinner Table and Shadis Magazine. Under his own company, Dicey Tales Productions LLC, he has written and published Dicey Tales , an RPG for pulp adventures, his favorite genre. DAVIN KLUTTZ (RPG Cartographer) is a freelance graphic artist, illustrator, and product developer based in Portland, Oregon. He has contributed maps, character and creature art, scenes, trap diagrams, and more for countless game products, such as Dungeon Crawl Classics . Catch him on his motorcycle in the Pacific Northwest or follow his work on his website or on Instagram . ANNALEIGH NIEDZIELSKI (Social Media Manager) has produced award-winning independent short films, served as a videographer for ad agencies, and built audiences for her university client through social media engagement. On John Carter of Mars®: The Audio Series , Annaleigh worked with the team to create social media content and spread the word! Find her on LinkedIn . ALEXANDER JONES (Video Editor) is a freelance video editor and animator who has done VFX and animation for award-winning short films and worked as a videographer capturing engaging event. Find him on LinkedIn . EDGAR RICE BURROUGHS, INC. : From its headquarters in Tarzana, California, Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc., administers the intellectual property of American author Edgar Rice Burroughs (1875–1950), one of the twentieth century’s most prolific and enduring writers. Mr. Burroughs’ stories have been adapted into 60 films and 250 television episodes, and are featured in comic books, video games, apparel, and other merchandise. Through worldwide ownership of copyrights and trademarks, Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc., oversees authorized adaptations of his literary works in film, television, radio, publishing, theatrical stage productions, licensing, and merchandising. The company is owned by Mr. Burroughs’ heirs and continues to operate from offices the author built in 1927 on the site of his former Tarzana ranch in the San Fernando Valley. Sign up for detailed updates at www.johncarterrises.com ! LEGAL NOTICE Trademarks John Carter of Mars®, John Carter®, Dejah Thoris®, Tars Tarkas®, Woola™, Barsoom®, Warlord of Mars®, Gods of Mars®, A Princess of Mars®, Tarzan®, and Edgar Rice Burroughs® owned by Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc. and used by permission. All characters and creatures trademarked and owned by Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc. Frank Frazetta "A Princess of Mars" art courtesy of Frazetta Girls . Other art copyright Edgar Rice Burroughs, Inc.

title: Categories
description: Collectors are passionate people. We are hunters and gatherers of pop culture. We consume it -- no we devour it! Cool & Collected is a magazine that will showcase the items we seek and the people who seek them. The magazine will feature items that may have been on store shelves 20 years ago or more, but we'll also cover products that can be found in stores today. Having run the website Cool & Collected for several years, I have interacted daily with like-minded collectors. The collecting community is incredibly strong, and while Facebook and Twitter may be great for interacting, a printed (and downloadable) magazine will be a welcome addition to the world of pop culture collectors. We love stuff to put on our shelves, and you just can't do that with a blog post! What to expect The articles in the magazine will be informative and engaging, not only to hard-core collectors, but also to casual fans and collectors who might just be getting started. This will be a fun, family-friendly read that anyone can enjoy. Whether you are a completist who has to have every last variant ever produced, or a flea market warrior on the hunt for whatever strikes your fancy, Cool & Collected Magazine is sure to entertain you. The magazine will be an extension of the Cool & Collected website and will tap into its vast resources of fellow collectors and experts to create articles that will engage and entertain its readers. The premiere issue will feature 64 glossy pages, covering topics such as collectible Nintendo 64 games, Kenner Super Powers action figures, Hot Wheels Retro Entertainment Series, and more! The topics run the gamut from comic books, movie memorabilia, toys, vintage video games  -- everything a pop culture collector could possibly want! Who's behind this? Allow me to introduce myself... My name is Brian Adams, and I have been the man behind the curtains of Cool & Collected , an online destination for collectors of cool stuff. I have owned my own graphic design business for over 20 years and have been involved in the production of countless publications. A couple of years ago, I started a blogging community known as the League of Extraordinary Bloggers , which is an active community of bloggers who are also collectors. Through the website, and the League, I have come to know many amazing people and collectors. Cool & Collected magazine is a collaborative effort, with articles from pop culture experts such as: Howie Decker, Under Scoop Fire M. Carrigan, The Surfing Pizza John Sholtz, The Batcave Toy Room Rich Delligatti, The Nerd Nook Cody Mix, Crooked Ninja Turtle Gang Todd Sheffer, Hakes Americana & Collectibles ...and more! Future plans I have every hope that this magazine will grow into a regularly-produced publication with new issues coming out every two months or quarterly, but that all depends on how this first issue is received. So far, this has been an all-volunteer endeavor but it's not realistic to believe that will feasible for future issues. Hopefully, the response will be overwhelmingly positive and we will be able to secure funding avenues that will allow us to produce future issues. Consider this first issue a big toe being dipped in some cold dark waters. Future considerations will also be made towards publishing for e-readers in addition to the printed magazine. Our current approach will be to offer PDF downloads, which are easy to produce and distribute, but if we are successful, an electronic format other than a PDF might be considered for smart phone and tablet users. Stretch Goal! Thank you so much! With seven days to go, the project has reached its initial funding goal. In order to keep things moving in the right direction, we have added a stretch goal: If the funding level reaches $7,500.00, every backer who signed up to receive a copy of the magazine will also receive a free PDF download of the second issue, which will be produced in early 2014. That's right, you will get both the first and second issues of the magazine for being a backer of the Kickstarter campaign! Thanks everyone!

description: For the past ten years, I have created an average of 300+ daily horoscopes and astrology videos per year, fundraising for the last 40 days of the year to support my work. Each year, all of you, my wonderful community of subscribers, have stepped up to support my content for the year ahead. This year, I am attempting to break last year's record of 1,936 backers, and I have also set a base goal of raising $150,000 to support my work and my staff in 2025. The proceeds from this work help me support my family while hiring a team of dedicated workers who help me produce and manage the free content I create each day. Your financial support also allows me to offer need-based tuition options and many full scholarships to students who otherwise could not afford astrological training. Since 2010, around 60% of our students have been on tuition assistance or scholarships to our programs. This is possible because my community of subscribers supports my work and our programs. We cannot do this work without everyone pitching in each year, so I can't thank you enough for your help! So, why read, watch, or listen to my daily horoscopes? For thousands of years, spiritual seekers from all around the planet have turned to the stars to understand the fluctuations and changes in human experience. Astrology helps us understand our daily experiences, and it also allows us to see the fingerprints of divinity amid our everyday lives, from the most minor and insignificant events to the most profound and pivotal changes we experience. Astrology, when paired with cultivating our inner lives, helps us grow wiser and kinder, more patient, discerning, and understanding of ourselves and others. And for this reason, I believe my horoscopes are unique and valuable for those who listen. My mission and daily prayer are that the content I create might help people feel even more at home in this unique cosmos. My daily content is always aimed at providing people with humor, wisdom, joy, and spiritual depth. I combine simple story-telling with rich astrological insights to help people understand the nature of the day-to-day planetary aspects. I also love to share your amazing stories in my "grabbed" episodes along the way! Finally, I aim to provide people with spiritual content as an alternative to the never-ending drama of the news cycles, the constant algorithm advertisements, or whatever else may be subtly draining us rather than filling us up each day. On this note, my daily blogs come straight from the inspired space of my daily spiritual practices. So, when you watch my content each day, you can know that I am doing the following: Exercising and sitting for silent prayer each day Living a healthy lifestyle, I am conscientious about diet, sleep, and exercise, and I believe this plays a significant role in the quality of the content I create Studying sacred texts, along with the history of astrology Spending time in prayer and meditation on my topic of the day, and asking for guidance before I create my content Creating an outline for my talk I thoughtfully consider and respond to thousands of comments and emails throughout the year from my audience. This process usually takes me an average of three hours per day, and I do it year-round (mainly with a smile!). I do this work because I want my viewers to trust that I am trying my best to provide content with spiritual integrity. Whatever your beliefs and practices, I promise that if you support my work, I will continue to follow these daily practices. In 2024, I will continue to be a trusted and friendly source of astrological insight and story-telling. I will continue to share this heavenly wisdom from my heart as a servant so that others may find some glimmers of the same hope, faith, and goodness that I have been lucky enough to find in my relationship with astrology. Thank you so much for your support! 2025 goals: * This year, our number one goal is to rebuild our website so that it is better equipped to handle the new content I am developing * Continue to employ regular help for the production of my content on social media * Continue to employ a personal assistant * Continue to support a growing staff of helpers that ease the burden of content creation each day * Continue to make all of our 2025 speaker series programs accessible to the public while paying our speakers well above the industry standard for an online lecture * Continue to host free community herbal medicine events, and continue to give free herbal medicine to community members * Support the update of my filming technology and website so that I can continue to improve the quality of my content * Support the continued development of our Planets and Plants content (Astrology & Herbalism) * Continue pursuing my private astrology studies * Continue to support our need-based scholarships

title: SPIRITUAL SEEDS THAT REAP CHRIST-LIKENESS
description:
My name is Ken. I'm publishing my first education series, and I want to do it with your help. The Dream. Experience the advanced knowledge other Christ Quest Institute students have: •	God says in Romans 8:28, “All things happen for the good...” Why not discover God’s specific, beneficial answers for life’s specific problems? •	Why not literally illustrate Christ so that your family feels as though an encounter with you is as an encounter with Christ? •	Why not live the meaningful, fulfilling, purposeful  life that God has designed for you •	Why not provide the Christ-like spiritual leadership that ends the need for divorce? •	Why not become a living example, demonstrating a rare understanding of spiritual maturity? •	Why not raise children who pursue God because of your example? •	Why not learn how to reinterpret all of life’s events into personal, spiritual lessons from God? •	Why not gain an understanding of the inner motives of yourself and others? This series of books, when published, will offer an exact roadmap to Christ-likeness. 
Jesus said, “Come to Me, all you who labor and are heavy-laden and overburdened, and I will cause you to rest. [I will ease and relieve and refresh your souls.]  (AMP)  This series of books will offer that kind of relief to every student who applies its contents. The CQI DVD’s, from which this series of books are derived, have already, successfully provided clear and applicable answers in the lives of thousands of Christians. These published books will be offered in two forms: The FIRST form is a series of books that are of very rich, high quality.  You will want this treasure of information to be passed down from generation to generation.  It’s splendid, embossed, hard cover design is a showpiece; ready for the family library. The SECOND form of this series of books, are designed to be working textbooks.  Each book will be available to the student as a spiral bound textbook. I’ve asked thousands of students, “Are you familiar with the books of the bible named, First and Second Peter?”  Their answer is, “Yes.”  I continue, “Are you familiar with the books named, First and Second Timothy?”  Again they answer, “Yes.”  Next I remark, “Are you aware that the men who wrote those books were simply men like you and me.  That being the case, have you ever thought that God wants to write a First and Second (Fill in your name).” (see II Corinthians 3:2) The Christ Quest Institute, through these books, is dedicated to personally guiding you to becoming a living Epistle, known and read of all men! Some Insight. Excerpted from the book SPIRITUAL SEEDS THAT REAP CHRIST-LIKENESS volume one Although I was married in 1959 it wasn’t until 1971 that my circumstances caused me to recognize that I was in serious trouble.  So much so, that it forced me to recognize that I needed to do some deep soul-searching and challenge myself. I was staring at a disaster; caused by conducting my marriage from my perspective. All the time, God was patiently waiting for circumstances to drive me to a place where I would turn to Him for direction. The time had come for my Christianity to become more than mere words and theological habits. I needed to search out; re-evaluate my life and then pursue God’s goals for my life and marriage; which I discovered, meant becoming more and more like Christ to my wife and children. I needed to accept the responsibility (as the spiritual leader in my home) to lead as Christ would. Those are also the ultimate purposes of this book. To accomplish those purposes, here are Five Challenging Concepts (which are like spiritual seeds for the gardener who wants spiritual growth) all of which I believe God revealed to me and to which I hold myself accountable. These concepts (or spiritual seeds) yielded for me and I believe will yield for every Christian man… the same brand of Christianity the Apostles died for.
These concepts presented me with a challenge, and I’m passing that challenge on. Are we willing to become the Christ-like spiritual leaders in our homes that God requires we be?  Not so incidentally, that requires an intense commitment to God. A commitment wherein we will need to determine; are we willing to pay the sacrificial price required to gain the necessary understanding that will enable us to become more and more Christ-like! God is going to bring every man to a place where he will have to decide; am I willing to accept the challenge of literally illustrating Christ. Exemplifying the ability to respond to all of the events in my life as Christ would? Let me take this opportunity to mention, it definitely is the intent of this series of books, to present every Christian man with a challenge: The most demanding concepts he may ever face; requiring that he meet some of the strictest opportunities for illustrating Christ in his Christian home (and community) he may ever be confronted with. This challenge centers on the following Five Concepts or Five Spiritual Seeds that are presented in question form. They represent seeds that need to be nurtured so they can grow and provide spiritual nutrition; becoming a part of who we are; since it’s said, We are what we eat!” SEED #1, answers this question: What needs to happen before a man can prove he understands his own human spirit well enough that he would be qualified to be a Christ-like spiritual leader?  Proverb 25:28 requires that a man understand his own human spirit; warning him about the trouble he will encounter if he does not have, “…rule over his own spirit.” (KJV) If we do not recognize, nor specifically understand, our own human spirit, how is it even possible that we could build spiritual maturity in ourselves or in those we are supposed to be spiritually responsible for? SEED #2, answers this question: What is specifically involved in dying to self; how does a man put his own “flesh” to death?  II Cor. 7:1 clearly instructs us to, “… cleanse ourselves from all filthiness of the flesh and filthiness of the spirit.” (KJV)  Do we know how to recognize our flesh in all of its manifestations?  Do we even recognize when and how our flesh manifests itself in our everyday lives? Knowing these things enables a man to understand what is necessary to put his flesh to death, and allows him to acquire greater spiritual strength. SEED #3, answers this question: What is necessary before a man is capable of literally understanding the mind of a woman as Christ would/does?  This capability would enable a man to discover even more about spiritual leadership and spiritual power. Then too, I Peter 3:7, commands that we, “… live with our wives in an understanding way… that our prayers be not hindered.” (ESV) SEED #4, answers this question: What is involved in a man developing the ability to actually illustrate Christ to his wife, every day in his life?  We know that Christ was/is, spiritually mature! And, Ephesians 5:23-27 requires that we be like Christ to our wives; Romans 8:29 also requires that we be conformed to the image of God’s Son. If we are struggling in our marriages, and the evidence clearly illustrates that there is a lack of harmony and oneness, then Christ-likeness in a husband (the spiritual leader) is missing. What resources will we draw upon to discover when, where or how we are not Christ-like? What resources will we use to help us discover how to discipline ourselves, so we would more effectively illustrate Christ not only to our wives but to everyone? How about drawing upon the help that God ordained for us? Do you know what that help is? SEED #5, answers this question: How does a man successfully discern God’s intentions and directions in every situation? Can he interpret all of life’s physical events, in such a way that he is able to turn them into beneficial spiritual lessons?  Thereby, daily, being an example; leading his family into victorious living? Romans 8:28 says, “…all things work together and are [fitting into a plan] for good …” (AMP) What is the evidence that we specifically do understand, what the good is, that God wants to accomplish in everything that take place in our lives, daily? If we are going to be an illustration of Christ to the world, doesn’t it make sense that we would have to learn these Five Concepts (plant these Five Spiritual Seeds in our own spiritual lives) before we could teach them to others? Doesn’t it make sense that we cannot bring stability to the Christian community without personally providing the kind of spiritual leadership (especially in our marriages) that Christ would? Doesn’t it make sense that we cannot bring stability to Christian marriages if we do not understand our own wife’s heart/spirit so well that in each situation we face in our marriages, our wives feel as though they’ve had an encounter with Christ? Doesn’t it make sense that we cannot offer the hope of stability in the homes of other Christian, unless we are the kind of Christ-like example in our own home that brings stability? That, like Christ, we would know our wife’s spirit so well that we know what our wives would be thinking in a given situation? We aren’t guessing or thinking we know… we know! And, our wives will readily testify that we do indeed, know what their thinking would be in a given situation? Doesn’t it make sense that we would not be able to pass on the necessary knowledge or specific understanding about the human spirit, if we don’t thoroughly understand our own human spirits, nor our wife’s spirit? Doesn’t it make sense that if we are unable to specifically identify each of our emotions, its living proof that we are emotionally unfamiliar and dysfunctional? And when that’s true, we will not be able to identify with the emotional needs of our wives (or others) and as such we will not be capable of caring for their emotional needs. That being the case we will not be able to minister to them as Christ would.  (See Hebrews 4:14-16) Now, having said all of that, may I propose that the most significant means God has devised; so we could develop spiritual understanding and leadership, is found within the framework of the home… marriage! It is the most challenging institution for building relationships! Marriage is the first institution that God Himself established. His purpose is, that it is the most significant and demanding resource for training a man to illustrate Christ. Marriage presents circumstances wherein a man’s true nature and character are revealed. Then, as necessary, he can alter his normal-to-man character; that it be conformed to Christ’s character. Marriage is also meant to be the most rewarding relationship in the world! Concerning this role of spiritual leadership and how serious God is about it: read Malachi 2:13-15. (NASB) In it, God explains what His expectations are of spiritual leadership; expectations that require a high degree of spiritual awareness, along with a complete ability to understand our own spirit and the spirits of our wives.  Then, we can successfully provide Christ-like care for our wives and others.
