We'd also like to thank the people behind our video! It was shot and edited by Squirl Corp with original composition by Jim Heffernan of TTO Productions . Thanks to Sidney Bensimon for our portrait too!

title: The Balance Bound Planner
description: Sign up for the Balance Bound Planner newsletter for exclusive access to freebies, info on NEW production plans for the 2020 planner, and future planner promotions! Learn more about the Balance Bound Planner at www.balancebound.co Follow along and join the self-care conversation on Instagram and Facebook Helping chronically busy and burnt out people create order, set boundaries, and prioritize self-care. Imagine what you could accomplish if you could see all of your daily tasks sorted by the different roles you play, allowing you to set clear boundaries to prioritize your own well-being — all in one beautifully designed, user-friendly planner. What would your life look like? By dividing your daily tasks into four signature sections — work, self, others, and home — the Balance Bound Planner creates order for all of your to-dos, and helps you visually take note of tasks you need to delegate in order to build in time for self-care. The innovative layout also gives you space for goal setting and note taking — while still leaving plenty of room for flexibility and personalization. Unlike other planners that squeeze a full week onto one spread (not enough space!), or designate one page per day (too much wasted space!), the Balance Bound Planner format features the perfect balance: TWO spreads per week. Spread one has space for your weekly goals & priorities, and vertical columns for Monday through Friday, all divided into the four signature sections. Spread two includes columns for Saturday and Sunday, a dedicated section for reminders and lists, and a FULL PAGE for notes, journaling, or whatever best suits you. We've worked to refine and perfect the design for months, maximizing every inch of space in a crisp, clean black & white layout. The book bound format, which is especially lefty-friendly, is available in three cover designs to suit your style: colorful stripe, monochromatic arches, or classic charcoal linen. Additional planner features: January through December 2020 dated layout 7" x 9" hardcover 60# smooth white opaque interior paper 256 total pages, including several blank notes pages at the end Two spreads (four pages) per week Space for each evening’s dinner plan Free space below each day's column to jot down quick notes 12 monthly calendar spreads with space for your monthly goals Space to keep track of a word/phrase of the year and yearly goals A satin ribbon placeholder Numbered pages for easy reference/indexing Designed and produced in Pennsylvania, USA No matter if you work for yourself or someone else; have a traditional job or volunteer; care for kids, parents, or four-legged family members; live in a dorm or own a home… you still juggle many responsibilities . On top of caring for everything and everyone else, you also need to take care of yourself. Now there's a planner to help you do just that. "THE BEST PLANNER I'VE EVER USED" Over the past year, we've tested and refined the Balance Bound Planner concept. We've also had several friends and planner aficionados do the same, using their feedback to make carefully considered changes. Here's some of the great feedback and praise we have received: "Seriously, this is the best planner I've ever used — and I've used a lot of planners!" "The Balance Bound Planner has helped me re-shape the way I think about my priorities. What other tool can tell you, at a glance, what areas of your life are receiving the most attention? And recognizing how much better I feel at the end of a well-balanced week is worth its weight in gold." "I’m not sure it would be an exaggeration to say the Balance Bound Planner changed my life!!" "When I fill up the 'others' area and have nothing in 'self,' it gives me pause and makes me contemplate how I can set aside time for me in the next few days." "I had always been intimidated by planners before, because of the pressure to fill out so many sections even though a lot were irrelevant to me, so I was instantly drawn to the simplicity of the Balance Bound layout. Yet, compared to my old blank notebook, I am astonished by how easy it is to feel organized and be able to flip back and track items and categories effortlessly." "I crave free space in my planner for miscellaneous lists, notes, thoughts, quotes, ideas. The Balance Bound Planner provides this space in a logical and perfectly designed format." "As a mom of three, I especially appreciate having a category for taking care of myself (WHAT?!) which plays a larger than expected role in my awareness of when I’m really feeling overly drained and need to prioritize myself for a minute." ADDITIONAL PRODUCTS Some of our Kickstarter rewards include additional products: a 4" x 3" delegation sticky note pad, and a 5.5" x 8.5" undated daily note pad, shown above. LOWEST PRICES OFFERED! All pledge levels include US shipping — an offer that is ONLY available to Kickstarter supporters ! Once we meet our Kickstarter goal and sell the planner via our website (if inventory is available), shipping charges will be added, so pledge now and save 10% or more off the future retail total! A NOTE ON "SELF-CARE" “Self-care.” It’s a buzzword. It’s everywhere. We wrestled with how to use the term in an authentic and meaningful way when describing the Balance Bound Planner that doesn’t just add to the self-care “industry” noise we’re being bombarded with every day. We want to do more than talk about getting mani-pedis and using sheet masks. While that pampering and indulgence may well be part of your self-care routine, it should be but a small piece of a larger puzzle. The concept of self-care actually became a political act during the rise of the women’s movement and the civil rights movement in the 1960s. It’s a concept that most often has a resurgence in popularity after a big or traumatic event — 9/11, the 2016 Presidential election — and its roots are tied to marginalized and oppressed communities who needed to practice self-care in order to get back up again each day and continue fighting for justice and equity. The self-care industry that we know today has left those communities behind and morphed into something much more superficial. We’re not into that. We ARE into honoring and amplifying the origins and history of self-care; recognizing the important role that self-care plays in a time that has challenged many of us to become activists; and creating thoughtful discussions around the type of ongoing self-care that allows us to be better humans who leave a positive mark on society . Check out the links on our website for a few great articles about the origins and history of self-care. FINANCIAL GOALS & FUTURE PLANS We calculated our Kickstarter goal based on several factors and expenses, the largest of which is printing an initial run of 1,000 Balance Bound Planners, which will cost approximately $32,000. This price tag is due to the small quantity (which results in a higher cost per planner), the quality materials to be used, the intricacy required for binding, and the fact that we have made it a priority to keep all production in the US . Additional funds will go towards freight from the printer, humidity-controlled storage, shipping costs (US shipping is included for all backer levels!), packaging materials, Kickstarter and Stripe fees (required of all successful campaigns), and printing the additional products that are available at our various backer levels. We have also built in some wiggle room for unforeseen expenses and to allow our small business to keep running while working hard to fulfill all Kickstarter rewards. If we reach our Kickstarter goal, it will also allow us to plan for future products that align with the Balance Bound brand. Potential future products include: academic year (August through July) planner versions evergreen/undated planner versions additional cover design options customized pens & desk accessories wall calendars desk calendars notebooks & journals ABOUT CURIOUS & CO. CREATIVE Curious & Co. Creative is an award-winning boutique design agency owned by Terry Packard Baker and Brooke Forry. Since 2010, we have created standout visual brands — logos, color palettes, and all the intricate visuals that represent a brand — for clients in a variety of industries. While visual branding has long been the foundation of our business, we never shy away from other creative pursuits that allow us to flex our creative muscles and reach a broader audience through impactful design. In 2017, we launched We the Postcard , a line of patriotic postcards encouraging individuals to take an active part in our government — and in the process were able to donate thousands of dollars to the ACLU, Americans for the Arts, and the NRDC via 50% of all We the Postcard proceeds. Curious & Co. has won a Best of Philly award for Stationery Design from Philadelphia Magazine, and an AIGA Philly Design Award for branding design. The Balance Bound Planner is our latest creative passion project, resulting from Brooke's inability to find a day planner that provided the proper structure and space she needed to divide her daily tasks based on the different roles she plays, and with a clean, beautiful, and intuitive design to boot. Terry and Brooke both live in the suburbs of Philadelphia (Wayne and Media, respectively) with their husbands and young children. - - - - - - - - - - Please note that all products, as shown in video and images, are prototypes or renderings. We have worked hard to create accurate visual representations to the best of our ability, and will work alongside our printers to ensure high quality final products; final results may vary slightly in color and placement from what you see on-screen. International shipping may be subject to additional fees based on exact location. - - - - - - - Photography by Sarah McKay Photography Thank you to Burlap & Bean Roast House in Media, PA for allowing us to shoot video and images in their space (and the general everyday deliciousness and awesomeness) Thank you to Terrain Gardens in Devon, PA for allowing us to shoot images in their space, in particular Catherine King Thank you to Amandine Liepmann of ListBurst for her endless support and Kickstarter expertise - - - - - - - Explore the Balance Bound Planner website Learn more about Curious & Co. Creative , the design firm behind the Balance Bound Planner Find us on Instagram: @balanceboundplanner | @curiousandco

title: Miles Magazine: The Olympic Trials
description: A Journey of a Thousand Miles Begins with a Single Step. A foodie find. A new musical favorite. A hidden-gem travel destination. The Pacific Northwest is rich with discovery opportunities and our magazine will guide locals and visitors alike to the very best of it all. Those of us lucky enough to call the Pacific Northwest home know the thrill of rounding a bend on a mountain pass to discover a breathtaking view of the Cascades. Seattle, Portland, San Francisco and Vancouver, B.C. are among the most beautiful and culturally rich cities in the world. From the ocean, to the mountains, to the forests, canyons and desert, our region is so expansive and diverse there is always something new to discover. Miles Magazine will take you there. Inspiring Travelers Near & Far with Meaningful Experiences Our magazine focuses on travelers who are willing to be exposed to learning and becoming immersed in the culture of a new place. Miles Magazine is also of interest to locals looking to break out of their stale routines and discover or learn something new in the place they live. We focus on destinations with premiere events. Events that are deeply rooted within communities, have passionate followings and attract a significant number of visitors from afar. Each issue is focused around a feature city and an event with a large built-in audience as well as additional nearby destinations. The First Mile: Olympic Trials Our first issue, out on newsstands and the iPad in June, features Eugene, Oregon and the U.S. Track & Field Olympic Trials at historic Hayward Field. TRACKTOWN, USA — Meet the Olympic hopefuls. Discover the magic of Hayward Field and learn how Eugene became track & field's mecca. EUGENE HOTSPOTS — Guides to prominent Eugene neighborhoods, showcasing excellent dining, entertainment and lodging. HIT THE ROAD — Escape the hustle of the Track Trials to discover the nearby mountains, beaches, wineries, big city and more. Why Kickstart Miles? The Kickstarter campaign will cover our production costs associated with the Olympic Trials issue. The bulk of which includes our printing and distribution costs. Miles is put together by a team of veteran writers, photographers and designers who are passionate about the region they call home. After a brief flirtation with a business partner for Miles , we’re just not in a position to take on a financier without giving up too much of our creative control. We feel we need that control in order to be different. The magazine industry is struggling. We can’t afford to do the same cookie-cutter approach that others are doing. We need to take risks. So, we’re looking to you, our supporters, to help us with this Kickstarter campaign. The Rewards We're really excited about the gifts we're able to offer as a thank you for your support. Savage Expedition Gear Savage Expedition Gear is a supplier of preparedness.  They want you to be ready, both with the know-how and the gear, to help yourself or others live through disasters. At the $35 level you can choose between: Key Grenade Paracord Key Chain — Not only does it carry the most paracord in such a small space and have a built in fire starter stick, but it looks like a little grenade!  How great is that?  You can even carry your keys by the Key Grenade handle, or attach a carabiner  there! Every Savage Expedition Gear Key Grenade is equipped with 7-8 feet of 7 strand 550 test US made paracord (survival cord) that can be used in a multitude of survival or emergency situations. Key Line Paracord Key Chain — An attractive way to make your keys way more hardcore.  Not only does this product look great, but it can get you out of a bind! Every Savage Expedition Gear Key Line is equipped with 5 feet of 7 strand 550 test US made paracord (survival cord) that can be used in a multitude of survival or emergency situations.  If you select a single color, your product will be one single strand.  If you choose 2 colors, it will have 2 shorter strands. At the $75 level you get: S.L.A.B. Wrist-Belt — The (Snare it, Light it, and Bind it) S.L.A.B. Wrist-Belt by Savage Expedition Gear is a wrist worn belt (bracelet just isn‘t a tough enough word for this product) that is a miniature version of the S.L.A.B. belt.  The S.L.A.B. Wrist-Belt is hand woven and wrapped right  in the Pacific Northwest, and offers a outdoorsy look that says, "I have tons of other survival gear just out of sight, just throw something crazy my way and watch what I do." Snare it, with 2 feet of animal snare wire integrated in the band. Light it, with a ¾ inch flint fire starter stick. And Bind it with 10 feet of super strong paracord! At the $100 level you can choose between: S.L.A.B. Belt — The (Snare it, Light it, and Bind it) S.L.A.B. Belt by Savage Expedition Gear is a revolutionary product that takes survival gear to the next level.  The belt and the buckle are both hand woven and wrapped right  in the Pacific Northwest, and offer a stylish fresh look as well as more functionality than any other belt on the market. Snare it, with 20 feet of animal snare wire in the buckle. Light it, with a 2 inch flint fire starter stick. And Bind it with 60 to 75 feet of super strong paracord! Savage Suave Belt — The Savage Suave Belt by Savage Expedition Gear is spittin image of the S.L.A.B. Belt without the totally intense outdoors look of the snare wire and fire starter buckle.  The belt is hand woven, not braided, to make a super strong, great looking, and functional belt.  You have your choice of either a double dee-ring style buckle, or a solid chrome metal style buckle.  Make your choice, you won’t regret it! Every belt is equipped with 60-75 feet (depending on belt size) of 7 strand 550 test US made paracord (survival cord) that can be used in a multitude of survival or emergency situations.  From shelter ties, bear bags, and fishing line, to truck tie-downs, emergency dog leashes, and rescue lines, this amount of paracord is almost necessary to have on your body at all times.  Even more importantly, approximately 30 feet of the paracord is accessible as lightning fast deploy; information on how to use this feature included with the S.L.A.B. Belt. At the $150 level you get: S.L.A.B. Combo Pack which includes the S.L.A.B. Belt and S.L.A.B. Wrist-Belt. Celadon Road: Celadon Road is dedicated to promoting greener, healthier and more socially responsible living by offering their customers the highest quality natural, eco-friendly products for their everyday needs.  Celadon Road inspires individuals to discover and explore their shade of green and to purchase with a purpose. They strongly believe if we all make small changes toward a greener lifestyle, collectively we can have a profound impact on our world. At the $50 level you get: Leaf Imprint Journal — Looking for inspiration? These journals with a whimsical design will allow your thoughts to pour onto the page. Whether it be a future best-seller or simply your everyday to-do list, they are perfect for on-the-go. The entire journal is handmade by Indian artisans and in their culture, it is a gift that is passed down from generation to generation. Make this fair-trade gift a part of yours! title: There is No Pose: The Yoga Better Movement Manual
description: A Book to Transform Your Understanding of Your Body Ready to More Deeply Understand Your Body? The Yoga Better Movement Manual by Andrew Royal Dugas solves problems we all have in yoga, sports, fitness, exercise with a lifetime of research packed into a book you will study for years and reference for decades. From the simplest beginner movements at any age to contortion to the highest movement performers to our grand-mother having trouble getting out of a chair We have created a huge Knowledge Base of Tools and Perspectives that empowers each of us to grow, avoid injury, recover, and improve. Movement practitioners: Do you have persistent injuries? Do you see your students get hurt and not know how to help them? Worse, do you not know how to protect your students from injuring themselves again? Are you tired of the old answers and ready for new tools to evolve your movement practice? Are you ready for a new approach to teaching? Yoga Better is the result of a life spent creating solutions to problems in all movement practices. Everything That Makes a Difference More than 1,000 Posture Variations , many of our invention, meticulously detailed. More than 100 Important Supporting Practices , with full chapters detailing our unique approach. Clarifying your thinking of body issues Pain Discomfort Measuring Progress Understanding Your Limits Suffering Goal Setting How to Learn and Progress as an Adult Making Positions Easier/More Difficult What is Mobility What is Strength How to Stretch Without Injury Balance Stress Relation Meditation A Life's Work Finally Gets Published Who is Andrew and what is Yoga Better? I am a transformer. I came to yoga with chronic back, neck, and shoulder pain and transformed my body with the help of a modern master of yoga, Robert Boustany. Yet, from the very beginning, I began accruing injuries from the traditional practice which created a new injury every month for years. Tears to everything in both of my knees, shoulders, hips, strains to my wrists, and the final straw, a herniated disc in my Lumbar spine. In 2006 I began asking what we can do better and began the process of putting everything I knew about human movement on the table and asking important questions. We call these The Big Four : What does this movement proport to give us? What does it actually deliver? What are the movement’s injury risks? Is there anything better, that gives us the benefits with fewer risks? If it didn’t deliver something we wanted, or there was something better, I got rid of it, no matter how much I liked it. No sacred cows. And from that point, two important things happened: I stopped getting injured doing yoga. My students stopped getting injured in my classes. In the nearly 20 years of applying those questions to everything we do and teach, a new style was born, transforming the yoga scene in Houston, Texas. The writing for the Yoga Better Manual Series comes from the development of the Yoga Better Training, a leadership course helping people transform their health and their lives since 2014. In the series: The YB Anatomy Manual The YB Postures Manual (This campaign!) The YB Pedagogy Manual The YB Business Manual The YB Assessment Manual (Coming Next Year!) The YB Movement Sequencing Manual I set out to systematize and document everything I know about human movement and this book, The Yoga Better Postures Manual, is quite literally my life’s work. What We Do Better Than Everyone The Yoga Better Postures Manual doesn’t keep any secrets. Why do you keep having Shoulder issues in class? How do I have a healthy Spine at 90? What is Balance, and how can I improve it? Why do I get sciatica and plantar fasciitis, and how can I never have them again? Why am I out of breath when I work out? Anatomy, physiology, and mechanics I have added more than 10 major insights into anatomy that transform our understanding of and control over human movement Every insight is based on the most comprehensive understanding of Anatomy in the business Breathing mechanics We regularly increase people’s lung capacity by 200% You’ll be able to use breathing to control your stress response and heartrate when exercising Huge insights into what breathing is and how to overcome your limitations Balance mechanics I had the worst balance of every person in every class I taught for the first 8 years of teaching. There was nothing to support me figuring out what the issue was, so I wrote a book on it, completely included in this Manual! Defining Profoundly Important and Helpful Ideas I have taken the time to define hundreds of ideas that empower us to better health, including "Empowerment", "Better", and "Health"! Why you can trust Yoga Better: My goal has always been to empower you to trust a set of tools you can use in any environment. I have self-studied here in Houston, Texas, with hundreds of books on anatomy and mechanics. I have written an anatomy manual that I use to train Medical Doctors in human movement and tested every word of this book and this style in nearly 50,000 classes, only keeping what works better. This movement style evolves because the goals is not to be ‘right’. The goal is that you feel better and are empowered to make yourself feel better for the rest of your life. That must be an ever-changing solution set as we learn more and discover new answers to the big 4 questions that evoked this whole journey. Timeline / Where The Book Is The document has been 95% complete for many years. The largest roadblocks being the immense task of creating the illustrations and photography for so many ideas and postures. Each illustration is hand-drawn by me. Each photo was taken by me. Currently the the book has more than 2,000 photos and illustrations, 100,000 words, and is more than 300 pages. Why We Need You There are about 10% more photos to take and place in the document and a few more rounds of editing. You contributing to this project will primarily fund the color printing of the highest level, allowing me to spend my free time to focus on making this first edition the best it can possibly be without taking on debt. REWARD LEVELS

title: The Future Generation - Second Edition!
description: "The Original Punk Parent Zine." Ariel Gore "Martens has been writing since long before the mommy wars were a media trope, but her work is a powerful response to punditry casting institutional and political problems as personal issues of 'work-life balance' for mothers (notably, not fathers)." - Lisa Jervis, Bitch Magazine China Martens started The Future Generation , a pioneer of the genre, especially when it comes to mamazines, in 1990. She was a young anarchist punk rock mother who didn't feel that the mamas in her community had enough support, so she began publishing articles on radical parenting in an age before the Internet made such a thing easy. Atomic Books published The Future Generation: The Zine-Book For Subculture Parents, Friends, & Others in 2007, an anthology of the best from issues of The Future Generation , the zine. For the first time, the 16 years of her zine and parenting writing life came together. This zine-book used individual issues as chapters, focused on personal writing, and retained the character of a zine that changed over the years—growing from her daughter’s birth to teenagehood and beyond. NOW – with your help – we will publish the tenth year anniversary, second edition!!! Help get The Future Generation back in print again!!! Pre-order and get a signed second edition copy delivered to your house in early March, 2017! With a new color cover and ALL NEW AFTERWORD written by China's daughter. And many other exciting zine perks. Why you should support (in any way that you can) this campaign: Because China Martens is an awesome writer and thinker! To celebrate the past and gather strength for the future! Because it is a historic 10th year commemorative issue of badass fierceness! TFG remains a timeless resource for parents, caregivers and those who care about them. Though first published in 1990, many of the essays and observations--about parenting, children, and surviving in a hostile political climate--still ring true today. To support a local (Baltimore) small press – Atomic Book Company. Because Writing is Important to envision a better world! And it’s a terrible job. Because this zine-book is her life’s work and putting it back in print gives it a chance to share with a wider audience and live on. Because China's grown 28-year-old daughter is going to write a new updated afterword – and you know you want to read that!!!!!! It’s the Anti-trump thing to do! Support single mother stories. This book is a documentation of the visceral, day to day, experience of being a poor, single mother in the 90s.  What it was like to be a poor single mother during 'welfare reform' and how it affected the day to day life of poor mothers. Because it’s the only publication of its kind, a vital alternative, and much needed resource. Because this book will inspire a new generation of artists and mothers trying to survive. Parenting is a political act and we need a network to build a sustainable future – for everyone – not just individuals or individual family members but all people everywhere. Navigating our world under its current political climate requires alternatives if we are going to survive. This book is necessary now and into the future to provide vision and camaraderie. The next four years are going to be especially demanding for those trying to balance parenting, politics, and survival. We're going to need the voices and experiences in TFG now more than ever. Because the future generation is our only hope!

title: Categories
description: NEW! Limited Haunted Ohio Mugs to "Add On" ANY PLEDGE . (To learn how, go here ). THE SEED Did you think Maine has a monopoly on horror? Think again... Truth be told, a multitude of well-known, award-winning authors of horror populate Ohio. Earlier this year, Cracked Skull Press, an assembly of Ohio-based horror writers Rami Ungar , Ray Pantle , Randall Drum , and David Day , formed with a single goal: to create an anthology of terrifying tales featuring their strange state. That Which Cannot Be Undone: An Ohio Horror Anthology will be published through Cracked Skull Press and is slated to be released in October of 2022. The anthology will include twenty stories that are a conglomeration of different horror subtypes. Whether supernatural, psychological, gothic, slasher, or eldritch, what unites each story is that they are all set in Ohio and revolve around the theme of "that which cannot be undone." THE EDITOR From the day she was born, Bram Stoker Award-winner Jessica Landry has always been attracted to the darker things in life. Since then, Jessica’s fiction has appeared in many anthologies, including Tales of the Lost , Twice-Told: A Collection of Doubles , Monsters of Any Kind , Where Nightmares Come From, Lost Highways: Dark Fictions from the Road , and Fantastic Tales of Terror , among others. Her debut collection, The Mother Wound , is out now from Independent Legions Publishing, which includes her Bram Stoker Award-winning short story, “Mutter,” and her Shirley Jackson Award-nominated novelette, “I Will Find You, Even in the Dark.” As well, There Is No Death, There Are No Dead , an anthology co-edited with Aaron J. French, is set to be released in August from Crystal Lake Publishing; and her short story, “Carbon Rites,” will be appearing in Aliens vs Predators: Ultimate Prey , edited by Jonathan Maberry and Bryan Thomas Schmidt, out December 2021 from Titan Books. THE CONTRIBUTORS Gary A. Braunbeck (Columbus) is a prolific author who writes mysteries, thrillers, science fiction, fantasy, horror, and mainstream literature. He is the author of 19 books; his fiction has been translated into Japanese, French, Italian, Russian and German. Nearly 200 of his short stories have appeared in various publications. His fiction has received several awards, including the Bram Stoker Award in 2003 for "Duty" and in 2005 for "We Now Pause for Station Identification"; his book Destinations Unknown won a Stoker in 2006. His novella "Kiss of the Mudman" received the International Horror Guild Award in 2005. Lucy A. Snyder (Columbus) is the Shirley Jackson Award-nominated and five-time Bram Stoker Award-winning author of 15 books and over 100 published short stories. Her most recent books are the collection Halloween Seaso n and the forthcoming novel The Girl With the Star-Stained Sou l. She also wrote the novels Spellbent, Shotgun Sorceres s, and Switchblade Goddes s, the nonfiction book Shooting Yourself in the Head for Fun and Profit: A Writer’s Survival Guid e, and the collections Garden of Eldritch Delight s, While the Black Stars Burn, Soft Apocalypses, Orchid Carousals, Sparks and Shadows, Chimeric Machines , and Installing Linux on a Dead Badge r. Her writing has been translated into French, Russian, Italian, Spanish, Czech, and Japanese editions and has appeared in publications such as Asimov’s Science Fiction, Apex Magazine, Nightmare Magazine, Pseudopod, Strange Horizons, and Best Horror of the Yea r. Critically acclaimed author Tim Waggoner (Dayton) has published over fifty novels and seven collections of short stories. He writes original dark fantasy and horror, as well as media tie-ins, and his articles on writing have appeared in numerous publications. He’s a three-time winner of the Bram Stoker Award, has won the HWA’s Mentor of the Year Award, and been a finalist for the Shirley Jackson Award, the Scribe Award, and the Splatterpunk Award. In addition, his fiction has received numerous Honorable Mentions in volumes of Best Horror of the Year , and he’s had several stories selected for inclusion in volumes of Year’s Best Hardcore Horror . He’s also a full-time tenured professor who teaches creative writing and composition at Sinclair College in Dayton, Ohio. Megan Hart (Dayton) writes books. Some of them use bad words, but most of the other words are okay. Some of them hit bestseller lists and win awards and some don’t, but that’s the way it goes. She can't live without music, the internet, or the ocean, but she and soda have achieved an amicable uncoupling. She loathes the feeling of corduroy or velvet, and modern art leaves her cold. She writes a little bit of everything from horror to romance, though she’s best known for writing steamy fiction that sometimes makes you cry. Gwendolyn Kiste (originally from Ohio) is the Bram Stoker Award-winning author of The Rust Maidens , from Trepidatio Publishing; And Her Smile Will Untether the Universe , from JournalStone; the dark fantasy novella, Pretty Marys All in a Row , from Broken Eye Books; the occult horror novelette, The Invention of Ghosts , from Nightscape Press; and the folk horror novel, Boneset & Feathers , with Broken Eye Books. Her short fiction and nonfiction have appeared in Nightmare Magazine, Tor's Nightfire, Vastarien, Black Static, Daily Science Fiction, Unnerving, Interzone, and LampLight, as well as Flame Tree Publishing's Gothic Fantasy series, among others. Kealan Patrick Burke Hailed by Booklist as “one of the most clever and original talents in contemporary horror,” Kealan Patrick Burke (Columbus) was born and raised in Ireland and emigrated to the United States a few weeks before 9/11. Since then, he has written five novels, among them the popular southern gothic slasher Kin , and over two hundred short stories and novellas, including Peekers , Blanky , Sour Candy , and The House on Abigail Lane , all of which are currently in development for film and TV. A five-time Bram Stoker Award-nominee, Burke won the award in 2005 for his coming-of-age novella The Turtle Boy , the first book in the acclaimed Timmy Quinn series. As editor, he helmed the anthologies Night Visions 12 , Taverns of the Dead , and Quietly Now , a tribute anthology to one of Burke’s influences, the late Charles L. Grant. Most recently, he completed a new novel, Mr. Stitch, a collection of novellas entitled Guests for Suntup Editions , and adapted Sour Candy as a graphic novel for John Carpenter's Night Terrors . Kealan is represented by Merrilee Heifetz at Writers House and Kassie Evashevski at Anonymous Content. Authors also contributing stories to the anthology include: Rob E. Boley D.M. Guay Patricia Lillie Tim McWhorter J. Thorn Ty Schwamberger Marvin Brown Steven Saus Weston Kincade Matt Betts And others... COVER ARTIST Cracked Skull Press provides nothing but the highest-quality cover art for its publications. Rooster Republic Press has partnered with our team to design the jacket cover for That Which Cannot Be Undone: An Ohio Horror Anthology. Rooster Republic Press publishes award-winning books and offers freelance design services. For nearly a decade, it has forged working relationships with fellow publishers and award-winning authors to create covers for strange and dark works. Those behind it are Nicholas Day, award-nominated author, editor, artist, and acquisition editor, and Don Noble, artist, writer, and designer, who handles the freelance cover art and production work.  You can take a look at their work here . No doubt their amazing talent will deliver visual art as superbly sinister as the stories you'll read. AUTHOR PAYMENT The staff of Cracked Skull Press believes that authors SHOULD and CAN be paid for their stories. We are committed to compensating all who contribute to the anthology. Here is the breakdown of how the funds will be used: THE TIMELINE This is our projected timeline for the anthology. However, all dates are subject to change. November 29, 2021: Launch Kickstarter December 19-January 28, 2022: Invite more authors January 28, 2022: Close Kickstarter March 31: Stories submitted August 1: Stories edited August 15: Format book and artwork September 1: Get print proof and print book October 1: Release anthology/ship books and perks to crowdfunding backers We are offering several pledge levels matched with a variety of “tempting” goodies that backers will receive. The various pledge options with the corresponding perks can be found to the right. 1) Five custom-designed, Ohio-inspired Tarot Cards: Death as Jeffrey Dahmer (grew up in Ohio/attended OSU) The Devil as Charles Manson (born in Cincinnati) The Tower as The Ohio State Reformatory ("Mansfield Reformatory") The Moon as The Moonville Tunnel Judgment as the Electric Chair from The Ohio State Reformatory 2) This exclusive, limited-edition creepy candle with dark earthy scents created by Missy Rose Studios . 3) Three custom pens featuring the Cracked Skull Press logo 4) Two silk bookmarks with the anthology title and bloody hands (RIBBON INCLUDED) (2" x 6") 5) One sticker of the Cracked Skull Press logo (3" x 4") 6) One hand-sewn, custom-made bag. We don't have a picture yet, but we guarantee it will be FANTASTIC! AND FOR THE MOST DEVOTED FREAKS: Haunted Ohio "Add On" Mugs Perfect for drinking blood, a mix of herbs to ward off demons, or just plain old coffee, this custom-designed, high-quality drinkware depicts six of the most infamous places in Ohio: The Ohio State Reformatory (aka "Mansfield Reformatory") Bellaire House in Bellaire, Ohio Moonville Tunnel Athens Lunatic Asylum Franklin Castle Licking County Jail To claim one, just use the Kickstarter "Add on" option to include it in your pledge. (To learn how, go here ). But there's a VERY limited supply, so make sure to grab yours right away. With your support, we will forge an anthology worthy of the rich tradition of Ohio horror. Every pledge helps, so please join us, and prepare to be scared by the terrors lurking within the Buckeye State! Appeared/Featured on: Live Interview on Horror Buzz Dungeon Crawlers Radio podcast Promote Horror Horror Tree The Daily Dead Northern Ohio Tourism: Ghostly Things

title: Book: She Handled It!® Adversity (Suspended)
description: I'm raising funds to launch my third book in the She Handled It!® series—a timely and inspiring work titled She Handled It!® Adversity: Stories of Courage, Faith, and the Power of One Moment . This book highlights women/girls who've faced major backlash for speaking out publicly against men in women's sports and spaces, and most importantly, how the difficulty made them stronger! If you've ever felt helpless in this fight, your support here is a direct, powerful way to stand with them. These aren't just stories of injustice—they are about how adversity forges incredible strength and resilience, while also serving to preserve this moment in women's history. Readers—especially women and girls—will be uplifted and empowered to trust their voice, follow God’s direction, and take bold action. It is shining a bright light on these women's extraordinary courage, resilience, and faith, sharing that inspiration with the world. The book features powerhouse voices like Riley Gaines (NCAA swimmer and leading advocate), Payton McNabb (the high school volleyball player whose life-altering injury sparked national awareness), Jen Sey (XX-XY Athletics founder), and Nancy Hogshead (Olympic gold medalist and Champion Women founder) and Tish Hyman (musician and future LA mayor!). It also includes 35 other courageous women, from a Real Housewives of Beverly Hills star fired for defending girls sports publicly, to 16-year-olds targeted by Antifa simply for sharing their stories from the stage. Each has endured fierce backlash, cancellation and threats—yet stood firm. Their testimonies reveal adversity's positive power: forged strength, deepened faith, and unshakeable resolve. Funding this initial phase through Kickstarter gives me maximum leverage with publishers (no upfront advances) allowing me to secure the strongest royalties. This way I can bolster the 50% of net proceeds I'm donating toward this mission through: XX-XY Athletics Courage Wins scholarship (for young women financially harmed by speaking out) Champion Women & ICONS for Title IX advocacy Together, through this book, I aim to raise ONE MILLION dollars for these vital causes. Funds from this campaign will cover essential costs for producing a polished, market-ready book that makes it highly attractive to publishers and strengthens my position to negotiate the best possible royalties for charity: $5,000 for 40+ interviews, reels, and highlights $7,500 for initial professional editing $2,500 for professional hype video $1,500 for social media marketing (podcasts, reels, YouTube, X) $1,000 for initial cover design $2,500 for XX-XY branded hats and stickers $3,500 for author copies and Kickstarter fulfillment shipping overage $1,500 for women-only reward package contents and shipping (up to 15 backers) Our goal: $25,000 to bring these courageous stories to life. Every dollar beyond that covers taxes, Kickstarter fees and processing fees ($5,000) first, then powers more podcast episodes with high-profile guests ($13,250 for 10). Every extra contribution multiplies the impact!! So, here is your chance, if you've ever felt helpless in this fight or unsure how to stand up for girls' fairness, this is your moment to act and add real positivity to the world. Back this project to protect women's sports/spaces, amplify faith-fueled courage, and receive a book you can place in a young woman's hands—a beacon of strength, resilience, and God's design for her power. Thank you for joining the movement! May God bless you richly! - Arwen #SaveWomensSports
